PN CN114389157-A
TI Cabinet for use in power distribution system, has driving unit provided with speed reducing motor installed on top wall of cabinet body, and screw rod is connected with control valve through transmission assembly with screw sleeve.
AU HU Y
   CHEN Z
AE NANJING SUJINSAIER ELECTRIC CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202260951L
AB    NOVELTY - The power distribution cabinet has a driving unit (300) provided with a speed reducing motor installed on a top wall of a cabinet body. A screw rod is connected with a control valve through a transmission assembly. The screw rod is in threaded connection with a screw sleeve. An end of the screw rod sleeve passes through an end of a flame retardant insulating plate (105) that is provided with sealing component for blocking a ventilation opening. An upper sealing cabinet door (102) is set on a side of the cabinet body. The flame retardant insulating plate is set on the inner side of the cabinet body (101). The two sides of the cabinet body are provided with a strip-shaped ventilation opening.
   USE - Cabinet for use in power distribution system.
   ADVANTAGE - The working state of the power distribution cabinet is reasonably sensed through the temperature control switch, when the temperature rises abnormally, the driving unit and the carbon dioxide gas storage unit quickly correspond to each other, and the speed reducing motor indirectly controls the control valve to open, so that the quick release of the carbon dioxide gas is realized, and the fire is timely extinguished.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for use in power distribution cabinet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of cabinet for use in power distribution system.
   Main unit (100)
   Upper sealing cabinet door (102)
   Lower sealing cabinet door (103)
   Flame retardant insulating plate (105)
   Emergency stop switch (106)
   Carbon dioxide gas storage unit (200)
   Driving unit (300)

PN CN114194582-A; CN114194582-B
TI Air-conditioned storage device for fruit, has refrigeration pipe that is provided on downward facing side of carrier partition and barrel blades that are evenly arranged on barrel housing of rotating barrel and air outlet head whose end face is evenly provided with air outlet holes.
AU JI W
   HAN W
   WANG Q
   LIU L
   ZHANG L
AE SHANDONG JOYWIN GREEN AGRIC DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202245141V
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a storage box (300) that is provided with several layers of carrier partitions. The top of the storage box is provided with a gas storage cylinder. The gas storage cylinder is filled with liquid carbon dioxide. The storage box is vertically inserted with a refrigerant inlet pipe and a refrigerant outlet pipe. The refrigerant inlet pipe is connected to the gas storage cylinder. The refrigerant outlet pipe is connected to a second air conduit (201). Air holes are evenly distributed on the carrier partition. A refrigeration pipe is provided on the downward facing side of the carrier partition. The inlet end of the refrigeration pipe is connected to a pipe housing of refrigerant inlet pipe, and the outlet end is connected to the pipe housing of refrigerant outlet pipe. Barrel blades are evenly arranged on a barrel housing of a rotating barrel. The end face of an air outlet head is evenly provided with air outlet holes.
   USE - air-conditioned storage device for fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses liquid carbon dioxide to cool the storage box, and the vaporized carbon dioxide enters the humidification tank for humidification and enters the storage box, thus increasing the humidity in the box, reducing the oxygen content in the box, and inhibiting the respiration of the fruit. The low temperature environment in the box greatly prolongs the fresh-keeping period of fruits and improves the effect of fresh-keeping.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air-conditioned storage device for fruit.
   Humidifying tank (100)
   First air conduit (200)
   Second air conduit (201)
   Storage box (300)

AU QIN J
AE CNFC ENERGY LTD (CNFC-Non-standard)
GA 202241177J
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a protective net cover (3) whose bottom end is set with an integrated annular base. A side seat is provided with a sliding column. A bottom end of the sliding column relative to a bottom of the side seat is fixed with an anti-dropping block. An inner sliding groove is provided with an inner sliding plate and a pull rod. A surface of the sliding column is provided with a circular hole. A surface of the side seat is provided with an L-shaped shifting block. An end of the pull rod is fixed with the inner sliding plate and the other end of the pull rod extends to an outer side of the side seat and is fixed with the L-shaped shifting block. Two inner springs are set between the inner sliding plate and an inner wall of the inner sliding groove.
   USE - Solar methanol reforming and recycling combined cycle process system of solar methanol reforming and recycling carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The protective net cover can be quickly disassembled and installed on the top part of the decomposing furnace, so that the dismounting convenience of the protective net can be greatly improved. The operation box is convenient for operating personnel and the assembling efficiency of the operation box can be improved, and the maintenance and repair of the top portion of decomposing furnaces can be performed easily and quickly.

PN CN114132926-A
TI Fluid distributor for use in activation furnace for producing active carbon, has distribution cavity connected to steam outlet pipe when range of steam inlets is adjusted to move to distribution cavity, and steam inlet pipe and steam outlet pipe connected with each other.
AU HUANG Z
AE FUJIAN SENSEN ACTIVATED CARBON IND SCI (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202244349N
AB    NOVELTY - The distributor has a fixing portion (200) provided with a steam outlet pipe (310). A distribution portion is attached to the fixing portion. The fixing portion is formed with a distribution cavity (211). A steam inlet pipe is connected with the distribution cavity. The distribution cavity is connected to the steam outlet pipe when range of steam inlets is adjusted to move to the distribution cavity. The steam inlet pipe and the steam outlet pipe are connected with each other. A distributing part (210) is fixed between the fixing portion and the adjusting part. The adjusting part is utilized for adjusting tension degree between the distributing part and the fixing portion. A cylinder (100) is fixed with a bracket (410).
   USE - Fluid distributor for use in an activation furnace for producing active carbon. Can also be used for producing carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide gas and carbon dioxide water.
   ADVANTAGE - The dispenser solves the problems of water vapor waste, low activation efficiency and high production material consumption caused by using active carbon physical method to activate the converter in the process of introducing water vapor in the existing technology. The distribution cavity is connected with the corresponding steam outlet pipe, so as to connect the steam inlet pipe and the corresponding steam outlet pipe when adjusting any steam outlet valve to move to the range corresponding to the distribution cavity, thus ensuring the normal operation of the fluid distributor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front sectional view of a fluid distributor.
   Cylinder (100)
   Fixing part (200)
   Distribution part (210)
   Distribution cavity (211)
   Steam outlet pipe (310)
   Steam outlet valve (330)
   Bracket (410)
   Shrinking piece (420)
   First supporting part (500)
   Second supporting part (510)
   Hose (630)

PT P
PN WO2022029739-A1
TI Silk fibroin hydrogel composition used in e.g. tissue engineering, regenerative medicine or drug screening, as replacement biomaterials, drug delivery platform, and as cell delivery platform comprises carbonic anhydrase.
AU PINTO RIBEIRO V
   ANTUNES CORREIA OLIVEIRA J
   DA SILVA CORREIA DE OLIVEIRA
   GONCALVES DOS REIS R L
AE A4TEC ASSOC ADVANCEMENT TISSUE ENG CELL (AFOU-Non-standard)
GA 202221730A
AB    NOVELTY - Silk fibroin hydrogel composition comprises carbonic anhydrase.
   USE - The hydrogel composition is used: in tissue engineering, regenerative medicine or drug screening; as replacement biomaterials, drug delivery platform, cell delivery platform, platforms for 3D cell culture and disease modelling, cell culture platform, cell encapsulation, organ development and tissue growth, artificial extracellular matrices, bioinks, scaffolds, or other biomedical and biological applications; in medicine, artificial extracellular matrix (all claimed); and in vivo and ex vivo, as artificial extracellular matrices, tissue disease three-dimensional (3D) in vitro models, biomedical and pharmacological applications, comprising drug release, drug discovery, and cancer therapy. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogel composition: provides high specificity and is non-toxic in nature, hence capable of being implanted in vivo without inducing adverse inflammatory responses; has excellent mechanical, biochemical and cellular properties coupled with its biocompatibility, flexibility, degradation properties, water-based processing; and has timely/spatially-responsive properties and cell encapsulation ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) preparing silk fibroin hydrogel comprising obtaining silk fibroin solution, adding peroxidase, preferably horseradish peroxidase, and peroxide solution, preferably hydrogen peroxide or calcium peroxide, to the silk fibroin solution, adding 1-900 mu g/ml, preferably 5-450 mu g/ml carbonic anhydrase solution to the silk fibroin solution containing the peroxidase and the peroxide solution to obtain pre-gel solution, inducing hydrogelation by incubating the solution at 37 degrees C for at least 30 minutes, preferably 30 minutes-1 hour to obtain a hydrogel, incubating the obtained hydrogel in carbon dioxide saturated environment, preferably carbon dioxide-saturated water or saturated carbon dioxide atmosphere, for at least 5 minutes, preferably 5-20 minutes, at 20-37 degrees C, to increase the beta -sheet content; and
   (2) composition comprising the silk fibroin hydrogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises step of placing the pre-gel solution inside polydimethylsiloxane molds, on tissue culture polystyrene coverslips or in 3D printing cartridges before inducing hydrogelation. The horseradish peroxidase concentration is 0.5-1 mg/ml, preferably 0.84 mg/ml. The peroxide solution comprises 0.2-0.4 wt.%, preferably 0.36 wt.% hydrogen peroxide. The peroxide solution is calcium peroxide solution, comprises 5-20 mg/ml, preferably 12 mg/ml calcium Preferred Components: The hydrogel is: enzymatically crosslinked by peroxidases, preferably horseradish peroxidase; or is enzymatically crosslinked in the presence of peroxide solutions, preferably hydrogen peroxide or calcium peroxide. The carbonic anhydrase is alpha -carbonic anhydrase, preferably cytosolic alpha -carbonic anhydrase. The carbonic anhydrase is immobilized into the silk fibroin hydrogel by di-tyrosine crosslinking. The beta -sheet content of the silk fibroin hydrogels increases by the carbonic anhydrase immobilization and activation in a carbon dioxide-saturated environment. The carbon dioxide saturated environment is carbon dioxide saturated water or saturated carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 1-16 wt.%, preferably 2-4 wt.% silk fibroin.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN IN202241003026-A
TI System for advanced indoor air quality (IAQ) control system and liquefied petroleum gas leak detector for cabin of commercial vehicle e.g. car, has sensors associated within housing to detect, monitor and control indoor airtight cabin.
AU LAWRENCE D I
   KANNAN R C
   B A
   S R
   P V
AE LAWRENCE D I (LAWR-Individual)
   KANNAN R C (KANN-Individual)
   B A (BAAA-Individual)
   S R (SRRR-Individual)
   P V (PVVV-Individual)
GA 202226303P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has several sensors (11-14) associated within a housing to detect, monitor and control an indoor airtight commercial vehicle cabin and transmits information automatically to an air-conditioning system (01). The sensor for temperature, relative humidity and carbon dioxide is operatively connected to the vehicle air conditioning system in communication with the sensors. The sensor detects liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) gas emission, where intensive temperatures conditions range is 20-25 degrees C, relative humidity is 45-65%, and the carbon dioxide is 0-1000 parts per million (ppm).
   USE - System for advanced IAQ control system and LPG leak detector for cabin of commercial vehicle e.g. car and taxi.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures comfortable journey to passengers by controlling indoor air characteristics including indoor temperature, relative humidity, particulates and carbon dioxide. The system provides an improved air quality of indoor air and identified LPG leak detection through continuous monitoring by sensors, so that a microcontroller compares with measured data to predetermined threshold values and operates the ventilation system. The processor transmits the signal to alarm to alert the passengers if the LPG Leak detector is outside a pre-determined range of standard, thus ensuring comfortable and safe journey to the passengers.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the advanced IAQ control system and the LPG leak detector in a state where from energy saver mode to swift mode of a vehicle cabin is being performed.
   Air-conditioning system (01)
   Temperature and humidity sensor (11)
   NDIR sensor (12)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The COF-5 material is two-dimensional sheet structure. PN CN113627666-A
TI Method for predicting value-added tax control-based carbon emission in energy economic research field, involves determining total carbon emission variation of to-be-predicted department after implementing tax rate regulation and next year.
AU ZHANG Y
   LI F
   CHENG H
AE CAS GEOGRAPHIC SCI & NATURAL RESOURCES (CAGN-C)
GA 2021D02458
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining multi-regional input-output (MRIO) data containing multiple areas. A value-added tax allowance of department, value-added tax income tax and import link value-added tax are calculated according to the MRIO data. A value added tax amount of the department is determined according to the value-added tax allowance of the same department, the value-added tax income and import value-added tax amount. A value-added tax rate adjusting parameter of the department to-be predicted is obtained to develop a linear situation reference index. A total carbon emission variation of the to-be-predicted department is determined according to the value added tax amount of the department to be predicted, the MRIO data, the value-added tax rate adjusting parameter and the development linear state reference index after implementing the value-added tax rate regulation and next year.
   USE - Method for predicting a value-added tax control-based carbon emission in an energy economic research field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively predicting the total carbon discharge variation of the to-be-predicted department after implementing the value Add Tax Rate regulation and next year, so that the MRIO data is used as the predicted data source, and the total Carbon dioxide change amount in the next year after the Carbon dioxide rate is adjusted and controlled effectively, thus providing the guidance effect for the appointment of the Carbon dioxide rate control policy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a model for predicting a value-added tax control-based carbon emission in an energy economic research field.

PN CN113603884-A; CN113603884-B
TI Manufacture of non-isocyanate polyurethane            involves reacting bio-based polyphenol and            epichlorohydrin under presence of tetrabutylammonium            halide as phase transfer catalyst to obtain bio-based            epoxy, reacting with carbon dioxide to obtain obtain            cyclic carbonate and reacting with diamine.
AU DUAN T
   ZHOU S
   ZHANG L
   LI J
   LI Z
   YAN R
   GUO K
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021D10330
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of non-isocyanate polyurethane                involves (i) reacting a bio-based polyphenol (I)                and epichlorohydrin under the presence of phase                transfer catalyst to obtain bio-based epoxy, (ii)                reacting the bio-based epoxy and carbon dioxide to                obtain five-membered cyclic carbonate under the                presence of a halogen bond donor catalyst and (iii)                reacting the five-membered cyclic carbonate and                diamine (III). The phase transfer catalyst is                tetrabutylammonium halide. The halogen bond donor                catalyst comprises amine compound (II).
   USE - Manufacture of non-isocyanate                polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of                non-isocyanate polyurethane by using economical and                easily available raw materials, and by green and                mild synthesis route, and with high atom                utilization rate, and reduced metal residues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of non-isocyanate polyurethane                involves (i) synthesizing bio-based epoxy by                reacting bio-based polyphenols and epichlorohydrin                under the presence of phase transfer catalyst to                obtain bio-based epoxy, (ii) reacting the bio-based                epoxy and carbon dioxide to obtain obtain                five-membered cyclic carbonate under the presence                of the halogen bond donor catalyst and (iii)                reacting the five-membered cyclic carbonate and                diamine of formula: H2N-R3-NH2 (III). The bio-based                phenol is of formula: HO-Ar'-R1-Ar'-OH (I). The                phase transfer catalyst is tetrabutylammonium                halide. The halogen bond donor catalyst comprises                amine compound of formula (II).
   R1 = 0-2C linear alkyl;Ar' = phenyl with 0-3C                branched alkyl substituents;R2 = H, linear or                branched 1-3C alkyl, 1-3C alkoxy, dimethylamino, or                pyrrolidinyl;X = halo;andR3 = polyether with                molecular weight of 200-2000 or 36C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phase transfer                  catalyst is preferably tetrabutylammonium                  bromide. The halogen bond donor catalyst (II) is                  preferably of formula (IV). The molar ratio of                  the bio-based phenol, epichlorohydrin and the                  phase transfer catalyst is 1:4:0.1-1:12:0.5. The                  diamine (III) is preferably polyetheramine D2000,                  polyetheramine D400, polyetheramine D230 or                  Priamine 1074 (RTM: low viscous, dimer diamine                  building block). In step (ii), the molar ratio of                  bio-based epoxy to halogen bond donor catalyst is                  1:0.01-1:0.05. In step (iii), the molar ratio of                  the functional groups of the five-membered cyclic                  carbonate carbonate to the amino groups in the                  diamine is 1:1.Preferred Process: The step (i) is performed                  by reacting at 80-100degrees Celsius, after the                  reaction is completed, adding an aqueous sodium                  hydroxide solution, extracting, drying, and                  filtering to obtain bio-based epoxide.Preferred Conditions: Th step (ii) is                  performed at reaction temperature of                  60-120degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours and at                  atmospheric pressure. In step (iii), the reaction                  temperature is 80-120degrees Celsius, and the                  reaction time is 12-24 hours.

AE HARBIN BOILER FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 2021C8158W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a shaft housing (2) that is fixed with an end enclosure. A circumferential surface of the shaft housing is inserted with multiple static ring control rods (5). The static ring control rods are fixed on a static ring (6). A movable ring (10) is sleeved on a shaft (1) and sealed with the shaft through an elastic sealing rings (4,7,9). The movable and static ring main sealing surface is formed between the movable rings and the static rings. The movable ring is connected with a fixed seat (12) on the shaft through an elastic device (11). The elastic device is provided with a first elastic device and a second elastic device. The first elastic device and a second elastic device are connected with each other.

The elastic sealing ring and the sealing head are sealed with the sealing surface, so that the leakage is prevented from happening.

PN CN113355518-A
TI Ammonia residue processing method, involves mixing            ammonia residue with isopropylamine, performing            solid-liquid separation on mixed turbid liquid            solution, performing solid-liquid separation on            alkali-removed residue, introducing carbon dioxide gas            for reaction, and precipitating.
AU DONG P
   JIANG H
   WANG L
   LIU Y
AE JIANGXI SIYUAN RENEWABLE RESOURCES CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7618T
AB    NOVELTY - Ammonia residue processing method, involves                (a) uniformly mixing ammonia residue with                isopropylamine at certain reaction temperature, and                performing reaction under condition of stirring,                (b) performing solid-liquid separation on mixed                turbid liquid solution after completing the                reaction to obtain dealkaline residue solid and                alkali solution, (c) performing solid-liquid                separation on alkali-removed residue, introducing                carbon dioxide gas for reaction for period of time,                and performing solid-liquid separation on mixed                turbid liquid obtained after reaction to obtain                calcium carbonate solid, (d) drying calcium                carbonate solid to obtain anhydrous calcium                carbonate product, (e) adjusting pH value of alkali                solution using acid, and performing solid-liquid                separation, and (f) repeatedly using regenerated                isopropylamine solution by low-temperature                distillation, continuously dissolving ammonia                residue, and precipitating.
   USE - Ammonia residue processing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of stacking of                residue and recovery of valuable metals in                residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ammonia residue processing method, involves                (a) uniformly mixing ammonia residue with                isopropylamine at certain reaction temperature, and                performing reaction under condition of stirring,                (b) performing solid-liquid separation on mixed                turbid liquid solution after completing the                reaction to obtain dealkaline residue solid and                alkali solution, (c) performing solid-liquid                separation on alkali-removed residue, introducing                proper amount of carbon dioxide gas for reaction                for period of time, and performing solid-liquid                separation on mixed turbid liquid obtained after                reaction to obtain calcium carbonate solid, (d)                drying calcium carbonate solid to obtain anhydrous                calcium carbonate product, (e) adjusting pH value                of alkali solution using acid, and performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain regenerated                isopropylamine solution and hydroxide precipitate                after solution is turbid, and (f) repeatedly using                regenerated isopropylamine solution by                low-temperature distillation, continuously                dissolving ammonia residue, and precipitating                hydroxide back to leaching system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the main                  components of ammonia residue comprise calcium                  sulfate, calcium hydroxide and metal, where the                  concentration of copper is less than or equal to                  0.01%, cadmium is less than or equal to 0.05%,                  lead is less than or equal to 0.2%, zinc is less                  than or equal to 0.2%, and controlling reaction                  temperature at below of 20 degrees C for 1-2                  hours, the addition amount of isopropylamine is                  excessive, and maintaining pH of mixed turbid                  liquid at greater than or equal to 13. In step                  (e), the main component of hydroxide precipitate                  comprises mixture of zinc hydroxide, lead                  hydroxide, copper hydroxide and cadmium                  hydroxide. Preferred Conditions: In step (c), the                  molar ratio of added amount of carbon dioxide and                  calcium content in distilled ammonia residue is                  1.2:1, and reacting for 1 hour. The step (d)                  involves (e) adjusting the alkali solution to pH                  of 8-9 with acid, where the acid comprises                  sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid,                  acetic acid or formic acid. The step (f) involves                  distilling isopropylamine at 35 degrees C, and                  precipitating hydroxide back to leaching system                  for secondary leaching. Preferred Device: The                  equipment used for solid-liquid separation is                  filter press, sedimentation centrifuge,                  sedimentation thickener, float clarifier, bag                  filter or separation column.

PN US2021226945-A1
TI Method for authenticating a genuine presence of a user, involves generating a probe for an electromagnetic radiation that corresponding to at least two of a water vapor, a carbon dioxide, and an oxygen absorption band with a wavelength between the various nanometers.
AU NEWELL A
   BUD A
AE IPROOV LTD (IPRO-Non-standard)
GA 2021822580
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating (102) a probe for an electromagnetic radiation corresponding to at least two of a water vapor, a carbon dioxide, and an oxygen absorption band. The electromagnetic radiation is provided with a wavelength between 760 nanometers and 2700 nanometers. The probe is modulated (104) according to a pattern without duplication when performing a succeeding authentication of the user using the probe. The modulated probe is directed (106) towards a body unit of the user. A response of the body unit is received (108) to the modulated probe. The data is sent that represents the response of the body unit to the modulated probe to a digital data processor.
   USE - Method for authenticating a genuine presence of a user.
   ADVANTAGE - Method prevents the successful use of recorded imagery by introducing the liveness tests that making a user perform certain gestures, reduce the user's convenience and user acceptance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (a) a mobile device for authenticating a genuine presence of a user;
   (b) an application for authenticating a genuine presence of a user in mobile device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for authenticating a genuine presence of a user.
   Generating a probe for an electromagnetic radiation corresponding to at least two of a water vapor, a carbon dioxide, and an oxygen absorption band (102)
   Modulating the probe according to a pattern without duplication when performing a succeeding authentication of the user using the probe (104)
   Directing the modulated probe towards a body unit of the user (106)
   Receiving the response of the body unit to the modulated probe (108)
   Authenticating through biometric template matching (110)

PN CN112970979-A
TI Bubble drink contains complex hawthorn concentrated juice, strawberry pulp, roselle concentrated juice, red jujube concentrated juice, dark plum concentrated juice, rose freeze-dried powder, hawthorn raw pulp and white granulated sugar.
AU YU C
   LI L
   SHI B
   HU D
AE HALMANA FOOD TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO LTD (HALM-Non-standard)
GA 202176175K
AB    NOVELTY - Bubble drink comprises 5-50 wt.% complex hawthorn concentrated juice, 0-5 wt.% strawberry pulp, 0-2 wt.% roselle concentrated juice, 0-2 wt.% red jujube concentrated juice, 0-5 wt.% dark plum concentrated juice, 0-1 wt.% rose freeze-dried powder, 0-1 wt.% hawthorn raw pulp, 1-10 wt.% white granulated sugar, and remainder of deoxygenated water (made up to 100 wt.%). The complex hawthorn concentrated juice comprises at least two hawthorn concentrated juice with soluble solid content. The bubble drink comprises carbon dioxide, where the gas content of carbon dioxide is 0.3-0.8%.
   USE - As bubble drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The bubble drink has good color, fragrance and taste, high juice content, strong hawthorn flavor, sour and sweet taste, unique flavor and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the bubble drink, which involves (a) adding white granulated sugar and rose freeze-dried powder to the same mass of deoxygenated water at 45-55 degrees C to dissolve and obtain mixed liquid (M1), (b) adding hawthorn concentrated juice, strawberry puree, roselle concentrated juice, red jujube concentrated juice, dark plum concentrated juice, and hawthorn puree into the mixed liquid (M1), and stirring and dissolving to obtain mixed liquid (M2), (c) performing ultra-high temperature (UHT) sterilization of the mixed liquid (M2) at 104-110 degrees C for 10-40 seconds, (d) adding the mixed liquid (M2) with the remaining deoxygenated water at less than 10 degrees C to constant volume, and obtaining the mixed liquid (M3), (e) keeping the temperature of the mixed liquid (M3) at 4-7 degrees C and filling with carbon dioxide gas to the target gas content and (f) filling and sterilizing by pasteurization at 50-85 degrees C for 5-30 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: In the bubble drink, the gas content of carbon dioxide is 0.36-0.44%. The drink comprises 23 wt.% complex hawthorn concentrated juice, 1 wt.% strawberry pulp, 0.6 wt.% roselle concentrated juice, 0.4 wt.% red jujube concentrated juice, 0.5 wt.% dark plum concentrated juice, 0.02 wt.% rose freeze-dried powder, 0.05 wt.% hawthorn raw pulp, 3.5 wt.% white granulated sugar and remainder of deoxygenated water (made up to 100 wt.%). Preferred Components: The complex hawthorn concentrated juice comprises first hawthorn concentrated juice, second hawthorn concentrated juice and third hawthorn concentrated juice. The first hawthorn concentrate juice has soluble solid content of greater than or equal to 46%. The second hawthorn concentrate juice has soluble solid content of greater than or equal to 50%. The third hawthorn concentrate juice has soluble solid content of greater than or equal to 50%. Preferred Method: In step (a), the temperature of deoxygenated water is 50 degrees C, and the temperature of the system during the dissolution process is 35-55 degrees C. The step (c)involves sterilizing at 105 degrees C for 30 seconds. In step (d), the temperature of deoxygenated water for constant volume is 3-8 degrees C. In step (e), the temperature maintained by the mixed liquid (M3) is 5 degrees C. The step (f) involves sterilizing by pasteurization at 60 degrees C for 10 minutes.

A switch is connected between the first battery pack and a battery combiner to block an external input. A driving voltage having an input from the first battery pack and the second battery pack to control an operation of the E-Mobility battery management system. The battery combiner includes a photo coupler to prevent complete discharge while an operation stop is delayed, and two resistance distribution circuits to reduce and deliver the driving voltage to the first and second battery packs. A cell pack (311) including a battery cell, and a BMS (312) operates electrically connect and disconnect the cell pack and a FET, is turned on. The BMS operates when a residual voltage of the cell pack is higher than a predetermined value, and the cell pack and the FET operate so that the voltage of the cell pack is output through a positive output terminal (313).

PN CN112143214-A
TI Latex glove comprises carbon dioxide-based polyether, diisocyanate, catalyst, chain extender, deionized water, natural rubber, sulfur, zinc dithiocarbamate, casein, and strong alkali.
AU ZHENG X
   LU D
AE TIANCHANG HENGSHENG MEDICAL DEVICES CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202103106P
AB    NOVELTY - Latex glove comprises 80-100 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polyether, 90-130 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 3-11 pts. wt. catalyst, 10-20 pts. wt. chain extender, 140-160 pts. wt. deionized water, 100-140 pts. wt. natural rubber, 2-6 pts. wt. sulfur, 2-5 pts. wt. zinc dithiocarbamate, 4-8 pts. wt. casein, and 3-7 pts. wt. strong alkali.
   USE - Used as latex glove.
   ADVANTAGE - The latex glove has strong performance in original tensile strength and elongation at break greatly retains original performance of tensile strength and elongation at break even after aging treatment, and shows obvious heat aging resistant performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing latex gloves, comprising (i) heating carbon dioxide-based polyether, diisocyanate, catalyst, and chain extender to 90-95 degrees C for reacting for 90-120 minutes to obtain prepolymer solution, (ii) adding deionized water into the prepolymer solution obtained in step (i) under high-speed stirring at 200-300 revolutions/minute for emulsification, where the emulsification time is 30-40 minutes to obtain emulsion, (iii) slowly adding natural rubber into the emulsion obtained in step (ii), uniformly stirring, smelting at 80-90 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and obtaining emulsion liquid, (iv) under the condition of 200-250 revolutions/minute, adding zinc dithiocarbamate, casein, and strong alkali in sequence, and vulcanizing at 60-80 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain latex material, (v) heating the hand mold to 110-120 degrees C for preheating, adding the latex material obtained in step (iv) to immerse for 5-10 seconds, introducing vulcanization box for drying, vulcanizing, molding, and demolding to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The diisocyanate comprises toluene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate and/or hexamethylene diisocyanate. The catalyst comprises dibutyltin dilaurate and/or stannous octoate. The chain extender comprises isophorone diamine (IPDA), 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid (DMPA) and/or 2,2-dimethylolbutanoic acid (DMBA). The zinc dithiocarbamate comprises zinc di-n-butyl dithiocarbamate and zinc diethyl dithiocarbamate. The strong alkali comprises sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) heating carbon dioxide-based polyether, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, dibutyltin dilaurate, and isophorone diamine to 90 degrees C, preferably 95 degrees C for reacting for 120 minutes, preferably 90 minutes to obtain prepolymer solution, (2) adding deionized water into the prepolymer solution under high-speed stirring at 200 revolutions/minute, preferably 300 revolutions/minute for emulsification, and the emulsification time is 40 minutes, preferably 30 minutes to obtain emulsion, (3) slowly adding natural rubber into the emulsion, evenly stirring, and smelting at 80 degrees C, preferably 90 degrees C for 2 hours, preferably 1 hour to obtain emulsion, (4) under the condition of 200 revolutions/minute, preferably 250 revolutions/minute, adding zinc di-n-butyldithiocarbamate, zinc diethyldithiocarbamate, casein and potassium hydroxide in sequence, and vulcanizing at 60 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C for 6 hours, preferably 4 hours to obtain latex material, and (5) heating the hand mold to 110 degrees C, preferably 120 degrees C for preheating, immersing the latex material for 5 seconds, preferably 10 seconds, introducing the vulcanization box for drying, vulcanizing, molding, and demolding to obtain product.

TI Modified carbonaceous material used e.g. in carbon dioxide-capturing acid-functionalized adsorbent, comprises hexagonal carbon networks in layered stacking structure and acidic functional groups bonded to hexagonal carbon networks.
AU WANG J
   CHOU H
   CHUNG P
AE ACAD SINICA (SNIC-C)
GA 2020C8326N
AB    NOVELTY - Modified carbonaceous material, comprises hexagonal carbon networks in a layered stacking structure and acidic functional groups bonded to the hexagonal carbon networks, where all or many acidic functional groups are covalently linked to in-plane sp2 carbons at edges of the hexagonal carbon networks, the many acidic functional groups at the sp2 carbons of the hexagonal carbon networks being greater in quantity than others covalently linked to sp3 carbons of the hexagonal carbon networks.
   USE - The material is useful in carbon dioxide-capturing acid-functionalized adsorbent and for cellulose hydrolysis (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has substantially no sp3 carbon peak in a 13C CP/MAS NMR spectrum of its, an ID/IG ratio of greater than or equal to 1 as measured by Raman spectroscopy, a C1s/O1s ratio of less than or equal to 3 as measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, a total acid site density of greater than or equal to 1.5 mmol/g, and an interlayer spacing of 0.34-0.37 nm as measured by X-ray diffraction; and exhibits good catalytic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide-capturing acid-functionalized adsorbent, comprising hexagonal carbon networks in a layered stacking structure and acidic functional groups bonded to the hexagonal carbon networks, where the hexagonal carbon networks substantially consist of aromatic carbons, and substantially all of the acidic functional groups are covalently linked to in-plane sp2 carbons at edges of the hexagonal carbon networks; and
   (2) cellulose hydrolysis, comprising mixing the modified carbonaceous material and a cellulose in an aqueous solution and hydrolyzing the cellulose at a predetermined temperature in the presence of the modified carbonaceous material as a catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic functional groups include at least one of sulfonic, carboxy, lactone and phenolic hydroxy groups. The modified carbonaceous material is: a nonporous material; prepared by modifying a pitch-based material with the acidic functional groups; and a pitch-based material treated with sulfuric acid, nitric acid or hypochlorite salt. The pitch-based material is further subjected to hydrothermal treatment after treated with the sulfuric acid. The modified carbonaceous material comprises the sulfonic groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.8 mmol/g, the phenolic hydroxy groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.5 mmol/g, the carboxy groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.1 mmol/g, and the lactone groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.4 mmol/g. The modified carbonaceous material comprises the phenolic hydroxy groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.3 mmol/g, the carboxy groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.2 mmol/g, and the lactone groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.3 mmol/g. The adsorbent is in a nonporous structure. The adsorbent is prepared by modifying a pitch-based material with the acidic functional groups. The adsorbent comprises the sulfonic groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.8 mmol/g, the phenolic hydroxy groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.5 mmol/g, the carboxy groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.1 mmol/g, and the lactone groups in a density of greater than or equal to 0.4 mmol/g.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI System used e.g. for forming carbon product,            comprises electrochemical cell comprising crucible with            molten mixture comprising calcium chloride, where            cathode and inert anode are positioned in molten            mixture and furnace has inert atmosphere.
AU RAPPLEYE D
   ZHANG C
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
   UNIV UTAH RES FOUND (UTAH-C)
GA 202069098W
AB    NOVELTY - System (100) comprises an electrochemical cell                (101) comprising a crucible (103) having a molten                calcium carbonate, calcium chloride and calcium                oxide mixture, where the cathode (104) and an inert                anode (106) are positioned in the molten mixture                and an inlet (109) for feeding carbon dioxide gas                into the molten mixture and a furnace having an                inert atmosphere (126), where the electrochemical                cell is sealed in the furnace having the inert                atmosphere. The inert anode is positioned a                distance from the cathode, where the distance is a                minimum distance between the inert anode and the                cathode to prevent contact between gas released at                the inert anode and the cathode. The system                comprises a spacer between the inert anode and the                cathode. The spacer is chosen from a sheath, a                membrane, a mesh and a spacer, where the surface                area of the inert anode is at least five-fold                larger than a surface area of the cathode. The                inert atmosphere is under a vacuum.
   USE - The system is useful for forming a carbon                product and in a power plant, steel mills and other                carbon dioxide emitting industrial processes to                utilize and convert carbon dioxide to high-value                carbon products such as graphite and used in energy                storage and electronic devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides the carbon product at a                high enough rate and at a cost-efficient rate to                offset the cost of a high value graphite product                useful in industries that rely on high purity                graphite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for forming a carbon product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                process of producing carbon.

PT P
PN CN110228887-A; CN110228887-B
TI Recovering carbon and nitrogen resources in            fermented biogas slurry of kitchen waste by stirring            waste in dinitrogen atmosphere, adding e.g. aluminum            solution, hydrothermally treating mixture, reacting            with ammonia gas and crystallizing it.
AU ZHOU T
   ZHAO Y
   WU S
   WU N
   GENG X
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2019819639
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synchronously recovering carbon and                nitrogen resources in a anaerobically-fermented                biogas slurry of a kitchen waste, involves (a)                adjusting pH of anaerobically-fermented biogas                slurry of the kitchen waste to 9-10, (b) stirring                the mixture in dinitrogen atmosphere, and adding                magnesium cation solution and aluminum cation                solution in a drop wise manner, (c) hydrothermally                treating suspension after precipitation occurs,                washing precipitate and collecting washed                precipitate to obtain a carbon resource product,                and (d) reacting ammonia gas with carbon dioxide to                form an ammonium hydrogen carbonate product and                crystallizing it to obtain a nitrogen resource                product.
   USE - The method is useful for synchronously                recovering carbon and nitrogen resources in                anaerobically-fermented biogas slurry of kitchen                waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective and                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system,                which comprises a fully automatic anaerobic                fermentation tank and an ammonia recovery device,                where the fully automatic anaerobic fermentation                tank comprises a tank portion, a mixer arranged on                the tank portion, a discharge port, a feed port, a                pH monitor, a temperature sensor, an N2 inlet port,                an air outlet port and multiple chemical liquid                automatic adding mechanisms and a water bath                heating jacket surrounding the tank portion, the                ammonia recovery device comprises an ammonia                recovery reactor, a carbon dioxide supply mechanism                and a condenser, and the ammonia recovery reactor                is respectively connected to an air outlet of the                fully automatic anaerobic fermentation tank and a                carbon dioxide supply mechanism, and the condenser                condenses ammonia carried in the gas outlet of the                automatic anaerobic fermentation tank outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  anaerobically-fermenting the kitchen waste,                  obtaining anaerobically-fermented biogas slurry                  of the kitchen waste and separating fermented                  residue. The method involves pulping the kitchen                  waste and deionized water by mass ratio of 1:1,                  anaerobically-fermenting the mixture, controlling                  temperature to 35 degrees C, adjusting and                  controlling pH to 5-6, performing natural                  fermentation, and reacting the mixture for 3                  days, where the pH is adjusted by using 4 M                  sodium hydroxide or 4M hydrochloric acid. In the                  step (b), the magnesium cation solution is a                  magnesium nitrate solution, and the aluminum                  cation solution is an aluminum nitrate solution,                  where the ratio of amount of magnesium cation                  ions in magnesium cation solution (mol), aluminum                  cations in aluminum cation solution (mol), and                  anaerobically-fermented biogas slurry of the                  kitchen waste (l) is 0.3:0.15:5. The step (c)                  involves hydrothermally-treating the mixture at                  70 degrees C for 24 hours. The step (c) involves                  obtaining carbon resource product, preferably an                  organic acid-based double metal hydroxide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification.    MARTIN F S (MART-Individual)
GA 201967516E
AB    NOVELTY - The insect-controlling water movement system comprises underwater insect attractant device for generating carbon dioxide in a water extermination zone, where underwater insect attractant device lures insects to cultivate future insect generations in the water extermination zone for pre-terrestrial, insect stages, and a water pumping device having one filter configured to function in cooperation with the underwater insect attractant device. The water pumping device directs flow of water containing the future insect generations from the water extermination zone to the one filter of the water pumping device or to a desired location. The underwater insect attractant device contains mycelia. The water pump device comprises a portable solar-powered pump (17) having plumbing parts coupled for directing the flow of water as desired.
   USE - Insect-controlling water movement system.
   ADVANTAGE - The insect-controlling water movement system is easily assembled and disassembled without need of tools according to a reliable, tight interference fit among component parts allowing for easy cleaning and repairs, portable, cost effective, and environmental friendly solution for eradicating insects that lay their eggs in water bodies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for eradicating insect populations, which involves forming a water extermination zone in an area that stores a volume of water, introducing carbon dioxide to the water extermination zone to attract insects that lay eggs in water to the water extermination zone luring insects to use the water, extermination zone to reproduce insect progeny, pumping the volume of water containing the insect progeny through one filter of a water pumping device, and controlling insect populations by trapping and drowning the insect progeny; and
   (2) an insect-population control system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of insect-controlling water movement system.
   Pump (10)
   Intake chamber (15)
   Solar-powered pump (17)

AU YANG Z
   ZHANG X

   LI Y
   HE W
AE HUDONG ZHONGHUA SHIPBUILDING GROUP CO LT (CHSB-C)
GA 2018A53344
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves processing a groove (6) surface of 20-40 degrees C at the ends of a first side outer panel (1) and a second side outer panel (2). The first side outer panel is tilted to the tire frame to maintain a set angle between the bottom edge and the centerline of the hull. A circular steel liner (3) is welded and fixed to the root edge of the groove surface of the first side outer panel. The groove surface of the first side outer panel and the groove surface of the second side outer panel together form a V-shaped symmetrical groove having a groove angle of 40-80 degrees C. A bottom welding is performed by using a carbon dioxide gas protection semi-automatic welder to fill a gap between the first side outer panel and the second side outer panel and a round steel liner (7). The V-shaped symmetric groove is welded by a multi-layer multi-pass welding process of carbon dioxide. The weld bead is polished, and the outer end surface of the weld is convex arc.
   USE - Method of docking outboard panel on either side of mast of ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple construction and convenient operation, and does not need to embed round steel in the middle of the two outer plates. The welding quality and the welding efficiency are improved, and the welding strength meets the class level.

GA 201857983J
AB    NOVELTY - A reduction catalyst comprises an electric                conductor and an organic layer having organic                modifying groups placed on the surface of the                conductor.
   USE - Reduction catalyst for use in chemical reactor                and reduction product-producing system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits improved energy                conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reduction catalyst comprises an electric                conductor and an organic layer having organic                modifying groups placed on the surface of the                conductor, where the organic modifying groups have                a structure of formula ((Ra1)2-N-(CH2)n-Y1) or                (Z-(CH2)m-Y1).
   Ra1=1-4C alkyl;
   Z=aromatic ring having two or more nitrogen                atoms, preferably imidazole group or a triazole                group;
   Y1=heteroatom-containing group capable of                linking to electric conductor, preferably mercapto                group;
   n=1-5; and m = 0-6.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   a chemical reactor comprising an oxidation                electrode, a reduction electrode provided with the                reduction catalyst and a power supply unit                connected to the oxidation and reduction                electrodes
   a reduction product-producing system                comprising a chemical reactor which comprises an                oxidation electrolytic bath provided with an                oxidation catalyst and a reduction electrolytic                bath provided with the reduction catalyst, so as to                produce a reduction product by a reduction reaction                of carbon dioxide, an electrolyte solution supply                unit by which an electrolyte solution is supplied                to reduction electrolytic bath, a carbon dioxide                supply unit by which carbon dioxide is dissolved in                electrolyte solution so as to keep the reduction                reaction in reduction electrolytic bath to increase                the concentration of reduction product in                electrolyte solution and a separation unit by which                reduction product is separated from the electrolyte                solution when the concentration is increased.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electric conductor                  contains a metal selected from gold, silver,                  copper, platinum, palladium, zinc, iron,                  titanium, tin, indium, mercury, bismuth and                  nickel. The electric conductor contains a metal                  oxide selected from silver oxide, copper oxide,                  tantalum pentoxide, titnaium oxide, zirconium                  oxide, aluminum oxide, silicon oxide, zinc oxide,                  indium tin oxide, and fluorine doped tin                  oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic modifying                  groups are derived from organic compounds                  selected from aminoethanethiol,                  aminopropanethiol, aminobutanethiol,                  methylaminoethanethiol,                  isopropylethylaminoethanethiol,                  dimethylaminoethanethiol,                  diethylaminoethanethiol, dibutylaminoethanethiol,                  mercaptoethylimidazole, mercaptopropylimidazole,                  mercaptobutylimidazole, mercaptohexylimidazole,                  mercaptotriazole, mercaptoethyltriazole,                  mercaptopropyltriazole, mercaptobutyltriazole,                  and mercaptohexyltriazole. The low molecular                  weight-carbon compound is selected from carbon                  dioxide, oxalic acid, and glyoxal.

PT P
PN US2018155227-A1; US10479716-B2
TI Treating waste comprises e.g. metabolically decomposing chemical oxygen demand of solid and liquid influents through anaerobic bacteria digestion to produce products e.g. liquid and solid effluents, and directing effluents into tank.
AU ZAPPI M E
   GANG D
   REVELLAME E
   CHISTOSERDOV A
   HERNANDEZ R
   BAJPAI R
   SHARP W
   FORTELA D
AE UNIV LOUISIANA (LOUU-C)
GA 2018450998
AB    NOVELTY - Treating waste comprises metabolically decomposing the chemical oxygen demand of solid and liquid influents through anaerobic bacteria digestion, where the decomposition produces products comprising hydrogen, methane, liquid effluents and solid effluents, directing the liquid effluents and solid effluents into a tank, where the tank houses aerobic bacteria, and the aerobic bacteria converts the organic fraction of the effluents into high lipid content microbial solids and produces liquid-phase and gaseous-phase effluents, employing a growth algal reactor, where green algae attached to a turf matrix removes nutrients from the liquid-phase effluent and scrubs carbon dioxide from the gaseous effluents and the employing step produces whole cells, extracting lipids from the microbial solids and the whole cells, and removing any residual chemicals from the liquid-phase and the gaseous-phase effluents using advanced adsorption polishing with a functionalized mesoporous adsorbent bed.
   USE - The method is useful for treating waste, and for treating human-derived waste and capturing value-added chemicals in an isolated environment (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces as process by-products i.e. recovered potable water, liberated free oxygen, edible protein cake (with and without lipids), soil amendments and machinery lube oils; and utilizes the system which captures and chemically binds carbon dioxide into microbial cells and associated by-products, thus producing recovered usable returned cabin air and eliminating any built-up carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for treating human-derived waste and capturing value-added chemicals in an isolated environment, comprising a series of biological processes where the chemical oxygen demand of waste to a zero concentration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, elements comprising ammonium, phosphate, iron and/or cobalt are integrated with the anaerobic bacteria as nutrients, and solids produced in the aerobic bacteria step are used as additional feedstock for the decomposition step. The aerobic bacteria step is eliminated, where the liquid effluents and the solid effluents are directed into a liquid-solid separation process after the decomposition step. The extracting step is performed using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, where any non-lipid products from the extracting step are recycled as additional feedstock for the decomposition step. The removing step is performed using ordered mesoporous carbon. The mesoporous adsorbent is doped with silver nanoparticles and iodine. The method further comprises a UV disinfection system. The series of biological systems comprises: metabolic decomposition of the chemical oxygen demand of influents, where the products of the decomposition comprise liquid effluents and solid effluents; conversion of the organic fraction of the liquid effluents and the solid effluents into high lipid content microbial solids and producing liquid-phase effluent and gaseous-phase effluent; removal of the nutrients from the liquid-phase effluent; scrubbing carbon dioxide from the gaseous effluents; extraction of lipids from the microbial solids; and removal of any residual chemicals from the liquid-phase and the gaseous-phase effluents using advanced adsorption polishing with a functionalized mesoporous adsorbent bed. The metabolic decomposition is performed by anaerobic bacteria digestion. The conversion of the organic fraction is performed by aerobic bacteria. Preferred Components: The influents comprise black water, grey water and food waste. The hydrogen and methane are used to power fuel cells. The functionalized mesoporous adsorbent is modified by metal nanoparticles. The nanoparticles comprise cerium(III) chloride.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

AE ANHUI RESOURCE SAVING & ENVIRONMENTAL (CHNB-C)
GA 202260118W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fixed discharge source flow measuring module (201) is adapted to obtain a current time of a carbon dioxide concentration instantaneous value and a delta T-accumulation value of a gas flow of a fixed-discharge source. A background atmospheric CO2 concentration measuring module (203) is adapted to calculate the carbon emission instantaneous value of the current time. The instantaneous value of the background atmospheric CO2 concentration, the instantaneous value of the gas flow of the fixed emission source, and the DeltaT cumulative value of the gas flow of the fixed emission source, and the instantaneous value and cumulative value of the carbon emission of the fixed emission source are calculated.

PN IN202221016607-A
TI Alkali free Kolbe-Schmitt reaction for production            of hydroxy benzoic acid by mixing substrate phenol and            solvent water in presence of heterogeneous catalyst,            purging reactor with carbon dioxide gas, heating,            separating catalyst from liquefied reaction mixture,            and separating p-hydroxybenzoic acid.
AU PAWAR H S
   PANDEY P H
AE PAWAR H S (PAWA-Individual)
GA 202254333H
AB    NOVELTY - Process for alkali free Kolbe-Schmitt reaction                for production of hydroxy benzoic acid, involves                (a) mixing substrate phenol and solvent water in                presence of heterogeneous catalyst, (b) purging the                reactor with carbon dioxide gas and pressurizing                the reactor at pressure of 20-60 bar, (c) heating                reaction mixture at 130-200&#176; C for 4-24                hours, (d) cooling the reaction mass to room                temperature of 2-40&#176; C, (e) separating                catalyst from liquefied reaction mixture obtained                in step (d) through filtration, and (f) separating                of p-hydroxybenzoic acid from the filtrate obtained                in step (e).
   USE - The process is used for alkali free                Kolbe-Schmitt reaction for production of hydroxy                benzoic acid, and is used for alkali metal                hydroxide free Kolbe-Schmitt reaction for                conversion of phenol into hydroxy benzoic acid                (p-HBA) using carbon dioxide and recyclable                heterogeneous catalyst comprising copper doped on                zirconium dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates catalyst for reusability,                and utilizes alkali metal hydroxide free reaction                conditions which provides greener, sustainable and                cost-effective approach for Kolbe-Schmitt                carboxylation reaction. The method ensures high                p-HBA yield and phenol conversion without formation                of any by-products, and maximum yield of p-HBA                without formation of other side products. The                method ensures high selectivity of p-HBA, provides                economic feasibility, and utilizes efficient                heterogeneous non-precious transition metal based                catalyst for Kolbe-Schmitt reaction at                non-stringent reaction conditions. The method is a                simple and robust process for alkali free                Kolbe-Schmitt reaction for production of hydroxy                benzoic acid, and is simple and cost-effective                heterogeneous catalyst mediated Kolbe-Schmitt                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure for step                  (b) is 40-60 bar. The time for step (c) is 12-24                  hours. The temperature for step (c) is                  140-200&#176; C. Preferred Components: The                  catalyst is copper doped on support zirconium                  dioxide prepared by co-precipitation method.                  Preferred Composition: The catalyst is copper                  loading of 0.63-10 wt.%/wt.% on support. The                  concentration of catalyst in step (a) is 0.8-8                  wt.%/wt.% wrt reaction mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  phenol in solvent water is 0.42-13.36                  vol.%/vol.%.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide that the process for                preparation catalyst, they do not support the                claims.

AB    NOVELTY - An exhaust gas processing system (108)                receives and cools the flow of exhaust gas after                the exhaust gas has passed through the first flow                path of an exhaust gas heat exchanger and prior to                exhaust gas being compressed by a turbo-compressor.                The processing system includes a cooling tower, a                cooling- tower heat exchangers, an absorption                chiller, an absorber chiller heat exchager, a                dehumidifier system and a vapor-compression                refrigeration system. The exhaust gas is compressed                by the turbo compressor after passing through the                turbo-expander.
   USE - Bottoming cycle power system for delivering                power such as mechanical or electrical power for                use in stationary and/or mobile applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The specific volume of flow of exhaust gas is                significantly and efficiently reduced after exiting                the turbo-expander and prior to entering the turbo                compressor. The overall efficiency of the bottoming                cycle power system is increased. The volume and                mass of water vapor in the flow of the exhaust gas                are efficiently decreased. The energy required in                any carbon dioxide capture system used to remove                the carbon dioxide from exhaust gas can be reduced.                The efficiency of internal combustion engine can be                maintained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a                combined power system comprising: a primary power                system including an internal combustion engine                having a rotatable crankshaft; (2) a carbon dioxide                capture system comprising: a first capture tank and                a second capture tank.

   YAO D
   LI X
   ZHENG Y
AE XIAN SHUNTU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 202215741Y
AB    NOVELTY - Low-viscosity I-type porous liquid constructed by a covalent cross-linking strategy with a porous body containing an amino functional group and a single-end epoxy polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), where the mass fraction ratioadding of the porous body to the single-end epoxy PDMS is 1-30%, and the molecular weight of the PDMS is 1000-4000 g.mol-1, is claimed.
   USE - The porous liquid is useful for carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption and CO2/nitrogen (N2) separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The low-viscosity I-type porous liquid: combines the permanent gap property of the porous main body and the excellent fluidity characteristic of the liquid adsorbent; has low viscosity, wide liquid range, high thermal stability, good circulation property, high efficient CO2 adsorption and CO2/N2 separation effect. The preparation method is universal, and is easy to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the low-viscosity type I porous liquid, comprising: (i) dissolving the porous main body containing amino functional groups in acetone solvent to form component A; (ii) dissolving single-end epoxy PDMS in acetone solvent to form component B; (iii) mixing component A and component B at 30-70degreesC, stirring magnetically for 12-24 hours, and centrifuging to obtain component C of mixed solution, after the component A and component B are mixed, the mass fraction ratio of the porous main body containing amino functional groups to the single-end epoxy PDMS is 1-30%; and (iv) placing the component C in a dialysis bag containing acetone for 48-72 hours, changing the acetone solvent for every 4-6 hours, and naturally volatilizing the solvent acetone at 50degreesC.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous main body containing amino functional groups includes amino functionalized metal-organic framework compounds (MOFs), zeolite-like imidazolate framework porous materials (ZIFs), covalent organic framework (COFs) or porous organic cage (POCs). The MOFs include UIO-66-NH2, UIO-67-NH2, MIL-53-NH2 and/or MIL-100-NH2. The ZIFs comprise ZIF-8-NH2, ZIF-67-NH2, ZIF-93-NH2 and/or ZIF-96-NH2. The particle size of the porous main body containing amino functional groups is 50 nm to 1 mum. The pore size distribution of the amino functional group-containing porous body is 0.5-4 nm. The specific surface area of the porous main body containing amino functional groups is greater than or equal to 500 cm3.g-1.

PN US2021309073-A1; US11370269-B2
TI Method for determining and communicating            recirculation mode in vehicle for improving cabin air            quality in vehicle, involves determining final            re-circulation mode based on outside air acceptability            state and carbon dioxide levels.
AU GOEL S
AE GOEL S (GOEL-Individual)
GA 2021B50344
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining the number of                occupants inside a vehicle (302). The image or                video of the traffic ahead of vehicle is captured                and analyzed to detect the presence of vehicles.                The traffic condition is determined as the weighted                sum of the sizes of vehicles detected in traffic.                The vehicle speed is acquired. The outside air                acceptability state is determined based on traffic                condition and vehicle speed. The carbon dioxide                (CO2) levels in vehicle cabin space are determined                based on number of vehicle occupants present. The                final recirculation mode is determined based on                outside air acceptability state and CO2 levels in                the vehicle cabin space. The final recirculation                mode is communicated to the vehicle controller or                vehicle occupant.
   USE - Method for determining and communicating                recirculation mode in vehicle for improving cabin                air quality in vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables operating the air inlet                valve in response to detected traffic condition                outside and estimated level of carbon dioxide in                cabin space that provides improved cabin air                quality automatically or via occupant                participation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a smartphone app for determining and                communicating recirculation mode in a vehicle for                improving cabin air quality in the vehicle;
   (2) a controller for determining and                communicating recirculation mode in a vehicle for                improving cabin air quality in the vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                relationship between the size of vehicle image and                the distance of vehicle from the vehicle with                camera.
   Vehicle (302, 310, 318, 326)
   Smartphone (304)
   Vehicle image height (312, 320, 328)
   Camera view height (314, 322, 330)
   Distance (316, 324, 332)

The driving power required to                pressurize carbon dioxide to a supercritical                pressure is reduced such that the highly efficient                carbon dioxide cycle system and the high-efficiency                combined cycle system can be realized. AE YUEYANG MINGKE CATALYTIC TECHNOLOGY CO (YUEY-Non-standard)
GA 2021342182
AB    NOVELTY - The carbonization reaction device has a carbonization reactor (1) whose bottom is provided with a discharging port (7). The top is provided with a tail gas discharging pipe (5) and a Venturi injection pump. The Venturi injection pump is provided with three inlets. An inlet is connected with a carbon dioxide feeding pipe (4). The inlet is connected with a tail gas discharging pipe through a tail gas circulating pipe (6). The inlet is connected with the discharging port through a circulating pump (2). The feeding pipeline of the circulating pump is provided with a sodium metaaluminate feeding port (8). The discharging port, the acirculating pump, Venturi jet pump (3) and the carbonizing reactor form a slurry circulating system. The tail gas discharging pipe, the tail gas circulating pipe, the Venturi jet pump and the carbonization reactor form a tail gas circulating system.
   USE - Carbonization reaction device for producing pseudo-boehmite used for catalyst, adsorbent, binder, catalyst carrier, dehydrating agent, grinding material and coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure of venture injection pump is used for quickly dispersing carbon dioxide and quickly reacting with sodium metaaluminate solution to produce pseudo-boehmite. The reaction is fast. The carbonization reaction device adopts gap operation or continuous production and efficiently prepares high specific surface area, large hole volume and good glue-soluble pseudo-boehmite product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing pseudo-boehmite.

   Sodium metaaluminate feeding port (8)
