PN WO2020154358-A1; US2022112146-A1
TI Dehydration membrane reactor for methanol production from carbon-dioxide hydrogenation, comprises one or more porous supports, a dehydration membrane on one or more porous supports, and a catalyst layer on dehydration membrane.
AU YU M
   LI H
AE RENSSELAER POLYTECHNIC INST (RNSS-C)
   RENSSELAER POLYTECHNIC INST (RNSS-C)
GA 202071225F
AB    NOVELTY - Dehydration membrane reactor (100) comprises one or more porous supports (102); a dehydration membrane on the one or more porous supports; and a catalyst layer (108) on the dehydration membrane, where one or more porous supports includes a lumen.
   USE - Dehydration membrane reactor for methanol production from carbon-dioxide (CO2) hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The dehydration membrane reactor allows to produce high purity methanol, that is 95%, much higher than that of obtained in current industrial process, which is 68%, ensures CO2 utilization to chemicals and fuels, convert waste C02 to value added chemicals and used in production of fuels and chemicals in an attractive approach to recycle the captured CO2, leading to a carbon-neutral energy cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing dehydration membrane reactor, which involves providing the porous support; applying multiple NaA zeolite crystal seeds to the porous support to form a seeded support; heating the seeded support at a temperature above 200 degrees C; growing an NaA zeolite membrane on the seeded support; and applying a catalyst layer to the zeolite membrane; and
   (2) method for producing methanol involves providing a dehydration membrane reactor, the dehydration membrane reactor including one or more porous supports, a dehydration membrane on the one or more hollow porous supports, and a catalyst layer on the dehydration membrane, where the one or more porous supports includes a lumen; supplying a feedstream including carbon dioxide to the catalyst layer; evolving a product stream from the feedstream, the product stream including methanol and water; and transporting water from the product stream through the dehydration membrane to the lumen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a dehydration membrane reactor
   Dehydration membrane reactor (100)
   Porous support (102)
   Catalyst layer (108)
   Feed stream inlet (110)
   Product stream (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The one or more porous supports include a tubular sidewall has an external surface and an internal surface, where the dehydration membrane is set on the exterior surface and the interior surface encloses the lumen. The one or more porous supports include hollow ceramic fibers. The method for preparing dehydration membrane reactor parrticularly involves applying multiple zeolite crystal seeds to the porous support to form a seeded support by dip-coating the porous support in a zeolite crystal seed solution; and drying the coated porous support. The dehydration membrane reactor according, further comprises a feedstream in communication with the catalyst layer, where the feedstream include 3/4 portion of C02, where the molar ratio of hydrogen (H2) to C02 is 3:1. The coated porous support is dried at a temperature below 80 degrees C. The porous support with the zeolite crystal seeds is heated above 200 degrees C. The zeolite crystal seeds are sized between 20-250 nm. Preferred Components: The dehydration membrane includes NaA zeolite. The catalyst layer includes copper, zinc oxide, and alumina. The one or more porous supports include ceramic fibers. The zeolite membrane includes NaA zeolite.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111437809-A
TI Preparing rare earth element doped bismuth silicate photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methanol, involves weighing attapulgite and ammonium sulfate and placing them in crucible.
AU LI X
   LIU Y
   WANG C
   YAO C
   WU F
   ZUO S
   WANG L
   LIU W
   MAO H
   YE L
AE JIANGSU NAOU NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202071781D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rare earth element doped bismuth silicate photocatalyst involves weighing attapulgite and ammonium sulfate and placing them in crucible, calcinating in muffle furnace at 550 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and then placing them in 3mol/L hydrochloric acid solution for treatment 2-4 hours. The tetramethylammonium hydroxide is added to deionized water. The silica prepared is added under vigorous stirring, then bismuth nitrate and hydrated rare earth nitrate compound (RE(NO3)3 nH2O) are added, transferred to microwave hydrothermal kettle for hydrothermal reaction to obtain rare earth element-doped bismuth silicate.
   USE - Method for preparing rare earth element doped bismuth silicate photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare rare earth element doped bismuth silicate photocatalyst that improves sunlight utilization rate of the bismuth silicate material, and photocatalytic efficiency of the material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using rare earth element doped bismuth silicate photocatalyst, which involves:
   (A) weighing 0.01g of the prepared rare earth ion-doped bismuth silicate material, dissolving in 100 mL of deionized water, and then adding to photocatalytic reaction device, and passing carbon dioxide into reaction device at flow rate of 60 mL/minute, using 300W xenon lamp as simulated light source to irradiate, collecting 1mL samples every 30 minutes, and analyzing methanol concentration by gas chromatograph, injection volume is 1 microliter, vaporization chamber and detector temperature is 250 degrees C, column temperature is 60 degrees C, maintaining column temperature at 60 degrees C for 1 minutes, keeping at 10 degrees C/minute to 100 degrees C for 1 min; and
   (B) measuring concentration of methanol in sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The rare earth elements in the hydrated rare earth nitrate compound includes Ce3+, Pr3+, Er3+, Tm3+, Yb3+, Sm3+, and La3+. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of attapulgite to ammonium sulfate is 1:0.33-1:3. The hydrochloric acid solution is diluted with 35% analytically pure hydrochloric acid to 3mol/L. The tetramethylammonium hydroxide is added to deionized water to form 25% tetramethylammonium hydroxide aqueous solution. The mass ratio of bismuth nitrate to silicon dioxide is 8:1. The photocatalyst comprises bismuth silicate (Bi12SiO20) and rare earth ions (RE), molar ratio of (Bi3+) and (RE) is 1:x, range of x is 0.001-0.003mol.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting a control center (4) with a wire feeding mechanism (1), a controllable table (5), a gas proportioner (8), and a TIG welder (9). A carbon dioxide gas cylinder (6), an argon gas cylinder (7) is connected to the gas proportioner and output to a TIG welding torch (10). The wire is sent to the upper surface of the titanium alloy substrate to be processed after the wire feeding mechanism passes a wire feeding arm (2) and a wire feeding nozzle (3). The method expands the use of titanium alloy materials.

TI Energy system for storing renewable energy for integral synthesis, has gasifier partially combusting feedstock and char, and hydrogen converter converting portion of reactor hydrogen and carbon dioxide discharged from separator into methane.
AU KAUFMAN J S
AE KAUFMAN J S (KAUF-Individual)
GA 2019607546
AB    NOVELTY - The system (500) has an oxygen enriched air blown gasifier partially combusting feedstock and char, where the combustion converts feedstock into pressurized syngas. A syngas separator (572) separates syngas from oxygen enriched air blown gasifier into purified syngas and oxygen and discharges carbon dioxide. A fuel cooler (580) cools the purified pressurized syngas. A thermo-chemical reactor (540) is heated by char recirculating from the oxygen enriched air blown gasifier and converts sulfuric acid and water into reactor oxygen and reactor hydrogen. A hydrogen converter (594) converts a portion of the reactor hydrogen and carbon dioxide discharged from the syngas separator into methane.
   USE - Energy system for storing renewable energy for integral synthesis for heat source cryofuel and heat sink refrigerant for distributed generation and motor vehicle prime movers and liquefiers.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides viable liquefier drive, amplification of wind due to the presence of structures and distributed energy recovery potential, and is clean and reliable to use, and can store the power generated from clean energy sources, and make power to be readily available when required in a wide range of applications. The system provides energy independence, conserves fossil fuels, and reduces emission of combustion products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of an integrated gas turbine combined cycle generating station with supplementary heating of a thermo-chemical reactor.

PT P
PN CN109569686-A; CN109569686-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst includes oxidizing activated carbon, dissolving primary amine organic substance in water, hydrothermal reaction, neutralizing, and ultraviolet reduction.
AU LU C
   NIE J
   LIU Q
   ZHOU Y
   ZHANG X
   FENG F
   MA L
   ZHANG Q
   ZHAO J
   XU X
   GUO L
   LV J
   CEN J
   LI X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201933615C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst comprises (a) taking activated carbon, where the activated carbon surface comprises carbon dioxide leaving group and the total content of the carbon dioxide leaving group not greater than 0.8 mu mol/g, then oxidizing the activated carbon by a treating agent, where the treating agent is oxalic acid or hydrogen peroxide to obtain pretreated activated carbon, (b) dissolving the primary amine organic substance in water, mixing with activated carbon, mixing uniformly, then placing in a hydrothermal kettle for carrying out hydrothermal reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, then separating the sample after completing the reaction, neutralizing the sample by washing with water, and drying to obtain nitrogen modified activated carbon, and (c) supporting the noble metal on the nitrogen-modified activated carbon by carrying out ultraviolet reduction, where the noble metal is palladium and/or platinum.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogenation catalyst: has high conversion rate, good selectivity, good stability and has fast hydrogenation rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst for catalyzing the hydrogenation of halogenated nitrobenzene to halogenated aniline comprising adding the halogenated nitrobenzene and nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst into a high-pressure hydrogenation reactor, then sealing the reactor, replacing the air, introducing the hydrogen, stirring at 20-100 degrees C, and then carrying out catalytic hydrogenation reaction under the hydrogen pressure conditions of 0.1-3 MPa to obtain halogenated aniline, where carrying out the catalytic hydrogenation reaction in a solvent or in the absence of a solvent, and the solvent is methanol, water, and/or product halogenated aniline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The noble metal palladium and/or platinum which is supported in the step (c) in an amount of 0.1-10 wt.%. Preferred Components: The activated carbon has a particle size of 200-300 mesh which is not less than 80%. The halogenated nitrobenzene is chloronitrobenzene, fluoronitrobenzene or bromonitrobenzene, preferably p-chloronitrobenzene, m-chloronitrobenzene or o-chloronitrobenzene. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises uniformly dispersing the activated carbon into the treating agent solution to obtain a suspension, then gradually heating the suspension at 30-60 degrees C while stirring for 10-20 hours, cooling the suspension to room temperature, then carrying out suction filtration, neutralizing the pH by washing and drying, where the treating agent solution is oxalic acid solution having a concentration of 5-20 wt.%, the volume of the treating agent solution is 2-30 ml/g by mass of the activated carbon, and then processing at 30-50 degrees C for 10-15 hours, or the treating agent solution is a hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution having a concentration of 20-30 wt.%, the volume of the treatment agent solution is 2-30 ml/g, and treating at 40-60 degrees C for 10-20 hours. The step (b) comprises primary amine organic substance comprising at least two amine groups, preferably 1,2-propylenediamine, ethylenediamine or hexamethylenediamine, where the mass ratio of the primary amine organic substance to the pretreated activated carbon is 1-10:1, and the mass ratio of the amine organic substance to water is 1:1-10, and then carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 150-250 degrees C for 10-20 hours in a carbon dioxide atmosphere under the pressure of 1-5 MPa. The ultraviolet light reduction method comprises dispersing nitrogen-modified activated carbon in water, then adding the solution containing noble metal ions in a drop wise manner to the nitrogen-modified activated carbon slurry while stirring, irradiating the ultraviolet for a certain period of time after completing the addition, where the ultraviolet light wavelength is controlled at 315-280 nm, illuminating for 5 seconds-5 minutes, continuing at for 0.5-2 hours, carrying out suction filtration, then neutralizing the pH by washing with water, and drying to obtain nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst. The method comprises drying in the step (a) at 80-120 degrees C for 3-5 hours under vacuum, in the step (b) at 100-150 degrees C under vacuum, and in the step (c) at 50-80 degrees C for overnight under vacuum. The particle mass content of activated carbon is 200-300 mesh which is not less than 80%, and then applying the catalyst to a microchannel reactor after satisfying the activated carbon particle size as a catalyst raw material.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AE SENSATRONIC GMBH (SENS-Non-standard)

   SENSATRONIC GMBH (SENS-Non-standard)
GA 2018706783
AB    NOVELTY - The measuring arrangement (10) has the                measuring cell (12) which is filled with the gas                mixture and provided with a measuring path for the                light generated by the thermal light source. The                bandpass filters (24,26) are associated with the                sensors (25,27). An evaluation device (18) is                arranged to determine the gas concentration to be                measured from the attenuation of the IR light in                the measuring cell. One bandpass filter is designed                for transmission in a measuring wavelength range in                which the gas to-be measured absorbs IR light, and                another bandpass filter is designed for                transmission in a reference wavelength range in                which the gas to-be measured IR light or little                absorbed in comparison to the measuring wavelength                range. The interference in                the measurement signal is eliminated.

TI System for controlling lamp and air quality in e.g. living room of home, has integrated controller for measuring lamp current, and wall pad connected to external server by Ethernet and portable terminal of user through radio link.
AU SANG Y Y
   BYUNG K K
AE YOO S Y (YOOS-Individual)
   KANG B K (KANG-Individual)
GA 201859482Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has an integrated controller (10) for measuring lamp current. A fine dust measuring sensor (31) measures concentration of fine dust. A carbon dioxide measuring sensor (32) measures concentration of carbon dioxide. A control processor (35) is connected with a human detection sensor (39) to sense presence of a human if a measured illumination value is greater than a set value such that a lighting apparatus (10'') installed at a living room is controlled when a lighting switch (33) is turned on, and the lighting apparatus installed at a kitchen is controlled when the lighting switch is turned off. A wall pad (40) is connected to an external server (43) by an Ethernet and a portable terminal (44) of a user through a radio link.
   USE - System for controlling a lighting apparatus i.e. lamp, and air quality in an indoor space e.g. living room and kitchen, of a premises i.e. home.
   ADVANTAGE - The system measures indoor space air pollution degree of an air purifier by the integrated controller, so that an air-conditioner is automatically cleaned up. The system provides electric power saving effect by controlling the lighting apparatus and wirelessly controlling the air-conditioner automatically.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system for controlling a lighting apparatus and air quality in an indoor space. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN US2018029914-A1; US10377653-B2
TI Concentrating condensate of ammonia gas and water for recovering phosphate, by mixing condensate with carbonic acid wash solution, discharging ammonia-deficient gas, decomposing ammonium bicarbonate solution and collecting ammonia gas.
AU BURKE D A
AE BURKE D A (BURK-Individual)
   COYNE B J (COYN-Individual)
GA 201808976M
AB    NOVELTY - Process for concentrating a condensate of ammonia gas and water involves (a) mixing the condensate in a chiller/condenser with a low-pH carbonic acid wash solution to form a concentrated bicarbonate solution and ammonia-deficient gas, (b) discharging the ammonia-deficient gas from the chiller/condenser, (c) decomposing the ammonium bicarbonate solution, (d) releasing the pressure in the chiller/condenser, (e) collecting and cooling the concentrated ammonia gas, and (f) collecting and chilling the remaining bicarbonate solution, and saturating the solution with carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for concentrating a condensate of ammonia gas and water and recovering phosphate and nitrogen from liquid streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is economical, rapidly and completely removes and recovers the phosphate precipitates from the liquid stream, uses autotrophic organisms that increase the pH and reduce the alkalinity of the liquid stream, hence does not require the addition of pH-increasing and bicarbonate-reducing chemicals and reduces the need to purchase chemicals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for concentrating a condensate of ammonia gas and water involves (a) mixing the condensate in a chiller/condenser with a low-pH carbonic acid wash solution to form a concentrated bicarbonate solution and ammonia-deficient gas, (b) discharging the ammonia-deficient gas from the chiller/condenser, (c) decomposing the ammonium bicarbonate solution from the chiller/condenser in a heated, thermal decomposition reactor under pressure to form decomposed solution which includes carbon dioxide gas and ammonia gas decomposition products, (d) releasing the pressure in the chiller/condenser to preferentially release concentrated ammonia gas, (e) collecting and cooling the concentrated ammonia gas to form a concentrated ammonium hydroxide solution, and (f) collecting and chilling the remaining bicarbonate solution, and saturating the solution with carbon dioxide to produce a wash solution for use in the chiller/condenser.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further involves (i) passing the stripped gas through a water-permeable membrane to remove water, before step (a), (ii) using a heat pump to chill the contents of the chiller/condenser while heating the ammonia-deficient gas discharged in step (b), and (iii) using a heat pump to heat the thermal decomposition reactor while cooling the product gases and the carbonic acid wash solution.

PN CN107638789-A
TI Method for purifying waste-derived fuel combustion flue gas, involves allowing exhaust gas in sequence of treatment comprising desulfurization and denitrification, removing dioxin and heavy metal, and removing carbon dioxide.
AU WANG Z
AE CHENGDU ZUNHUA RONGYU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018109005
AB    NOVELTY - A waste-derived fuel combustion flue gas purifying method involves allowing the exhaust gas in sequence of treatment comprising desulfurization and denitrification, removing dioxin and heavy metal, performing semi-dry deacidification, removing particulate matter, absorbing dust and removing carbon dioxide, where the desulfurization and denitrification is performed by entering waste derived fuel into an incinerator, controlling the temperature in incinerator to 250-600 degrees C and passing the flue gas generated during the incineration through the flue to enter the bottom of desulfurization tower.
   USE - Method for purifying waste-derived fuel combustion flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purifying waste-derived fuel combustion flue gas with improved desulfurization and denitrification effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A waste-derived fuel combustion flue gas purifying method involves allowing the exhaust gas in sequence of treatment comprising desulfurization and denitrification, removing dioxin and heavy metal, performing semi-dry deacidification, removing particulate matter, absorbing dust and removing carbon dioxide, where the desulfurization and denitrification is performed by entering waste derived fuel into an incinerator for incineration, controlling the temperature in incinerator to 250-600 degrees C and passing the flue gas generated during the incineration through the flue to enter the bottom of the desulfurization tower, the desulfurization tower is equipped with primary desulfurization and denitrification unit, secondary desulfurization and denitrification unit, the primary desulfurization and denitrification unit are located in the middle and lower parts which is sprayed with white mud as a desulfurization and denitrification agent, and the secondary desulfurization and denitrification unit are located on the top which adopts a mixture of urea and ammonia liquid as desulfurization and denitrification agent for spraying.

PN CN114733628-A
TI Method for sorting dissociation of composite            high-pressure carbon dioxide ore, involves completing            dissociation of target mining component when target            mineral content is greater than preset mining component            content threshold value.
AU SONG G
   ZUO J
   DING C
   WANG Y
   ZHANG G
   YANG R
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202296120T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a composite ore in                a high-pressure vessel (S101). (S102) Carbon                dioxide is injected preset pressure into the high                pressure vessel. (S103) The high-pressure carbon                dioxide is released from the high-pressure vessel                within a preset time. First dissociation process is                performed on the composite ore to obtain composite                ore particles. (S104) A target composite ore                particle is extracted from the composite iron ore                particles according to a preset rule. (S105) Target                mineral component content of the target composite                metal ore particles is detected. The target mineral                component is compared with a preset mineral content                threshold value. (S106) Dissociation of a target                mining component is completed when the target                mineral content is greater than a preset mining                component content threshold value.
   USE - Method for sorting dissociation of composite                high-pressure carbon dioxide ore. Uses included but                are not limited to sort dissociation of vanadium,                titanium, chromium, scandium, cobalt, nickel,                copper, manganese and gallium.
   ADVANTAGE - The target composite ore particles are                re-dissociated, when the target mineral component                content is less than the preset mineral component                threshold value, so as to sort the composite ore in                a simple and quick manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for sorting dissociation of composite                high-pressure carbon dioxide ore. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   Step for placing a composite ore in a                high-pressure vessel (S101)
   Step for injecting carbon dioxide preset                pressure into the high pressure vessel                (S102)
   Step for releasing the high-pressure carbon                dioxide from the high-pressure vessel within a                preset time (S103)
   Step for extracting a target composite ore                particle from the composite iron ore particles                according to a preset rule (S104)
   Step for detecting target mineral component                content of the target composite metal ore particles                (S105)
   Step for completing dissociation of a target                mining component when the target mineral content is                greater than a preset mining component content                threshold value. (S106)

GA 202215489S
AB    NOVELTY - The motor vehicle has a liquid pump (4) to pump liquid carbon dioxide from a storage liquid through a suction pipe (10). The liquid carbon dioxide is flowed into a cavity above a piston (8) through the air inlet valve. A heat storage tank (2) is directly connected with the cavity above the piston through a pipeline. An adjustable ultrasonic jet device is set on side wall of shell (1) of piston cavity, and provided with an ultrasonic jet guide plate and a copper plate. The guide plate is installed in the shell near the piston. The copper plate is electrically connected by a lead. A straight insertion portion is tightly attached to the inner wall of the piston and the shell. The conductive high-frequency oscillation of ultrasonic jet guide plate is performed when the liquid carbon dioxide enters the upper stop line downwards, and the air pressure enters into the piston to obtain a flying rise.
   USE - Small-sized motor vehicle carrying carbon dioxide as power fuel for transporting light-made products, light semi-products and waste resources of light products.
   ADVANTAGE - The small-sized motor vehicle has simple structure, stable power using carbon dioxide from liquid into gaseous expansion, synergistic with the ultrasonic wave, promotes the reciprocating movement of the piston, and effectively provides power for carrying vehicle continuously.

The effervescent mineral composition is provided in form of compressed solid or powder; and in enclosed pack which has water permeable layer.    ACTIVITY - Metabolic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The effervescent mineral composition is administered by subcutaneous and cutaneous route.

PN WO2020122664-A1; KR2020073161-A; CN112272653-A; EP3786112-A1; EP3786112-A4; US2021230006-A1; JP2021526122-W; JP7083048-B2; US11365125-B2; KR2434826-B1
TI Supercritical drying of silica hydrogel blanket            using supercritical extractors by placing silica            hydrogel blanket inside extractors respectively and            supplying carbon dioxide to extractor without supplying            carbon dioxide to another extractor.
AU OH K S
   BAEK S W
   KIM M R
   BAEK S
   WU J
   BAI S
   JIN M
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
   LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202054318T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for supercritical drying of a silica                hydrogel blanket using first and second                supercritical extractors involves (i) placing a                silica hydrogel blanket inside the first and second                supercritical extractors respectively, (ii)                supplying carbon dioxide to the first supercritical                extractor and not supplying carbon dioxide to the                second supercritical extractor and (iii) supplying                carbon dioxide to the second supercritical                extractor without supplying carbon dioxide to the                first supercritical extractor, where the steps (ii)                and (iii) are alternately repeated for two or more                times, respectively.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical drying                of a silica hydrogel blanket used for producing a                silica aerogel blanket.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of improving efficiency                of the supercritical drying by reducing the amount                of carbon dioxide without additional investments in                equipment or inputs of energy and drying the silica                hydrogel blanket with a more economical method. The                aerogel blanket has excellent physical properties                with low thermal conductivity and moisture                absorption at the inner end.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for producing a silica aerogel                blanket involving preparing a silica sol,                impregnating the silica sol with a blanket                substrate and gelling, surface-modifying the silica                hydrogel blanket formed by the gelation reaction                and carrying out the supercritical drying of the                silica hydrogel blanket to obtain the product,                where the supercritical drying step is performed by                the above method; and
   (2) the silica aerogel blanket, where the                state in which the blanket is wound, the difference                in thermal conductivity at room temperature (25                degrees C) of the inner and outer ends of the                blanket is less than or equal to 1 mW/mK and the                inner and the outer ends of the blanket refer to                both ends of the compartment in the case of three                divisions in the longitudinal direction in a state                in which the winding of the blanket is                released.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  alternately repeating the steps (ii) and (iii)                  for four or more times, respectively. The method                  involves raising the temperature inside the first                  and second supercritical extractors before                  placing the silica hydrogel blanket inside the                  first and second supercritical extractor, where                  the step of raising the temperature is to heat                  the interior of the first and second                  supercritical extractors at 40-100 degrees C. The                  steps (ii) and (iii) which are first performed                  among the steps (ii) and (iii) are performed                  repeatedly while supplying carbon dioxide to the                  first or second supercritical extractor while                  raising the inside of the first or second                  supercritical extractor to 75-250 bar. The method                  involves supplying carbon dioxide at a                  temperature of 50-100 degrees C to the first and                  second supercritical extractors, where the                  capacity of carbon dioxide supplied to the first                  or second supercritical extractor is 300-700                  kg/batch. The method involves performing the                  steps (i) and (ii) each independently for 5-30                  minutes. Preferred Properties: The method has a                  solvent recovery rate of greater than or equal to                  90% calculated by and equation: solvent recovery                  rate (%) = ((amount of solvent discharged and                  recovered from the supercritical                  extractor)/(amount of solvent in the silica                  hydrogel blanket injected into the supercritical                  extractor))x 100. The thermal conductivity at                  room temperature (25 degrees C) of the inner end                  of the silica aerogel blanket is 13-20 mW/mK. The                  silica aerogel blanket has a water repellency of                  less than or equal to 5% which is calculated by                  an equation: water absorption (%) = ((weight of                  silica aerogel blanket after impregnation in                  water (wt.%) -weight of silica aerogel blanket                  before impregnation in water (wt.%))/(weight of                  silica aerogel blanket before impregnation in                  water (wt.%)))x 100.

TI Fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer for preparing a fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene polymer in a supercritical carbon dioxide system, has low transformation pressure in supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN J
   LI R
   LIU Z
   YIN F
   SHEN S
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2018607347
AB    NOVELTY - Fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer is represented by (I), where n is an integer of from 1-14.
   USE - Fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer used for preparing a fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene polymer in a supercritical carbon dioxide system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer is simple and efficient, has excellent solubility in a supercritical carbon dioxide medium, and is miscible with supercritical carbon dioxide under mild conditions, has low transformation pressure in supercritical carbon dioxide, low polymerization pressure, mild conditions, good homogeneity in polymerization process, environmental protection and no solvent residue, obtained product has excellent performance, high performance fluoropolymer functional materials for supercritical carbon dioxide systems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer is represented by formula (I), where n is an integer of from 1-14.. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer, which involves:
   (A) synthesizing p-styryl magnesium bromide under the protection of inert gas, adding tetrahydrofuran and catalytic amount of iodine to the magnesium powder, and then adding p-bromostyrene at room temperature for 1-3 hours to obtain p-styrene magnesium bromide solution;
   (B) synthesizing p-styryl copper (I) by inert gas protection and ice bath, adding obtained p-styryl magnesium bromide solution dropwise to the suspension of cuprous chloride in tetrahydrofuran, reactive at room temperature for 1-2 hours to obtain a p-styryl copper (I) solution; and
   (C) synthesizing fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer, adding fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based acid halide dropwise to the styrene obtained under inert gas protection and ice bath conditions, carrying out reaction at room temperature for 4-6 hours, and hydrolyzing reaction product and separating and purifying to obtain a fluorine-containing epoxy oligomer-based styrene monomer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The fluorine-containing epoxy oligomeric acid halide is represented by structure Rf-COX, where X is F or Cl, and Rf is represented by formula (II), where n is an integer of from 1-14.. The molar ratio of the p-bromostyrene to magnesium powder, cuprous chloride, fluorine-containing epoxy oligomeric acid halide is 1:1.1-1.4:1 - 1.4:0.7-1.1.

PN CN114946934-A
TI Air conditioning fresh-keeping method of Sanhua            plum fruit comprises picking Sanhua plum fruit,            sorting, vacuum pre-cooling, sterilizing pre-cooled            Sanhua plums, placing in container with a nitrogen,            oxygen and carbon dioxide, packing and            refrigerating.
AU JIANG L
   SHANG F
   SHUAI L
   CHEN Z
   SONG M
AE UNIV HEZHOU (UHEZ-C)
GA 2022B4838C
AB    NOVELTY - Air conditioning fresh-keeping method of                Sanhua plum fruit comprises (a) picking the Sanhua                plum fruit, (b) sorting the picked fruits based on                the maturity and fruit size, (c) vacuum pre-cooling                of Sanhua plum fruits after picking and sorting,                (d) sterilizing the pre-cooled Sanhua plums, (e)                placing the sterilized Sanhua plum in a container                with a nitrogen concentration of 80-90%, an oxygen                concentration of 5-10% and a carbon dioxide                concentration of 5-8%, packing and (f) placing the                packaged Sanhua plum fruit into a refrigerator and                refrigerating.
   USE - The method is useful for air conditioning                fresh-keeping of Sanhua plum fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can inhibit the metabolism- Sanhua                plum; maintains the moisture of the Sanhua plum                fruit; reduce the rotten rate and prolongs the                fresh-keeping period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The packaging material                  of the modified atmosphere packaging is set as a                  transparent packaging bag.Preferred Method: The fruit picking time is                  set as the morning on a sunny day. In the fruit                  grading process, the fruits with diseases and                  insect pests, rotten and mechanical damage are                  removed and the ripeness of the fruit in the                  process of fruit grading is selected to be 8                  ripe. The pre-cooling time of the vacuum                  pre-cooling is more than 4 hours, so that the                  fruit core is lowered to 4&#8451;. The                  sterilization method comprises using 5-7 mg per                  liter of ozone water for immersion and                  sterilization, the soaking time is 2-4 minutes,                  and finally washing with clean water and drying.                  The steps of the air conditioning packaging                  comprises placing the sterilized and dried Sanhua                  plum fruit in a packaging container, filling the                  packaging container with a mixed gas of oxygen,                  carbon dioxide and nitrogen, so that the gas                  concentration in the packaging container reaches                  the state of nitrogen 80-90%, oxygen                  concentration 5-10% and carbon dioxide                  concentration 5-8% and packing. The refrigerator                  temperature is 2-4&#8451;.

TI Preparation of vinylene carbonate involves adding            e.g. 1,3-bis(2-diphenylphosphino-6-alkylpyridine-4)-2,            3-dihydroimidazole palladium chloride,            1,3-bis[2-diphenylphosphino-6-alkylpyridine-4]-2,3-dihydroimidazole            palladium chloride as catalyst, carbon dioxide and            anhydrous chloroacetaldehyde.

   GUAN X

   WANG X
AE SUZHOU HUAYI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022A3429Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of vinylene carbonate involves                adding anhydrous solvent, acid-binding agent and                catalyst into closed high-pressure reaction kettle,                protecting by inert gas, adding carbon dioxide and                anhydrous chloroacetaldehyde, raising temperature                at 60-80&#8451;, controlling initial pressure                of high-pressure reaction kettle to be 1.0-2.0 MPa                for reaction, reducing pressure in high-pressure                reaction kettle to 0.1 MPa, stopping reaction,                cooling to room temperature, filtering, taking                filtrate for later use, distilling filtrate at                pressure of -0.08 MPa to -0.07 MPa at                50-60&#8451; to remove solvent, continuously                raising distillation temperature at                80-100&#8451;, adjusting distillation pressure                to -0.098 MPa to -0.095 MPa to obtain a crude                product, and rectifying. The catalyst is                1,3-bis[2-diphenylphosphino-6-alkylpyridine-4]-2,                3-dihydroimidazole palladium chloride.
   USE - Preparation of vinylene carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.

The acid-binding agent                  comprises one or more of triethylamine, pyridine,                  pyrrole and diethylenetriamine, preferably                  pyridine.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  anhydrous chloroacetaldehyde to carbon dioxide is                  1:1.2.Preferred Process: The rectification is                  carried out at 90-92&#8451; at a pressure of                  -0.098 MPa to -0.095 MPa.

PN KR2417097-B1
TI Blue hydrogen production apparatus, has adsorption            device that adsorbs and removes a large amount of            nitrogen contained in the hydrogen discharged from the            cooler in a buffer tank.
AU CHOI B Y
AE CHOI B Y (CHOI-Individual)
GA 202289173A
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a carbon dioxide removal                device provided with a settling tank for                transferring nitrogen containing hydrogen in gas                discharged from a pressure circulation-type                adsorption device. A compressor increases pressure                of nitrogen containing hydrogen at 29-30 and 23 bar                from a settling tank bar. A cooler cools the                nitrogen containing the pressurized hydrogen with                fresh water. A hydrogen separator stores a small                amount of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane.                An adsorption device adsorbs and removes a large                amount of nitrogen contained in the hydrogen                discharged from the cooler in a buffer tank.
   USE - Blue hydrogen production apparatus.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The alkaline polymer                  includes 7-20C aryl-containing olefin and/or                  7-20C aryl-containing alkyne polymer, the aryl                  group does not include heteroatoms; the                  heteroatom includes oxygen atom, sulfur atom                  and/or phosphorus atom; the alkaline polymer                  compound includes                  1,1-dimethyl-4-(4-(4-phenylphenyl)phenyl)piperidin-1-ium                  hydroxide polymer of formula (I) and/or                  1,1,3,3-tetramethyl-2-oxo-4-(4-(4-phenylphenyl)phenyl)imidazolidine-1,3-diium                  dihydroxide of formula (II). The number average                  molecular weight of the alkaline polymer is                  200,000- 300,000 g/mol. The                  amount of alkaline polymer is 5-99 pts. wt. based                  on 100 pts. wt. pores-containing substrate; the                  amount of inorganic nano-materials is 1-3 pts.                  wt. based on 100 pts. wt. pores-containing                  substrate.n = 100-6000.Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises activating the pores-containing                  substrate before pouring; the activation                  treatment includes plasma activation, laser                  treatment, electrolytic reduction treatment,                  surfactant treatment and/or modification of the                  surface of the substrate with hydrophilic                  functional groups; the alkaline polymer is                  prepared by polymerizing aryl groups, piperidone                  groups and/or imidazolone groups-containing                  monomers, then performing quaternization reaction                  and anion exchange reaction to obtain alkaline                  polymer; the degree of substitution of the                  quaternization reaction is 80-100%; the method of                  dissolving the alkaline polymer includes                  performing a constant temperature water bath or                  an oil bath under shear; the temperature of the                  dissolution is 80-120degrees Celsius; the                  dissolution time is 2-12 hours; the shear rate is                  150-1000 revolutions/minute; the concentration of                  the inorganic nano-materials in the mixed                  solution is 1-50 wt.%; the addition amount of the                  solvent in the mixed solution is 3-5 times the                  mass of the alkaline polymer; the dynamic                  viscosity of the mixed liquid is 500-10000 cp;                  the solid content of the mixed liquid is 10-25%;                  the solvent in the mixed solution is the first                  solvent; the first solvent includes a polar                  solvent; the polar solvent includes dimethyl                  sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone, ethanol, ethylene                  glycol, methanol and/or water. In the                  preparation, the casting includes vacuum                  filtration, vacuum adsorption, dipping, hot                  pressing, positive pressure filtration, spraying,                  knife coating and/or centrifugation; the pressure                  of the positive pressure filtration is 0.2-0.4                  MPa; the method further comprises pre-treatment                  and post-treatment before the pouring; the                  pre-treatment comprises soaking the                  pores-containing substrate in the second solvent                  for 1-60 seconds; the post-treatment comprises                  filtering and washing the poured substrate with                  positive pressure in a second solvent, where the                  number of washings is 2-3 times; the second                  solvent includes methanol, ethanol, ethylene                  glycol, isopropanol and/or propanol. The                  preparation method comprises (1) polymerizing                  aryl groups, piperidone groups and/or imidazolone                  groups containing monomers, then performing                  quaternization and anion exchange reactions to                  obtain the alkaline polymer; dissolving the                  alkaline high molecular polymer in the first                  solvent, and then mixing it with the inorganic                  nano-materials to obtain a mixed solution, (2)                  performing activation treatment on the pores                  containing substrate, and then soaking the                  activated pores containing substrate in the                  second solvent for 1-60 seconds, then casting the                  mixed solution obtained in step (1) on the                  substrate, and (3) filtering and washing                  substrate obtained in step (2) with second                  solvent for 2-3 times under positive pressure,                  and drying to obtain the anion exchange composite                  membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic                  nanomaterials includes silica, titanium dioxide,                  zirconium dioxide, zirconium diphosphide, cerium                  oxide, iron trioxide, tungsten trioxide,                  molybdenum trioxide, graphene and/or carbon                  quantum dots.

TI Recycling lithium in lithium salt solution comprises injecting lithium salt solution into carbonizing device, starting stirring and heating, introducing carbon dioxide, carrying out solid-liquid separation, adding phosphoric acid.
AU YUAN Z
   DAI Z
AE HUNAN YONGSHAN LITHIUM IND CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021803958
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling lithium in lithium salt solution comprises injecting the lithium salt solution into the carbonizing device, starting stirring and heating, continuously introducing carbon dioxide, after reacting completely, carrying out solid-liquid separation, obtaining the lithium precipitation mother liquor and lithium carbonate, injecting the lithium precipitation mother liquor into the carbonizing device, adding phosphoric acid, discharging the gas generated by the reaction and when the pH value of the reaction solution is 7.8-9, solid-liquid separation to obtain the secondary lithium precipitation mother liquor and lithium phosphate.

   ADVANTAGE - The recycling rate of the lithium can reach 99.8%, the by-product can be used as the fertilizer liquid change the sale, the comprehensive recycling efficiency is high, and it is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a reaction system for recycling lithium from lithium salt solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium salt solution contains high concentration potassium and sodium ion.

TI Producing dimethyl carbonate by adding to reactor            reactants comprising carbon dioxide and methanol for            reacting, where reactor operates in continuous mode            with continuous flow of feed of reactants into reactor            and products leaving reactor.
AU ELBASHIR N O
   CHOUDHURY H A
   CHALLIWALA M A
AE QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
   QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 202170412C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing dimethyl carbonate,                involves adding to a reactor reactants comprising                carbon dioxide and methanol for reacting at 100-200                degrees C under a pressure of 1-100 bar, where the                reactor operates in a continuous mode with a                continuous flow of feed of the reactants into the                reactor and products leaving the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dimethyl                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high yield, high selectivity                and high conversion, and provides alternative and                optimized solution towards the problem of formation                of water in dimethyl carbonate synthesis by                reporting optimal feed ratio and scavenger to                methanol feed ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for removing byproduct water from the method by                increasing conversion of the feed, involving adding                a water scavenger to the reactor to remove water in                the reactor in a second reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  pressurizing the carbon dioxide and methanol                  before or after being added to the reactor.                  Preferred Components: The water scavenger                  comprises ethylene oxide. Preferred Composition:                  The feed ratio of the ethylene oxide to the                  methanol is 0-4, preferably 1. The feed ratio of                  methanol/carbon dioxide/ ethylene oxide is 1:1:1.                  Preferred Conditions: The pressure in the reactor                  is 10 bar or 5 bar. The conversion of the feed is                  85-92%. The pressure in the reactor is below 10                  bar. The pressure in the reactor is up to 1 bar.                  The conversion of the methanol is 1-99.9%, and                  the dimethyl carbonate selectivity is 1-99.9%.                  The initial reaction is reacting carbon dioxide                  and methanol to produce dimethyl carbonate and                  water, and the final reaction is reacting                  ethylene oxide and water to obtain ethylene                  glycol.

   X- = halogen ion, preferably F-, Cl-, Br- or I-.

PN CN112694111-A
TI Preparing pseudo-boehmite comprises e.g. preparing            alkaline solution containing aluminum and aluminum            sulfate solution and introducing mixed gas containing            carbon dioxide into aluminum-containing alkaline            solution for carbonization reaction.
AU HAN Y
   YUAN S
   ZHANG C
   WANG Y
   GUAN Y
   DUAN R
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
GA 2021503291
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pseudo-boehmite comprises (i)                respectively preparing alkaline solution containing                aluminum and aluminum sulfate solution; (ii)                introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide                into the aluminum-containing alkaline solution for                carbonization reaction; (iii) adding bottom water                into the reaction kettle, heating to the reaction                temperature, and then adding the material reacted                in the step (ii) and the aluminum sulfate solution                into the reaction kettle for reaction; and (iv)                aging the slurry obtained in the reaction in the                step (iii); filtering after aging; washing the                obtained filter cake and drying to obtain the                pseudo-boehmite.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                pseudo-boehmite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes little amount of washing                water, produces low impurity content, obtains the                alumina with pore structure by calcining, which                meets the requirements of the residue hydrogenation                catalyst carrier, and is simple in process and easy                to operate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  carbonization reaction is performed to reduce the                  pH of the aluminum-containing alkaline solution                  by 0.2-2.2, so the pH of the reacted material in                  step (ii) is 11.8-12.8. In the step (iii), the                  reaction temperature is 50-90 degrees C,                  preferably 55-75 degrees C, and the reaction time                  is 40-80 minutes. In the step (iii), controlling                  the flow rate of the added substance to control                  the pH of the slurry obtained in the reaction                  kettle is 7.5-9.5. In the step (iv), the aging                  condition is: the pH is 7.5-9.5; the temperature                  is 50-90 degrees C, and the time is 30-120                  minutes. In the step (iv), the drying condition                  is: drying temperature is 100-150 degrees C, and                  the time is 6-10 hours. Preferred Components: In                  the step (i), the aluminum-containing alkaline                  solution is at least one of sodium metaaluminate                  solution or potassium metaaluminate solution,                  preferably sodium metaaluminate solution. In the                  step (i), the caustic ratio of the                  aluminum-containing alkaline solution is                  1.15-1.75, preferably 1.25-1.65; the                  concentration of the aluminum-containing alkaline                  solution is 50-350 g /l, preferably 130-280 g                  alumina/l; and the concentration of the aluminum                  sulfate solution is 40-120 g alumina/l in terms                  of alumina/l, preferably 60-90 g alumina/l. In                  the step (ii), the volume percentage of carbon                  dioxide in the mixed gas containing carbon                  dioxide is 30-70%. In the step (iv), the dosage                  of the washing water is 8-25 times of the weight                  of the prepared pseudo-boehmite dry basis                  weight.

PT P
PN IN201941042598-A
TI Preparing amorphous edetate calcium disodium, used to treat hypercalcemia and lead poisoning, comprises degassing solution of edetate calcium disodium in water to remove carbon dioxide, adjusting pH and lyophilizing obtained solution.
AU NANDI S
   REDDY S K
   SIVAKUMARAN M
AE AUROBINDO PHARMA LTD (AUMA-C)
GA 2021437658
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amorphous edetate calcium disodium, comprises: (a) providing a solution of edetate calcium disodium in water; (b) degassing to remove carbon dioxide; (c) adjusting a pH of the solution of the step (b); and (d) lyophilizing the solution of the step (c).
   USE - The process is useful for preparing amorphous edetate calcium disodium (claimed), which is useful for treating hypercalcemia and lead poisoning. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides the edetate calcium disodium, which has impurity content of disodium edetate of less than 0.5 wt.%, and has assay by titrimetry of greater than 100 wt.%; and enables the steps of degassing and pH adjustment of the solution of edetate calcium disodium prior to lyophilization, hence improves the stability of reconstitution solution, and avoids inconsistent pH of the solution and particulate formation or precipitation after reconstitution of the lyophilized product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of degassing is: carried out prior to the lyophilization; and performed by purging ultrapure nitrogen gas. The pH is adjusted: to 7-8; by EDTA; and at 20-30 degrees C. The solution of the step (c) is filtered through 0.22 mu filter.
   ACTIVITY - Cardiovascular-Gen; CNS-Gen; Gastrointestinal-Gen; Antidote.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - 56 g Hydrochloric acid was added to a solution of 100 g edetate disodium dihydrate in 1000 ml water at 20-30 degrees C slowly over a period of 30 minutes. Then pH of the reaction mixture was adjusted to 2-3, and the product was filtered and washed with water. The obtained solid was suspended into 1500 ml water, heated to 40-50 degrees C and stirred for 1 hour. The suspension was filtered, washed with water and dried at 50-60 degrees C to obtain EDTA. Then 17.12 g calcium carbonate was added to a solution of 50 g EDTA in 175 ml water at 40-50 degrees C over a period of 45 minutes. The reaction mixture was cooled to 20-30 degrees C to precipitate edetate calcium. Aqueous sodium hydroxide solution (13.8 g sodium hydroxide in 75 ml water) was added to the formed edetate calcium suspension over a period of 45 minutes, where the observed pH of the solution was 5. Then ultra-high pure nitrogen was purged into the reaction solution at 20-30 degrees C for 6 hours (where observed pH was 10). Then the pH of the reaction mixture was adjusted to 7.2-7.5 using EDTA, and the reaction mixture was stirred continuously for 30 minutes at 20-30 degrees C and filtered through 0.22 mu filter. The obtained clear filtrate was subjected for lyophilization to obtain 57.82 g amorphous edetate calcium disodium having water content of 5-10 wt.%.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

TI Positive electrode plate for lithium-ion battery,            comprises oxide coating layer or nitride coating layer            containing coating material source covering surface and            pores of positive electrode plate.
AU WU P
   ZENG J
   LI S

GA 202128786F
AB    NOVELTY - A positive electrode plate comprises coating                layer covering the surface and pores of positive                electrode plate. The coating layer is oxide coating                layer or nitride coating layer. The coating layer                is formed by reacting coating material source (A)                and coating material source (B).
   USE - Positive electrode plate for lithium-ion                battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The positive electrode plate solves the defect                of existing high water-absorption positive                electrode plate that reacts with trace moisture and                carbon dioxide in the air to affect the                electrochemical performance of the final battery. The coating layer is coated on                  the surface and/or the pores of the positive                  electrode active material layer on the surface of                  the positive electrode sheet. The positive                  electrode active material layer comprises                  positive electrode active material, conductive                  agent and binder. The positive electrode active                  material is at least one of lithium-iron-metal                  phosphate (LiFexM1-xPO4, where x is 1-0                  (excluding 1), and M is manganese, nickel,                  cobalt, copper or zinc),                  lithium-nickel-cobalt-manganese oxide                  (LiNixCoyMnzO2, where x, y and z are more than 0                  and x+y+z is 1), lithium-nickel-cobalt-manganese                  oxide (LizNixCoyMn1-x-yO2, where z is 0.95-1.05,                  x and y are more than 0 and x+y is less than 1),                  lithium-nickel-cobalt-aluminum oxide                  (LizNixCoyAl1-x-yO2, where z is 0.95-1.05, x, y                  are more than 0 and x+y is 0.8-1 (excluding 1)),                  lithium-nickel-cobalt oxide (LiNixCoyO2, where x                  and y are more than 0 and x+y is 1),                  lithium-nickel-titanium-magnesium oxide                  (LiNixTiyMgzO2, where x, y and z are more than 0                  and x+y+z is 1),                  lithium-nickel-cobalt-manganese-aluminum oxide                  (LizNixCoyMnwAl1-x-y-wO2, where z is 0.95-1.05,                  x, y and w are more than 0 and x+y+w is 0.8-1                  (excluding 1)), lithium-manganese oxide-lithium                  metal oxide (xLi2MnO3.(1-x)LiMO2, where M is                  nickel, cobalt or manganese), lithium titanate                  (LiTiO2), layered lithium manganate (LiMnO2),                  lithium nickelate (Li2NiO2) and lithium spinel                  manganate (LiMn2O4). The inert gas used in                  preparation is at least one of nitrogen, argon                  and helium. The coating material source (A) is                  organometallic compound or metal halide. The                  metal halide is at least one of aluminum                  tribromide, aluminum trichloride, boron                  tribromide, titanium tetraiodide, titanium                  tetrachloride, zirconium tetrachloride, zirconium                  tetraiodide and tantalum pentachloride. The                  coating material source (B) is gas that can react                  with coating material source (A) to generate                  oxides or nitride, or substance that can be                  atomized or vaporized and reacted with coating                  substance source (A) to generate oxide or                  nitride, or plasma gas that can react with                  coating material source (A) to generate oxide or                  nitride. The gas is water vapor, ozone and                  oxygen. The substance is hydrogen peroxide,                  methanol or ethanol. The plasma gas is chosen                  from ozone, oxygen, ammonia, helium, hydrogen,                  argon, neon, water, nitric oxide,                  tetrafluoromethane, hexafluoroethane and carbon                  dioxide. Preferred Composition: The positive                  electrode active material layer comprises 75-99                  wt.% positive electrode active material, 0.5-15                  wt.% conductive agent and 0.5-10 wt.% binder.                  Preferred Property: The coating layer has                  thickness of less than or equal to 10 nm.

TI Surface-treated lithium metal cathode used for lithium metal battery, comprises lithium metal cathode, and protective layer comprising lithium carbon dioxide, lithium oxalate, lithium carbonate and/or lithium carbonite formed on cathode.
AU KIM S W
AE GRINERGY CO LTD (GRIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021222960
AB    NOVELTY - A surface-treated lithium metal cathode comprises a lithium metal cathode, and a protective layer (a) comprising lithium carbon dioxide, lithium oxalate, lithium carbonate and/or lithium carbonite formed on at least one surface of the lithium metal cathode.
   USE - Surface-treated lithium metal cathode used for lithium metal battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The surface-treated lithium metal cathode provides lithium metal battery having desired capacity and energy density, stable charging/discharging efficiency, and improved life performance, as compared to battery containing non-treated lithium metal cathode, and suppressed life deterioration and safety accidents due to internal short circuits. The lithium metal cathode can be surface-treated using dry ice blasting. The surface-treatment of lithium metal cathode suppresses non-uniform growth of lithium dendrite due to contamination and impurities on the surface of lithium metal cathode, and minimizes lithium reaction during life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The protective layer (a) further comprises lithium fluoride. The surface-treated lithium metal cathode further comprises a protective layer (b) comprising lithium fluoride formed on the layer (a).

PN CN112408415-A
TI Preparing granular low-silicon 13X molecular sieve useful as an adsorbent for efficient removal of carbon dioxide impurities in cryogenic air separation comprises mixing normal silicon 13X molecular sieve, inorganic binder and pore former.
AU CHEN W
   WANG P
   ZHOU Y
   CHEN C
   LI Y
   XU H
   WEN Y
   WEN X
AE SHANGHAI LUQIANG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHNR-C)
   SHANGHAI CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (SHNR-C)
GA 2021270376
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing granular low-silicon 13X molecular sieve comprises (i) mixing the normal silicon 13X molecular sieve, inorganic binder and pore former, and then drying, roasting to obtain a granular 13X molecular sieve, (ii) processing alkali treatment of the granular 13X molecule with an alkaline solution of sodium silicate, and then liquid-solid separation and drying to obtain a granular 13X molecular sieve, (iii) immersing the alkali-treated granular 13X molecular sieve into a high-basicity sodium aluminate solution for conversion treatment, and washing, drying and activating the granular low-silicon 13X molecular sieve.
   USE - The particulate low-silicon 13X molecular sieve is useful as an adsorbent for efficient removal of carbon dioxide impurities in cryogenic air separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, and more stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for particulate low-silicon 13X molecular sieve prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the silicon to aluminum ratio of the normal silicon 13X molecular sieve is 2.40-2.80. In the step (iii), the silicon-to-aluminum ratio of the particulate low-silicon 13X molecular sieve is 2.00-2.10. In the step (i), the inorganic binder is a clay binder. The pore-forming agent includes methyl cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose, maltodextrin and/or corn starch. The mass ratio of the normal silica 13X molecular sieve to the inorganic binder is (50-90):(10-50). The mass of the pore-forming agent is 1-10% of the total mass of the normal silica 13X molecular sieve and the inorganic binder. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molding adopts extrusion molding or granulation molding, during the drying process, the temperature is 100-180 degrees C and the time is 1-24 hours, during the baking process, the temperature is 400-650 degrees C and the time is 1-6 hours. In the step (ii), the alkaline solution of sodium silicate is a mixed aqueous solution of sodium silicate and sodium hydroxide, and in the alkaline solution of sodium silicate, The mass percentage of silicon oxide is 5-25%. The mass percentage of sodium oxide is 2-20%, according to the solid-liquid mass ratio 1:(1-50), immerse the granular 13X molecular sieve into the alkaline solution of sodium silicate. In the step (ii), the alkali treatment process adopts dynamic alkali treatment under continuous pump feeding is carried out at 80-100 degrees C for 2-36 hours, drying at 100-150 degrees C for 2-36 hours. In the step (iii), in the high alkalinity sodium aluminate solution, the mass percentage of sodium aluminate is 5-35%, according to the solid-liquid mass ratio 1: (1-20), dipping the alkali-treated granular 13X molecular sieve into the high alkalinity sodium aluminate solution. The conversion treatment process is carried out under continuous stirring at 80-120 degrees C for 4-48 hours, washing to a pH value of 9-10, during the drying process, the temperature is 120-180 degrees C for 2-10 hours. The activation is carried out under the protection of vacuum or inert gas at 300-550 degrees C for 2-8 hours.

TI Manufacture of dispersion liquid for e.g. pharmaceuticals, involves bubbling gas chosen from oxygen, carbon dioxide and methane to mixture of dispersion medium and particles of preset diameter, stirring and performing ultrasonication.
AU RONALD N
   KYURO S
AE UNIV KYUSHU NAT CORP (UKUY-C)
GA 202108499D
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of dispersion liquid involves bubbling a gas chosen from oxygen, carbon dioxide and methane to a mixture comprising a dispersion medium and particles having mean particle diameter of 100 nm or less, stirring, and performing ultrasonication of the mixture during bubbling of gas.
   USE - Manufacture of dispersion liquid (claimed) useful in preparation of pharmaceuticals and foodstuffs.
   ADVANTAGE - The dispersion liquid having excellent storage stability can be produced by simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) storage stability improvement method of dispersion liquid, which involves bubbling gas to mixture comprising dispersion medium and particles having mean particle diameter of 100 nm or less, stirring, and performing ultrasonication of the mixture during bubbling of gas; and
   (2) dispersion liquid containing dispersion medium and particles. The interfacial tension between crude oils is 1/2 or less of the interfacial tension between water and crude oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixture is stirred for predetermined time until the dispersed state of the particles does not substantially change in the dispersion medium. The processing time of the ultrasonication is 0.3-3 seconds. Ultrasonication is carried out using ultrasonic wave having frequency of 20 kHz or more, for 1 hour or less. Preferred Components: The particles contain metal, metal oxide, nonmetallic substance and/or carbon nanotubes. The metal oxide contains silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, zinc dioxide and/or titanium dioxide. The particles are surface-treated.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersion medium contains water, polyvinyl alcohol and/or ethylene glycol, and surfactant chosen from a nonionic surfactant, an anionic surfactant and a cationic surfactant. Preferred Properties: The interfacial tension between the crude oils is 25 mN/m or less. The pH of the dispersion medium including water is 5-8.

PN CN111689484-A; CN111689484-B
TI Preparing aerogel-derived nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped porous carbon comprises e.g. preparing nitrogen-phosphorus co-doped hydrogel by one-step hydrothermal method, freeze-drying and carbonizing or activating with carbon dioxide.
AU HAN B
   SUN Y
   ZHAO L
   SUI Z
AE NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
GA 2020953772
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aerogel-derived nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped porous carbon comprises preparing nitrogen-phosphorus co-doped hydrogel by one-step hydrothermal method, freeze-drying the hydrogel to prepare nitrogen-phosphorus co-doped aerogel, and carbonizing or activating the aerogel with carbon dioxide at high temperature.
   USE - The co-doped porous carbon is useful for fuel cell oxygen reduction reaction electrocatalysis, super capacitor and gas adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The co-doped porous carbon: has porous structure and uniform nitrogen and phosphorus co-doping, high specific surface area, obvious pore structure and heteroatom doping. The method has mild reaction conditions; utilizes easily available and low cost raw materials; and is simple and suitable for large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for aerogel-derived nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped porous carbon prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding block copolymer P123, phytic acid and pyrrole into water, mixing well and placing in a hydrothermal kettle, and hydrothermally reacting to prepare nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped hydrogel, and freeze-drying the hydrogel to obtain nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped aerogel, and (ii) carbonizing or activating the nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped aerogel with carbon dioxide at 850-950 degrees C to prepare nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped porous carbon. In step (i), the hydrothermal reaction is performed at 160-200 degrees C for 10-15 hours. In step (i): the freeze-drying is performed at -45 to -55 degrees C for 60-85 hours. In step (ii), the high-temperature carbonization is performed by heating to 850-950 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-8 degrees C/minutes under the protection of nitrogen or argon, and maintaining for 60-100 minutes, and cooling. In step (ii), the carbon dioxide activation is performed by heating to 850-950 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-8 degrees C/minute under the protection of nitrogen or argon, maintaining and activating under carbon dioxide atmosphere for 60-100 minutes, and finally cooling under the protection of nitrogen or argon. Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio between block copolymer P123, phytic acid and pyrrole is 0.01-0.03:0.8-1.2:0.8-1.2. The nitrogen and phosphorus co-doped porous carbon specific surface area of 1700-2900 m2/g, average pore diameter of 0.5-1.5 nm, and pore volume of 0.8-2.3 cm3/g.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN111675685-A
TI Extracting proanthocyanidins in noni fruit seed residue by washing noni fruit seed residue by salt water and clean water, freezing, blasting, juicing, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting, soaking filter residue, extracting and purifying.
AU LI J
AE AISHIOUDI BIOTECHNOLOGY HAINAN CO LTD (AISH-Non-standard)
GA 202095767A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting proanthocyanidins in noni fruit seed residue involves cleaning noni fruit seed residue, washing using salt water, washing using clean water, draining, putting processed noni fruit seed residue into refrigerator for freezing, putting into blasting tank for blasting, taking out to obtain pre-treatment material A, juicing, using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to remove grease, filtering and removing filtrate to obtain filter residue B, soaking filter residue B into ethanol solution, ultrasonically extracting, filtering and removing filter residue to obtain extracting liquid C, purifying, freeze-drying, and taking out to obtain powder.
   USE - The method is used for extracting proanthocyanidins in noni fruit seed residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can completely eliminates influence of grease in noni fruit seed residue on product purity and content, is simple, is green and environment-friendly, has low energy consumption and high extracting efficiency, realizes industrial extraction, reduces extraction cost, and improves production efficiency of enterprise.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The temperature of clean water is 33-35 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass concentration of sodium chloride in salt water is 6-8%. Preferred Method: Freezing is done at - 10 to - 12 degrees C for 4-5 hours. Blasting is done by putting noni fruit seed residue into blasting tank, filling nitrogen into blasting tank, keeping blasting tank at pressure of 0.5-0.55 MPa and temperature of 50-55 degrees C, pressure maintaining under heat preservation condition for 80-90 seconds, and unloading blasting tank to normal temperature and normal pressure. Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is using entrainer 50 vol.% ethanol solution at 32-35 degrees C for 10-14 minutes at 18-20 MPa. Ultrasonic extraction is done using 75 vol.% ethanol solution for 8-12 minutes at 400-450 W and frequency of 200-220 kHz. Purification involves using macroporous adsorption resin to absorb extracting liquid C, using acid aqueous solution to wash macroporous absorption resin absorbed with proanthocyanidins until washing liquid is clear, using purified water to wash macroporous absorption resin, orderly using different concentrations of ethanol to carry out gradient elution to macroporous absorption resin, mixing collected eluent with different gradients, and vacuum concentrating mixed eluent to obtain extract. Freeze-drying is done at - 30 to - 35 degrees C.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC A12-L04A; E06-A01; E11-K03; E11-Q01C; E11-W; E31-N05C; E33-B

GA 202082838L
