The composition (A) and/or (B) contain 1-70 parts mass powdery flame retardant with respect to 100 parts mass polyol of composition (A). The composition (A) has viscosity of 50-2000 mPa.second at 20 degrees C. The composition (B) has viscosity of 50-1500 mPa.second at 20 degrees C.
   USE - Manufacture of polyurethane foam. Uses include but are not limited to wall material, ceiling and roofing for construction, panel, electric refrigerator, insulation material for building apartment and cold storage warehouse, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane foam having excellent flame retardance, flexibility, workability and uniform density distribution can be manufactured. The generation of the contractile difference in interlayers can be suppressed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The powdery flame retardant has average particle diameter of 50 mu m or less and specific gravity of 1.0-5.0. The composition (A) contains the powdery flame retardant, and specific gravity of powdery flame retardant is 1-5 times the specific gravity of composition (A). Preferred Components: The powdery flame retardant is a red phosphorus, phosphate-containing flame retardant, stannate-containing flame retardant, boron-containing flame retardant, metal hydroxide and/or metal oxide. The composition (A) and/or (B) further contain a liquid flame retardant chosen from phosphate ester and a bromine-containing flame retardant. The powdery flame retardant contains red phosphorus as main component, and other flame retardant excluding red phosphorus. The composition (A) and/or (B) further contain a foaming agent comprising water, hydrocarbon, hydrofluorocarbon and halogenated olefin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polyurethane foam has difference of density and core density of 0.1-12 kg/m3. The polyurethane foam has total calorific value of less than 9 MJ/m2 when heated at radiant heat intensity of 50 kW/m2 for 20 minutes measured according to ISO-5660.

TI Material in form of sheet for e.g. adsorbing gases            e.g. ethylene, carbon dioxide, or oxygen for preserving            e.g. fruits, comprises metal or its alloy comprising            porous metal oxide layer(s).
AU FANG J
AE UNIV SYDNEY TECHNOLOGY (UYSY-Non-standard)
   UNIV SYDNEY TECHNOLOGY (UYSY-Non-standard)
   ALOXITEC PTY LTD (ALOX-Non-standard)
   ALOXITEC PTY LTD (ALOX-Non-standard)
   ALOXITEC PTY LTD (ALOX-Non-standard)
GA 202057299K
AB    NOVELTY - A material comprises metal or its alloy                comprising a porous metal oxide layer(s).
   USE - Material in the form of sheet used for                adsorbing gases e.g. ethylene, carbon dioxide, or                oxygen for preserving perishable product e.g.                fruits e.g. banana, apple, or cherry, or vegetable                for slowly ripening of fruit and purification of                water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent flexibility, and                ensures preservative effect for up to 10 weeks, and                is manufactured economically.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of material, which involves                anodizing metal or its alloy in the presence of the                electrolyte in which the voltage is varied                throughout the anodization;
   (2) preservation of product, which involves                placing the material in the vicinity of the                product; and
   (3) purification of water, which involves                contacting the material with water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide                  layer(s) comprises a three-dimensional disordered                  network of channels in which the pores have                  non-constant diameter. The metal or its alloy                  comprises a metal oxide layer on each side. The                  metal is aluminum, copper, zinc, manganese,                  palladium, or titanium, preferably aluminum. The                  material is attached to the base structure chosen                  from wood, glass, quartz, silicon, water-proof                  paper, plastic or cloth. Preferred Properties:                  The material has thickness of 1 micron to 1 mm.                  The metal oxide layer(s) has thickness of 300 nm                  to 1 mm, surface area of 20-50 m2/g, preferably                  20-40 m2/g. and non-porous constant volume of                  200-600 cm3/g. The metal or its alloy has                  thickness of 300 nm to 50 microns. The pores have                  non-constant diameter of 1.5-200 nm, or 1.5-250                  nm. The electrolyte is phosphoric acid. Preferred                  Process: The preservation of product involves                  placing product in container together with the                  material, and sealing the container, flushing                  container an inert gas prior to sealing, and                  wrapping the product with the material. Preferred                  Conditions: The preparation of the material is                  performed by applying 0-400 V, preferably 0-140                  V, the voltage is varied throughout the                  anodization by increasing the voltage linearly,                  and applying the voltage in a series of pulses,                  by increasing voltage at a rate of 0.05-0.3                  V/second, or 0.1-0.2 V/second, in a series of                  pulses by repeatedly switching the voltage                  between the voltage of 100-200 V, and 0 V each                  second, for 10-30 minutes, or 10-20 minutes, or                  30-150 minutes, 50-150 minutes, 90-150 minutes,                  or 120-150 minutes. The anodization is performed                  at 0-10 degrees C, preferably 5 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The metal alloy is                  aluminum alloy or zinc alloy, preferably aluminum                  alloy. The aluminum alloy is aluminum foil,                  comprises aluminum, and one or more of copper,                  zinc, iron, manganese, palladium, silicon, or                  titanium. Preferred Composition: The aluminum                  alloy comprises at least 90 wt.%, preferably at                  least 99 wt.% aluminum. Preferred Property: The                  aluminum alloy has thickness of 3-10                  micron.

PT P
PN CN111308015-A; CN111308015-B
TI Method for automatically identifying suspect peaks of ship exhaust gas telemetry data, involves determining number of identified suspect peaks, and determining time serial number corresponding to number of identified suspect peaks.
AU PENG S
   SU N
   HU J
AE TIANJIN RES INST WATER TRANSPORT ENG (TWTE-C)
   TIANJIN RES INST WATER TRANSPORT ENG (TWTE-C)
GA 2020586876
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide concentration data. Sampling time interval is determined. A time scale is determined based on the sampling time interval. Noise reduction filtering process is performed. Sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide correlation discriminant index is determined. Comprehensive discrimination index is calculated. Peak time is recorded when the comprehensive discrimination index exceeds threshold value. Number of identified suspect peaks is determined. Time serial number is determined corresponding to the number of identified suspect peaks.
   USE - Method for automatically identifying suspect peaks of ship exhaust gas telemetry data.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding artificially suspect peaks identifying issues and identification results inaccuracy, reducing heavy workload and automatically identifying suspect ships that exceed sulfur dioxide emissions so as to provide basis and support for maritime supervision.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graph representing a method for automatically identifying suspect peaks of ship exhaust gas telemetry data. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN210689399-U
TI Carbon dioxide phase change expansion induced cracking device, has pressing middle part equipped with rotary channel, where right end of rotary channel is connected with clamping groove, and one-way valve fixed with right end of charging passage.
AU ZHANG L
AE HUBEI ZHAODONG MACHINERY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202052288F
AB    NOVELTY - A cracking device, belonging to the technical field of breaking carbon dioxide phase change expansion, comprising outer tube, a bottom plate, a flange, an activator, a lead, an inflation joint mechanism; the bottom plate is sealed in the outer right end, the flange is sealed the outer left end, activator is set in the outer pipe, one end of the lead connected with the activator, other end of the lead extends through the flange external pipe; the inflation joint mechanism is set on the flange, comprising a main body, a sleeve, a clamping piece, a rotating head, a one-way valve. the structure is simple, the operation is convenient, flexible and practical, it does not need special machine can directly for filling through the filling pipe in the field, greatly reducing storage of safety hazard during transportation.

PT P
PN KR2020055518-A
TI Extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain use in composition for improving skin, involves incubating Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain, removing culture solution from culture and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU LIMCHANGSOON
   KIM H R
   AHN Y S
   GWAK J Y
AE PHYCOILBIOTECH KOREA INC (PHYC-Non-standard)
GA 202043806X
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain involves incubating Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain at a photon density of 0.1 to 60 micro-mole photons/square meter, removing the culture solution from the culture, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the culture from which the culture medium is removed.
   USE - Method for extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain used in a composition for improving skin, and the composition is intended to be used as a raw material for a cosmetic, an external preparation for skin, or a quasi-drug, and the skin improvement is at least one selected from whitening, skin elasticity recovery, anti-aging, wrinkle improvement and anti-inflammatory (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method of extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain enables to increase the lutein content in the strain extract more than twice, to obtain a strain extract with improved skin improvement function, the skin improvement is at least one selected from whitening, skin elasticity recovery, anti-aging, wrinkle improvement and skin inflammation response reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain is preserved at Korean collection for type cultures (KCTC NO: 18635P). The composition comprises an extract extracted by the method of extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain. Preferred Conditions: The culture is performed at a photon density of 0.1 to 1 micro-mole photon/square meter/second in the first culture step and 30 to 60 micro-mole photon/square meter/second in the second culture step. The supercritical extraction is made of 80 to 700 bar at an extraction temperature of 40 degrees C to 70 degrees C. Preferred Process: The culture comprises a first culture step of shaking culture at 110 to 120 revolutions/minute for not less than 90 hours and not greater than 100 hours at 26-30 degrees C, and a second culture step of shaking the culture in the first culture step at 110 to 120 revolutions/minute at 26-30 degrees C for not less than 100 and not greater than 140 hours. The method of extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain further involves lyophilizing the culture before supercritical extraction. The method of extracting Auxenochlorella protothecoides strain further involves the step of purifying the supercritical extract.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Antiinflammatory.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

   CNRS CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 202042073G
AB    NOVELTY - A metal-cyclam complex (I), is new.
   USE - New metal-cyclam complex is used as catalyst for catalysis, preferably electrocatalysis for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide in aqueous medium, and electrocatalytic oxidation of water to oxygen, and detection and/or capture of chloride ion, bromide ion, fluoride ion, hydrogen phosphate ions, monovalent manganese ion, and sulfate ions (all claimed). Can also be used for purification of liquids and gases.
   ADVANTAGE - Complex (I) is economical and environmentally-friendly, and has excellent stability, and enables detection of metal ions with excellent sensitivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal-cyclam complex of formula (I), is new.
   R1-R4 = H, 1-5C alkyl, or (CH2)mR';
   m = 0-3;
   R' = C6H5(Ph), NO2, CO2(1-3C alkyl), O(1-3C alkyl), Cl, F, Br, or I;
   S' = conductive material, preferably nanoparticles or electrode;
   M = optionally Ni, Cu, Hg, Zn, Co, Cd, or Pb;and
   n = 1-4.
TF Preferred Definitions:
   S' = carbon electrode or gold electrode.

PN CN110922604-A; CN110922604-B
TI Preparing multi-level pore structure cobalt-based metal organic framework material used for e.g. adsorption of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide, dissolving cobalt salt and organic ligand in solvent and adding pore-forming agent.
AU WU Y
   LI F
   CHEN Q
   FENG L
   FU J
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202025898B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing multi-level pore structure cobalt-based metal organic framework material, involves dissolving cobalt salt and organic ligand in a solvent, adding pore-forming agent before or after the organic ligand is added to obtain a multi-level porous metal-organic framework material ZIF-67 which is the finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multi-level pore structure cobalt-based metal organic framework material for adsorption of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide, activated persulfate degradable organic pollutant atrazine or controlled synthesis of hollow structure tricobalt tetrasulfide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides multi-level pore structure cobalt-based metal organic framework material having higher adsorption and catalytic performance than micropore ZIF 67, cheap and easily available raw materials under short reaction time, by environmentally-friendly, simple, easy and industrial process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of cobalt salt and organic ligand is 1:(1-20). The mass-to-volume ratio of cobalt salt and organic ligand of solvent is 1:(10-40) (g/ml). The concentration of pore-forming agent is 1-10 g/l, preferably 1-7.5 g/l. Preferred Components: The cobalt salt comprises cobalt chloride, cobalt nitrate or cobalt acetate. The pore-forming agent comprises sodium borohydride and/or potassium borohydride. Preferred Method: The method involves (i) dissolving the cobalt salt and the organic ligand in the solvent, completely mixing and stirring the reaction, (ii) adding pore-forming agent to the reaction solution obtained in step (i), stirring the reaction and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain a crude product, and (iii) washing the crude product with methanol and drying to obtain the multi-level porous metal-organic framework material ZIF-67, which is the multi-level pore structure cobalt-based metal organic framework material. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is controlled at 20-50 degrees C, preferably 25-50 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand comprises imidazole, 2-methylimidazole or 4-methylimidazole, preferably 2-methylimidazole. The solvent comprises N,N-dimethylformamide, methanol or deionized water, preferably methanol.

TI Preparation of porous carbon nitride material used for photocatalytic degradation of methylene blue by mixing lime nitrogen and deionized water, magnetically stirring, introducing carbon dioxide gas, reacting, drying, roasting and washing.
AU CAI K

AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 2019A6743K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of porous carbon nitride material comprises mixing lime nitrogen and deionized water at mass ratio of 1: (3-6) in beaker, putting beaker in water bath, magnetically stirring, controlling temperature of water bath at 65-85 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas into deionized water, controlling pH at 8-10, reacting for 2-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, uniformly milling, drying in oven to obtain precursor, adding precursor into crucible with cover, putting into muffle furnace, roasting at 540-560 degrees C for 2-4 hours, naturally cooling, soaking in hydrochloric acid, washing and removing calcium salt.

PN CN110436523-A
TI Preparing vanadium product and sodium chromate by using vanadium chromium solution comprises e.g. reacting vanadium precipitating agent, vanadium chromium solution, oxidizing agent and vanadium removing agent, heating and crystallizing.
AU WU Z
   JIANG L
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 201997208B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vanadium product and sodium chromate by using vanadium chromium solution comprises (a) adjusting the pH of the vanadium chromium solution using organic acid to 2-4, then adding vanadium precipitating agent for reaction, solid-liquid separating, washing the solid, drying and calcining to obtain vanadium pentoxide, (b) heating solid-liquid separation liquid prepared in step (a), adding oxidizing agent and vanadium removing agent, solid-liquid separating, and passing carbon dioxide into the liquid to obtain white precipitate, continuing passing carbon dioxide until the pH of the solution reaches 8-9, cooling, and solid-liquid separating, and (iii) concentrating the liquid obtained in step (b) and crystallizing to obtain sodium chromate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vanadium product and sodium chromate by using vanadium chromium solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high product purity; can recycles vanadium product and high rate of chromate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vanadium chromium solution is obtained by sodium treatment roasting or alkali leaching. The vanadium chromium solution has a vanadium content of 5-80 g/l, and the chromium content is 5-80 g/l. The vanadium precipitation agent is ammonium hydrogencarbonate and/or ammonium carbonate, preferably the addition of vanadium precipitation agent in an amount of given as specification. In step (b), the oxidizing agent is hydrogen peroxide. In step (b), the vanadium removing agent is ferrous oxalate, calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide, and the addition of vanadium removing agent in a molar ratio of vanadium is 1-1.2:1. Preferred Method: In step (b), the heating temperature is 120-200 degrees C, and the time is 30-120 minutes. In step (b), the reaction time of adding vanadium removing agent is 15-30 minutes. In step (b), the cooling is carried out to -5 to 0 degrees C. In step (c), the concentration temperature is 150-300 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid is formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid, octanoic acid, maleic acid, valeric acid and/or acrylic acid.

PT P
PN CN110373432-A
TI System useful for co-producing succinic acid and alcohol, comprises succinic acid synthesis unit, membrane system treatment unit and alcohol synthesis unit.
AU WAN Y
   CAO W
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 201990249X
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises succinic acid synthesis unit, membrane system treatment unit and alcohol synthesis unit. The synthesis gas enters succinic acid. The purified synthesis gas is obtained by purifying synthesis unit by succinic acid synthesis unit entering alcohol synthesis unit to synthesize the alcohol, and synthesizing succinic acid-containing material solution synthesized by succinic acid synthesis unit entering membrane system processing unit.
   USE - The system is useful for co-producing succinic acid and alcohol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves purity and synthesis gas fermentation process of gas utilization efficiency, and reduces cultivation of cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of processing the system, which involves subjecting synthetic gas to succinic acid synthesis reaction through succinic acid synthesis unit to remove carbon dioxide from synthesis gas, obtaining reaction solution, entering synthesis gas after removing carbon dioxide to alcohol synthesis unit to synthesize the alcohol, and treating solution by membrane system treatment unit to obtain succinic acid and by-produced acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred System: The system further comprises syngas storage tank, and a decompression valve is arranged on an outlet line of the syngas storage tank. A pressure gauge is arranged on gas outlet pipe along the gas circulation direction, upstream and downstream of the pressure reducing valve. The succinic acid synthesis unit comprises succinic acid reactor, the succinic acid reactor comprises an exhaust gas discharge line, a tail gas valve is arranged on the exhaust gas discharge line, and the reaction solution is constructed in succinic acid reactor. A gas distributor is arranged below the liquid level of the reaction solution, and the gas inlets of the gas distributor are respectively connected to the syngas inlet line and the exhaust gas intake line, and the exhaust gas intake line is connected to the exhaust gas discharge line, and the exhaust gas is exhausted of connection between the intake line and the exhaust gas discharge line is located upstream of the exhaust valve. A vacuum pump is arranged on the exhaust gas intake pipeline. The succinic acid reactor further comprises a stirring device. The succinic acid reactor further comprises reaction condition detecting device, preferably a pressure gauge, a temperature electrode, and a pH electrode. The membrane system processing unit comprises first filtration membrane module and second filtration membrane module. The liquid outlet of the succinic acid synthesis unit is feed port of the filter membrane module is connected, the retentate of the first filter membrane module is returned to the succinic acid synthesis unit, and the permeate outlet of the first filter membrane module is connected to the feed port of the second filter membrane module. The membrane system processing unit further comprises concentrated crystallization unit and bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit. A permeate outlet of the second filtration membrane module is connected to a feed port of the concentrated crystallization unit, and a crystallization mother liquid outlet of the concentrated crystallization unit is a feed port connection of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit. The feed liquid in the acid chamber of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit is used to adjust the pH of permeate in the first filter membrane module after the acid is supplemented. The acid supplemented to the feed liquid in the acid chamber of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit is any one or a combination of two of hydrochloric acid, acetic acid or formic acid. The feed liquid in the alkali chamber of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit is used to adjust pH of the liquid in the succinic acid synthesis unit, and the concentrate of the second filtration membrane module or the alcohol synthesis unit. The base supplemented to the feed liquid in the alkali chamber in the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit is sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate or liquid ammonia. The concentrate of the second filtration membrane module is returned to the succinic acid synthesis unit after adjusting the pH. The filtration membrane in the first filtration membrane module comprises an organic microfiltration membrane having a pore diameter of 0.1-1 mu m and inorganic microparticle having a pore diameter of 0.1-1 mu m, filter membrane, organic ultrafiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut-off of 1-1000 KDa or inorganic ultrafiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut-off of 1-1000 KDa, or its combination. The filtration membrane in the second filtration membrane module comprises organic nanofiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut off of 100-1000 Da and/or inorganic nanofiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut off of 100-1000 Da. The bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit comprises a bipolar membrane electrodialysis device. The bipolar membrane electrodialysis device comprises bipolar membrane electrodialysis membrane stack, the bipolar membrane electrodialysis membrane stack comprising at least one set of unit comprising an anion exchange membrane, a cation exchange membrane and polar membranes which are arranged in order. The system comprises syngas storage tank, a succinic acid synthesis unit, a membrane system treatment unit, and an alcohol synthesis unit, and a synthesis gas storage tank. The outlet pipe is connected with the succinic acid synthesis unit, and the purified synthesis gas purified by the succinic acid synthesis unit enters the alcohol synthesis unit to synthesize the alcohol, and the succinic acid synthesized by the succinic acid synthesis unit enters the membrane system treatment unit. The outlet pipe of the syngas storage tank is provided with a pressure reducing valve, and a pressure gauge is arranged upstream and downstream of the pressure reducing valve along the gas circulation direction. The membrane system processing unit includes a first filtration membrane module, a second filtration membrane module, a concentrated crystallization unit, and a bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit; wherein the liquid discharge port of the succinic acid synthesis unit and the first filtration membrane module The material port is connected, the retentate of the first filter membrane module is returned to the succinic acid synthesis unit, the permeate outlet of the first filtration membrane module is connected to the feed port of the second filtration membrane module. The permeate of the second filtration membrane module outlet is connected to the feed port of the concentrated crystallization unit, and the crystallization mother liquid outlet of the concentrated crystallization unit is connected with the feed port of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit. The feed liquid in the acid chamber of the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit is used for adjustment after supplementing the acid The pH of the permeate in the first filtration membrane module. The liquid in the alkali chamber in the bipolar membrane electrodialysis unit is used to adjust the succinic acid synthesis unit, the second filtration membrane module concentrate or the alcohol synthesis unit after replenishing the alkali pH of the medium liquid. The concentrate of the second filtration membrane module is returned to the succinic acid synthesis unit after adjusting the pH. The method further involves adding carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The method involves performing succinic acid synthesis reaction in succinic acid reactor, where the tank pressure of the succinic acid reactor is 0.01-0.15 MPa, and the gas circulation flow rate in the reactor is 0.01-1 vessel volumes per minute (vvm), when the carbon dioxide volume concentration in the gas after the reaction is less than 10%, entering synthesis gas to alcohol synthesis unit to synthesize the alcohol, where the microorganism in the succinic acid synthesis reaction is Anaerobiospirillum succiniciproducens, Mannheimia succiniciproducens, Actinobacillus, Corynebacterium glutamicum or genetically engineered bacteria Escherichia coli. The fermentation reaction process in the succinic acid synthesis reaction is continuous fermentation process and or batch fermentation process. The fermentation reaction process in the succinic acid synthesis reaction is supplemented with a medium component containing a carbon source. The microorganism used for alcohol synthesis in the alcohol synthesis unit is Clostridium ljungdahlii, C.carboxidivorans or Butyribacterium methylotrophicum. The fermentation reaction process in the alcohol synthesis unit is supplemented with a medium component not containing a carbon source. The synthesized alcohol comprises ethanol and/or butanol.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC D05-A03; E10-C02D2; E10-E04E; E11-M; J01-E03

PN WO2019170931-A1
TI Suppressor filter system installed in e.g. chimney, for suppressing polluting and non-polluting particles in e.g. carbon dioxide, has fabric structure that is provided with arc of several polygonal openings.
AU CANADA SIERRA L
AE TECHNOKONTROL GLOBAL LTD (TECH-Non-standard)
   CANADA SIERRA L (SIER-Individual)
GA 2019784769
AB    NOVELTY - The suppressor filter system has a fabric structure that is made of perforated material. The fabric structure is provided with an arc of several polygonal openings, and the polygonal openings are arranged irregular with respect to contiguous polygonal opening. A unit volume of surface area of fabric structure is about 3200 times contact surface of flammable fluids, which have specific heat conduction capacity.
   USE - Suppressor filter system installed in chimney, smoke outlet, catalytic converter, exhaust pipe, and filter for suppressing polluting and non-polluting particles in or without atmospheric gaseous or liquid suspension such as carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide, and nitrous oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The filter system is enabled to prevent the particles from reaching living beings and/or environment as whole.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the suppressor filter system.
   Distribution layers (A)
   Water/liquid heater (B)
   Water chiller (C)
   Vapor direction (D)
   Interspersed gases (E)

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN209149168-U
TI Internet-of-things based melon patch environment monitoring system, has environment detection sensor fixed in melon patch, and illumination intensity detecting sensor and carbon dioxide concentration detecting sensor fixed together.
AU YANG H
   LUO D
   BAI L
AE SICHUAN ILINK TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SCCE-C)
GA 201966348L
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model belongs to the field of application of the Internet of Things, and discloses a melon patch environment monitoring system based on the Internet of Things, which automatically monitors the environment of the melon patch, thereby improving management efficiency. The system comprises: a microprocessor disposed on a management center, a display screen and a lora gateway, and an environment detecting sensor distributed in the melon patch; the lora gateway is connected to the microprocessor, and the microprocessor is connected to the display screen; each environment detecting sensor is provided with a lora module; each environment detecting sensor is in a networking connection to the lora gateway through a lora module; the environment detecting sensor includes an environment temperature detecting sensor, an environment humidity detecting sensor, a soil moisture detecting sensor, a light intensity detecting sensor, and a carbon dioxide concentration detecting sensor.
DC T01 (Digital Computers); T06 (Process and Machine Control)

PT P
PN CN109734838-A; CN109734838-B
TI Synthesizing a high concentration anionic            polyacrylamide involves mixing the acrylamide monomer            and the anionic monomer, adjusting pH for the first            time, followed by adding photoinitiator and an azo            initiator, and adjusting pH a second time.
AU HE G
   TANG Y
   WANG Q
   WEI X
   GONG M
   ZHANG L
AE JIANGSU FEYMER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201945171L
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing a high concentration anionic                polyacrylamide involves mixing the acrylamide                monomer and the anionic monomer, adjusting pH for                the first time, followed by adding photoinitiator                and an azo initiator, and adjusting pH a second                time, passing carbon dioxide to remove oxygen, then                add foaming agent or continue to pass carbon                dioxide. The polyacrylamide rubber block is                obtained by the photoinitiator light initiation and                the azo initiator relay initiation. After cutting,                granulating, drying, grinding and sieving, a                powdery polyacrylamide product is obtained.
   USE - Method for synthesizing a high concentration                anionic polyacrylamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize a high                concentration anionic polyacrylamide, which                increases the bulkiness of the rubber block,                reduces the hardness of the rubber block, solves                the problem of cutting, pre-grinding and                granulation of high-concentration products, and                makes the production of high-concentration products                coherent and increases the production                capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The anionic monomer                  includes acrylic acid and/or                  2-acrylamido-2-methyl-propanesulfonic acid. The                  foaming agent is ammonium sulfate, ammonium                  carbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate, ammonium                  chloride, and dry ice. The photoinitiator is                  2-hydroxy-2-methyl-1-phenyl-1-propanone,                  2-hydroxy-4'-(2-hydroxyethoxy)-2-methylpropiophenone,                  1-hydroxycyclohexyl phenyl ketone,                  2-methyl-2-(4-morpholinyl)-1-(4-(methylthio)phenyl)-1-propanone,                  2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl-diphenylphosphine oxide,                  2-hydroxy-2-methyl-1-(4-(2-hydroxyethoxy)phenyl)-1-propanone.                  The azo initiator is azobisisobutylphosphonium                  hydrochloride, azobisisobutyrazole, porphyrin                  hydrochloride, azobiscyanovaleric acid,                  azodiisopropylimidazoline, and                  azobisisobutyronitrile. Preferred Condition: The                  pH is adjusted to 5-6 for the first time by                  adding sodium hydroxide solution. The                  hydrochloric acid or lye is added for a second                  time to adjust the pH 5-6.

TI Preparing Trichosanthes kirilowii seed oil involves crushing seeds, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide, and concentrating extract under reduced pressure.
AU INVENTOR U
AE QIDONG XIANGRUI CONSTR CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3558L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Trichosanthes kirilowii seed oil involves crushing seeds, passing through 90 mesh sieve, and extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide at 40-45 degrees C temperature under 25-27 MPa pressure for 4-5 hours, and concentrating the extract under reduced pressure to obtain an extract, dissolving the extract in an appropriate amount of water, extracting with petroleum ether for 2-4 times, and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain a petroleum ether extract. The petroleum ether extract is separated by reverse-phase silica gel column chromatography, and eluted with chloroform-methanol in a volume ratio of 45:1, 35:1, 25:1, 10:1 and 1:1 to collect 25:1 elution, and concentrated.
   USE - Method used for preparing Trichosanthes kirilowii seed oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate, can quickly obtain the components of seed oil, has the anti-thrombotic and anti-cancer effects, and can be used as for the preparation of medicine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: Preparing Trichosanthes kirilowii seed oil involves extracting the seeds at 42 degrees C temperature under 26MPa pressure for 4.5 hours, and extracting by petroleum ether for 3 times.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Thrombolytic.

TI Preparing solid for e.g., removal of sulfur            compounds, by mixing basic copper carbonate powder,            metal oxide powder e.g., copper, and binder, contacting            mixture with aqueous solution and kneading paste,            extruding paste, and calcining.
AU BAZER-BACHI D
   CHICHE D
   LOPEZ J
   SERRES T
   FRISING T
   DUCREUX O
   EUZEN P
   BAZE-BACHI D
   CHISHO D
   CERE T

   DICKOVA O

AE AXENS (AXEN-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   AXENS (AXEN-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   AXENS (AXEN-C)
GA 201898657V
AB    NOVELTY - A solid is prepared by mixing a set of                compounds comprising greater than or equal to 1                basic copper carbonate (Cu2(OH)2CO3) powder, metal                oxide powder selected from copper, zinc, iron,                and/or manganese, and greater than or equal to 1                binder; contacting mixture with aqueous solution                and kneading paste; extruding paste at 3-25 MPa;                and calcining extrudates at 140-500 degrees C for                10 minutes to 6 hours under a gaseous flow                comprising oxygen.
   USE - Preparation of a solid for removal of sulfur                compounds from gaseous or liquid feedstocks, such                as natural gases, biogas, synthesis gases, gases                containing carbon dioxide carbon dioxide, or liquid                hydrocarbons, for removal of carbon monoxide,                compounds containing mercury, or compounds                containing arsenic in gas or liquid feedstocks, and                for catalysis of Dussan reaction (water-gas shift                reaction).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid has improved adsorption                performances, particularly sulfur compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Extrudates are dried at                  70-160 degrees C for 1-24 hours before being                  calcined. Metal oxide powder content, introduced                  into the set of compounds and expressed as ratio                  of metal oxide(s) powder mass introduced into the                  set of compounds to total mass of malachite and                  metallic oxide(s) powder(s) introduced into the                  set of compounds, is 0.01-1. Preferred                  Properties: Cu2(OH)2CO3 powder has bimodal                  distribution. Preferred Compositions: Cu2(OH)2CO3                  powder comprises 0.1-99.9 wt.% malachite                  particles having D50 of 1-15 mu m, and 0.1-99.9                  wt.% malachite particles having D50 of 25-100 mu                  m, relative to the total weight of malachite.                  Metal oxide included in the set of compounds is                  copper oxide, where the set of compounds is free                  of zinc oxide. Metal oxide included in the set of                  compounds is zinc oxide, where the set of                  compounds is free of copper oxide. Aqueous                  solution contains acid or base peptizing agent.                  Aqueous solution contains nitric acid (HNO3),                  where ratio of HNO3 mass/metallic oxide mass is                  0.5-10 wt.%. Basic peptizing agent is selected                  from sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  aqueous ammonia, tetraethylammonium hydroxide                  (TEAOH), and/or ammonium carbonate, where ratio                  of basic peptizing agent mass/metal oxide mass is                  1-10 wt.%. Aqueous solution is deionized water.                  Preferred Condition: Calcination is carried out                  at 200-500 degrees C.

AU SHANG J
AE GUIZHOU ZHIJIN ZIMEI BAMBOO FUNGUS IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3010N
AB    NOVELTY - A bamboo fungus cultivation method involves                paving cultivation material in flat and open                container to a thickness of 8-16 cm, placing bamboo                fungal strain in the cultivation material for every                6-10 cm, placing the open container with the bamboo                fungal strain in cultivation room, maintaining                temperature of the cultivation room at 20-25                degrees C, monitoring concentration of carbon                dioxide in the cultivation room, ventilating the                cultivation room for 8 hours, when concentration of                carbon dioxide exceeds 0.04%, monitoring moisture                content of surface of cultivation material in                cultivation room within 3 cm, spraying wet aerosol,                when moisture content is less than 60%, spraying                water for 30-60 minutes every time and harvesting                bamboo fungus, after 75-85 days of                cultivation.

   ZHAO Y

AE TONGFU HEAT TREATMENT KUNSHAN CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0468G
AB    NOVELTY - The processing method of automobile shock absorber piston rod involves washing automobile shock absorber piston rod using water and 6-8% detergent at 40-60 degrees C for 2-3 seconds and then drying at 220-280 degrees C, oxidizing at 410-450 degrees C and then heating for 50-60 minutes, placing in furnace at 560-580 degrees C, introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide into the furnace and then processing for 360-380 minutes, adding water vapor and then processing at 500-520 degrees C for 60-70 minutes, introducing water vapor into the furnace and then processing for 40-50 minutes, immersing in lubricant for 15-20 minutes and naturally drying.

The invention adopts multi-layer structure, at least one layer of middle interlayer is an interlayer of the graphene, the graphene structure compact, low oxygen and carbon dioxide permeability rate, increases the beer bottle after graphene interlayer, the permeability can reach 120 days is less than 1 asterisk 10-6 g, carbon dioxide loss is far less than 15%, which can meet the requirement of beer storage, transportation, it can be widely used for storage of beer.

AU SONG M

   BI Y
   YAN J
   REN Y
   ZHAO S
   CUI Q
AE SHANDONG TRALIN PAPER CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A23353
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous production of ethanol, fulvic acid and carbon dioxide with straw comprises performing solid-liquid separation of products after enzymolysis, fermenting, distilling residues and performing sulfonation of solid phase.
   USE - The method is for simultaneous production of ethanol, fulvic acid and carbon dioxide with straw.
   ADVANTAGE - The method converts more than 70% of the lignin in the residue into water-soluble lignin sulfonate (fulvic acid).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sulfonation reaction comprises adding sulfonating agent to solid phase and reacting for 2-6 hours at 155-180 degrees C, preferably for 4 hours at 165-170 degrees C. The enzymolysis comprises adjusting pH of material to 4.5-5.5 and reacting at 40-55 degrees C for 48-96 hours after adding enzyme. The fermentation comprises adjusting pH of liquid phase to 4.5-5.5, adding yeast and fermenting for 24-48 hours at 35-45 degrees C. Preferred Components: The sulfonating agent is ammonium sulfite and/or sodium sulfite, preferably ammonium sulfite. The dosage of enzyme is 3-8%. The amount of yeast is 0.5-3%.

PT P
PN CN114275781-A
TI Preparing carbon material porous by using            regenerated strong alkali activated biomass raw            material, involves activating suspension of biomass,            introducing carbon dioxide gas, filtering, separating            filtrate and solid material, and pyrolyzing.
AU JIANG B
   SU W
   ZHANG B
   HU J
   XING Y
   HONG C
AE BEIJING FANGXING KECHUANG ENVIRONMENTAL (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202251911E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous carbon material by                regenerating strong base activated biomass raw                material, involves (i) activating the suspension of                biomass raw materials to obtain first material, the                activator includes alkali metal hydroxide. (ii) The                carbon dioxide gas is introduced into first                material to obtain second material. The second                material is filtered, and filtrate first material                and solid material are separated. (iii) The solid                material is subjected to pyrolysis reaction and                subsequent processing to obtain a finished porous                carbon material. The alkali metal hydroxide                includes sodium hydroxide and potassium                hydroxide.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon material porous                preparing carbon material by using the regenerated                strong alkali activated biomass raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to form a suspension by                configuring filtered substance one and filtered                substance two, which is used for the absorption and                purification of pyrolysis waste gas. The method has                cost-effective, good pore-forming effect, and less                waste generation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The amount of                  biomass raw material and sodium hydroxide or                  potassium hydroxide is the mass ratio of                  1:0.1-1:2. The amount of carbon dioxide gas                  introduced is 0.002-0.06 mole carbon di-oxide per                  gram of biomass, where pH value of material 1less                  than or equal to11. The molar ratio of the dilute                  sulfuric acid and/or oxalic acid to the potassium                  ions and/or sodium ions in first raw carbon is                  1:2-1.1:2. Preferred Conditions: The pyrolysis                  temperature is 300-800degrees Celsius, and the                  heating rate is 5-20degrees Celsius/min. The raw                  carbon is dried in an oven at 80-105degrees                  Celsius to a constant weight. Preferred Process:                  The solid material is pyrolyzed to obtain first                  raw carbon, which is filtered after being treated                  with dilute sulfuric acid and/or oxalic acid to                  obtain filtrate and second raw carbon. The second                  raw carbon is washed to be neutral and then dried                  to obtain a finished product of the porous carbon                  material. The calcium hydroxide and/or calcium                  oxide are added to the filtrate 1 to obtain a                  precipitate. The molar ratio of calcium hydroxide                  and/or calcium oxide to potassium ions and/or                  sodium ions in the filtrate one is                  1:2-1.2:2.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Performing geological improvement, by adding oxygen-containing organic powder or aqueous solution and calcium salt powder or aqueous solution to soil, fermenting oxygen-containing organic substance using microorganisms, and forming calcium carbonate in soil.
AU SUZUKI Y
   SAMURAI A
AE UNIV TOKYO (UYTY-C)
GA 202255479Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing geological improvement by forming calcium carbonate in soil using anaerobic fermentation involves (a) adding at least an oxygen-containing organic powder or aqueous solution and a calcium salt powder or aqueous solution to the soil, (b) generating carbon dioxide by fermenting the oxygen-containing organic substance in an anaerobic environment using microorganisms, and (c) forming calcium carbonate (CaCO3) in soil by the reaction of the calcium salt with the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for performing geological improvement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs geological improvement without producing harmful by-product by using microorganisms in soil even under condition where oxygen deficiency and competition between microorganisms are present.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a geological improvement kit, which comprises a powder or an aqueous solution of an oxygen-containing organic substance, and a powder or an aqueous solution of a calcium salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Microorganism: The microorganism is a microorganism having a gene encoding methylmalonyl-CoA decarboxylase. The microorganism is a microorganism belonging to phylum Bacteroidota or order Bacteroides. The microorganism is a microorganism originally present in soil or a microorganism added from the outside before step (b). Preferred Method: The method involves 1-100 mM oxygen-containing organic substance, and 100-5000 mM calcium salt i.e. calcium halide. In step (a), the nitrate powder or aqueous solution is further added to the soil, where the nitrate reduction reaction is allowed to proceed in the soil. The step (a) involves adding a phosphate powder or aqueous solution to the soil, for forming hydroxyapatite in the soil. Preferred Kit: The kit further comprises (i) a nitrate powder or an aqueous solution, (ii) a powder or an aqueous solution of phosphate, and (iii) microorganisms having ability to produce carbon dioxide by fermentation in an anaerobic environment using the oxygen-containing organic substance as a raw material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The oxygen-containing organic substance is an organic acid. The organic acid is succinic acid, propionic acid or pyruvic acid.

PT P
PN KR2022026285-A
TI Recovering lithium from waste battery e.g. in smartphone comprises e.g. obtaining waste cathode material particle from lithium secondary battery, heat-treating by carbon-dioxide and immersing in leaching solvent to obtain lithium carbonate.
AU LEE J
   CHOI Y
   KYU K
AE LG ENERGY SOLUTION LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202237088G
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering lithium from waste batteries, comprises (i) obtaining a waste cathode material particle from a lithium secondary battery; (ii) heat-treating the waste cathode material particles under a carbon-dioxide/deionized water atmosphere; (iii) immersing the heat-treated waste cathode material particles in a leaching solvent; and (iv) obtaining lithium carbonate from the leachate.
   USE - The lithium is useful in various industrial fields, namely small portable devices e.g. smartphones, laptops and electric vehicles (EVs).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require the use of a separate solvent extracting agent, e.g. a sodium compound, for extracting the lithium component, and since the lithium component of the waste cathode material can be obtained in the form of high-purity lithium carbonate, the obtained It is eco-friendly and economical as lithium carbonate can be used immediately for production of cathode materials without a separate high-purity treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the cathode of the lithium secondary battery is pulverized and classified. In the step (ii), heat treatment is performed at 450-800 degrees C, and the humidity in the carbon-dioxide/deionized water atmosphere is 40% or more. The step (ii) is performed by bubbling carbon-dioxide into deionized water. In the step (iii) the leaching solvent used includes water. In the step (iv), the leachate is distilled under reduced pressure to obtain lithium carbonate particles. The method further comprises (v) bicarbonating the lithium carbonate particles obtained in the step (iv) to obtain a solution containing lithium bicarbonate; and (vi) obtaining lithium bicarbonate particles from the solution containing lithium bicarbonate.
   EXAMPLE - Cathode was separated from the waste-treated lithium secondary battery, pulverized and classified to obtain 20 g of powdery waste cathode material particles. Then, 20 g of waste battery particles including particles of waste cathode material in powder form were obtained by separately pulverizing and classifying the waste-treated lithium secondary battery without cathode separation. A three-dimensional ultrasonic vibrating classifier was used as the classification device. The sieve size of the upper sieve was 30 mesh (595 mu m), and the sieve size of the lower sieve was 60 mesh (283 mu m). The obtained waste cathode material particles and waste battery particles were heat-treated under the conditions of carbon-dioxide/deionized water at 550 degrees C for 2 hours, and carbon-dioxide 50 cc/minute deionized water at 80 degrees C bubbling) at 850 degrees C for 2 hours.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN113599405-A
TI Extracting effective ingredients from Acanthopanax fruit, by drying, crushing and sieving Acanthopanax fruit, extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide, heating and refluxing raffinate with aqueous ethanol solution, extracting, filtering, concentrating and performing ultrafiltration.
AU JIANG L
   FAN B
   ZHANG B
AE HUNAN LVMAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9315N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting a variety of effective ingredients from Acanthopanax fruit involves (i) washing and drying Acanthopanax fruit, crushing and sieving Acanthopanax fruit and extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain Acanthopanax oil and raffinate, (ii) placing the raffinate obtained in step (i) in an aqueous ethanol solution for heating and refluxing, performing solid-liquid separation to obtain filtrate and filter residue, and (iii) ultrasonically extracting the filter residue obtained in step (ii) with water, filtering and concentrating to obtain an aqueous extract, removing protein by successively salting out aqueous extract, performing ultrafiltration to remove small molecules and decolorizing resin, concentrating and drying to obtain Acanthopanax fruit polysaccharide.
   USE - The method is used for extracting multiple active ingredients in Acanthopanax fruit (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves comprehensive utilization effect on Acanthopanax, significantly shortens the time required for the process, reduces the loss of plant raw materials and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (iii) further involves after concentrating the filtrate obtained in the step (ii) to recover ethanol, diluting with water, filtering out the insoluble matter, and separating with macroporous resin chromatography, after washing and removing impurities, eluting with ethanol aqueous solution to obtain Acanthopanax polyphenols. In step (iii), the salting out process involves adding calcium chloride to water extract in a mass-volume ratio of 1 g:(20-40) ml, controlling the pH to 5-6, and precipitating for 30-60 minutes. In step (iii), the ultrafiltration involves using an ultrafiltration membrane to trap molecules with a molecular weight greater than 40000 Dalton and collecting the retentate.Preferred Composition: In step (iii), the concentration of the ethanol aqueous solution in the elution process is 60-70 vol.%. In step (ii), the content of ethanol in the ethanol aqueous solution is 75-88 vol.%. In ultrasonic extraction process, based on 1 g of filter residue, the addition amount of water is 10-25 ml. In step (iii), the concentration process involves controlling the volume of the water extract to be 0.1-0.2 times the volume before concentration. Preferred Conditions: The heating and refluxing process is carried out at 66-75degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours. In step (i), the extraction pressure for the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 15-50 MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-60degrees Celsius, and the extraction time is 1-6 hours. In step (iii), the ultrasonic extraction is carried out at power of 100-600 W for 10-30 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing titanium dioxide nano                square sheet rich in oxygen vacancies/gold-copper                alloy involves (a) preparing titanium dioxide nano                square slices, (b) adding the nano square slices,                polyvinylpyrrolidone and potassium bromide in                sequence to a flask, dispersing into methanol under                ultrasonic treatment, adding copper(II) chloride                dehydrate and hydrogen tetrachloroaurate(III)                tetrahydrate under gentle stirring, heating for                boiling in an oil bath, adding sodium borohydride                to the mixture, reacting under vigorous stirring,                slowly dripping to room temperature, washing the                resulting sample with distilled water and ethanol,                centrifugally separating, finally drying in an oven                to obtain gold-copper alloy-rich titanium dioxide                nano square flakes, and (c) calcining the obtained                nano square flakes in a reducing atmosphere to                obtain the product.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  adding titanium n-butoxide and hydrogen fluoride                  to a PTFE reactor in sequence, reacting at                  100-300 degrees C for 12-36 hours, repeatedly                  washing the obtained solid with ethanol and                  water, centrifuging and baking in an oven at                  40-80 degrees C for 12-20 hours, where the volume                  ratio of titanium n-butoxide and hydrogen                  fluoride is (3-7) ml:(0.6-1) ml. The step (b)                  involves boiling in the oil bath at 80-120                  degrees C for 0.5-2 hours and drying at 40-80                  degrees C for 12-20 hours. The step (c) involves                  calcining at 100-300 degrees C for 1-3 hours at a                  rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute. Preferred Composition: The                  titanium dioxide, polyvinylpyrrolidone, potassium                  bromide, methanol, copper(II) chloride dehydrate,                  hydrogen tetrachloroaurate(III) tetrahydrate and                  sodium borohydride are all used in an amount of                  (80-120) mg, (30-50) mg, (100-120) mg, (50-100)                  ml, (8-10) mg, (2-5) mg and (5-10) mg,                  respectively.

PN CN112774693-A
TI Preparation of monoatomic silver/zinc salt catalyst, involves using silver salt as silver precursor, zinc salt and sulfide are used as zinc sulfide precursor, by one-step hydrothermal synthesis method and hydrogen reduction.
AU GUAN C
   GUO W
   LANG J
   ZHAO G
   HUANG Z
AE BEIJING HYPERION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202154915X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of monoatomic silver/zinc salt catalyst involves using silver salt as silver precursor, zinc salt and sulfide are used as zinc sulfide precursor, and obtaining monoatomic silver/zinc sulfide catalyst through one-step hydrothermal synthesis method and hydrogen reduction.
   USE - Preparation of monoatomic silver/zinc salt catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple and controllable. The prepared catalyst has the active component silver uniformly distributed on the surface of the zinc sulfide carrier in atomic size, and can convert formaldehyde into completely non-toxic carbon dioxide and water under normal temperature conditions. The catalyst's ability to catalyze the degradation of formaldehyde hardly attenuates and the catalyst performance is stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an application of the monoatomic silver/zinc salt catalyst to degrade formaldehyde at room temperature, and can convert formaldehyde into completely non-toxic carbon dioxide and water under room temperature conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silver salt is any one or a mixture of two or more of silver nitrate, silver chloride, silver carbonate, and silver sulfate. The sulfide is any one or a mixture of two or more of thioacetamide, thiourea, and sodium sulfide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the silver salt to the zinc salt is (0.001-0.01):1, and the molar ratio of the zinc salt to the sulfide is 1:(2-8). The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 100-200 degrees C, and the time of the hydrothermal reaction is 6-24 hours. The temperature of the reduction is 200-300 degrees C, and the time of the reduction is 1-10 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Preparation of monoatomic silver/zinc salt catalyst, involves adding a mixed solution of silver salt, zinc salt, sulfide, and ethylene glycol into the autoclave, and the hydrothermal reaction is complete, washing and drying the reaction product to obtain silver ion/zinc sulfide, placing the silver ion/zinc sulfide in a hydrogen atmosphere for high-temperature reduction, and then lowering to room temperature to obtain a single-atom silver ion/zinc sulfide catalyst.

TI Color-changing gas desiccant used for gas analysis and gas purification, and gas includes hydrogen, oxygen, chlorine, hydrochloric acid, ammonia or carbon dioxide, comprises anhydrous magnesium perchlorate and cobalt chloride.
AU WANG G
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 202154176K
AB    NOVELTY - Color-changing gas desiccant comprises anhydrous magnesium perchlorate and cobalt chloride in a mass ratio of (98-99):(1-2), where the desiccant particles have a diameter of 2-4 mm.
   USE - The color-changing gas desiccant useful for gas analysis and gas purification, and the gas includes hydrogen, oxygen, chlorine, hydrochloric acid, ammonia or carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas desiccant has less air resistance, strong water absorption capacity, color change, granular shape and no volatile components, and is convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the desiccant, involving (a) evenly mixing anhydrous magnesium perchlorate, cobalt chloride and water to obtain a mixed solution, (b) removing the moisture to obtain a large piece of desiccant, (c) crushing the large pieces of desiccant to obtain a small-particles of desiccant, and (d) deeply dehydrating the small-particles of desiccant to obtain the gas desiccant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), anhydrous magnesium perchlorate is dissolved in water under stirring, and cobalt chloride is added, where the mass ratio of anhydrous magnesium perchlorate, cobalt chloride and water is (98-99):(1-2):(120-150), and the rotation speed of the stirring process is 300-600 rpm, and the temperature is normal temperature, and the time is 0.5-1 hour. In step (b), the moisture removal process is to place the mixed solution in an oven for heating and dehydration, and the heating temperature is 150-180 degrees C, and the heating time is 2-4 hours, and the moisture content of the bulk desiccant is 1-5%. Preferred Device: The crushing process uses a coarse crusher. Preferred Method: The step deeply dehydrating involves adding the small-particles of desiccant to a vacuum drying oven for vacuum heating and dehydration, where the vacuum degree is -0.1 MPa to -0.06 MPa, and the heating temperature is 120-150 degrees C, and the heating time is 1-3 hours.

The                  nitrogen or carbon monoxide is maintained in the                  head space above the drink, before closing the                  container. TI Culturing precursor cells derived from autologous fat mesenchymal stem cells by isolating cells from human tissues, placing in self-made culture medium, cultivating in carbon dioxide incubator, and transferring to induction medium.
AU QIAO A
AE QIAO A (QIAO-Individual)
GA 2020A9597F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for intelligently culturing precursor cells derived from autologous fat mesenchymal stem cells, involves isolating mesenchymal stem cells from human tissues, placing mesenchymal stem cells in a self-made culture medium to expand mesenchymal stem cells, placing the culture medium in a 4-6% carbon dioxide incubator at 35-36 degrees C with saturated humidity, changing the medium regularly and subculturing, transferring the stably passaged autologous adipose mesenchymal stem cells to induction medium, and after induction, culturing for 5-12 days to obtain neural precursor cells.
   USE - The method is used for intelligently culturing precursor cells derived from autologous fat mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly shortens the growth cycle of neural stem cells and neurons, induces excellent cell growth, meets the needs of clinical stem cell transplantation, and solves many problems caused by other culture media and stimulating factors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The subculture is subculture according to the conventional adherent cell subculture method, where the seeding density during the passage of the conventional adherent cell passage method is 1.5x 104/cm2, the culture system is oligodendrocyte precursor cells serum-free basal medium containing 1% oligodendrocyte precursor cells culture additive, and the culture surface is a culture surface coated with oligodendrocyte precursor cells culture surface coating solution. The adherent cells are digested with trypsin, centrifuged, and resuspended in mesenchymal stem cells culture medium to obtain a cell suspension when the mesenchymal stem cells are passed to the third generation, followed by adjusting the cell concentration of the mesenchymal stem cells in the cell suspension, dropping the cell suspension on the cover glass, and covering the cover glass with basal medium, growing the cells on the cover glass to obtain the fourth generation cell, when the cells grow to cover 62-66% of the surface of the glass slide, pouring out the mesenchymal stem cells culture solution, adding the induction medium, and culturing at 36 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 8 days to obtain neural precursor cells. Preferred Components: The mesenchymal stem cells comprise mesenchymal stem cells derived from bone marrow, umbilical cord, placenta, and/or adipose tissue. The self-made culture medium is a culture medium for clinical use without animal serum and no heterotype protein, where the self-made culture medium further comprises human peripheral venous blood or body fluid. The induction medium is a basal medium supplemented with growth factors comprising epidermal growth factor, basic fibroblast growth factor, insulin-like growth factor-1 and neurotrophic factor 3. The basic medium is a low-sugar Gibco Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's Medium solution containing 2% fetal bovine serum and 1% N2 adjuvant.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. GA 202091506D
AB    NOVELTY - Industrial cooling wastewater treatment and                recycling process comprises taking water from                cooling water circulation tank near circulation                return port, and using regulating valve for                controlling outflow, adding hydrogen ion-containing                agent for conducting chemical reaction against                bicarbonate ion in the wastewater, and discharging                carbon dioxide gas, adding hydroxide-containing                agent for carrying out chemical precipitation                reaction against magnesium ion in wastewater to                form magnesium hydroxide suspended particles,                adding carbonate ion-containing agent to                wastewater, carrying out chemical precipitation                reaction against gallium ions in the wastewater,                adding coagulant to wastewater for flocculating                magnesium hydroxide and calcium carbonate suspended                matter, performing efficient precipitation of                turbid liquid in inclined tube, precipitation                separating and collecting concentrated suspended                particles, discharging supernatant and passing to                sintered metal microporous filtration system.
   USE - The method is useful for treating cooling                wastewater in the electric power, petroleum,                chemical, metallurgy, coking, building materials,                and medicinal industries.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The reactor is                  pipeline disperser. The step (c) uses                  environmentally friendly sodium hydroxide,                  ammonia water or ammonia, and barium hydroxide                  agents. The step (d) uses environmentally                  friendly sodium carbonate agents. Preferred                  Method: The method further comprises selecting a                  backflow port at a position close to the                  circulating water delivery pipe, where the                  cooling water circulation pool is a certain                  distance from the water intake point, then                  merging with circulating water in the pool after                  passing through a check valve, or directly                  connecting to water supply pipe of the                  circulation pool through a check valve, omitting                  the bicarbonate ion in the raw water if the                  amount of bicarbonate ion is small or the                  treatment is not required, or performing the                  second process after sixth process, collecting                  the suspended particles by a mud scraper or a mud                  bucket under the high-efficiency sedimentation                  tank to form concentrated mud, which is                  controlled by a valve and intermittently                  discharging to a single-chamber filter press,                  dehydrating the mud in the closed filter press                  with auxiliary agent of its own weight or                  compressed air, and performing continuous                  dehydration, discharging back the filtrate to the                  nearest collector, completing the filter press                  dehydration, and finally discharging the solid                  mud cake through the valve. The step (c)                  comprises using pipe-type mixer and hose random                  multi-point dispersion dosing device for dosing.                  The step (d) comprises using flexible multi-point                  dispersion dosing device in the mixing tank for                  dosing, and then using flexible multi-point                  dispersion dosing device in the mixing tank for                  dosing.

   ADVANTAGE - The compound increases free volume and flexibility of the polyimide molecular chain, improves poor solubility of traditional gas separation membranes due to rigid chains, has good solubility in common organic solvents, and increases permeability to the acidic greenhouse gas carbon dioxide and increase separation efficiency of other gasses and carbon dioxide.

   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I) or (II);
   (2) spiropyran-containing polyimide of formulae (III) or (IV) are new; and
   (3) preparation of preparation of the compound (III) or (IV).
   r=one or more of 4 groups of formulae (e)-(h);
   Ar'=one or more of 4 groups of formulae (i)-(l); and
   n=100-140.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first catalyst is ferric trichloride, aluminum trichloride, ferrous chloride, or zinc chloride. The second catalyst is potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate, cesium fluoride, lithium carbonate, or sodium hydride. The third catalyst is sodium hydroxide or palladium/carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) or (II) is prepared by mix mesityl oxide, hydroxyl compound, first catalyst, and first reaction solvent, performing isomerization reaction, where hydroxyl compound is 2,5-dihydroxytoluene or resorcinol, mixing with mononitro compound, second catalyst, and second reaction solvent, carry out nucleophilic substitution reaction, mixing with organic solvent, reducing agent, and third catalyst, and performing reduction reaction. The mononitro compound is of formula X-R'. Compound (III) or (IV) is prepared by mixing diamine monomer, dianhydride monomer, and reaction solvent, polymerizing, mixing with catalyst and dehydrating agent, and chemically imidizing.X=F, Cl or Br; andR'=one or more of 4 groups of formulae (m)-(p).Preferred Components: The first reaction solvent is toluene, xylene, biphenyl, or cumene. The molar ratio of mesityl oxide, hydroxyl compound, first catalyst, and first reaction solvent is 1:4-7:0.2-0.5:14-16. The second reaction solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, or dimethylsulfoxide. The reducing agent is sodium arsenite and/or hydrazine hydrate. Preferred Method: The isomerization reaction is done at 80-130 degrees C. The nucleophilic substitution reaction is done at 100-150 degrees C. The reduction reaction is done at 80-110 degrees C for 15-18 hours.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 8 Compounds (III) or (IV) are specifically claimed, e.g. compounds of formulae (IIIa)-(IIIe).

TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate by using chitin as crystal form control agent involves preparing calcium chloride solution with anhydrous calcium chloride, adding chitin solution to calcium chloride solution, passing carbon dioxide gas.
AU XIE X
   GAO P
   SUN L
   LEI J
AE UNIV QUANZHOU NORMAL (UQZN-C)
   UNIV QUANZHOU NORMAL (UQZN-C)
GA 202003547N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate by using chitin as crystal form control agent involves preparing Calcium chloride solution with 0.9-1.1 mole/L and pH of 11.5-12.5 anhydrous calcium chloride, adding chitin solution to above Calcium chloride solution, then passing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system. The reaction is performed under magnetic stirring conditions at 25-35 degrees C for 5-6 minutes. The reaction products is collected, the reaction products are thoroughly washed, and then dried to constant weight with constant temperature in blast drying oven to obtain nano calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing nano calcium carbonate by using chitin as crystal form control agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nano calcium carbonate by using chitin as crystal form control agent that has smaller and more uniform particle size, has a good popularization and application prospect, and in simple and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The concentration of Calcium chloride solution is 1.0 mole/L and pH is 12.5. The concentration of the chitin solution is 1-5 %. The ratio of the Calcium chloride solution to the chitin solution is 30:10-30mL. The reaction temperature is 35 degrees C. The reaction time is 6 minutes. The speed of the carbon dioxde gas is 1 L/minute.

PN CN110550647-A; CN110550647-B
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate using chitinase as crystal form control agent, comprises e.g. preparing calcium chloride solution using anhydrous calcium chloride, mixing with chitinase solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas and reacting.
AU XIE X
   SUN L
   LV F
   WENG W
AE UNIV QUANZHOU NORMAL (UQZN-C)
   UNIV QUANZHOU NORMAL (UQZN-C)
GA 2019A61196
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate using chitinase as crystal form control agent, comprises (i) preparing calcium chloride solution having concentration of 0.9-1.1 mol/l and pH of 11.5-12.5 using anhydrous calcium chloride, (ii) adding chitinase solution into the above calcium chloride solution, then introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system, and carrying out reaction at 25-35 degrees C for 5-6 minutes under magnetic stirring condition, and (iii) collecting the reaction product, washing thoroughly, and drying to a constant weight using constant temperature blast drying oven to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano calcium carbonate using chitinase as crystal form control agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes chitinase as crystal form control agent to make the particles of nano calcium carbonate to be smaller and more uniform; has simple operation; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The concentration of calcium chloride solution is 1 mol/l, and pH is 12.5. The concentration of chitinase solution is 0.04435-1.3304 g/l, and volume ratio between calcium chloride solution and chitinase solution is 30:10-12 ml. The reaction temperature is 35 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6 minutes. The carbon dioxide gas is introduced at a rate of 1 l/minute.
