PN CN110510650-A
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate, comprises e.g.            calcining calcium carbide slag and adding into hot            water, filtering dissolved mixture, formulating into            calcium hydroxide suspension, passing gas containing            carbon dioxide, and reacting.
AU YI D
   ZHANG L
   SHA Z
AE SHANGHAI BLUE SILANE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2328V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate, comprises                calcining calcium carbide slag at 600-700&#176;                C, adding calcined calcium carbide slag into hot                water at 80-90&#176; C in a weight ratio of                1:5-7 to dissolved it to obtain dissolved mixture,                filtering the dissolved mixture to obtain dissolved                slurry, formulating dissolved slurry and water into                3-10 wt.% calcium hydroxide suspension, passing gas                containing carbon dioxide into calcium hydroxide                suspension, and reacting at 20-50&#176; C to                obtain carbonized slurry.
   USE - The method is useful preparing nano-calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate has high whiteness                and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                nano-calcium carbonate prepared by above method,                where nano-calcium carbonate has average particle                diameter of 50-150 nm and whiteness of &#8805;                90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises adding auxiliary agent and dispersant,                  where auxiliary agent is hydrochloric acid,                  citric acid, acetic acid, acrylic acid, sulfuric                  acid and/or phosphoric acid; dispersant is sodium                  polyacrylate, phosphoric acid and/or                  pyrophosphate. The method further comprises                  whitening step carried out by adding 0.05-1%                  ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid and 0.01-1%                  sodium dithionite into the carbonized slurry. The                  method further comprises subjecting 2-5 wt.%                  whitened slurry to modification at                  50-90&#176; C during surface coating step,                  where modifier is stearic acid, phosphate                  coupling agent, titanate coupling agent, and/or                  aluminate coupling agent. Preferred Components:                  The addition amount of auxiliary agent is 0.1-1                  wt.% of the amount of dissolved mixture. The                  addition amount of dispersant is 0.1-1 wt.% of                  the solid content of calcium hydroxide. The                  volume content of carbon dioxide in the gas is                  30-90%. The flow rate of carbon dioxide in the                  gas is 20-40 m3/minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of quaternary alkylammonium                hypochlorite solution involves preparing quaternary                alkylammonium hydroxide solution, and reacting                quaternary alkyl ammonium hydroxide solution with                chloride. The carbon dioxide level of gaseous phase                portion in reaction process is 100 volume ppm or                less, and the pH of liquid phase in reaction                process is 10.5 or more.
   USE - Manufacture of quaternary alkylammonium                hypochlorite solution used for processing                semiconductor wafer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of                quaternary alkylammonium hypochlorite solution                having excellent storage stability.

   (1) method for processing semiconductor wafer,                which involves processing surface of semiconductor                wafer and quaternary alkylammonium hypochlorite                solution; and
   (2) quaternary alkylammonium hypochlorite                solution, comprising 1 ppb or less of each metal of                sodium, potassium and aluminum.

   Ice water (10)

Preferred                  Condition: The reaction process has reaction                  temperature of -35-15 degrees C. The quaternary                  alkylammonium hypochlorite solution has pH of                  9.0-12.5 at 23 degrees C. Preferred Composition:                  The quaternary alkylammonium hydroxide solution                  comprises 500 ppm or less carbon dioxide and                  chloride gas comprising 10 volume ppm or less                  moisture content. Preferred Process: The method                  for manufacture of quaternary alkylammonium                  hypochlorite solution involves filtering                  quaternary alkylammonium hypochlorite solution                  having pH of 12.5 at 25 degrees C and storing                  quaternary alkylammonium hypochlorite solution                  having pH of 12-14 at 25 degrees C and adjusting                  pH of stored reaction solution having pH 0-7 at                  25 degrees C by dilution process, the solution                  having pH 8.0-12.0 at 25 degrees C.

PT P
PN WO2019220258-A1; CN112135796-A; EP3793947-A1; US2021206660-A1; IN202017052447-A; EP3793947-B1
TI Recovering water from off gas from chemical plant, by separating off gas into gas phase and liquid phase, removing portion of organic compounds of liquid phase by ion-exchange resin bed(s) and granular activated carbon bed(s).
AU IMRAN M
   NAIR S
   SHARMA D
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201998269Q
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering water from an off gas (comprising carbon dioxide, water, and organic compounds) from a chemical plant, involves separating the off gas into a gas phase and a liquid phase; removing a portion of the organic compounds of the liquid phase of the off gas by ion-exchange resin bed(s) and granular activated carbon bed(s); and recovering a liquid that comprises primarily water and less than or equal to 10 wt.% unwanted organic compounds in the liquid phase of the off gas.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering water from off gas (comprising carbon dioxide, water, and organic compounds) from chemical plant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can recover water of a certain quality from an off gas from a chemical plant in a manner that is more energy efficient and less capital intensive than conventional methods.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The off gas comprises saturated hydrocarbons, unsaturated hydrocarbons, chlorinated hydrocarbons, organic acids, glycol, aldehydes, and iron. The organic compounds removed from the liquid phase comprise aldehydes, acids, and chlorides. Iron is removed from the liquid phase of the off gas. The method further involves flowing the liquid phase to separation equipment that comprises a cation ion-exchange resin bed, an anion ion-exchange resin bed, and the granular activated carbon bed, arranged in series, where the separation equipment performs the removing. The liquid phase of the off gas is flowed to the cation ion-exchange resin bed, effluent from the cation ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to the anion ion-exchange resin bed, and effluent from the anion ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to the granular activated carbon bed. The liquid phase of the off gas is flowed to the cation ion-exchange resin bed, effluent from the cation ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to the granular activated carbon bed, and effluent from the granular activated carbon bed is flowed to the anion ion-exchange resin bed. Liquid phase of the off gas is flowed to the anion ion-exchange resin bed, effluent from the anion ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to the cation ion-exchange resin bed, and effluent from the cation ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to the granular activated carbon bed. Liquid phase of the off gas is flowed to the anion ion-exchange resin bed, effluent from the anion ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to an activated carbon bed, and effluent from the activated carbon bed is flowed to the cation ion-exchange resin bed. The liquid phase of the off gas is flowed to the granular activated carbon bed, effluent from the granular activated carbon bed is flowed to the anion ion-exchange resin bed, and effluent from the anion ion-exchange resin bed is flowed to the cation ion-exchange resin bed. The liquid phase of the off gas is flowed to the granular activated carbon bed, effluent from the granular activated carbon bed is flowed to the cation ion-exchange resin bed, and effluent from the cation ion- exchange resin bed is flowed to the anion ion-exchange resin bed. Preferred Compositions: The off gas comprises 45.0-55.0 wt.% water, 45-65 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.1-0.5 wt.% saturated hydrocarbons, 0.01-0.1 wt.% unsaturated hydrocarbons, 1.0-5.0 ppm wt. chlorinated hydrocarbons, 5.0-15.0 ppm wt. organic acids, 0.5-1.0 wt.% glycol, 10.0-30 ppm wt. aldehydes, 1.0-2.0 ppm wt. iron. The gas phase comprises 7.0-10.0 wt.% water, 92.0-94.0 wt.% carbon dioxide 0.1-0.5 wt.% saturated hydrocarbons, 0.01-0.1 wt.% unsaturated hydrocarbons, 1.0-3.0 ppm wt. chlorinated hydrocarbons, and 5.0-8.0 ppm wt. aldehydes. The liquid phase comprises 97.0-99.0 wt.% water, 0.5-0.8 wt.% carbon dioxide, 1.0-2.0 ppm wt. chlorinated hydrocarbons, 5.0-15.0 ppm wt. organic acids, 0.5-1.0 wt.% glycol, 5.0-25.0 ppm wt. aldehydes, and 1.0-2.0 ppm wt. iron. Preferred Parameters: The recovered liquid has a conductivity of less than or equal to 30 mu S/cm. Flow rate through the ion-exchange resin bed(s) and granular activated carbon bed(s) is 2-4 bedvolumes (BV)/hour. Preferred Conditions: The ion-exchange resin bed(s) and granular activated carbon bed(s) are operated at 59-100 degrees F. Preferred Plant: The chemical plant comprises an ethylene glycol production plant.
DC A91 (Ion-exchange resins, polyelectrolytes.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN MY170218-A
TI Extraction of Swietenia mahagoni seed oil used e.g. in antibacterial composition for making body ointment and hair oil, by subjecting ground Swietenia mahagoni seeds to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under specified conditions.
AU SALLEH L M
   HARTATI
AE UNIV MALAYSIA TEKNOLOGI (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 2020534027
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of Swietenia mahagoni seed oil comprises providing ground S. mahagoni seeds; subjecting the ground S. mahagoni seeds to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction at 20-30 MPa, 40-60 degrees C and CO2 flow rate of 2-4 mL/minute; and obtaining oil extract of S. mahagoni seed.
   USE - The method is for extracting Swietenia mahagoni seed oil used in antibacterial composition for manufacturing soap, body ointment and hair oil; for making handmade detergent-free bath bars; as alternative medicine therapy for treating atomic dermatitis or eczema; as alternative body ointment therapy for skin cuts, itches and wounds to ameliorate healing process; and as good natural treatment for promoting good heart health and better blood circulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The Swietenia mahagoni seed oil has antibacterial property. A composition containing the oil extract of Swietenia mahagoni seeds is effective against broad spectrum of pathogenic bacteria including Bacillus subtilis, Staphylococcus aureus, Escherichia coli, or Pseudomonas aeruginosa.
TF ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Antiinflammatory; Dermatological; Vulnerary; Antipruritic; Cardiant; Vasotropic. An oil extract of S. mahagoni seed obtained at CO2 flow rate of 2 mL/minute was tested for its antibacterial activity against Escherichia coli, Bacillus subtilis, or Staphylococcus aureus using disc diffusion method. Results showed zone of inhibition of 9.5 plus minus 0.71 mm against E. coli, 8.5 plus minus 2.12 mm against B. subtilis and 9.5 plus minus 0.71 mm against S. aureus, as compared to zone of inhibition of 15.5 plus minus 0.71 mm against E. coli, 15 mm against B. subtilis and 13 plus minus 1.41 mm against S. aureus for positive control.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109966325-A
TI Extracting Ginkgo biloba leaf extract, by mixing Ginkgo biloba leaf powder in solvent, extracting, introducing carbon dioxide gas, filtering, placing leaf extract on silica gel column, collecting liquid, and performing rotary evaporation.
AU INVENTOR U
AE SUZHOU MIAOWEN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201960607R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting a Ginkgo biloba leaf extract, involving (i) cleaning G.biloba leaves in a ultrasonic wave cleaning machine, drying and pulverizing dried G.biloba leaves into a powder 70-100 mesh, (ii) mixing the G.biloba leaf powder in a solvent, extracting under ultrasonic wave at 50-70 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the solvent while heating and extracting, filtering after extracting, where the filtrate is a crude extract of G.biloba leaf, (iii) placing crude extract of G.biloba leaf obtained in step (ii) on a silica gel column and passing through the column to ethyl acetate, and (iv) collecting the liquid, and performing rotary evaporation by a rotary evaporator to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for extracting G.biloba leaf extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved extraction rate and purity of G.biloba leaf extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The solvent in step (ii) is added in an amount of 10-20 times of the amount of powdered ginkgo leaf. The amount of carbon dioxide introduced is 5-15 ml/hour, preferably 10 ml/hour. Preferred Components: The solvent in step (ii) comprises ethanol, ethylene glycol and/or dimethylformamide. The ethanol is anhydrous ethanol. Preferred Conditions: The extraction under ultrasonic waves in step (ii) is carried out at 60 degrees C. The extraction in step (ii) is carried out for more than 3 hours. The water bath temperature of rotary evaporator is 50-70 degrees C, preferably 65 degrees C, and the rotating speed is 80-120 rpm, preferably 100 rpm. Preferred Property: The dried G.biloba leaf in step (i) is pulverized into an 80 mesh powder.

TI Treating wastewaters for clean environment, involves a cyanide compound and a metallic compound, where wastewaters are subjecting to a single oxidation step during which cyanides compounds are converting into carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
AU GOMEZ LEIVA P
   MENENDEZ DELMIRO V
   PADILLA VIVAS B
   LEIVA P G
   DELMIRO V M
   VIVAS B P
AE ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
   ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
GA 201951837H
AB    NOVELTY - Treating wastewaters involves a cyanide compound and a metallic compound, where wastewaters are subjecting to a single oxidation step during which cyanides compounds are converting into carbon dioxide and nitrogen, this oxidation step comprising the mixing of wastewaters with a chlorine solution and an alkaline agent so as to obtain a mixture, and the alkaline agent is adding in such a quantity so as to maintain the pH of the mixture between 8.8 and 9.5 and the chlorine solution is adding in such a quantity so as to maintain the oxydo-reduction potential of the mixture between 150-450 megavoltz.
   USE - Method for treating wastewaters is used to make our environment clean.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treate wastewaters by transforming all kind of cyanides compounds in nontoxic compounds with a better efficiency, notably in terms of reactive consumption and time of treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The chlorine solution is a sodium hypochlorite solution. The alkaline agent is lime. Preferred Conditions: The pH of the mixture is maintained between 8.9-9.1. The oxydo-reduction potential of the mixture is maintained between 350-400 megavoltz. The wastewaters initially contain between 1.5-15 parts per million in weight of cyanides, including between 1-10 parts per million (ppm) in weight of weak acid dissociable cyanides, between 0.8-3 ppm in weight of zinc, up to 8 ppm in weight of iron and between 0.05-0.5 ppm in weight of lead. The wastewaters are derived from blast furnace gas cleaning. After the oxidation step, the mixture is further subjected to a clarification step where it is separated between clarified water and sludge. The clarified water comprises less than 0.4 mg/liter of cyanides, less than 2 mg/L of zinc, less than 5 mg/L of iron, less than 0.5 mg/L of lead and less than 30 mg/L of ammonia nitrogen. The quantity of chlorine solution used for treating 1 m3 of wastewaters is less than or equal to 6 litres. The quantity of alkaline agent used for treating 1 m3 of wastewaters is less than or equal to 10 litres.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109453817-A; CN109453817-B
TI Preparing nanocomposite catalyst useful for converting carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate, comprises crushing, screening natural attapulgite ore, removing impurities, dispersing, stirring, centrifuging, washing and drying.
AU LIU W
   WANG G
   WANG W
   TANG X
   WANG Y
   DOU W
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV LANZHOU (ULAN-C)
GA 2019254408
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanocomposite catalyst comprises (1) crushing, screening natural attapulgite ore, and removing impurities to obtain attapulgite powder, (2) dispersing the attapulgite powder into a first solvent, and adding a modifier, stirring the reaction, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain solid powder, (3) dispersing the solid powder into a second solvent, adding organic ligand and metal salt, stirring to make mixture completely dissolved, and allowing to stand to obtain pre-product, and (4) placing the pre-product at room temperature or high temperature oven to react sufficiently, centrifuging, washing, drying the product to obtain nanocomposite catalyst, where the metal salt is a rare earth salt and/or transition metal salt.
   USE - The nanocomposite catalyst is useful for converting carbon dioxide into cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanocomposite catalyst: is economical; has stable structure, high catalytic efficiency and easy separation for recycling. The method: is simple, and utilizes easily available raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt is a mixture of a rare-earth salt and transition metal salt, where the weight ratio of the rare earth salt to the transition metal salt is 0.01:1000-1000:0.01. In step (2) the first solvent is methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, dimethylformamide, acetone and/or water, the modifying agent is hydrochloric acid, sodium hexametaphosphate, polyethylene glycol, polyvinylpyrrolidone or sodium tetrafluoroborate and sodium hexafluorophosphate. The second solvent is dioxane, methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, dimethylformamide or acetone and/or water. The organic ligand is terephthalic acid, trimesic acid, 2-methyl imidazole or 2-amino acid and/or imidazole. Preferred Method: In the step (2) the stirring reaction conditions comprise that the mechanical stirring at 10-10000 revolutions/minute for 0.5-100 hours and/or ultrasonic dispersion is carried out while stirring reaction, and the power of ultrasonic dispersion is 100 W, the condition of the drying process comprises that the drying temperature is -70 degrees C to 200 degrees C. In the step (4) the adequate reaction condition comprises that the constant temperature is 25-200 degrees C, and the constant temperature time 25-360 hours.

PN CN109160495-A
TI Synthesizing silver selenide modified graphene useful in solar cells and lithium batteries, comprises e.g. dispersing graphite oxide in deionized water, and dispersing ultrasonically to obtain sol.
AU WANG Y
AE SHANGHAI CUILI ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201906460Q
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing silver selenide (Ag2Se) modified graphene comprises dispersing graphite oxide in deionized water, dispersing ultrasonically to obtain sol, sequentially adding silver nitrate, strong alkali, selenium-containing compound, complexing agent and reducing agent, and stirring for 20-30 minutes; pumping the sol into the high pressure reactor of supercritical device; pumping out air, pumping carbon dioxide for 20-30 minutes, stopping the ventilation, heating to 50-60 degrees C, continuously pressurizing and pumping the carbon dioxide into the high pressure reactor, stabilizing constant temperature and constant pressure for 1.5-2 hours when the pressure reaches 100-120 bar; releasing pressure, filtering precipitate, and washing to obtain silver selenide-modified graphene.
   USE - The modified graphene is useful in solar cells, lithium batteries, thermoelectric devices, nonlinear optical devices, memory and sensors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for silver selenide modified graphene is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the added graphite oxide is 0.2-0.5 g/l, the concentration of silver nitrate is 1-5 mmol/l, and the strong alkali is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide, and the concentration is 0.1-0.5 mol/l; the complexing agent includes mercaptoacetic acid or mercaptopropionic acid, and concentration is 1-2 times the concentration of silver nitrate. The selenium-containing compound includes selenium dioxide, sodium selenite, or potassium selenite, and the molar amount is half of silver nitrate; the reducing agent includes sodium borohydride, potassium borohydride, or hydrazine hydrate, and has molar concentration of 20-40 times that of silver nitrate. Preferred Method: In the method, washing comprising alternately washing with deionized water and ethanol, filtering by centrifuge sedimentation or suction filtration device after each washing, re-dispersing the filtered product in deionized water or ethanol, repeatedly filtering until the pH of the filtrate is 7, last time washing with ethanol to facilitate drying; drying in vacuum oven at 50-80 degrees C, where the vacuum drying condition is vacuum degree not less than 133 Pa.

The container has internal pressure of 2.5-4 MPa when discharging.
   USE - Manufacture of thermoplastic polyurethane foaming particles used for forming molded product. Uses include but are not limited to absorbing material, damping material, sporting article and motor vehicle component.

Preferred Composition: The thermoplastic polyurethane particles comprise 300-950 mass ppm inorganic powder. Preferred Properties: The container has internal pressure of 2.6-3.4 MPa when releasing the particles. The thermoplastic polyurethane particles have melt flow rate of 10-50 g/10 minutes in 10 kg load when heated at 190 degrees C. The thermoplastic polyurethane particles have type A durometer hardness of 90 or less. When the temperature of aqueous medium at the time of impregnation with foaming agent is 80 degrees C or more, the thermoplastic polyurethane particles have melting temperature of -20 degrees C or less and the container has internal pressure of 2.5-7.0 MPa. When the temperature of aqueous medium at the time of releasing is 60 degrees C or more, the thermoplastic polyurethane particles have melting temperature of -20 degrees C or less.

The Bacillusgenus is chosen from                B.licheniformisstrain F1017, B.pumilusstrain F1337,                B.sonorensisstrain F1005 and B.subtilisstrain                F1236. The yeast is chosen from Rhizopus,                Aspergillus usami, A.oryzaeand A.sojae.
   USE - The hair care product is useful for protecting                scalp and improving hair. The hair care product is                chosen from hair conditioner, hair tonic, hair                cream, hair lotion, hair gel, hair pack, hair                massage, hair liquid, hair spray, hair mousse,                treatment agent, non-aerosol mousse, non-aerosol                spray, aerosol mousse, aerosol spray, perm,                bleaching agent, dye, ampoules, shampoo and                conditioner (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing hair care product, which involves (a)                preparing 30-50 pts. wt. goji berry extract, 60-80                pts. wt. iris extract, 90-120 pts. wt. fermented                product of safflower extract, and 60-80 pts. wt.                rooibos extract with respect to 100 pts. wt.                drumstick seed extract, where (i) the goji berry                extract is prepared by preparing a wolfberry powder                by grinding the dried fruits of the wolfberry tree,                obtaining an extract by performing supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction of the goji berry powder,                filtering the extract, and concentrating and                freeze-drying the extract, (ii) the fermented                product of safflower extract is prepared by                preparing safflower powder by grinding the                safflower seeds, performing a hot water extraction                process on the safflower powder to obtain an                extract, and preparing a diluted solution in which                the extract is diluted with water in a volume ratio                of 1:0.5-1.5, inoculating the anaerobic bacteria in                the diluted solution, performing anaerobic                fermentation at 40-50&#176; C for 10 -20 days                to obtain a fermented solution, sterilizing the                fermentation broth and filtering to prepare a                fermented product, aging, concentrating, and                freeze-drying the fermented product, and (iii) the                hot water extraction process is performed by mixing                safflower powder and water in a weight ratio of                1:3-5, and performing a hot water extraction                process at 70-90&#176; C for 5-10 hours.

PN CN114970383-A
TI Numerical simulation of slag/gold melt drop            decarburization behavior under carbon and water            atmosphere involves dividing gas phase and liquid phase            calculation areas according to structure of droplet,            and establishing geometric model of decarburization            process of slag/gold droplet.
AU WU Y
   LIAO Z
   CHEN X
   HAN L
   WANG J
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2022B5225R
AB    NOVELTY - Numerical simulation of slag/gold melt drop                decarburization behavior under carbon and water                atmosphere involves dividing the gas phase and                liquid phase calculation areas according to the                structure of the droplet, establishing a geometric                model of the decarburization process of the                slag/gold droplet, using the block unstructured                grid to mesh the calculation area, establishing                basic physical models for the grid by continuity                equation, momentum equation, composition equation                and VOF model, defining the material properties of                the gas phase and the slag/metallographic phase,                and setting the boundary conditions, specifying the                initial conditions, defining the interaction                between the slag-metal gas phase and the molecular                diffusion volume. The programming coupling custom                model is used, that is, the atmospheric condition                and its component change model.
   USE - Numerical simulation method of slag/gold melt                drop decarburization behavior under CO2 and H2O                atmosphere used in computer numerical simulation                technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient and efficient to                reveal the carbon dioxide and water atmosphere                slag/gold melt drop decarburization behavior,                provides powerful theoretical basis and technical                support for steel making technology improvement and                optimization design.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Numerical simulation of slag/gold melt drop                decarburization behavior under carbon and water                atmosphere involves dividing the gas phase and                liquid phase calculation areas according to the                structure of the droplet, establishing a geometric                model of the decarburization process of the                slag/gold droplet, using the block unstructured                grid to mesh the calculation area, establishing                basic physical models for the grid by continuity                equation, momentum equation, composition equation                and VOF model, defining the material properties of                the gas phase and the slag/metallographic phase,                and setting the boundary conditions, specifying the                initial conditions, defining the interaction                between the slag-metal gas phase and the molecular                diffusion volume. The programming coupling custom                model is used, that is, the atmospheric condition                and its component change model, set monitoring                variables, such as gas phase velocity and carbon                element mass fraction, and decarburization rate                change, discretized the above-mentioned governing                equations, and solved them iteratively, initialized                the entire calculation area, and iterate the                algebraic equations in the calculation area                repeatedly until the decarburization conditions of                slag/gold droplets are satisfied, and the                conservation law is satisfied, that is, the                decarburization of slag/gold droplets under carbon                dioxide and water atmosphere is completed numerical                simulation of behavior, post-processed the                calculation results according to the slag/gold                droplet change preservation mechanism to obtain the                phase distribution and component concentration                distribution under different carbon mass change                fractions, as well as the diffusion coefficient,                the mass change of each component, and the                decarburization rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The droplet adopts a                  two-dimensional semicircular structure. The                  semicircular slag/gold droplets are horizontally                  tangent, and the grid adopts Quad/pave format.                  The numerical simulation method of slag/gold                  droplet decarburization behavior under O                  atmosphere is characterized in that, the material                  property comprises density and viscosity, and                  boundary condition upper and lower sides are                  symmetrical boundaries, the atmosphere inlet is                  the velocity boundary, and the outlet is the                  pressure outlet boundary. The atmosphere                  condition includes considering the mass transport                  of carbon in slag/gold droplets under carbon                  dioxide and water atmosphere according to the                  decarburization characteristics of slag/gold                  droplets. The concentrations of hydrogen                  components carbon dioxide and water at                  equilibrium are calculated from the equilibrium                  partial pressures. The decarburization rate                  between carbon elements in slag/gold droplets is                  mainly controlled by gas phase mass transfer. The                  diffusion coefficient Di of component i,                  &#961; is the gas phase density, Di, m is the                  diffusion coefficient of component i, Xi is the                  mole fraction of component i, Dij is the binary                  diffusion coefficient, R is the heterogeneous                  reaction rate, &#945; is the gas phase volume                  fraction, and A is Gas-liquid interface area, V                  is the unit volume, Yi diff is the difference                  between the concentration of component i and the                  equilibrium concentration, u is the gas velocity,                  Yi is the mass fraction of component i, Ji is the                  mass flux of component i, and R is the                  heterogeneous reaction rate. The finite volume                  method is used to discretize the control                  equations, the calculation process adopts the                  second-order upwind method and the SIMPLE                  velocity-pressure coupling algorithm, the                  pressure interpolation method adopts the PRESTO                  format, the default value is used for the                  relaxation factor of the equation solving, and                  the phase interface is reconstructed by the                  Geo-reconstruct method, and the iteration size is                  1e-5s. The data file is automatically saved                  according to the mass change rate of the                  slag/gold droplet carbon element, the                  decarburization state of the slag/gold droplet is                  determined, when the slag/gold droplet carbon                  mass fraction reaches 1%, then the                  decarburization of the slag/gold droplet is                  completed, and the results of each stage are                  stored according to the carbon mass fraction                  &#8486; is &#946;i(&#964;) where the                  &#946;i is the carbon mass change rate at                  time i, and &#8486; is the preservation                  mechanism.

TI Measuring soil mechanical composition for            evaluating soil basic property, involves taking dry            soil, passing air-dried soil through aperture sieve,            drying, adding hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring            evenly, heating, continue stirring with glass rod, and            discarding supernatant after cooling.
AU LI J
   YU J
   ZOU J
   LIU H
AE JILIN INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (JLCC-C)
GA 2022B3430B
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring soil mechanical composition involves                taking 5g dry soil, passing the air-dried soil                through 2mm aperture sieve, drying, adding 5-10ml                10% hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring evenly,                heating, continue stirring with a glass rod, and                discarding the supernatant after cooling, adding                5ml 0.2 mole/L hydrochloric acid solution, stirring                with a glass rod, let stand, settling, filtering                the supernatant, adding 5ml 0.2 mole/L hydrochloric                acid solution, continue stirring, let standing,                discarding the supernatant, repeating 5 times until                no carbon dioxide is generated, rinsing the sample                with 0.05mol/L hydrochloric acid solution on the                filter paper. The appropriate amount of filter                residue is taken in an oven at 105&#8451;                &#177;2&#8451;, dried, cooled and weighed,                flushed the above filter residue with water into                500ml conical flask, added 5ml 0.5 mole/L sodium                hydroxide solution, added water to 125ml, covered                with a small bucket.
   USE - Method for measuring soil mechanical                composition used for evaluating soil basic                property, revealing the forming environment,                providing basic data for soil conservation,                environmental evolution and other field                quantification inference.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good repeatability, high                accuracy, fast measuring time, improves the                traditional method, realizes wide measuring range,                avoids the disadvantages of the traditional test                method is complex operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Measuring soil mechanical composition involves                taking 5g dry soil, passing the air-dried soil                through 2mm aperture sieve, drying, adding 5-10ml                10% hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring evenly,                heating, continue stirring with a glass rod, and                discarding the supernatant after cooling, adding                5ml 0.2 mole/L hydrochloric acid solution, stirring                with a glass rod, let stand, settling, filtering                the supernatant, adding 5ml 0.2 mole/L hydrochloric                acid solution, continue stirring, let standing,                discarding the supernatant, repeating 5 times until                no carbon dioxide is generated, rinsing the sample                with 0.05mol/L hydrochloric acid solution on the                filter paper. The appropriate amount of filter                residue is taken in an oven at 105&#8451;                &#177; 2&#8451;, dried, cooled and weighed,                flushed the above filter residue with water into                500ml conical flask, added 5ml 0.5 mole/L sodium                hydroxide solution, added water to 125ml, covered                with a small bucket, heated, cooled after slight                boiling, and determined the pore size of the                solution as needed after sieving, placed the filter                residue on the sieve into an oven at                105&#8451;&#177;2&#8451; for 6 hours,                and weighed after cooling and analyzed the sieved                solution with a particle image analyzer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Conditions: The method includes                  stirring until the earth color becomes light. The                  supernatant is then cooled and discarded. The                  supernatant is filtered under vacuum. The                  solution is filtered and rinsed 4 times, and then                  rinsed 3 times with water. The drying time is 8                  hours. The weighing is accurate to 0.001 g, and                  the hydrochloric acid washing vector is                  calculated after weighing. The duration of the                  slight boiling is between 0.5-1 hour. The                  weighing is accurate to 0.001g.

Interferon-&#947; and autologous plasma is                added.
   USE - Method for amplifying natural killer (NK) cell                by using K562 cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables using a specific                recombinant lentivirus to infect K562 cells, and                acts as a trophoblast to amplify NK cells, which                achieves explosive proliferation of NK cells in a                short period of time. AE HEILONGJIANG JIANLONG IRON & STEEL CO LT (BJJL-C)

   USE - Method for gas-based shaft furnace and coke                oven coupling production energy gradient                utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The system avoids the coke oven gas heating                carbon deposition problem, and avoids waste gas                discharge in the whole process of preparing                reducing gas. The system increases the mixing                amount of the furnace top purifying gas, and                increases the effective component of the mixed                reducing gas, thus increasing the production                efficiency of the gas-based shaft furnace.

PN WO2022050901-A1
TI Producing purified silicon oxide by providing aqueous solution containing sodium carbonate and mixing with oxide or hydroxide of alkaline earth metal, adding acid-pretreated crude silicon oxide and filtering.
AU YEO T Y
   BU J
   RAYSON M
AE AGENCY SCI TECHNOLOGY & RES (SGST-C)
   ORICA INT PTE LTD (ORCA-C)
GA 202234647Q
AB    NOVELTY - A purified silicon oxide (SiO2) is produced by (a) providing an aqueous solution containing sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) and mixing it with an oxide or hydroxide of an alkaline earth metal to produce an aqueous solution containing sodium hydroxide (NaOH) and a precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal; (b) adding acid-pretreated crude silicon oxide (SiO2), containing greater than or equal to 50 wt.% SiO2, to the aqueous solution of step (a) and scavenging NaOH from the aqueous solution to form an aqueous sodium silicate (Na2SiO3) solution at less than or equal to 100 degrees C; (c) filtering the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution to remove the precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal; (d) partitioning the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution into greater than or equal to 2 portions, where the mixture comprising a first portion of the purified SiO2 alongside sodium hydrogen carbonate (NaHCO3) solution is produced by adding gaseous carbon dioxide (CO2) to a first portion of the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution; and (e) filtering the mixture.
   USE - Production of purified silicon oxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a combination of two initial chemical reactions that synergistically support each other to shift the reaction equilibrium towards the products, and/or proceed at comparably mild conditions that allow for easy onward processing of the reaction products. The silicon oxide dissolution kinetics are increased via the increased alkalinity in the aqueous solution environment resulting from the continuous regeneration of sodium hydroxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A purified silicon oxide (SiO2) is produced by (a) providing an aqueous solution containing Na2CO3 and mixing it with an oxide or hydroxide of an alkaline earth metal to produce an aqueous solution containing NaOH and a precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal; (b) adding acid-pretreated crude silicon oxide (SiO2), containing greater than or equal to 50 wt.% SiO2, to the aqueous solution of step (a) and scavenging NaOH from the aqueous solution to form an aqueous sodium silicate (Na2SiO3) solution at less than or equal to 100 degrees C; (c) filtering the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution to remove the precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal; (d) partitioning the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution into greater than or equal to 2 portions, where the mixture comprising a first portion of the purified SiO2 alongside sodium hydrogen carbonate (NaHCO3) solution is produced by adding gaseous carbon dioxide (CO2) to a first portion of the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution; and (e) filtering the mixture to separate and obtain purified SiO2 and NaHCO3 solution, where the NaHCO3 solution is added to a further portion of the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution to precipitate an additional portion of the purified SiO2. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a purified SiO2 having a purity of greater than or equal to 98.5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Na2CO3 is present in the aqueous solution in a concentration of greater than or equal to 10 wt.%. The oxide or hydroxide of an alkaline earth metal is an oxide or hydroxide of calcium or magnesium. The oxide or hydroxide of calcium or magnesium is an oxide or hydroxide of calcium. The acid-pretreated crude SiO2 contains greater than or equal to 60 wt.% SiO2. The concentration of NaOH in the aqueous solution of step (b) is kept less than or equal to 5 wt.%. Preferred Process: The step (a) comprises (a-i) filtering the aqueous solution obtained from step (a) to remove the precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal before addition of the acid-pretreated crude SiO2. Step (b) is carried out at less than or equal to 90 degrees C. Step (b) is carried out at a pressure of 1-5 bar. Step (c) is carried out at a lower temperature than the temperature in step (b). The precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal, which is removed in step (c) or optionally in step (a-i), is decomposed into an oxide or hydroxide of an alkaline earth metal and recycled in step (a). Step (e) comprises (e-i) precipitating the additional portion of the purified SiO2 to further produce Na2CO3. The Na2CO3 of step (e-i) is recycled in step (a). The Na2CO3 of step (e-i) is present in an aqueous solution for recycling in step (a). The method further comprises (f) decomposing the precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal into an oxide or hydroxide of the alkaline earth metal and purified CO2. The reaction in step (f) involves purifying the precipitated carbonate of an alkaline earth metal by reacting it with diluted CO2 to give an aqueous solution of a bicarbonate of an alkaline earth metal and decomposing the bicarbonate of an alkaline earth metal to give a purified carbonate of an alkaline earth metal. Preferred Parameters: The pH of the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution prior to the addition of gaseous CO2 is 9-14. The addition of gaseous CO2 in step (d) lowers the pH of the aqueous Na2SiO3 solution to 6-10. The purified SiO2 has surface area of greater than or equal to 500 m2/g.

PN CN113880498-A
TI Preparation of magnesium slag building product for, e.g. wall material, involves using magnesium slag and chitosan, sieving, collecting undersized material, mixing undersized material with water, hydrating to obtain mixture, curing and demolding to obtain test piece, and carbonizing test piece.
AU LI Y
   REN P
   SUN Y
   CAO J
   ZHU M
AE SHAANXI CONSTR SCI RES INST CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2022116512
AB    NOVELTY - A liquid carbonization preparation of high-strength magnesium slag building products involves (i) placing magnesium slag, chitosan and standard sand in a mill for grinding, sieving, and collecting undersized material, (ii) mixing the undersized material with water at 22-28degrees Celsius, and adding to a mixer for stirring to carry out hydration reaction to obtain a mixture, (iii) placing the mixture in a mold and vibrating evenly, curing in a standard curing box, and demolding to obtain a test piece, and (iv) placing the test piece in a carbonization box, carrying out medium-high temperature carbonization curing under the condition that the volume content of carbon dioxide is more than 20%, taking out and drying at room temperature. The magnesium slag building product has compressive strength of 60-70 MPa and flexural strength of more than 20 MPa.
   USE - Liquid carbonizing preparation method of high strength magnesium slag building product used for preparing wall material or producing building cement, building brick and industrial product powder for waste stacking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses chitosan as carbonizing agent and water to realize the liquid, without the raw material silicon heat method magnesium-magnesium slag for air cooling and watering pre-treatment, after carbonization, thus reducing the process steps, saving the preparation cost, improving the stability and mechanical property of the magnesium slag building product, reducing the energy consumption and realizing the higher strength performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A liquid carbonization preparation of high-strength magnesium slag building products involves (i) placing magnesium slag, chitosan and standard sand in a mill for grinding, sieving, and collecting undersized material, (ii) mixing the undersized material with water at 22-28degrees Celsius, and adding to a mixer for stirring to carry out hydration reaction to obtain a mixture, (iii) placing the mixture in a mold and vibrating evenly, curing in a standard curing box, and demolding to obtain a test piece, and (iv) placing the test piece in a carbonization box, carrying out medium-high temperature carbonization curing under the condition that the volume content of carbon dioxide is more than 20%, taking out and drying at room temperature. The magnesium slag building product has compressive strength of 60-70 MPa and flexural strength of more than 20 MPa. The mass of the chitosan is 0.4-0.6% of the mass of the magnesium slag smelting by the silicon thermal method, and the mass of the standard sand is 3-4 times the mass of the magnesium slag smelting by the silicon thermal method. The mass of water is 40-60% of the mass of the magnesium-magnesium slag by the silicon thermal method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The chitosan has molecular weight of 1000.

PN CN113755532-A
TI Co-producing biogas-oil-charcoal by anaerobic fermentation of kitchen waste coupled with straw pyrolysis comprises e.g. crushing kitchen waste, then passing into anaerobic fermentation reactor for anaerobic fermentation reaction, and solid-liquid separating.
AU YANG T
   LI R
   XING W
   LIU P
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV SHENYANG AEROSPACE (UYAE-C)
GA 202203025H
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing biogas-oil-charcoal by anaerobic fermentation of kitchen waste coupled with straw pyrolysis comprises e.g. crushing kitchen waste, and then passing to the anaerobic fermentation reactor for anaerobic fermentation reaction to generate biogas, biogas residue and biogas slurry, discharging the biogas residue and biogas slurry from the anaerobic fermentation reactor, and solid-liquid separating, mixing the biogas slurry with the crushed kitchen waste and entering into a anaerobic fermentation reactor together, mixing the biogas residue and straw, pulverizing, drying to remove moisture, and then placing into a pyrolysis reactor to generate syngas, pyrolysis liquid and biochar through pyrolysis reaction, passing the synthesis gas into the anaerobic fermentation reactor, discharging pyrolysis liquid and biochar from pyrolysis reactor, solid-liquid separating, adding biochar into anaerobic fermentation reactor, and separating and collecting bio-oil.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing biogas-oil-charcoal by anaerobic fermentation of kitchen waste coupled with straw pyrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has functions of anaerobic fermentation of organic matter, in-situ purification of biogas, and anaerobic biomethanation of synthetic gas; improves the yield and concentration of biogas; obtains high value-added gas oil and carbon products; and achieves zero waste discharge in the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing biogas-oil-charcoal by anaerobic fermentation of kitchen waste coupled with straw pyrolysis comprises (i) crushing kitchen waste, and then passing to the anaerobic fermentation reactor for anaerobic fermentation reaction to generate biogas, biogas residue and biogas slurry, (ii) discharging the biogas residue and biogas slurry from the anaerobic fermentation reactor, and solid-liquid separating, mixing the biogas slurry with the crushed kitchen waste and entering into a anaerobic fermentation reactor together, (iii) mixing the biogas residue and straw, pulverizing, drying to remove moisture, and then placing into a pyrolysis reactor to generate syngas, pyrolysis liquid and biochar through pyrolysis reaction, (iv) passing the synthesis gas into the anaerobic fermentation reactor, generating hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the synthesis gas to methane under the action of methanogens, excess hydrogen in the synthesis gas converts the carbon dioxide in the biogas into methane under the action of the hydrogenophilic methanogen; and discharging the high methane biogas formed in the anaerobic fermentation reactor is and collecting, (v) discharging the pyrolysis liquid and biochar from the pyrolysis reactor, and solid-liquid separating, adding the biochar into the anaerobic fermentation reactor as additive, and (vi) separating the pyrolysis liquid from oil and water to separate the water-phase small molecule solution and the oil-phase bio-oil, adding the small-molecule solution into the anaerobic fermentation reactor as fermentation substrate, and collecting the bio-oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the temperature of the anaerobic fermentation reaction is 30-40degrees Celsius, and the time is 25-40 days. In step (iii), the temperature of the pyrolysis reaction is 600-800degrees Celsius, and the time is 1-2 hours.Preferred Components: In step (i), the solid mass content of the kitchen waste is 20-40%. In step (ii), the mixing mass ratio between biogas slurry and pulverized kitchen waste is 1:1-3. In step (iii), the mixing mass ratio between biogas residue and straw is 1:1-3. In step (iv), the main components of the synthesis gas are hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and methane. In step (iv), the reaction formula of hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to generate methane is given as specification; and in the above-mentioned step (iv), the reaction formula of carbon dioxide into methane is given as specification. In step (v), the addition amount of biochar is 15-20 g per liter of kitchen waste in the anaerobic fermentation reactor, and the rest of the biochar is collected. In step (vi), the addition amount of the small molecule solution is 1-2 g per liter of kitchen waste in the anaerobic fermentation reactor.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN BR102020021577-A2
TI Recovering corn starch from Araraquara sewage treatment station waste for obtaining binder used in adhesive manufacturing industries, involves collecting homogeneous mixture liquid that comes from disposal process and adding into mixer.
AU DA SILVA M N
   TEIXEIRA DA SILVA G
AE DA SILVA M N (DSIL-Individual)
   TEIXEIRA DA SILVA G (SILV-Individual)
GA 2021482416
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering corn starch from Araraquara sewage treatment station (ETE) waste involves collecting homogeneous mixture liquid that comes from the disposal process and adding into a mixer with 3-bladed, low-speed propeller, where mixer rotates at a speed of 55-65 revolutions/minute (rpm), then adding 1.0-2.0 wt.% calcium hydroxide for each 1m3 of the disposal, leaving in mixture for a period between 10-20 minutes. The mixture is injected with 0.5 kg and 2.0 kg liquid carbon dioxide (CO2), where it undergoes a process of particle size increase, and form precipitated calcium carbonate, where the solids have approximately 2 microns on average and the starch particles and starch agglomerate with carbonate forming heavy flakes, and it get dense, when it reaches pH 8.0.
   USE - Method for recovering corn starch from Araraquara sewage treatment station waste for obtaining binder used in adhesive manufacturing industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in a simple and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering corn starch from Araraquara sewage treatment station (ETE) involves collecting homogeneous mixture liquid that comes from the disposal process and adding into a mixer with 3-bladed, low-speed propeller, where mixer rotates at a speed of 55-65 revolutions/minute (rpm), then adding 1.0-2.0 wt.% calcium hydroxide for each 1m3 of the disposal, leaving in mixture for a period between 10-20 minutes. The mixture is injected with 0.5 kg and 2.0 kg liquid carbon dioxide (CO2), where it undergoes a process of particle size increase, and form precipitated calcium carbonate, where the solids have approximately 2 microns on average and the starch particles and starch agglomerate with carbonate forming heavy flakes, and it get dense, when it reaches pH 8.0. The sedimentation and dewatering are carried out after all the materials are sedimented by a clarifier as sedimentation cyclone, where the force carried out inside the cyclone that receives the flocculated material generates an ultra fast sedimentation and the supernatant is a pure and pure water without sediment to get product in the form of mud. The product in the form of mud is collected after sedimentation at the bottom of the cyclone and at that moment it is sent to the centrifuge, where the solid particles are separated, abd obtaining mud with a high solids content between 50-55 wt.%. The disposal liquid is a clean water with low polishing need for reuse. The process subsequently generates the sludge retained in the centrifuge in raw material, transported by a conveyor belt directly to big storage bags, and then obtaining the starch residue, which becomes a binder for adhesives.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Obtaining biodiesel using waste cooking oil having a blend nano additives, involves heating waste cooking oil having predetermined proportion of free fatty acids and adding sulfuric acid and methyl alcohol in predetermined proportion.
AU PADURANG B V
   PATIL D P
   GAIKWAD P U
   TUKARAM B G
   JANDEO K D
   SENTHILKUMAR G
   SHAH Y
   BOBADE S
   BIRAJDAR S S
   NISHCHAL G U
AE PADURANG B V (PADU-Individual)
   PATIL D P (PATI-Individual)
   GAIKWAD P U (GAIK-Individual)
   TUKARAM B G (TUKA-Individual)
   JANDEO K D (JAND-Individual)
   SENTHILKUMAR G (SENT-Individual)
   SHAH Y (SHAH-Individual)
   BOBADE S (BOBA-Individual)
   BIRAJDAR S S (BIRA-Individual)
   NISHCHAL G U (NISH-Individual)
GA 2021353647
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining biodiesel using waste cooking oil having a blend nano additives, involves heating the waste cooking oil having predetermined proportion of free fatty acids at a temperature of 55-57 degrees C, and adding sulfuric acid and methyl alcohol in a predetermined proportion to oil under stirring. Methyl alcohol is added to the mixture under continuous stirring at a speed of 650 revolutions/minute (rpm) at a controlled temperature of 55-57 degrees C for a predetermined time period of 90 minutes. The fatty ester is separated by natural cooling and then mixture is subjected to transesterification by adding methoxide to the fatty ester. The mixture is heated at a controlled temperature of 65 degrees Cor a period of 2 hours, cooled for 8 hours for facilitating formation of glycerin at the bottom of vessel and separating by a separating funnel and collecting biodiesel containing impurities. The impurities are separated from biodiesel by water wash and air bubbles wash and obtaining pure biodiesel.
   USE - Method for obtaining biodiesel using waste cooking oil having a blend nano additives.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows to reduce emission of NOx up to 6 vol.%, where NOx emission B30 is an effective blend, emission of carbon dioxide from 18.62 vol.% to 14.21 vol.% for blend B30+25PPM AL2O3+ 25PPM CNT, carbon dioxide emission or blend of B20+50PPM CNT, and consumption of hydrocarbon (HC) by 26.93%, where B30 is an effective blend.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Obtaining biodiesel using waste cooking oil having a blend nano additives, involves heating the waste cooking oil having predetermined proportion of free fatty acids at a temperature of 55-57 degrees C, and adding sulfuric acid and methyl alcohol in a predetermined proportion to oil under stirring. Methyl alcohol is added to the mixture under continuous stirring at a speed of 650 revolutions/minute (rpm) at a controlled temperature of 55-57 degrees C for a predetermined time period of 90 minutes. The fatty ester is separated by natural cooling and then mixture is subjected to transesterification by adding methoxide to the fatty ester. The mixture is heated at a controlled temperature of 65 degrees Cor a period of 2 hours, cooled for 8 hours for facilitating formation of glycerin at the bottom of vessel and separating by a separating funnel and collecting biodiesel containing impurities. The impurities are separated from biodiesel by water wash and air bubbles wash and obtaining pure biodiesel. The biodiesel formed is mixed with a predetermined proportion of pure diesel and nano additives to form a blend of biodiesel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano additives are selected from Carbon Nano Tubes (CNT) or aluminum oxide (Al2O3). Preferred conditions: The predetermined proportion of biodiesel, diesel and nano additives are mixed in a way such that biodiesel is in a range of 15-30 vol.% The predetermined proportion of biodiesel, diesel and nano additives are mixed in a way such that the nano additives are in a range of 25 ppm to 60 ppm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112022926-A
TI Jingkang capsule comprises Rehmannia glutinosa, Polygonum multiflorum, Eucommia ulmoides, Lycopersicon esculentum, Drynaria, Uncaria, Pueraria lobata, Panax notoginseng and radix Scutellariae (stir-fried).
AU LI Y
   YANG D
   DU S
   BAI Z
   HU Y
   ZHOU X
AE DISHA PHARM IND GROUP CORP LTD (DISH-Non-standard)
   DIJIA PHARM GROUP CO LTD (DIJI-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4329N
AB    NOVELTY - Jingkang capsule comprises 204.5 pts. wt. Rehmannia glutinosa, 272.8 pts. wt. Polygonum multiflorum, 136.4 pts. wt. Eucommia ulmoides, 136.4 pts. wt. Lycopersicon esculentum, 136.4 pts. wt. Drynaria (hot), 100 pts. wt. Uncaria, 100 pts. wt. Pueraria lobata, 50 pts. wt. Panax notoginseng, 86.2 pts. wt. Radix Scutellariae (stir-fried). The method for preparing a Jingkang capsule includes crushing Pueraria lobata and Panax notoginseng until they all pass through an 80-mesh sieve and placing them in a supercritical extraction kettle. The supercritical extraction is carried out to obtain supercritical extracts and drug residues. The supercritical extraction is supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction.
   USE - Jingkang capsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The Jingkang capsule improves the extraction rate of effective components, effectively use the effective ingredients of Chinese herbal medicine, and improves product quality and treatment effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Jingkang capsule comprises 204.5 pts. wt. Rehmannia glutinosa, 272.8 pts. wt. Polygonum multiflorum, 136.4 pts. wt. Eucommia ulmoides, 136.4 pts. wt. Lycopersicon esculentum, 136.4 pts. wt. Drynaria (hot), 100 pts. wt. Uncaria, 100 pts. wt. Pueraria lobata, 50 pts. wt. Panax notoginseng, 86.2 pts. wt. Radix Scutellariae (stir-fried). The method for preparing a Jingkang capsule includes crushing Pueraria lobata and Panax notoginseng until they all pass through an 80-mesh sieve and placing them in a supercritical extraction kettle. The supercritical extraction is carried out to obtain supercritical extracts and drug residues. The supercritical extraction is supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction. The entrainer is ethanol-acetic acid aqueous solution, where the content of acetic acid in the acetic acid aqueous solution is 5.5-7.0%, the extraction temperature is 52 degrees C, and the extraction pressure is 38 megapascal (MPa). The Rehmannia glutinosa, Polygonum multiflorum, Eucommia ulmoides, Lycopersicon esculentum, Drynaria (hot), Uncaria, Radix Scutellariae (stir-fried) are combined with the drug residue after supercritical extraction, and placed it in the extraction tank. The 10 times the amount of water added to the mass-to-volume ratio of the feed, extracted at normal pressure, and extracted for 2 hours, and filtered the water extract. The filtrate is concentrated to a relative density of 1.30-1.35, the vacuum concentration pressure is 0.07 megapascal (MPa), and the concentration temperature is 80 degrees C. The thick water extract paste are vacuum dried, crushed and sieved to obtain fine powder of dry extract. The supercritical extract and the fine powder of dry extract are mixed uniformly by an equal increment method, then added 10% corn starch, mixed well, and granulated in a dry granulating machine. The obtained granules are sieved and sized, adding an appropriate amount of magnesium stearate, mixing them, and filling them into a capsule filling machine to obtain capsules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The feed ratio of the entrainer ethanol to the aqueous acetic acid solution is 60:40 by volume. The content of acetic acid in the acetic acid aqueous solution in the entrainer is 6.25%. The amount of entrainer is 4-6 vol.% of the sum of Pueraria and Panax notoginseng. The amount of entrainer is 5% by volume of the sum of Pueraria and Panax notoginseng.

PN CN111809790-A
TI TRC recycled aggregate concrete block comprises            block body is provided with weight reduction groove            inside and weight reduction groove is arranged in            rectangular structure, and weight reduction trough is            provided with strengthening plate.
AU LIANG G
   YOU C
   YAN X
   ZHANG T
   LI X
   ZHANG R
   LIN Z
AE CHUZHOU VOCATIONAL & TECH COLLEGE (CZVT-C)
GA 2020A57232
AB    NOVELTY - Totally recycled concrete (TRC) recycled                aggregate concrete block comprises block body (1)                is provided with a weight reduction groove (4)                inside and the weight reduction groove is arranged                in a rectangular structure. The weight reduction                trough is provided with a strengthening plate (6).                The two sets of the strengthening plate are                arranged symmetrically along the center line. The                regular hexagonal supporting structure is formed                between the two groups of reinforcing plates. The                regular hexagonal ventilation hole (5) is formed in                the middle. The silencing grooves (3) are provided                on both sides of the weight reduction groove. The                silencer grooves are arranged in a long strip                structure. The woven nets (2) are provided on both                sides of the muffler groove.
   USE - TRC recycled aggregate concrete block.
   ADVANTAGE - The TRC recycled aggregate concrete block is                used to reduces the weight of the block body while                ensuring the structural strength of the block body,                and facilitates lightweight assembly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for processing TRC recycled aggregate concrete                blocks, which involves:
   (A) using waste concrete, waste bricks, and                sand ash waste generated by construction on the                construction site as raw materials;
   (B) collecting and transporting to the                crushing device for crushing, sieving the crushed                raw materials, and removing metal objects, wood                blocks and other impurities in the raw materials                during the screening process to obtain preliminary                recycled aggregates for use;
   (C) putting the recycled aggregate in a closed                container with a relative humidity of 40-80 wt.%                and a carbon dioxide volume concentration of 10-90                wt.% for carbon strengthening treatment, until the                treatment percentage of the recycled aggregate is                70-90%;
   (D) adding the recycled aggregate to cement,                lime, anhydrite, aluminum powder, and foaming agent                in proportion to the mixing device for mixing at                30-50 degrees C for 10-20 minutes to obtain a mixed                slurry;
   (E) pouring the mixed slurry obtained in step                3 into the forming mold of the block, and add the                woven net made by using the reinforcing fiber and                the flat weave method to it during the pouring                process; and
   (F) transporting the initial embryo of the                concrete block to the steam curing equipment, steam                curing in a closed container with a relative                humidity of 40-80% for 2-3 hours, and after the                steam curing is completed, putting it into a closed                container with a carbon dioxide volume                concentration of 10-90% for strengthening treatment                to obtain low shrinkage TRC recycled aggregate                concrete block.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of TRC                recycled aggregate concrete block.
   Block body (1)
   Woven nets (2)
   Silencing grooves (3)
   Weight reduction groove (4)
   Regular hexagonal ventilation hole (5)
   Strengthening plate (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The foaming agent is                  sodiumdodecyl sulfate or sodium alkyl benzene                  sulfonate The foaming agent is sodiumdodecyl                  sulfate or sodium alkyl benzene sulfonate.                  Preferred Compositions: The weight of each                  component of the slurry raw material includes 50                  wt.% recycled aggregate, 20 wt.% cement, 10 wt.%                  lime, 10 wt.% anhydrite, 8 wt.% aluminum powder                  and 2 wt.% foaming agent.

PN CN111621473-A
TI Preparing novel human adipose stem cell composition, by collecting human adipose tissue, placing fat in mixed collagenase, digesting, obtaining adipose stem cells for cell count and detection, and performing cryopreservation.
AU HAN Y
   TANG J
   ZHU X
AE CHENGXI SHANGHAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202090181K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing novel human adipose stem cell composition, involves collecting human adipose tissue, performing physical examination of the donor under state of local anesthesia, using swelling method liposuction to obtain fat, placing the fat in mixed collagenase, digesting with uniform shaking at 37 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, adding to 0.25% trypsin-EDTA digestion solution, shaking and digesting, adding trypsin inhibitor, mixing to terminate digestion, after centrifugation at 900 g for 10 minutes, aspirating the upper layer mixture, washing bottom cells with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), filtering, using trypsin-EDTA digestion solution for digestion, passaging to obtain adipose stem cells, obtaining adipose stem cells for cell count and detection, determining whether meet the eligibility criteria, after passing the test, adding the adipose stem cells to cell cryopreservation solution, resuspending, and adding the cells cell cryopreservation tubes for cryopreservation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing novel human adipose stem cell composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high fat stem cell survival rate, and uniform cell morphology size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing novel human adipose stem cell composition, involves collecting human adipose tissue, performing physical examination of the donor under state of local anesthesia, using swelling method liposuction to obtain fat, placing the fat in 0.2-0.4% mixed collagenase, digesting with uniform shaking at 37 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, adding to 0.25% trypsin-EDTA digestion solution, shaking and digesting at 37 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, adding trypsin inhibitor, mixing to terminate digestion, after centrifugation at 900 g for 10 minutes, aspirating the upper layer mixture, washing bottom cells with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), filtering through 100 meshes sieve, centrifuging the filtrate at 210 g for 10 minutes, aspirating the supernatant to obtain original substituted adipose stem cells, adding primary adipose-derived stem cells to a serum-free medium, resuspending, after cell counting, seeding at a density of more than 3x 104/cm2, coating with laminin-521 solution in cell culture flask, culturing in 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 2 days, discarding the culture medium and replacing with fresh serum-free medium, culturing the cells, maintaining culture bottle at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, replacing fresh serum-free medium for every two days, when the adipose stem cell growth and fusion reaches more than 80%, using trypsin-EDTA digestion solution for digestion, passaging to obtain adipose stem cells, obtaining adipose stem cells for cell count and detection, determining whether meet the eligibility criteria, after passing the test, adding the adipose stem cells to cell cryopreservation solution, resuspending, and adding the cells cell cryopreservation tubes for cryopreservation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves obtaining fat by tumescent liposuction, where the maximum negative pressure is 430 mmHg, and passing fat through 300-800 mu m filter holes filter out grease and water to obtain light yellow fat. The method further involves preprocessing the collected fat, washing the fat with PBS solution for several times until the washing solution is clear and bright. The serum-free medium comprises alpha -minimum essential medium (MEM), 20% human platelet lysate, 105 U/l leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF), 5-10 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), 5-10 ng/ml transforming growth factor beta (TGF beta ) and 500-1000 IU/l heparin sodium. The cell cryopreservation solution comprises 10% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), 40% HPL serum substitute and alpha -DMEM. The method involves storing the cell cryopreservation tube at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes, storing at -20 degrees C for 2 hours, after storing at -80 degrees C for more than one week, and transferring to a liquid nitrogen tank for long-term storage.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F02B; B04-L05C; B10-A10; B11-A04A; B11-B05; B14-C08; D05-A02C; D05-H08B
