PT P
PN CN111574219-A; CN111574219-B
TI Preparing a photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block involves recycling positive electrode materials for lithium ferrite batteries by crushing and sieving the positive pole pieces of waste lithium ferrite batteries.
AU XUE Q
   WU X
AE HENAN ZHONGSHUN FILTRATION RES INST CO (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202084784H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block involves recycling positive electrode materials for lithium ferrite batteries by crushing and sieving the positive pole pieces of waste lithium ferrite batteries to make the particle size no greater than 70 meshes. The resulting powder is roasted at 400-600 degrees C for no less than 1 hour to obtain ferric acid lithium cathode powder. The obtained lithium ferrite positive electrode powder is granulated and then placed into a mold to be pressed into a block, and the block is sintered at 900-1200 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain a lithium ferrite block. The obtained lithium ferrite block is placed in a chemical vapor deposition device with a titanium oxide coating, and heated to 550-800 degrees C. The titanium tetrachloride is vaporized at 55 degrees C and mixed with hydrogen and carbon dioxide by molar ratio TCl4:H2:CO2=1:4:2.
   USE - Method for preparing a photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block, where reaction is more thorough and energy-saving and has a loose and porous internal microstructure and high photocatalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block involves recycling positive electrode materials for lithium ferrite batteries by crushing and sieving the positive pole pieces of waste lithium ferrite batteries to make the particle size no greater than 70 meshes. The resulting powder is roasted at 400-600 degrees C for no less than 1 hour to obtain ferric acid lithium cathode powder. The obtained lithium ferrite positive electrode powder is granulated and then placed into a mold to be pressed into a block, and the block is sintered at 900-1200 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain a lithium ferrite block. The obtained lithium ferrite block is placed in a chemical vapor deposition device with a titanium oxide coating, and heated to 550-800 degrees C. The titanium tetrachloride is vaporized at 55 degrees C and mixed with hydrogen and carbon dioxide by molar ratio TCl4:H2:CO2=1:4:2. The argon gas is used as a carrier gas, the mixed gas is passed into the reaction chamber at 900-1200 cm3/min, and reacted for 12-25 minutes. The surface of the lithium ferrite block is coated with a titanium oxide layer. After heat treatment, the obtained lithium ferrite block with the titanium oxide coating is kept at 350-400 degrees C for 2-3 hours in an air atmosphere to obtain a photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Condition: The lithium ferrite positive electrode powder after baking needs to be cleaned with methanol or ethanol. The binder used for granulation is a polyvinyl alcohol resin solution with a mass percentage of 4%. The particle size of the granulation is 0.25 mm-0.60 mm. The calcination temperature is 600 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1 hour. The sintering temperature is 950 degrees C, and the sintering time is 4 hours. The photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block is a flaky block. The photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block is a sheet with a diameter of 4mm-10mm and a thickness of 0.5mm-2mm. The photocatalytic lithium ferrite-titanium oxide composite block is a thin sheet with a diameter of 4 mm and a thickness of 0.5 mm.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC J04-E04C; J04-E11; L02-A01; L02-A02B; L02-A03; L02-A04; L02-A13; L02-G01E; L02-G01M; L02-G07A; L02-G12A; L02-G12M; L02-J02C; L03-E01B8A; L03-E06; L03-E08B; A10-E05B; A10-E09B1; A12-E06A; X16-B01F1; X16-E01C1; X16-E01G; X16-M

PN CN111521552-A; CN111521552-B
TI Method for testing aging of intumescent steel            structure fireproof coating in industrial environment,            involves sandblasting steel substrate, spraying primer,            applying fireproof coating, aging, and performing e.g.            infrared spectroscopy.
AU DING Q
   DU Y
   NIE X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202078610H
AB    NOVELTY - A surface of a steel plate substrate is                sandblasted, to remove oil and floating dust on the                substrate surface. Primer is sprayed on the                substrate, a fireproof coating is coated, and                cured. The obtained test sample is placed in an                aging test box filled with 1% sulfur dioxide and                carbon dioxide, aged, the aged test sample is                cured, and a small amount of sample from the aged                and unaging coating are scraped, respectively.                Scanning electron microscopy, thermogravimetric                analysis and infrared spectroscopy are performed on                the coated sample, and mechanism changes before and                after the coating aging is compared. The test                sample is placed in a fire test furnace, a standard                fire resistance test is performed, the relationship                between the temperature of the back fire surface of                the test sample and the heating time is tested, and                the attenuation of heat-insulation efficiency of                the coating is calculated.
   USE - Method for testing aging of intumescent steel                structure fireproof coating in industrial                environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves safety performance of                steel structure buildings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A surface of a steel plate substrate is                sandblasted, to remove oil and floating dust on the                substrate surface. Primer is sprayed on the                substrate, a fireproof coating is coated, the                coating thickness is controlled to the design                thickness, and cured at 23 plus minus 3 degrees C                and relative humidity of 50 plus minus 5% for 17                days. The obtained test sample is placed in an                aging test box filled with 1% sulfur dioxide and                carbon dioxide, aged at 35 degrees C and relative                humidity of 95% for 14 days, the aged test sample                is cured at 23 plus minus 3 degrees C and relative                humidity of 50 plus minus 5% for 17 days, and a                small amount of sample from the aged and unaging                coating are scraped, respectively. Scanning                electron microscopy, thermogravimetric analysis and                infrared spectroscopy are performed on the coated                sample, and mechanism changes before and after the                coating aging is compared. The test sample is                placed in a fire test furnace, a standard fire                resistance test is performed, the relationship                between the temperature of the back fire surface of                the test sample and the heating time is tested, and                the attenuation of heat-insulation efficiency (in                %) of the coating is calculated based on the                formula: ((T0-T)/T0)asterisk100%, where T0 is                reference fire resistance limit, in minutes, T is                fire resistance limit of the sample after aging, in                minutes. The changes in the mechanism before and                after aging of the coating are comprehensively                referred. The attenuation of the thermal insulation                efficiency is 50% or less. The optimal intumescent                steel structure fireproof coating is                selected.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The coating sample is                  sprayed with gold, before scanning electron                  microscope detection. The sample and potassium                  bromide crystal are mixed and ground in a mass                  ratio of 1:100, and then pressed to a transparent                  sheet, before infrared spectrum detection.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC S03-E01B3; S03-E04A; S03-E06B1

PN US2020115309-A1; WO2020076793-A1; CN112912364-A; EP3860970-A1; US11174213-B2
TI Forming alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid, comprises contacting transition metal precursor compound having e.g. first ligand and metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide and subjecting the reaction mixture to reaction conditions.
AU IACONO P
   HLAVINKA M L
   HLAVINKA M
   M HLAVINKA
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 202029952Y
AB    NOVELTY - Forming (P1) an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or a salt of it, comprises: (a) contacting in any order (i) a transition metal precursor compound comprising at least one first ligand, (ii) optionally, at least one second ligand, (iii) an olefin, (iv) carbon dioxide, (v) a diluent, and (vi) a metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide to provide a reaction mixture, where the chemically-modified solid oxide comprises a sulfur oxoacid anion-modified solid oxide, a phosphorus oxoacid anion-modified solid oxide, or a halide ion-modified solid oxide; and (b) subjecting the reaction mixture to reaction conditions suitable to form the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, where the transition metal precursor compound is present in the reaction mixture at a concentration of less than 0.2 mM.
   USE - The process is useful for forming alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides improved forming and regenerating metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide; provides effective to provide higher yields of alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or with decreasing concentrations of transition metal precursor compound; and is heterogeneous system, thus separating ease of product from catalytic system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) forming (P2) the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, comprising (a1) contacting (ia) a metalalactone compound, (iia) a diluent and (iiia) metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide to provide a reaction mixture, where the chemically-modified solid oxide comprises a sulfur oxoacid anion-modified solid oxide, a phosphorus oxoacid anion-modified solid oxide, or a halide ion-modified solid oxide and (b1) subjecting the reaction mixture to reaction conditions suitable to form the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, where the metalalactone is present in the reaction mixture at a concentration of less than 0.2 mM; and
   (2) forming (P3) the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, comprising (a2) contacting in any order (ib1) group 8-11 transition metal catalyst, (iib1) an olefin, (iiib1) carbon dioxide, (ivb1) diluent, and (vb1) sulfur oxoacid anion-modified solid oxide, phosphorus oxoacid anion-modified solid oxide, or halide ion-modified solid oxide that has been treated with sodium base, or sodium salt and an acid, to provide a reaction mixture, and (b2) contacting the reaction mixture with alkali metal-containing base selected from an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal oxide, hydroxide, alkoxide, aryloxide, amide, alkyl amide, arylamide, carbonate or alkali metal salt and an acid selected from sulfuric acid, fluorosulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, fluorophosphoric acid, triflic (trifluoromethanesulfonic) acid, 1-10C alkyl sulfonic acid, or a 6-14C aryl sulfonic acid to produce an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises: a step of isolating the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid; and (c) regenerating the metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide, comprising contacting the metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide remaining following step (b) with metal-containing base or metal-containing salt in combination with an acid. The metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide is prepared by contacting a solid oxide with a sulfur oxoacid anion, phosphorus oxoacid anion, or halide ion to provide the chemically-modified solid oxide, followed by contacting the chemically-modified solid oxide with metal-containing base or metal-containing salt in combination with an acid. The diluent comprises carbon dioxide, or where at least a portion of the diluent comprises the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, formed in the process. In the process, the contacting step and/or the subjecting step is/are conducted: at a pressure of 45-1000 psig and at a temperature of 0-95 degrees C; and at a weight hourly space velocity of 0.05-50/hour, based on the amount of the metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide. The olefin is ethylene, and the step of contacting a transition metal precursor compound with the olefin and carbon dioxide is conducted an ethylene pressure of 10-1000 psig, preferably 20-2000 psig. The metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide is arranged as fixed bed, bubbling bed, moving bed, or stirred bed. Preferred Components: The transition metal precursor compound is present in the reaction mixture at a concentration of 0.02-0.001 mM. The solid oxide of the chemically-modified solid oxide comprises silica, alumina, titania, zirconia, magnesia, boria, calcia, zinc oxide, silica-alumina, silica-coated alumina, silica-titania, silica-zirconia, silica-magnesia, alumina-titania, alumina-zirconia, zinc-aluminate, alumina-boria, silica-boria, aluminum phosphate, aluminophosphate, aluminophosphate-silica, magnesium aluminate and/or titania-zirconia. The chemically-modified solid oxide comprises solid oxide that is chemically modified with an electron-withdrawing anion, the electron-withdrawing anion comprising sulfate, bisulfate, fluoro sulfate, phosphate, fluorophosphates, triflate, mesylate, tosylate, thiosulfate, 1-10C alkyl sulfonate, 6-14C aryl sulfonate, fluoride and/or chloride. The metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide comprises metal cations comprising group 1, 2, 12 or 13 metals. The metal-treated chemically-modified solid oxide comprises sodium-treated sulfated alumina, sodium-treated fluorided silica-coated alumina, sodium-treated fluorided silica-alumina, potassium-treated sulfated alumina, potassium-treated fluorided silica-coated alumina, and/or potassium-treated fluorided silica-alumina. The diluent comprises ether diluent, carbonyl-containing diluent, aromatic hydrocarbon diluent, halogenated aromatic hydrocarbon diluent or alcohol diluent. The olefin comprises ethylene, propylene, butene, pentene, hexene, heptane, octene or styrene. The alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid comprises acrylic acid or methacrylic acid salt of it. The transition metal of the transition metal precursor compound comprises chromium, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, molybdenum, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, and tungsten, and silver, iridium, platinum or gold. The transition metal of the transition metal precursor compound is a group 8-11 transition metal. The metalalactone comprises a group 8-11 transition metal.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

ER

PT P
PN CN110923053-A
TI Method for extracting tea oil with high antifreeze performance involves treating Camellia seeds with plant disinfectant crystallizing agent, subjecting to water enzyme treatment using enzymolysis solution, centrifuging, and extracting.
AU LI G
AE GUIZHOU SHIQIAN FODINGSHAN WILD TEA-SEED (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020270832
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting tea oil with high antifreeze performance involves (1) treating the Camellia seeds with plant disinfectant, adding crystallizing agent, taking out the Camellia seeds, draining and activating, (2) subjecting to water enzyme treatment by mixing the activated Camellia seed with enzymolysis solution, (3) maintaining the enzymolyzed Camellia seeds under a pressure of 1-1.8 MPa for 60-90 seconds, obtaining a slurry, (4) feeding the slurry into the pressure tank, using high-speed disperser for shearing, centrifuging to separate the oil layer, emulsified layer and residue, feeding the emulsified layer into the microwave processing equipment, adding modified sodium carboxymethyl cellulose powder, centrifuging, obtaining a Camellia oil by combining oil layers, (5) feeding the slurry to the supercritical carbon dioxide device for extraction, collecting raffinate, and obtaining finished tea oil.
   USE - Method for extracting tea oil with high antifreeze performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides tea oil having high quality, high stability and excellent anti-freezing performance. The prepared tea oil contains richer vitamin E, vitamin B and other nutrients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting tea oil with high antifreeze performance involves (1) treating the Camellia seeds with a plant disinfectant in a mass ratio of 1:3-7 under constant voltage field of 1-3 V for 10-20 seconds, adding crystallizing agent with a plant disinfectant quality of 1-5%, increasing the voltage for 10-20 seconds under the condition of 15-20 V, taking out the Camellia seeds, draining and activating, (2) subjecting to water enzyme treatment by mixing the activated Camellia seed with enzymolysis solution in a mass ratio of 1:1.5-2, placing under the condition of 30-35 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, taking out the enzymolysis Camellia seeds, (3) maintaining the enzymolyzed Camellia seeds under a pressure of 1-1.8 MPa for 60-90 seconds, obtaining a slurry, (4) feeding the slurry into the pressure tank, filling with inert gas after vacuuming, maintaining the pressure at 2-2.5 Pa for 120-240 seconds, using high-speed disperser for shearing, centrifuging to separate the oil layer, emulsified layer and residue, feeding the emulsified layer into the microwave processing equipment, adding modified sodium carboxymethyl cellulose powder with emulsified layer quality of 0.1-0.5% at a microwave frequency of 300-400 MHz and power 300-400 W for 60-120 seconds, placing in a high-speed disperser for shearing and centrifugal separation, obtaining a Camellia oil by combining oil layers, (5) feeding the slurry to the supercritical carbon dioxide device for extraction, collecting raffinate, and obtaining finished tea oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of the plant disinfectant involves taking Chenopodium quinoa husks and grinding, adding in a frying pan with a temperature of 40-50 degrees C and frying for 10-20 minutes, taking the leaves of Artemisia argyi under steam conditions at a temperature of 130-150 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, mixing the Chenopodium quinoa husk powder with the A.argyileaves, adding purified water in an amount of 20-30 times with respect to the mass of the mixture, and extracting at 70-90 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The enzymolysis solution is prepared by mixing 40-50 %mass citric acid solution and soy protein powder in a mass ratio of 1:0.2-0.6 to prepare a soy protein solution, heating to 92-95 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, cooling to 15-20 degrees C, adding 200-300 U beta -mannanase, 300-400 U xylanase, 2000-3000 U protease and 200-300 mL water with respect to 1 g of soybean protein solution, and mixing, increasing the temperature to 27-35 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, increasing the temperature 42-46 degrees C for 20-30 minutes where the heating rate is increased by 1-2 degrees C for every 3-5 minutes and reducing the temperature to 15-20 degrees C at a rate of 1-3 degrees C/minute. Preferred Condition: The extraction is carried out under continuous pressure of 4-5 MPa as the initial pressure, and the pressure is increased to 11-12 MPa at a rate of 0.1-0.3 MPa/minute, and the pressure is maintained for 20-25 minutes. Preferred Composition: The crystallizing agent is 2.3-2.9 pts. wt. sodium citrate and 11-15 pts. wt. betaine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The modified sodium carboxymethylcellulose powder is prepared by mixing pine oleic acid, trehalose, sodium carboxymethylcellulose in a mass ratio of 1:0.3-1.7:13-17 at a temperature of 80-90 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, and carrying out microwave treatment at microwave frequency of 48-62 MHz and power of 500-800W for 200-240 seconds.

PN WO2020041857-A1; CA3112339-A1; EP3844434-A1; US2021324282-A1; EP3844434-A4
TI Processing biogas comprises providing mobile tank            containing biogas at initial pressure, connecting            mobile tank containing biogas to pressure let down            system and depressurizing the mobile tank from the            initial pressure to a final pressure.
AU FOODY B
   FOODY P J
   TOLAN J S
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
   IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 202019399P
AB    NOVELTY - Processing biogas comprises: (a) providing a                mobile tank containing biogas at an initial                pressure of less than or equal to 1000 psig; (b)                connecting the mobile tank containing biogas to a                pressure let down system; and (c) depressurizing                the mobile tank from the initial pressure to a                final pressure, where depressurizing comprises (i)                removing gas from the mobile tank using the                pressure let down system, and (ii) introducing a                warming gas into the mobile tank.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) processing biogas comprising steps (a) and                (b) as per se, (c1) feeding biogas in the mobile                tank to the pressure let down system, (d1) reducing                the pressure in the mobile tank from the initial                pressure to a final pressure using the pressure let                down system, (e1) maintaining a temperature within                the mobile tank within a predetermined range as the                pressure is reduced to the final pressure by                introducing a warming gas into the mobile tank, and                (f1) feeding a gas stream comprising the biogas                removed from the mobile tank to a biogas upgrading                system and/or a fuel production system;
   (2) processing biogas comprising, (a2)                receiving a mobile tank containing a first gas                comprising biogas having a carbon dioxide content                of at least 10% and a methane content of at least                40%, where the mobile tank at a first pressure of                greater than or equal to 1000 psig, (b2) decanting                the first gas from the mobile tank, where decanting                reducing the pressure in the mobile tank to a                second pressure of less than or equal to 500 psig,                and (c2) transporting the decanted mobile tank,                where decanting the first gas from the mobile tank                comprises connecting the mobile tank to a pressure                let down system, reducing the pressure in the                mobile tank using the pressure let down system and                introducing a second gas into the mobile tank, the                second gas comprising biogas having the carbon                dioxide, and introducing comprises mixing the first                and second gases, and the second gas is warmer than                the first gas prior to mixing; and
   (3) producing a fuel from biogas comprising                filling a mobile tank with biogas to a first                pressure, where the biogas comprising raw biogas or                partially purified biogas from a first source,                transporting the mobile tank containing the biogas,                unloading the transported biogas from the mobile                tank and producing upgraded biogas and/or fuel from                the unloaded biogas, where unloading the                transported biogas from the mobile tank comprises                removing a mixture comprising the biogas from the                first source and biogas from a second other source,                and the biogas from the second other source is raw                biogas or partially purified biogas introduced into                the mobile tank during the unloading.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises:                  monitoring a pressure of the mobile tank, where                  the step (b) is conducted in dependence upon the                  monitored pressure; monitoring a temperature of                  the mobile tank, where step (b) is conducted in                  dependence upon the monitored temperature;                  heating the warming gas prior to introducing it                  into the mobile tank; providing a second other                  mobile tank containing biogas at pressure greater                  than or equal to 1000 psig, providing a fluid                  connection between the second mobile tank and the                  mobile tank and removing biogas from the second                  mobile tank and using the biogas removed from the                  second mobile tank to provide the warming gas to                  the mobile tank; partially purifying the raw                  biogas to provide the biogas from the biogas                  source; maintaining a temperature within the                  mobile tank within a predetermined range as it is                  depressurized by adjusting at least one of a                  temperature and amount of the warming gas                  introduced into the mobile tank; processing the                  biogas removed from the mobile tank to produce                  renewable natural gas, hydrogen, methanol,                  ethanol, gasoline, diesel and/or dimethyl ether;                  processing the biogas removed from the mobile                  tank to produce a transportation fuel; and                  warming the gas removed from the second mobile                  tank with a heat exchanger, where introducing a                  warming gas into the mobile tank comprises                  introducing the warmed gas removed from the                  second mobile tank. The depressurizing the mobile                  tank from the initial pressure to the final                  pressure comprises removing at least half of the                  biogas by mass initially present in the mobile                  tank. The warming gas introduced into the mobile                  tank is least 20 degrees C warmer than the gas                  present in the mobile tank. The warming gas                  comprises raw biogas, partially purified biogas                  and/or renewable natural gas. The heating the                  warming gas comprises heating the warming gas to                  a temperature of 40-120 degrees C, preferably                  80-105 degrees C. The biogas received in the                  mobile tank has a carbon dioxide content of at                  least 10%, preferably at least 20%. The final                  pressure is below 500 psig. The initial pressure                  is greater than or equal to 2000 psig, and the                  warming gas is introduced into the mobile tank                  when the pressure in the mobile tank is reduced                  to an intermediate pressure, where the                  intermediate pressure is 1500-500 psig. The                  depressurizing the mobile tank from the initial                  pressure to a final pressure comprises                  simultaneously performing steps (a) and (b). The                  depressurizing the mobile tank from the initial                  pressure to a final pressure comprises                  iteratively alternating between steps (a) and                  (b), where for each iteration, the amount of gas                  removed in (a) is at least 10% w/w of the gas                  present in the mobile tank and the amount of                  warming gas introduced in (b) is at least 10% w/w                  of the gas present in the mobile tank. The                  pressure of the biogas removed from the second                  mobile tank is greater than a pressure of the                  mobile tank before it is introduced. The biogas                  contained in the mobile tank is derived from a                  different source than the biogas contained in the                  second mobile tank. Providing the mobile tank                  containing biogas at the initial pressure                  comprises collecting raw biogas from a biogas                  source, compressing biogas from the biogas source                  and filling the mobile tank to the initial                  pressure, transporting the mobile tank to a                  central processing facility, the central                  processing facility comprising the pressure let                  down system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI High-magnetic iron red mud strong magnetic preselection-suspension direct reduction-high temperature melting comprises preselecting wet magnetic separation iron red mud, calcining, discharging calcium aluminate slag,leaching and decomposing.
AU HAN Y
   GAO H
   YU J
   LI Y
   LV Y
AE LIAONING NEU MINING & METALLURGICAL ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019A23231
AB    NOVELTY - High-magnetic iron red mud strong magnetic preselection-suspension direct reduction-high temperature melting comprises (i) preselecting stage of wet magnetic separation high-speed iron red mud to obtain magnetic separation concentrates and magnetic separation tailings, (ii) directly reducing pre-selected concentrate in the suspended state, and part of the iron oxide, (iii) high-temperature charge after suspension reduction and calcination added to the lime and mixed into the deep reduction furnace, and melting and discharging the pig iron from the molten iron outlet of the reduction furnace and and realize slag iron separation, discharging the pig iron from the molten iron outlet of the reduction furnace, (iv) discharging calcium aluminate slag and (v) passing sodium aluminate solution by leaching into the tail gas of the suspension roasting furnace containing carbon dioxide , and sodium aluminate, decomposing into aluminum hydroxide by stirring, and discarding tailings.
   USE - The method is useful for high-magnetic iron red mud strong magnetic preselection-suspension direct reduction-high temperature melting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers iron at the rate of less than 96%, and aluminum leaching rate is less than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-magnetic iron red mud strong magnetic preselection-suspension direct reduction-high temperature melting comprises (i) preselecting stage of wet magnetic separation high-speed iron red mud to obtain magnetic separation concentrates and magnetic separation tailings. Where , the magnetic separation magnetic field strength is 636.62-795.77 kA/m, and the pre-selected iron grade TFe is 40-47%, silicon dioxide mass fraction is 2-5%, (ii) directly reducing pre-selected concentrate in the suspended state, and part of the iron oxide is reduced to metallic iron to obtain a high-temperature charge, controlling roasting time metallization rate of iron after reduction roasting of the magnetic separation concentrate is 90-95%, (iii) high-temperature charge after suspension reduction and calcination added to the lime and mixed into the deep reduction furnace, and melting at increased temperature and re-reduction, so that all the iron minerals in the charge are reduced to metallic iron, and iron recovery rate is less than 96%, and realize slag iron separation, discharging the pig iron from the molten iron outlet of the reduction furnace, and the mass ratio of the molten iron is 92-98%, discharging generated calcium aluminate slag from the slag discharge port, (iv) discharging calcium aluminate slag from the deep reduction furnace and cooling, slag is self-powdered into loose powder during cooling, leaching with sodium carbonate solution to obtain sodium aluminate solution where alumina leaching rate is less than 90%, and (v) passing sodium aluminate solution by leaching into the tail gas of the suspension roasting furnace containing carbon dioxide , and sodium aluminate, decomposing into aluminum hydroxideby stirring, and discarding tailings.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The blending amount of the lime is n (calcium oxide) / n (aluminum oxide) in the mixed material is 1.80-2.10. Preferred Method: The suspended direct reduction temperature is 800-1000 degrees C, and the reduction time is 30-60 minutes. The re-reduction temperature is 1250-1400 degrees C, and the reduction time is 30-50 minutes. The cooling rate of the calcium aluminate slag is controlled to 4-6 degrees C/minutes and reducing to 1200 degrees C and naturally cooling in a cooling tank. The concentration of the sodium carbonate solution is 80-100 g/l. The leaching temperature is 70-90 degrees C. The leaching time is 85-110 minutes. The solution mass/solid mass is 6:1-10:1. The exhaust gas flow is 11-16 L/hours. The carbon temperature is 50-60 degrees C, and the stirring rate is 200-250 r/minutes.

TI Preparing composite plant enzyme useful for preparing daily washing product i.e. detergent, comprises crushing plant material, fermenting, sterilizing fermentation culture medium, homogenizing, filtering, extracting and concentrating.
AU HE W
AE GUANGZHOU LIFENG COSMETICS MFG CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A1456H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite plant enzyme comprises (i) crushing the required plant material to obtain fermentation culture medium; (ii) sterilizing fermentation culture medium, inoculating yeast culture solution, stirring and fermenting at 32 degrees C for 72 hours in continuous dark conditions with sterile air ventilation rate of 1:0.4 m3 vol./vol./minute and tank pressure of 0.04 MPa and reducing pH of fermentation liquid to 4.5-5; (iii) adding 1 wt.% calcium carbonate into the fermentation liquid, reducing acidity at 30 degrees C for 3 hours, reducing pH of the fermentation liquid to 3-3.5 until the total acidity is 2.8-3, homogenizing the fermentation liquid by colloid mill and by high pressure and filtering through 200 meshes plate frame; and (iv) extracting the obtained fermentation liquid is by supercritical carbon dioxide at 33 degrees C and 7.15 MPa and concentrating the extract to 25% (based on solid content).
   USE - The complex plant enzyme is useful for preparing daily washing product i.e. detergent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The complex plant enzyme: completely eliminates pesticide residue; and synergistic effect complex plant enzyme and Sapindus fruit extract in the detergent has effect of decontaminating and removing pesticide residues and reduces the irritation of the skin on the hand to protect the hand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite plant enzyme comprises (i) crushing the required plant material to obtain fermentation culture medium, where the fermentation culture medium comprises 10 wt.% Phellodendron, 15 wt.% Artemisia leaf, 3 wt.% saffron, 10 wt.% motherwort, 2 wt.% radix-polygoni multiflori, 5 wt.% Angelica dahurica, 37 wt.% Panax notoginseng, 4 wt.% Japanese honeysuckle, 8 wt.% Penthorum 8 wt.% Ganoderma lucidum, 2 wt.% Ginseng and water (balance amount) and has pH of 6-7; (ii) sterilizing fermentation culture medium, inoculating yeast culture solution, stirring and fermenting at 32 degrees C for 72 hours in continuous dark conditions with sterile air ventilation rate of 1:0.4 m3 vol./vol./minute and tank pressure of 0.04 MPa and reducing pH of fermentation liquid to 4.5-5; (iii) adding 1 wt.% calcium carbonate into the fermentation liquid, reducing acidity at 30 degrees C for 3 hours, reducing pH of the fermentation liquid to 3-3.5 until the total acidity is 2.8-3, homogenizing the fermentation liquid by colloid mill and by high pressure and filtering through 200 meshes plate frame; and (iv) extracting the obtained fermentation liquid is by supercritical carbon dioxide at 33 degrees C and 7.15 MPa and concentrating the extract to 25% (based on solid content). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) composite plant enzyme, prepared by the above mentioned method; and
   (2) detergent, comprising the composite plant enzyme.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The detergent comprises 1-3 wt.% complex plant enzyme, 1-5 wt.% Sapindus fruit extract, 6-15 wt.% other surfactant, 5-10 wt.% humectant, 1.5 wt.% sodium chloride, 0-1 wt.% preservative, and water (balance amount), preferably 4 wt.% Sapindus fruit extract, 7 wt.% fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate, 5 wt.% glycerin, 3 wt.% propylene glycol, 5 wt.% cocamidopropyl betaine, 2.5 wt.% complex plant enzyme, 1.5 wt.% sodium chloride, 0-1 wt.% preservative and water (balance amount). The other surfactant comprises fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate and cocamidopropyl betaine. The humectant is glycerin, sodium pyroglutamic acid, hydroxyethylurea and/or propylene glycol.

PN CN110499158-A
TI Preparing rare earth samarium-doped tin oxide nano            hollow sphere-sensitized luminous body useful for            preparing fluorescent display panel comprises e.g.            adding glucose to mixed solution of deionized water and            ethanol to obtain clear solution.
AU CHEN C
   LI Y
   ZHU S
AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
GA 2019A4071E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rare earth samarium-doped tin oxide                nano hollow sphere-sensitized luminous body                comprises e.g. adding glucose to mixed solution of                deionized water and ethanol to obtain clear                solution, cooling naturally to room temperature,                washing carbon spheres obtained by centrifugation                subsequently many times with ethanol and deionized                water and drying, synthesizing core-shell                microspheres using carbon spheres as a template,                dispersing carbon spheres in ethanol to obtain                mixed solution, dissolving hydrated tin chloride                and samarium nitrate hydrate in dimethylformamide                and adding dropwise to a mixed solution of carbon                spheres and ethanol and magnetically stirring,                sealing final solution, cooling to room                temperature, washing coated spheres after                centrifugation alternately many times with ethanol                and deionized water, collecting and drying to                obtain core-shell microspheres, calcining                core-shell microspheres in air and converting                internal carbon spheres into carbon dioxide.
   USE - The hollow sphere-sensitized luminous body is                useful for preparing fluorescent display panel                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple equipment requirements,                mild reaction conditions and good controllability,                is environmentally-friendly; hollow sphere                synthesized has strong yellow-red photosensitized                emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing rare earth samarium-doped tin oxide                nano hollow sphere-sensitized luminous body                comprises (i) adding glucose to mixed solution of                deionized water and ethanol at 1:20-25 g/ml to                obtain clear solution, maintaining at 170-200                degrees C for 7-8 hours, cooling naturally to room                temperature, washing carbon spheres obtained by                centrifugation subsequently many times with ethanol                and deionized water and drying; (ii) synthesizing                core-shell microspheres using carbon spheres as a                template, dispersing carbon spheres in ethanol at a                rate of 0.1:5-10 g/ml to obtain mixed solution,                dissolving hydrated tin chloride and samarium                nitrate hydrate in dimethylformamide at 1.5-5.5:10                mol/ml and adding dropwise to a mixed solution of                carbon spheres and ethanol and magnetically                stirring for 1-2 hours, where molar ratio of                hydrated tin chloride and samarium nitrate hydrate                is 1.5-5.5, sealing final solution and maintaining                at 170-200 degrees C for 6-10 hours, cooling to                room temperature, washing coated spheres after                centrifugation alternately many times with ethanol                and deionized water, and collecting and drying to                obtain core-shell microspheres; and (iii) calcining                core-shell microspheres in air at 700-800 degrees C                for 2-2.5 hours, converting internal carbon spheres                into carbon dioxide to obtain trivalent samarium                ion-doped tin oxide hollow sphere structure. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for hollow                sphere-sensitized luminous body, prepared by above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i) ratio                  of deionized water and ethanol in the mixed                  solution is 3:1. In the step (ii) molar ratio of                  hydrated tin chloride and samarium nitrate is x,                  x = Sm/(Sm + Sn), Sm and Sn represent the molar                  amount or atomic number of samarium and tin,                  respectively. The hydrated tin chloride and                  samarium nitrate have a purity of more than                  99.99%.The luminous body has an average diameter                  of 100-150 nm and spherical shell thickness of                  20-30 nm.

PN CN110396607-A
TI Treating waste ternary lithium ion battery powder            comprises supplying oxygen, heating, performing            reduction leaching, extracting, adding hydrazine            hydrate, performing oxidizing roasting, subjecting to            aluminum removal and and purifying.
AU ZHANG D
   YANG T
   LIU W
   CHEN L
   WANG H
   REN G
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201992712W
AB    NOVELTY - Treating waste ternary lithium ion battery                powder comprises (1) placing the waste ternary                lithium ion battery powder in a tube furnace,                supplying oxygen, heating, the carbon in the waste                ternary lithium ion battery powder enters the                subsequent absorption bottle in the form of carbon                dioxide, cooling, (2) performing reduction leaching                in a sulfuric acid system, extracting lithium,                nickel, cobalt, manganese, iron, aluminum and                copper, adding hydrazine hydrate, reacting,                transferring the leaching slag to the intermediate                temperature oxidizing roasting process, (3) adding                the calcined product to the leachate, adding                hydrazine hydrate, reacting, filtering, washing to                obtain copper containing slag and the                copper-removing liquid, transferring the copper                containing slag to the reduction leaching process,                subjecting the copper removal liquid to aluminum                removal and (4) adding sodium benzoate, adjusting                pH, reacting to obtain aluminum-containing slag and                aluminum-removed liquid and purifying.
   USE - The method is useful in treating waste ternary                lithium ion battery powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: requires simple equipments; has                low operation cost; has less waste water discharge;                has good copper removal and aluminum removal                effect; saves energy; has high recovery rate of                valuable metals; and can be easily promoted.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating waste ternary lithium ion battery                powder comprises (1) placing the waste ternary                lithium ion battery powder in a tube furnace,                continuously supplying oxygen at a rate of 60-300                ml/minute for 30-60 minutes, heating to 400-800                degrees C for 15-75 minutes, the carbon in the                waste ternary lithium ion battery powder enters the                subsequent absorption bottle in the form of carbon                dioxide, cooling the furnace, turning off oxygen to                obtain a calcined product, (2) performing reduction                leaching in a sulfuric acid system having a 2-6                mol/l sulfuric acid, extracting lithium, nickel,                cobalt, manganese, iron, aluminum and copper, where                the liquid solid ratio is 2-6:1, adding 1-6%                hydrazine hydrate, reacting at 40-80 degrees C for                30-150 minutes, filtering, washing the leaching                residue and the leachate, transferring the leaching                slag to the intermediate temperature oxidizing                roasting process, subjecting the leaching liquid to                remove copper, (3) adding the calcined product to                the leachate in a liquid-solid ratio of 3-7:1,                adding 0.8-4% hydrazine hydrate, reacting at 50-90                degrees C for 30-120 minutes, filtering, washing to                obtain copper containing slag and the                copper-removing liquid, transferring the copper                containing slag to the reduction leaching process,                subjecting the copper removal liquid to aluminum                removal and (4) adding 0.50-1.50% sodium benzoate,                adjusting the pH of the solution to 3-5, reacting                at 30-70 degrees C for 30-90 minutes, filtering,                washing to obtain aluminum-containing slag and                aluminum-removed liquid and purifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen, sulfuric                  acid, hydrazine hydrate, sodium hydroxide and                  sodium benzoate utilized in the treatment process                  are industrial grade reagents. The waste ternary                  lithium ion battery powder comprises 1-5%                  lithium, 10-20% nickel, 5-10 cobalt, 10-15%                  manganese, 0.1-1 iron, 0.2-5% aluminum, 0.2-5%                  copper and 30-40% carbon.

PN CN110180544-A
TI Preparing supported iron(II,III) oxide/iron(III) oxide-ACSs photocatalyst useful for photoreduction of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. adding phenols, aldehyde compound and ethylene glycol to three-necked flask containing water.
AU ZHANG X
   REN G
   ZHANG C
   LI R
   LIU J
   WANG Y
   FAN C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201976096K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supported iron(II,III) oxide/iron(III) oxide-ACSs photocatalyst comprises (i) adding phenols, aldehyde compound and ethylene glycol to a three-necked flask containing water, stirring for 0.5-1.5 hour, adding 2-5 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate, controlling the temperature in the oil bath to 70-120 degrees C, and stirring for 0.5-2 hours to obtain a mixed solution, (ii) adding iron salt and polyvinyl alcohol to the mixed solution of the step (i), maintaining the temperature at 60-120 degrees C, continuing the reaction for 0.5-2 hours, adding 1-3 pts. wt. hexamethylenetetramine to the above solution, reacting for 4-8 hours, filtering, washing using dilute alkali solution, and drying at 60-100 degrees C for 10-24 hours to obtain transition metal iron-phenolic resin ball, and (iii) placing the transition metal iron-phenolic resin ball obtained in the step (ii) in a tube furnace and carbonizing under the protection of nitrogen at 600-1000 degrees C.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photoreduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple preparation process and mild conditions; obtains stable product; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing supported iron(II,III) oxide/iron(III) oxide-ACSs photocatalyst comprises (i) adding 30-70 pts. wt. phenols, 40-90 pts. wt. aldehyde compound and 15-35 pts. wt. ethylene glycol to a three-necked flask containing 200-300 pts. wt. water, stirring for 0.5-1.5 hour, adding 2-5 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl sulfate, controlling the temperature in the oil bath to 70-120 degrees C, and stirring for 0.5-2 hours to obtain a mixed solution, (ii) adding 5-10 pts. wt. iron salt and 1-9 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol to the mixed solution of the step (i), maintaining the temperature at 60-120 degrees C, continuing the reaction for 0.5-2 hours, adding 1-3 pts. wt. hexamethylenetetramine to the above solution, reacting for 4-8 hours, filtering, washing using dilute alkali solution, and drying at 60-100 degrees C for 10-24 hours to obtain transition metal iron-phenolic resin ball, and (iii) placing the transition metal iron-phenolic resin ball obtained in the step (ii) in a tube furnace and carbonizing under the protection of nitrogen at 600-1000 degrees C, switching the water vapor to be activated at 1.5-5 degrees C/minutes at 700-1000 degrees C for 1-3 hours, after completing the reaction, reducing the temperature to room temperature, taking sample, washing using dilute acid for 3 times, washing using distilled water until neutral, and drying at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phenol in the step (i) is phenol, p-phenylphenol and/or 6-tert-butyl m-cresol, and the aldehyde compound is formaldehyde or furfural. The dilute alkali solution in the step (ii) is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide or aqueous ammonia, and the iron salt is ferric nitrate, ferrocene or ferric chloride. The dilute acid in the step (iii) is dilute nitric acid, sulfuric acid or hydrochloric acid.

TI Treating die casting factory wastewater comprises            e.g. initial filtrating, adjusting pH value, combined            gas flotation, ozone oxidating and photocatalytic            oxidating.
AU FAN H
   JIANG J
AE SUZHOU JIEPINGTAI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION              TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019673832
AB    NOVELTY - Treating die casting factory wastewater                comprises e.g. raw water of wastewater enters                wastewater pool from inlet of lower end of                wastewater tank, filtering grid by grid filter                device, adjusting pH value of initially filtered                wastewater, pumping wastewater with adjusted pH                into inner cylinder of air floating tower,                introducing combined gas of oxygen and ozone,                separating and precipitating, oxidizing and                decolorizing organic matter in waste water through                oxidative decolorizer provided in oxidation tower,                separating water and impurities by using medium                filter provided in oxidation tower, catalyzing                oxygen atoms into hydroxy radicals, accelerating                oxidation reaction rate, and completely oxidizing                organic matter into carbon dioxide and water, and                discharging indicator-compliant wastewater after                photocatalytic oxidation treatment into company's                water supply pipe network for recycling or                discharge.
   USE - The method is useful for treating die casting                factory wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to operate; has                no chemicals, no new solid waste, and no secondary                pollution is generated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating die casting factory wastewater                comprises (i) raw water of wastewater enters                wastewater pool from inlet of lower end of                wastewater tank, and filtering grid by grid filter                device to remove suspended solids, floating matter                and large particles of impurities in the                wastewater, (ii) the wastewater treated by initial                filtration overflows into regulating tank through                overflow port at upper end of separator of                wastewater tank, and adjusting pH value of                initially filtered wastewater in regulating tank,                (iii) pumping wastewater with adjusted pH into                inner cylinder of air floating tower by water pump,                and introducing combined gas of oxygen and ozone                into inner cylinder to perform combined gas                flotation, separating and precipitating water and                impurities by utilizing oxidizing ability of oxygen                and ozone, and separating water and impurities by                medium filter disposed in outer cylinder of air                floating tower, (iv) wastewater after combined gas                flotation treatment enters inside of oxidation                tower through clean water outlet at lower end of                gas floating tower, oxidizing and decolorizing                organic matter in waste water through oxidative                decolorizer provided in oxidation tower, and                separating water and impurities by using medium                filter provided in oxidation tower, (v)                ozone-oxidized wastewater flows out from outlet of                upper end of oxidation tower, and flows into                interior of catalytic tower through water inlet of                lower end of catalytic tower, and uses ultraviolet                light of photocatalytic processor to open chemical                bond of organic pollutant, and through ozone to                rapidly decompose oxygen atoms, catalyzing oxygen                atoms into hydroxy radicals, accelerating oxidation                reaction rate, and completely oxidizing organic                matter into carbon dioxide and water, and (vi)                discharging indicator-compliant wastewater after                photocatalytic oxidation treatment into company's                water supply pipe network for recycling or                discharge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the step (i), grid                  filtering adopts double-layer grid filtering, and                  grid adopts stainless steel grid, and filter pore                  diameter of grid is 0.1-2 mm. In the step (i),                  initial filtration further includes agitation and                  scraping treatment during the filtration process.                  In the step (v), catalytic tower is provided with                  at least two groups which are successively                  connected in sequence, and the wastewater is                  oxidized and dehydrated corresponding to at least                  two photocatalytic oxidations. In the step (iii),                  (iv) and (v), further comprises tail gas recovery                  treatment process, introducing excess gas or                  newly generated gas in step (iii), (iv) and (v)                  into exhaust gas treatment device through exhaust                  pipe, air flotation tower, oxidation tower and                  exhaust gas outlet of catalytic tower for exhaust                  gas treatment.

PN CN109748309-A
TI Preparing highly dispersible nano calcium carbonate comprises e.g. taking calcium hydroxide suspension into a glass carbonization tower adding reaction product to the carbonization tower, stirring and mixing.
AU TAN Z
AE TAN Z (TANZ-Individual)
GA 2019445184
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly dispersible nano calcium carbonate comprises taking a calcium hydroxide suspension with a mass fraction of 10% into a glass carbonization tower with an aeration device at the bottom, adding reaction product to the carbonization tower, stirring and mixing for 10-20 minutes to obtain the pre-formed liquid and (ii) adding the carbon dioxide into the pre-formed liquid in the glass charring tower through an aeration device at aeration rate of 100 ml/minute, carrying out the aeration reaction for 1-2 hours, taking out the reaction product, separating, rinsing the filter residue with deionized water for 3-5 times and drying naturally.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing highly dispersible nano calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing highly dispersible nano calcium carbonate comprises taking a calcium hydroxide suspension with a mass fraction of 10% into a glass carbonization tower with an aeration device at the bottom, adding reaction product to the carbonization tower, stirring and mixing for 10-20 minutes to obtain the pre-formed liquid and (ii) adding the carbon dioxide into the pre-formed liquid in the glass charring tower through an aeration device at aeration rate of 100 ml/minute, carrying out the aeration reaction for 1-2 hours, taking out the reaction product, separating, rinsing the filter residue with deionized water for 3-5 times and drying naturally. The reaction product is obtained by adding potassium permanganate to a three-necked flask, stirring the reaction at 200-300 revolutions/minute at 60-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, using 0.5 mol/l after the reaction is completed, adjusting the sodium hydroxide solution to a pH of 9-10 and stirring the reaction at 30-40 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain a reaction product. The preparation step of the reactant includes mixing the enzymatic hydrolysis product and 0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid into a three-necked flask equipped with a stirrer and a thermometer, placing the three-necked flask into a water bath, stirring the reaction at normal temperature for 10-12 hours and obtaining the reactants. The preparation steps of the enzymatic hydrolysis product is obtained by taking the pine bark into a tissue crusher, crushing for 20-30 minutes, obtaining a mixed pulverized material, adding the mixed pulverized material into a steam blasting tank for steam explosion to obtain blasting product, mixing the blasting product with cellulase, xylanase and pectinase into the enzymatic hydrolysis tank, placing the enzymatic hydrolysis tank in an incubator at 28-32 degrees C, adding enzyme, carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis for 12-15 hours an dobtaining the enzymatic hydrolysis product after the end of enzymatic hydrolysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the preparation step (i) of the highly dispersible nano calcium carbonate, the adding amount of the reaction product is 10% by mass based on the mass of the calcium hydroxide suspension. In the preparation step (ii) of the highly dispersible nano calcium carbonate, the temperature of the continuous aeration reaction is 15-20 degrees C. Preferred Method: In the preparation step of the reactant, the mass ratio of the enzymatic product of 0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid is 1:8. In the step (i), the temperature of the steam explosion treatment of the enzymatic hydrolysis product is 200-220 degrees C and the pressure of the steam explosion treatment is 1-1.2 MPa. The mass ratio of the blasting product to the cellulase, xylanase and pectinase in the preparation step (ii) of the enzymatic hydrolysate is 20:1:1:1.

AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 2019358144
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 5-aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride i.e. 4-carboxy-2-oxobutan-1-aminium chloride (I) comprises (i) adding furan-2-ylmethanamine (II), alkali and protective reagent in an organic solvents, reacting for 0.5-4 hours, washing, concentrating to obtain N-(furan-2-ylmethyl)formamide compounds (III), where alkali is triethylamine, diisopropylethylamine, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate or nano potassium carbonate; and organic solvent is dichloromethane, chloroform, tetrahydrofuran, toluene, acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide, (ii) dissolving (III) in an organic solvent, adding buffer and oxidizer, reacting at -20 degrees C to 20 degrees C for a period of time to obtain N-((2-hydroxy-5-oxo-2,5-dihydrofuran-2-yl)methyl)formamide compounds (IV), where oxidizing agent is sodium chlorate, sodium chlorite, sodium perchlorate, carbon dioxide, sodium hypobromite, chlorine gas or bromine water.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 5-aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple preparation process; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 5-aminolevulinic acid hydrochloride i.e. 4-carboxy-2-oxobutan-1-aminium chloride of formula (I) comprises (i) adding furan-2-ylmethanamine of formula (II), alkali and protective reagent in an organic solvents, reacting for 0.5-4 hours, washing, concentrating to obtain N-(furan-2-ylmethyl)formamide compounds of formula (III), where alkali is triethylamine, diisopropylethylamine, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate or nano potassium carbonate; and organic solvent is dichloromethane, chloroform, tetrahydrofuran, toluene, acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide, (ii) dissolving (III) in an organic solvent, adding buffer and oxidizer, reacting at -20 degrees C to 20 degrees C for a period of time to obtain N-((2-hydroxy-5-oxo-2,5-dihydrofuran-2-yl)methyl)formamide compounds of formula (IV), where oxidizing agent is sodium chlorate, sodium chlorite, sodium perchlorate, carbon dioxide, sodium hypobromite, chlorine gas or bromine water; and buffer is sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate or ammonium chloride; and solvent is methanol, ethanol, tert-butanol, N-butanol or octanol, (iii) adding (IV) into the reaction vessel, adding organic or inorganic acids, adding reducing reagent in batches at 100-115 degrees C, carrying out a reduction reaction, suction filtering, concentrating solvent under reduced pressure to obtain 5-formamido-4-oxopentanoic acid compounds of formula (V), where organic acid is formic acid or acetic acid; and inorganic acid is hydrochloric acid or hydrobromic acid, and (iv) heating (V) hydrolyzing and decarboxylating in an aqueous hydrochloric acid solution, concentrating and then recrystallizing to obtain (I), where reaction temperature is 100-115 degrees C, hydrochloric acid concentration is 4-8 N, and reaction time is 1-3 hours.
   R = an acetyl, tert-butoxycarbonyl, benzyloxycarbonyl, benzoyl, P-toluenesulfonyl, trifluoroacetyl, phthaloyl, Fmoc- or benzyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), alkali is triethylamine, diisopropylethylamine, potassium carbonate or potassium carbonate; organic solvent is chloroform or dichloromethane; protective reagent is acetyl chloride, di-tert-butyl dicarbonate, benzyl chloroformate, acetic anhydride, benzoyl chloride, P-toluenesulfonyl chloride, trifluoroacetyl chloride, phthaloyl chloride, Fmoc-chloride or benzyl bromide; and reaction temperature is -20 degrees C to 20 degrees C. In step (ii), oxidizing agent is chlorine dioxide, sodium hypochlorite, sodium chlorite and/or chlorine gas; buffer is sodium dihydrogen phosphate or potassium dihydrogen phosphate; and reaction temperature is -10 degrees C to 10 degrees C. The solvent is methanol, ethanol, tert-butanol, N-butanol or a mixed solvent of water and n-butanol; and volume ratio between alcohol and water is 4-6:1. In step (iii), reducing reagents are zinc, iron, hydrogen/palladium, hydrogen/palladium-carbon, hydrogen/active nickel or hydrogen/Rani nickel; and reaction time is 1-3 hours. The reducing agent if using organic acids are zinc, iron or hydrogen/palladium-carbon; and reaction time is 1.5 hours. In step (iv), molar ratio between hydrochloric acid and (V) is 10-12:1. The concentration of hydrochloric acid is 6 N. Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding anhydrous dichloromethane, furan-2-ylmethanamine (II) into vessel, adding triethylamine, stirring in an ice bath (0 degrees C), adding acetyl chloride, reacting at -10 degrees C to 10 degrees C for 0.5 hours, performing thin layer chromatography, washing 3 times with water, then washing with saturated sodium chloride solution, drying the organic phase with an anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering, then concentrating to obtain a viscous yellow liquid product (III), where volume ratio between anhydrous dichloromethane, (II) and triethylamine is 20:1:2.12, (ii) adding (III) into the vessel 2, adding methanol and distilled water, stirring under ice bath (0 degrees C), cooling solvent to 0 degrees C, adding 19.05 g sodium dihydrogen phosphate and 32.92 g sodium hypochlorite, reacting at 0 degrees C for 3 hours, performing thin layer chromatography, transferring the reaction solution into a separatory funnel, allowing to stand for stratification, collecting the upper methanol solution, then suction filtering with diatomaceous earth, then concentrating filtrate to obtain (IV), where volume ratio between methanol and distilled water 5:1, and mass ratio between sodium dihydrogen phosphate and sodium hypochlorite is 1.73:1, (iii) adding the oxidation product (IV) into the vessel three, adding 150 ml formic acid, heating at 100 degrees C, adding 69 g zinc powder in batches, stirring, reacting at 100 degrees C for 1.5 hours, performing thin layer chromatography, filtering, concentrating solvent under reduced pressure to obtain (V), where ratio between oxidation product (IV), formic acid and zinc powder is 1:15:8, and (iv) adding (V) into the vessel four, adding 6 N hydrochloric acid solution, heating at 115 degrees C, refluxing for 1.5 hours, performing thin layer chromatography, concentrating the solvent, recrystallizing from methanol/dichloromethane to obtain (I), where molar ratio between hydrochloric acid and (V) is 10-12:1.

PN CN108358363-A; CN108358363-B
TI Treating high-salt organic waste water comprises e.g. sorting high-salt organic waste water through a coarse mechanical grid, and entering into the rotational flow settling tank, passing the effluent into a combined reactor and processing.
AU XU Q
AE XU Q (XUQQ-Individual)
   JIANGXI CHUHANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201862905S
AB    NOVELTY - Treating high-salt organic waste water comprises (i) sorting high-salt organic waste water through a coarse mechanical grid, and entering into the rotational flow settling tank for treatment; (ii) adjusting the pH of the effluent from the tank and entering into a electroflocculation cell and electrolyzing the aluminum ion to form colloid; (iii) passing the effluent from cell into a ultraviolet-ozone-ultrasonic combined reactor and processing; (iv) passing the combined reaction effluent into a electrolytic cell, suspending particle electrode string in between the two electrodes, and oxidatively degrading the three-dimensional electrode under aeration condition for oxidizing organic matter in the waste water into carbon dioxide and water; (v) passing the effluent from the electrolytic cell through a three-dimensional graphene sponge filter; and (vi) providing a sulfonated polyaryl ether sulfone ultrafiltration membrane on the electrodialysis chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for treating high-salt organic waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not contain biological treatment process; has high process flexibility compared to common sewage processing method, short treatment cycle, high recovery rate, and high desalting rate; and is simple, environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating high-salt organic waste water comprises (i) sorting high-salt organic waste water through a coarse mechanical grid, and entering into the rotational flow settling tank for treatment after crushing the impurities by a fine mechanical grating; (ii) adjusting the pH to 3-4 of the effluent from the rotational flow settling tank and entering into a electroflocculation cell comprising aluminum as anode and stainless steel as cathode, and electrolyzing the aluminum ion to form colloid for absorbing the suspended particles in waste water, where the colloid, organic matter and hydroxide generating by cathode combines with the heavy metal ions to form a precipitate; (iii) passing the effluent from electroflocculation cell into a ultraviolet-ozone-ultrasonic combined reactor and processing for 1-2 hours; (iv) passing the combined reaction effluent into a electrolytic cell comprising polypyrrole-modified dimensionally stable anode electrode as anode and barbed wire as cathode, suspending 50-100 particle electrode string in between the two electrodes, where the electrolyte is 0.2 mol/l sodium sulfate, and oxidatively degrading the three-dimensional electrode under aeration condition for 1-2 hours for oxidizing organic matter in the waste water into carbon dioxide and water; (v) passing the effluent from the electrolytic cell through a three-dimensional graphene sponge filter for adsorbing the residual heavy metal ions; and (vi) providing a sulfonated polyaryl ether sulfone ultrafiltration membrane on the surface of anion exchange membrane of the electrodialysis chamber, where the salt removal rate reaches 99.4%, and reusing the recycled water in chemical, printing and dyeing industry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolysis current in the electroflocculation cell is a pulse current, and the pole-changing period is 600-800 seconds. Preferred Method: The preparation of three-dimensional graphene sponge comprises ultrasonically dispersing graphene oxide to obtain graphene oxide dispersion, adding aminotris(methylenephosphonic acid) solution, reacting the mixture in polytetrafluoroethylene reactor at 150 degrees C for 2 hours, soaking the product in distilled water for 48 hours and freeze-drying.

TI Burner useful to produce synthetic gas comprises            e.g. device to separately feed carbonaceous fuel,            oxygen-containing oxidant and moderator, and second            supply channel surrounding the first supply channel for            oxygen-containing oxidant.

   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)

   AIR LIQUIDE ETUDE & EXPL PROCEDES CLAUDE (AIRL-C)
GA 201840341H
AB    NOVELTY - Burner comprises a device for separately                feeding carbonaceous fuel, oxygen-containing                oxidant and moderator, a central, circular                cross-sectional first supply channel for                oxygen-containing oxidant, a second supply channel                surrounding the first supply channel and an annular                gap is formed between the outer wall of the first                supply channel and the inner wall of the second                supply channel through which the moderator is fed.                A third supply channel is coaxially and                concentrically surrounding the second supply                channel and the outer wall of the third supply                channel forms the outer wall of the burner.
   USE - The burner is useful for producing synthetic                gas by partial oxidation of liquid or gaseous,                carbonaceous fuel in the presence of an                oxygen-containing oxidizing agent and a moderator                containing water vapor and/or carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The burner: can be operated without cooling,                i.e. without passing through a fluid coolant, thus                preventing failure of the burner and unplanned                shutdown of the synthesis gas production plant; has                simple structure; and reduces the load on the                burner during operation with transient conditions;                ensures that the mixing is carried out outside the                burner without backmixing with media parts                remaining inside the burner, which prevents                undesired oxidation reactions within the burner;                and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Burner comprises a device for separately                feeding carbonaceous fuel, oxygen-containing                oxidant and moderator, a central, circular                cross-sectional first supply channel (11) for                oxygen-containing oxidant, a second supply channel                (21) coaxially and concentrically surrounding the                first supply channel and an annular gap is formed                between the outer wall of the first supply channel                and the inner wall of the second supply channel                through which the moderator is fed. A third supply                channel (31) is coaxially and concentrically                surrounding the second supply channel, where an                annular gap is formed between the outer wall of the                second supply channel and the inner wall of the                third supply channel through which the fuel is                supplied and the outer wall of the third supply                channel forms the outer wall of the burner (35).                The supply channels are designed so that the mixing                of the fuel, the moderator and the oxidizing agent                takes place only outside the burner. The burner                produces synthetic gas by partial oxidation of                liquid or gaseous, carbonaceous fuel in the                presence of an oxygen-containing oxidizing agent                and a moderator containing water vapor and/or                carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the burner.
   Burner (1)
   First supply channel (11)
   Second supply channel (21)
   Third supply channel (31)
   Outer wall of the burner (35)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the burner outer                  wall: a partition wall between the second and the                  third supply channel and a partition between the                  first and the second supply channel terminate in                  a common plane, which is perpendicular to the                  longitudinal axis of the burner and forms the                  burner mouth. The burner does not contain any                  device for passing a fluid coolant through the                  burner. At least two of the feeding channels are                  equipped with a turbulence device, where the                  directions of rotation, subjected to the                  moderator flowing through at least two supply                  channels, are in opposite directions with respect                  to the longitudinal axis of the burner. All the                  supply channels are equipped with turbulence                  devices, where the directions of rotation of the                  moderator flowing through the first supply                  channel and the third supply channel are in the                  same direction with respect to the longitudinal                  axis of the burner and the direction of rotation                  of the moderator flowing through the second                  supply channel is opposite to the longitudinal                  axis of the burner.

GA 2022705249
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas mixing tank (9) whose air inlet end is connected with a carbon dioxide pipeline (1). An oxygen pipeline (3) is provided with an oxygen heater (7). The oxygen pipeline is provided with an oxygen heater bypass pipeline (4). A front of the oxygen heater is provided with a heater front adjusting valve (5). The oxygen heater is fixed behind an oxygen heater rear adjusting valve (6). The oxygen heater bypass pipeline is provided with an oxygen heater bypass pipeline adjusting valve (8). An air outlet end of the gas mixing tank is connected with a mixed gas main pipeline (11). An output end of the mixed gas main pipeline is connected with a secondary air chamber (23).
   USE - Oxygen and carbon-dioxide supply oxygen-enriched combustion air supply system for use in a tangential boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces energy loss caused by the temperature difference between carbon dioxide and oxygen in the oxygen-enriched combustion process, so that amount of oxygen and carbon dioxide is controllable, fuel of the burnout air chamber is ensured and combustion efficiency is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an oxygen and carbon-dioxide supply oxygen-enriched combustion air supply system.

   Oxygen heater rear adjusting valve (6)
   Oxygen heater (7)
