PN CN114262606-A
TI Low-damage carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid useful for coalbed methane wells, comprises foaming agent, foam stabilizer, clay stabilizer and water.
AU CHEN G
   LIU F
   MA Z
   YUAN H
   WEI Y
   ZHUO Z
   CHEN B
   HE P
   YUAN Z
   SONG R
   LI X
   SONG Z
AE CNOOC ENERGY TECHNOLOGY & SERVICES LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 202251353A
AB    NOVELTY - Low-damage carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid comprises 0.20-0.60 wt.% foaming agent, 0.40-1 wt.% foam stabilizer, 0.80-1.10 wt.% clay stabilizer and 97.30-98.60 wt.% water.
   USE - The fracturing fluid is useful for coalbed methane wells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fracturing fluid: does not contain macromolecular polymers; has low damage to the coal seam; can promote the desorption of adsorbed methane; has high foaming performance, foam stabilization performance, pulverized coal dispersion performance and clay anti-swelling performance; and can meet the requirements of on-site construction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the fracturing fluid, comprising (1) adding 0.20-0.60 wt.% foaming agent, 0.40-1 wt.% foam stabilizer and 0.80-1.10 wt.% clay stabilizer to 97.30-98.60 wt.% water, and stirring until completely dissolved to obtain base liquid, (2) placing the base liquid prepared in the step (1) in bubbler with stirrer, sealing the opening of the bubbler with sealing plug, using the air hose to penetrate the sealing plug of the bubbler, connecting the air inlet port of the air hose to the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, extending the gas outlet port of the gas delivery hose into the bubbler, and locating the gas outlet port of the gas delivery hose below the liquid level of the base liquid, (3) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the bubbler at speed of 4-6 l/minute for 10-15 minutes to discharge the air dissolved in the base liquid and increase the dissolved amount of carbon dioxide in the foam base liquid, (4) lifting the air outlet port of the gas delivery hose to 1-2 cm above the liquid level of the base liquid, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the bubbler at speed of 8-12 l/minutes for 5-8 minutes to remove the bubbler air in the headspace, and (5) lifting the air outlet port of the air hose to the position of the sealing plug of the bubbler, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the bubbler at a speed of 12-16 l/minute, starting the stirrer and stirring for 1-5 minutes at rotating speed of 6000-10000 revolutions/minute to foam, and obtaining carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fracturing fluid comprises 0.40-0.50 wt.% foaming agent, 0.46-0.90 wt.% foam stabilizer, 1 wt.% clay stabilizer and 97.60-98.14 wt.% water.Preferred Components: The foaming agent adopts sodium alpha-olefin sulfonate, sodium lauryl sulfate, sodium dodecyl sulfonate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate or sodium lauryl polyoxyethylene ether sulfate. The foam stabilizer adopts diethanolamine laurate, alkyl glycoside, dodecylamine polyoxyethylene ether, dodecylphenol polyoxyethylene ether or lauryl alcohol polyoxyethylene ether. The clay stabilizer adopts sodium chloride and/or potassium chloride. The mass ratio of sodium chloride and potassium chloride in the mixture is 0:1 or 1:1.Preferred Method: In the step (3), carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the bubbler at a rate of 5 l/minute for 10 minutes. In the step (4), lifting the gas outlet port of the gas delivery hose to 1 cm above the liquid level of the base liquid, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the bubbler at a speed of 10 l/minute for 5 minutes. In the step (5), carbon dioxide gas is continuously introduced into the bubbler at a speed of 15 l/minute, and the agitator is turned on to stir at a rotational speed of 7000-9000 revolutions/minute for 1-3 minutes to bubble. The low damage carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid, under normal temperature and pressure, the foam half-life is 1320-1570 seconds, and the foam quality is 65-72%.

PN US2022080353-A1
TI Converting carbonaceous feedstock to syngas-fermentation product involves introducing carbonaceous feedstock and oxidant to gasifier under suitable gasification conditions to produce raw syngas stream comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
AU HOHMAN J
   WINTER J D
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2022397163
AB    NOVELTY - Converting a carbonaceous feedstock involves introducing a carbonaceous feedstock and an oxidant to a gasifier under suitable gasification conditions to produce a raw syngas stream comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide. A portion of raw syngas stream is feed to a syngas-cleanup unit to produce an intermediate syngas stream. A portion of raw syngas stream and/or a portion of intermediate syngas stream feds to remove at least some of carbon dioxide and produce a conditioned syngas stream. A portion of conditioned syngas stream feeds to a fermentor under suitable fermentation conditions and in the presence of suitable microorganisms and nutrients to biologically convert one or more of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide to a fermentation product and generate a product stream comprising fermentation product and a tail gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and unconverted carbon monoxide or hydrogen followed by recycling a first portion.
   USE - Method of converting a carbonaceous feedstock to a syngas-fermentation product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables converting a carbonaceous feedstock, utilizes a renewable resource to produce syngas because of the rising economic, environmental, and social costs associated with fossil resources, and improves process efficiency and better overall biorefinery economics for conversion of biomass to ethanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting a carbonaceous feedstock involves introducing a carbonaceous feedstock and an oxidant to a gasifier under suitable gasification conditions to produce a raw syngas stream comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide. A portion of raw syngas stream is feed to a syngas-cleanup unit to produce an intermediate syngas stream. A portion of raw syngas stream and/or a portion of intermediate syngas stream feds to remove at least some of carbon dioxide and produce a conditioned syngas stream. A portion of conditioned syngas stream feeds to a fermentor under suitable fermentation conditions and in the presence of suitable microorganisms and nutrients to biologically convert one or more of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide to a fermentation product and generate a product stream comprising fermentation product and a tail gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and unconverted carbon monoxide or hydrogen followed by recycling a first portion of the tail gas to the fermentor and second portion of the tail gas to the acid-gas removal unit or both.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonaceous feedstock comprises biomass, and/or fossil fuels. The no tail gas is recycled to the fermentor and acid-gas removal unit for a period of time. The oxidant comprises air, oxygen, and steam. The gasifier is a fluidized-bed gasifier, bubbling fluidized bed gasifier, a circulating fluidized bed gasifier, a fixed bed gasifier, an entrained-flow slagging gasifier, entrained flow non-slagging gasifier, or a transport gasifier. The acid-gas removal unit additionally removes some hydrogen sulfide. The total conversion of carbon monoxide and hydrogen is five percentage points higher than the total conversion of carbon monoxide and hydrogen that is attained in a comparable method without recycling at least a first portion of the tail gas to the fermentor and without recycling at least a second portion of the tail gas to the acid-gas removal unit. Preferred Methods: The method further involves compressing the first portion of the tail gas recycled to the fermentor, the second portion of the tail gas recycled to the acid-gas removal unit, or both to increase the pressure within fermentor and increase the amount of syngas entering the liquid phase for bioconversion, reformer arranged in gasifier and acid-gas removal step, and recycling cells from fermentor back to fermentor, gasifier, or both.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate by using natural calcium carbonate comprises decomposing natural calcium carbonate mineral into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide by calcining at high temperature; introducing carbon dioxide into ammonium carbonate reactor, condensing the carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed with more ammonia, and reacting to generate ammonium carbonate solution and nitrogen mixed with a small amount of ammonia; reacting calcium oxide and ammonium chloride solution, blowing and removing ammonia by nitrogen, obtaining calcium chloride solution and nitrogen mixed with more ammonia, where a portion of the nitrogen is returned to the second step for preparing ammonium carbonate, and the other part of nitrogen is involved in calcium carbonate reaction; introducing the nitrogen to the calcium chloride solution at high speed, adding additive, adding ammonium carbonate solution under stirring condition, and reacting to prepare calcium carbonate slurry and nitrogen mixed with a small amount of ammonia; returning nitrogen gas generated in the fourth step and nitrogen gas obtained in the second step to the third step together to blow off the ammonia; and filtering calcium carbonate slurry to obtain nano-grade calcium carbonate and ammonium chloride solution, and returning the ammonium chloride solution to the third step to realize recycling.

PN CN111180835-A
TI Performing carbon di-oxide hydrogen-based power generation process based on zinc or aluminum air battery system involves introducing carbon dioxide gas into cathode of battery, and easily dissolving carbon dioxide gas in solution.
AU BU Y
   LI F
   BU J
AE NANJING ERXIANGBO NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202047689V
AB    NOVELTY - Performing carbon di-oxide hydrogen-based power generation process based on a zinc or aluminum air battery system involves introducing carbon dioxide gas into the cathode of the battery. The carbon dioxide gas is easily dissolved in the solution to form carbonic acid. The hydrogen protons are enriched on the surface of the electrode catalyst on the cathode side of the battery system to combine with the electrons transferred from the anode side to generate hydrogen gas. The hydrogen gas is automatically discharged from the solution. The consumed hydrogen is continue to break this chemical balance to promote More carbon dioxide dissolved on the anode side of the battery system, metallic zinc or metallic aluminum is used as the source of proton electrons.
   USE - Method for performing carbon di-oxide hydrogen-based power generation process based on zinc or aluminum air battery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon di-oxide hydrogen-based power generation method is used to consume carbon di-oxide at the cathode and produce a large amount of hydrogen while still providing electrical energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing carbon di-oxide hydrogen-based power generation process based on a zinc or aluminum air battery system involves introducing carbon dioxide gas into the cathode of the battery. The carbon dioxide gas is easily dissolved in the solution to form carbonic acid. The hydrogen protons are enriched on the surface of the electrode catalyst on the cathode side of the battery system to combine with the electrons transferred from the anode side to generate hydrogen gas. The hydrogen gas is automatically discharged from the solution. The consumed hydrogen is continue to break this chemical balance to promote More carbon dioxide dissolved on the anode side of the battery system, metallic zinc or metallic aluminum is used as the source of proton electrons. When E0 is (-)1.25 volt, zinc is reacted with 4 mole hydroxide ion to form zinc hydroxide ion and release 2 electron. When E0 is (-)2.31 volt, aluminum is reacted with 3 mole hydroxide ion to form aluminum hydroxide ion and release 3 electron. When E0 is 0.00 volt, aluminum is reacted with 2 mole hydroxide ion to form aluminum hydroxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution is seawater, deionized water or potassium hydroxide solution. The cathode and anode of the battery system adopt a variable-size dual-chamber structure, which can be changed according to the user's generation of electric energy or hydrogen gas. Preferred Conditions: The cathode and anode of the battery system are respectively injected with different kinds of alkaline solutions. The anode of the battery system is equipped with metal zinc sheets or aluminum sheets. The cathode is inserted with a special catalyst electrode, and the special catalyst electrode is specifically a platinum/carbon electrode or a perovskite oxide electrode.

PN CN110205238-A
TI Apparatus for converting hydrogen and carbon            dioxide into acetic acid, comprises sealed electrolytic            cell, anode electrode, cathode electrode, external DC            power source, intake and exhaust pipe, and deoxidized            medium in electrolytic cell.
AU JIANG H
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIN-C)
GA 201979179H
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a sealed electrolytic cell                (1), an anode electrode (2), a cathode electrode,                an external DC power source, an intake pipe and an                exhaust pipe, and a deoxidized medium in the                electrolytic cell. The anode electrode and the                cathode electrode (4) are placed in the                electrolytic cell, and are respectively connected                to the high potential end and the low potential end                of the external DC power source through the wires.                The intake pipe (6) and exhaust pipes(8) are                connected to the inside of the electrolytic                cell.
   USE - Apparatus for converting hydrogen and carbon                dioxide into acetic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for converting hydrogen and carbon dioxide into                acetic acid, which involves:
   (A) connecting the electroactive anaerobic                methane nutrient archaea and methanogens to an                electrolytic cell containing a deoxygenated medium,                and sodium acetate is added to culture the                electroactive anaerobic methane nutrient                archaea;
   (B) introducing mixed gas of hydrogen and                carbon dioxide into the electrolytic cell through                the intake pipe, and the volume ratio of hydrogen                and carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 80:20, and                the methanogenic bacteria are cultured;
   (C) stopping the sodium acetate when the                electroactive anaerobic methane nutrient archaea                and the methanogen culture are completed;
   (D) adding oxidation state 2,6-disulfonate                or/and ferric ion iron to the electrolytic cell, so                that the voltage of the external DC power source 7                is set to -0.3-2.0V; and
   (E) introducing methanogens convert hydrogen                and carbon dioxide into the cell into methane under                the mediated oxidation of                anthraquinone-2,6-disulfonate or/and ferric ion,                oxidizing methane by electroactive anaerobic                methane nutrient archaea, and producing acetic                acid, carbon dioxide, reduced                ruthenium-2,6-disulfonate and/or ferric ion, and                protons, then the reduced                anthraquinone-2,6-disulfonate and/or ferric ion are                oxidized at the anode electrode to generate                electrons, and the generated electrons migrate to                the cathode electrode under the applied voltage to                combine with protons to form hydrogen, and the                methanogens again hydrogen and carbon dioxide                synthesizes methane, which is oxidized by                electroactive anaerobic methane bacterium to                produce acetic acid, thus circulating to produce                acetic acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                apparatus.
   Sealed electrolytic cell (1)
   Anode electrode (2)
   Cathode electrode (4)
   Intake pipe (6)
   Exhaust pipes (8)

The                  methanogens are Methanococcus maripaludis. PN PL422196-A1; PL240481-B1
TI Obtaining bioethanol, feed yeast and carbon dioxide using vegetable biomass hydrolysis, comprises e.g. separating carbohydrates in initial hydrolysis of hemicelluloses and after dissolution of simple carbohydrates contained in biomass.
AU FOSS E
   KOSECKA W
AE KOSECKA W (KOSE-Individual)
GA 201913888Q
AB    NOVELTY - Waste-free method for obtaining bioethanol, feed yeast and carbon dioxide using vegetable biomass hydrolysis, comprises e.g.: separating carbohydrates in the initial hydrolysis of hemicelluloses and after dissolution of simple carbohydrates contained in the biomass, hydrolyzing the remaining mass by the alkaline method; maximum hydrolysis of cellulose to simple sugars and together with previously separated sugar solution by acidic method, subjecting it to alcoholic fermentation, and transferring the decoction after ethanol distillation, to yeast culture; and after the cultivation, separating the yeast, and purifying the solution by acidifying with ortho-phosphoric acid and then making alkaline with limestone milk, separating the precipitate, and acidifying the solution with carbon dioxide from ethanol fermentation or yeasting.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining bioethanol, feed yeast and carbon dioxide using vegetable biomass hydrolysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Waste-free method for obtaining bioethanol, feed yeast and carbon dioxide using vegetable biomass hydrolysis, comprises: separating carbohydrates in the initial hydrolysis of hemicelluloses and after dissolution of simple carbohydrates contained in the biomass, hydrolyzing the remaining mass by the alkaline method; maximum hydrolysis of cellulose to simple sugars and together with previously separated sugar solution by acidic method, subjecting it to alcoholic fermentation, and transferring the decoction after ethanol distillation, to yeast culture; after the cultivation, separating the yeast, and purifying the solution by acidifying with ortho-phosphoric acid and then making alkaline with limestone milk, separating the precipitate, and acidifying the solution with carbon dioxide from ethanol fermentation or yeasting; and separating the precipitate, adding the mass after acid hydrolysis which is used as fertilizer lime, and using the solution obtained at the same time for the preparation of raw materials and in the cooling node. The system for producing bioethanol, feed yeast and carbon dioxide, comprises a junction node connected through the hydrolysis node I with the filtration unit I through the ethanol fermentation block and a distillation block which through the yeast culture block and further the yeast filtration and drying block is connected to the filtrate and wastewater purification unit at the outlet connected to the product IV block. The filtration block I is connected to the hydrolysis node II at the outlet and through the filtration block II with the neutralization block connected to the ethanol fermentation block and has a scrubber block connected at the entrances with blocks and outputs with blocks and carbon dioxide condensing node.

PN CN108085226-A
TI Production of organic health fruit wine comprises interplanting plants with anthelmintic and insecticidal effects into orchards, carrying out orchard management and organic cultivation, and mixing fruit juices, plant peptides and base wines.
AU WANG Y
   LIANG H
AE YANTAI ZHIQI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018434589
AB    NOVELTY - Production of organic health fruit wine comprises interplanting plants with anthelmintic and insecticidal effects into orchards, carrying out orchard management and organic cultivation, and mixing fruit juices, plant peptides and base wines at mass ratio of 1-80:0.5-30:20-98 to obtain organic health wine. The preparation of fruit juice comprises harvesting fresh, healthy and disease-free organic fruits in orchards and storing harvested fruits at 0-5 degrees C; adding 60-120 mg/kg sulfur dioxide, crushing, taking out juice, filling obtained juice into containers, and filling with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to exhaust air oxidation; adding 20-50 mg/L enzymes into juice, controlling temperature at 5-8 degrees C, adding clarifying agent, precipitating for 24-48 hours and filling nitrogen or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen; and storing juice at 0-55 degrees C, capping with nitrogen or carbon dioxide, and filling with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to protect against oxidation.
   USE - Method for producing organic health fruit wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has insect killing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of organic health fruit wine comprises interplanting plants with anthelmintic and insecticidal effects into orchards, carrying out orchard management and organic cultivation, and mixing fruit juices, plant peptides and base wines at mass ratio of 1-80:0.5-30:20-98 to obtain organic health wine. The insecticidal plants is garlic, lavender, Geranium, pitcher plant, insect-eater, and leaf. The base wine is more than 2 brewing alcohol, distilled wine, compound wine and/or edible wine. The preparation of fruit juice comprises harvesting fresh, healthy and disease-free organic fruits in orchards and storing harvested fruits at 0-5 degrees C; adding 60-120 mg/kg sulfur dioxide, crushing, taking out juice, filling obtained juice into containers, and filling with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to exhaust air oxidation; adding 20-50 mg/L enzymes into juice, controlling temperature at 5-8 degrees C, adding clarifying agent, precipitating for 24-48 hours and filling nitrogen or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen; and storing juice at 0-55 degrees C, capping with nitrogen or carbon dioxide, and filling with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to protect against oxidation. The enzyme is cellulase, hemicellulase, pectinase and/or protease. The clarifying agent is gelatin, bentonite, polyvinylpolypyrrolidone (PVPP), and/or egg white.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The orchard fertilizer is organic fertilizer, organic-inorganic fertilizer and/or bio-organic fertilizer. The orchard pesticide is lime sulfur mixture, Bordeaux mixture, mineral oil, Trichoderma harzianum, quinolinone-carboxamide suspending agent, alcohol, soda, Melia azedarach, rotenone, Pyrethrum, miscible oil and/or matrine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The preparation of plant peptides comprises crushing dried plants into 80-200 meshes sieve, placing into ultrasonic container, adding plant powder with water at mass ratio of 1:5-15, and extracting for 1 time at 20-70 degrees C for 10-40 minutes under 2.0-10 pH; adding 0.2-2% enzyme into obtained extract, and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis for 3-12 hours to obtain hydrolyzate; heating enzymatic hydrolysate at 70-150 degrees C for 5-20 minutes to inactivate the enzyme; sterilizing inactivated enzyme product through 0.20-0.45 mu m membrane devices to obtain clear vegetable peptide solution; and freeze-drying vegetable peptide solution, subjecting into centrifugal separation, pressure filtration separation and filtration separation. The preparation of ecological seaweed biostimulating hormone comprises crushing washed seaweed, adding crushed seaweed material and water into stirring tank at mass ratio of 5-30:70-95 and stirring at 50-100 degrees C for 1-12 hours to obtain saccharified seaweed; crushing obtained saccharified seaweed and filtering seaweed filtrate to obtain seaweed residue; adding seaweed residue, urea, magnesium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and glucose into sterilized fermenter at mass ratio of 95.5:1:0.5:1:1:1, mixing, adjusting water content into 50-70% to obtain mixed medium, adding Candida utilis and mixed medium at mass ratio of 0.2-2:98-99.8, fermenting at 25-35 degrees C under 0.1-0.5 mg/L oxygen content for 2-7 days, and sterilizing at 70-150 degrees C for 20-90 minutes to obtain seaweed fermented material; adding seaweed filtrate into seaweed fermented material, stirring and mixing to obtain mixture, adding Corynebacterium ammoniagenes strain at mass ratio of 96-99.5:0.5-4, fermenting at 25-35 degrees C under 1-5 mg/L oxygen content for 2-7 days, subjecting obtained filtrate under pressure filtration to obtain ecological seaweed liquid biostimulating hormone; and concentrating ecological seaweed liquid biostimulating hormone, and spray drying to obtain ecological seaweed biostimulating hormone. The preparation of Corynebacterium ammoniagenes comprises inoculating 10% strain into liquid medium, culturing under logarithmic growth phase at 30-160 degrees C for 30 hours, and centrifuging under 8000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 10 minutes to obtain expanded culture strain; freezing expanded re-cultured strain at - 20 degrees C for 4 hours by placing into refrigerator and taking out and settling at room temperature. The preparation of liquid medium comprises washing and chopping 550 g lean beef, soaking into 1375 ml water for 1 night, filtering, and sterilizing filtrate for 0.6 minutes to obtain 0.6 kg/cm2 liquid medium. Preferred Components: The enzyme is cellulase, hemicellulase, pectinase, and/or protease. The liquid medium with 7.2-7.4 pH comprises 1.0 L beef leachate, 10.0 g peptone, 5.0 g sodium chloride and 20.0-25.0 g agar. The organic healthcare fruit wine comprises cellulase, hemicellulase, pectinase and protease at mass ratio of 2:1:1:2.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide; Fertilizer.

TI Culture solution adjusting device comprises flow volume adjustment portions which adjust flow volume of oxygen, carbon dioxide and inert gas, mixing portion, storage portion, oxygen concentration sensor, pH sensor and flow-control portion.
AU ITO M
AE CKD CORP (CKDC-C)
GA 2018382492
AB    NOVELTY - A culture solution adjusting device (10) comprises 1st, 2nd and 3rd flow volume adjustment portions which adjust flow volume of oxygen, carbon dioxide and inert gas, respectively, mixing portion (15) which mixes oxygen, carbon dioxide and inert gas, storage portion (20) which supplies mixed gas which flows out of mixing portion and stores culture solution, oxygen concentration sensor which detects dissolved oxygen concentration of culture solution, pH sensor which detects pH of culture solution, flow rate set portion, and flow-control portion which controls flow volume adjustment portions.
   USE - A culture solution adjusting device.
   ADVANTAGE - Device easily adjusts property of culture solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A culture solution adjusting device (10) comprises 1st flow volume adjustment portion (12) which adjusts flow volume of oxygen supplied with specified pressure, 2nd flow volume adjustment portion (13) which adjusts flow volume of carbon dioxide supplied with specified pressure, 3rd flow volume adjustment portion (14) which adjusts flow volume of inert gas supplied with specified pressure, mixing portion (15) which mixes oxygen, carbon dioxide and inert gas, storage portion (20) which supplies mixed gas which flows out of mixing portion and stores culture solution, oxygen concentration sensor (21) which detects dissolved oxygen concentration of culture solution, pH sensor (22) which detects pH of culture solution, flow rate set portion which subtracts flow volume of oxygen from predetermined flow volume, and flow volume of carbon dioxide, and sets flow volume of inert gas, flow-control portion which controls 1st flow volume adjustment portion, 2nd flow volume adjustment portion and 3rd flow volume adjustment portion to become flow volume of oxygen, flow volume of carbon dioxide, and flow volume of inert gas. Flow volume of oxygen is set based on target value of dissolved oxygen concentration detected by oxygen concentration sensor and dissolved oxygen concentration. Flow volume of carbon dioxide is set based on target value of pH detected by pH sensor, and pH.

PN WO2022187729-A1
TI Sorbent polymer composite product used for            adsorption for direct air capture of carbon dioxide,            comprises composite of sorbent and flexible porous            polymer having flexibility, comprising            adsorptive/desorptive configuration in which sorbent            polymer is arranged to adsorb/remove component of feed            stream.
AU CULLY E H
   SCOTTI C M
AE GORE & ASSOC INC W L (GORE-C)
GA 2022B5912M
AB    NOVELTY - Sorbent polymer composite product comprises a                composite of a sorbent and a flexible porous                polymer having a flexibility. The sorbent polymer                composite product has an adsorptive configuration                in which the sorbent polymer composite product is                arranged to adsorb components of a feed stream, and                a desorptive configuration in which the sorbent                polymer composite product is arranged to remove the                components from the sorbent polymer composite                product. The flexibility of the flexible porous                polymer facilitates a transfiguration between the                adsorptive configuration and the desorptive                configuration.
   USE - The composition is used for adsorption for                direct air capture (DAC) of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is efficient, maximizes amount                of carbon dioxide removed from atmosphere while                minimizing energy required in the process, has                ability to efficiently cycle between adsorbing and                desorbing states, has capability of volume                reduction (folding, rolling, and spooling) result                in reduction in energy usage and economic, allows                reduction in storage space at the carbon dioxide                capture site, inventory space at the manufacturing                site and shipping and packaging costs by minimizing                volume of an air contactor or module, and requires                one technician to perform same tasks that once took                multiple technicians.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of using the sorbent polymer composite product                comprising providing the sorbent polymer composite                product comprising a porous composite portion                comprising a sorbent and a flexible porous polymer,                exposing the sorbent polymer composite product in a                first configuration to a feed stream containing                carbon dioxide, adsorbing at least a portion of the                carbon dioxide onto the sorbent while the sorbent                polymer composite product is in the first                configuration, positioning the sorbent polymer                composite product into a second configuration after                the adsorbing step, and desorbing the carbon                dioxide from the sorbent polymer composite product                while the sorbent polymer composite product is in                the second configuration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feed stream                  comprises carbon dioxide. The feed stream further                  contains steam or heat.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The sorbent polymer                  composite product further comprises a carrier.                  The sorbent polymer composite product is                  substantially laminar in the adsorptive                  configuration and substantially cylindrical in                  the desorptive configuration. The sorbent polymer                  composite product comprises an extended                  arrangement in the adsorptive configuration; and                  a compressed arrangement in the desorptive                  configuration. The sorbent polymer composite                  product is substantially unfolded in the                  adsorptive configuration and substantially folded                  in the desorptive configuration. The sorbent                  polymer composite product further comprises a                  non-porous portion that lacks the sorbent, where                  the non-porous portion is coupled to the                  composite. The non-porous portion is coupled at                  an outermost end of the composite. When the                  sorbent polymer composite product is in the                  desorptive configuration, the porous polymer of                  the composite is temporarily covered by the                  non-porous portion. The sorbent polymer composite                  product further includes an end-sealing region                  that protects the sorbent. Preferred Method: Once                  an adsorption capacity or adsorption equilibrium                  of the sorbent polymer composite product has been                  reached, the sorbent polymer composite product                  transitions from the adsorptive configuration to                  the desorptive configuration. The sorbent polymer                  composite product returns from the desorptive                  configuration to the adsorptive configuration.                  The method further comprises maintaining the                  sorbent polymer composite product in the first                  configuration until the sorbent reaches a carbon                  dioxide capacity or equilibrium, wherein the                  positioning step occurs once the carbon dioxide                  capacity or equilibrium has been reached. The                  providing step further comprises coupling a                  non-porous portion having a flexible polymer to                  the flexible porous polymer of the porous                  composite portion, the exposing step further                  includes positioning the sorbent polymer                  composite product into a substantially laminar                  form, and the positioning step further includes                  positioning the sorbent polymer composite product                  into a substantially cylindrical form with the                  porous composite portion concealed by the                  non-porous portion. The desorbing step comprises                  injecting water vapor into a center of the                  sorbent polymer composite product in the second                  configuration and collecting at least some of the                  carbon dioxide. The method further comprises                  returning the sorbent polymer composite product                  from the second configuration to the first                  configuration subsequent to the desorbing step.                  The method further comprises rotating the sorbent                  polymer composite product along a path having a                  first portion and a second portion, where during                  the exposing step with the sorbent polymer                  composite product in the first configuration, a                  portion of the sorbent polymer composite product                  is positioned in the first portion of the path,                  and during the positioning step with the sorbent                  polymer composite product in the second                  configuration, a portion of the sorbent polymer                  composite product is positioned on the second                  portion of the path. The rotating step results in                  a reduced volume occupied by the sorbent polymer                  composite product. The desorbing step further                  includes submerging the porous composite portion                  in the second configuration in a substance to                  desorb the carbon dioxide. The substance is water                  or water vapor. The method further comprises                  collecting the extracted carbon dioxide                  subsequent to the desorbing step. The rotating                  step is performed continuously such that the                  sorbent polymer composite product continuously                  transitions between the first configuration and                  the second configuration. The exposing step                  includes positioning the sorbent polymer                  composite product in an extended configuration,                  and the positioning step includes positioning the                  sorbent polymer composite product in a compressed                  configuration. The desorbing step further                  includes submerging the sorbent polymer composite                  product in the second configuration in a                  substance that desorbs the carbon dioxide. The                  method further comprises moving the sorbent                  polymer composite product to substantially remove                  any liquid droplets. Preferred Property: A height                  of the sorbent polymer composite product is                  greater in the extended configuration than a                  height of the sorbent polymer composite product                  in the compressed configuration.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2022075351-A1; TW202229169-A
TI Removal of oxygen molecules used in purification            of carbon monoxide, involves heating platinum catalyst            while supplying and passing inert gas to container,            removing moisture contained in catalyst, contacting            carbon monoxide-containing gas with catalyst, heating            and removing oxygen molecule from gas.
AU DAICHI K
   JUN D
   KAZUNARI K
   KAWAUCHI D
   DOU J
   KAGA K
AE SHOWA DENKO KK (SHOW-C)
GA 202250942R
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of oxygen molecules involves placing a                platinum catalyst (18) containing platinum in a                heat treatment container in which pressure is kept                below atmospheric pressure, heating the platinum                catalyst in the heat-treated container while                supplying and passing the inert gas to the                container, removing moisture contained in the                platinum catalyst, until the concentration of water                contained in the inert gas discharged from the heat                treatment container, is 1000 ppm, contacting a                carbon monoxide-containing gas containing 10 vol%                or more carbon monoxide and an oxygen molecule as                an impurity and 1000 vol.% water, with the platinum                catalyst, heating and removing the oxygen molecule                from the carbon monoxide-containing gas to obtain                gas after removing the oxygen molecule having                reduced concentration of the oxygen molecule.
   USE - Removal of oxygen molecules used in                purification method of carbon monoxide (claimed)                used as additive gas in dry etching process during                manufacturing silicon semiconductors of                non-volatile memory such as NAND flash                memory.
   ADVANTAGE - The method easily removes oxygen molecules                mixed in the carbon monoxide-containing gas. The                carbon monoxide has high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for purifying carbon monoxide, which involves                removing carbon dioxide generated in the oxygen                molecule from the gas after removing the oxygen                molecules using a carbon dioxide adsorbent (22),                obtaining a gas (26) after removing carbon dioxide                having a reduced concentration of carbon dioxide,                recovering carbon monoxide from the gas after                removing carbon dioxide by the pressure swing                adsorption method to obtain purified carbon                monoxide having purity of 99.95 vol.% or                more.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                purification treatment apparatus.
   Carbon monoxide gas container (1)
   Desiccant (14)
   Platinum catalyst (18)
   Carbon dioxide adsorbent (22)
   Gas after carbon dioxide removal (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the oxygen molecule                  removal step, the carbon monoxide-containing gas                  is introduced into the reaction tube filled with                  the platinum catalyst, heated and passed through                  the reaction tube, in which the speed at which                  the carbon monoxide-containing gas is introduced                  into the reaction tube is 500-1500/hour or less.                  The method further involves removing water in the                  carbon monoxide-containing gas using a desiccant                  (14) before the oxygen molecule removing step.                  The pressure swing adsorption step is performed                  by adsorbing carbon monoxide in the gas after                  removing carbon dioxide is removed under pressure                  of 0.3-0.9 MPaG, to a pressure swing adsorbent                  containing a monovalent copper compound and                  zeolite, placing the pressure swing adsorbent                  adsorbing carbon monoxide under a pressure of                  -0.08-(-0.01) MPaG to desorb the carbon monoxide                  adsorbed by the pressure swing adsorbent.                  Preferred Components: The concentration of the                  oxygen molecules contained in the carbon                  monoxide-containing gas is less than 2000 volume                  ppm. The carbon monoxide-containing gas further                  contains at least one chosen from hydrogen                  molecules, nitrogen molecules, carbon dioxide,                  argon, helium, and methane. In the oxygen                  molecule removing step, the concentration of the                  oxygen molecules contained in the gas after                  removing the oxygen molecules is reduced to less                  than 1 volume ppm. The inert gas is at least one                  chosen from nitrogen molecules, helium, and                  argon. The desiccant is at least one chosen from                  molecular sieves 3A, molecular sieves 4A, and                  high silica zeolite. The carbon dioxide adsorbent                  is at least one chosen from molecular sieves 4A,                  molecular sieves 13X, activated carbon, and a                  metal oxide. In the carbon dioxide removing step,                  the concentration of the carbon dioxide contained                  in the gas after removing the carbon dioxide is                  reduced to less than 50% ppm. Preferred                  Conditions: The pressure condition of the heat                  treatment is -0.09-(-0.01) MPaG, and the                  temperature condition is 150-300 degrees C.

The                carbon dioxide separation process generates carbon                dioxide and a rest gas. A portion of the rest gas                is recycled by feeding a portion of the rest gas                back into one of the performed processes in the                method of producing hydrogen. The portion of the                rest gas that is recycled is 50%, preferably 80%,                and preferably 90%.

The method                provides lower cost, smaller footprint arid a                higher hydrogen recovery factor. The hydrogen                production increases significantly for all recycle                cases. The method provides ease of separating                carbon dioxide (CO2) by cryogenic cooling, high                hydrogen productivity and low CO2 emission by                recycle of rest gas, and low content of inerts in                the ammonia synthesis loop. The overall                efficiencies and effectiveness of the system are                improved.

TI Reduction of emission of amine e.g. primary amine            to atmosphere, involves capturing carbon dioxide from            gas using absorber to form gas having baseline carbon            dioxide content, and washing gas to remove amines from            gas.
AU RAYER R A V
   GUPTA V
   MOBLEY P D
   TANTHANA J
   ZHOU S
   TOBIESEN F A
   MEJDELL T
   GRIMSTVEDT A
   HAUGEN G
AE RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
GA 2020C83451
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of amine emission to atmosphere                involves introducing a gas comprising carbon                dioxide into an absorber, flowing the gas through                the absorber comprising an absorbent comprising a                water-lean solution comprising less than 50% water                and amine(s) to capture carbon dioxide by the                absorbent and form a gas having a baseline carbon                dioxide content, washing the gas in a wash column                with a wash solution comprising carbonic acid                formed by addition of gaseous carbon dioxide to the                wash solution to remove the amines from the gas,                and exiting the gas having a reduced amine content                from the wash column.
   USE - Reduction of emission of amine chosen from                chosen from primary amines, secondary amines,                diamines, triamines, tetraamines, pentamines,                cyclic amines, cyclic diamines, amine oligomers,                polyamines, alcoholamines, guanidines, and                amidines, preferably chosen from                1,4-diazabicyclo-undec-7-ene,                1,4-diazabicyclo-2,2,2-octane, piperazine,                triethylamine, 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine,                1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene, monoethanolamine,                diethylamine, ethylenediamine, 1,3-diaminopropane,                1,4-diaminobutane, hexamethylenediamine,                1,7-diaminoheptane, diethanolamine,                diisopropylamine, 4-aminopyridine, pentylamine,                hexylamine, heptylamine, octylamine, nonylamine,                decylamine, tert-octylamine, dioctylamine,                dihexylamine, 2-ethyl-1-hexylamine,                2-fluorophenethylamine, 3-fluorophenethylamine,                3,5-difluorobenzylamine,                3-fluoro-N-methylbenzylamine,                4-fluoro-N-methylbenzylamine, imidazole,                benzimidazole, N-methylimidazole,                1-trifluoroacetylimidazole, 1,2,3-triazole, and                1,2,4-triazole to atmosphere (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces amine emission to                atmosphere by enhancing scrubbing efficiency of the                water-wash section, thus reducing amine fugitive                emission and any waste produced from the acid wash                column, with reduced solvent loss and operating                cost.

PN GB2572409-A; WO2019186145-A1
TI Upgrading an input gas for producing input syn gas, involves removing carbon dioxide from input syngas to produce syngas at a variable output flow rat.
AU MKUSHI G H M
AE MKUSHI G H M (MKUS-Individual)
GA 201983098Q
AB    NOVELTY - Upgrading an input gas involves removing carbon dioxide (CO2) from input syngas to produce syngas at a variable output flow rate.
   USE - Method for upgrading an input gas for producing input syn gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to achieve maximum CO2 removal that makes it possible to meet the fuel specification for higher efficiency prime movers (gas engines, gas turbines, fuel cells) instead of steam rankine cycle where the efficiency is in the range of 5-23%, makes it possible to use a low-quality feedstock, generate a low-quality syngas, and significantly and consistently upgrade it to a higher specification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) using a buffer element before delivery of the output syngas to an end user (pre or post CO2 removal), to minimize the variation in the output flowrate to the end user;
   (2) a system for upgrading an input syngas, which comprises a gas scrubber for removing carbon dioxide from the input syngas to produce output syngas at a variable output flowrate, and a buffer element for storing the output syngas before delivery of the output syngas to an end user to minimize the variation in the output flowrate to the end user;
   (3) a method of producing a syngas, which involves heating input fuel comprising renewable fuel of variable calorific value in a gasifier, and producing producer gas; and scrubbing the producer gas to remove carbon dioxide and minimize carbon dioxide in the resulting syngas;
   (4) a system for producing a syngas, which comprises a gasifier for receiving input fuel comprising renewable fuel of variable calorific value and for producing a producer gas, and a gas scrubber for removing carbon dioxide from the producer gas to minimize carbon dioxide in the resulting syngas; and
   (5) a method of delivering a consistent and/or high-quality syngas from a variable and or/low quality input fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The step of removing CO2 comprises maximizing the amount of CO2 removed from the input gas, preferably simultaneously removing any trace hydrogen sulfide from the input syngas. The input syngas is produced from input fuel comprising biomass or sustainable feedstock, preferably waste or refuse derived fuel. A maximal amount of CO2, preferably 85-100% CO2 is removed from the input gas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2028285-B1; WO2019221379-A1
TI Performing carbon dioxide fixation method involves culturing the urea degrading microorganism, and removing microorganism from the urea-degrading microbial culture medium to prepare in vitro polymer material culture medium of microorganism.
AU ROH Y
   KIM Y M
AE UNIV CHONNAM NAT IND FOUND (UCHO-C)
GA 2019839451
AB    NOVELTY - Performing carbon dioxide fixation method involves culturing the urea degrading microorganism. The microorganism are removed from the urea-degrading microbial culture medium to prepare an in vitro polymer material (EPS) culture medium of the microorganism. The in vitro polymeric polymer (EPS) culture of the microorganisms is treated and recated with contaminated water, hence fixed the dissolved carbon dioxide in the contaminated water with calcite. The step of immobilizing the carbon dioxide with calcite is fixed in the form of carbonate minerals by the carbonate ions contained in the in vitro polymer material (EPS) culture medium. The carbonate ions contained in the contaminated water reacted with calcium ions in the contaminated water, and the polymer material promotes carbonate mineralization.
   USE - Method for performing carbon dioxide fixation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform carbon dioxide fixation, that makes it possible to precipitate crystalline calcite in a short time using a simple method using an extracorporeal polymer (EPS) of microorganisms, and to fix the dissolved carbon dioxide in an environmentally friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a microcracks repair method using in vitro polymer material of microorganisms, which involves:
   (A) culturing the urea degrading microorganism;
   (B) removing the microorganism from the urea-degrading microbial culture medium to prepare an in vitro polymer material (EPS) culture medium of the microorganism; and
   (C) reacting the extracorporeal polymeric material (EPS) culture medium and calcium ion solution of the microorganism by dropping it continuously in a microcrack of concrete or rock, hence filling calcite in the microcrack, and step of filling the calcite in the microcracks is that the carbonate ions containing in the microbial in vitro polymer material (EPS) culture medium reacting with calcium ions to precipitate crystalline calcite of micrometer size, and using the in vitro polymeric material of the microorganism is used for carbonate mineralization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Process: The method for cultivating of urea-decomposing microorganism involves collecting a rock or precipitate formed with a carbonate mineral of biological origin in the natural environment, and culturing aerobic three days or more in a basic microorganism growth medium containing urea (pH 8-9). Preferred Components: The urea-degrading microorganism is one selected from the group consisting of Proteus mirabilis, Marinobacterium coralli, and Sporosarcina pasteurii. The basic microorganism growth medium containing the urea is characterized in that the D-1 medium, urea medium (saline) or saline (saline) medium. The calcium ion is selected from the group consisting of calcium acetate, calcium lactate, calcium chloride or calcium formate. Preferred Conditions: The extracorporeal polymeric material (EPS) culture of the microorganism is obtained by centrifuging the urea-degrading microbial culture and filtering the supernatant with a 0.2-0.45 micrometer filter. The method for fixing carbon dioxide using the in vitro polymer material of the microorganism, further involves adding calcium ions to the contaminated water. The manufactured calcite has a rhombohedral shape having a size of 5-20 micrometer. The microcracks are microcracks having a width of 100 micrometer to 2 mm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN110115709-A
TI Chitosan microsphere capable of double crosslinking by carbon dioxide and photopolymerization, used for performing sustained drug release, has carbon dioxide stimulating responsiveness.
AU HE Y
   GUO Y
   NIE J
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2019716037
AB    NOVELTY - Chitosan microsphere has carbon dioxide stimulating responsiveness, is liquid before being introduced into carbon dioxide, and can be converted into a semi-solid half-liquid gel state after being introduced into carbon dioxide.
   USE - Chitosan microsphere capable of double crosslinking by carbon dioxide and photopolymerization, used for performing sustained drug release (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chitosan microsphere has high modulus and hardness, and are non-toxic and have good biocompatibility, are safe, low in cost, simple in post-treatment, and uniform in particle size. The introduction of photopolymerization further increases the hardness and modulus of microspheres. The carbon dioxide used in the ball forming method is a truly mild and green non-polluting safety gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the chitosan microsphere, which involves:
   (A) dissolving the chitosan and amino protection reagents to DMF and reacting under nitrogen protection, adding a large amount of ice water to precipitate, and subjecting the product to suction filtration, washing and drying;
   (B) adding polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether to a three-necked bottle, and then adding triphenyl phosphite to react with an activating reagent, reacting, cooling at room temperature;
   (C) dissolving the obtained product in toluene, adding with a solvent to precipitate, suction filtering, and drying;
   (D) dissolving the amino-protected chitosan in DMF, and then reacting the catalyst Ag2o and activated polyethylene glycol, adding the deprotection reagent and deionized water continuously, and continuing the reaction, cooling at room temperature, and subjecting the product to suction filtration, followed by dialysis, rotary evaporation, and vacuum drying;
   (E) dissolving the product in water, adjusting the pH, adding glycidyl methacrylate, placing the product in a dialysis bag for dialysis, and lyophilizing;
   (F) taking the selected equipment as a dual-channel microinjection pump;
   (G) dissolving the product in water, adding an initiator; and
   (H) injecting the aqueous product solution into a solvent containing CO2 by a two-channel microinjection pump, and collecting the obtained microspheres and irradiating under a mercury lamp, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Chitosan microsphere preparation involves adding phthalic anhydride as amino protecting reagent. The activating reagent is ethoxyl polyethylene glycol-iodine. The de-protecting reagent is hydrazine hydrate. The initiator is photoinitiator 2959 or photoinitiator 1173. The solvent is hexane, octane or petroleum ether. Preferred Conditions: The reaction time is 14-19 hours, preferably 15-17 hours. Further, the reaction time is 13-18 hours, preferably 14-16 hours. The pH is adjusted to 7-11, preferably 8-9. Further, the reaction time is 7-12 hours, preferably 9-11 hours. The light intensity is 30-100 mW/cm2, and illumination time is 20-40 minutes, preferably 25-35 minutes.

TI Desulfurization device for purifying carbon dioxide, comprises compressor, adsorption bed module, electric heater, cooler, desulfurization tower, centrifuge, regeneration tank and solution tank.
AU JIN X
   XIA Z
   ZHANG Q
   SUN M
   ZHOU T
   LIU B
   LIU T
AE KUNSHAN JINHONG CARBON CO LTD (KUNS-Non-standard)
GA 201853525H
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization device comprises a compressor, an adsorption bed module, an electric heater, a cooler, a desulfurization tower, a centrifuge, a regeneration tank and a solution tank. An air inlet of the compressor is connected to a carbon dioxide feed gas delivery end, where an air outlet of the compressor is connected to an air inlet of the adsorption bed module. An air inlet of the electric heater is externally connected with a regeneration gas, where the outlet port is connected to the regeneration gas inlet of the adsorption bed module. The regeneration gas outlet of the adsorption bed module is connected to the inlet of the cooler, where the outlet of the cooler is connected to the bottom inlet of the desulfurization tower. A circulation pump is disposed in the solution tank, where the outlet line of the circulation pump is connected to the washing liquid inlet of the desulfurization tower.
   USE - Desulfurization device for purifying carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The desulfurization device achieves effect of deep desulfurization, and thus reduces pollution to the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for purifying carbon dioxide, which involves:
   (A) compressing carbon dioxide raw material gas with a compressor;
   (B) passing the compressed carbon dioxide raw material gas into the adsorption bed module for three-bed temperature-temperature adsorption to complete desulfurization of the carbon dioxide raw material gas;
   (C) cooling the regeneration gas of the adsorption bed module by a cooler;
   (D) placing to a desulfurization tower for spray washing;
   (E) removing sulfur-containing component from the regeneration gas;
   (F) discharging desulfurized regeneration gas from the top of the desulfurization tower; and
   (G) passing washing liquid flowing out from the bottom of the desulfurization tower into a centrifuge to remove solid impurities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the desulfurization device.
   Compressor (1)
   Adsorption bed module (2)
   Electric heater (3)
   Cooler (4)
   Desulfurization tower (5)
   Regeneration tank (7)
   Solution tank (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide raw material gas has 100 parts per million sulfur-containing impurities. The electric heater heats the regeneration gas at 350-600 degrees C. The adsorbent in the adsorption bed module is strip-shaped or spherical particles, and has a particle diameter of 2-5 mm. The washing liquid in the desulfurization tower is limestone slurry, calcium-based desulfurization liquid or calcium sodium double-based desulfurization liquid.

TI Composite dehumidifying system, has regenerated            air heated by heat end of trans-critical carbon dioxide            heat pump, and entering regeneration area of dehumiding            rotating wheel to realize regeneration of hygroscopic            agent.

TI Producing inorganic compound using recycled            resources, by mixing desulfurized gypsum and water,            supplying carbon dioxide-containing gas to desulfurized            gypsum slurry, and collecting carbon dioxide through            carbonation reaction, where prepared inorganic compound            is in form of dried solid powder.
AU LEE Y H
   CHUNG Y K
   SUNG D J
   KIM C H
   JUNG H W
   HO L
   CHUNG Y
   SEONGDAE-JIN
   CHEONG H
AE HYUNDAI OILBANK CO LTD (HYUN-Non-standard)
GA 202242957P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing an inorganic compound                using recycled resources, involves mixing                desulfurized gypsum and water to prepare a                desulfurized gypsum slurry, supplying a carbon                dioxide-containing gas to the desulfurized gypsum                slurry, and collecting carbon dioxide through a                carbonation reaction to prepare an inorganic                compound, where the prepared inorganic compound is                in the form of a dried solid powder.
   USE - The method is useful for producing inorganic                compound comprising gypsum and calcium carbonate,                using recycled resources (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes carbonation, maintains the                reaction temperature using the temperature of the                exhaust gas, minimizes the generation of greenhouse                gases, minimizes the process cost, and greatly                improves the basic unit of the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The desulfurized                  gypsum is prepared in a circulating fluidized bed                  combustion boiler, and contains 40-80 wt.%                  calcium oxide and 15-35 wt.% sulfur trioxide. The                  carbon dioxide containing gas is an exhaust gas                  of a circulating fluidized bed combustion boiler.                  Preferred Conditions: The desulfurized gypsum and                  water in the slurry preparation step are mixed in                  a weight ratio of (25-45):(55-75), where an                  additive for adjusting pH is not used in the                  slurry preparation step. The process temperature                  is heat-preserved at 100-150 degrees C using a                  carbon dioxide-containing gas as a heat source in                  the inorganic compound manufacturing step. The                  carbonation reaction occurs by contacting the                  carbon dioxide-containing gas and the                  desulfurized gypsum slurry in a cross flow or a                  co-current flow in the inorganic compound                  production step. The carbonation reaction is                  carried out by contacting the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas and the desulfurized                  gypsum slurry in a cross flow or a co-current                  flow in the inorganic compound production                  step.

PN TW623125-U
TI Handheld breathalyzer i.e. portable hand-held respiration metabolism detector, has carbon dioxide concentration detecting element connected to sampling pipe, and processor connected to flow meter and oxygen and carbon dioxide detecting elements.
AU WU M
AE GENE HUB TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GENE-Non-standard)
GA 202255614Y
AB    NOVELTY - According to the present disclosure, a handheld respiration analyzer includes a sampling tube, a flow meter, an oxygen concentration detector, a carbon dioxide concentration detector and a processor. An air inlet is disposed on one end of the sampling tube, and an air outlet is disposed on the other end of the sampling tube. The flow meter, the oxygen concentration detector and the carbon dioxide concentration detector are respectively communicating with the sampling tube and electrically connected to the processor. With the abovementioned arrangement, the volume of the handheld respiration analyzer is significantly smaller than the conventional respiration analyzing instrument, and various analytical data can also be obtained in a single test. It allows users to take the test anytime and anywhere, which improves the convenience in carrying and metabolic testing.

PT P
PN CN111592190-A
TI Treating carbonate waste water, by adding acid to carbonate system waste water, passing aeration into waste water to react, collecting carbon dioxide, performing hydrolysis reaction, adding flocculant and performing decalcification.
AU GAO W
   HUANG X
   JIANG G
   LIU Y
   TAO Z
   LIAO Y
   MAO C
   MENG Y
AE SCI ENVIRONMENTAL CO LTD (SCEN-Non-standard)
GA 202085777S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating carbonate waste water, involves adding acid to the carbonate system waste water to adjust the pH to less than 4, passing aeration into the waste water to react, collecting the carbon dioxide generated during the reaction, adding biological preparations to the waste water pretreated by acidification, performing compounding reaction under stirring, adding alkali to adjust the pH to 8-9, performing hydrolysis reaction, adding flocculant to precipitate separation to obtain waste residue and supernatant liquid, and passing carbon dioxide decalcification supernatant liquid through the carbon dioxide for decalcification to obtain purified water.
   USE - The method is useful for treating carbonate waste water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high impact resistance, high purification efficiency, stable operation, less neutralizing effect and low operation cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The acid is chosen from nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, and sulfuric acid, and aeration is introduced to react for 15-30 minutes. The mass ratio of the biological agent to the heavy metals in the waste water is 0.1-2:1. The biological agent is prepared by culturing complex flora of Thiobacillus ferrooxidans and T.thiooxidans in 9K medium, adding 10-150 g ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, controlling the temperature during the culture process at 20-40 degrees C, where the pH is 1.5-2.5, mixing the bacterial solution with the iron source, stirring the mixture at 20-40 degrees C for 1-7 hours to obtain a biological agent solution with a mass volume concentration of 100-160 g/l, where mass-volume ratio of iron source and the bacterial solution is 10-85 g:100 ml, the iron source is chosen from ferrous chloride, ferrous sulfate, ferric sulfate, polyferric sulfate, ferric chloride, ferrous nitrate, ferric nitrate, ferrous acetate, and ferrous perchlorate, subjecting the biological agent solution to solid-liquid separation, and drying solid phase at 100-200 degrees C to obtain biological agent. The rotation speed of the stirring is 100-400 rpm. The time of the compounding reaction is 15-30 minutes. The alkali is chosen from sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, calcium oxide, and calcium carbide residue. The hydrolysis reaction time is 10-20 minutes. The solid-liquid mass volume ratio of the flocculant to the waste water pretreated by acidification is 2-70 g: 1 m3. The flocculant is polyaluminum chloride and polyacrylamide. The solid-liquid mass volume ratio of polyaluminum chloride and acidified waste water is 40-60 g:1 m3, and the solid-liquid mass volume ratio of polyacrylamide and acidified waste water It is 3-8 g:1 m3.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Mold for downhole tool or component, comprises grain material, sodium silicate and carbon dioxide reaction product, and graphite.
AU HENG S
   PREDTETCHENSKI D
   VARNER J W

   HALLIBURTON ENERGY SERVICES INC (HALL-C)
GA 202060572P
AB    NOVELTY - Mold comprises a grain material, a sodium silicate and carbon dioxide reaction product, and 0.1-10% graphite.
   USE - Mold for downhole tool or component.
   ADVANTAGE - The mold helps to prevent cracking or breaking of the mold by distributing heat more quickly and uniformly throughout the mold.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for forming mold for downhole tool or component, which involves:
   (A) filling a mold housing with graphite, sodium silicate or grain material mixture including 0.1-10 wt.% graphite:
   (B) shaping the mixture into a pre-mold having the same shape as the mold to be formed; and
   (C) subjecting the pre-mold to an atmosphere having elevated amounts of carbon dioxide as compared to ambient air for a time sufficient to allow the sodium silicate to react with the carbon dioxide to form sufficient reaction products to bind the other pre-mold components and solidify a mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The grain material comprises sand. The mold comprises between 1-10% sodium silicate and carbon dioxide reaction products. The mold comprises kaolin clay, carbon fiber, glass fiber, and their combinations.

TI Preparation of polyionic liquid loaded metal-organic framework for catalyzing carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction, involves mixing material containing chromium terephthalate metal organic framework, reacting, washing, and drying.
AU XU W
   PAN Y
   LIU L
   HU J
   HAN Z
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 2020347107
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyionic liquid loaded metal-organic framework involves adding chromium terephthalate metal organic framework, ionic liquid 1-vinyl-3-hydroxyethylimidazole bromide and p-divinylbenzene to N,N-dimethylformamide, mixing at room temperature, adding azobisisobutyronitrile, reacting at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling, washing with N,N-dimethylformamide and acetone, filtering and drying.
   USE - Preparation of polyionic liquid loaded metal-organic framework for catalyzing carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces metal-organic framework having excellent carbon dioxide cycloaddition catalytic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage method of polyionic liquid loaded metal-organic frame work, which involves adding epoxide, and polyionic liquid-divinyl benzene doped chromium terephthalate metal organic frame work, passing carbon dioxide, heating at 80 degrees C under pressure of 1 MPa, and stirring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chromium salt is chromium nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves mixing chromium salt, terephthalic acid and water, reacting at 200 degrees C for 18 hours, washing the reaction with N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol, filtering, and drying to obtain chromium terephthalate metal organic framework. The method involves boiling the reaction with N,N-dimethylformamide for 3 hours, filtering and drying to obtain chromium terephthalate metal organic framework, boiling chromium terephthalate metal organic framework with ethanol for 3 hours, filtering and drying. The method of ionic liquid 1-vinyl-3-hydroxyethylimidazole bromide involves placing 1-vinylimidazole and 2-bromo ethanol in acetone, reacting at 70 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature, washing with ether, filtering, and drying to obtain ionic liquid 1-vinyl-3-hydroxyethylimidazole bromide. Preferred Conditions: The reaction is carried out under anhydrous and anaerobic conditions, and acetone is initially degassed before use. Preferred Components: The epoxide is propylene oxide. Preferred Compositions: The amount of polyionic liquid-divinyl benzene doped chromium terephthalate metal organic frame work added is 1.5 mg for every 1 mmol epoxide.

   R1-R8=hydrogen, hydroxy and fluorine group; or 1-6C alkyl;
   R1-R4=hydrogen;
   R1-R4'=hydrogen;
   R5-R8=hydrogen;
   R5'R8'=hydrogen; and
   R9, R10 and R11=1-4C alkyl group or atropisomers and salts.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The iron complex of porphyrin is represented by formula (II). Preferred Condition: The reduction is performed with iron at zero oxidation state. The binder is used which comprises conductive materials as additives. The carbon dioxide gas is present only in cathodic compartment at 1-30 bars. The solvent of the electrolyte solutions is selected from dimethylformamide, acetonitrile or water. The solvent is water, the cathodic supporting electrolyte is devoid of buffer, and anodic supporting electrolyte comprises phosphate buffer. Preferred Components: The solvent comprises dimethylformamide and/or acetonitrile, proton donor with pKa value in dimethylformamide of 18 and 31, and water, trifluoroethanol, phenol and acetic acid. Preferred Process: The mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen (syngas) is produced, the pH of the aqueous solution and the potential applied to the cathode are adjusted to set carbon monoxide/hydrogen molar ratio of the produced gas..
