PT P
PN EP4019610-A1; WO2022137139-A1
TI Making hydrocarbons and/or oxygenates, comprises            reforming feed of methane with water and/or oxygen to            get reforming product stream, and contacting            hydrocarbon synthesis mixture of hydrogen and carbon            monoxide with Fischer-Tropsch catalyst.
AU PATERSON A J
AE BP PLC (BRPE-C)
   BP PLC (BRPE-C)
GA 202285070H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrocarbons and/or oxygenates,                comprises: reforming a reforming feed comprising                methane with water and/or oxygen to produce a                reforming product stream comprising carbon monoxide                and hydrogen; and contacting a hydrocarbon                synthesis mixture comprising hydrogen and carbon                monoxide with a Fischer-Tropsch hydrocarbon                synthesis catalyst, where the hydrocarbon synthesis                mixture comprises at least a portion of the                reforming product stream to produce a hydrocarbon                product stream comprising 5C+ hydrocarbons and/or                oxygenates, e.g. with a selectivity for 5C+                hydrocarbons of at least 50% and/or a selectivity                for oxygenates of at least 20%.
   USE - The process is useful for producing                hydrocarbons and/or oxygenates.
   ADVANTAGE - The process efficiently converts carbon                dioxide and hydrogen to methane using a                Fischer-Tropsch catalyst without relying on the                reverse water gas shift reaction and operates at                much lower temperatures than those used for a                reverse water gas shift reaction, this allowing for                lower energy consumption and lower capital cost                associated operating a reverse water gas shift                reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: At least a portion of the                  methane of the reforming feed is produced by a                  process comprising contacting a methane synthesis                  mixture comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  with a supported methane synthesis catalyst to                  form a methane product stream, where the                  supported methane synthesis catalyst comprises                  1-35 wt.% cobalt, to provide the methane product                  stream with a selectivity for methane of at least                  75%. At least 50% (e.g. at least 60%, 70%, 80%,                  90%, 95% or 99%) of the methane provided in the                  reforming feed is produced by the contacting of                  the methane synthesis mixture with the supported                  methane synthesis catalyst. The contacting is                  performed at 150-325 degrees C (e.g. 150-300                  degrees C, or 150-275 degrees C, or 150-250                  degrees C, or 175-325 degrees C, or 175-275                  degrees C, or 175-250 degrees C, or 200-325                  degrees C, or 200-275 degrees C, or 200-250                  degrees C). The selectivity for methane is at                  least 80% (e.g. at least 85%, or at least 90%, or                  at least 95%), and the methane product stream is                  provided with a carbon dioxide conversion of at                  least 5%, e.g. at least 25%. The contacting of                  the hydrocarbon synthesis feed to provide the                  hydrocarbon product stream has a selectivity for                  5C+ hydrocarbons of at least 50 wt.% (e.g. at                  least 60 wt.%, or at least 70 wt.%, or at least                  80 wt.%, or at least 90 wt.%). The hydrocarbon                  product stream comprises oxygenates, and the                  contacting of the hydrocarbon synthesis feed to                  provide the hydrocarbon product stream has an                  oxygenate selectivity of at least 20% (e.g. at                  least 30%, or at least 40%, or at least 50%). The                  hydrocarbon product stream is separated to                  produce a 5C+ product stream and/or an oxygenate                  product steam and a 1-4C hydrocarbon product                  stream. The process further comprises: recycling                  the 1-4C product stream to provide at least a                  portion of the reforming feed; combining the                  hydrocarbon product stream with the methane                  synthesis mixture, where the conversion of the                  methane synthesis mixture to the methane product                  stream is performed in the presence of the                  hydrocarbon products stream, to provide a                  combined product stream, separating the resulting                  combined product stream into a 5C+ product stream                  and/or an oxygenate product stream and a 1-4C                  product stream and recycling the 1-4C product                  steam to provide at least part of the reforming                  feed; and combining the methane product stream                  with the hydrocarbon product to form a combined                  product stream, separating the combined product                  stream into a 5C+ product stream and/or an                  oxygenate product stream, and 1-4C product                  stream, and recycling the 1-4C product stream to                  provide at least part of the reforming feed.                  Preferred Components: The methane synthesis                  mixture comprises no more than 2 wt.% carbon                  monoxide. At least 50 vol.% of the methane                  synthesis mixture is hydrogen, carbon dioxide and                  nitrogen, e.g. at least 60 vol.%, at least 70                  vol.%, at least 80 vol.%, or at least 90 vol.%.                  The supported methane synthesis catalyst further                  comprises 0.5-15 wt.%, preferably 7-12 wt.%                  manganese on an elemental basis. The hydrogen                  comprises green hydrogen (e.g. hydrogen generated                  through electrolysis, where the electrolysis is                  powered, at least in part, by renewable energy),                  and/or the carbon dioxide comprises captured                  carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide from biomass                  gasification.
   EXAMPLE - Several catalysts of 10% cobalt/titanium                dioxide analogous to conventional Fischer-Tropsch                catalysts were prepared by heating 25-150 degrees C                at 2 degrees C/minute, and then heating at 1                degrees C/minute from 150-300 degrees C in a 16                channel reactor with common feed, temperature and                pressure between catalyst channels, with online                analysis for 1-8C. The catalysts were activated at                300 degrees C under a 100% hydrogen at atmospheric                pressure and a 5000 per hour gas hourly space                velocity. The carbon dioxide conversion reaction                was performed at 30 barg with a hydrogen:carbon                dioxide ratio of 2:1, 1:1, 3:1 and 1.8:1                respectively with 51% nitrogen. No carbon monoxide                was present in the feed. Methane was subjected to                purification to remove 2C+ hydrocarbons and also                removed undesirable contaminants. The methane was                then transferred to a steam reformer with an outlet                temperature of 1065 degrees C and pressure of 32                bara. Within the steam reformer, the methane was                contacted with a combination of oxygen and steam to                produce a reformer product stream comprising carbon                monoxide and hydrogen.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E10-J02D; E11-D02; H04-E04; H04-E05; H04-F02E; N02-B01; N03-B01A; N07-B02

PN CN114591382-A
TI Preparing high content hesperidin comprises            crushing raw material, leaching with hot water,            performing membrane filtration to obtain membrane            treatment liquid, concentrating, adding petroleum            ether, benzene and glycerol, fully mixing, diluting            glycerol layer, concentrating desorbed solution.
AU HE A
AE JIANGXI HAIFU BIOTECH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202280643C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high content hesperidin comprises                (i) crushing raw material, leaching with hot water,                performing membrane filtration to obtain membrane                treatment liquid, (ii) concentrating filtered                solution, adding petroleum ether, benzene and                glycerol, fully mixing and adjusting the acid under                high temperature conditions, cooling, extracting                under low temperature conditions, and collecting                glycerol layer, (iii) diluting glycerol layer with                water and adjusting to alkali, applying                chromatographic column, washing with                alcohol-containing dilute brine, desorbing by                high-concentration brine solution, and collecting                the desorbed solution, (iv) concentrating the                desorbed solution, transferring into                low-temperature saturated carbon dioxide atmosphere                for crystallization and (v) washing drying,                pulverizing and sieving to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high                content hesperidin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: The yield of hesperidin is                exceeding 90%, and the purity is &#8805; 99%;                requires low equipment requirements; has simple                operation and strong industrialization                ability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the hot                  water is 80-95&#8451;, the ratio of material                  to liquid is 1:20-50 (kg/l), the extraction is                  performed for 2-5 times and extraction time is                  1-4 hours, preferably, the extraction times are                  3-4 times, and each extraction time is 2-3 hours;                  the ceramic membrane filtration has a molecular                  weight cut-off of 10,000 to 100,000 D and the                  nanofiltration membrane filtration has a                  molecular weight cut-off of 200-400 Daltons. The                  high boiling range of petroleum ether is                  90-120&#8451;, the molar ratio between                  concentrated liquid solid, petroleum ether,                  benzene and glycerol is 1:2-4:0.5-1:10-15. In                  step (ii) high temperature condition is                  50-65&#8451; for 0.5-1 hours with stirring                  and the low temperature is carried out at                  temperature 10&#8451; for 3-5 hours. In step                  (iii) the dilution ratio of the dilution is 15-30                  times, The alkali adjustment is to adjust the pH                  value to 9-13; alkali is sodium hydroxide and/or                  potassium hydroxide, chromatographic column is a                  chromatographic column with anion exchange resin                  as filler, preferably D941, LX-T5, LXD-762 or                  LX-94; anion exchange resin (L) concentrated                  liquid solid is 20-40:1; the ratio of diameter to                  height of anion exchange resin chromatography                  column is 1:3-7; the upper column flow rate is                  0.5-1.5 bv/h. In step (iv), the low temperature                  is 1-10&#8451; and the saturated carbon                  dioxide atmosphere is that the concentrated                  solution is in a sealed carbon dioxide                  environment, i.e. carbon dioxide gas is                  continuously blown into the bottom of the                  concentrated liquid and kept for 1-2 hours, and                  then the carbon dioxide is changed to the top of                  the pressure-maintaining device and injected, and                  the carbon dioxide high pressure state is                  maintained for crystallization, maintaining the                  carbon dioxide high pressure state is maintaining                  the carbon dioxide partial pressure at 0.01-0.05                  MPa. The crystallization is allowed to stand for                  6-12 hours.Preferred Components: The raw material is                  citrus aurantium, aurantium citrus peel,                  tangerine peel or lime in Rutaceae citrus plants;                  The pulverization is carried out to pulverize to                  over 10-80 mesh; the water leaching is performed                  by adding the raw materials into hot water for                  soaking and solid-liquid separation, the membrane                  filtration is performed by first pass through a                  ceramic membrane to collect permeate, and then                  passing the solution filtered through a                  nanofiltration membrane to collect the                  retentate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the acid                  used for the acid adjustment is hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid and/or                  phosphoric acid; the pH value after the acid                  adjustment is 2-6, preferably 3-5.

AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Partially hydrogenating a triple bond comprises partially hydrogenating alkyne raw material using alcohol amine and palladium-containing catalyst to control hydrogenation reaction to obtain olefin.
   USE - The method for hydrogenating triple bond partial or catalyst for partially hydrogenating alkyne raw material to prepare olefin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst in the hydrogenation method has good partial hydrogenation activity and selectivity, can realize partial hydrogenation of carbon-carbon triple bond with high yield, and obtains olefin product. The method introduces carbon dioxide and a small amount of alcohol amine to the reaction system, reduces the occurrence of raw material alkynol dimerization side reaction and improves the selectivity of partial hydrogenation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising dipping the carrier material in palladium salt and auxiliary agent metal salt solution, calcining, and reducing to obtain catalyst precursor, immersing the catalyst precursor in poisoning agent solvent, and poisoning and modifying the catalyst in the presence of a reducing agent to reach the target catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The palladium-containing catalyst includes 84.5-98.8 wt.% carrier, 1-10 wt.% main catalyst metal palladium, 0.1-0.5 wt.% auxiliary metal, 0.1-5 wt.% poisoning agent, where the carrier is calcium carbonate, barium sulfate, aluminum oxide and silicon oxide. The auxiliary metal is elemental tungsten, elemental molybdenum and elemental manganese. The poisoning agent is lead salts, zinc salts and chromium salts. The alcoholamine is triethanolamine, diethanolamine, diisopropanolamine and triisopropanolamine, and the dosage of the alcohol amine is 0.05-0.5 wt.% of the mass of the substrate. The palladium salt is at least one of palladium chloride, palladium nitrate, palladium acetate and dihydrotetrachloro palladium acid and/or the auxiliary agent metal is at least one of molybdenum nitrate, molybdenum acetate, manganese acetate, manganese nitrate and tungsten tetrachloride. The alkyne raw material is methylbutynol, methylpentynol, dehydrolinalool, dihydrodehydrolinalool, dehydronerolidol, 1,4-butynediol and dehydroisophytol.Preferred Method: The hydrogenation method is a batch reaction or a continuous reaction, preferably, the reaction time of the batch reaction is 2-6 hours, the temperature of the reaction is 40-80degrees Celsius, the reaction gas pressure is 0.2-2 MPa, the partial pressure of carbon dioxide accounts for 1-5%, and the rest is hydrogen, and the gas pressure remains stable during the reaction, the amount of the catalyst is 0.5-2 wt.% relative to the mass of the substrate. The partial hydrogenation reaction can be performed without adding a solvent or by adding a solvent, if a solvent is added, the solvent is at least one of methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, acetone and tetrahydrofuran, preferably ethanol.

PN JP2021185862-A
TI New recombinant bacterium derived from thermophilic homoacetic acid bacterium useful for producing acetone, has metabolic pathway that produces acetic acid from carbon source through acetyl-coenzyme A (CoA) as intermediate.
AU NAKASHIMADA Y
   KATO J
   KATO S
   TAKEMURA K
AE UNIV HIROSHIMA (UHIR-C)
GA 2021E7281L
AB    NOVELTY - A recombinant bacterium derived from a thermophilic homoacetic acid bacterium having a metabolic pathway that produces acetic acid from a carbon source through acetyl-coenzyme A (CoA) as an intermediate and metabolism from acetyl-CoA to produce acetic acid by genetic engineering method, is new. The recombinant bacterium has a foreign heat-resistant enzyme that catalyzes a condensation reaction that produces acetoacetyl-CoA from two molecules of acetyl-CoA, in which some of the enzymes involved in the pathway are deleted, a foreign thermostable enzyme that catalyzes the reaction that produces acetoacetic acid from acetoacetyl-CoA and acetic acid, and expresses a foreign thermostable enzyme that catalyzes the decarboxylation reaction that produces acetone from acetoacetic acid.
   USE - The recombinant bacterium is useful for producing acetone (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant bacterium improves production of acetone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing acetone, which involves culturing the recombinant thermophile in the presence of sugar, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Organism: The thermophilic homoacetic acid bacterium is Moorella. The thermophilic homoacetic acid bacterium is characterized by a deficiency of a part of an enzyme that produces acetyl phosphate using acetyl-CoA as a substrate. The thermophilic homoacetic acid bacterium is deficiency of phosphate propanoyltransferase (PduL)-1 or PduL2, which is a phosphoacetyltransferase. Preferred Biomolecules: The foreign thermostable enzyme that produces acetoacetyl-CoA using acetyl-CoA as a substrate is characterized by being a thiolase derived from Caldanaerobacter subterraneus subsp. tengcongensis. The foreign thermostable enzyme that produces acetoacetic acid using acetoacetyl-CoA as a substrate is characterized by being a CoA transferase derived from Thermosipho melanesiensis. The foreign thermostable enzyme that produces acetone using acetoacetic acid as a substrate is characterized by being acetolactate decarboxylase derived from Clostridium acetobutylicum. The recombinant thermophile is deposited as receipt number NITE AP-03217. Preferred Method: The method further involves recovering the produced acetone. The recombinant thermophile is cultured under the condition that at least one of hydrogen and methanol is present in addition to sugar, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide. The method involves culturing the recombinant thermophile at 55-65 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of evaluation of acetone-producing ability of recombinant strain that do not support the claims.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PN CN113620323-A
TI Preparation of flame retardant magnesium hydroxide used for ultra-high engineering plastic composite material for filter by grinding magnesium oxide waste, calcining, acid dissolving, adding ammonium hydroxide, filtering, and drying.
AU ZHANG N
   ZHAO X
   MA X
   XI Z
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 2021D12096
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flame retardant magnesium hydroxide comprises taking magnesium oxide waste, grinding and crushing, putting in crucible, calcining at 600degrees Celsius, reacting carbon in magnesium oxide waste with oxygen in air, generating carbon dioxide, weighing remaining solid, adding residual solid into hydrochloric acid, acid dissolving, immediately reacting magnesium ion (Mg2+) and ferrous ion (Fe2+) in waste, filtering solution, generating precipitate which is the impurity, adding hydrogen peroxide to filtered solution while stirring, changing solution from green to yellow by convert Fe2+ to ferric ion (Fe3+), precipitating, filtering to obtain filtered yellow solid which is Fe3+, adding dropwise ammonium hydroxide to filtered solution to required pH value, generating precipitate which is the mangesium hydroxide, filtering, and drying.
   USE - The method is for preparing flame retardant magnesium hydroxide used for ultra-high engineering plastic composite material for filter by sintering technology (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is green and environment-friendly and recycles silicon steel-grade magnesium oxide to reduce environment pressure.

Acid dissolving is done by water bath stirring at 60degrees Celsius for 5 hours. The pH value of solution is 8-9. The filtered solution is further added with polyethylene glycol in addition to ammonium hydroxide.Preferred Components: The impurity contains silicon dioxide and unreacted carbon.

In which,                the sensor is installed inside the furnace                assembly, connected and controlled by the                controller through the transmission channel. Inside the furnace cluster also takes                place the combustion of fuel gas to catalyze the                reactions to help convert hydrocarbons in the fuel                gas stream, large, this heat will be taken to the                rear of the furnace and used by the heaters for                many different purposes. The control valve is used                to regulate the temperature generated inside the                furnace assembly. The gas collector assembly is                used to collect the syngas coming out of the                furnace assembly to bring to the secondary                reformer.
   USE - The device is useful for optimizing energy and                improves efficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The device optimizing energy and improves                efficiency.

   USE - Method for processing radioactive solid waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing the high-efficiency degradation of the radioactive solid waste, and finally converting it into carbon dioxide and water, thus realizing the effective volume reduction of the radioactive solid waste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by weighing 1-5 activated carbon, weighing 20-30 copper nitrate and 25-35 saturated ferrous nitrate solution and mixing uniformly to obtain a mixed solution, putting the activated carbon into the mixed solution, stirring uniformly and let it stand for 24 hours, putting in an oven to dry for 12 hours, calcining at 300 degrees C for 3 hours in a nitrogen atmosphere, washing and drying, to prepare the catalyst. Preferred Components: The oxidant is hydrogen peroxide. The solvent uses a portion of de-ionized water and rehydration.

GA 202180397K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cross-linked polyvinyl chloride                structural foam material comprises (i) melt-mixing                the polyvinyl chloride resin, modified resin,                isocyanate, acid anhydride, nucleating agent and                heat stabilizer to obtain blank, and (ii) soaking                the blank in the foaming gas to foam to obtain                foaming body, then crosslinking and curing the                foaming body to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cross                linked polyvinyl chloride structure foam                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can adjust composition and ratio                of polyvinyl chloride and other raw materials in a                wide range; adopts carbon dioxide or nitrogen                foaming which can makes the polyvinyl chloride foam                preparation process safer and environmentally                friendly and economical; improves the compatibility                or mutual solubility of polyvinyl chloride and                other raw materials thus provides better material                performance; and can reduces the cost;The foam                material which has high glass transition                temperature and excellent heat resistance                property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                cross linked polyvinyl chloride structure foam                material obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nucleating agent                  is nano-silicon dioxide, carbon black, calcined                  kaolin, molecular sieve, ultrafine aluminum                  silicate, calcium carbonate and/or glass powder.                  The heat stabilizer is calcium stearate, zinc                  stearate, barium stearate, organotin heat                  stabilizer, tribasic lead sulfate, dibasic lead                  phosphite, epoxidized soybean oil and/or epoxy                  resin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The foaming gas                  comprises carbon dioxide or nitrogen. The                  isocyanate is toluene diisocyanate,                  carbodiimide-uretonimine modified                  4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate and/or                  polymethylene polyphenyl polyisocyanate. The acid                  anhydride is phthalic anhydride, maleic                  anhydride, succinic anhydride, tetrahydrophthalic                  anhydride, hexahydrophthalic anhydride,                  methyltetrahydrophthalic anhydride,                  methylhexahydrophthalic anhydride, trimellitic                  anhydride and/or pyromellitic anhydride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The modified resin is                  polymethyl methacrylate, methyl                  methacrylate-butylacrylate copolymer,                  styrene-acrylonitrile copolymer, chlorinated                  polyvinyl chloride, chlorinated polyethylene                  and/or thermoplastic polyurethane. The mass ratio                  between polyvinyl chloride resin, modified resin,                  isocyanate, acid anhydride, nucleating agent and                  heat stabilizer is 100:0-50:30-150:0-80:0-5:1-5.                  Preferred Method: The method for obtaining the                  foam comprises soaking the blank in a foaming gas                  under pressure, and releasing the pressure to                  obtain foam, the pressure is 6-15 MPa.

GA 2021788828
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing the waste gas discharged from a target area into a gas separating device to obtain the separated carbon monoxide. The gas separating device is provided with an adsorption device and a purification device. The adsorption device is provided with a copper-carrying adsorbent layer. Carbon monoxide discharge amount of the carbon monoxide is introduced into a reaction layer. Carbon monoxide electro-catalytic reaction is performed, such that ethylene is generated according to the electro-catalytic reaction. The adsorption device is provided with a carbon dioxide absorbing layer and a carbon monoxide absorbing layer.
   USE - Method for the utilization of carbon monoxide discharged during industrial combustion using an electronic device (Claimed).

PT P
PN CN113004853-A
TI Environmentally friendly adhesive useful for decorative panel, comprises water-proof base material, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer emulsion, modified montmorillonite, Neoprene latex, mineral oil antifoaming agent, and thickening agent.
AU LEI X
AE ANHUI YANGZI FLOOR CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202175998X
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally friendly adhesive comprises 35-50 pts. wt. water-proof base material, 15-20 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer emulsion, 5-10 pts. wt. modified montmorillonite, 10-25 pts. wt. Neoprene (RTM: Synthetic rubbers) latex, 0.5-1 pts. wt. mineral oil antifoaming agent, 0.5-1 pts. wt. thickening agent, The environmentally friendly decorative panel adhesive is obtained by adding Neoprene (RTM: Synthetic rubbers) latex to ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer emulsion, stirring at 150-200 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes, adding waterproof base material, continuously stirring for 30 minutes, adding mineral oil antifoaming agent, increasing the speed to 200-500 revolutions/minute, stirring at a constant speed for 50 minutes, then adding a thickener, and continuously stirring for 2 hours to obtain an environmentally-friendly decorative panel adhesive.
   USE - The adhesive is useful for decorative panel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not generate carbon dioxide, and avoids bubble phenomenon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The water-proof base material is obtained by (S1) adding polyoxypropylene triol and ethyl acetate into a three-necked flask, heating in a water bath at 45-55 degrees C, magnetically stirring for 30 minutes, adding sodium persulfate magnetically stirring for 15 minutes to obtain mixed solution A, pouring the mixed solution A into a beaker, heating to 70-80 degrees C, stirring at a high speed of 1000 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes, transferring to the reactor, reacting for 2 hours at a vacuum of -0.10MPa and a temperature of 80-85 degrees C, taking out, adding ethylene glycol, allowing to stand for 30 minutes, stirring at a constant speed for 10 minutes to obtain mixed solution B, (S2) heating the mixture B to 85-90 degrees C, stirring at a constant speed and add isocyanate and ethyl acetate, stirring at a constant speed of 150-200 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes, adding triethanolamine, reacting at this temperature for 2 hours, reducing the temperature to 50 degrees C, and the reacting at this temperature for 2 hours to obtain initial material, and (S3) shearing initial material by a high-speed shearing machine, where the shearing speed is controlled to 6000 revolutions/minute, and shearing for 15 minutes the waterproof base material. The modified montmorillonite is prepared by uniformly mixing the montmorillonite, urea and ethanol in a weight ratio of 3:10:1, transferring to a three-necked flask, heating in a water bath at 45-55 degrees C, stirring at a speed of 140 revolutions/minute for 2-3 hours to obtain mixed slurry, filtering, washing with deionized water three times, washing three times with absolute ethanol, drying, and grinding to obtain modified montmorillonite. Preferred Components: In step (S1), the mass ratio of polyoxypropylene triol, ethyl acetate and sodium persulfate is controlled to be 1:3-5:0.3-0.5. The amount of ethylene glycol is 20% of the weight of the mixture A. In step (S2), the mass ratio of the mixed solution B, isocyanate, and ethyl acetate is controlled to be 2:1:1. The mass ratio of ethyl acetate to ethyl acetate is 1:1.

PN CN112795486-A; CN214361448-U
TI Simultaneous production of ethanol from livestock            and poultry manure microalgae cultivation comprises            e.g. collecting primary fermentation in gas storage            tank to obtain fermentation gas, adjusting pH and            performing secondary fermentation.
AU LI J
   XU S
   DAI J
AE XINGYUAN ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XYHJ-C)
   ZHEJIANG SUPER MAX ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO (XYHJ-C)
GA 202156804E
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous production of ethanol from                livestock and poultry manure microalgae cultivation                comprises (i) collecting the fermentation gas                obtained in the first fermentation in a gas storage                tank, and separating the fermentation product into                a solid-liquid to obtain a primary liquid and solid                mixture,(ii) adding the primary liquid to the                microalgae reactor, adjusting the pH value to                7.2-7.5, adjusting the growth environment of                microalgae, inoculating microalgae, turning on the                microalgae reactor, and passing the fermented gas                into the gas storage tank for microalgae                cultivation, and (iii) performing secondary                fermentation.
   USE - The method is useful for simultaneous                production of ethanol from livestock and poultry                manure microalgae cultivation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides a new idea for the                fermentation of livestock and poultry manure to                produce ethanol; recycles the waste gas produced by                fermentation; and realizes the value-added of                energy and the efficient use of energy while                reducing carbon dioxide emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the primary                  fermentation step, adding protease and cellulase                  to ferment the livestock manure, where the                  livestock manure includes 0.5-1% protease. The                  livestock and poultry manure includes 0.5-1%                  cellulase. The culture temperature of the                  microalgae reactor is 25-30 degrees C. The air                  ventilation rate is 2.5-3.5 l/minute. The                  fermentation gas is fed every 6 hours. The                  ventilation rate is 4.5-5.5 l/minute. The                  ventilation time is 30-60 minutes. The method                  specifically includes (1) adding poultry and                  livestock manure into the reactor, adding                  protease and cellulase, mixing well, maintaining                  the fermentation at 30-40 degrees C for 4-7 days,                  collecting the fermented gas produced by the                  fermentation into the gas storage tank, and                  completing the solid-liquid fermentation to                  separate primary liquid and solid waste residue,                  (2) pouring the primary liquid into a microalgae                  reactor, diluting with water, adding diammonium                  phosphate-citrate buffer, adjusting the pH to                  7.2-7.5, adding diammonium phosphate and                  magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, and yeast                  extract, adjusting the growth environment of the                  microalgae to obtain a microalgae culture                  solution, inoculating the microalgae into the                  microalgae culture solution, turning on the                  microalgae reactor, passing in air,                  intermittently passing in the fermentation gas,                  maintaining the pH value at 7.2-7.5, and                  culturing the microalgae for 18-25 days, (3)                  collecting, culturing the microalgae, adding to                  the fermentation tank, while adding the solid                  waste residue, stirring and mixing uniformly,                  inoculating mixed fermentation strains and                  brewing auxiliaries, and maintaining the                  fermentation at 40-60 degrees C for more than 24                  hours, storing the secondary fermentation gas                  produced by the fermentation in the gas storage                  tank, separating the secondary fermentation                  product from the solid and liquid, distilling the                  separated mash to obtain ethanol, and returning                  the solid to the field as organic fertilizer.                  Preferred Components: The microalgae reactor                  includes three tubular reactors and mixing tanks                  connected in series, and the center of the three                  tubular reactors is provided with a white                  fluorescent light source. The light source for                  the cultivation of microalgae adopts a                  combination of a fluorescent light source and a                  solar light source according to 12:12 to 14:10                  hours light and dark cycle, where the time ratio                  of white fluorescent light source and solar light                  source is 1:1-1:4, and the fluorescence intensity                  is 1000-3000lux, which increase to 255-15000 lux                  after 10-15 days. The microalgae includes                  Scenedesmus, Chlorella, and/or Chlamydomonas                  reinhardtii. The mixed fermentation strain is a                  mixture of Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces                  cerevisiae. The dosage of the mixed fermentation                  strain is 50-100 g/l. The mass ratio of                  Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae is                  1:1. The brewing auxiliary is a mixture of                  glucoamylase, acid protease and cellulase. The                  dosage of the saccharification enzyme is 50-75                  g/l. The dosage of the acid protease is 25-100                  g/l. The dosage of the cellulase is 25-75                  g/l.

The acidic solution is aqueous solution of hydrochloric acid, glacial acetic acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, and/or phosphoric acid. Preferred Composition: The concentration of acidic solution is 1-5 mol/L. The mass ratio of titanium precursor, silver precursor, and activated carbon is (0.01-0.1):(0.001-0.01):1. The molar ratio of reducing agent and silver in the silver precursor is (5-100):1. The ratio of acidic solution and titanium precursor is (0.1-1 g):(1-5 mL).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium precursor is an alkoxide of titanium. The organic solvent is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, ethylene glycol, and/or glycerol.

TI Method for identifying vegetation moisture in desert area, involves selecting desert area vegetation soil, sampling desert area vegetation typical plant and soil, processing vegetation sample and soil, followed by determining water content.
AU XU D
AE CAS GEOGRAPHIC SCI & NATURAL RESOURCES (CAGN-C)
GA 2021550514
AB    NOVELTY - Identifying vegetation moisture in desert area comprises selecting desert area vegetation soil, and respectively sampling the desert area vegetation typical plant and soil; processing the vegetation sample and soil respectively; testing the water respectively the soil, discharging the water in the soil, and determining the water content in the soil; taking different plant variety seedling; observing the growth condition of plant seedling and summarizing the condition; and analyzing the data, determining the desert area vegetation water utilization condition, and drawing the model.
   USE - The method is useful for identifying vegetation moisture in desert area.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing stable isotope process to estimate temperature, water and carbon dioxide and other environment parameters in atmosphere so as to determine desert vegetation growth soil carbon and oxygen content, and researching different carbon in water, and comparing oxygen content with influence caused by growth of desert vegetation so as to observe three desert vegetation for water utilization condition.

PT P
PN CN112640905-A
TI Preparing high-efficiency Asian tiger mosquito attractant by adding ammonium bicarbonate, n-hexanoic acid, 2-hydroxypropionic acid and 1-octen-3-ol to four-glass reaction flask, heating, using stirring device to stir, reacting and cooling.
AU CHU H
   ZHANG Y
   WU Y
   WANG T
   WANG J
   ZHU D
   LAN C
AE JIANGSU DISEASE CONTROL & PREVENTION CEN (JIAN-Non-standard)
   NANJING BEST PEST CONTROL SERVICE CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
   WUXI CENT DISEASE CONTROL & PREVENTION (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202148620G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-efficiency Asian tiger mosquito attractant, involves (i) adding raw materials comprising ammonium bicarbonate, n-hexanoic acid, 2-hydroxypropionic acid and 1-octen-3-ol into a four-glass reaction flask in a mass ratio and mixing to obtain a mixture, (ii) putting four-neck glass reaction flask on heating device to heat mixture, (iii) using a thermometer to measure temperature of the mixture on the heating device to control the temperature of the mixture at 100-110 degrees C, (iv) using a stirring device to stir the mixture for 8-10 hours, (v) cooling after reacting of the mixture which releases carbon dioxide and water, and (vi) obtaining attractant ammonium 2-hydroxypropionate product after cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-efficiency Asian tiger mosquito attractant.
   ADVANTAGE - The attractant attracts mosquitoes through volatile odor substances, uses ammonium 2-hydroxypropionate as attractant for mosquitoes and plays an important role in mosquito control.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: In step (ii), the heating device is an electric heating mantle. In step (iv), the stirring device is an electric stirrer, and the rotating speed of the electric stirrer is 700-800 rpm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ammonium bicarbonate in the raw material is replaced with ammonium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate or calcium carbonate, preferably ammonium bicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: When the raw material is used as 1 pt. wt. ammonium bicarbonate, 0.33 pt. wt. n-hexanoic acid, 2 pts. wt. 2-hydroxypropionic acid, and 0.038 pt. wt. 1-octen-3-ol, the yield of the obtained attractant 2-hydroxypropionate product is 99.1%, and the content is 97.2%. Preferred Composition: In step (i), the mass ratio of ammonium bicarbonate, n-hexanoic acid, 2-hydroxypropionic acid and 1-octen-3-ol is 1:0.33:2:0.038. The raw materials comprise 1 pt. wt. ammonium bicarbonate, 0.33 pts. wt. n-hexanoic acid, 2 pts. wt. 2-hydroxypropionic acid, and 0.038 pt. wt. 1-octen-3-ol. Preferred Components: The n-hexanoic acid in the raw materials is replaced with n-propionic acid, n-butyric acid, n-valeric acid, n-heptanoic acid or n-octanoic acid, preferably n-hexanoic acid. The 2-hydroxypropionic acid in the raw materials is replaced with 2-hydroxybutyric acid, 2-hydroxyvaleric acid or 2-hydroxyhexanoic acid, preferably 2-hydroxypropionic acid. The 1-octen-3-ol in the raw materials is replaced with 1-penten-3-ol, 1-hexen-3-ol or 1-hepten-3-ol, preferably 1-octen-3-ol.
   ACTIVITY - Insect Attractant.

PN CN112501484-A
TI Inducing precipitation of T-type second phase in deformed magnesium alloy, involves e.g. treating deformed magnesium alloy after solid solution treatment, cooling at room temperature, aging and cooling at room temperature.
AU LUO S
   MENG L
   WU Y
   GAO Z
AE UNIV CHONGQING JIAOTONG (UYCJ-C)
GA 202128591Y
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing the precipitation of the T-type second phase in a deformed magnesium alloy comprises taking 5-5.45 wt.% tin, 2.75-3 wt.% zinc, less than or equal to 0.15 wt.% unavoidable impurity, and magnesium (remaining amount), solid solution treating, cooling at room temperature, aging at 175-200 degrees C for 6-25 hours, and cooling at room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing the precipitation of the T-type second phase in a deformed magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the mechanical property of the deformed magnesium alloy, and further expands the use range of the deformed magnesium alloy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the deformed magnesium alloy by smelting industrial pure magnesium at 720-740 degrees C under carbon dioxide containing 0.5-1.5 vol.% sulfur hexafluoride as under protective atmosphere; and
   (2) a deformed magnesium alloy containing a T-type second phase prepared by above mentioned method.

   Ultrasonic deaeration apparatus (11A)
   Processing tank (12)
   Liquid level (31)
   Liquid level fluctuation-suppressing component (32)
   To-be-processed liquid (W1)

   MURATA MFG CO LTD (MURA-C)
GA 202117347E
AB    NOVELTY - Graft copolymer comprises a stem polymer and a branch polymer bonded to the stem polymer, where the stem polymer comprises a cellulosic resin, and the branch polymer comprises polycarbonate.
   USE - The graft copolymer is useful in a paste composition, a resin composition, a molded product, an adhesive composition, an ink composition, a coating composition, a binder resin and a dispersant (all claimed), and as molded objects, such as a sheet and a film.
   ADVANTAGE - The graft copolymer eliminates fault of cellulose resin, exhibits function caused by graft portion, maintains certain level of performance of the cellulosic resin, improves yield, and has excellent mechanical characteristic, pyrolysis character, thixotropic property, transparency, dispersion stability, thermal decomposability, moldability, strength, elongation, fracture toughness, printability and dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing the graft copolymer, involving copolymerizing epoxide and carbon dioxide in the presence of a cellulosic resin, or performing ring-opening polymerization of cyclic carbonate in the presence of a cellulosic resin.

The cellulosic resin is at least one chosen from alkyl cellulose, hydroxyalkyl cellulose, cellulose ester and ethyl cellulose. PT P
PN CN112221530-A
TI Preparing non-noble metal monoatomic bifunctional electrocatalyst by adding activated carbon balls to deionized water, adding metal salt to deionized water, mixing solution, washing product with deionized water, grinding, and calcining.
AU ZONG L
   WU W
   CHEN X
   ZHANG W
   WANG L
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 202109218J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing non-noble metal monoatomic bifunctional electrocatalyst, involves (a) weighing activated carbon balls, and adding to deionized water to form a solution (A), (b) weighing the metal salt, and adding to the deionized water to form a solution (B), (c) mixing the solution (A) and solution (B), and stirring the reaction at room temperature to obtain the product, (d) washing the product with deionized water, drying under the an inert gas protection, and grinding the product with a nitrogen source to obtain a solid powder, and (e) calcining the solid powder to obtain the electrocatalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing non-noble metal monoatomic bifunctional electrocatalyst used in oxygen reduction, oxygen evolution reactions hydrogen evolution reaction, chlorine evolution reaction, nitrogen reduction reaction, carbon dioxide reduction reaction, methanol oxidation reaction or methane oxidation reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, and suitable for industrialized large-scale production, and adopts mild reaction, and the prepared electrocatalyst has large specific surface area and excellent electrochemical performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the solution (A) is 1-1000 g/l. The concentration of the solution (B) is 0.0001-100 g/l. The mixing concentration ratio of the solution (A) and solution (B) is (1-100):1. Preferred Components: The metal salt comprises ferric chloride, manganese chloride, ferrous chloride, cupric chloride, cobalt chloride, nickel chloride, zinc chloride, rhenium chloride and their corresponding hydrated nitrate or acetate. The nitrogen source comprises dopamine, urea, melamine, peptone, ammonium salt and/or thiourea, where the mass ratio of the product to the nitrogen source is 1:(1-20). Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the stirring speed is 200-1600 rpm, and the stirring time is 10-20 hours. The drying temperature is 40-80 degrees C, and the drying time is 6-12 hours. In step (e), the calcination temperature is 600-1100 degrees C, the heating rate is 1-20 degrees C/minute, and the heat-preserving time is 1-10 hours. Preferred Method: The synthesis method of the catalyst is defect and vacancy adsorption.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC J04-E04A; J04-E04D; L03-A02B; L03-E01B3; L03-E08; X16-E06A1

PN KR2192071-B1; VN79032-A
TI Pure water production method for recycling wastewater treated water using an electric deionization device, comprises storing the permeated water in permeated water storage tank, and supplying to electric deionization device.
AU KIL B
   KIL B K
AE BK-ENG CO LTD (BKEN-Non-standard)
   BK-ENG INC (BKEN-Non-standard)
GA 202101021Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises storing the filtered water that has undergone the pretreatment of the pretreatment step in a filtered water storage tank, producing water by separating and removing inorganic ions and low-molecular organic substances by permeating the flat-membrane-type polyamide-based primary reverse osmosis filtration membrane using a first high-pressure pump; removing carbon dioxide contained in the water by adjusting the water of the first reverse osmosis filtration to pH 8-10; re-separating and removing residual inorganic ions contained in water by passing through a polyamide-based secondary reverse osmosis membrane using second high pressure pump; and storing the permeated water passed through the second reverse osmosis filtration step in the permeated water storage tank, and supplying the electric deionization device through the third supply pump to finally process residual ions using current and ion exchange resin to produce pure water.
   USE - The method is useful to produce pure water for recycling wastewater treated water using an electric deionization device.

PN KR2020139877-A
TI Vehicle cooling water additive manufacturing involves pulverizing mineral and metallic substances respectively to obtain optical powder and metallic powder, and then mixing optical powder and metal powder.
AU JUNG C
   HYUN Y J
AE JUNG C (JUNG-Individual)
   HYUN Y J (HYUN-Individual)
GA 2020C5694E
AB    NOVELTY - Vehicle cooling water additive manufacturing involves pulverizing mineral and metallic substances respectively to obtain an optical powder and a metallic powder, mixing the optical powder and metal powder, and then adding an acidic solution to prepare solution at 200-250 degrees C for 2-3 hours. The prepared suspension is neutralized by adding a basic substance to the obtained solution. 1000-2000 pts. wt. purified water is added to the obtained suspension to remove impurities for preparing a cooling water additive. The cooling water additive includes 10-35 wt.% optical powder, 10-35 wt.% metal powder, 25-60 wt.% acid solution, and 20-50 wt.% basic substance.
   USE - Method for manufacturing vehicle cooling water additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The vehicle cooling water additive promotes combustion of fuel to improve combustion efficiency by improving soot including carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxides, prevents environmental pollution, improves fuel efficiency by increasing output of an internal combustion engine, prevents phenomenon of clogging the cooling water circulation pipe due to impurities, improves circulation, prevents noise, and reduces performance of the cooling water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mineral material is selected from sunggit, pozzolan, sericite, diatomaceous earth, monazite, jungseok, bentonite, zeolite and talc. The metal material is manganese, iron, tungsten, zinc, cobalt, copper, yttrium, lithium, cerium, hafnium, magnesium, nickel, aluminum, and calcium. The acidic solution is selected from lactic acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, citric acid, and hydrofluoric acid. The basic material is selected from sodium peroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, ammonia, and ammonium carbonate.

PT P
PN CN111994934-A; CN111994934-B
TI Production of light calcium carbonate from calcite            includes crushing calcite, adding aragonite particles,            drying to obtain fine abrasives, mixing fine abrasive            with fine coal powder for calcination and digesting            calcined materials.
AU ZHOU Y
AE JIANDESHI HONGXING CALCIUM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C14014
AB    NOVELTY - Production of light calcium carbonate from                calcite comprises crushing calcite by grinder to                obtain calcite particles, adding aragonite                particles into calcite particles, wet grinding,                cleaning, drying to obtain fine abrasives, mixing                fine abrasive with fine coal powder for calcination                to obtain calcined material, digesting calcined                materials by spray washing, collecting digestion                solution, adding carbon dioxide to digestion                solution, reacting, drying to obtain calcium                carbonate, wet grinding to obtain secondary fine                abrasive, drying calcium carbonate, mixing                secondary fine abrasive with combustible gas for                secondary calcination to obtain secondary calcined                material, placing secondary calcined material in                water, cooling to obtain secondary digestion                solution, placing mixture of sodium carbonate and                nano calcium carbonate in secondary digestion                solution, mixing mixture, standing, cleaning,                drying and crushing.
   USE - The method is for production of light calcium                carbonate from calcite.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate has higher whiteness,                finer particle size, more concentrated particle                size distribution, uniform particle size and higher                purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of                  aragonite particles is 20-30 wt.% of calcite                  particles. The water is used as wet grinding                  medium. The amount of water added is 1-1.3 times                  of the weight of calcite particles. The 0.05-0.1                  times the weight of calcite particles is added as                  dispersant. The weight of stainless steel ball is                  0.7-1.0 times of calcite particle. The                  combustible gas is natural gas, liquefied                  petroleum gas, coal gas and/or biogas. The weight                  of combustible gas is 0.01-0.05 times of                  secondary fine abrasive. The weight of water is                  10-20 times of secondary calcined material. The                  weight of sodium carbonate is 2-5 times of the                  weight of secondary calcined material. The weight                  of nano calcium carbonate is 0.1-0.2 times of                  weight of secondary calcined material. The                  dispersant is cellulose derivative,                  polyacrylamide and/or fatty acid polyethylene                  glycol ester.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Method for treating electron beam for air pollutants, involves performing a reaction for converting air pollutants into non-pollutants in gas-liquid contact space of aqueous solution, and treating gas by containing air pollutants.
AU KIM T H
   CHO S
   OH Y H
   KIM K M
   SHINJAEKYUNG
   SONYOUNSUK
AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 2020B83318
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing a reaction for converting air pollutants into non-pollutants in the gas-liquid contact space of an aqueous solution by irradiating electron beams. The gas is to be treated by containing air pollutants in a humidified atmosphere, which is derived from the aqueous solution. The humidified atmosphere is maintained at an aqueous solution temperature of 10 to 100 degrees centigrade. The aqueous solution is pure water, ammonium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide , sodium chloride, calcium hydroxide, calcium chloride, and hydrogen peroxide. The non-polluting material is provided nitrogen, oxygen, nitric acid, sulphuric acid, water, and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for treating electron beam for air pollutants.
   ADVANTAGE - An efficiency of the method in treating gaseous air pollutants is improved by using an electron beam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for treating electron beam for air pollutants.

PN CN211894410-U
TI Indicating leakage function featured sodium bicarbonate injection plastic packaging system, has indicator indicating label provided with protective film, and ampoule bottle provided with handle part, sealing cap part and splash-proof liquid bottle opening part.
AU ZHOU Y
   LI Z
   MA J
AE CHINA OTSUIKA PHARM CO LTD (CHOT-Non-standard)
GA 2020B41709
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a sodium bicarbonate injection plastic packaging system with indicating leakage function, comprising an ampoule bottle filled with sodium bicarbonate injection, the sealing cover is set outside the outer package of the ampoule bottle; between the ampoule bottle and the outer package is filled with protective gas; between the ampoule bottle and the outer package is further provided with an indicator indicating label for indicating whether the outer package is leaking; the indicator indicating label is provided with a protective film; the ampoule bottle comprises a handle part connected in turn, a sealing cap part, a splash-proof liquid bottle opening part and a bottle body part. The utility model can be used for normal indication when the concentration of the protective gas is changed or the leakage of the outer package, and can prevent the interference of water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide gas.

PT P
PN CN111888900-A; CN111888900-B
TI Removing carbon disulfide based on low-temperature plasma reactor and coupled catalyst, comprises carrying modified powder preparation, modified powder spraying, carrying coupled catalyst preparation, and low-temperature plasma treatment.
AU YANG R
   ZHANG X
   YANG M
   LOU J
   GUO X
   LIU M
AE UNIV SHANDONG FIRST MEDICAL & SHANDONG (UYSH-Non-standard)
   TAIAN HUAGUI PHARM TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (TAIA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4600X
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon disulfide based on low-temperature plasma reactor and coupled catalyst, comprises carrying modified powder preparation, modified powder spraying, carrying coupled catalyst preparation, and carrying low-temperature plasma treatment; the method specifically comprises preparing the modified powder, pre-treating the titanium dioxide powder, shearing to predetermined particle size, calcining, adding it into deionized water dispersed with aluminum oxide, manganese dioxide, diiron nickel tetraoxide (Fe2NiO4), irradiating with ultraviolet light, filtering and drying it; modified powder spraying, after spraying the modified powder onto the electrode plates of low-temperature plasma reactor, sintering and fixing, to obtain low-temperature plasma reactor electrode plate sprayed with modified powder.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon disulfide based on low-temperature plasma reactor and coupled catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can completely decomposes carbon disulfide waste gas into carbon dioxide, water, N2 and other pollution-free substances and achieves emission standards: has low energy consumption, large waste gas treatment, and no by-products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of the coupled catalyst comprises activating carbon fiber, loading and calcining; activating carbon fiber by immersing the phenolic-based fiber and viscose-based fiber in sodium hydroxide solution, filtering and heating to obtain activated carbon fiber; loading by adding calcium oxide, nickel, and Fe2NiO4 under the protection of inert gas, under the environment of acetone, ultrasonically dispersing to prepare a mixed solution, throwing the activated carbon fiber into the mixed solution, heating and stirring, and filtering out; and performing microwave activation on the filtered activated carbon fiber. In the method, in the low-temperature plasma treatment, using the low-temperature plasma reactor electrode plate sprayed with modified powder as the electrode plate of the low-temperature plasma reactor, filling the coupled catalyst into the catalyst packed bed of the low-temperature plasma reactor, and passing the exhaust gas containing carbon disulfide into the low-temperature plasma reactor for exhaust gas treatment. In the pre-treating of the titanium dioxide powder, adding the titanium dioxide powder of 30-40 mesh into 5 times the volume of the 3-5 wt.% zirconium nitrate solution, stirring at 60-120 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes, allowing to stand for 30 minutes; dropwise adding 5-7 wt.% phosphoric acid, stirring for 1 hours, and filtering out. In the shearing, performing high-speed shearing on pretreated titanium dioxide powder, the shearing speed is 6000-7500 revolutions/minute, and the solid particle size D50 is 90-120 mu m. The preparation of the modified powder comprises loading step, dispersing aluminum oxide, manganese dioxide, Fe2NiO4 in deionized water under the protection of inert gas, adding the solid powder that has been calcined and cooled, stirring at 20-30 revolutions/minute, irradiating with ultraviolet light for 30-40 minutes, filtering out solids, drying at 180-200 degrees C until the moisture content is less than 20 ppm to prepare the modified powder. In the modified powder spraying, uniformly spraying the modified powder onto the electrode plate of low-temperature plasma reactor, sintering the electrode plate and the modified powder are sintered at a high temperature of 1100-1200 degrees C, sintering time is 10-15 minutes to obtain low temperature plasma reactor electrode plate sprayed with the modified powder. In the method, for the activated carbon fiber, soaking the phenolic-based fiber and the viscose-based fiber in 2-3 times the volume 40% sodium hydroxide solution, stirring at 20-30 revolutions/minute, soaking time is 1 to 1.5 hours, filtering out; heating the mixture of the phenolic-based fiber and the viscose-based fiber to 500-550 degrees C for 40-60 minutes to obtain activated carbon fiber. In the microwave activation, the microwave radiation power is 400 W, the microwave frequency is 1800-2000 MHz, and microwave activation time is 10-12 minutes; the weight ratio of the activated carbon: calcium oxide: nickel: Fe2NiO4 is 1:(0.07-0.12):(0.11-0.15):(0.04-0.06). In the low-temperature plasma treatment, the voltage is 15-25 kV, the discharge frequency is 6-10 kHz, the exhaust gas flow rate is 2000 m3/h, and the exhaust gas residence time is 10-15 seconds.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)
MC E11-K03; E11-Q02C; E11-W; E31-N04E; E31-N05D; E34-C02; E34-D01; E35; J04-E04; J04-E11; V05-F04; V05-F05C; V05-F05E3

TI Mask for protection against viruses, bacteria, germs, dust, and pollution, comprises area that is provided between nose and mouth of user, and diffuser that is incorporated deflectors to direct exhaled air in lateral-inferior direction.
AU NISA GARCIA M
AE NISA GARCIA M (GARC-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - The mask comprises an area that is provided between a nose (2) and a mouth (8) of a user. A diffuser (5) is established in a transverse partition (6) that participates for a flexible and hygienic nature. PT P
PN CN111826347-A
TI Obtaining primary mesenchymal stem cells from human adipose tissue, by e.g. washing human adipose tissue sample with D-Hank's solution containing penicillin and streptomycin, cutting, digesting by penicillinase-containing D-Hank's solution.
AU YANG G
   ZHAO J
   ZHAO Y
AE HARMONY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HARM-Non-standard)
GA 2020A7519A
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining primary mesenchymal stem cells from human adipose tissue, comprising (1) washing the human adipose tissue sample with D-Hank's solution containing penicillin and streptomycin, and cutting into pieces; (2) adding penicillinase-containing D-Hank's solution and I type collagenase solution into the sample processed in the step (1), digesting for 60 minutes in the constant temperature water bath at 37 degrees C, centrifuging and removing the supernatant after digestion; and (3) adding culture solution into the sample obtained in the step (2) for blowing cell precipitate; filtering by cell sieve, changing the culture solution, at 37 degrees C, when the volume ratio is 5% of carbon dioxide saturated humidity condition, culturing for 24 hours, when the cell grows to 70-80%, and then in vitro sub-culturing the obtained adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining primary mesenchymal stem cells from human adipose tissue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can efficiently obtain adipose mesenchymal stem cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the tool used to cut the human adipose tissue sample is an iris scissors. In the step (1), cutting the human adipose tissue sample is to the degree of paste. In the step (2), the concentration of penicillinase in the D-Hank's solution added is 10 units/ml. In the step (2), the concentration percentage of the type I collagenase solution added is 0.15%. In the step (3), the culture medium is high glucose-Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (HG-DMEM) medium containing 12% fetal bovine serum and 100X double antibodies. In the step (3), the adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell obtained is digested and passaged with a mixture of 0.25% trypsin and physiological saline in a ratio of 1:1.5.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F02B; B04-L05; D05-A02C; D05-H08B
IP C12N-005/0775

PN CN111807923-A
TI Producing 1,1-dichloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene            products using trifluoromethane and trichloroethylene            comprises e.g. passing trifluoromethane feed stream and            trichloroethylene feed stream into reactor, purifying            and post treating.
AU LIU W
   WANG S
   ZHOU F
   ZHANG J
   HAN W
AE ZHEJIANG CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (SNCM-C)
   ZHEJIANG LANTIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (SNCM-C)
   SINOCHEM LANTIAN CO LTD (SNCM-C)
GA 2020A49352
AB    NOVELTY - Producing 1,1-dichloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene                products using trifluoromethane and                trichloroethylene as raw materials comprises (a)                passing trifluoromethane feed stream and the                trichloroethylene feed stream are respectively into                the reactor in the presence of the catalytic system                composed of the main catalyst and the catalytic                promoter, the gas phase reaction generates the                reaction product stream. The active component of                the main catalyst includes Group VIII metals and/or                Group IB metal elements, the carrier is multi-level                micro/mesoporous chromium(III) oxide, adding                reaction promoting gas containing carbon dioxide in                the reaction stage, (b) adding the reaction product                stream into the separation system, separating                1,1-dichloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropen, passing excess                trifluoromethane through the extraction tower,                purifying and recycling into the initial reactor.                The extract includes tetrafluoroborate or                tetrafluoroethanesulfonate.
   USE - The method is useful in producing                1,1-dichloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene products using                trifluoromethane and trichloroethylene as raw                materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes the resource utilization                of by-product trifluoromethane, and broadens the                raw material types of products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active components                  of the main catalyst are iron, cobalt, nickel,                  ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, platinum, copper,                  silver and/or gold. The multi-stage                  micro/mesoporous chromium(III) oxide has specific                  surface area of 56-120 m2/g; and the average pore                  diameter is 0.2-12 nm. The pore size distribution                  of the multi-stage micro/mesoporous chromium(III)                  oxide has: 40-70% pore diameter of 0.2-2nm; and                  10-20% pore diameter of 15-50 nm. The catalyst                  promoter includes potassium, sodium, zinc,                  titanium and/or a rare earth promoter. The rare                  earth additives are oxides of rare earth elements                  or their chlorides, carbonates, nitrates,                  acetates and sulfates. The rare earth element is                  lanthanum and/or samarium, and the catalytic                  promoter includes metal oxide of zinc or                  titanium. The extracting liquid includes                  imidazole tetrafluoroborate, imidazole                  tetrafluoroethane sulfonate, pyridine                  tetrafluoroborate or pyridine                  tetrafluoroethanesulfonate. The reaction                  promoting gas includes carbon dioxide, chloride,                  carbon tetrachloride, oxygen, and/or nitrogen                  oxides. The mass ratio between the carrier and                  the active component is 100:0.5-25.

PT P
PN CN111560623-A
TI Preparing hypochlorous acid involves using mesh titanium-based coating (RuO2/TiO2) anode and a stainless steel plate as the cathode, and controlling the electrolyte flow rate by a constant flow pump,.
AU LI X
AE LI X (LIXX-Individual)
GA 202083015Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hypochlorous acid involves using mesh titanium-based coating (RuO2/TiO2) anode and a stainless steel plate as the cathode, where the distance between the plates is 5mm, and the mass concentration of calcium chloride solution is 3.5%, controlling the electrolyte flow rate by a constant flow pump, and adding to the electrolytic cell from the bottom. The current density is controlled between 120mA/cm2. The electrolysis temperature is controlled below 25 degrees C, and the electrolysis time is 1 hour. The solution flowing out of the top of the electrolytic cell (the effluent of the electrolytic cell) is in the reactor with tap water which is mixed with a volume ratio of 1:65. The carbon dioxide is poures into the solution, stirred and filtered to obtain the filtrate.
   USE - Method for preparing hypochlorous acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare hypochlorous acid that realizes the continuous preparation of hypochlorous acid solution, and effectively increases the production and storage stability of hypochlorous acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The electrolysis temperature is controlled at 15-25 degrees C. The electrolyte flow rate is controlled at 4-10L/h. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to calcium chloride solution is 1:1. The feed rate of carbon dioxide is controlled at 0.3-0.5kg/m3 per minute.

TI Preparation of magnesium ammonium phosphate and            calcium carbonate involves mixing magnesium phosphorus            tailings with hydrochloric acid, acidolysis reacting,            calcium precipitating, aging, phosphorus precipitating,            arbonizing, and filtering.
AU WU H
   PAN Z
   ZHANG H
   PAN J
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 2020781185
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of magnesium ammonium phosphate                and calcium carbonate comprises mixing high                magnesium phosphorus tailings with hydrochloric                acid, acidolysis reacting, keeping temperature,                filtering, and washing; mixing mixed acidolysis                liquid and ammonium sulfate solution, calcium                precipitating, aging, and filtering to obtain                calcium sulfate and calcium precipitation mother                liquor; neutralizing with ammonia water, phosphorus                precipitating, aging, and filtering to obtain                ammonium magnesium phosphate; adding calcium                sulfate and ammonia water into calcium sulfate                slurry; introducing carbon dioxide to calcium                sulfate slurry, carbonizing, and filtering; and                circulating ammonium sulfate solution and                performing calcium precipitation.
   USE - The method is used for preparing magnesium                ammonium phosphate and calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively utilizes valuable                elements in high-magnesium phosphate tailings, has                more than 95% recovery rate of calcium and                phosphorus in phosphorus tailings, obtains                effective components phosphorus pentoxide,                magnesium oxide, and total nitrogen at amount of                28%, 18%, and 9% respectively, prepares more than                97% purity of calcium carbonate, and meets the                requirements of qualified products in the standard                HG2226-91.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass fraction of                  hydrochloric acid is 10-35 wt.%. The molar ratio                  of hydrochloric acid and calcium magnesium is                  (2.5-4.6):1. The acidolysis temperature is 50-90                  degrees C. Preferred Method: The second step is                  done by calcium precipitating at 70-110 degrees C                  and aging at 15-50 degrees C for 1-5 hours. The                  carbonization reaction is done at 15-55 degrees C                  for 1.5-5.5 hours.

TI Thin plates welding deformation controlling and            correction involves dividing component into multiple            small components and component segments, and            distributing welding deformation on each component to            avoid excessive concentration.
AU CHANG J
   WANG J
   ZHAO T
   WANG P
   TANG X
   WU D
AE JIANGSU CHINA RAILWAY SHANHAIGUAN BRIDGE (CREN-C)
GA 2020519722
AB    NOVELTY - Thin plates welding deformation controlling                and correction involves dividing component into                multiple small components and component segments,                distributing the welding deformation on each                component to avoid excessive concentration and                cross layout of welding seam. The component is                fixed by clamping the tire clamp to increase the                rigidity of the component and reduce the welding                deformation. The gap between the slits during                clamping is less than 0.05 mm. The tire clamp                mechanically adjusts the workpiece to reach the                preset reverse deformation state, where the welding                area is thoroughly cleaned with acetone solution.                The argon and carbon dioxide mixed gas shielded arc                welding is used for welding. The steel structure is                corrected by flame correction method, where the                area of the heating adjustment point is defined on                the welding seam of the adjustment model.
   USE - Method for controlling and correcting welding                deformation of thin plates.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces welding deformation and                stress concentration during the welding,                significantly releases welding residual stress of                the component and corrects the welding                deformation.
