PN CN111229227-A
TI Preparing cobalt catalyst involves dissolving            2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid and cobalt salt into            mixed solution includes dimethylformamide, water,            placing mixed solution in polytetrafluoride reactor,            carrying out hydrothermal reaction.
AU HUANG Y
   LIU S
   FAN S
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202051997N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cobalt catalyst involves dissolving                2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid and cobalt salt into                mixed solution includes dimethylformamide, ethanol                and water. The volume ratio dimethylformamide and                ethanol is 1:3-3:1, ratio of dimethylformamide, and                water is 1:3-3:1. The mixed solution is then placed                in polytetrafluoride reactor, and hydrothermal                reaction is carried out in oven. The obtained solid                is washed and dried which is called Co-MOF-74. The                Co-MOF-74, carbon powder and ammonium salt are                mixed and carbonized at high temperature in inert                atmosphere of 800-1000 degrees C to obtain crude                cobalt catalyst. The obtained crude cobalt catalyst                is washed under acidic conditions, and carbonized                at high temperature under inert atmosphere of                700-900 degrees C for 10 minutes-10 hours to obtain                cobalt catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing cobalt catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cobalt catalyst                that improves activity of the catalyst for the                electric reduction of carbon dioxide, improves                catalytic stability, inhibits hydrogen evolution                reaction, and enhances selection of the product                cobalt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt salt is one                  or more of cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate, cobalt                  sulfate or cobalt hydrochloride. The ammonium                  salt is one or more of ammonium nitrate, ammonium                  chloride, ammonium sulfate or ammonium acetate.                  The gas diffusion electrode is carbon paper,                  carbon felt, carbon cloth or carbon                  fiber.Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of                  2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid and cobalt salt is                  1:4-4:1. The optimized acid is hydrochloric acid,                  nitric acid, sulfuric acid or HClO4 and has molar                  concentration of 0.5-5M. The material can be used                  in the catalytic reaction of electroreduction of                  carbon dioxide. The gas diffusion electrode is                  loaded with carbon dioxide electroreduction                  catalytic material applied to electroreduction                  carbon dioxide catalytic reaction. The gas                  diffusion electrode is loaded with cobalt                  catalyst, and weight of the load is 0.1-10                  mg/cm2. Preferred Process: The method involves                  preparing gas diffusion electrode of cobalt                  catalyst, dispersing cobalt catalyst into mixed                  solution of isopropyl alcohol and water, adding                  1-10 wt.% of Nafion solution of perfluorosulfonic                  acid resin and stirring to obtain mixed solution,                  then applying resulting mixed solution to gas                  diffusion electrode and drying, adding                  isopropanol in mixed solution of isopropanol and                  water in ratio of 1:5-5:1. The ratio of the                  metal/carbon material to the mixed solution of                  isopropyl alcohol and water is 1-20mg:1mL. The                  volume ratio of the mixed solution of 1-10 wt.%                  of perfluorosulfonic acid Nafion solution and                  isopropyl alcohol and water is 1:100-1:10.

In step (iv), the microwave sterilization is carried out at 75-80 degrees C and power of 300 MHz-300 GHz for 3-5 minutes.

AU QIAN C

   RUI Y

   WANG K

GA 202028241T
AB    NOVELTY - Regulating and controlling the grain size of calcite induced by microorganism comprises (a) inoculating the spores of Bacillus mucilaginosus, photosynthetic bacteria or Bacillus pasteurii into the corresponding culture medium to obtain concentrated bacterial solutions of Bacillus mucilaginosus, photosynthetic bacteria or Bacillus pasteurii , (b) adding the calcium source and urea to the concentrated bacterial solution of Bacillus mucilaginosus, photosynthetic bacteria or Bacillus pasteurii and (c) precipitating, washing the precipitate, filtering and drying to a constant weight and analyzing for the crystal grain size to prepare calcite with a crystal grain size of 20-60 nm.
   USE - The method is useful for regulating and controlling the grain size of calcite induced by microorganism.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high efficiency and environmental protection and can adjust the size of the calcite grains based on the needs. The formed calcite mineral has stable properties and captures and utilizes carbon dioxide during the process to slow the greenhouse effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The preparation of calcite with a grain size of 40-60 nm comprises (a) inoculating the spores of photosynthetic bacteria into the corresponding culture medium to obtain concentrated bacterial solutions of photosynthetic bacteria, (b) adding the calcium source and urea to the concentrated bacterial solution of photosynthetic bacteria and (c) precipitating, washing the precipitate, filtering and drying to a constant weight and analyzing for the crystal grain size to prepare calcite with a crystal grain size of 40-60 nm. The preparation of calcite with a grain size of 30-40 nm comprises (a) inoculating the spores of Bacillus mucilaginosus into the corresponding culture medium to obtain concentrated bacterial solutions of Bacillus mucilaginosus, (b) adding the calcium source and urea to the concentrated bacterial solution of Bacillus mucilaginosus and (c) precipitating, washing the precipitate, filtering and drying to a constant weight and analyzing for the crystal grain size to prepare calcite with a crystal grain size of 30-40 nm. The preparation of calcite with a grain size of 20-30 nm comprises (a) inoculating the spores of Bacillus pasteurii into the corresponding culture medium to obtain concentrated bacterial solutions of Bacillus pasteurii, (b) adding the calcium source and urea to the concentrated bacterial solution of Bacillus pasteurii and (c) precipitating, washing the precipitate, filtering and drying to a constant weight and analyzing for the crystal grain size to prepare calcite with a crystal grain size of 20-30 nm. The method comprises controlling the pH to 7-8 after inoculating the respective culture solutions with the Bacillus mucilaginosus, photosynthetic bacteria or Bacillus pasteurii, shaking culture at 30-35 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain bacterial liquids of Bacillus mucilaginosus, photosynthetic bacteria or Bacillus pasteurii. In the step (b), the standing is in a constant temperature and humidity environment, the temperature is 30-35 degrees C, and the standing time is 120-150 hours. Preferred Components: The bacterial cell concentration of the concentrated bacterial solution in the step (a) is 106-107 cells/ml. The medium solution of Bacillus mucilaginosus comprises 8-12 g sucrose, 2-3 g sodium hydrogen phosphate, 0.4-0.6 g magnesium sulfate, 0.5-1.5 g calcium carbonate, 0.1-0.2 g potassium chloride and 0.4-0.6 g ammonium sulfate. The medium solution of photosynthetic bacteria comprises 1-1.5 g ammonium chloride, 3-4 g sodium acetate, 0.1-0.2 g magnesium chloride, 0.1-0.2 g calcium chloride, 0.5-0.6 g monopotassium phosphate, 0 4-0.5 g dipotassium phosphate and 0.1-0.2 g yeast extract. The medium solution of Bacillus pasteurii comprises 10-15 g glucose, 0.1-0.2 g yeast extract, 0.5-0.6 g monopotassium phosphate, 0.4-0.5 g dipotassium phosphate, 0.1-0.2 g magnesium sulfate, 2-2.5 g calcium carbonate, 0.1-0.2 g sodium chloride and 0.1-0.2 g manganese sulfate. The mass ratio of the calcium source, urea, and bacterial solution is (1-2):(0.6-1.2):(96.8-98.4). The calcium source is calcium chloride.

   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes reaction vessel and cathode material i.e. carbon is cheap material, and use of sacrificial carbon anode benefits over other electrode systems due to ability to extract electrons more efficiently to evolve carbon dioxide, thus improving system efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for elemental phosphorus manufacturing system comprising a housing, a cathode within the housing, an anode within the housing, a voltage supply configured to apply an electrolysis voltage to the anode and the cathode, a gas inlet to the housing, a first outlet of the housing to release an oxidized product, and a second outlet of the housing to release an elemental phosphorus product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: carrying the elemental phosphorus away from the melt using a gas; maintaining the melt at a temperature less than 1500 degrees C, preferably less than 850 degrees C; adding phosphoric acid to the melt to replenish phosphorus; and adding an additive to the melt. Preferred Components: The elemental phosphorus includes P4. The oxidized product includes oxygen gas. The oxidized product includes oxygen gas. The metaphosphate salt includes sodium metaphosphate, lithium metaphosphate and/or potassium metaphosphate. The electrolysis voltage is 0.1-5 V, 0 preferably 0.6-1.7 V. The current density during electrolysis is greater than 20 mA cm-2, preferably greater than 100 mA cm-2. The melt is substantially free of transition metals or free of halides. The melt is contained in a crucible. The anode is an electrode material that facilitates oxygen production. The anode includes platinum or carbon. The cathode includes carbon. The cathode is carbon having a sharp end. The additive includes metal salt, phosphate mineral, promoter or other compound. In the system, the cathode includes a tip that facilitates release of gaseous products produced at the cathode. The system further comprises a casing surrounding the anode. The housing includes a crucible.

PT P
PN CN110683523-A; CN110683523-B
TI Preparing carbon nitride used as photocatalyst in e.g. photocatalytic hydrogen production, calcining nitrogen-rich precursor molecule, calcining yellow carbon nitride in tubular furnace and carrying out vapor deposition in tubular furnace.
AU QIN D
   WEI Q
   NIU L
   HAN D
   TAO C
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 2020074285
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon nitride, involves (i) calcining nitrogen-rich precursor molecule at high temperature to obtain yellow carbon nitride, and (ii) calcining the yellow carbon nitride in a tubular furnace under the protection of nitrogen or inert gas for a second time, and obtaining carbon nitride in the tubular furnace by vapor deposition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon nitride used as a photocatalyst, and used in photocatalytic hydrogen production, photocatalytic degradation of dyes, sewage treatment, air pollution treatment, and carbon dioxide reduction field (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon nitride is environmentally-friendly, with uniform composition, good dispersibility and high stability, retains the non-metal nature of the original carbon nitride and the two-dimensional lamella structure, changes energy level structure of carbon nitride, where the spectral absorption cut-off wavelength is significantly red-shifted which further expands the absorption range of graphitic carbon nitride in the visible light region and promotes the utilization of solar energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high-temperature calcination in step (i) is carried out by heating at 500-550 degrees C and at a rate of 2.3-10 degrees C/minute in a muffle furnace, and calcining at a constant temperature for 2-4 hours. The calcination in step (ii) is carried out by heating at 650-800 degrees C and at a rate of 3-10 degrees C/minute under the condition of nitrogen or inert gas, and calcining at a constant temperature for 30-180 minutes. The step (ii) further involves carrying out purification process after calcination by collecting carbon nitride obtained in the tube furnace, washing with water, washing with ethanol, drying to obtain carbon nitride, where the speed of washing with water and ethanol is controlled at 8000-10000 rpm, centrifuging 3 times for 3-5 minutes, and drying at 60 degrees C for 12-24 hours. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of the nitrogen or inert gas in step (ii) is 1-2 seconds to emit a bubble.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-rich precursor in step (i) is dicyandiamide, melamine, thiourea or urea.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110563217-A; CN210915674-U
TI Treating wastewater by separating sewage to separate oil and impurities insoluble in sewage, electrolytically treating separated sewage, and oxidizing amino and nitrogen-containing organic substances and impurities dissolved in sewage.
AU ZHONG J
   ZHANG W
AE GUANGDONG SHANGZHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECT (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A66736
AB    NOVELTY - Treating wastewater involves: (S1) carrying out separation treatment on sewage to separate oil and impurities insoluble in the sewage; (S2) performing electrolytic treatment on the separated sewage, electrolytically oxidizing amino and nitrogen-containing organic substances and impurities dissolved in the sewage to generate carbon dioxide and water; (S3) using a micron filter to filter the sewage after electrolysis, and filtering the impurities generated by the electrolytic reaction; (S4) reoxidizing the filtered sewage with an oxidizing agent to dissolve residual organic matters in the sewage; and (S5) using a super-filter to filter the reoxidized sewage.
   USE - The method is useful for treating wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The treatment method uses series of strong oxidizing groups prepared by electrolytic sewage, and then uses the oxidizing agent to continuously degrade reducing substances in the sewage until it meets the discharge standard, thus achieving efficient degradation of impurities in the sewage, where the amount of sludge produced in the whole process is relatively small.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a sewage treatment device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The treatment method further involves (S6) detecting the chemical oxygen demand (COD) value of the sewage filtered by the super-filter, and discharging if the COD of the sewage reaches the standard and if the COD of the sewage does not reach the standard, performing the step (S4). The step (S1) is carried out by using a jet separation device to separate sewage. In step (S2), an electrolytic reaction device using a diamond material as an anode is used for electrolytic treatment of the separated sewage. Preferred Properties: The diameter of the filter micropores of the micron filter is 0.1-1 mu m. The diameter of the filter micropores of the super-filter is less than 0.01 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidizing agent includes ozone, hydroxide or hydrogen peroxide.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN110521491-A
TI Method for cultivating Hypsizygus marmoreus            mushrooms, involves preparing culture medium, adding            wooden chips while continuously stirring, uniformly            sprinkling lime and calcium carbonate, and performing            steam sterilization after bottling.
AU HUANG Q
   XU X
   CHEN C
   WANG Z
   YIN J
   YAN H
AE XICHONG XINGHE BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (XICH-Non-standard)
   SHAOGUAN STARWAY BIOTECH CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3097L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing culture medium                by stirring corn cob, cottonseed husk, bran, corn                flour, rice bran and soybean husk, and then adding                wooden chips while continuously stirring for 20                minutes, and uniformly sprinkling lime and calcium                carbonate. The culture bottle is immediately filled                after adding water and stirred, and steam                sterilization is performed after bottling. The                culture bottle is sterilized with ultraviolet                disinfection and ozone disinfection. The Hypsizygus                marmoreus strain liquid is inoculated after                cooling. The culture is divided into three stages                followed by mushroom management for the budding                period and bud control period, where the humidity                and carbon dioxide concentration of the warehouse                are controlled during the fertile period. The                products are collected and packed in the room at 15                to 18 degrees C and the products packed on the same                day are stored in the warehouse.
   USE - Method for cultivating Hypsizygus marmoreus                mushrooms.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the production cycle and                ensures quality and safety of the fresh Hypsizygus                marmoreus mushrooms produced.

PN WO2019215764-A1; IN201811017845-A; IN398448-B
TI Sachet for extending shelf-life of e.g. fresh fruits, is made of permeable membrane filled with chemical e.g. oxygen absorber/reducer, microbial/bio-inhibitor, and moisture absorber/releaser, which provides specific functionality to sachet.
AU SAREEN S
   SAREEN M
AE SAREEN S (SARE-Individual)
   SAREEN M (SARE-Individual)
   SAREEN S (SARE-Individual)
   SAREEN M (SARE-Individual)
GA 201995348F
AB    NOVELTY - A shelf-life extending sachet is made of a permeable membrane filled with chemical(s) chosen from ethylene adsorber, ethylene oxidizer, carbon dioxide releaser, oxygen absorber/reducer, microbial/bio-inhibitor, and moisture absorber/releaser, which provides specific functionality to the sachet.
   USE - Sachet is used for extending shelf-life of fresh fruits, preferably bananas, vegetables and flowers.
   ADVANTAGE - The user-friendly shelf-life extending sachet is capable of extending the life of fresh fruits and vegetables by up to 3 times compared to unprotected storage, and can be prepared by economical method. The sachet is able to increase the shelf life of bananas from 17 days to up to 27 days without spoilage, by placing sachets in a box of unripe bananas picked from the farm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of shelf-life extending sachet, which involves homogeneously mixing the chemicals in the form of powder/granules, and filling the mixture in the permeable membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ethylene adsorber comprises a zeolite or an activated alumina. The ethylene oxidizer comprises potassium permanganate. The carbon dioxide releaser comprises sodium bicarbonate. The oxygen absorber/reducer comprises a metal powder. The microbial/bio-inhibitor comprises sodium metabisulfite, a silver powder, a copper powder, and an organic acid. The moisture absorber/releaser comprises clay and a silica gel. The ethylene adsorber is used to absorb ethylene. The ethylene oxidizer is used to oxidize ethylene. The bio-inhibitor is used to release microbial inhibitor, and to prevent fungal or bacterial spoilage. The carbon dioxide releaser is used to slow down the oxidation rate by having high carbon dioxide atmosphere. The oxygen absorber/reducer is used to reduce surrounding oxygen levels. The moisture absorber is used to reduce moisture. The chemicals can be selected to perform just one function or multiple functions depending upon the type of fresh produce and its characteristics including water content, ethylene release properties, storage conditions and period required.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The permeable membrane comprises Tyvek (RTM: high-density polyethylene fibers), a nonwoven fabric, a woven fabric, a perforated paper, and/or a non-perforated paper, which is coated with a sealing layer that allows for permeation of the gases and water vapor, such that the gases can come into contact with the contents of the sachets. The sealing layer comprises polyethylene, Tyvek (RTM: high-density polyethylene fibers), and a combination of paper and polyethylene. The sachet comprises 0.1 g to 100 kg contents. Preferred Property: The sachet has a thickness of 10-300 mu m or 10-300 gsm.

TI Method for measuring mine volume by using gas concentration difference, involves selecting original preselected gas concentration value in mine, recording mass of pre-selected gas and gas value in mine, and obtaining volume of mine.
AU YANG M
   GUO C
   LEI B
   WANG L
AE SHANDONG PROVINCIAL BUREAU GEOLOGY & MIN (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201979120P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a drilling borehole with mine during drilling process when a position of mine is determined. An original preselected gas concentration value in mine is selected by using a concentration detecting sensor. Multiple pre-selected gas storage devices are connected with each other to inject pre-selected gas into mine. Mass of injected pre-selected gas and the pre-selected gas concentration value in the mine is recorded, where a preset pressure reaches a threshold value. A volume of the mine is obtained, where preselected gas is carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for measuring mine volume by using a gas concentration difference.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables can accurately obtaining the volume of mine without considering specific shape of the mine, thus providing accurate data for mine backfilling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for measuring mine volume by using a gas concentration difference.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a pre-selected gas storage device.

PT P
PN CN110127804-A; CN110127804-B
TI Sewage treatment material is spherical and made of a triple-layer structure comprising core layer comprising activated sludge and active carbon-based filbert shell, middle layer, and outer layer.
AU INVENTOR U
AE SHANDONG HULI MACHINERY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   HIT ENVIRONMENT CO LTD (HITE-Non-standard)
GA 201972759P
AB    NOVELTY - A sewage treatment material is spherical and made of a triple-layer structure comprising core layer, middle layer and outer layer. The core layer comprises activated sludge and active carbon-based filbert shell at mass ratio of 10-15:1. The filbert shell is prepared by crushing filbert shell by 120-mesh sieve, performing rsupercritical carbon dioxide treatment at 6 Mpa for 20-25 minute, carbonizing at 380 degrees C for 2 hours, and carrying out activation treatment at 620 degrees C for 90 minutes. The middle layer is prepared by mixing alginic oligose, cyclodextrin, and gelatin at mass ratio of 6-8:3-5:4-6. The outer layer is prepared by modified processing mixture of attapulgite and montmorillonite as main raw material, adding super-fine carbon powder, amd sintering auxiliary agent and adhesive at mass ratio of 18:2.
   USE - Sewage treatment material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material removes chemical oxygen demand and suspended solid from the sewage, and has good removal rate, high removing effect, and high efficiency.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

TI Converting renewable energy source electricity and            hydrocarbon feedstock into liquid fuel involves            generating oxygen in an oxygen separation unit            utilizing electricity from wind or solar power,            supplying oxygen from oxygen separation unit.
AU COHN D R
   SURMA J E
   BROMBERG L
AE INENTEC INC (INEN-Non-standard)
   INENTEC INC (INEN-Non-standard)
GA 201969707F
AB    NOVELTY - Converting renewable energy source electricity                and a hydrocarbon feedstock into a liquid fuel                involves generating oxygen in an oxygen separation                unit utilizing electricity from wind or solar                power, supplying the oxygen from the oxygen                separation unit to a synthesis gas generation unit,                supplying steam to the synthesis gas generation                unit, producing synthesis gas in the synthesis gas                generation unit using a combination of steam                reformation and partial oxidation of a hydrocarbon                feedstock in the synthesis gas generation unit and                converting the synthesis gas into a fuel.
   USE - Method for converting renewable energy source                electricity and a hydrocarbon feedstock into a                liquid fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes improved use of                electricity from renewable energy sources, such as                wind or solar energy, through the conversion of                hydrocarbon feedstocks into a liquid fuel, where                feedstocks include municipal and industrial waste,                biomass, coal and natural gas, and can both reduce                the cost of producing liquid fuels from hydrocarbon                feedstocks and also decrease carbon dioxide                emissions.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23)); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)

PN CN110038592-A
TI Preparation of cerium selenide/titanium dioxide nanosheet composite, involves adding titanium dioxide to water, pouring into reaction vessel together with cerium selenide nanosheet and performing hydrothermal reaction.
AU YAN E
   XIE Y
   QI G
   XIN H
   LI C
   JIANG Z
   WU J
   TANG W
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 201966089G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cerium selenide/titanium dioxide nanosheet composite, involves (1) dissolving cerium nitrate hexahydrate in water to obtain solution (A), adding thioacetamide to solution (A), stirring uniformly, and then centrifuging, and drying the obtained solid material to obtain a cerium selenide precursor, calcining the cerium selenide precursor to obtain a cerium selenide nanosheet and (2) adding titanium dioxide to water, performing ultrasonic dispersion, and pouring into the reaction vessel together with the cerium selenide nanosheet obtained in the step (1), and performing a hydrothermal reaction at 80-90 degrees C, then separating the solid matter, washing, drying, and heat treating at 300-350 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of cerium selenide/titanium dioxide nanosheet composite used as photocatalyst for degrading organic matter and for degrading rhodamine B in sewage (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cerium selenide/titanium dioxide nanosheet composite rapidly degrade organic pollutants in waste water, especially dye organic pollutants, into non-polluting substances such as water, carbon dioxide, etc. under normal temperature, normal pressure and illumination, and has durable photocatalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the cerium selenide precursor is calcined at 300-350 degrees C under nitrogen protection. In step (2), the hydrothermal reaction is carried out at a temperature of 80-90 degrees C for 8-10 hours. In step (2), the heat treatment is carried out at 300-350 degrees C for 2-3 hours.

PT P
PN CN110013869-A; CN110013869-B
TI Carbon nitride nanosheet loaded titanium carbide            quantum dots comprises titanium carbide quantum dot is            uniformly distributed on the surface of the carbon            nitride nanosheet.
AU CAO S
   LI R
   YU J
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 201963811T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon nitride nanosheet loaded titanium                carbide quantum dots comprises titanium carbide                quantum dot is uniformly distributed on the surface                of the carbon nitride nanosheet.
   USE - Used as carbon nitride nanosheet loaded                titanium carbide quantum dots.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanosheet has good stability, no toxicity                and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the quantum dots, comprising (i)                removing titanium aluminum carbide by etching to                obtain titanium carbide powder, dispersing obtained                titanium carbide powder in dimethyl sulfoxide,                stirring, washing and removing the dimethyl                sulfoxide solvent, dispersing in deionized water,                sonicating under an inert atmosphere, adjusting pH                to 8-10 with a base, hydrothermal treating at                100-120 degrees C, and filtering to obtain an                aqueous solution of titanium carbide quantum dots,                and (ii) adding a precursor of carbon nitride to                the aqueous solution of titanium carbide quantum                dots, stirring, mixing, freezing quickly, vacuum                drying, and calcining under an inert atmosphere;                and
   (2) photocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide, comprising carbon nitride                nanosheet-supported titanium carbide quantum dot as                a catalyst under visible light.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass percentage of                  titanium carbide quantum dot is 0.2-0.8 wt.%. The                  thickness of carbon nitride nanosheet is 1-2 nm.                  The diameter of titanium carbide quantum dots is                  2-5 nm. The etchant is 40% hydrofluoric acid and                  the volume-mass ratio of titanium aluminum                  carbide and the etchant is 20 ml/g. In the step                  (ii), the precursor of the carbon nitride is                  urea, and the mass ratio of the urea to the                  titanium carbide quantum dot is 5 g:0.2-0.8 ug.                  Preferred Method: In the step (i), the etching                  treatment comprises adding titanium aluminum                  carbide to the etchant, stirring in an oil bath                  at 60 degrees C for 20-24 hours, and removing the                  supernatant after centrifugation. The step (i)                  comprises washing and removing the dimethyl                  sulfoxide solvent, preferably ethanol, and the                  washing method is centrifugal washing with                  ethanol on a high-speed centrifuge, preferably                  adding ethanol, and washing in an ultracentrifuge                  at a rotational speed of 3500 revolutions/minute                  for 4 minutes, hydrothermal treating for 6 hours,                  where the filter paper is filter paper of a 220                  nm film. In the step (ii), the freezing in liquid                  nitrogen, and the calcination conditions were                  carried out by heating at a heating rate of 5                  degrees C/minutes to 550 degrees C for 2                  hours.

PT P
PN CN109879303-A; CN109879303-B
TI Preparation of nano calcium carbonate, involves            subjecting nano calcium carbonate slurry to            carbonization to obtain nano-calcium carbonate cooked            slurry, adding hydrophilic treatment agent, obtaining            modified nano calcium carbonate slurry.
AU HE L
   MO S
   LONG F
   WANG J
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
   UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
GA 2019549630
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of nano calcium carbonate                involves (1) preparing nano calcium carbonate                slurry by carbonization method, the solid content                of the raw material calcium hydroxide slurry is                8-10%, the carbonization starting temperature is                not higher than 25 degrees C, and the carbon                dioxide content is higher than 28%, the                carbonization to slurry pH is 6.0-7.0 to end                carbonization, (2) subjecting nano-calcium                carbonate slurry to carbonization for 5-10 minutes                to obtain a nano-calcium carbonate cooked slurry,                (3) placing nano calcium carbonate cooked slurry in                sealed container, adding hydrophilic treatment                agent at room temperature to 120 degrees C,                stirring at a treatment of 1-24 hours to obtain a                modified nano calcium carbonate slurry, and (4)                subjecting modified nano calcium carbonate slurry                to centrifugation or pressure filtration, drying,                and pulverization.
   USE - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The specific surface energy is reduced, so                that the nano calcium carbonate is mono-dispersed                and stably suspended in aqueous solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (3), The                  hydrophilic treatment agent in step (3) is                  ethanol, propanol, butanol, isopropanol, ethylene                  glycol, benzyl alcohol, glycerol, ethylene glycol                  methyl ether, triethanolamine, decyl alcohol,                  secondary pentanol, sec-butanol, crotyl alcohol,                  acryl alcohol, polyethylene glycol, polyacrylic                  acid, and/or citric acid. The hydrophilic                  treatment agent is ethanol. The hydrophilic                  treatment agent is composed of ethanol and                  ethylene glycol. The hydrophilic treatment agent                  is composed of ethylene glycol methyl ether,                  and/or crotyl alcohol. The hydrophilic treatment                  agent is composed of propanol, butanol,                  isopropanol, and/or polyethylene glycol. The                  hydrophilic treatment agent is composed of decyl                  alcohol and secondary pentanol. Preferred                  Composition: The hydrophilic treating agent is                  used in an amount of 1-5 %mass based on dry mass                  of calcium carbonate. The amount of ethanol is                  1.0% of dry mass of calcium carbonate, and the                  mixture is stirred at 120 degrees C for 1 hour                  after adding a hydrophilic treatment agent. The                  amount of ethanol and ethylene glycol is 0.5% of                  dry mass of calcium carbonate, and the mixture is                  stirred at 100 degrees C for 2 hours after adding                  a hydrophilic treatment agent. The amount of                  ethylene glycol methyl ether is 0.6 %mass calcium                  carbonate dry basis. The amount of crotyl alcohol                  is 0.8 %mass calcium carbonate dry basis. The                  amount of propanol, butanol, isopropanol, and                  polyethylene glycol is 0.3 %mass calcium                  carbonate dry basis. The decyl alcohol and                  secondary pentanol are each used in an amount of                  0.5 %mass based on dry basis of calcium                  carbonate.

TI Method for filling liquid into container, involves comparing primary pressure with secondary pressure, and judging whether leakage is occurred in container or not if secondary pressure is lower than primary pressure.
AU FICKERT H
AE KHS GMBH (SALZ-C)
GA 201949100N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling a container (1) i.e. barrel with primary gas i.e. Carbon dioxide, where volume of the container is about 15-40. The container is closed and extended entire valves to outer environment. Pressure in the container is detected as primary pressure. The pressure in the container is detected as secondary pressure after predetermined time. The detected primary pressure is compared with the detected secondary pressure. Judgment is made to check whether leakage is occurred in the container or not if the secondary pressure is lower than the primary pressure. The barrel is made of polyethylene terephtalate material.
   USE - Method for filling liquid into a container.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easily detecting the leakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for filling liquid into a container.

AU TIKHONOV I A

AE TIKHONOV I A (TIKH-Individual)
   VASILEV A V (VASI-Individual)
GA 201937342J
AB    NOVELTY - Invention can be used to produce deaerated and decarbonised water and its use in heat engineering. Method of water degassing includes preliminary clarification of initial water, supply to Na-cation filters, wherein hardness of softened water is maintained in range of 0.020.1 mg-eq/l. Solution of caustic soda is dosed into the softened water; the amount of sodium hydroxide is not more than 1015% of the amount of carbon dioxide in the water. Solution of sodium sulphite is then dosed into the water, wherein the amount of sodium sulphite is selected based on the concentration of dissolved oxygen in the treated water. Then, water is directed to reverse-osmosis water desalination unit, at that ratio of permeate to initial flow is set within 7590%. Obtained partially desalted and degassed permeate is supplied to consumer, concentrate is drained into sewerage.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Method provides higher efficiency of chemical bonding of oxygen dissolved in water, excludes increase of salt content of treated water, and also considerably reduces total salt content of treated water.

AE FUJIAN LONGKING CO LTD (FJLJ-C)
GA 2019394723
AB    NOVELTY - Loaded metal catalyst comprises                three-dimensional multi-stage structure titanium                dioxide and a palladium-gold core-shell bimetal                loaded on the titanium dioxide, where                three-dimensional multi-stage structure titanium                dioxide is formed of nanorod assembled with                titanium dioxide nanoparticle and/or nanosheet                assembled of titanium dioxide nanoparticle.
   USE - The metal catalyst is useful for processing                volatile organic compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal catalyst: has carbon dioxide                selectivity greater than or equal to 99%; and                improves the catalytic oxidation volatile organic                compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing carrying out hydrothermal                reaction of glacial acetic acid with tetrabutyl                titanate, and calcining to obtain three-dimensional                multi-level structure titanium dioxide, then                impregnating the three-dimensional multi-stage                structure titanium dioxide with palladium salt and                gold salt, and carrying out reduction reaction to                obtain loaded metal catalyst; and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The three-dimensional                  multi-stage structure titanium dioxide is                  dendritic framework with nanorod assembled by                  titanium dioxide nanoparticle; and nanosheet                  assembled by the titanium dioxide nanoparticle is                  distributed along the dendritic framework. The                  palladium-gold core-shell bimetal has palladium                  as shell and gold as core. The palladium loading                  amount of the palladium-gold core-shell bimetal                  is 1-10 wt.%; and atomic ratio between palladium                  and gold in the palladium-gold core-shell bimetal                  is 1-3:1-3. The size of the palladium-gold                  core-shell bimetal is 2-16 nm; and thickness of                  the palladium shell in the palladium-gold                  core-shell bimetal is 1-1.5 nm. The loaded metal                  catalyst further comprises three-dimensional                  multi-stage structure titanium dioxide and active                  component loaded on the titanium dioxide, where                  three-dimensional multi-stage structure titanium                  dioxide is formed by nanorods assembled by                  titanium dioxide nanoparticles and nanosheets                  assembled by titanium dioxide nanoparticles,                  nanorods assembled by titanium dioxide                  nanoparticles are dendritic framework,                  nano-sheets assembled by titanium dioxide                  nanoparticles are distributed along the dendritic                  framework, and active component is palladium and                  gold. The three-dimensional multi-level structure                  titanium dioxide comprises nanorods assembled                  from titanium dioxide nanoparticles and                  nanosheets assembled with titanium dioxide                  nanoparticles, nanorods assembled by titanium                  dioxide nanoparticles are dendritic framework,                  and nanosheets assembled by the titanium dioxide                  nanoparticles are distributed along the dendritic                  framework.

PN CN109569236-A
TI Absorbing electrolytic tail gas generates in molten salt electrolysis of rare earth metals and alloy comprises discharging electrolytic tail gas, removing fluoride particle, spraying, sedimenting and reacting with fluorine-containing gas.
AU LUAN W
   YAN H
   ZENG J
   HAN L
   ZHOU L
   PANG Z
   DENG Z
   YANG G
   YAN J
   XIA S
   GUO L
   ZHU Q
   XIAO Y
   XU J
   WANG X
   YOU F
   WEN T
   HE J
AE LESHAN GRIREM ADVANCED MATERIALS CO LTD (BFER-C)
GA 201933626T
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbing electrolytic tail gas generates in molten salt electrolysis of rare earth metals and alloys comprises (i) discharging the electrolytic tail gas into a cyclone dust collecting device, removing fluoride particles from the tail gas to obtain tail gas, (ii) discharging the tail gas into a rinsing tower, spraying tail gas with an aqueous solution of rare earth oxide, sedimenting the particulate matter in the tail gas, reacting with the fluorine-containing gas and carbon dioxide in the tail gas to generate rare earth fluoride precipitate to obtain recycled liquid and tail gas.
   USE - The method is useful for absorbing electrolytic tail gas generates in molten salt electrolysis of rare earth metals and alloys.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; reduces harmful substances in the exhaust gas; generates rare earth fluoride is added to the electrolytic furnace as an electrolytic raw material, which ensures the recycling of the electrolytic tail gas treatment products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises taking solid rare earth oxide or the solid rare earth hydroxide as a filler in the exhaust chimney, discharging the tail gas into a exhaust stack, absorption of the fluorine-containing gas in the tail gas to obtain rare earth fluoride solid. The (iv) comprises replacing the filler in the chimney after one cycle of production. The step (v) comprises subjecting the recovered liquid to pressure filtration, drying and washing to obtain rare earth fluoride solid. The step (vi) comprises adding the rare earth fluoride solids obtained in the steps (ii) and (iv) to an electrolytic furnace as an electrolytic raw material. The step (ii) comprises controlling the temperature of the rinsing tower at 0-60 degrees C, preferably 25-40 degrees C . Preferred Components: The rare earth oxide comprises lanthanum oxide, praseodymium oxide, cerium(III) oxide, neodymium(III) oxide, samarium(III) oxide, europium(III) oxide, gadolinium(III) oxide, terbium oxide, dysprosium(III) oxide, holmium(III) oxide, erbium oxide, thulium(III) oxide, ytterbium(III) oxide, lutetium oxide, yttrium oxide and/or scandium oxide.

AU HOU J

   GUO H
   JIANG J
   WANG X
   PEI Y
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
   UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 201928764G
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-layer composite artificial blood vessel with adjustable compliance preparing method involves dissolving polycaprolactone, poly(lactic acid) and thermoplastic polyurethane to (7-9): (1-3) : (0- 2) blend granulation, and melting the pellet at 190-220 degrees C and hot pressing to obtain a film. The aluminum foil paper layer is wrapped on the solid cylinder, the film is wrapped on the aluminum foil paper layer, followed by wrapping a layer of aluminum foil paper, placing it in a hollow cylinder, placing it in a high pressure reactor using supercritical carbon dioxide micropores to obtain a single-layer composite foam tube by a bubble method, nesting three different elastic modulus single-layer composite foam tubes, and filling the gap between the adjacent single-layer composite foam tubes with gel or protein to obtain a multi-layer composite artificial blood vessel with adjustable compliance.

GA 201919110H
AB    NOVELTY - A raw materials of rifaximin and its components detection method involves preparing sample by dissolving raw materials of rifaximin and its components using organic solvent, performing supercritical fluid chromatography by utilizing carbon dioxide and lower alcohol as mobile phase and performing gradient elution and utilizing detector to determine composition of the sample and its content, where components of the sample include rifaximin and impurities and the impurities include 2-amino-4-methylpyridine, rifamycin B, rifamycin SV, rifaximin Y, rifamycin S, rifamycin O, impurity A, impurity B or hydroxy-rifaximin.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent includes ethanol, methanol or isopropyl alcohol. The lower alcohol includes methanol, ethanol or isopropyl alcohol. The lower alcohol is ethanol or mixture of ethanol and isopropanol. Preferred Process: The method involves utilizing pH adjusting agent including formic acid, glacial acetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid or ammonia solution. The method involves performing supercritical fluid chromatography using carbon dioxide as mobile phase A and ethanol-isopropyl alcohol (90:10 volume/volume%) as mobile phase B.

PN CN109329394-A
TI Composite preservation method of late-maturing grapes by pre-cooling grapes, placing in closed environment, fumigating with bactericide comprising calcium chloride, adding slow-release preservative comprising beta-cyclodextrin and storing.
AU JI M
   JI J
   MAO W
   KUMIGEN K
   RUI D
   DONG X
   XU M
   LI W
   JI K
   LI L
   MU Y
   MEN H
   LIU G
AE JURONG DINGZHUANG GRAPE RES INST (JURO-Non-standard)
GA 2019179212
AB    NOVELTY - Composite preservation method of late-maturing grapes involves (a) selecting fresh picked late-maturing grapes without mechanical damage and disease and insect pests and pre-cooling after trimming, (b) placing the pre-cooled grapes in a closed environment and fumigating with a bactericide and (c) placing the obtained grapes in step (b) in an air-conditioning fresh-keeping box, adding a slow-release preservative, adjusting the volume percentage of carbon dioxide in the storage atmosphere to 20-30% and the volume percentage of oxygen is 50-60% and storing for more than 150 days, where the bactericide comprises clove oil, cinnamon oil, Tween 80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate), sodium alginate, calcium chloride and citric acid. The slow-release preservative is beta -cyclodextrin inclusion compound of thyme essential oil, lemon essential oil and ginger juice.
   USE - Composite preservation method of late-maturing grapes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves storage life, ensures quality and is substantially free of residual sulfur dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The pre-cooling in step (a) is carried out at 0-4 degrees C for 18-24 hours. The storage atmosphere in step (c) further comprises 100-150 mu l/l nitrogen and 300-400 mu l/l sulfur dioxide. The fresh-keeping box in step (c) has a relative humidity of 75-80% at -1-1 degrees C. The ginger juice is prepared by cutting ginger blocks into fragments, mixing the ginger blocks in distilled water in a material-to-liquid ratio of 1:10, performing microwave-assisted extraction at 70-80 degrees C for three times, evaporating and concentrating to obtain the juice. Preferred Composition: The bactericide comprises 0.5-1 wt.% clove oil, 0.5-1 wt.% cinnamon oil, 0.05-0.2 wt.% Tween 80 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate), 1-2 wt.% sodium alginate, 0.5-1 wt.% calcium chloride and 0.2-0.4 wt.% citric acid. The slow-release preservative is beta -cyclodextrin inclusion compound of 3-6 wt.% thyme essential oil, 1-3 wt.% lemon essential oil and 0.5-1 wt.% ginger juice.

PT P
PN US10160675-B1
TI Filtering and treating contaminated aqueous liquid comprising methyl-tert-butyl ether involves pre-filtering contaminated aqueous liquid with one selected from submicron filter, carbon filter, and oil filter to remove particulate matter.
AU KEDIR A J
   TAWABINI B
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2018A3036C
AB    NOVELTY - Filtering and treating a contaminated aqueous liquid comprising methyl-tert-butyl ether (MTBE) involves pre-filtering the contaminated aqueous liquid with a selected from submicron filter, carbon filter, and oil filter to remove particulate matter and long-chain fatty organic molecules, mixing chlorine and/or a hypochlorous acid salt with the filtered contaminated aqueous liquid to form a chlorinated aqueous liquid comprising 10-50 parts per million of chlorine and/or the hypochlorous acid salt, relative to the total mass of the chlorinated aqueous liquid, and irradiating the chlorinated aqueous liquid with light having a wavelength of 254 nm from a monochromatic light source that generates a 254 nm wavelength of light or with a polychromatic light source having wavelengths between 200-370 nm to oxidize MTBE and form a degradation byproduct selected from tert-butyl formate (TBF), tert-butyl alcohol (TBA), acetone, carbon dioxide and water, and forming a decontaminated aqueous liquid.
   USE - Method for filtering and treating contaminated aqueous liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The process further involves mixing the chlorinated aqueous liquid with an oxygen radical generating compound, and adjusting the pH of the contaminated aqueous liquid to a pH of 5-7 before the mixing. Preferred Conditions: The oxygen radical generating compound is ozone and/or hydrogen peroxide. The ozone and/or hydrogen peroxide is present at 10-25 times the concentration of the chlorine and/or the hypochlorous acid salt in the chlorinated aqueous liquid. The concentration of the chlorine and/or hypochlorous acid salt is 10-50 times a concentration of the MTBE in the chlorinated aqueous liquid. The pH of the contaminated aqueous liquid is adjusted with hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, or sodium hydroxide. The light source has a power between 10-200 watt. The light source has an intensity between 5x 10 3-7x 10 2 watt/cm2. The irradiating generates a chlorine radical, hydroxyl radical, or both from the chlorine and/or a hypochlorous acid salt present in the chlorinated aqueous liquid. The concentration of MTBE is 1 parts per million in the contaminated aqueous liquid and the concentration of MTBE is reduced by more than 92% in the decontaminated aqueous liquid. The chlorine is removed from the decontaminated aqueous liquid by thiosulfate, UV photolysis, radical oxidation, and chemical oxidation by free chlorine.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PN EP3399008-A1; EP3399008-B1
TI Petrol used for operating internal combustion engine, comprises component comprising dimethyl carbonate, and components comprising methyl formate and/or dimethoxymethane and ethanol and/or methanol, and having preset amount of methanol.
AU WILHARM T
   SEIDENSPINNER P
   JACOB E
AE ASG ANALYTIK SERVICE GMBH (ASGA-Non-standard)
GA 2018869174
AB    NOVELTY - Petrol comprises a component (c1) comprising dimethyl carbonate, a component (c2) comprising methyl formate, dimethoxymethane or a mixture of methyl formate and dimethoxymethane, optionally component (c3) comprising ethanol, methanol or a mixture of ethanol and methanol, and optionally up to 10 wt.% component (c4). The total content of methanol in the petrol is at most 1.75 vol.%.
   USE - Petrol is used as fuel for operating internal combustion engines with spark ignition (claimed) used for passenger cars and hybrid vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The petrol is environmentally-friendly and potentially carbon dioxide-neutral petrol based on the 1C chemistry. The petrol allows a significant reduction in soot particle emissions and achieves sub-zero emissions, with reduced pollutants of a particular-type leaving the vehicle than being supplied to the combustion air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of petrol, which involves mixing dimethyl carbonate with methyl formate and/or dimethoxy methane and optionally with ethanol and/or methanol and optionally mixing 10 wt.% further components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The petrol preferably comprises 2 wt.% or less component (c4). The total content of methanol in the petrol is preferably 0.10 vol.%. The component (c2) comprises at least 50 vol.%, preferably 100 vol.% methyl formate. The petrol comprises at least 40 vol.%, preferably at least 65 vol.% component (c1), at least 30 vol.%, preferably at most 40 vol.% component (c2), 0-10 vol.% component (c3), at most 1.0 vol.% preferably at most 10 ppm (volume/volume)% hydrocarbons, at most 10.0 vol.%, preferably at most 0.1 vol.% compounds with a direct carbon-carbon bond excluding ethanol, and at most 0.5 vol.%, preferably at most 0.1 vol.% water. Preferred Properties: The petrol has a volatility index (VLI) of 1150 or less, a dry vapor pressure equivalent (at 37.8 degrees C) of 90 kPa or less, preferably 60 kPa or less, a content of diesel fuels such as diesel, paraffin diesel and dimethyl ether measured according to EN590 and EN15490 of at most 0.2 wt.%, and an octane number of at least 90, preferably at least 105.

PT P
PN CN108707502-A; CN108707502-B
TI Preparing crocodile oil in preparing medicine or cosmetic, involves mixing crocodile fat tissue and pretreatment liquid, and soaking to obtain pre-treated crocodile fat tissue.
AU ZHOU H
   DUAN M
AE ZHOU H (ZHOU-Individual)
   HUBEI EBAO BIOTECHNOLOGY RES & DEV CO (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 201886118M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing crocodile oil involves mixing crocodile fat tissue and pretreatment liquid in (0.5-15) kg:(3-45) L ratio, and soaking for 0.5-2 hours to obtain pre-treated crocodile fat tissue, where pretreatment liquid as mixed aqueous solution containing sodium chloride and first antioxidant, and first antioxidant selected from citric acid, vitamin C, tea polyphenols and carnosic acid, crushing the pretreated crocodile adipose tissue with adsorbent, alkane organic solvent and second antioxidant in 1:(0.5-3):(0.5-10):(0.0001-0.001) ratio, mixing, and subjecting to subcritical extraction to obtain crude crocodile oil, where second antioxidant selected from vitamin E, tea polyphenol and carnosic acid, and decolorizing and depurinating crude oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain crocodile oil.
   USE - Method used for preparing crocodile oil in preparing medicine or cosmetic (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method offers crocodile oil with high purity and good quality, can be used in cosmetics, and effectively improve the moisture content of the user's skin, play a role in moisturizing and repairing the skin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: Preparing crocodile oil involves using pretreatment liquid containing 0.1-2% sodium chloride and 0.01-0.05% first antioxidant. Preferred Components: The absorbent is selected from active carclazyte, alumina, silica gel and activated carbon. The alkane organic solvent is butane or propane. Preferred Conditions: The subcritical condition involves using 20-50 degrees C extraction temperature and 0.1-1 MPa extracting pressure. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction decolorization and depurination involves 30-40 degrees C extraction temperature, 15-45 MPa extraction pressure, 10-25 MPa separation pressure and 30-40 degrees C separation temperature. The cosmetic is formulated as oil solution, oil stick, emulsion or cream.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN CN108623293-A
TI Preparation of high-white ceramic material and process acid from phosphogypsum and red mud involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifier, grinding, adding water, dissolving, and solid-liquid separating.
AU CHEN X
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201880227P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-white ceramic material and process acid from phosphogypsum and red mud comprises mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifier, grinding, adding water at volume ratio of 4-6:1, dissolving, solid-liquid separating, collecting calcium oxide, stirring, filtering, feeding carbon dioxide gas to high-purity sodium metaaluminate solution, filtering, adding hydrochloric acid to precipitate, washing, stirring, filtering, roasting, adding aluminum oxide powder, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, calcium carbonate, silicon dioxide, silicate, lithium carbonate, barium sulfate, yttrium oxide, cerium oxide, boron, titanium dioxide and zinc oxide, mixing, grinding, calcining, cooling, ball milling, performing flotation, separating sulfide, roasting at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours under oxygen-rich environment, catalytic reacting with vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid.
   USE - Method for preparing high-white ceramic material and process acid from phosphogypsum and red mud.
   ADVANTAGE - The material is low cost, and has high density and low thermal expansion coefficient. The preparation method has high waste utilization rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or sodium hydroxide. The mass ratio of phosphogypsum and red mud is 1:0.7-1.8. The mixed raw material comprises 9-12 pts. wt. aluminum oxide powder, 12-14 pts. wt. potassium carbonate, 7-9 pts. wt. sodium carbonate, 1-1.3 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 29-32 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 8-12 pts. wt. silicate, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. lithium carbonate, 0.7-1.2 pts. wt. barium sulfate, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. yttrium oxide, 1-3 pts. wt. cerium oxide, 1-2 pts. wt. boron, 19-21 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, and 0.05-0.2 pt. wt. zinc oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue.

PT P
PN CN108622964-A
TI Performing plasma evaporation involves using hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution as excitation target, generating plasma by direct current discharge method, and ejecting plasma from bottom of evaporation chamber.
AU YIN H
AE JIANGSU WOQING ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201880652F
AB    NOVELTY - Performing plasma evaporation involves using hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution as an excitation target, a plasma having a temperature of 4000 to 4200 degrees C is generated by a 270-280 A direct current discharge method. The plasma is ejected from the bottom of the evaporation chamber and directly contacts the liquid for heat and mass transfer. The liquid absorbs heat and evaporates, and the generated steam exits the evaporation chamber together with the plasma. The large amount of atomic oxygen, superoxide anion radicals, hydroxyl radicals and high-energy electrons carried in the plasma have strong oxidizing power and decompose volatile organic pollutants in steam into carbon dioxide and water, and improves the removal rate of organic pollutants in wastewater by evaporation process. The steam is finally condensed to obtain the evaporator water.
   USE - Method for performing plasma evaporation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform plasma evaporation, which improves the removal rate of organic pollutants in wastewater by evaporation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Condition: The hydrogen peroxide solution was used as the excitation target of the plasma. The hydrogen peroxide is specifically hydrogen peroxide and sodium percarbonate. Due to the difference in wastewater quality, the amount of hydrogen peroxide used is determined by a small test. The plasma is generated by a 270-280 A DC current discharge. The generated plasma temperature is between 4,000 and 4,200 degrees C.The large amount of atomic oxygen, superoxide anion radicals, hydroxyl radicals and high-energy electrons carried in the plasma have strong oxidizing power and is capable of degrading volatile organic pollutants in steam.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC D04-A01A; D04-A01K2; E11-Q01B; E31-E01; E31-E02

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrode comprising a support                containing an aluminum-based material, vertically                aligned carbon nanotubes, and an electroconductive                polymer matrix, involves (i) synthesizing a mat of                carbon nanotubes vertically aligned on the support                at a temperature of 650 degrees C or less, and (ii)                electrochemically depositing the polymer matrix on                the carbon nanotubes using an electrolytic solution                comprising at least one precursor monomer of the                matrix, at least one ionic liquid and at least one                protic or aprotic solvent.
   USE - Preparation of electrode used as                positive/negative electrode for device e.g.                supercapacitor or battery for storing and                recovering electricity, electrode for photovoltaic                device, material for storing carbon dioxide or                electrode for electrochemical sensors (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of electrode                with improved capacitance performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) device, which comprises an asymmetrical                assembly with the electrode and a single or double                layer activated carbon electrode; and
   (2) manufacture of device, which involves                assembling the two electrodes with an interface                electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Process: The step (i) is carried                  out at a temperature of 500-620 degrees C. The                  method involves subjecting the vertically aligned                  carbon nanotubes to an oxidizing treatment after                  the step (i) and before the step (ii), rinsing                  and optionally drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The density of the                  vertically aligned carbon nanotubes is 106 to                  1013 nanotubes/cm2, preferably 1011 to 1012                  nanotubes/cm2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ionic liquid is                  an ionic liquid having at least one optionally                  substituted protic or aprotic cation chosen from                  pyridinium, pyridazinium, pyrimidinium,                  pyrazinium, imidazolium, pyrazolium, thiazolium,                  oxazolium, triazolium, ammonium, pyrrolidinium,                  pyrrolinium, pyrrolium and piperidinium and at                  least one optionally substituted organic or                  non-organic anion chosen from F-, (Claimed), Br,                  I-, NO3-, N(CN)2-, BF4-, ClO4-, RS04, RSO3-,                  RCOO-, (CF3)2PF4, (CF3)3PF3-, (CF3)4PF3-,                  (CF3)5PF-, (CF3)6P-, (CF2SO3-)2, (CF2CF2SO3)2,                  (CF3SO2)2N-, CF3CF2(CF3)2CO-, (CF3SO2)2CH-,                  (SF5)3C-, (CF3SO2)3C-, CF3(CF2)7SO3-,                  bis(oxalato)borate and anion derived from                  imidazole. The ionic liquid is chosen from                  dialkylpyrrolidinium                  bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide,                  dialkylpyrrolidinium                  bis(fluoromethylsulfonyl)imide,                  dialkylpyrrolidinium tetrafluoroborate,                  dialkylpyrrolidinium hexafluorophosphate,                  dialkylpyrrolidinium selenocyanate,                  dialkylpyrrolidinium thiocyanate,                  dialkylpyrrolidinium bromide,                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium                  bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide,                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium                  bis(fluoromethylsulfonyl)imide,                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate,                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate,                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium selenocyanate,                  1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium thiocyanate,                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium                  bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide,                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate,                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate,                  1-methyl-3-n-hexylimidazolium iodide and                  butyltrimethylammonium                  bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide. The aprotic                  solvent is acetonitrile.R = alkyl or phenyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The electroconductive                  polymer matrix comprises one or more (co)polymers                  chosen from polypyrroles, polycarbazoles,                  polyanilines and polythiophenes. Preferred                  Property: The viscosity of the electrolytic                  solution is 0.37-200 mPa.second, preferably                  1.0-36 mPa.second under standard conditions of                  temperature and pressure.

TI Nuclear explosion type fluid piston two-stroke engine water pump combination, has motive power machine for transferring fluid piston power pump water, where motive power machine is repeatedly operated according to program.
AU WANG D
AE WANG D (WANG-Individual)
GA 201871225E
AB    NOVELTY - The combination has a motive power machine for transferring fluid piston power pump water. The motive power machine is repeatedly operated according to filling process. A lower container injects water into an explosion chamber through an inlet pipe. A nuclear explosive device is placed in an explosive indoor medium to fill a proper position through a filling tube. A water inlet valve injects pump water into a position container through a drain pipe. An admission pipe generates carbon dioxide is during valve operation. A cylinder tail is bended at an exhaust device to prevent a water vapor.
   USE - Nuclear explosion type fluid piston two-stroke engine water pump combination.
   ADVANTAGE - The combination satisfies running requirement of a water inlet valve, and completes corresponding valve operation after completing filling process in simple manner so as to easily circulate water back and forth in the water inlet valve.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a nuclear explosion type fluid piston two-stroke engine water pump combination.

PT P
PN CN108432993-A
TI Preparing green algae beverage based on marine microbial enzyme, by washing fresh green algae, cutting, homogenizing, adding e.g. Lactobacillus plantarum, fermenting, mixing with e.g. hop pellets, and syrup, and carbonating product.
AU YIN H
   DONG J
   YU J
   TIAN Y
   HUANG S
   QIAN Z
   LI M
   ZHANG Y
   WANG J
   HU X
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 2018706617
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a green algae beverage based on marine microbial enzyme, involves (a) washing fresh green algae raw material or drying green algae, cutting washed or dried green algae, adding water, homogenizing, transferring into fermenter for sterilization, and adding Bacillus subtilis suspension and Lactobacillus plantarum suspension for fermentation at different temperature, and performing solid-liquid separation, (b) mixing hop pellets with purified pure water in a ratio of 1:100-150, boiling for 1 minute, cooling the boiled product to room temperature and filtering the cooled product, (c) mixing 0.25-3 %mass product obtained in step (a), 1-10 %mass product obtained in step (b), 2-5 %mass syrup, 0.01-0.05 %mass acidulant, and water (remainder) and uniformly stirring mixture, and (d) carbonating product using carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing green algae beverage based on marine microbial enzyme.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of seaweed fishy odor, and has excellent drinking property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (c) involves adding 0.5-1 %mass product obtained in step (a), and 2-6 %mass product obtained in step (b). The content of carbon dioxide in beverage is 0.35-0.65%. The method involves inoculating 1.5% B.subtilis suspension and culturing at 40 degrees C for 3 days, reducing temperature to 30 degrees C, adding 1% L.plantarum suspension, culturing at 30 degrees C for 5 minutes and allowing the culture to stand for 2 days. The syrup is fructose syrup or glucose syrup. The acidulant is citric acid, malic acid or lactic acid. The hops are fragrant hops or bitter fragrant hops include Qingdao Big Flower, Tettanger, Saaz, Hallertauer MFR, or citral. The green algae includes Ulva lactuca or Enteromorpha.

PN CN108276748-A
TI Plastic film material contains polycarbonate, modified poly-beta-hydroxybutyrate, soybean polysaccharide, wood fiber, silane coupling agent, sodium diacetate, diisononyl phthalate, oxidized starch, kaolin nano-powder and antioxidant.
AU ZHAO J
   HUANG L
AE FOSHAN YINNUOWEI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201856091V
AB    NOVELTY - A plastic film material comprises 20-25 pts. wt. polycarbonate, 65-70 pts. wt. modified poly-( beta )-hydroxybutyrate, 13-18 pts. wt. konjac powder, 15-18 pts. wt. soybean polysaccharide, 3-4 pts. wt. wood fiber, 1.1-1.3 pts. wt. silane coupling agent, 1.1-1.6 pts. wt. sodium diacetate, 2-2.2 pts. wt. diisononyl phthalate, 5-7 pts. wt. epoxidized butyl oleate, 2-4 pts. wt. oxidized starch, 20-23 pts. wt. nano-white carbon black, 6-8 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate, 3-5 pts. wt. kaolin nano-powder, and 1-1.4 pts. wt. antioxidant. The modified poly-( beta )-hydroxybutyrate is obtained by mixing poly-( beta )-hydroxybutyrate and high cis-butadiene rubber.
   USE - Plastic film material.
   ADVANTAGE - The plastic film material can be degraded by microorganisms into carbon dioxide and water without irreversible damage to the environment, and has greatly improved tensile strength, elongation at break, wear resistance and tear resistance, and prolonged service life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antioxidant is pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate) (antioxidant 1010), octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate (antioxidant 1076), and N,N'-hexamethylenebis(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyhydrocinnamamide) (antioxidant 1098).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The plastic film material is prepared by adding silane coupling agent to soybean polysaccharide and wood fiber, stirring at speed of 1000 rpm for 10 minutes, adding konjac powder, stirring at speed of 500 rpm for 30 minutes, adding polycarbonate and modified poly-( beta )-hydroxybutyrate, increasing the temperature to 210 degrees C, continuously stirring the mixture at speed of 500 rpm for 5 minutes, adding sodium diacetate, diisononyl phthalate, epoxidized butyl oleate, oxidized starch, nano-white carbon black, light calcium carbonate, kaolin nano-powder, and antioxidant, and stirring at speed of 25-30 rpm for 25-28 minutes to obtain mixed material, adding the mixed material to a flat vulcanizing machine, hot pressing for 5-10 minutes, taking out, and cooling to room temperature, where the forming pressure of the flat plate is 13-16 MPa and the forming temperature is 153-155 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The plastic film material preferably comprises 25 pts. wt. polycarbonate, 70 pts. wt. modified poly-( beta )-hydroxybutyrate, 18 pts. wt. konjac powder, 1.8 pts. wt. soybean polysaccharide, 4 pts. wt. wood fiber, 1.3 pts. wt. silane coupling agent, 1.6 pts. wt. sodium diacetate, 2.2 pts. wt. diisononyl phthalate, 7 pts. wt. epoxidized butyl oleate, 4 pts. wt. oxidized starch, 23 pts. wt. nano-white carbon black, 8 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate, 5 pts. wt. kaolin nano-powder, and 1.4 pts. wt. antioxidant.

PN CN108238617-A; CN108238617-B
TI Producing anhydrous magnesium carbonate by carbonization method comprises e.g. preparing reaction slurry using magnesium hydroxide and deionized water and then adding reaction slurry and phase transfer catalyst into carbonization tower.
AU LIU R
   DONG W
   ZHAO H
   HU Y
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 201853954Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing anhydrous magnesium carbonate by carbonization method comprises (i) preparing reaction slurry using magnesium hydroxide and deionized water, (ii) adding reaction slurry obtained in step (i) into carbonization tower, adding phase transfer catalyst to carbonization tower, and then introducing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction to obtain anhydrous magnesium carbonate slurry; the phase transfer catalyst is soluble metal chloride salt solution, (iii) filtering the anhydrous magnesium carbonate slurry obtained in step (ii) to obtain anhydrous magnesium carbonate solid filter cake and filtrate, and (iv) drying the anhydrous magnesium carbonate solid filter cake to obtain anhydrous magnesium carbonate product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing anhydrous magnesium carbonate by carbonization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low production cost; and is environmentally friendly and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for anhydrous magnesium carbonate obtained by above method, where the particle diameter of resultant anhydrous magnesium carbonate is 2-8 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the phase transfer catalyst has a concentration of the reaction slurry of 10-100 g/l; and the phase transfer catalyst is sodium chloride (preferred), potassium chloride, lithium chloride, magnesium chloride, strontium chloride, barium chloride, and/or ammonium chloride. The concentration of sodium chloride in the reaction slurry is 20-80 g/l. In step (i), the solid content of magnesium hydroxide in the reaction slurry is 50-100 g/l; and the magnesium hydroxide has a particle size of 200-300 nm. Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises returning filtrate to reaction slurry obtained in step (i). In step (ii), the carbon dioxide content of the carbon dioxide gas is greater than 99 wt.%, the pressure at the top of carbonization column is 0.2-1 MPa, the carbonization reaction temperature is 120-180 degrees C, and the carbonization time is 6-20 hours.

GA 201846352M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting target value of multiple indexes associated with tunnel environment. Multiple detecting points are set in a tunnel, where the tunnel environment includes nitrogen oxide concentration, carbon dioxide concentration, visibility and temperature in the tunnel. Detection value of each index is collected at current time. A predicting model is established by deep learning according to each index of the detection value. Predicted value of each index is predicted according to the detection value of each index at next time. Ventilation air quantity of the tunnel is adjusted based on the predicted value and the target value.
   USE - Environment prediction based tunnel ventilation controlling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing hysteresis of air amount feedback control, and improving control precision of the ventilating air quantity.
