TI Preparation of plant-based industrial cleaning agent based on barley and hops involves preparing germinated barley solution, preparing hop flower extract, preparing plant-based cleaning agent, adding additives and mixing.
AU LIU W
AE WANFENG AVIATION IND CO LTD (WANF-Non-standard)
GA 201844966Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of plant-based industrial cleaning agent based on barley and hops involves crushing barley, soaking barley in water for 36-48 hours for saccharification, taking out barley to obtain germinated barley solution, picking buds of fresh hops, dipping in 70-75% ethanol solution, filtering, removing residues, concentrating, treating to obtain hop flower extract, adding hop flower extract to germinated barley solution by spraying, adding water, heating, stirring, cooling to room temperature to obtain malt/hop composite pulp, introducing oxygen into malt/hop compound pulp, adding wild yeast for fermentation for 8-12 hours, filtering, transferring filtrate into wooden barrel for continuous fermentation, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering to obtain plant-based cleaning agent, adding additives into plant-based cleaning agent and uniformly mixing.
   USE - Preparation of plant-based industrial cleaning agent based on barley and hops (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The industrial cleaning agent prepared is green and environmentally-friendly, has excellent decontamination performance and is prepared by combining biological fermentation with physical technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The additive is higher fatty acid salt, acid salt, sulfonate salt, sulfate salt, phosphate salt and/or fatty acid salt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The particle size of crushed barley is 100-300 mu m. The hop stamen extract is pellet, jelly or essential oil. Preferred Composition: The malt concentration of germinated barley solution is 55-60% and ( alpha )-amino nitrogen content is 180-200 mg/100 g wort. The ratio of hop stamen to ethanol solution is 1-2 g:5-10 mL. The solid content of malt/hop composite pulp is 34-45 wt.%. The oxygen gas flow is 0.2-0.4 mL/minute. The carbon dioxide gas flow is 0.3-0.5 mL/minute.

PT P
PN CN114291794-A; CN216725869-U
TI Method for leaching high-sulfur slag by wet metallurgical oxygen pressure leaching, involves introducing liquid carbon dioxide into extracting tank, and extracting phase after extraction enters into receiving separating tank and buffer tank.
AU LIU G
   SHI Q
   CHENG L
   ZHANG M
   LIU Z
AE SHAANXI JUTAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
   SHAANXI JUTAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202262955M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves entering the receiving separation tank (11) and the buffer tank (12) from the extraction tank (7) by the extraction phase after the extraction is completed. The carbon dioxide in the non-supercritical state separated by the receiving separation tank and the buffer tank is discharged from the top of the buffer tank in gaseous form, and is stored in a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (9) after being compressed into a liquid state by a compressor (13). The pressure in the receiving separation tank and the buffer tank are dropped to normal pressure after the carbon dioxide separation of the described non-supercritical state is completed. The elemental sulfur remaining at the bottom of the tank is extracted after the separation of the receiving separation tank and the buffer tank. The liquid is discharged from the bottom valve in the form of liquid after the temperature adjustment of the receiving separation tank and the buffer tank.
   USE - Method for leaching high-sulfur slag by wet metallurgy oxygen pressure leaching. Uses include but are not limited to nickel, cobalt, copper, vanadium, molybdenum, gold, silver, lead, zinc, indium, germanium and gallium.
   ADVANTAGE - The wet metallurgy oxygen pressure leaching high-sulphur slag recycling sulfur process achieves high efficiency, environment-friendly, not only recycles the valuable elemental sulfur with high quality, but also reduces the leaching slag, lays the foundation for further recycling valuable rare element in the slag.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the overall structure of a device for recovering sulfur by wet metallurgical oxygen pressure leaching of high-sulfur slag.

PT P
PN CN114471124-A
TI Carbon-sulfur emission reducing and carbon-fixing            sulfur fixing method in technical field of            environmental protection, involves preparing liquid            ammonia and water into ammonia water with certain            concentration, and crushing carbon dioxide and sulfur            oxide absorption tower.
AU WANG J
AE NANJING WOYE CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202275955K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-sulfur emission reducing and                carbon-fixing sulfur fixing method involves                preparing liquid ammonia and water into ammonia                water with a certain concentration, crushing carbon                dioxide and sulfur oxide absorption tower,                controlling the temperature and pressure in the                absorption tower, removing the flue gas discharged                from the flue, introducing the sulfur oxide in the                air oxidation flue gas, then introducing into the                absorption tower, reacting with ammonia water for                gas-liquid phase reaction, absorbing and                neutralizing the carbon dioxide in the flue gas,                sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide for gas-liquid                reaction, respectively ammonium carbonate, ammonium                sulfite and ammonium sulfate, ammonium carbonate,                absorbing carbon dioxide to form ammonium hydrogen                carbonate.
   USE - Method for reducing emission of carbon and                sulfur and fixing carbon and sulfur used in the                technical field of environmental protection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short process flow, mature                technology, reliable, low device investment, low                operation cost, eliminates greenhouse effect caused                by carbon dioxide emission and environment                pollution caused by sulfur dioxide discharge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon-sulfur emission reducing and                carbon-fixing sulfur fixing method involves                preparing liquid ammonia and water into ammonia                water with a certain concentration, crushing carbon                dioxide and sulfur oxide absorption tower,                controlling the temperature and pressure in the                absorption tower, removing the flue gas discharged                from the flue, introducing the sulfur oxide in the                air oxidation flue gas, then introducing into the                absorption tower, reacting with ammonia water for                gas-liquid phase reaction, absorbing and                neutralizing the carbon dioxide in the flue gas,                sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide for gas-liquid                reaction, respectively ammonium carbonate, ammonium                sulfite and ammonium sulfate, ammonium carbonate,                absorbing carbon dioxide to form ammonium hydrogen                carbonate, oxidizing ammonium sulfite by                ventilation to form ammonium sulfate after the                solution in the absorption tower reaches                supersaturation and forming certain amount of                ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate crystal,                using pump to extract and send into the reactor                with stirring, under the stirring of the stirrer,                reacting with the milled metal oxide or metal                hydroxide or the mixture of them in any proportion,                generating ammonia water and slightly soluble                carbonate and sulfate, carrying out solid-liquid                separation, separating the insoluble carbonate and                sulfate, and returning the separated ammonia water                to the absorption tower for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The metal oxide or                  metal hydroxide or the mixture of the two is                  milled to 50-200 meshes, and placed in a reactor                  with stirring. The concentration of the ammonia                  water is 10-45% weight of the ammonia. The                  pressure in the absorption tower is between                  0.20-2.5 megaPascal, and the temperature is                  between 10-45&#8451;. The rotating speed of                  the stirrer in the reactor is set at 10-500 rpm.                  The ammonium hydrogen carbonate and ammonium                  sulphate generated in the absorption tower are                  supersaturated to form the solid suspension with                  the mass ratio of 20-60%, the pump is pumped out                  and sent to the reactor.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN114349195-A
TI Marine seawater desalting system useful for carbon            dioxide recovery, comprises e.g. right interface of            liquefied natural gas inlet set to left interface of            compressor through pipeline, and first electric control            valve whose upper interface communicates with right end            of compressor through pipeline.
AU ZHANG Q
   PENG Y
   LIU C
   YANG X
AE UNIV JIANGSU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJG-C)
GA 202259309U
AB    NOVELTY - Marine seawater desalting system comprises a                smoke treatment unit, an energy conversion unit and                a sea-water desalting unit. The smoke processing                unit comprises a liquefied natural gas (LNG) inlet                (1), a LNG pump (2), an internal combustion engine                (3), a flue gas catalytic converter (4), a waste                heat (5), an adsorption device and a heat                exchanger. The right interface of the LNG inlet is                connected to the left interface of a compressor                through a pipeline. The right interface is                connected with the lower interface of first                pressure sensor through the pipeline. The upper                interface of pressure sensor I is communicated with                the PLC controller through signal cable. The first                electric control valve whose upper interface                communicates with the right end of the compressor                through pipeline.
   USE - The marine seawater desalting system is useful                for carbon dioxide recovery, and also useful in                environment protection and energy efficient                utilization field, and water-producing                projects.
   ADVANTAGE - The marine seawater desalting system uses                critical pressure of the carbon dioxide through                coupling gas processing unit and energy conversion                unit, avoids pressure that enhances the recycling                of the energy, prepares fresh water and collects                liquid carbon dioxide, avoids direct discharge                environment pollution, improves recovery rate of                carbon dioxide, uses LNG cold energy and seawater                cooling amount to directly capture carbon dioxide                generated by natural gas combustion, and does not                use secondary cooling medium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                working method of marine seawater desalting system                for carbon dioxide recovery.

PT P
PN CN113061728-A
TI Method for extracting valuable metal element from            coal gangue comprises e.g. separating organic liquid            phase and solid slag phase from activated coal gangue,            adding sodium carbonate and calcium carbonate and            introducing carbon dioxide.
AU LI K
   DU H
   BAO Z
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIN-C)
GA 2021804079
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting valuable metal element                from coal gangue comprises (i) crushing and                grinding coal gangue; (ii) activating the crushed                and grinded coal gangue; (iii) separating the                organic liquid phase and the solid slag phase from                the activated coal gangue through a separation                device; (iv) supercritically reacting the organic                liquid phase to synthesize carbon dioxide and                water, producing aluminum hydroxide using carbon                dioxide in the subsequent sodium aluminate                reaction; (v) sieving the solid slag phase by a                shaking table to screen out the enrichment                containing carbon and silicon dioxide, aluminum                oxide, silicate and ilmenite; (vi) electroselecting                the enriched material containing carbon and silicon                dioxide; (vii) adding sodium carbonate and calcium                carbonate into aluminum oxide, silicate and                ilmenite; (viii) adding sodium hydroxide into the                solution; and (ix) introducing carbon dioxide into                sodium aluminate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting valuable                metal element from coal gangue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains slag of calcium silicate                and calcium titanate, which can be used as                coatings, and sodium aluminate is introduced into                carbon dioxide to generate aluminum hydroxide which                is used in inorganic flame retardant                additives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting valuable metal element                from coal gangue comprises (i) crushing and                grinding coal gangue; (ii) activating the crushed                and grinded coal gangue under supercritical water                or subcritical water conditions; (iii) separating                the organic liquid phase and the solid slag phase                from the activated coal gangue through a separation                device; (iv) supercritically reacting the organic                liquid phase to synthesize carbon dioxide and                water, producing aluminum hydroxide using carbon                dioxide in the subsequent sodium aluminate reaction                and replenishing water on the shaking table using                water; (v) sieving the solid slag phase by a                shaking table to screen out the enrichment                containing carbon and silicon dioxide, aluminum                oxide, silicate and ilmenite; (vi) electroselecting                the enriched material containing carbon and silicon                dioxide to obtain high-quality carbon and silicon                dioxide; (vii) adding sodium carbonate and calcium                carbonate into aluminum oxide, silicate and                ilmenite, and roasting, and dissolving to obtain                the slag of calcium silicate and calcium titanate;                (viii) adding sodium hydroxide into the solution to                obtain iron hydroxide and sodium aluminate; and                (ix) introducing carbon dioxide into sodium                aluminate to obtain aluminum hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  particle size of the coal gangue after crushing                  and grinding is 2 mu m to 2 mm. In the step                  (iii), the separation device is water conservancy                  cyclone, solid-liquid separating centrifuge or                  spiral chute. In the step (v), the lateral angle                  of the shaking table is 1-5 degrees , and the                  longitudinal angle is 1-5 degrees . In the step                  (viii), the dissolving solution comprises sodium                  aluminate and sodium ferrite. Preferred Method:                  In the step (ii), the activation treatment is                  performed under supercritical water or                  subcritical water conditions, the activation                  temperature is 250-400 degrees C, the activation                  pressure is 8-20 MPa, and the activation time is                  0.1-3 hours. In the step (iv), the temperature of                  the supercritical reaction is 373-500 degrees C,                  and the pressure is 15-40 MPa. In the step (vi),                  the method for separating the enriched substances                  of carbon and silicon dioxide is                  electro-selective separation by                  electro-selection. In the step (vii), aluminum                  oxide, silicate and ore containing a small amount                  of ilmenite are added with sodium carbonate and                  calcium carbonate for roasting, and the roasting                  temperature is 300-1200 degrees C.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)
MC H09-A; M25-E; M25-G01; M25-G04; M25-G05; M25-G24; M25-J; P41-E03; P41-V28
IP B03B-007/00; C01B-032/00; C01B-033/12; C01F-007/08; C01F-007/14; C01G-049/00; C01G-049/02; C22B-021/00; C22B-026/10; C22B-026/20; C22B-034/12; C22B-007/00

PT P
PN CN110411972-A; CN110411972-B
TI Method for simultaneously detecting total volatile            organic pollutants and non-methane total hydrocarbons            in gas, involves subjecting gas through high            temperature catalytic oxidation column and converting            organic compound in gas.
AU ZHANG Z
   LI G
   HAO Z
   LI N
   ZHANG X
   CHENG J
AE UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 201994189U
AB    NOVELTY - A total volatile organic pollutants and                non-methane total hydrocarbon simultaneously                detecting method involves subjecting the gas to a                catalytic oxidation at 440 degrees C through a high                temperature catalytic oxidation column, completely                converting the total volatile organic compound in                the gas into carbon dioxide, and detecting the                concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas after                catalytic oxidation by a non-dispersive infrared                detector instrument to obtain the concentration of                total volatile organic compound in the gas by                conversion. The gas is subjected to catalytic                oxidation at 260-380 degrees C through a medium                temperature selective catalytic oxidation column,                that is, the non-methane hydrocarbons in the gas is                completely converted into carbon dioxide, and the                carbon dioxide in the catalytically oxidized gas is                detected by a non-dispersive infrared detector                instrument. The concentration of non-methane                hydrocarbons in the gas is converted.
   USE - Method for simultaneously detecting total                volatile organic pollutants and non-methane total                hydrocarbons in gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting total volatile                organic pollutants and non-methane total                hydrocarbons in efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A total volatile organic pollutants and                non-methane total hydrocarbon simultaneously                detecting method involves subjecting the gas to a                catalytic oxidation at 440 degrees C through a high                temperature catalytic oxidation column, completely                converting the total volatile organic compound in                the gas into carbon dioxide, and detecting the                concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas after                catalytic oxidation by a non-dispersive infrared                detector instrument to obtain the concentration of                total volatile organic compound in the gas by                conversion. The gas is subjected to catalytic                oxidation at 260-380 degrees C through a medium                temperature selective catalytic oxidation column,                that is, the non-methane hydrocarbons in the gas is                completely converted into carbon dioxide, and the                carbon dioxide in the catalytically oxidized gas is                detected by a non-dispersive infrared detector                instrument. The concentration of non-methane                hydrocarbons in the gas is converted. The                composition of the catalyst loaded in the high                temperature catalytic oxidation column and the                medium temperature selective catalytic oxidation                column comprises a carrier and an active metal                supported on the carrier, where the carrier is a                molecular sieve, the active metal is palladium and                the active metal has a mass content of                0.1-10%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular sieve is                  a zeolite socony mobil series molecular sieve, a                  beta molecular sieve, a mordenite molecular                  sieve, a Y molecular sieve, a type A molecular                  sieve or an X molecular sieve. The catalyst                  further comprises an auxiliary metal component                  supported on the molecular sieve and the                  auxiliary metal is potassium, sodium, manganese,                  iron, cobalt, nickel, ruthenium, osmium,                  chromium, vanadium and tin.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC E10-J02D2; E10-J02D3; E11-Q03; E31-N05C; E31-P02B; E31-P02D; N05-A; N07-L03B; S03-E04; S03-E14P; S03-F06A
IP G01N-021/3504

PN CN109519158-A
TI Method for integrally developing organic shale and adjacent oil-bearing reservoirs, involves injecting heat into shale layer and heating shale, followed by increasing carbon dioxide concentration and completing oil-bearing reservoirs mining.
AU MO W
AE AMMAN SHALE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (AMMA-Non-standard)
GA 2019302086
AB    NOVELTY - An organic shale and adjacent oil-bearing reservoirs integrally developing method involves injecting heat into a shale layer containing organic matter, heating the shale, producing a natural gas by an organic matter cracking, recovering carbon 2 to carbon 5 light hydrocarbon gases and light crude oils from a production wells in a shale reservoirs, depositing by-products of cracking is coke and extremely heavy asphaltenes, injecting oxygen from a production well in the shale reservoir, oxidizing the deposited asphaltene in the shale reservoir to produce a carbon dioxide-rich gas, entering the carbon dioxide-rich gas to the adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, producing a crude oil in the oil-bearing reservoir, placing a carbon oxidation catalyst in a passage connecting the shale and the oil-bearing reservoir to minimize the concentration of carbon and oxygen in the exhaust gas, increasing the carbon dioxide concentration and completing a mining of oil-bearing reservoirs.
   USE - Method for integrally developing organic shale and adjacent oil-bearing reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables integrally developing organic shale and adjacent oil-bearing reservoirs with reduced exhaust gas, improved concentration of carbon dioxide, high yield and improved economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An organic shale and adjacent oil-bearing reservoirs integrally developing method involves injecting heat into a shale layer containing organic matter, heating the shale to above 250 degrees C, producing a natural gas by an organic matter cracking, recovering carbon 2 to carbon 5 light hydrocarbon gases and light crude oils from production wells in a shale reservoirs, depositing by-products of cracking is coke and extremely heavy asphaltenes in the shale reservoirs, injecting oxygen or oxygen-enriched gas from a production well in the shale reservoir, oxidizing the coke and the extremely heavy asphaltene in the shale reservoir to produce a carbon dioxide-rich gas, entering the carbon dioxide-rich gas to the adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, producing a crude oil in the oil-bearing reservoir from the production well installed in the reservoir, placing a carbon oxidation catalyst in a passage connecting the shale and the oil-bearing reservoir to minimize the concentration of carbon and oxygen in the exhaust gas, increasing the carbon dioxide concentration and completing a mining of oil-bearing reservoirs.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

TI Carbon dioxide application apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide into greenhouse, has adsorption tanks with first and second adsorption tanks such that first adsorption is arranged upstream of second adsorption tank in flow path.
AU FUKUOKA S
AE FUTABA SANGYO KK (FUTS-C)
GA 2019261862
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has several adsorption tanks (6a-6d) in which an adsorbent that adsorbs carbon dioxide in the flue gas is arranged. A flow path is configured for supplying the combustion exhaust gas to the several adsorption tanks, and supplying the combustion exhaust gas having passed through the several adsorption tanks to the downstream side of several adsorption tanks. The several adsorption tanks are composed of a first adsorption tank (6a) and several second adsorption tanks (6b-6d) such that the first adsorption tank is arranged upstream of the second adsorption tank in the flow path. One second adsorption tank is several second adsorption tanks. The several second adsorption tanks are connected in parallel and series with each other.
   USE - Carbon dioxide application apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide contained in combustion exhaust gas into greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The increase in the number of maintenance steps of several adsorption tanks can be suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The flow path is branched into a first branch flow path and a second branch flow path at a branch portion of the flow path such that the first branch flow path and the second branch flow path are located downstream of the branch portion. The several adsorption tanks are provided in the first branch flow channel, and are joined at a junction. A switching unit is configured to switch between a first switching state in which the combustion exhaust gas flows through the first branch flow channel, and a second switching state in which the combustion exhaust gas flows through the second branch flow channel. A control unit is configured to control the switching unit. A determination unit is configured to determine whether the amount of adsorption of the carbon dioxide in several adsorption tanks is increased and reached a preset reference. The switching processing unit is set to the first switching state when determined by the determination unit that the amount of adsorption is not reached the reference. The switching processing unit is configured to bring the switching unit into the second switching state when determined by the determination unit that the amount of adsorption is reached the reference.

PN CN108531935-A
TI Preparing dimethyl carbonate from dimethyl ether and carbon dioxide by electrochemical method comprises e.g. placing deionized water, aqueous electrolyte solution or ionic liquid as electrolyte in electrolytic cell and magnetic stirring.
AU WU J
   ZI Z
   LIU G
   WANG H
   LI J
   HE T
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 201874803E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dimethyl carbonate from dimethyl ether and carbon dioxide by electrochemical method comprises e.g. taking deionized water, aqueous electrolyte solution or ionic liquid as an electrolyte, placing electrolyte in the electrolytic cell, then placing magnetic stirring rotor in electrolytic cell, and placing cathode electrode, anode electrode and reference electrode in the electrolytic cell to be assembled into a three-electrode system as a one-chamber type electrolytic cell device; the electrolytic cell device further includes inlet and outlet ports, and sealing all joints of electrolytic cell unit, and placing electrolytic cell device in a water bath of 20-80 degrees C, then magnetic stirring under nitrogen atmosphere, introducing carbon dioxide gas, then electrolysis cell device is connected, turning on power, and performing the electrolysis, continuing constant gas velocity passed through carbon dioxide, introducing dimethyl ether into electrolytic cell and reacting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dimethyl carbonate from dimethyl ether and carbon dioxide by electrochemical method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dimethyl carbonate from dimethyl ether and carbon dioxide by electrochemical method comprises (i) taking deionized water, aqueous electrolyte solution or ionic liquid as an electrolyte, where the electrolyte has a mass concentration of 0-50 wt.%, (ii) placing 20-100 ml electrolyte in the electrolytic cell, then placing magnetic stirring rotor in the electrolytic cell, and placing cathode electrode, anode electrode and reference electrode in the electrolytic cell to be assembled into a three-electrode system as a one-chamber type electrolytic cell device; the electrolytic cell device further includes inlet and outlet ports, and sealing all joints of the electrolytic cell unit, and (iii) placing electrolytic cell device in a water bath of 20-80 degrees C, then magnetic stirring at a speed of 200-800 revolutions/minute under nitrogen atmosphere, introducing carbon dioxide gas at a flow rate of 5-200 ml/minute, then electrolysis cell device is connected for 20-60 minutes, turning on the power at a constant voltage of 2-30 V, and performing the electrolysis for 1-3 hours, continuing the constant gas velocity passed through carbon dioxide, and introducing dimethyl ether into the electrolytic cell at a velocity of 5-200 ml/minute, and reacting for 30 minutes to obtain dimethyl carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cathode electrode, the anode electrode and the reference electrode are respectively ground with 1000 mesh sandpaper before use, performing ultrasonication in water for 10-20 minutes and vacuum drying before use. The anode electrode is pure metal electrode copper, a pure metal electrode platinum, pure metal electrode magnesium, a stainless steel electrode, a molecular sieve or alpha -alumina supported metal electrode. The cathode electrode is pure metal electrode copper, a pure metal electrode platinum, or a stainless steel electrode. The reference electrode is a saturated potassium chloride-silver/silver chloride reference electrode. The electrolyte in the electrolyte solution is phosphoric acid, acetic acid and/or monopotassium phosphate. The ionic liquid is 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate and/or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate.

TI Theoretical model for quantifying quartz            water-carbon dioxide-sodium chloride-type hydrothermal            fluid mineralization processes, comprises fixed fluid            components and temperature pressure coordinates at            point on phase boundary under specified            temperature.
AU DENG W P
   WEI Q
   ZHAO G
   HE A H
   JIA H F
AE INST CRUSTAL DYNAMICS CHINA EARTHQUAKE A (CRUS-Non-standard)
GA 202216448Q
AB    NOVELTY - Theoretical model for quantifying quartz                water-carbon dioxide-sodium chloride-type                hydrothermal fluid mineralization process involves                (i) using a water-carbon dioxide-sodium                chloride-based phase boundary calculation module to                acquire a liquid phase/gas-liquid mixed phase                boundary curve by calculating and obtaining                temperature and pressure coordinates (T,P) of a                certain point on a phase boundary of fixed fluid                components under a preset temperature, repeating                the calculation module at the selected temperature                interval, and obtaining a set of mineralized fluid                temperature and pressure coordinates within the                temperature-pressure range, (ii) using a                water-carbon dioxide-sodium chloride-based quartz                solubility calculation module to acquire a quartz                solubility curve by calculating and obtaining the                temperature and pressure coordinates (T,P) of the                fixed fluid components at a temperature of                specified quartz solubility, and repeating the                calculation module at the selected temperature                interval.
   USE - Theoretical model for quantifying quartz                water-carbon dioxide-sodium chloride-type                hydrothermal fluid mineralization process.
   ADVANTAGE - The dynamic evolution of components and phases                of mineralization fluid is quantitatively                solved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Theoretical model for quantifying quartz                water-carbon dioxide-sodium chloride-type                hydrothermal fluid mineralization process involves                (i) using a water-carbon dioxide-sodium                chloride-based phase boundary calculation module to                acquire a liquid phase/gas-liquid mixed phase                boundary curve by calculating and obtaining                temperature and pressure coordinates (T,P) of a                certain point on a phase boundary of fixed fluid                components under a preset temperature, repeating                the calculation module at the selected temperature                interval, and obtaining a set of mineralized fluid                temperature and pressure coordinates within the                temperature-pressure range, (ii) using a                water-carbon dioxide-sodium chloride-based quartz                solubility calculation module to acquire a quartz                solubility curve by calculating and obtaining the                temperature and pressure coordinates (T,P) of the                fixed fluid components at a temperature of                specified quartz solubility, repeating the                calculation module at the selected temperature                interval, and obtaining a series of mining area                mineralized fluid temperature and pressure                coordinates under the specified quartz solubility                conditions within the temperature and pressure                range, and (iii) using a water-carbon                dioxide-sodium chloride-based quartz-based                solubility curve area boundary calculation module                to acquire a series of temperature-pressure                coordinates of the quartz solubility curve area                boundary under fixed fluid component conditions                such as temperature-sensitive areas,                pressure-sensitive areas and degenerative area                boundary curves of quartz-like solubility curve                areas. The phase boundary curve, quartz-like                solubility curve and quartz-like solubility curve                region boundary curve obtained from each                calculation module are used to construct a                combination of quartz-like solubility phase                diagrams of different mineralized fluid components.                The hydrothermal fluid mineralization process was                quantified based on the quartz-like solubility                phase diagram, and used for studying phase boundary                curves, quartz-like solubility curves, quartz-like                solubility curve region boundary curves within a                subdivision fluid temperature and pressure range of                mineralized fluids of different components.

PT P
PN NL2025251-B1; WO2021201671-A1
TI Method for producing colored paperboard in paperboard manufacturing apparatus, involves pumping acidified colored pulp mixture to carton manufacturing portions, and manufacturing colored cardboard from acidified colored pulp mixture.
AU UIL A
AE ESKA BV (ESKA-Non-standard)
GA 202123005B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing pulp in a first pulp reservoir (11) with sulfur dye. The sulfur dye mixed with pulp is pumped from first pulp reservoir to a second pulp reservoir (12) via a pulp line structure (4-7). A pulp pumping unit (8) is comprised with a discharge pressure greater than 200 kPa, and carbon dioxide is injected into pulp line structure by carbon dioxide injection unit (9). The carbon dioxide is mixed with pulp and sulfur dye mixed with pulp coming from first pulp reservoir. An acidified colored pulp mixture (14) from pulp line structure (7) is injected into second pulp reservoir by pulp injection unit (10). The acidified colored pulp mixture to be injected produce static pressure in pulp line structure. The acidified colored pulp mixture is pumped from the second pulp reservoir to carton manufacturing portions (3) of carton manufacturing device (1). The colored cardboard is manufactured from acidified colored pulp mixture by cardboard manufacturing portions.
   USE - Method for producing colored paperboard in paperboard manufacturing apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The sulfur dyes ensures high-quality coloring of paperboard. The discoloration of paperboard is prevented, thereby ensuring reduced cost and enhanced environmentally-friendly effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves utilizing pulp coloring device, a first pulp reservoir, a second pulp reservoir, a pulp line structure, pulp pumping units, carbon dioxide injection unit and pulp injection units. The pulp in first pulp reservoir is mixed with sulfur dye, and the sulfur dye mixed with pulp is pumped from first pulp reservoir to second pulp reservoir via pulp line structure. The carbon dioxide is injected into pulp line structure by carbon dioxide injection unit, such that carbon dioxide is mixed with pulp and sulfur dye mixed with pulp coming from first pulp reservoir. The residence time of carbon dioxide in pulp line structure is controlled longer than 20 seconds, so that pulp and sulfur dye mixed with pulp are injected through dam. The dissolved carbon dioxide in the pulp line structure is acidified to acidified colored pulp mixture with pH value of 6.5-7.8. The acidified colored pulp mixture is injected from the pulp line structure into second pulp reservoir by pulp injection unit. The acidified colored pulp mixture to be injected is configured to produce static pressure in pulp line structure that is greater than 150 kPa, and where the injection of the acidified colored pulp mixture is carried out at distance of 2 meters below level of acidified colored pulp mixture in the second pulp reservoir. The acidified colored pulp mixture from the second pulp reservoir is pumped to carton manufacturing portions of the carton manufacturing device, and colored cardboard is manufactured from acidified colored pulp mixture by cardboard manufacturing portions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the paperboard manufacturing apparatus.
   Carton manufacturing device (1)
   Carton manufacturing portion (3)
   Pulp line structures (4-7)
   Pulp pumping unit (8)
   Carbon dioxide injection unit (9)

   ZUO D
   LIU Y
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B1007V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing fertilizer anticaking agent raw materials involves (a) using nanofiltration seawater as raw material, adding talc powder and evenly stirring, (b) capturing carbon dioxide using nanofiltration seawater mixed with talc powder, so that the high-concentration salt in the nanofiltration seawater is fully mixed and reacted with carbon dioxide to produce precipitation, (c) concentrating the nanofiltration seawater containing the precipitate, crystallizing and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a solid mixture comprising magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, and (d) drying the solid mixture and pulverizing into powder to obtain product.

PT P
PN JP2019205969-A; JP7091842-B2
TI Catalyst for producing paraxylene, reducing carbon dioxide and as raw material for polyethylene terephthalate, comprises chromium oxide and H- zeolite Socony Mobil-5 zeolite having surface coated with silicalite-1.
AU SUZUKI K
   TSUBAKI N
   YONEYAMA Y
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 2019A2382B
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a first catalyst comprising chromium oxide and a second catalyst comprising H- zeolite Socony Mobil (ZSM)-5 zeolite having a surface coated with silicalite-1.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing paraxylene, which is useful for reducing carbon dioxide and as a raw material for polyethylene terephthalate.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst efficiently produces paraxylene using carbon dioxide and hydrogen as raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing paraxylene, involving contacting carbon dioxide and hydrogen with the catalyst to synthesize paraxylene; and
   (2) a method for producing the catalyst, involving hydrothermally synthesizing the second catalyst using a silicon compound and H-ZSM-5 zeolite in which silicalite-1 is coated on the surface of the H-ZSM-5 zeolite, and mixing the first catalyst and the second catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 10-1000 %mass second catalyst with respect to the first catalyst. Preferred Properties: The average pore diameter of the first catalyst is 0.5-100 nm. The specific surface area of the first catalyst is 3-500 m2/g. The specific surface area of the second catalyst is 20-1000 m2/g. The average pore diameter of the second catalyst is 0.1-2.5 nm. Preferred Method: The first catalyst and the second catalyst are physically mixed. The catalyst catalyzes the synthesis of paraxylene from carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The catalyst preparation method further involves obtaining chromium hydroxide by a wet precipitation method, and obtaining the first catalyst by drying and/or calcining the chromium hydroxide, and synthesizing the H-ZSM-5 zeolite from a silicon compound and an aluminum compound by a hydrothermal synthesis method. Preferred Components: The chromium oxide in the first catalyst comprises chromium (III) oxide.
   EXAMPLE - 40 g chromium nitrate was precisely weighed and dissolved in 100 ml of pure water. The aqueous solution was heated at 70 degrees C, an aqueous ammonium carbonate solution was added to precipitate chromium as a hydroxide, and the mixture was sufficiently stirred with a stirrer. The mixture was aged by continuous stirring for 3 hours at 70 degrees C, then filtered, and sufficiently washed with pure water at 80 degrees C. The precipitate obtained after washing was dried at 100 degrees C and coarsely pulverized, and calcined at 500 degrees C in air to obtain a chromium oxide powder. Tetraorthosilicate, aluminum nitrate, water, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and ethanol were precisely weighed in a molar ratio of 1:1/40:50:0.24:4 and dissolved. The solution was introduced into a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene)-sealed autoclave, stirred for 4 hours with a magnetic stirrer, and reacted at 180 degrees C for 24 hours. The product obtained was washed with distilled water, dried at 120 degrees C overnight, and calcined in the air at 550 degrees C for 5 hours to decompose and remove organic substances present in the zeolite pores to obtain H-zeolite Socony Mobil (ZSM)-5 zeolite powder. 1 g H-ZSM-5 zeolite, silica, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, ethanol and water were immersed in a molar ratio of 1:0.06:16:240. The mixture was charged in a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene)-sealed autoclave and reacted at 180 degrees C for 24 hours while rotating at a speed of 3 rom. By repeating this operation twice, a zeolite coated with silicalite-1 as the second catalyst was obtained. The chromium oxide powder and the silicalite-1-coated H-ZSM-5 zeolite were precisely weighed in a weight ratio of 1: 1, and physically mixed in a mortar for 10 minutes. The mixture was press-molded into 3 mm tablets using a compression molding machine to obtain a tablet-molded product, pulverized and sized to 20-40 mesh to obtain the catalyst.

PN US10047027-B1
TI Forming methanol used as feedstock in various            chemical and/or petrochemical processes, comprises            irradiating a mixture comprising water, carbon dioxide,            and photocatalyst with UV light to reduce carbon            dioxide thus forming methanol.
AU GONDAL M A
   DASTAGEER M A
   BAIG U
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201862316P
AB    NOVELTY - Forming methanol, comprises: irradiating a                mixture comprising water, carbon dioxide, and a                photocatalyst with UV light to reduce the carbon                dioxide thus forming methanol, where the                photocatalyst comprises non-templated indium-oxide                nanoparticles with a specific pore volume of                0.01-0.05 cm3/g and/or templated indium-oxide                nanoparticles with a specific pore volume of                0.08-0.15 cm3/g.
   USE - The method is useful for forming methanol                (claimed) which is used as feedstock in various                chemical and/or petrochemical processes,                methanol-to-olefin processes, preferably                methanol-to-propylene, metathesis, propane                dehydrogenation, high severity fluid catalytic                cracking, olefin cracking, for manufacturing                organic compounds such as form aldehyde, acetic                acid, dimethyl ether, methyl tert-butyl ether,                ethylene, propylene, biodiesel, and as a diluent                for gasoline.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixture has a                  temperature of 10-40 degrees C during the                  irradiating. The irradiating is carried out                  without added hydrogen gas. The method further                  comprises injecting carbon dioxide into the                  mixture at 10-80 psi during the irradiating. The                  mixture is irradiated with UV light for 1-3                  hours. The method further comprises stirring the                  mixture during the irradiating. Preferred                  Components: The templated indium-oxide                  nanoparticles are present and the templated                  indium-oxide nanoparticles comprise cubic                  nanocrystals having a crystallite size of 50-80                  nm, and the cubic nanocrystals are arranged in an                  ordered structure and have a specific surface                  area of 30-80 m2/g and an average pore size of                  1-10 nm. The templated indium-oxide nanoparticles                  have a band gap energy of 2.9-3.3 eV. The                  non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles are                  present and the non-templated indium-oxide                  nanoparticles comprise cubic nanocrystals having                  a crystallite size of 80-120 nm, and the cubic                  nanocrystals are randomly arranged. The                  non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles are                  present and the non-templated indium-oxide                  nanoparticles have a specific surface area of                  2-10 m2/g and an average pore size of 10-40 nm.                  The non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles are                  present and the non-templated indium-oxide                  nanoparticles have a band gap energy of 3.1-3.5                  eV. The photocatalyst comprises the non-templated                  indium-oxide nanoparticles and the templated                  indium-oxide nanoparticles, and weight ratio of                  the non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles to                  the templated indium-oxide nanoparticles is 10:1-                  1:10. The photocatalyst consists of the templated                  indium-oxide nanoparticles. The UV light has a                  wavelength of 150-300 nm. The UV light is in a                  form of a single-frequency laser beam with a                  wavelength of 150-300 nm. The photocatalyst                  comprises the templated indium-oxide                  nanoparticles, and yield is 400-600 mu mol hour-1                  per gram of the photocatalyst. The photocatalyst                  comprises the templated indium-oxide                  nanoparticles, where a conversion efficiency of                  carbon dioxide to methanol is 30-60 mole%,                  relative to an amount of carbon dioxide, and                  quantum efficiency of forming methanol is 1-10                  vol.% to an amount of photons absorbed. The                  photocatalyst comprises the non-templated                  indium-oxide nanoparticles, and methanol yield is                  400-500 mu mol hour-1 per gram of the                  photocatalyst. The photocatalyst comprises the                  non-templated indium-oxide nanoparticles, where a                  conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide to                  methanol is 30-45 mole% to an amount of carbon                  dioxide, and a quantum efficiency of forming                  methanol is 1-4 vol.% relative to an amount of                  photons absorbed.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Injection fluid comprises liquid or supercritical            carbon dioxide and one or more alkoxylated alcohols,            where alkoxylated alcohol incorporates both one PO            block and one EO block.

   SOTTMAN T

   SOTTMANN T
   RENKE R
   THOMAS S
   HARUN B

AE SASOL PERFORMANCE CHEM GMBH (SASO-C)
   UNIV STUTTGART (STUT-C)
   SASOL PERFORMANCE CHEM GMBH (SASO-C)
   SASOL PERFORMANCE CHEM GMBH (SASO-C)
   SASOL CHEM GMBH (SASO-C)
   SASOL CHEM GMBH (SASO-C)
   SASOL CHEM GMBH (SASO-C)
GA 202082454M
AB    NOVELTY - Injection fluid comprises liquid or                supercritical carbon dioxide and one or more                alkoxylated alcohols which is represented by linear                formula.
   USE - Injection fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The injection fluid has improves miscibility                of carbon di-oxide and crude oil has more than one                beneficial effect. The carbon di-oxide utilization                is enhanced what comes with higher oil recovery                rates for the same amount of carbon dioxide                injected.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Injection fluid comprises liquid or                supercritical carbon dioxide and one or more                alkoxylated alcohols which is represented by linear                formula R-O-(AO)m+nH, where R is a linear alkyl                group having from 4-9 carbon atoms, and/or a                branched alkyl group having from 4-18C carbon                atoms, AO is an ethoxy group (EO) and/or a propoxy                group (PO), m is 0 to 12, n is 0 to 12, and sum of                m and n is 1. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                following:
   (1) a method for flooding of oil wells by                carbon di-oxide injection, which involves injecting                an injection fluid into the oil well; and
   (2) a method for using alkoxylated alcohols                which involves utilizing liquid or supercritical                carbon dioxide the injection liquid in the recovery                of a hydrocarbonaceous fluid from a                reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The (AO)m+n is                  (A'O)m-(A''O)n, where m is 1 to 12, n is 1 to 12,                  A'O is an ethoxy group (EO) and A''O is a propoxy                  group (PO) or A'O is a propoxy group (PO) and                  A''O is an ethoxy group (EO), AO is EO,                  preferably PO. The swelling factor of the                  hydrocarbonaceous fluids is increased by 4% upon                  addition of the alkoxylated alcohols when                  compared to the swelling factor without the                  addition of the alkoxylated alcohols. The                  alkoxylated alcohol(s) is added to the carbon                  dioxide in a range of 0.1-10.0 wt.%, preferably                  0.1-2.0 wt.%. The hydrocarbonaceous fluid is a                  crude oil and the reservoir is a well. The                  alkoxylated alcohol incorporates both one PO                  block and one EO block, and the PO block is in a                  terminal position.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2020085633-A; KR2184266-B1
TI High purity methane production system comprises an absorption liquid inlet for receiving an absorption liquid in which carbon dioxide is dissolved and a methanation reaction tank provided with a reducing metal for reducing hydrogen ions.
AU LEE J
   CHOI O
   ZHAOXIN
   DONGDANDAN
   JANG M S
   HO S
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202068108N
AB    NOVELTY - High-purity methane production system comprises an absorption liquid inlet (10) for receiving an absorption liquid in which carbon dioxide is dissolved; a methanation reaction tank (20) provided with a reducing metal for reducing hydrogen ions contained in the absorbent liquid (142) to hydrogen molecules; a methane producing bacteria (MB) for producing methane from the reduced hydrogen molecules and the carbon dioxide; and a methane generating unit (100) including methane outlet (30) to discharge the produced methane.
   USE - High-purity methane production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables to produce high purity methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a high purity methane production method, which involves introducing an absorbent solution in which carbon dioxide is dissolved; reducing hydrogen ions contained in the absorbent liquid to hydrogen molecules by a reducing metal; producing methane from reduced hydrogen molecule and the carbon dioxide by methane producing bacteria; and discharging the produced methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a methane generating unit.
   Absorption liquid inlet (10)
   Methanation reaction tank (20)
   Methane outlet (30)
   Absorbent liquid return port (40)
   Methane generating unit (100)
   Absorbent liquid (142)
   Methane producing bacteria (MB)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reducing metal further includes a carrier attached to the surface, and the reducing metal and the methane producing bacteria are spaced apart from each other by the carrier. The carrier reduces the hydrogen ions to hydrogen molecules. The size of the carrier is 75-425 mu m. The main component of the reducing metal is iron (0). Preferred Process: The method for high purity methane production further involves introducing biogas containing carbon dioxide and methane; dissolving the carbon dioxide in the absorbent; preparing a carrier to which the reducing metal is attached; preparing a reducing metal ion solution by adding a reducing metal in a solvent; preparing a mixed solution by mixing a carrier with the reducing metal ion solution; and adding a reducing agent to the mixed solution to attach the reducing metal ion to the carrier.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111088110-A
TI Applying supercritical carbon dioxide to extract fructus broussonetiae oil, involves drying and crushing fructus broussonetiae, passing through sieve, taking powder into supercritical extraction kettle, and heating.
AU INVENTOR U
AE HAIMEN YUANMEI ART DESIGN CO LTD (HAIM-Non-standard)
GA 2020388118
AB    NOVELTY - Applying supercritical carbon dioxide to extract fructus broussonetiae oil, involves drying and crushing fructus broussonetiae, passing through a 10-20 mesh sieve, taking 10-20 pts. wt. powder into the supercritical extraction kettle, heating or cooling the extraction kettle and storage tank separately, when the temperature reaches 35-45 degrees C, and 0-4 degrees C, opening the carbon dioxide gas bottle and pressurizing the extraction kettle with a compression pump, when the pressure reaches 25-27 MPa, starting circulating extraction, and adjusting the flow rate of carbon dioxide to 23-25 L/hour, keeping the constant temperature and constant pressure for 2.5-3.5 hours, and collecting the extraction liquid. The extract is concentrated under reduced pressure, and then separated by silica gel column chromatography, followed by elution with chloroform-methanol in a volume ratio of 60:1, 54:1, 43:1, 26:1, and 11:1, and the 26:1 elution fraction is collected, and concentrated.
   USE - Method for applying supercritical carbon dioxide to extract fructus broussonetiae oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively obtain the volatile oil components in the fructus broussonetiae, and has the functions of improving eyesight and diuresis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: Applying supercritical carbon dioxide to extract fructus broussonetiae oil, involves opening the carbon dioxide gas bottle, when the temperature reaches 40 degrees C, and 2 degrees C. The pressure is reached to 26 MPa, then circulating extraction is started. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 24 L/hour. The constant temperature and constant pressure are maintained for 3 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Diuretic; Ophthalmological.

PN CN110683969-A; CN110683969-B
TI Preparing urea derivative comprises e.g. taking amine compound, adding amine catalyst, then introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction bottle, adding solvent, hydrosilane and Lewis acid catalyst and catalyzing the reaction.
AU ZHAO Y
   SI Z
   GUO X
   HU Y
   SUN Y
   DONG Y
   GAO B
   LU X
   WU B
   CHENG Q
   ZANG Q
AE UNIV QUFU NORMAL (UNQN-C)
GA 2020074189
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing urea derivative (I) comprises taking amine compound as a raw material, adding amine catalyst and then introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction bottle, and then adding solvent, hydrosilane, and Lewis acid catalyst into the reaction flask under the atmosphere of carbon dioxide gas, and then catalyzing the reaction by one-pot method to obtain final product, where the hydrosilane is phenylsilane or triethoxysilane.
   USE - (I) is useful in the fields of synthesis and medicinal chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction conditions, simple post-treatment process and excellent product yield; utilizes easily available raw materials, amine catalysts and Lewis acid catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing urea derivative of formula (R-NH-C(=O)-NH-R) (I) comprises taking amine compound of formula (R-NH2) as a raw material, adding amine catalyst and then introducing carbon dioxide gas into reaction bottle, and then adding solvent, hydrosilane, and Lewis acid catalyst into the reaction flask under the atmosphere of carbon dioxide gas, and then catalyzing the reaction by one-pot method to obtain final product, where the hydrosilane is phenylsilane or triethoxysilane.
   R = aryl or alkyl substituent.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for urea derivative obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The purity of the carbon dioxide gas is greater than 99.9%.The amine catalyst is 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene and/or 4-dimethylaminopyridine. The Lewis acid catalyst is ferric chloride, boron trifluoride etherate and/or iron(III) trifluoromethanesulfonate. The molar ratio between amine compound, carbon dioxide, hydrosilane, amine catalyst and Lewis acid catalyst is 1:1-2:1-2:0.1-0.5:0.1-0.5. The solvent is tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile, and/or 1,4-dioxane. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is 100-120 degrees C. The reaction time is 24-48 hours.

TI Generation of gas mixture for use in            hydroformylation, involves feeding steam to solid oxide            electrolytic cell, utilizing effluent cell gas            comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and separating            product gas from liquid.
AU SCHJODT N C

   BLENNOW B P G
   KINGAS R

AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)

   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2019863369
AB    NOVELTY - Generation of a gas mixture involves                evaporating water to steam, feeding the steam to a                solid oxide electrolytic cell (SOEC) or an SOEC                stack at a sufficient temperature for the cell or                cell stack to operate by supplying an electrical                current to the cell to effect a partial conversion                of steam to hydrogen, utilizing the effluent SOEC                gas comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide from an                external source as feed for a reverse water gas                shift (RWGS) reactor in which the RWGS reaction                takes place, converting some of the carbon dioxide                and hydrogen to carbon monoxide and water, removing                some of or all remaining steam from the raw product                gas stream by cooling allowing for condensation of                at least portion of steam as liquid water and                separating the remaining product gas from the                liquid. The gas mixture comprises carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide and water in which carbon monoxide                and hydrogen as reactants, while recycling carbon                dioxide to the RWGS reactor.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                generation of syngas having high purity.

PN CN110306199-A; CN110306199-B
TI Carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction film            used as working electrode of carbon dioxide            electrochemical catalyst device, comprises material            comprising nano copper-tin homogeneous solid alloy            compound.
AU LIN P
   WU T
   XU Z
   CAO Y
   ZENG X
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
   UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 201994459A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction film                comprises a material comprising a nano copper-tin                homogeneous solid alloy compound (I), where the                ratio of copper and tin is continuously                adjustable.
   USE - The film is useful as a working electrode of a                carbon dioxide electrochemical catalyst device                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film has high electrocatalytic efficiency                and strong selectivity of the product, adopts                simple preparation process and can be prepared                according to the product requirement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide electrocatalytic reduction film                comprises a material comprising a nano copper-tin                homogeneous solid alloy compound of formula:                CuxSn100-x (I), where the ratio of copper and tin                is continuously adjustable.
   x = 0-100.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the film, involving placing the                copper wire and the tin particles at different                positions of a thermal evaporation chamber as                different evaporation sources, fixing the thermal                evaporation chamber with a substrate attached to                the turntable above the evaporation source,                vacuuming the thermal evaporation chamber,                initiating the thermal evaporation process by                evacuating the thermal evaporation chamber to less                than 5x 10-7 Pa, and preparing the film by                controlling the thermal evaporation rate of copper                and tin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material comprises                  a nano copper-tin alloy compound of formula:                  Cu10Sn90 (I). Preferred Method: In the                  preparation method, the thermal evaporation rate                  of the copper is controlled to 0-1 Angstrom                  /second, the thermal evaporation rate of the tin                  controlled to 1-0 Angstrom /second, the sum of                  the thermal evaporation rate of copper and the                  thermal evaporation rate of tin is 1 Angstrom                  /second, the thermal evaporation time is                  controlled such that the film has a thickness of                  10-100 nm, and after starting the thermal                  evaporation process, the three parameters                  proportional (P), integral (I) and derivative (D)                  are adjusted to keep the thermal evaporation                  rates of copper and tin stable.

TI Foam formed from foamable composition used in closed cell foam which is used in heat transfer fluids and heat transfer systems, comprises blowing agent comprising carbon dioxide and trifluoromethyl compounds.
AU SINGH R R
   PHAM H T
   SHANKLAND I
AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
GA 201942252D
AB    NOVELTY - Foam formed from a foamable composition comprises a blowing agent comprising 1-40 wt.% carbon dioxide and 60-99 wt.% at least one trifluoromethyl compounds (I), where the foam comprises carbon dioxide and (I) in a weight ratio of 1:99-40:60, respectively.
   USE - The foam is useful in a closed cell foam (claimed) which is used in wide variety of applications including heat transfer fluids and heat transfer systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam: provides heat transfer fluids that possess highly desirable and unexpectedly superior combination of properties; provides fluids that possess properties e.g. chemical stability, low toxicity, non-flammability, and efficiency in-use, while at the same time substantially reducing or eliminating the deleterious ozone depletion potential of such compositions; and substantially reduce or eliminate the negative global warming effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Foam formed from a foamable composition comprises a blowing agent comprising 1-40 wt.% carbon dioxide and 60-99 wt.% at least one trifluoromethyl compounds of formula (X1CF3) (I), where the foam comprises carbon dioxide and (I) in a weight ratio of 1:99-40:60, respectively.
   X1 = 2C unsaturated, substituted, alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a closed cell foam comprising the foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of carbon dioxide to (I) is at least 5:95, preferably at least 3:97. The blowing agent is non-flammable. The foamable composition further comprises at least one adjuvant comprising dispersing agents, surfactants, supplemental flame suppressants, cell stabilizers, polyols, and/or polyol premix components. The foam is thermoplastic foam. The thermoplastic foam has at least one of an extruded thermoplastic foam, a polystyrene foam, a polyethylene foam, a low density polyethylene foam, a polypropylene foam, and a polyethyleneterphthalate foam. (I) comprises 1,1,1,3-tetrafluoropropene, where the composition has a vapor pressure of at least 30 psia at 35 degrees F. (I) comprises trans-1,1,1,3-tetrafluoropropene, where the composition has a vapor pressure of at least 30 psia at 35 degrees F. (I) and carbon dioxide together comprise at least 5 (preferably at least 50) wt.% the blowing agent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN DE102018007001-A1
TI Process for decentralized generation of electrical energy for electromobility, involves removing power generation plant which is located in vicinity of charging stations or parking lots for vehicles with batteries from gas network.
AU DIETRICH K W
   VON BORCKE Y P
AE DIETRICH K W (DIET-Individual)
   VON BORCKE Y (VBOR-Individual)
GA 202019345S
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves converting electrical solar or wind energy into hydrogen by water electrolysis. The hydrogen is converted to methane with carbon dioxide or methane is introduced into the natural gas network with carbon monoxide. Methane is transferred together with the natural gas in the gas network. Methane or the equivalent amount of natural gas is taken from the gas network and converted back into electrical energy in a power generation plant. The power generation plant which is located in the vicinity of charging stations or parking lots/car parks for electrically powered vehicles with batteries is removed from the gas network, so that the capacity of the power generation plant is adjusted to the power requirements of the vehicles in the parking lots or charging stations.
   USE - Process for decentralized generation of electrical energy for electromobility in charging station for electric vehicle used for commercial, military and private application. Uses include but are not limited to car, truck, industrial truck, rail vehicle, ship, aircraft such as plane and drone and manned or unmanned vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The electricity is generated efficiently. The fuel cell is a solid fuel cell in which natural gas/methane is emitted directly. The hydrogen is converted into electrical energy in a fuel cell in loss-free manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The natural gas is withdrawn from the gas network and is converted into electrical energy. The carbon dioxide is converted with hydrogen from water electrolysis into methane, and methane is returned to the natural gas network and electricity is generated from natural gas without carbon dioxide emissions. Biological carbon dioxide or biological carbon monoxide is reacted with hydrogen from the hydrogen electrolysis and carbon dioxide formed during power generation is emitted in a climate-neutral manner. The electricity is generated by converting natural gas/methane into hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a steam reformer and using the hydrogen to generate electricity in a fuel cell. The battery is added to the power generation system. The battery of electrically operated rail vehicles is charged at the charging station which is connected to the power generation unit. The natural gas is broken down into hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a steam reformer and the hydrogen is converted into electrical energy in a fuel cell, and carbon dioxide is collected. The decomposition of methane/natural gas into carbon dioxide and hydrogen is carried out and the hydrogen is transferred to decentrally distributed fuel cells in pipelines or in the pressure vessel. The fuel cell is assigned to a group of parking spaces or charging stations or the mobile fuel cell, and is located in parked vehicle to generate electricity for other vehicles.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); ; W07 (Electrical Military Equipment and Weapons); X12 (Power Distribution/Components/Converters); X16 (Electrochemical Storage); X21 (Electric Vehicles)

PN CN109681325-A; CN109681325-B
TI Zero-carbon natural gas-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power generation process by drawing outside air using compressor, gradually increasing pressure and air temperature, and heating partial pressurized liquid oxygen.
AU TIAN Y
   QIAO Y
   XIE K
   SUN L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM HUADONG (UYPE-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2019410653
AB    NOVELTY - A zero-carbon natural gas-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power generation process includes drawing outside air using compressor, gradually increasing pressure to 0.5-0.8 MPa while increasing air temperature to carry out partial pressurized liquid oxygen preheating, adding 0.5-0.8 MPa pressurized air to air separation unit to obtain liquid oxygen and liquid nitrogen, pumping pressurized liquid oxygen to carry out heat exchange and natural gas combustion reactions to generate electricity, and pumping pressurized liquid nitrogen to drive nitrogen turbine generators to generate electricity; and mixing high-pressure vaporized oxygen and circulating water vapor with injected natural gas in combustion chamber of gas turbine, adding high-temperature and high-pressure combustion flue gas, and subjecting high-temperature combustion flue gas to supercritical carbon dioxide heat generation reaction.
   USE - Method for zero-carbon natural gas-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power generation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A zero-carbon natural gas-supercritical carbon dioxide combined cycle power generation process comprises:
   (A) drawing outside air using compressor, gradually increasing pressure to 0.5-0.8 MPa by axial compression while increasing air temperature to carry out partial pressurized liquid oxygen preheating, adding 0.5-0.8 MPa pressurized air to air separation unit for air separation to obtain liquid oxygen and liquid nitrogen, pumping pressurized liquid oxygen to carry out heat exchange and natural gas combustion reactions to generate electricity, and pumping pressurized liquid nitrogen to drive nitrogen turbine generators to generate electricity;
   (B) mixing high-pressure vaporized oxygen and circulating water vapor with injected natural gas in combustion chamber of gas turbine, adding high-temperature and high-pressure combustion flue gas to turbine to drive the compressor and generator at high speed partially converting chemical energy of natural gas to mechanical work and outputting electrical work, subjecting high-temperature combustion flue gas to supercritical carbon dioxide heat generation reaction through supercritical carbon dioxide heat exchanger, cooling with first-stage pressurized liquid oxygen and/or liquid nitrogen coolant, and pressurizing supercritical carbon dioxide by high pressure pump to close the circuit;
   (C) heat exchanging medium temperature flue gas with high pressure water to obtain circulating water vapor, heat exchanging with secondary pressurized liquid oxygen and/or liquid nitrogen, dehydrating cooled flue gas, separating and recovering liquid flue gas using distillation tower to obtain carbon dioxide as product for sale; and
   (D) pressurizing portion of dewatered water using water pump to obtain high pressure water, discharging remaining water, vaporizing pressurized liquid to obtain high pressure oxygen, and returning back to gas turbine combustion chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supercritical carbon dioxide has pressure of 7-40 MPa.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide amplification process for supercritical extraction, comprises providing a main process stream comprising supercritical carbon dioxide into a system pump discharge (115), pushing the initial process stream by activation of a system pump (110) to an extraction bed (120) containing organic material, extracting essential material from the organic material in the extraction bed, providing the main process stream to a main process discharge (125), diverting an auxiliary process stream from the main process stream into an auxiliary pump feed (130) by increasing negative pressure in the auxiliary pump feed by activating an auxiliary pump, pushing the auxiliary process stream through an auxiliary pump discharge via the auxiliary pump, providing the auxiliary process stream from the auxiliary pump discharge to the system pump discharge, and providing an amplified process stream comprising the main process stream with the auxiliary process stream to the extraction bed.
   USE - The process is useful for supercritical extraction of essential material from organic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The process allows passage of more mass of carbon dioxide over the organic material in extraction bed per unit of time and thus helps to extract more amount of essential material from the organic material, hence efficient and effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an apparatus for increasing the mass flow of supercritical carbon dioxide over an extraction bed, comprising: an auxiliary pump feed configured to receive an auxiliary process stream from a main process stream discharged from the proximal end of an extraction bed containing organic material; an auxiliary pump which is connected to the auxiliary pump feed and is configured to create negative pressure in the auxiliary pump feed, thus siphoning the auxiliary process stream from the main process stream; an auxiliary pump discharge which is proximally connected to the auxiliary pump and distally connected to a system pump discharge and configured to receive the auxiliary process stream from the auxiliary pump and discharge the auxiliary process stream into the system pump discharge, where the auxiliary process stream combines with a process stream to form an amplified process stream that is transported to the distal end of the extraction bed and the mass of supercritical carbon dioxide in the amplified process stream flowing over organic material is the extraction bed is greater than the mass of supercritical carbon dioxide in the main process stream per unit of time.

   System pump (110)
   System pump discharge (115)
   Extraction bed (120)
