The variable frequency device modulates the pressure generated by the auxiliary pump based on the chemical composition of the main process stream detected by the inline monitor. Preferred Components: The auxiliary pump: is capable of withstanding static pressure of 5000 psi; stream provides a differential pressure in excess of 100 psi; and provides a volumetric flow rate of 1-1.5 l/minute. The apparatus further comprises: a variable frequency device i.e. configured to modulate the pressure generated by the auxiliary pump; a metering restriction valve which is configured to adjust the flow rate of the auxiliary pump stream from the auxiliary pump; and an inline monitor connected to the main process discharge, where the inline monitor is configured to detect the chemical composition of the main process stream.

PT P
PN CN110350165-A; CN110350165-B
TI Improving the stability and electrical conductivity of a high nickel anode material comprises e.g. taking high-temperature sintering furnace, mixing lithium salt and pushing into the carbon dioxide plasma generator.
AU ZHANG W
   CHEN A
   WANG K
   XIA Y
   WU H
   QIAN Z
   MAO Q
   HUANG H
   ZHANG J
   GAN Y
   LIANG C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
   ZHEJIANG MEIDU HAICHUANG LITHIUM BATTERY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201988991Y
AB    NOVELTY - Improving the stability and electrical conductivity of a high nickel anode material comprises e.g. (i) taking high-temperature sintering furnace; (ii) mixing high nickel anode material and lithium salt, adding into a high-temperature sintering furnace and sealing furnace body; (iii) opening the oxygen valve of the furnace body and heating; (iv) closing oxygen-passing valve, heating furnace body and collecting excess ozone in the furnace body by the exhaust gas absorption device; (v) closing ozone valve, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the furnace body for annealing treatment for 10-120 minutes at 400-700 degrees C and using exhaust gas absorption device for charging excess carbon dioxide in the furnace; (vi) closing carbon dioxide valve and cooling the material in the furnace naturally to room temperature to obtain high nickel anode material; and (vii) pushing high nickel anode material obtained into the carbon dioxide plasma generator.
   USE - The method is useful for improving the stability and electrical conductivity of a high nickel anode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: shortens the sintering time of the material; increases productivity; reduces amount of gas, cost, lithium-nickel mixing degree of the high-nickel anode material, residual alkali content on the surface of the material and sensitivity of the material to air; improves consistency and stability of the material, storage time of the material and processing properties of the material; prolongs service life of the battery; and increases conductivity of the material and material rate performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving the stability and electrical conductivity of a high nickel anode material comprises (i) taking high-temperature sintering furnace with a porcelain boat, where the porcelain boat jacket is provided with a corundum tube, and the air inlet of the corundum tube is provided with a carbon dioxide passage, an oxygen passage and an ozone passage with a valve in parallel, an ozone generator is further disposed on the ozone channel, and the gas outlet of the corundum tube is provided with an exhaust gas absorption device; (ii) mixing high nickel anode material and the lithium salt are uniformly, adding into a high-temperature sintering furnace and sealing furnace body to ensure that the pipeline valves of the furnace body are all in a closed state; (iii) opening the oxygen valve of the furnace body, passing oxygen and/or air, heating the furnace body to 400-600 degrees C for the first time at 1-10 degrees C/min, and maintaining temperature for 0.5-3 hours for pre-sintering; (iv) closing oxygen-passing valve, opening ozone-opening valve at the same time, introducing oxygen into the furnace body, heating furnace body to 700-850 degrees C for the second time at 0.5-10 degrees C/min, when the furnace body reaches the highest sintering temperature, turning on the ozone generator to convert a part of oxygen into ozone, placing the steel for 3 to 16 hours for sintering, and collecting excess ozone in the furnace body by the exhaust gas absorption device; (v) closing ozone valve, opening carbon dioxide valve at the same time, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the furnace body for annealing treatment for 10-120 minutes at 400-700 degrees C and using exhaust gas absorption device for charging excess carbon dioxide in the furnace; (vi) closing carbon dioxide valve and cooling the material in the furnace naturally to room temperature to obtain high nickel anode material; and (vii) pushing high nickel anode material obtained into the carbon dioxide plasma generator for 10-120 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The step (iv) comprises turning on ozone generator for 1-10 times, with an interval of 0.5 -1.5 hours after the furnace body reaches the maximum temperature and opening the ozone generator each time for 10-30 minutes. The step (iii) comprises mixing oxygen and air in the furnace body in any ratio. The reaction process of high nickel anode material in the plasma generator in step (vii) comprises (a) laying high nickel anode material into the container, placing the container into the cavity of the plasma generator, opening the vacuum pump, and pumping the plasma generator chamber to a vacuum; (b) introducing dry carbon dioxide gas into the cavity, maintaining dry carbon dioxide gas air pressure in the cavity at 500-700 Pa for 20-60 s, and extracting carbon dioxide gas, so that the vacuum degree of the cavity is maintained at 40-50 Pa; and (c) turning on the plasma generator, adjusting the power, and reacting for 10-120 minutes. Preferred Composition: The high nickel anode material has a tiling thickness of 0.3-10 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium salt in the step (i) comprises at least one of lithium hydroxide, lithium carbonate or lithium acetate, and the chemical formula of the high nickel anode material precursor is Ni(1-x-y)CoxMy(OH)2, wherex+y = less than or equal to 0.7; andM = manganese or aluminum.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the high nickel anode material precursor to the lithium salt is 1:1-1.2.

AU ZHOU X
   CHEN Z
   LU Q
   CAI H
   WANG H
   BAO Z
AE ZHEJIANG JUHUA TECHNOLOGY CENT CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202191463T
AB    NOVELTY - Copolymer, which is terpolymer composed of vinylidene fluoride, trifluoroethylene, and tetrafluoroethylene, is claimed.
   USE - Used as copolymer containing vinylidene fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The copolymer has improved dielectric constant and higher piezoelectric voltage constant g33.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the copolymer, comprising polymerizing, coagulating, washing, and drying, where the polymerizing method is suspension polymerization, emulsion polymerization, solution polymerization, or supercritical carbon dioxide polymerization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The molar content of vinylidene fluoride is 40-90%, preferably 50-80%, the molar content of trifluoroethylene is 10-55%, preferably 15-45%, and the molar content of tetrafluoroethylene is 3-20%, preferably 5-15%. Preferred Method: The polymerizing method is suspension polymerization. The preparation method of vinylidene fluoride copolymer specifically comprises placing vinylidene fluoride, trifluoroethylene and tetrafluoroethylene into the polymerization kettle, carrying out free radical suspension polymerization in the presence of initiator, dispersant, chain transfer agent and deionized water, then washing and drying to obtain vinylidene fluoride polymer. In the polymerization step, placing 100 pts. wt. deionized water, 16-27 pts. wt. vinylidene fluoride monomer, and 5-18 pts. wt. trifluoroethylene monomer, 0.5-5 pts. wt. tetrafluoroethylene monomer, 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.005-0.1 pts. wt. dispersant and 0.0002-0.01 pts. wt. chain transfer agent into the polymerization kettle, and terminating the reaction after 3-20 hours of polymerization reaction at 20-70 degrees C. The polymerization temperature is 25-50 degrees C, and the polymerization reaction time is 5-10 hours. Preferred Components: The initiator is diethyl peroxydicarbonate, dicyclohexyl peroxydicarbonate, tert-butyl peroxyneodecanoate, diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, di-n-propyl peroxydicarbonate, and/or tert-butyl peroxypivalate, the organic initiator is 0.03-0.5 pts. wt.. The dispersant is methyl cellulose, hydroxyethyl cellulose ether, hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose ether, and/or polyethylene glycol, the dispersant is 0.01-0.05 pts. wt.. The chain transfer agent is hydrogen, and the chain transfer agent is 0.0006-0.006 pts. wt..

PN CN112159579-A
TI Functional degradable material with controllable degradation period comprises polybutylene terephthalate, carbon dioxide copolymer, polybutylene succinate, compatilizer, lubricant, plasticizer and antioxidant as raw materials.
AU QIAO Z
   GAN G
   WEN S
AE GUANGDONG GUOLI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202103975R
AB    NOVELTY - Functional degradable material with controllable degradation period comprises polybutylene terephthalate, carbon dioxide copolymer, polybutylene succinate, compatilizer, lubricant, plasticizer and antioxidant as raw materials.
   USE - Functional degradable material with controllable degradation period.
   ADVANTAGE - The functional degradable material with controllable degradation period prepares the full biodegradable material with different degradation period and is suitable for different degradation period of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing functional degradable material with controllable degradation period, which involves:
   (A) weighing the corresponding weight of polybutylene terephthalate, carbon dioxide copolymer, polybutylene succinate, polyglycolic acid, polydioxanone mixture and compatibilizer, mixing and stirring uniformly in a high-speed mixer at 70-90 degrees C and 2000-2500 revolutions per minute (r/min) for 15-30 minutes to obtain component A;
   (B) mixing the lubricant, antioxidant and modified starch in a high-speed mixer and stirring at 40-60 degrees C and 1500-2000 r/min for 5 -12 minutes to obtain component B;
   (C) adding the component A into the double-screw extruder from the main feeding hopper, adding the component B from the side feeding hopper into the double-screw extruder, adding plasticizer from the oil pump into the double-screw extruder, extruding the material strip into cold water, cooling, granulating to obtain the blend granules, where main machine screw rotating speed is 150-200 r/min and temperature of first to eigth section of the double-screw from the feeding section to the machine head ranges from 150-185 degrees C;
   (D) placing the blend granules in a drying box to dry at 60-80 degrees C, until the water content of the blend grain material is less than 0.01%; and
   (E) processing the dried blend granules on the common injection machine to obtain the functional degradable material with controllable degradation period, where the temperature of molding machine is 100-160 degrees C, the nozzle temperature is 170-185 degrees C, the mold temperature is 30-60 degrees C, the injection pressure is 4-8 megapascal for is 2-5 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The raw material comprises 30-50 pts. wt. polybutylene terephthalate, 5-8 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer, 10-40 pts. wt. poly(butylene succinate), 3-5 pts. wt. compatilizer, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. lubricant, 3-5 pts. wt. plasticizer and 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. antioxidant. Preferred Components: The raw material comprises modified starch, and the mixture of polyglycolic acid and poly-p-dioxanone. The compatilizer is selected from maleic anhydride, acrylic ester, silane coupling agent and titanic acid ester coupling agent. The lubricant is selected from erucamide, oleic acid amide and polyethylene wax. The plasticizer is selected from glycerol, stearic acid, epoxy fatty acid octylester, acetyltributylcitrate, dioctyl adipate and dioctyl sebacate. The antioxidant is selected from antioxidant 1024, antioxidant 1010, antioxidant B215, antioxidant B225, antioxidant 1076, antioxidant B68 and antioxidant B900. Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of the modified starch to the poly (butylene succinate) is (0.2-1.2):1. The addition amount of the mixture of polyglycolic acid and poly (p-dioxanone) is 2-6 wt.% of the total weight of the raw material. The weight ratio of the polyglycolic acid to the poly-p-dioxanone is (5-12):(2-5).

PN DE102019209043-A1; WO2020254625-A1; EP3799592-A1; EP3799592-B1; US2022112089-A1; ES2914872-T3
TI Manufacture of fired end product from educt e.g. limestone, involves forming reaction zone is in form of fluidized bed or fluidized bed with starting material and fluidizing gas, heating zone for burning starting material and discharging.
AU NELLES F
   STUMPF T
   STUMPF T D
AE LITHERM TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (LITH-Non-standard)
   LITHERM TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (LITH-Non-standard)
   LITHERM TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (LITH-Non-standard)
   LITHERM TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (LITH-Non-standard)
GA 2020C9589R
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of fired end product involves forming a reaction zone (4) in the form of a fluidized bed or a fluidized bed with the starting material and a fluidizing gas, heating the reaction zone to at least one predetermined temperature range for burning the starting material for a predetermined residence time of the starting material in the reaction zone, and discharging the end product from the reaction zone after the predetermined residence time.
   USE - Manufacture of fired end product e.g. quicklime from natural, carbonate-containing, granular substances as educt such as limestone and dolomite (all claimed) used in industrial processes, manufacture of building materials and in environmental applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of soft, medium and hard fired end product by setting precise operating conditions with which the two important quality parameters t60 and residual carbon dioxide content can be variably set within limits by setting the fluidized bed temperature and the particle dwell time. Because the carbonate-containing substances to be burned are heated in a fluidized bed or a fluidized bed, fine-grained starting materials can be processed into end product by thermal treatment. The residence time in the reaction zone can be adjusted. The fluidized beds in calcination are in homogeneous reaction conditions (e.g. temperature) and have high heat transfer. The fuels such as biomass, waste wood or sewage sludge or other biogenic fuels are used, and the carbon dioxide emissions are reduced in accordance with the specific fuel-related carbon dioxide emissions. The fine-grained particles of limestone can be calcined with significantly shorter residence times than in the case with lump lime. In the case of fine-grained limestone, this risk of sintering is reduced due to the particle size, and the chemical impurities are distributed over the entire contents of the reactor and so there are no local increased concentrations of chemical impurities.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for manufacturing fired end products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the manufacture of fired end products.
   Calciner (2)
   Reaction zone (4)
   Fluidizing gas feed line (10)
   Electric heater (11)
   Gas supply (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: As the fluidizing gas, carbon dioxide and/or air is used.

PN CN111718243-A
TI Preparation of high purity cannabidiol from industrial hemp by performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction on dried powder, obtaining crude oil of extract, and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid temperature gradient rectifying.
AU ZHU J
   CUI H
   SU R
   DENG D
   WANG Y
   YU T
AE DALIAN BOMEXC SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 202098385F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high purity cannabidiol from industrial hemp comprises performing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid extraction on dried powder, and obtaining crude oil of extract and medicinal residue; rectification comprising subjecting the extract crude oil in the rectifying tower to supercritical CO2 fluid temperature gradient rectification, separating the product in the separating kettle, obtaining hemp essential oil in separating kettle C2, and obtaining crude residual oil and cannabidiol substance in other separating kettle C3 at tower bottom; supercritical chromatography comprising putting the separated hemp essential oil and phenol in the chromatographic separation column D5, using supercritical CO2 fluid and entrainer as mobile phase for chromatographic separation, obtaining cannabidiol and separated non-cannabidiol phenolic substance; and subjecting the separated cannabidiol to low temperature solvent crystallization and recrystallization method.
   USE - The method is for preparation of high purity cannabidiol from industrial hemp.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high purity product, has simple operation, low cost, and small pollution, and realizes sealed and green environment-friendly industrial production of cannabidiol from industrial hemp.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high purity cannabidiol from industrial hemp comprises performing supercritical CO2 fluid extraction on dried powder, and obtaining crude oil of extract and medicinal residue; rectification comprising subjecting the extract crude oil in the rectifying tower to supercritical CO2 fluid temperature gradient rectification, separating the product in the separating kettle, obtaining hemp essential oil in separating kettle C2, and obtaining crude residual oil and cannabidiol substance in other separating kettle C3 at tower bottom; supercritical chromatography comprising putting the separated hemp essential oil and phenol in the chromatographic separation column D5, using supercritical CO2 fluid and entrainer as mobile phase for chromatographic separation, obtaining cannabidiol and separated non-cannabidiol phenolic substance; and subjecting the separated cannabidiol to low temperature solvent crystallization and recrystallization method to obtain cannabidiol with content of more than 99%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for preparing high purity cannabidiol from industrial hemp comprising CO2 supply and recovery module, supercritical fluid extraction module, rectification module and supercritical chromatographic separation module.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises pretreatment step comprising crushing the selected industrial hemp flower and leaf to 50-120 mesh, and drying for 1-2 hours at 105-150 degrees C to control the water content of the medicine material below 5%. In the first step, the extraction temperature is 30-60 degrees C, the pressure is 10-30 MPa, the extraction time is 2-6 hours, and the CO2 flow rate is 30-150 kg/kg raw material. The chromatographic separation mobile phase comprises supercritical CO2 fluid and entrainer i.e. ethanol, methanol, acetone, chloroform, methylene dichloride, and/or ethyl acetate. The volume ratio of entrainer in the supercritical CO2 fluid is 0.2-15%. The low temperature solvent is petroleum ether, n-hexane, n-heptane, ethanol, and/or methanol.

The outlet side of a first throttle valve (7) is connected with the feeding port of a deethanizer (8). PT P
PN CN111514898-A; CN111514898-B
TI Highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst for methanation of carbon dioxide, comprises carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotubes as a carrier to support active metallic nickel.
AU ZHOU S
AE TAIZHOU HEYI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (TAIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202078759X
AB    NOVELTY - Highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst comprises carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotubes as a carrier to support 2-15 wt.% active metallic nickel.
   USE - Highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst for methanation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst exhibits high catalytic activity and selectivity in the carbon dioxide methanation reaction, and has good application prospects, and contains active metal that is not easy to fall off and agglomerate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst, which involves dissolving a certain amount of template cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) in a mixed solution of water and ethanol, and magnetically stirring to dissolve; adding 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES) and 1,2-bis(trimethoxysilyl)ethane (BTME) in a mass ratio of 1:1-10 to the obtained solution, stirring continuously for 1-2 hours, pouring the mixture into a polytetrafluoroethylene liner and allowing the mixture to stand at 60-100 degrees C for 24-48 hours; centrifuging, extracting the resulting product with a mixed solution of ethanol and hydrochloric acid at 40-80 degrees C to reflux the template, and centrifuging and washing to obtain organic bridged periodic mesoporous organic silicon oxide material containing amino groups (PMO-NH2); dissolving soluble nickel salt in ethanol solution to obtain 0.01-0.1 M nickel salt precursor solution, and immersing the PMO-NH2 as a carrier in the nickel salt precursor solution and ultrasonicating for 2-4 hours; subjecting the amino group in the PMO and the metal nickel ion to perform the coordination reaction, and anchoring the nickel ion on the surface of the carrier by the coordination bond; separating the obtained product by centrifugation, thoroughly washing with deionized water and ethanol, and drying at 60-100 degrees C; and placing the obtained product in a tube furnace, and heating at 500-900 degrees C at a heating rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute for 2-6 hours under the protection of nitrogen to obtain the desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of nickel in the catalyst is 5-10 wt.% based on the metal. The nickel salt is selected from nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, and nickel sulfate, and the concentration of the nickel salt precursor solution is 0.02-0.05 M. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the template agent and APTES is 1:1-5, and the mass ratio of APTES and BTME is 1:3-8. The mixture is allowed to stand at 60-90 degrees C for 24-36 hours. The calcination is performed at 600-800 degrees C for 3-4 hours.

PN WO2019173387-A1; US2020397968-A1
TI Infusing blood with gas comprises creating bubbles of gas in medium, reducing bubbles to nanobubbles, introducing medium containing nanobubbles into bloodstream of patient and rupturing nanobubbles using external energy at target location.
AU YANNOPOULOS D
   WILSON R F
AE YANNOPOULOS D (YANN-Individual)
   WILSON R F (WILS-Individual)
   YANNOPOULOS D (YANN-Individual)
   WILSON R F (WILS-Individual)
GA 201978852W
AB    NOVELTY - Method of infusing blood with a gas comprises (a) creating bubbles of gas in a medium, (b) reducing the bubbles to nanobubbles, where the diameter of the nanobubbles is less than 500 nm, (c) introducing the medium containing the nanobubbles into a bloodstream of a patient and (d) rupturing the nanobubbles using external energy at a target location.
   USE - The method is used for infusing blood with a gas and removing carbon dioxide from a bloodstream (claimed). The method is used for oxygenating blood.
   ADVANTAGE - The method facilitates rapid oxygenation or gas release in the blood vessels. The nanobubbles can stay in solution for days to a month. The ultrasound induced rupture facilitates effective oxygenation of oxygen nanobubbles and enables selective oxygenation or delivery of gas to a particular organ and increases the gas concentration in the plasma, interstitial fluid or cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system (A) for infusing blood with a gas comprising a gas source, a liquid medium, a gas infuser connected to the gas source and located in the liquid medium that creates gas bubbles when gas from the gas source flows through the gas diffuser, an ensonifier located in the liquid medium and connected to a power source, where the ensonifier reduces the gas bubbles to nanobubbles, when energized, and a catheter useable to deliver the nanobubbles in the medium to a bloodstream of a patient; and
   (2) a system (B) for removing carbon dioxide from a bloodstream comprising a first chamber useable to receive blood from a patient, a second chamber, a permeable membrane separating the first chamber from the second chamber, a vacuum useable to lower a pressure in the second chamber such that flow of carbon dioxide from the blood through the membrane is increased, and a flow pump connected to the first chamber on an input side of the flow pump and connected to the patient on an output side of the flow pump such that when energized, the flow pump delivers blood from the first chamber to the patient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves creating bubbles of oxygen in a container in vivo or creating bubbles of gas in blood from the bloodstream that has been routed into a catheter. The step (d) comprises energizing the nanobubbles using ultrasonic energy at a resonant frequency of the nanobubbles. The step (b) comprises ensonifying the bubbles. The step (c) comprises releasing the blood that has been routed into the catheter back into the bloodstream through blood outflow holes created in the catheter. Preferred Components: The gas source comprises an oxygen source. The liquid medium comprises saline or blood. The gas diffuser comprises a ceramic stone. The ensonifier comprises a piezoelectric ultrasound transducer or an ultrasound generator attached to a wire. The ensonifier is located within the catheter. The system (B) further comprises: a system for infusing blood with a gas connected between the first chamber and the flow pump; a system for infusing blood with a gas connected to the output side of the flow pump; a sonification system connected between the first chamber and the flow pump to prevent gas bubble coalescence and to reduce a diameter of any bubbles present in the blood to less than 10 micrometer; and a carbon dioxide infusion controller that controls the pressure in the second chamber.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment)

TI Non-metallic element doped cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide composite catalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide as active component and non-metallic element as dopant.
AU WANG Y
   LI X
   XIA Q
   SHEN Z
   YIN C
   LI Y
   GE Z
AE UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
GA 201962867H
AB    NOVELTY - Non-metallic element doped cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide composite catalyst comprises cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide as active component and a non-metallic element as dopant. The molar ratio of cobalt(II,III) oxide and cerium(IV) oxide in the cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide active component is 0.1-2:1. The non-metallic element is nitrogen, sulfur, boron and/or fluorine.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has high specific surface area, rich nanopore structure, narrow band gap width and visible light absorption performance, and exhibits high activity and selectivity in photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide. The method is simple and easy to expand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing non-metallic element doped cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide composite catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving a compound containing a target non-metallic element and a metal nitrate containing cobalt and ruthenium in deionized water to obtain a precursor mixed solution, stirring and mixing for 0.5-6 hours, and then air-drying at 30-100 degrees C to obtain a first powder, where the compound containing a target non-metallic element is urea, thiourea, boric acid and/or ammonium fluoride, the compound containing a target non-metallic element has function of a pore former, the metal nitrate containing cobalt is cobalt nitrate, the metal nitrate containing cerium is cerium nitrate and/or cerium ammonium nitrate, and the amount of the compound containing a target non-metallic element, metal nitrate and deionized water used above is calculated as a ratio of metal nitrate, non- compound containing a target non-metallic element and deionized water of 2-40 mmol: 20-300 mmol: 10-100 ml, and (ii) placing the first powder into a muffle furnace, raising temperature to 200-800 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-20 degrees C/minute under an oxygen atmosphere, calcining for 1-12 hours, naturally cooling to obtain non-metallic element doped cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide composite catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite catalyst has a specific surface area of 10-200 m2/g, a pore diameter of 1-30 nm, and a pore volume of 0.05-1.85 cm3/g. The molar ratio of the non-metallic element dopant to cobalt(II,III) oxide-cerium(IV) oxide active component is 1:11.1-22.5. Preferred Method: The air drying is carried out at 40-70 degrees C. The calcination is carried out at 400-550 degrees C for 1-6 hours.

TI Tea saponin instant-soluble solid detergent for removing fruits and vegetables pesticides, comprises tea saponin formula, effervescent inclusion compound, binder, lubricant and filler.
AU HUANG Q
   WU J
   YOU R
   TAN Q
   LU Y
AE UNIV FUJIAN (UYFJ-C)
GA 201933464Q
AB    NOVELTY - Tea saponin instant-soluble solid detergent comprises 30-55 pts. wt. tea saponin formula, 25-50 pts. wt. effervescent inclusion compound, 3-6 pts. wt. binder, 1-5 pts. wt. lubricant and 1-4 pts. wt. filler.
   USE - Tea saponin instant-soluble solid detergent is used for removing fruits and vegetables pesticides.
   ADVANTAGE - The tea saponin instant-soluble solid detergent can efficiently clean fruit and vegetable surface, has good removal force,because the tea saponin is excellent plant non-ionic surface active agent, and has good emulsifying, and solid detergent in the disintegrating agent can reach the effect of adsorption and surface bacteria and removing pesticide residue, safety and non-toxicity, long-term use does not stimulate skin, green and environment-friendly, and bringing convenient use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing tea saponin instant-soluble solid detergent, which involves:
   (A) weighing 30-55 pts. wt. tea saponin formula and 9-25 pts. wt. effervescent acid source inclusion compound, and mixing uniformly at room temperature;
   (B) drying the mixture in a hot air drying oven at 55 degrees C, and grounding for 1-2 minutes, passing through a 20 mesh sieve, and mixing with 95 wt.% alcohol in a clockwise direction, until the material is caught, it can be agglomerating and will not be easily dispersing, that is, mixed particles;
   (C) drying 12.5-32 pts. wt. effervescent carbon dioxide source inclusion compound is at 50 degrees C, grounding for 1-2 minutes, the granules are passing through a 20 mesh sieve, and using 95 wt.% alcohol, and spraying in a clockwise direction, until the material is in a state of being caught and can not be easily dispersed, that is, an effervescent carbon dioxide source clathrate particle is obtained; and
   (D) mixing particles, effervescent carbon dioxide source clathrate particles, 3-6 pts. wt. binder, 1-5 pts. wt. lubricant, and 1-4 pts. wt. filler uniformly, drying at 50 degrees C, the material obtained after drying can be directly pressed, the tableting temperature is controlled at 20-30 degrees C, and the tea saponin fast-dissolving solid effervescent tablet is prepared by controlling the pressure of the tablet press at 50-100 kilonewtons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The tea saponin formula comprises 30-40 pts. wt. Camellia saponin, 30-40 pt. wt. saponin, 12-20 pts. wt. alkyl betaines and 8-20 pts. wt. oleic acid potassium glycinate. The effervescent inclusion compound is composed of an effervescent acid source inclusion compound and an effervescent agent carbon dioxide source inclusion compound, and the acid source in the effervescent acid source inclusion compound is oxalic acid, citric acid, tartaric acid, One or two of adipic acid, the carbon dioxide source in the effervescent carbon dioxide source inclusion compound is two or three kinds of sodium carbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate and sodium percarbonate. Preferred Process: The method for extracting the Camellia tea saponin involves weighing 30 g of the pulverized defatted tea seed cake, and immersing with 120 mL of a 75 wt.% ethanol solution in an ultrasonic assisted manner, and filtering the filtrate to be heated by a rotary evaporator concentrate, and drying in an oven at 70 degrees C to obtain Camellia tea saponin. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of acid source to carbon dioxide source is 1:1-2. Preferred Components: The lubricant is one of magnesium stearate or talc.

PN CN109172621-A
TI Utilizing resources of ginkgo, by taking ginkgo, filtering enzymatic hydrolyzate, subjecting wet powder to steam distillation, introducing carrier and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and separating extract into rectification column.
AU CHANG Y
AE JIANGSU PUHUI GINKGO IND DEV CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201908411V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for utilizing resources of ginkgo involves taking 40-50 pts. wt. ginkgo, drying the material at 110-130 degrees C for 1-2 hours, crushing the dried material into powder, adding 150-200 parts mass polyethylene glycol aqueous solution (1-2 %mass), stirring the material, heating at 50-60 degrees C for 2-4 hours and cooling the heated material, adding 1-4% trypsin, 0.4% sodium chloride, 0.2% lecithin, 0.5% ammonium acetate, 0.3% sodium hydrogencarbonate and 0.4% ascorbic acid to the leach solution, heating the buffer solution to 35-45 degrees C for enzymatic hydrolysis, filtering the enzymatic hydrolyzate and pressing the filter residue to remove excess water to obtain a wet powder, subjecting the wet powder to steam distillation at 50-70 degrees C and drying the wet powder after steam distillation, adding into the extraction vessel, introducing the carrier and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and separating the extract into a rectification column through a pressure reducing valve.
   USE - The method is useful for utilizing resources of ginkgo.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for utilizing resources of ginkgo involves (a) taking 40-50 pts. wt. ginkgo, drying the material at 110-130 degrees C for 1-2 hours, crushing the dried material into powder, adding 150-200 parts mass polyethylene glycol aqueous solution (1-2 %mass), stirring the material, heating at 50-60 degrees C for 2-4 hours and cooling the heated material, (b) adding 1-4% trypsin, 0.4% sodium chloride, 0.2% lecithin, 0.5% ammonium acetate, 0.3% sodium hydrogencarbonate and 0.4% ascorbic acid to the leach solution, heating the buffer solution to 35-45 degrees C for enzymatic hydrolysis, (c) filtering the enzymatic hydrolyzate obtained in the step (b) and pressing the filter residue to remove excess water to obtain a wet powder, (d) subjecting the wet powder obtained in the step (c) to steam distillation at 50-70 degrees C and (e) drying the wet powder after steam distillation, adding into the extraction vessel, introducing the carrier and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and separating the extract into a rectification column through a pressure reducing valve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Property: The pH of the phosphate buffer is 6-7. Preferred Composition: The ratio of the phosphate buffer solution to the ginkgo is 3-20 ml: 1 g. Preferred Method: In step (d), the distillation is performed for 2-4 hours. In step (e), the extraction is performed at 45-60 degrees C, 25-30 MPa and 300-320 kg/hour for 1-2 hours and the rectification is performed at 4-70 MPa and 25-30 degrees C for 1-3 hours.

PT P
PN US2019002661-A1; EP3421214-A1; CN109203342-A; US11034812-B2; EP3421214-B1
TI Apparatus for dispersion and mixing of expansion            gas such as carbon dioxide in reactive resin, comprises            mixing chamber having supply input for gas and supply            input for resin is provided, where chamber is            dispersion and containment chamber.
AU VOLPATO M
   CORTI M
AE AFROS SPA (AFRO-Non-standard)
   AFROS SPA (AFRO-Non-standard)
   AFROS SPA (AFRO-Non-standard)
   AFROS SPA (AFRO-Non-standard)
GA 201900164V
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus (1) comprises a mixing chamber                having a supply input (27) for the gas and a supply                input for the resin is provided, where chamber is a                dispersion and containment chamber made in a casing                (2) of predetermined high resistance susceptible to                sustain high pressure. The chamber is divided into                two sections by a head (14) of a dispersion and                mixing cylinder-piston group (4). The two sections                are in fluid communication between themselves by                using a pouring passage, and provided witha static                mixer in pouring passage, and a motor for piston to                control the movement of mixing cylinder-piston                group.
   USE - Apparatus for dispersion and mixing of                expansion gas such as carbon dioxide in reactive                resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus provides for an unusually                efficient mechanical dispersion of the gas in the                resin maintained in supercritical conditions with                high pressures and prefixed temperature adjusting                the dosage in mass ratio of the reactive                resin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process for the formation of                polyurethane foam starting from the dispersion of                an expansion gas, e.g. carbon dioxide, in a                reactive resin, which involves using an initial                dispersion and mixing phase of the two components                in order to form an intermediate resin-gas mixture                as a first reactive component of a final chemical                reaction, a subsequent dosing phase of first                reactive component in a mixing and reaction plant                with a second reactive component in order to form                polyurethane foam, where a controlled dispersion                and mixture of the two components is provided in a                dispersion and containment chamber kept under                pressure and divided into two sections by a head of                a cylinder-piston mixing group; and
   (2) system for the formation of a polyurethane                foam starting from a first reactive component                comprises an intermediate resin-gas mixture and a                second reactive component comprising a reactive                resin, e.g. isocyanate, where intermediate                resin-gas mixture is obtained in an                apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                apparatus.
   Apparatus (1)
   Casing (2)
   Cylinder-piston group (4)
   Head (14)
   Supply input (27)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gas is carbon                  dioxide maintained in supercritical temperature                  and pressure conditions. The casing of                  predetermined high resistance is formed by the                  containment walls of dispersion and mixing the                  cylinder-piston group. The high pressures vary                  from 75-350 bar. The carbon dioxide is maintained                  in supercritical temperature and pressure                  conditions during the dispersion, mixing and                  dosing phase. The intermediate resin-gas mixture                  that forms first reactive component is sent under                  pressure to a mixing head to be mixed at high                  pressure with second reactive component, e.g.                  isocyanate, dosed in a stoichiometric mass ratio,                  and the mixture is recirculated through sections                  of the cylinder-piston mixing group to set its                  exact flow. The expansion gas of resin-gas is                  carbon dioxide kept in supercritical temperature                  and pressure conditions. The process of forming                  the polyurethane foam provides for a control loop                  by using a flow and pressure transducer installed                  on a circuit in a closed flow loop feedback by                  using a flow transducer. The first dosing phase                  of the first reactive component a re-circling is                  provided towards a section of the cylinder-piston                  mixing group in order to precisely set the flows                  in a stoichiometric ratio by using a valve member                  or mixing distributor installed in a mixing                  head.

PN WO2018206709-A1; EP3400810-A1; AR111929-A1; CA3059503-A1; AU2018265209-A1; IN201917042216-A; CN110612030-A; KR2020006117-A; EP3621451-A1; BR112019023663-A2; US2020187538-A1; JP2020519271-W; MX2019013315-A1; US2022104525-A1; RU2019139422-A; JP7142647-B2
TI Edible composition used to coat e.g. food product            comprises water and edible oil and reaction product of            natural ground calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide            which is supplied externally and hydronium ion            (surface-reacted calcium carbonate).
AU BUDDE T
   HECKER A
   SHARMA L
   DE MIGUEL L
   MIGUEL L D
   DE M L
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2018897262
AB    NOVELTY - Edible composition comprises water and/or at                least one edible oil and a surface-reacted calcium                carbonate having a volume median particle size d50                0.1-90 mu m, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium                (H3O+) ion donor, where the carbon dioxide is                formed in situ by the hydronium ion donors                treatment and/or is supplied from an external                source.
   USE - The edible composition is used as a food                coating, sugar coating, sugar-free coating, nutra                coating, food decoration, food filling,                pharmaceutical coating, where the food product                comprises cakes, cookies, candies, cereals, cereal                bars, chips, chewing gum, ice cream or wafer, or                the pharmaceutical product or neutraceutical                product comprises tablets, mini-tables, pellets,                capsules or granules.
   ADVANTAGE - The edible composition utilizes the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate: has a volume                median particle size d50 is 0.1-75 mu m, preferably                1.5-15 mu m; has a specific surface area of from                15-200 m2/g, preferably 30-140 m2/g, measured using                nitrogen and the BET method; acts as replacement                agent for titanium dioxide, hence provides                composition free of titanium dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) an edible coating and/or filling                comprising the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate;
   (2) a food product or pharmaceutical product                or neutraceutical product at least partially coated                and/or filled with the edible composition;                and
   (3) producing a food or pharmaceutical product                or neutraceutical products at least partially                coated and/or filled with the edible composition                comprising (a) mixing water and/or edible oil with                the surface-reacted calcium carbonate to obtain the                composition and (b) applying the composition                obtained in step (a), at least one time, at least                partially on the surface of a food product or                pharmaceutical product or neutraceutical product,                or filling the composition obtained in step (a)                into a food or pharmaceutical or neutraceutical                products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 1-50 (preferably 2.5-12) wt.%                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The                  composition further comprises: at least one                  additive comprising icing sugar, starches, such                  as wheat starch, rice starch, tapioca starch,                  corn starch, potato starch and/or pre-gelatinized                  acetylated distarch adipate, binding agents, such                  as gum arabic and/or locust bean gum, waxes, such                  as carnauba wax and/or montan wax, preservatives,                  such as propionic acid, sorbic acid, benzoic acid                  and its salt, antioxidants, such as                  butylhydroxyanisol (BHA), ascorbic acid,                  tocopherol, propyl gallate and/or octyl gallate,                  stabilizers or texture modifier, such as agar                  agar and/or gelatin, proteins, emulsifier                  comprising sucrose ester or lecithin and/or                  sorbitane monostearates, flavors, artificial                  sweetener, sugar such as fructose, glucose,                  sucrose, glucose-fructose mixtures and/or maltose                  and polyols such as mannitol, sorbitol, xylitol                  and/or isomalt, defoaming agents, surfactants,                  colorants, plasticizers and film-forming agents;                  at least one natural ground calcium carbonate                  (NGCC) and/or at least one precipitated calcium                  carbonate (PCC) (both preferred) has/have a                  weight median particle size d50 0.05-100,                  preferably 0.5-40 mu m. Preferred Components: The                  natural ground calcium carbonate comprises                  marble, chalk and/or limestone, or the                  precipitated calcium carbonate comprises                  precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal                  form. The hydronium ion donor comprises                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid (all preferred), oxalic acid, an                  acidic salt, acetic acid, citric acid and/or                  formic acid or dihydrogen phosphate, at least                  partially neutralized by a cation comprises                  lithium, sodium and/or potassium, magnesium                  and/or calcium. The surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate is used as whitening agent or opacifier                  and/or sweetness reduction agent and/or calorie                  reduction agent in an edible coating and/or                  filling of a food product or pharmaceutical                  product or neutraceutical product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The edible oil                  comprises palm oil, sunflower oil, soybean oil,                  coconut oil, peanut oil and/or olive oil.                  Preferred Method: The step (b) is carried out by                  brushing or pouring, pan coating, curtain or dip                  coating, fluidized bed coating, hot melt coating                  and/or compression coating or by injecting the                  composition into the food product or                  pharmaceutical product or neutraceutical                  product.
   EXAMPLE - Typical edible coating composition comprised                of 525 wt.% a high purity natural calcium                carbonate; 975 wt.% crystal sugar and 500 wt.%                demineralized water.

PT P
PN CN108239582-A; CN208395130-U
TI Beer production carbon dioxide recycling device includes group of tubular heat exchangers as condensing tower provided between refrigerating unit and drying tower, and another group of heat exchangers as precooling tower.
AU HUANG X
   LIU Y
AE YANJING BEER GUILIN LIQUAN CO LTD (YANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201856582P
AB    NOVELTY - Beer production carbon dioxide (CO2) recycling device comprises adsorbing tower, drying tower, refrigerating unit and CO2 air supply buffer tank, where a group of tubular heat exchangers as condensing tower is provided between refrigerating unit and drying tower, another group of heat exchangers as precooling tower is provided between the adsorbing tower and drying tower, the liquid CO2 from the refrigerating unit enters the shell side of the condensing tower, the gaseous CO2 from the drying tower enters the tube side of the condensing tower, the gaseous CO2 releases heat, is converted into low temperature liquid CO2 and flows out of the condensing tower by self-gravity, the liquid CO2 absorbs heat to generate low temperature gaseous CO2, which enters the tube side of the precooling tower, and the high temperature gaseous CO2 from the adsorbing tower enters the shell side of the precooling tower.
   USE - Beer production CO2 recycling device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device fully uses the energy released by liquid CO2 and reduces the power consumption and steam consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Beer production carbon dioxide (CO2) recycling device comprises adsorbing tower, drying tower, refrigerating unit and CO2 air supply buffer tank. A group of tubular heat exchangers as condensing tower is provided between refrigerating unit and drying tower. Another group of heat exchangers as precooling tower is provided between the adsorbing tower and drying tower. The liquid CO2 from the refrigerating unit enters the shell side of the condensing tower. The gaseous CO2 from the drying tower enters the tube side of the condensing tower. The gaseous CO2 releases heat and is converted into low temperature liquid CO2 and flows out of the condensing tower by self-gravity. The liquid CO2 absorbs heat to generate low temperature gaseous CO2, which enters the tube side of the precooling tower. The high temperature gaseous CO2 from the adsorbing tower enters the shell side of the precooling tower. The low temperature gaseous CO2 has temperature raised to room temperature and enters the CO2 air supply buffer tank. The high temperature gaseous CO2 becomes low temperature gas CO2 and enters the tube side of the condensing tower.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC D05-B02; Q75-A02H; Q75-T03

PN CN108165531-A
TI Umbilical cord blood mononuclear cell culture method using hematopoietic stem cells comprises separating the umbilical cord blood, screening the blood carrying a virus, culturing, removing un-adhered cells and digesting.
AU WANG Y
   WU S
   WU X
AE HUAIBEI ZHIHUAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2018491399
AB    NOVELTY - Umbilical cord blood mononuclear cell culture method using hematopoietic stem cells comprises: (i) separating the umbilical cord blood, then screening the blood carrying a virus or genetic family history; (ii) culturing the cord blood mononuclear cells by directed culture to obtain a primary culture; (iii) culturing, removing un-adhered cells and changing the culture medium for every 1-2 days during directed differentiation; (iv) continuing to culture for 2-6 days to obtain primary culture, replacing the culture medium once in 1-2 days during cultivation; (v) digesting the resultant primary culture at 35-39 degrees C under 3-7% carbon dioxide condition, passaging culture medium under aseptic condition and culturing hematopoietic stem cells for 4-5 passages; and (vi) counting cell number and cell viability, mixing the cells and the complex electrolyte solution according to the counting results, subjecting to endotoxin and bacterial tests and storing at low temperature.
   USE - Used as umbilical cord blood mononuclear cell culture method using hematopoietic stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Umbilical cord blood mononuclear cell culture method using hematopoietic stem cells comprises: (i) separating the umbilical cord blood, then screening the blood carrying a virus or genetic family history; (ii) culturing the cord blood mononuclear cells by directed culture to obtain a primary culture; (iii) culturing at 35-39 degrees C under 3-7% carbon dioxide aseptic condition, culturing the umbilical cord blood mononuclear cells with culture medium for 4-6 days, removing un-adhered cells and changing the culture medium for every 1-2 days during directed differentiation; (iv) continuing to culture for 2-6 days to obtain primary culture, replacing the culture medium once in 1-2 days during cultivation; (v) digesting the resultant primary culture at 35-39 degrees C under 3-7% carbon dioxide condition, passaging culture medium under aseptic condition and culturing hematopoietic stem cells for 4-5 passages; and (vi) counting cell number and cell viability, mixing the cells and the complex electrolyte solution according to the counting results, subjecting the samples to endotoxin and bacterial tests, then storing at low temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The culture solution includes: MEL/F12 medium, adenylyl cyclase activator (both in a volume ratio of 1:0.8-1.2), serum as substitute, L-ascorbic acid, L-titanium compound derivative, anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) inhibitor and glycogen synthase kinase-3 (GSK-3) inhibitor. The separation method is medical lymphatic separation fluid or Picoll-paque density gradient separation. The cord blood is obtained by collecting umbilical cords from healthy full-term maternal volunteers, filtering and separating by screening method.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PT P
PN CN107956112-A
TI Preparing degradable modified fiber comprises e.g. taking plant ash and sodium hydroxide, adding into water, stirring uniformly and boiling, adding polycaprolactone to boiled mixture and blending carbon dioxide/ethylene oxide copolymer.
AU HU X
   YU J
   HU Y
AE WUXI HAOYU ENERGY CONSERVATION & ENVIRON (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2018329990
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing degradable modified fiber comprises e.g. taking plant ash and sodium hydroxide, adding into water, stirring uniformly and boiling, adding polycaprolactone to the boiled mixture, blending carbon dioxide/ethylene oxide copolymer into a high-mixer unit and mixing, adding calcium stearate and polyhexene wax and mixing uniformly, adding ramie fiber into mixed solution, immersing completely and cooking, washing cooked ramie fiber with distilled water, drying naturally at room temperature to obtain preliminary treatment of ramie fiber and taking polyethylene glycol and phthalic anhydride.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing degradable modified fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates the tingling sensation when the ramie fiber contacts the human body, improves the fineness and the elongation at break of the ramie fiber and maintains the antibacterial and antibacterial effect of the natural ramie fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing degradable modified fiber comprises (i) taking 30-35 pts. wt. plant ash and 2-4 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, adding into 150-180 pts. wt. water, stirring uniformly and boiling; (ii) adding 10-15 pts. wt. polycaprolactone to the boiled mixture, blending 4-6 pts. wt. carbon dioxide/ethylene oxide copolymer into a high-mixer unit at 100-120 degrees C and mixing for 10-15 minutes, adding 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. calcium stearate and 1-2 pts. wt. polyhexene wax and mixing uniformly; (iii) adding 35-46 pts. wt. ramie fiber into the mixed solution prepared in step (ii), immersing completely and cooking for 3-5 hours; (iv) washing the cooked ramie fiber with distilled water, dry naturally at room temperature to obtain a preliminary treatment of ramie fiber; and (v) taking 5-10 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol and 2-4 pts. wt. phthalic anhydride, dissolving in 80-90 pts. wt. water, stirring uniformly when the temperature reaches to 60-75 degrees C, adding the initially treated ramie fiber, magnetically stirring for 30-60 minutes, taking out and drying naturally at room temperature, and further drying in an oven.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), adding 32 pts. wt. plant ash and 3 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, adding into 160 pts. wt. water, stirring uniformly and boiling. In the step (ii), adding 12 pts. wt. polycaprolactone to the boiled mixture, blending 5 pts. wt. carbon dioxide/ethylene oxide copolymer into a high-mixer unit at 110 degrees C and mixing for 13 minutes, adding 0.3 pts. wt. calcium stearate and 1.5 pts. wt. polyhexene wax and mixing uniformly. In the step (iii), mixing 40 pts. wt. ramie fiber in mixed solution, immersing completely and cooking for 4 hours. In the step (v), taking 8 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol and 3 pts. wt. phthalic anhydride, dissolving in 85 pts. wt. water, stirring uniformly when the temperature reaches to 70 degrees C, adding the initially treated ramie fiber and magnetically stirring for 45 minutes.
DC A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)

PT P
PN EP3275948-A1; WO2018019710-A1; TW201811674-A; AR109150-A1; CN109689797-A; EP3487939-A1; US2019270890-A1; JP2019527666-W; US10876004-B2; CN109689797-B; JP6915041-B2; EP3487939-B1; ES2907584-T3
TI Producing surface-reacted calcium carbonate used in e.g. polymer applications, comprises providing aqueous suspension of calcium carbonate-comprising material, phosphoric acid and dicarboxylic acid, and adding phosphoric acid to material.
AU GERARD D E
   GANE P A C
   SCHOELKOPF J
   GANE P A
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 201808708H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a surface-reacted calcium carbonate, comprises: (a) providing an aqueous suspension of calcium carbonate-comprising material; (b) providing phosphoric acid; (c) providing at least one dicarboxylic acid (I); and (d) adding the phosphoric acid to the calcium carbonate-comprising material over a phosphoric acid addition time period in the presence of carbon dioxide to form an aqueous suspension of surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
   USE - The process is useful for producing a surface-reacted calcium carbonate which is used in: polymer applications, paper coating applications, paper making, paints, coatings, sealants, printing inks, adhesives, food, feed, pharmaceuticals, concrete, cement, cosmetics, water treatment, engineered wood applications, plasterboard applications, packaging applications and/or agricultural applications; and an article including paper products, engineered wood products, plasterboard products, polymer products, hygiene products, medical products, healthcare products, filter products, woven materials, nonwoven materials, geotextile products, agriculture products, horticulture products, clothing, footwear products, baggage products, household products, industrial products, packaging products, building products or construction products (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides the surface-reacted calcium carbonate, which has a specific surface area (Brunauer-Emmett-Teller) of 55-200 (preferably 100-170) m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller method (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a surface-reacted calcium carbonate, comprises: (a) providing an aqueous suspension of calcium carbonate-comprising material; (b) providing phosphoric acid; (c) providing at least one dicarboxylic acid of formula (I) or its salt; and (d) adding the phosphoric acid to the calcium carbonate-comprising material over a phosphoric acid addition time period in the presence of carbon dioxide to form an aqueous suspension of surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The carbon dioxide is formed in-situ by the addition of phosphoric acid and/or is supplied from an external source, and the at least one dicarboxylic acid is added in the step (d) during the phosphoric acid addition time period.
   n = 1-3;
   X = -CH2-, -CHR1a-, -CR1aR1b or -CR1a=CR1b(cis)-;
   R1a, R1b = H, OH (both preferred), OR1c, -CH3, -CH2CH3, -CH2CH2CH3, -CH(CH3)2, tertiary-butyl, secondary-butyl, or n-butyl; and
   R1c = Li, Na or K.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the surface-reacted calcium carbonate obtained by the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The dicarboxylic acid is: added between the beginning of the phosphoric acid addition time period at to and before 60% of the phosphoric acid addition time period has elapsed, preferably between the beginning of the phosphoric acid addition time period at to and before 10% of the phosphoric acid addition time period has elapsed; and added over a dicarboxylic acid addition time period is 1-20 (preferably 3-10)% of the total phosphoric acid addition time period. The step (d) is carried out at a temperature of 20-90 degrees C, preferably 60-70 degrees C. The additional dicarboxylic acid is added during the step (d), preferably together with the dicarboxylic acid. The process further comprises (e) separating the surface-reacted calcium carbonate from the aqueous suspension, and optionally (f) drying the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate-comprising material is: a natural ground calcium carbonate and/or a precipitated calcium carbonate, preferably the natural ground calcium carbonate is marble, chalk and/or limestone, and/or the precipitated calcium carbonate is precipitated calcium carbonates having an aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal form; and in form of particles having a weight median particle size d50(wt) of 0.05-10 (preferably 0.6-1.2) mu m, and/or a weight top cut particle size d98(wt) of 0.15-55 (preferably 3-7) mu m. The phosphoric acid is provided in a concentration of 1-60 (preferably 20-50) wt.%, based on the total weight of the calcium carbonate-comprising material. The surface-reacted calcium carbonate comprises a weight ratio of hydroxylapatite to calcite of 1:99 to 99:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dicarboxylic acid: comprises malic acid, tartaric acid (both preferres), maleic acid and/or malonic acid which is optionally substituted with at least one methyl and/or ethyl; is provided in a concentration of 0.1-50 (preferably 1-5) wt.%, based on the total weight of the calcium carbonate-comprising material; and is provided in a concentration of 1-25 (preferably 9-11) wt.%, based on the total weight of the phosphoric acid. The phosphoric acid and the at least one weak carboxylic acid or its salt are added together in the step (d) in form of mixture, or the phosphoric acid and the weak carboxylic acid are added in the step (d) simultaneously in form of separate streams.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); ; F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

PT P
PN CN113930549-A; CN216337724-U
TI System for cleaning, comprises ash juice heater, hot main ash bucket, carbon-filled tank, filter press, and pre-mixer provided with juice inlet, lime milk inlet and juice outlet which is connected with upper inlet of cold main ash bucket.
AU LI S
   LI Y
   HAN J
   ZHENG X
AE HULUNBUIR SHENGTONG SUGAR TECHNOLOGY CO (HULU-Non-standard)
GA 202218070H
AB    NOVELTY - A cleaning system for sugar beet processing comprises a pre-mixer (100), a cold main ash bucket (200), an ash juice heater (300), a hot main ash bucket (400), a carbon-filled tank (500) and a filter press (600). The pre-mixer is provided with a juice inlet (101), a lime milk inlet (104) and a juice outlet (103). The juice outlet of the pre-mixer is connected with an upper inlet of the cold main ash bucket through a pipeline (107) which is provided with an automatic ash adding device (120). The bottom outlet of the cold main ash bucket is connected with a tube side inlet of the ash juice heater through a pipeline (207). A tube side outlet of the ash juice heater is connected with an upper inlet of the hot main ash bucket through a pipeline (301) which is provided with an automatic ash adding device (302). The upper outlet of the hot main ash bucket is connected with an upper inlet of the carbon-filled tank through a pipeline (406).
   USE - System for cleaning (claimed) for sugar beet processing.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively reduces sugar loss in cleaning steps.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cleaning system for sugar beet processing comprises a pre-mixer (100), a cold main ash bucket (200), an ash juice heater (300), a hot main ash bucket (400), a carbon-filled tank (500) and a filter press (600). The pre-mixer is provided with a juice inlet (101), a lime milk inlet (104) and a juice outlet (103). The juice outlet of the pre-mixer is connected with an upper inlet of the cold main ash bucket through a pipeline (107) which is provided with an automatic ash adding device (120). The bottom outlet of the cold main ash bucket is connected with a tube side inlet of the ash juice heater through a pipeline (207). A tube side outlet of the ash juice heater is connected with an upper inlet of the hot main ash bucket through a pipeline (301) which is provided with an automatic ash adding device (302). The upper outlet of the hot main ash bucket is connected with an upper inlet of the carbon-filled tank through a pipeline (406). The lower outlet of the carbon-filled tank is connected with the filter press through a pipeline (602). The bottom outlet of the carbon-filled tank is connected with a reflux pipeline (508) whose tail end is connected with a middle inlet of the carbon-filled tank. The reflux pipeline is provided with a pump unit (208). The bottom outlet of the hot main ash bucket is connected with the reflux pipeline through a pipeline (407).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cleaning method for sugar beet processing, which involves feeding sugar juice containing organic substance and other non-sugar impurities at 50-55degrees Celsius into the pre-mixer through a juice inlet at the upper portion of the front end of gradual pre-mixer, uniformly dividing the pre-mixer into chambers along an axial direction by a clapboard (102), allowing the sugar juice to continuously enter the pre-mixer from the juice inlet, flowing backward through a gap (109) between the clapboard and the bottom surface of tank, and simultaneously adding lime milk from the lime milk inlet at the lower portion of the rear end of the pre-mixer. A movable baffle (119) hinged at the upper end of the clapboard is opened at an angle forward. A rotating stirrer (118) continuously throws the material in the chamber through the gap between the baffle and the clapboard into a front chamber. Each chamber is provided with materials having different lime concentration to reflow to the front chamber to reach cumulative pre-ash so that pH of materials in different chambers gradually increases from front to back. To mix the sugar juice and lime milk uniformly, 1.5-3 wt.% lime with respect to raw sugar beet is added. After adding water, the lime emulsifies into lime milk, and 10 wt.% lime milk is added in the pre-mixer. The pH of the pre-lime juice output from the juice outlet of the pre-mixer is 10.5-11.5. The pre-ash juice overflows from the juice outlet at the upper portion of the rear end of the pre-mixer into the pipeline (107). The lime milk is added into the pipeline (107) through the automatic ash adding device (120). The pre-ash juice and the added lime milk enter into the cold main ash bucket through the pipeline (107), stirred in the cold main ash bucket to uniformly mix the material, and decomposed to convert into non-sugar content. The automatic ash adding device (120) is added with 40 wt.% lime milk with respect to total weight of clean lime milk. The pH value of the material in the cold main ash bucket reaches low temperature and high alkalinity state of 13-14. The cold main ash juice and lime sludge output from the bottom outlet of the cold main ash bucket enter the ash juice heater through the pipeline (207) and heated at 80-90degrees Celsius to enter into the pipeline (301). The pipeline (301) is added with the lime milk by the automatic ash adding device (302). The automatic ash adding device (302) is added with 50 wt.% lime milk with respect to total weight of the clean lime milk, and enters the hot main ash bucket. In the hot main ash bucket, the material is stirred by hot ash stirring shaft to fully mix the material. In the hot main ash bucket, the pH value of the material reaches a high temperature and high alkali state of 15-16 so that the non-sugar impurities are decomposed in a high temperature and high alkali environment. The hot main ash juice after mixing through the upper outlet of the hot main ash bucket overflow into the pipeline (406), and enters the carbon-filled tank through the pipeline (406). The bottom material in the carbon-filled tank continuously passes through the bottom outlet of the carbon-filled tank, the reflux pipeline and a feed pipe (504) of the carbon-filled tank. The slag slurry at the bottom of the hot main ash bucket passes through the pipeline (407) and the reflux pipeline, and enters the feed pipe into the carbon-filled tank. The slag slurry containing 13% water content at the bottom of the hot main ash bucket is discharged into the carbon-filled tank through the pipeline (407) and the reflux pipeline to recover the slag slurry sugar without loss of sugar. The carbon dioxide gas from the bottom of the saturated tank is introduced through an air inlet pipe (507). The carbon dioxide gas moves up in countercurrent upward movement by fully contacting with the material. The multilayer buffer baffle of the saturated tank blocks the upward movement of carbon dioxide gas, prolongs the residence time of carbon dioxide, and makes the carbon dioxide gas fully react with the material to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate. The output from the carbon-filled tank enters the filter press through the lower outlet of the tank, and the pipeline (602) into the filter. The calcium carbonate precipitate and other non-sugar impurities attached to the precipitate are filtered to obtain a clear juice.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of whole structure of cleaning system.
   Pre-mixer (100)
   Juice inlet (101)
   Clapboard (102)
   Juice outlet (103)
   Lime milk inlet (104)
   Pipelines (107,207,301,406,407,602)
   Gap (109)
   Rotating stirrer (118)
   Movable baffle (119)
   Automatic dust adding device (120,302)
   Cold main ash bucket (200)
   Pump unit (208)
   Ash juice heater (300)
   Hot main ash bucket (400)
   Carbon-filled tank (500)
   Feed pipe (504)
   Air inlet pipe (507)
   Reflux pipeline (508)
   Filter press (600)
DC D17 (Sugar and starch industry (C07H, C13).)

PN CN109499623-A
TI Coking treatment of acrylic acid oxidation catalyst comprises heating and odor removing stage, odor removing constant stage, carbon removing stage, and ash removing stage.
AU ZHENG Z
   CHEN H
   LI J
   GAO Y
   JIANG S
   ZHENG X
AE SHENYANG PARAFFIN CHEM CO LTD (CNCC-C)
GA 201929207H
AB    NOVELTY - A coking treatment of acrylic acid oxidation catalyst comprises (A) heating and odor removing stage, (B) odor removing constant stage, (C) carbon removing stage, and (D) ash removing stage. The step (C) comprises continuing to raise temperature inside the reactor after ending odor removing, heating with rate of 2-3 degrees C/hour; monitoring content of carbon dioxide and oxygen in theexhaust gas, if carbon dioxide gas content exceeds 0.3%, or the oxygen content is less than 11%, stopping heating, until the carbon dioxide gas content does not exceed 0.3%, when the oxygen content is not less than 11%; and when the temperature is raised to 360 degrees C, keeping temperature constant until the carbon dioxide gas content is 0.038%. The step (D) comprises after removing carbon, keeping constant temperature at 360 degrees C, increasing introduced air amount to 5000 m3/hour, continuing for 24 hours, when carbon dioxide content in exhaust gas is 0.038%, and when the oxygen reaches 21%, ending charring.
   USE - Method for coking treatment of acrylic acid oxidation catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of removing cokes attached to the catalyst by charring, restores activity of atalyst, and prolonges service life of the catalyst. It also saves energy and has low labor cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A coking treatment of acrylic acid oxidation catalyst comprises (A) heating and odor removing stage, (B) odor removing constant stage, (C) carbon removing stage, and (D) ash removing stage. The step (A) comprises heating reactor to 260-300 degrees C with speed of 5 degrees C/hour; and supplying air at rate of 200m3/hour, monitoring combustible gas content and oxygen content in exhaust gas, and measuring once every hour, when the combustible gas exceeds 0.5% or the oxygen content is less than 11%, stopping heating. The step (B) comprises controlling temperature of reactor to not more than 300 degrees C, continuing to introduce air into reactor with gas supply rate of 200 m3/hour, and maintaining at least 5 hours; and monitoring combustible gas content and the oxygen content in the exhaust gas, and measuringonce every hour, when the combustible gas content exceeds 0.5% or the oxygen content is less than 11%, stopping heating; when combustible gas content is 0, ending odor removing. The step (C) comprises continuing to raise temperature inside the reactor after ending odor removing, heating with rate of 2-3 degrees C/hour; monitoring content of carbon dioxide and oxygen in theexhaust gas, if carbon dioxide gas content exceeds 0.3%, or the oxygen content is less than 11%, stopping heating, until the carbon dioxide gas content does not exceed 0.3%, when the oxygen content is not less than 11%, continuing to raise the temperature; and when the temperature is raised to 360 degrees C, keeping temperature constant until the carbon dioxide gas content is 0.038%, finishing the carbon removing. The step (D) comprises after removing carbon, continuously keeping constant temperature at 360 degrees C, increasing introduced air amount to 5000 m3/hour, continuing for 24 hours, when carbon dioxide content in exhaust gas is 0.038%, and when the oxygen reaches 21%, ending charring.
