PT P
PN US2018320481-A1
TI Sequestering system for preventing carbon dioxide from being released into ambient air, has fixed emission source that is attached to pipeline that pumps emissions from fixed emission source into injection well.
AU RICHARDS S B
AE RICHARDS S B (RICH-Individual)
GA 2018869126
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an injection well (52) that is located over a depleted hydrocarbon reservoir (18). A fixed emission source is attached to a pipeline that pumps emissions from the fixed emission source into the injection well which pumps the emissions into the void. A lime kiln (12) is attached to a calcium carbonate source, air source and coal, oil or natural gas source for producing carbon dioxide discharge, lime discharge and water discharge (36). Te carbon dioxide discharged is trapped by the injection into the void. A distillery (38) produces a second carbon dioxide discharge, nitrogen stock discharge and an oxygen discharge. A injection well is connected to second carbon dioxide discharge, and is configured to pump the carbon dioxide discharge into the non depleted reservoir for enhanced oil recovery (EOR). A carbonator is configured to receive lime solely from the discharge, to remove the carbon dioxide from the emission source.
   USE - Sequestering system for preventing carbon dioxide from being released into ambient air.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is removed from tailpipes and smokestacks by not discharging it into the atmosphere by using the lime to react to form a carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system has a carbonator that is provided with settlement chamber surrounding the reaction chamber, where calcium carbonate slurry settles and drains into a calcium carbonate slurry tank. A water pipe is attached to the calcium carbonate slurry tank and is configured to direct water through the calcium carbonate slurry tank driving the calcium carbonate slurry into a calcium carbonate slurry output pipe. The depleted reservoir is provided with Permian basin and an Anadarko basins, where high voltage direct current transmission line is located in one of the set consisting of a right of way of a Texas Eastern natural gas pipeline and a Tennessee natural gas pipelines, and high voltage direct current transmission line is further located in one of the set consisting of a Transco natural gas pipeline and Colonial pipeline. The coal fired power generation station is provided for receiving coal from Tulsa at a terminus of a navigable waterway system. A paddle wheel inside the lime input shoot is connected to an electric motor. An acidity sensor is attached to the reaction chamber and is communicatively coupled to the electric motor to control the lime released through the lime input shoot into the reaction chamber. A pressurized storage container is connected to the reaction chamber. A tail pipe emission source is connected to the pressurized storage container with an air pump, where emissions are pumped from the tail pipe emission source into the pressurized storage container and are then released into the reaction chamber. A gas recycling tube is connected to the reaction chamber and an automatic valve is configured to permit and restrict access to the gas recycling tube. A water holding tank is connected to the water pipe and to the settlement chamber with a water discharge tube and the reaction chamber with a water intake tube, where a flow of water in and out of the water holding tank affects a level of water in the reaction chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the sequestering system for preventing carbon dioxide from being released into ambient air.
   Lime kiln (12)
   Depleted hydrocarbon reservoir (18)
   Water discharge (36)
   Distillery (38)
   Injection well (52)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H03 (Transportation and storage - only large scale systems are included. In the utility model,                generates carbon dioxide that is injected, without                liquefied storage, therefore, not at the                corresponding danger caused by using carbon dioxide                gas, that is to say without monitoring, so as to                leave out the monitoring instrument, reach the                purpose of electric welding device with simplified                structure, the external welding device electricity                from the fuel cell, sufficient self-welding, also                can work without external power supply, use                limitation of electric welding device is                reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using a protective gas self-generating welding                apparatus, which involves:
   (A) reforming a hydrogen production unit with                methanol water to carry out a reforming reaction to                generate hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide,                generating generated hydrogen gas into a fuel cell                for power generation, and transporting the                generated carbon dioxide to the gas spraying                portion; and
   (B) carrying out welding, where electric                welding head takes power from fuel cell,                irradiating arc to welded portion of the welding                object, and using gas spraying portion for spraying                carbon dioxide to the welded portion.

The gas                supply device is a methanol water reforming                hydrogen production device (1), and is used to                reform methanol water to produce hydrogen and                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Protective gas self-generating welding                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The protective gas self-generating welding                device eliminates requirement of monitoring                instrument, simplifies structure of electric                welding head, is operated without external power                supply, and reduces use limitation of electric                welding device.

The demineralized water is used to purify the                  methanol in mixed gas. The carbon molecular sieve                  is used to separate hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  in mixed gas. The generated gaseous carbon                  dioxide is pressurized and sent to gas spraying                  portion.

TI Preparing corrosion inhibitor resistant to carbon dioxide corrosion useful for delaying corrosion of oil-water transmission pipeline, comprises prepolymerizing monomer and N-vinylpyrrolidone, adding alkynyl to polymerize, and adding antimony trichloride and mixing.
AU WU Y
   ZHANG X
   GAO R
   GUO Y
   SONG X
   WANG L
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 202203618P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing corrosion inhibitor resistant to carbon dioxide corrosion comprises prepolymerizing first monomer and N-vinylpyrrolidone to obtain first intermediate, reacting the first intermediate, the second monomer and the third monomer to obtain second intermediate, adding alkynol into the second intermediate to polymerize, adding antimony trichloride and mixing, where, the first monomer is allyl polyether and/or bis allyl polyether, the second monomer is N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, acrylamide and/or N-(methylol)acrylamide, and the third monomer is formaldehyde, trioxymethylene and/or paraformaldehyde.
   USE - The corrosion inhibitor resistant to carbon dioxide is useful for delaying corrosion of oil-water transmission pipeline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The corrosion inhibitor can react with carbon dioxide, reduces the generation of acid solution, and can be adsorbed on the metal surface to form a protective film, prevents the acid from contacting the metal, which reduces the corrosion of the metal, has stable high temperature performance and can play a good corrosion inhibition effect in the bottom hole with high temperature. The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a corrosion inhibitor resistant to carbon dioxide, prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The weight ratio of the first monomer and N-vinylpyrrolidone is 1:1-10, the weight ratio of the first intermediate, the second monomer and the third monomer is 5-15:10-50:5-10, and/or the weight ratio of the second intermediate, alkynol and antimony trichloride is 10-80:5-10:1-5. The first monomer is mixture of allyl polyether and bis allyl polyether in weight ratio of 1:4-9. The allyl polyether is allyl polyoxyethylene methyl ether, allyl polyoxyethylene propyl ether, allyl polyoxyethylene propyl ether, allyl polyoxyethylene epoxy ether, allyl polyoxyethylene acetate and/or allyl polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene acetate. The average molecular weight of the bis allyl polyether is 4000-10000. The second monomer is a mixture of N,N-methylenebis acrylamide and acrylamide in a weight ratio of 5:2.Preferred Method: The method comprises dissolving first monomer, N-vinylpyrrolidone and initiator into the first solvent, uniformly stirring, introducing nitrogen, heating to 50-75degrees Celsius, and reacting for 1-5 hours to obtain the first intermediate, where the total monomer content is 30-60 wt.%, the initiator content is 0.2-1 wt.%, and the first solvent (remaining amount), and the first solvent is hexane, benzene, toluene, xylene, diethyl ether and/or acetone; dissolving the first intermediate, the second monomer, the third monomer and the catalyst into the second solvent, uniformly stirring, adjusting the pH to 2-3, heating to 80-90degrees Celsius, and reacting for 5-7 hours to obtain the second intermediate, where the total monomer content is 40-55 wt.%, the catalyst content is 0.2-1 wt.%, and the second solvent (remaining amount), and the second solvent is deionized water, methanol, and/or absolute ethanol; adding alkynol into the second intermediate, polymerizing at 80-90degrees Celsius for at least 3 hours, cooling to 40-50degrees Celsius, adding antimony trichloride and mixing for 0.5-1 hour to obtain the corrosion inhibitor.

TI Method for fully recycling carbon dioxide discharged by catalytic cracking regenerating device, involves using air to support combustion in initial stage, and circulating flue gas by catalytic cracking regenerating device.
AU WU W
   WEI T
   ZHANG X
   LIU Q
   LI X
   YAN L
   ZHANG Y
AE SHANGHAI YUANHAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP PETROCHEM RES (CNPC-C)
   LUOYANG RONGHUI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LUOY-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9668F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using air to support combustion in an initial stage. An air combustion-supporting is replaced. Flue gas is circulated from a catalytic cracking regeneration device. Mixing oxygen and circulating flue gas in a carbon-based gas mixer is transmitted to prepare carbon-based gas. Prepared carbon-based gas is used as a combustion improver. Combustion-supporting gas for little carbon monoxide is provided in a catalytic cracking regenerating device. The flue gas is circulated by the catalytic cracking regenerating device. A peripheral wall of a hollow cylinder is uniformly provided with multiple small holes.
   USE - Method for fully recycling carbon dioxide discharged by a catalytic cracking regenerating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables circulating the catalytic cracking regeneration device flue gas, improving the carbon dioxide concentration, creating favorable conditions for low-cost carbon dioxide capture, reducing the emission of carbon dioxide and reducing greenhouse effect, avoiding generation of nitrogen oxide in the combustion process, and reducing de-nitration cost. The method enables enhancing the combustion effect, reaching obvious effect of saving energy and reducing consumption, prolonging the service life of the catalyst, reducing the consumption of catalyst, and improving product quality.

TI Production of synthetic fuel involves decomposing water into oxygen and hydrogen, generating biogas comprising methane and carbon dioxide, generating electricity in gas combustion plant using biogas, treating exhaust gas leaving plant, and producing fuel using carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU SCHORN F
   SAMSUN R C
   LOHSE D
   PETERS R
   CAN SAMSUN R
AE FORSCHUNGSZENTRUM JUELICH GMBH (KERJ-C)
GA 2021B4982P
AB    NOVELTY - Production of synthetic fuel involves (a) decomposing water into oxygen and hydrogen using an electrolyzer (2), (b) generating a biogas comprising methane and carbon dioxide using a biogas plant (6), (c) generating electricity and/or heat in a gas combustion plant (8) using the biogas and oxygen obtained from electrolysis, (d) treating exhaust gas containing water and carbon dioxide leaving the gas combustion plant in such a way water is partially withdrawn by condensation, and (e) producing synthetic fuel in a fuel synthesis system (15) using carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas of the gas combustion system and hydrogen from electrolysis.
   USE - Production of synthetic fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and environmentally-friendly production of synthetic fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system (1), which comprises at least one electrolyzer, at least one biogas system, at least one gas combustion system and at least one fuel synthesis system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
   System (1)
   Electrolyzer (2)
   Biogas plant (6)
   Gas combustion plant (8)
   Fuel synthesis system (15)
   Transport container (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The gas combustion system is a block-type thermal power station. The fuel synthesis system is a methanol synthesis system. The design of the electrolyzer, the biogas system, the gas combustion system and the fuel synthesis system are coordinated in such a way that the gas combustion system and the fuel synthesis system operate continuously.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (c) is performed using cooled carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas to regulate combustion temperature. In the process (c), heat is partially used to operate biogas plant. The synthetic fuel is filled into a transport container (16) at the fuel synthesis system.

PN CN112934185-A; CN112934185-B
TI Preparing organic acid hydrophobic modified            biological carbon based on supercritical carbon dioxide            useful for adsorbing volatile organic compounds,            comprises heating dried biological carbon in            supercritical carbon dioxide medium and drying.
AU HUANG H
   WANG X
   YIN L
   CHENG H
   YE G
   WANG Y
   JIAO Y
   YAO F
   ZHU W
   YE D
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202170823J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic acid hydrophobic modified                biological carbon based on supercritical carbon                dioxide medium comprises (i) mixing biological                carbon and hydrogen peroxide solution, stirring and                filtering to obtain hydroxylated biological carbon;                (ii) mixing hydroxylated biological carbon sodium                borohydride, and absolute ethanol, stirring,                filtering, and drying in an inert gas atmosphere at                50-100 degrees C to obtain dried biological carbon;                (iii) adding dried biological carbon into a                solution dissolved with natural macromolecular                organic acids, anhydrous ethanol, and dehydrating                agent, and heating and reacting in a supercritical                carbon dioxide medium to obtain a reacted mixed                liquid; and (iv) filtering the mixed solution,                washing the precipitate with ethanol and deionized                water, and drying at 50-100 degrees C in an inert                gas atmosphere to obtain modified biological                carbon.
   USE - The biological carbon is useful for adsorbing                volatile organic compounds under environment of                relative humidity more than 70% (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biological carbon has simple operation,                little influence on the pore structure of the                carbon material, and good adsorption performance                for volatile organic compounds in a high-humidity                environment, and is economical and environment                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: a                organic acid hydrophobic modified biological carbon                based on supercritical carbon dioxide medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  biological carbon is coconut shell biological                  carbon, bagasse biological carbon and/or walnut                  shell biological carbon, and amount of hydrogen                  peroxide solution is 5-30 wt.%. In the step (ii),                  the mass ratio of hydroxylated biological carbon                  and sodium borohydride is 10:1-1:1, and the ratio                  of the volume of absolute ethanol and the mass of                  hydroxylated biological carbon is 1:1-1:50 l/g.                  In the step (iii), the natural macromolecular                  organic acid is cinnamic acid, caffeic acid,                  myristic acid and/or lauric acid, the mass ratio                  of dried biological carbon and natural                  macromolecular organic acid is 10:1-1:1, the mass                  ratio of the volume of absolute ethanol and the                  mass of natural macromolecular organic acid is                  1:1-500 l/g, and the amount of the dehydrating                  agent is 10-50 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the                  steps (i) and (ii), the stirring time is 1-6                  hours. In the step (iii), the pressure of the                  supercritical carbon dioxide is 7-10 Mpa, the                  temperature of the reaction is 30-80 degrees C,                  and the time of the reaction is 0.5-10                  hours.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111701586-A; CN111701586-B
TI Construction method of Pickering microbubble system used for preparing methanol by modifying surface of carrier material, loading photocatalyst, adding to closed reactor containing deionized water, and filling with carbon dioxide gas.
AU YANG H
   LIU X
   XUE N
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202096899W
AB    NOVELTY - A construction method of Pickering microbubble system comprises modifying surface of carrier material with hydrophilic or hydrophobic groups to obtain amphiphilic catalyst carrier, loading photocatalyst using chemical or physical method to obtain amphiphilic Pickering photocatalyst, adding to closed light-transmitting reactor containing deionized water, filling with carbon dioxide gas while keeping system pressure, and stirring.
   USE - The method is for construction of Pickering microbubble system used for preparing methanol by photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system has reduction efficiency increased by 10-80% compared with traditional gas-liquid two-phase reaction, and selectivity of reduction product methanol increased by 20-80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the preparation of the methanol comprising increasing contact area of the reactant carbon dioxide and water with solid photocatalyst using large gas-liquid-solid three-phase interface of microbubbles, reducing activation energy required for photocatalytic reaction to improve the efficiency of carbon dioxide reduction, suppressing by-product methane production using microbubble confinement effect, and converting by-product methane into methanol; and suppressing side reaction of hydrogen evolution, and converting hydrogen produced into water for recycling and reuse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier material is porous silica microsphere, porous carbon material, graphene, graphyne, metal organic framework material and/or zeolite molecular sieve. The photocatalyst is titanium dioxide, carbon nitride, silver phosphate, bismuth oxychloride, molybdenum sulfide, silver, gold, copper, platinum and/or palladium. Preferred Method: The chemical method is sol-gel method, photoreduction method, hydrothermal method, chemical reduction method and/or chemical bonding. The physical method is electrostatic self-assembly method and/or adsorption method.

PN US2020299586-A1; WO2020191407-A1
TI Forming high molecular weight coal tar involves combining supercritical carbon dioxide and an amount of coal tar, and fractionating the amount of coal tar to form the high molecular weight coal tar.
AU ATKINS C A
   TARGETT M
AE CARBON HOLDINGS INTELLECTUAL PROPERTIES (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON HOLDINGS INTELLECTUAL PROPERTIES (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202094548L
AB    NOVELTY - Forming high molecular weight coal tar involves combining supercritical carbon dioxide and an amount of coal tar, and fractionating the amount of coal tar to form the high molecular weight coal tar.
   USE - Method for forming high molecular weight coal tar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to form high molecular weight coal tar with high mechanical strength and high modulus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for processing coal tar, which involves providing an amount of coal tar and converting at least some of the amount of coal tar into mesophase pitch using supercritical carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a system, which comprises a high molecular weight coal tar formation apparatus comprising a coal tar input and configured to form HMW coal tar from coal tar using supercritical carbon dioxide and a co-solvent; a mesophase pitch formation apparatus in communication with the HMW coal tar formation apparatus and configured to form mesophase pitch from HMW coal tar using carbon dioxide source in communication with the HMW coal tar formation apparatus and the mesophase pitch formation apparatus and co-solvent source in communication with the HMW coal tar formation apparatus to provide a co-solvent to the HMW coal tar formation apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: the method further involves forming the amount of coal tar from coal, forming the amount of coal tar from coal comprises extracting the coal tar from an amount of coal using cargbon dioxide, adding toluene to the coal tar, fractionating the amount of coal tar to form HMW coal tar comprises collecting a toluene insoluble fraction, fractionating the amount of coal tar to form HMW coal tar comprises rinsing the toluene insoluble fraction in toluene, adding quinolone to the coal tar. Preferred Process: Fractionating the amount of coal tar to form HMW coal tar comprises heating the coal tar to a temperature of between about 32 degrees C and about 700 degrees C and pressurizing the amount of coal tar to a pressure of between about 7.5 MPa and about 16 MPa.

PT P
PN DE102017010788-A1; CN109809963-A; EP3521267-A1; IN201844043370-A; ZA201807845-A; RU2018140857-A; EP3521267-B1; ES2856258-T3; IN407479-B
TI Production of dimethyl ether used as e.g. coolant,            involves reacting feedstock in reaction unit containing            hydrogen and carbon monoxide, separating resultant            crude product containing dimethyl ether, and recycling            obtained product.
AU PESCHEL A
   ANDREAS P
   PESHEL A
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 201944884P
AB    NOVELTY - Production of dimethyl ether involves reacting                feedstock in a reaction unit containing hydrogen                and carbon monoxide to obtain crude product                containing dimethyl ether, water, methanol, carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen, subjecting                crude product or its portion to separation process                in a separation unit, in which major portion of                dimethyl ether, water and methanol, and minor                portion of carbon monoxide and hydrogen are                separated off, obtaining intermediate mixture which                is poor or free of dimethyl ether, water and                methanol, separating intermediate mixture or its                portion, in which major portion of carbon dioxide                is separated, obtaining carbon dioxide fraction,                and carbon dioxide-poor- or free radical mixture                containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and                recycling radical mixture or portion of radical                mixture in the reaction unit, such that molar ratio                of hydrogen and carbon monoxide in feedstock is                less than 2, and feedstock contains 2-10 mol%                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of dimethyl ether used as coolant                and substitute for fuel gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of                dimethyl ether on large-scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production apparatus of dimethyl ether, which                comprises reaction unit and separation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The intermediate                  mixture contains 95 mol% carbon dioxide, less                  than 1 mol% dimethyl ether, and less than 1 mol%                  water and methanol, in total. The molar ratio of                  hydrogen and carbon monoxide in fresh feedstock                  is 1-1.8. The carbon dioxide fraction contains 99                  mol% or more carbon dioxide. Preferred Process:                  The intermediate mixture is formed by                  condensation and/or distillation. During                  separation process, major portion or only                  methanol fraction is formed. The production of                  dimethyl ether involves separating major portion                  of carbon dioxide from intermediate mixture or                  its portion using a carbon dioxide separation                  column or by relaxation of intermediate mixture                  or its portion. The separation unit includes                  separation column (C1) comprising bottom liquid                  (b1) enriched in carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide                  and hydrogen, and top gas (g1) containing                  dimethyl ether, water and methanol. The bottom                  liquid (b1) is processed in separation column                  (C2) to obtain bottom liquid (b2) containing                  water and methanol. The bottom liquid (b2) or its                  portion is processed in separation column (C3) to                  obtain bottom liquid (b3) containing water, and                  methanol-containing top gas (g3). The top gas                  (g1) is cooled to form condensate. The condensate                  is processed in separation column (C4) to obtain                  top gas (g4) containing carbon dioxide, carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen, and bottom liquid (b4)                  which is poor or free from dimethyl ether. The                  top gas (g4) is condensed and partly recycled as                  intermediate mixture (from which carbon dioxide                  product is recovered). Preferred Component: The                  carbon dioxide separation column is operated with                  a bottom evaporator and without a top condenser.                  The carbon dioxide separation column contains                  5-20 theoretical plates.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

The second expanded                  synthesis gas partial product stream is added to                  a fuel gas product stream or forms a fuel gas                  product stream. The fourth synthesis gas partial                  product stream is added to a hydrogen product                  stream or forms a hydrogen product stream. The                  recompressor is designed in several stages, the                  gaseous overhead products from the low-pressure                  flash containers of the first stage and the                  gaseous overhead products from the                  medium-pressure flash containers are fed to a                  subsequent stage of the multi-stage                  recompressor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN113915085-A
TI Small fluorine salt cooling high temperature stack            and tower type solar combined power generation system            in energy and renewable energy application field for            developing water energy, comprises nuclear reactor            power generation unit.
AU SU G
   QIU S
   TIAN W
   WANG C
   ZHOU X
   LI X
   JIANG D
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202221126N
AB    NOVELTY - Small fluorine salt cooling high temperature                stack and tower type solar combined power                generation system comprises a nuclear reactor power                generation unit, tower-type solar power generation                unit and heat compensation unit, where the nuclear                reactor power generation unit comprises modularized                reactor (1), two-loop molten salt pump (2), molten                salt tank (3), molten salt tank temperature                monitoring unit (4), molten salt tank temperature                measuring unit (5), flourine lithium sodium                potassium-carbon dioxide heat exchanger (6) and                nuclear reactor-supercritical carbon dioxide                Brayton cycle system (7), the outlet of the modular                reactor is connected with the inlet of the molten                salt tank, the outlet of the molten salt tank is                connected with the inlet of the two-loop molten                salt pump, the outlet of the two-loop molten salt                pump is connected with the inlet of the modular                reactor.
   USE - Small fluorine salt cooling high temperature                stack and tower type solar combined power                generation system used in energy and renewable                energy application field for developing water                energy, geothermal energy, wind energy and solar                energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The small fluorine salt cooling high                temperature reactor and tower type solar combined                power generation system can realize high-efficient                utilization of energy, and can improve the                stability and reliability of the tower-type solar                power generation. The system generating efficiency                is high, the requirement of water is small, and it                can generate electricity in the water shortage                area. The molten salt tank can be used to store                energy, by monitoring the temperature level of                different depths of the molten salt in the                solar-supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle                system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a working                method of small fluorine salt cooling high                temperature stack and tower type solar energy                combined power generation system, involving: a.                using the heliostate field automatically tracking                solar radiation, reflecting the solar energy                irradiated to the heliostat field and concentrating                on the receiver above the receiving tower; b.                heating molten salt in the molten salt flow                pipeline, the molten salt after heating flows                through the shunt valve and the confluence valve                and then enterning the heat side of the potassium                nitrate/sodium nitrate-carbon dioxide heat                exchanger to heat the carbon dioxide in the                solar-supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle                system, potassium nitrate/sodium nitrate-carbon                dioxide heat exchanger cold side of the heated                carbon dioxide in the solar-supercritical carbon                dioxide Brayton cycle system to finish the                circulation, and conveying electric energy to the                external grid; and c. pressurizing fused salt after                heat releasing by the solar energy system molten                salt pump and then enters the molten salt flowing                pipeline in the receiver to be heated by the solar                energy, when the receiver does not receive the heat                from the solar energy, the shunt valve switching                outlet, confluence valve switching inlet, molten                salt flow dividing valve, controlling the flow rate                through the flow control system, heating cold side                of the flourine lithium sodium potassium-potassium                nitrate heat exchanger and then entering the                tower-type solar power generation system through                the confluent valve.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system.
   Modularized reactor (1)
   Two-loop molten salt pump (2)
   Molten salt tank (3)
   Molten salt tank temperature monitoring unit                (4)
   Molten salt tank temperature measuring unit                (5)
   Flourine lithium sodium potassium-carbon                dioxide heat exchanger (6)
   Nuclear reactor-supercritical carbon dioxide                Brayton cycle unit (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modular reactor                  uses flourine lithium berrylium salt as the main                  coolant of the modularized reactor, the mol                  respectively of lithium flouride and beryllium                  flouride is 67 % and 33%, the second loop of the                  two-loop molten salt pump adopts flourine lithium                  sodium potassiumn salt as cooling working medium,                  the mol fraction respectively lithium floride,                  sodium flouride and potassium flouride is 46.5%,                  11.5% and 42%.. The tower-type solar power                  generation system uses mixed salt of nitiric acid                  and sodium nitrate as circulating working medium,                  mass fraction of potassium nitrate and sodium                  nitrate is 40 respectively and 60%.

PN CN112646776-A
TI Extracting autologous adipose-derived stem cells for scar repair, by selecting portion of patients body as fat extraction site, placing in carbon dioxide incubator, culturing, diluting, centrifuging and obtaining supernatant.
AU ZHAN Z
   LI Q
AE SHANGHAI NANBINJIANG CELL BIOTECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202143572F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting autologous adipose-derived stem cells for scar repair, involves selecting portion of the patient's body as the fat extraction site, marking, using local anesthetic swelling solution to treat the site, sucking 50 ml adipose tissue fluid, placing the syringe with adipose tissue fluid at 4-6 degrees C, placing in 500 ml normal saline, transferring the remaining liquid to 50 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 4 degrees C and a speed of 2000-2500 rpm for 8-10 minutes, after centrifugation, obtaining bottom layer, adding initial extract to phosphate buffered saline (PBS) buffer and Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium containing human autologous serum, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, culturing, adding trypsin and EDTA digestion solution, adding DMEM medium containing human autologous serum, diluting the cell density, adding physiological saline after the end of the culture, placing in a high-speed refrigerated centrifuge and obtaining supernatant.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting autologous adipose-derived stem cells for scar repair (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides good differentiation and proliferation ability, which promotes division and renewal of the body's own adipocytes, accelerates repair of facial scars, and maintains cells in a good growth state and vigorous proliferation to satisfy scars.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting autologous adipose-derived stem cells for scar repair, involves selecting portion of the patient's body as the fat extraction site, marking, using local anesthetic swelling solution to treat the site, after the local anesthesia swelling is treated, using 50 ml syringe and a 4 mm liposuction needle to insert under the skin in the direction of the skin incision adipose tissue, sucking 50 ml adipose tissue fluid, placing the syringe with adipose tissue fluid at 4-6 degrees C, placing in 500 ml normal saline, maintaining for 10-15 minutes, draining the bottom layer, transferring the remaining liquid to 50 ml centrifuge tube, placing at the freezing high speed, centrifuging at a temperature of 4 degrees C and a rotation speed of 1000-1200 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, after centrifugation, obtaining upper layer to obtain fat particles, adding fat particles into 50 ml syringe, using dual-channel chyle device to repeatedly push through 3 holes, 5 holes, and 9 holes respectively, after the chylo is broken, adding broken fat particles to the beaker, adding physiological saline and zhengtaimycin, mixing and stirring at a speed of 50 rpm for 3-5 minutes, transferring to 50 ml centrifuge tube, placing in a high-speed centrifuge, centrifuging at 4 degrees C and a speed of 2000-2500 rpm for 8-10 minutes, after centrifugation, obtaining bottom layer, adding initial extract to phosphate buffered saline (PBS) buffer and Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium containing human autologous serum, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, culturing at 30-37 degrees C for 5-7 days to obtain primary cells, after 5-7 days, when the degree of fusion reaches 70-80%, washing for twice with 500 ml PBS buffer, adding trypsin and EDTA digestion solution, adding DMEM medium containing human autologous serum, diluting the cell density by 3-4 times, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 35-37 degrees C for 5-7 days, adding physiological saline after the end of the culture, transferring to 50 ml centrifuge tube, placing in a high-speed refrigerated centrifuge at 4 degrees C, and 1500-2000 rpm for 5-8 minutes, and obtaining supernatant, which is the autologous adipose-derived stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The local anesthetic swelling liquid is prepared by adding 500 ml normal saline into a beaker, adding 30 ml lidocaine with a mass fraction of 2%, 1 mg of epinephrine, and 10 ml sodium bicarbonate, stirring and mixing to obtain a local anesthetic swelling solution. The local anesthesia and swelling treatment involves obtaining No. 11 scalpel blade to cut at 3-4 mm marked position skin incision, using 2.8 mm diameter water injection needle to inject at the marked position until the marked position is swollen and white, and where the skin has the appearance of orange peel. The dosage ratio of fat particles, physiological saline and chintomycin is 20 ml:17-19 ml:1-3 ml, in which the volume ratio of the volume of fat particles to the total volume of saline and chintomycin is 1:1. The volume ratio of the initial extract, PBS buffer and DMEM medium containing human autologous serum is 1:1:5. The concentration of pancreatin is 0.2 g/ml, the mass fraction of the EDTA digestion solution is 0.04%, and the dosage ratio of the pancreatin, EDTA digestion solution, DMEM medium containing human autologous serum, and physiological saline is 1 ml:1 ml:20 ml:40 ml. The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide incubator is 5%. The steps are all under sterile conditions, where the portion of the patient's body includes upper arm, the abdomen and the inner thigh.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN112175903-A
TI Preparation of activated and proliferated            cytotoxic T lymphocytes, by preparing peripheral blood,            collecting mononuclear cells, staining, counting,            resuspending mononuclear cells in AIM-V medium,            incubating and collecting CTL cells.
AU YANG H
AE LANLIAN HANGZHOU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LANL-Non-standard)
GA 202106270D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparation of activated and                proliferated cytotoxic T lymphocytes, involves                preparing 200-400 ml peripheral blood of healthy                people, using leukocyte separation solution to                remove red blood cells, platelets and granulocytes,                collecting mononuclear cells, staining with trypan                blue, counting, resuspending mononuclear cells in                AIM-V medium containing 5% human albumin,                incubating, counting non-adherent cells,                resuspending the collected non-adherent cells in                AIM-V medium, adding A magnetic beads and B                magnetic beads, transferring the mixed suspension                of cells and magnetic beads, incubating,                centrifuging, filtering, obtaining cytotoxic T cell                (CTL) cell activation solution, preparing 10% CTL                cell activation medium, 10% autologous serum and                80% AIM-V culture medium by conventional methods,                adjusting the CTL cells, incubating, adding 10% CTL                cell activation solution, 10% autologous serum, and                80% as the existing culture medium every three                days, and collecting CTL cells.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                activated and proliferated cytotoxic T lymphocytes                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides CTL cell purity of more                than 99% and proliferation rate in 14 days by more                than 1200 times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparation of activated and                proliferated cytotoxic T lymphocytes, involves                preparing 200-400 ml peripheral blood of healthy                people, using leukocyte separation solution to                remove red blood cells, platelets and granulocytes,                collecting mononuclear cells, staining with trypan                blue, counting to obtain number of mononuclear                cells, resuspending mononuclear cells in AIM-V                medium containing 5% human albumin, adjusting cell                concentration to 0.5x 106/ml, transferring to the                tissue culture flask, incubating in 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 1.8-2.2                hours, washing and collecting non-adherent cells                after the incubation, counting non-adherent cells                by trypan blue staining, resuspending the collected                non-adherent cells in AIM-V medium containing 5%                human albumin and adjusting the cell concentration                to 1x 106/ml, adding A magnetic beads and B                magnetic beads to mix with suspended cells,                suspending, where the volume ratio of cells,                magnetic beads A and magnetic beads B is                0.8-1.2:0.6-1.0:0.1-0.3, the magnetic beads A are                human T cell activation magnetic beads cluster of                differentiation (CD)3/CD28, and the B magnetic                beads are human T cell activation magnetic beads                CD3/CD28/CD137, transferring the mixed suspension                of cells and magnetic beads to a tissue culture                flask, incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at                37 degrees C for 70-74 hours, after incubation,                centrifuging to remove all cells and magnetic                beads, using the supernatant using 0.22 micron                filter element filter sterilization, filtering,                obtaining CTL cell activation solution, using                conventional methods to prepare 50% cytotoxic T                cell (CTL) cell activation medium, 2% autologous                serum and 48% AIM-V culture medium, obtaining 1x                108 mononuclear cells from the patient, adjusting                to 8x 106 with AIM-V medium/ml, transferring to a                tissue culture flask, incubating at 37 degrees C                for 22-24 hours in 5% carbon dioxide incubator,                collecting and washing the cells after the                incubation, separating the CTL cells with the human                negative CD8 cell separation kit, preparing 10% CTL                cell activation medium, 10% autologous serum and                80% AIM-V culture medium by conventional methods,                adjusting the CTL cells to 0.5x 106/ml-1.5x 106/ml,                incubating at 37 degrees C for 13-15 days in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator, adding 10% CTL cell                activation solution, 10% autologous serum, and 80%                as the existing culture medium every three days,                and collecting CTL cells by centrifugation, which                is the final product cytotoxic T lymphocytes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  resuspending mononuclear cells with AIM-V medium                  containing 5% human albumin, and adjusting cell                  concentration to 0.8x 106-1x 106/ml. The volume                  ratio of suspension cells, A magnetic beads, and                  B magnetic beads is 1:0.8:0.2. The incubation                  time is 72-73 hours. The incubation time in the                  incubator is 23 hours. The concentration of the                  separated CTL cells is adjusted to 0.8x 106/ml-1x                  106/ml.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN111826344-A
TI Improving proliferation activity of human adipose stem cells by washing and digestion fat, washing cells, inoculating and culturing cells, preparing replacement medium, harvesting cell, inoculating and passaging cell, and culturing cell.
AU LU M
AE LU M (LUMM-Individual)
GA 2020A7366U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving the proliferation activity of human adipose stem cells involves (a) washing fat by transferring the fat from the transport bottle to a 50 ml centrifuge tube and adding sodium chloride injection to wash, (b) performing fat digestion, (c) washing cells, (d) performing cell inoculation by adding appropriate amount of UltraCULTURE medium to resuspend the washed cell pellet, pipetting evenly and inoculating into a culture flask, (e) performing cell culture, (f) preparing primary replacement medium by taking out the gas-permeable cell culture flask every 3 days, gently aspirating portion of the old culture medium with a serological pipette and placing in a 50 ml centrifuge tube, making up an equal amount of fresh complete medium and adding in a carbon dioxide constant temperature and humidity incubator to continue cultivation, (g) performing cell harvest, (h) performing cell inoculation and passage, and (i) performing cell culture.
   USE - The method is useful for improving the proliferation activity of human adipose stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens the primary cell culture time, activates and maintains the multidirectional differentiation and self-renewal ability of adipose stem cells, avoids the introduction of xenobiotic pathogens, avoids drastic changes in the culture environment, shortens the adaptation time of cells, and avoids frequent digestion and damage to cells by using low-density inoculation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving the proliferation activity of human adipose stem cells involves (a) washing fat by transferring the fat from the transport bottle to a 50 ml centrifuge tube and adding sodium chloride injection to wash, (b) performing fat digestion by adding collagenase to the processed fat, placing on a 37 degrees C constant temperature shaker for shaking and performing digestion, (c) washing cell by diluting the mixture digested with sodium chloride injection, centrifuging and washing twice to remove the supernatant and upper fat, (d) performing cell inoculation by adding appropriate amount of UltraCULTURE medium to resuspend the washed cell pellet, and pipetting evenly and inoculating into a culture flask, (e) performing cell culture by placing the gas-permeable cell culture flask in a carbon dioxide constant temperature and humidity incubator for culture, (f) preparing primary replacement medium by taking out the gas-permeable cell culture flask every 3 days, gently aspirating portion of the old culture medium with a serological pipette and placing in a 50 ml centrifuge tube, making up an equal amount of fresh complete medium and adding in a carbon dioxide constant temperature and humidity incubator to continue cultivation, (g) performing cell harvest by when the cells are cultured until the cloning group reaches 80-90% confluence, adding the culture solution, gently rinsing the gas-permeable cell culture flask with sodium chloride injection, adding cell digestion enzyme digestion to each group of gas-permeable cell culture flasks, then adding culture supernatant to each group of gas-permeable cell culture flasks to terminate the digestion process, tapping the side wall of the container until most of the cells fall off, transferring to a 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding sodium chloride injection to make the volume to 45 ml, centrifuging, removing the supernatant after centrifugation, mixing the fresh medium with the old medium, resuspending the cells, and filtering with a 40 um cell filter, (h) performing cell inoculation and passage by inoculating the cell suspension into a new T175 culture flask according to the counting result, and (i) performing cell culture by horizontally shake the culture flask to evenly distribute the cell suspension on the bottom of the flask, and placing the culture container in a carbon dioxide constant temperature and humidity incubator to start culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The sodium chloride injection for washing is added with 1-2% penicillin-streptomycin solution. The tissue digestive enzyme used is 0.1-0.3% collagenase II. The volume ratio of adipose tissue to collagenase is 1/2-1/3. The collagenase digestion time is 30-60 minutes. The centrifugation is performed at a centrifugal force of 300-500 g for 5-10 minutes. The UltraCULTURE used is added with 10-20 vol.% human cord blood serum. The method involves adding 2-4 mM/l L-Glutamin is added to the UltraCULTURE. The method involves using 0.05-0.1% cell digestive enzyme. The volume ratio of new and old medium used is 1/3-1/2, and the seeding density of cell passage is 5000-8000/cm2.

TI Metal and non-metal high-temperature smelting            device comprises reduction furnace, hydrator,            centrifugal separator, carbonization reactor, pump            unit, ferrosilicon separator and transform separation            system.
AU JIA P
AE SHANGHAI KELAIPU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LT (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201909367N
AB    NOVELTY - Metal and non-metal high-temperature smelting                device comprises a reduction furnace (1), a                hydrator (2), a centrifugal separator (3), a                carbonization reactor (4), a pump unit (5), a                ferrosilicon separator, a transform separation                system, a liquid-solid separator, an ammonia                splitter and a hydrogen storage system. The                reduction furnace is a rotary kiln structure. The                rotary kiln is divided into a mixed combustion                chamber, a preheating section and a reduction                section, which are sequentially connected. The                mixing combustor is provided with a finished                product outlet, a fuel and reductant inlet, an                oxygen enriched inlet and a mineral inlet. The                reduction section is provided with a carbon                monoxide outlet. The shift separation system is                provided with a hydrogen outlet, a water vapor                inlet, a carbon monoxide inlet, and a carbon                dioxide outlet. The hydrogen outlet is connected to                the hydrogen storage system.
   USE - Used as a metal and non-metal high-temperature                smelting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the heat utilization rate,                reduces metal recycling, changes waste into                valuable product and completely utilizes the                natural resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal and non-metal high-temperature smelting                device comprises a reduction furnace, a hydrator, a                centrifugal separator, a carbonization reactor, a                pump unit, a ferrosilicon separator, a transform                separation system, a liquid-solid separator, an                ammonia splitter and a hydrogen storage system. The                reduction furnace is a rotary kiln structure. The                rotary kiln is divided into a mixed combustion                chamber, a preheating section and a reduction                section, which are sequentially connected. The                mixing combustor is provided with a finished                product outlet, a fuel and reductant inlet, an                oxygen enriched inlet and a mineral inlet. The                reduction section is provided with a carbon                monoxide outlet. The shift separation system is                provided with a hydrogen outlet, a water vapor                inlet, a carbon monoxide inlet, and a carbon                dioxide outlet. The hydrogen outlet is connected to                the hydrogen storage system. The carbon monoxide                inlet is connected to the carbon monoxide outlet of                the reduction furnace through a chilling dust                collector. The carbon dioxide outlet is connected                to the carbon dioxide inlet of the carbonization                reactor. The finished product outlet of the                reduction furnace is connected to a liquid-solid                separator. The solid phase outlet of the                liquid-solid separator is connected to the                centrifugal separator through a hydrator. The                hydrator has a water inlet. The liquid-liquid                outlet of the liquid-solid separator is connected                to the ferrosilicon separator. The ferrosilicon                separator is provided with a heavy metal mixture                outlet and a ferrosilicon outlet. The heavy metal                mixture outlet is connected to the metal product                bin. The ferrosilicon outlet is connected to the                ferrosilicon product warehouse. The centrifugal                separator is provided with a solid product outlet                and a liquid product outlet. The liquid product                outlet is divided into two ways, one is connected                to the ammoniation separator, and the other is                connected to the carbonization reactor. The                carbonization reactor is provided with a light                calcium carbonate outlet and a magnesium hydroxide                outlet. The light calcium carbonate outlet is                connected to a light calcium carbonate product                warehouse. The magnesium hydroxide outlet is                connected to the magnesium hydroxide product                storage. The ammoniation separator is provided with                a calcium hydroxide outlet and a magnesium                hydroxide outlet. The calcium hydroxide outlet is                connected to the calcium hydroxide product bin. The                magnesium hydroxide outlet is connected to a                magnesium hydroxide product compartment. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for metal and                non-metal high-temperature smelting method using                the metal and non-metal high-temperature smelting                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation                of the metal and non-metal high-temperature                smelting device (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   Reduction furnace (1)
   Hydrator (2)
   Centrifugal separator (3)
   Carbonization reactor (4)
   Pump unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The effluent of                  centrifugal separator liquid outlet is an                  emulsion of calcium hydroxide and magnesium                  hydroxide. PT P
PN US2020190459-A1
TI Continuously measuring oxygen and carbon dioxide            concentrations inside cell culture container, by            establishing controlled hypoxic atmosphere within cell            culture container, and using environmental sensor            device to measure concentrations.
AU LE ROYAL Q
   SON K
   ROTHENBERG B E
AE BILLUPS-ROTHENBERG INC (BILL-Non-standard)
GA 202053942D
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously measuring oxygen and carbon                dioxide concentrations inside a cell culture                container, involves providing an environmental                sensor device and placing the environmental sensor                device into a cell culture container, where the                sensor device comprises a solid state oxygen sensor                and a solid state carbon dioxide sensor;                establishing a controlled hypoxic atmosphere within                the cell culture container, and placing the cell                culture container into an incubator; using the                environmental sensor device to continuously measure                oxygen and carbon dioxide concentrations within the                cell culture container while the cell culture                container is located within the incubator; and                storing data from the solid state oxygen sensor and                the solid state carbon dioxide sensor in a memory                and/or wirelessly transmitting the data to a user                while the cell culture container is located within                the incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously                measuring oxygen and carbon dioxide concentrations                inside a cell culture container (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The improved devices and methods allow for                accurate and rapid determination of atmospheric                parameters, and particularly oxygen (O2) and carbon                dioxide (CO2). The systems and methods have some                advantages over currently known technologies,                including compact size, rapid and real-time                measurement of various environmental parameters                including oxygen and carbon dioxide concentration,                barometric pressure, temperature, and/or humidity,                all of which contribute to high accuracy while                enabling a form factor that allows placement of the                sensor in the cell culture container during                continuous operation. Moreover, where the sensors                measure barometric pressure and temperature, test                results can be normalized to any geographic                location (e.g. compensation for higher altitude)                and/or other variables (e.g. high- or low                temperature growth conditions). Thus, and viewed                from a different perspective, contemplated devices                are self-calibrating. In addition, all data can be                stored (typically in association with a time stamp)                and/or wirelessly transmitted to a remote device,                which allows continuous monitoring in situ without                disturbance while the cell or tissue culture is in                the cell culture container. The devices may                communicate with external systems to store data,                and multiple devices can be simultaneously                monitored. Further, because the system is placed                inside the cell culture incubator, continuous and                instant monitoring of the conditions inside the                incubator is possible. Oxygen sensors are                preferably pressure and temperature compensated,                enabling accurate operation over a wide                environmental range without the need for additional                calibration. The oxygen and carbon dioxide                monitoring system has a small footprint and fits in                compact enclosures. It does not require                re-calibration in normal operation because it is                pre-calibrated. It comprises a safe rechargeable                battery technology and thus provides maintenance                free use. The system provides reliable and accurate                performance even in harsh environments; it has a                long lifetime and thus low maintenance; the solid                state sensors have no moving parts, non-depleting                technology; the system is easy to use, there are                virtually no gas cross-sensitivity, works where                other sensor technologies do not; it is vibration                and shock resistant; compensated for temperature,                humidity and pressure; and it is wireless tested to                work in water jacketed incubators.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                environmental sensor device comprising a housing at                least partially enclosing 1st and 2nd solid state                sensors; where the 1st solid state sensor comprises                an oxygen sensor having an oxygen quenchable                fluorescent dye; where the 2nd solid state sensor                comprises a non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) carbon                dioxide sensor; and an electronic component that is                coupled to the 1st and 2nd solid state sensors and                that comprises a memory for data storage and a                wireless communication module that allows for                transmission of data while the sensor device is                located inside the cell culture incubator; where                the electronic component is configured to                compensate sensed amounts of oxygen and carbon                dioxide for temperature, pressure, and humidity, to                so determine adjusted quantities for oxygen and                carbon dioxide; and where the housing is configured                to form a self-contained sensor device suitable for                placement inside a cell culture incubator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves measuring ambient pressure, humidity,                  and/or temperature while the cell culture                  container is located within the incubator; using                  the measured ambient pressure, humidity, and/or                  temperature to determine adjusted quantities for                  the oxygen and carbon dioxide concentrations; and                  displaying the adjusted quantities for the oxygen                  and carbon dioxide concentrations on a display                  while the cell culture container is located                  within the incubator. The controlled hypoxic                  atmosphere in the cell culture container is                  established using a gas feed into the cell                  culture container, where the gas feed is provided                  from a gas source outside the incubator. The user                  sends a wireless signal to the environmental                  sensor device to wirelessly transmit the data to                  the user. The data are stored in predetermined                  intervals and/or associated with a time                  stamp.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. a second heat exchanger connected to an upper portion of the second chamber, and provided to perform heat exchange through a refrigerant to lower the temperature of the mixed gas flowing from the second chamber; a third chamber (123) disposed at the upper end of the third gas separation unit and provided with a mixture gas composed of carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide and hydrogen flowed from the second chamber to perform a catalytic conversion reaction with the catalyst; TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the first chamber, a conversion reaction between the mixed gas and the catalyst occurs at a temperature of 400-500 degrees C, and the mixed gas is primarily cooled in the first heat exchanger. In the second chamber, the mixed gas cooled by the first heat exchanger and the catalyst undergo a conversion reaction at a temperature of 350-400 degrees C. In the third chamber, the mixed gas cooled by the second heat exchanger undergoes a conversion reaction with a catalyst at a temperature of 250-350 degrees C. The mixed gas passing through the first chamber, the second chamber, and the third chamber is sequentially cooled by the first heat exchanger, the second heat exchanger, and the third heat exchanger to gradually decrease the temperature. The refrigerant flows in the order of the second heat exchanger and the first heat exchanger, starting with the third heat exchanger, and the third chamber. The hydrogen is surplus electricity produced through renewable energy to separate and supply oxygen and hydrogen through electrolysis. The carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide captures and supplies carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide emitted through greenhouse gases. The first heat exchanger cools the first chamber to produce steam by heating the refrigerant having an elevated temperature to carbon monoxide or water generated through the reaction of carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The integrated multi-stage reactor for the methanation of carbon oxide is applied to the methanation process to produce methane produced through the reaction of carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Composition: The first heat exchanger is arranged in a coil-shaped long tube to lower the temperature of the mixed gas and catalyst flowed from the first chamber, and a first inlet through which a refrigerant is introduced is provided at one end. The second heat exchanger is disposed in a coiled elongated tube to lower the temperature of the mixed gas flowed from the second chamber, and a second inlet through which the refrigerant flows from the first outlet to one end. The third heat exchanger is arranged in a coil-shaped long tube to lower the temperature of the mixed gas flowed from the third chamber, and a third inlet through which the refrigerant flows from the second outlet to one end.

AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
   NORTH CHINA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NCSI-C)
GA 202283295T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a structure equation model (SEM) of coal spontaneous combustion danger under an inerting condition. Observation variable index sample data of coal spontaneous combustion is obtained. Reliability and efficiency of the sample data are checked. A sample data input SEM of confidence and efficiency test is input. Maximum likelihood process is used to fit the SEM. A coal spontaneous combustion risk evaluation model is obtained. Sample data efficiency test is performed by using SPSS software factor analyzing module.
   USE - Method for constructing coal spontaneous combustion risk evaluation model under idling condition of a mining area.
   ADVANTAGE - The method establishes the coal spontaneous combustion risk evaluation model aiming at the working surface of the injection inert gas carbon dioxide as goaf fire-extinguishing technical unit, using structure equation evaluation method to construct the model under the mining area inerting condition based on SEM algorithm, thus realizing the special evaluation of the risk of the carbon dioxide working face, and reducing the risk evaluation accuracy difference of the combustion caused by the difference of anti-fire extinguishing technical units.

PT P
PN CN114525251-A
TI Rapidly culturing three-dimensional (3D) breast cancer cells in vitro comprises thawing MCF-7 cells, adding complete culture medium, transferring to culture dish, adding digestive juice to digest, adding 3D culture medium, compacting MCF-7 cell ball and confirming successful in vitro culture.
AU DENG C
   CHEN J
   LIANG H
   XIE Y
   JIAO C
AE GUANGDONG YUEWEI EDIBLE MUSHROOM TECHNOL (GDIN-C)
GA 202274305K
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly culturing 3D breast cancer cells in vitro comprises (a) resuscitating MCF-7 adherent cells: thawing MCF-7 cells stored in liquid nitrogen, adding complete culture medium to mix uniformly, obtaining cell suspension, transferring cell suspension to culture dish, placing in carbon dioxide culture box for culturing, (2) digesting adherent cells: washing cultured cell by phosphate buffered saline (PBS) solution, adding digestive juice to digest, adding complete culture medium to centrifuge after the cell is dropped, removing supernatant, adding 3D culture medium, transferring the obtained cell suspension to the ultra-low adsorption cell culture plate, placing in carbon dioxide culture box for culturing and (3) 3D cell culture: observing cell state every day, changing 1/2 of 3D culture medium every 1-2 days, culturing until the size is uniform under microscope, compacting MCF-7 cell ball and confirming the successful in vitro culture of the 3D breast cancer cell.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly culturing 3D breast cancer cells in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is based on the tumor cell culturing technology; adopts a commercial ultra-low adsorption cell culturing plate; finds a technical approach to significantly improve the 3D breast cancer cell culturing efficiency; has simple operation and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The thawing process comprises immediately placing into 37degrees Celsius water bath, slightly rotating less than 1 minute and quickly thawing cell. In the step (a), adding proper pre-heated complete culture medium before adding the complete culture medium, blowing and hanging uniformly, centrifuging at 800revolutions/minute for 5-10 minutes and removing the supernatant. The condition of the carbon dioxide culture box is: 5% carbon dioxide, 37degrees Celsius and saturated humidity. The washing time of the PBS solution in the step (b) is 2 times. The digestion process of adding digestive juice comprises adding digestive juice on the side of the culture dish, lightly shaking the culture dish to obtain completely covered cell layer and allowing to stand for 2 minutes at room temperature. The centrifuging in the step (b) comprises centrifuging for 5-10 minutes at 800revolutions/minute. The 3D culture medium is prepared by using complete culture medium as matrix and adding Matrigel glue with volume fraction of 0.5-2%. The culture time in the step (c) is 5-7 days.Preferred Components: The complete culture medium in the step (a) and step (b) is prepared by Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) high-sugar culture medium as substrate and comprising 10 vol.% fetal bovine serum and 1 vol.% double antibody.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN113621745-A; CN113621745-B
TI Carbon cycle-based blast furnace-converter steel production method, involves pressurizing, deoxidizing, dehydrating and decarbonizing untreated converter gas to obtain desorption gas and treated converter gas, and circulating treated converter gas.
AU LONG M
   LI M
   HONG Z
   XU J
   HOU S
   NIU Q
   ZHAO Y
   WANG G
   ZOU Z
   XIAO X
AE CISDI ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2021D0925G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves ironmaking in a blast furnace (1) to obtain molten iron. The molten iron is introduced into the converter, and steel is made in the converter to obtain molten steel and untreated converter gas. The untreated converter gas is pressurized, deoxidized, dehydrated and decarbonized to obtain desorption gas and treated converter gas. The treated converter gas is circulated back into the blast furnace to adjust the proportion of reducing gas in the hearth gas in the blast furnace. The untreated converter gas is pressurized to 0.50Mpa0.65Mpa by a gas pressurizing device to obtain pressurized converter gas. The carbon-dioxide concentration in the desorption gas is less than or equal to 95%.
   USE - Carbon cycle-based blast furnace-converter steel production method used for supporting steel industry low carbon development.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing recycling of the converter gas after decarburization is recycled back to the blast furnace, thus improving the reducing gas content in a blast furnace belly gas, promoting indirect reduction in the blast-furnace, reducing direct reduction so as to reduce the consumption of carbon fuel in blast furnace iron-making process, and effectively reducing the discharge of carbon dioxide (CO2) in an efficient manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon cycle-based blast furnace-converter steel production method.
   Blast furnace (1)
   Gas blowing device (3)
   Furnace structure (11)
   Tuyere (12)

PN CN113603120-A
TI Method for recovering battery-grade lithium by short-process acid leaching of waste lithium iron phosphate, involves saturating lithium carbonate into filter solution, and settling battery level lithium carbonates and recycling through carbon dioxide.
AU MU J
   YANG Q
   FU Y
   KUANG G
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021C9105Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method invovles mixing the waste lithiumiron phosphate powder, concentrated sulfuric acid and water, stirring, separating the carbon black and PVDF. The waste lithiumIron phosphate powder is added to the filtrate to neutralize excess acid in the solution, and the pH value is adjusted and filtered. The filter residue is recovered. The calcium carbonate is added to the filter liquid to primarily adjust the pH, the local pH is prevented, and lime is continuously added to adjust pH. The lithium carbonate is saturated into the filter solution. The battery level lithium carbonates are settled and recycled through carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for recovering battery-grade lithium by short-process acid leaching of waste lithium iron phosphate, for indispensable energy storage device.
   ADVANTAGE - The processeffectively reduces the loss of acid and alkali, and avoids the defect that the required process parameters of each time different batches of lithium iron phosphate powder are to be fine adjusted. The process is simple and efficient and the universality is strong. The process recovers lithium from waste lithium ion battery, using waste lithium iron phosphorus to neutralize pH value of acid leaching liquid, and avoids the waste of alkali.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for recovering battery-grade lithium by short-process acid leaching of waste lithium iron phosphate. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
