PN CN113546630-A
TI Graphite-based supported iron-based catalyst            useful in the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to            olefin, comprises graphite as carrier, metal iron as            active component, and sodium, potassium or manganese as            promoter metal.
AU YIN Q
   MENG X
   ZHAO R
   XIN Z
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021C4651B
AB    NOVELTY - Graphite-based supported iron-based catalyst                comprises graphite as carrier, metal iron as active                component, and sodium, potassium or manganese as                promoter metal. The content of metallic iron is                10-40 pts. wt..The content of the promoter metal is                0.5-5 pts. wt.. and content of the graphite is                remaining amount.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in the hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide to olefins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has moderate metal carrier                interaction force with the carrier graphite,                uniform iron particle size, good dispersibility                good, high catalytic activity, good selectivity,                good thermal stability, and long catalyst                life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the graphite-based supported iron-based                catalyst, comprising (a) using oxidant for                performing thermal oxygen treatment on graphite to                obtain thermal oxygen treatment graphite, (b)                dissolving the metal precursor in the solvent to                obtain a metal precursor solution, (c) immersing                the thermally oxygenated graphite obtained in the                step (a) in the metal precursor solution obtained                in the step (b), and performing drying treatment,                (d) roasting the dried product obtained in the step                (c), and (e) dissolving the promoter metal in the                solvent to obtain promoter metal salt solution,                immersing the product obtained in the step (d) in                the promoter metal salt solution, and drying to                obtain graphite-based supported iron-based                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The specific surface                  area of the graphite is 15-250 m^2/g, and the                  particle size is 60-300 meshes. The metallic iron                  exists in the form of ferric oxide. In the step                  (a), the oxidant includes concentrated nitric                  acid, concentrated sulfuric acid, potassium                  permanganate, sodium permanganate, potassium                  dichromate, sodium dichromate, phosphoric acid,                  acetic acid and/or perchloric acid. The mass                  ratio of graphite and oxidant is 1:(5.1-9). In                  the step (b), the metal precursor includes ferric                  nitrate, ferric citrate, ferric ammonium citrate,                  ferric oxalate, ferrous ammonium oxalate, ferrous                  lactate and/or ferrous acetate. The solvent                  includes deionized water, ethanol, acetic acid,                  chloroform and acetone. The mass ratio of the                  metal precursor and the solvent is                  (1.35-5.42):50. In the step (e), the promoter                  metal includes sodium nitrate, sodium carbonate,                  sodium bicarbonate, potassium nitrate, potassium                  carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, manganese                  nitrate and/or manganese carbonate. The solvent                  includes deionized water, ethanol, acetic acid,                  chloroform and/or acetone. The mass ratio of the                  promoter metal and the solvent is                  (0.18-1.8):150.Preferred Method: The temperature of the                  thermal oxygen treatment is 80-90degrees Celsius,                  and the time is 60-120 minutes. In the step (c),                  the impregnation adopts an excess impregnation                  method or an equal volume impregnation method,                  immersion temperature is 15-60degrees Celsius,                  and the time is 1-8 hours, the drying temperature                  is 60-100degrees Celsius, and time is 8-12 hours.                  In the step (d), the calcination temperature is                  300-600degrees Celsius, and the time is 2-8                  hours. In the step (e), the impregnation adopts                  an excessive impregnation method or an equal                  volume impregnation method, and the impregnation                  temperature is 15-60degrees Celsius, and time is                  1-8 hours, drying temperature is 60-100degrees                  Celsius, and the time is 8-12 hours.

PN CN112980466-A
TI Carbonized waste smokeless boiler comprises two furnace with dual identities and two condensers, exhaust pipes and valves, carbonization furnace cavity, and combustion furnace cavity.
AU WANG G
AE WANG G (WANG-Individual)
GA 2021720912
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonized waste smokeless boiler comprises two furnace cavities (1, 3, 5, 7 and 2, 4, 6, 8) with dual identities and two condensers (41,42), and exhaust pipes and valves. The two sets of furnace cavities with dual identities are exchanged for each other after completing their respective tasks. One group is a carbonization furnace cavity, and the other group is a combustion furnace cavity. The heat energy is transferred to each other, and the gas is absolutely incommunicable, and each gas pipeline is used. The two condensers respectively condense raw gas and carbon dioxide gas, so that the high-temperature gas is condensed to the temperature required by humans. The various exhaust pipes and valves control the gas flow of the two furnace cavities. The combustion furnace cavity must have an oxygen inlet and a carbon dioxide outlet. Except for the waste gas outlet, the entire carbonization furnace cavity is absolutely sealed.
   USE - Used as carbonized waste smokeless boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The boiler: is based on the principle of first carbonization and then combustion, and the heat energy required for carbonization comes from the combustion chamber of the next wall; produces solid carbon and combustible gases after carbonization; is smokeless when burned; and produces various tars that are important chemical raw material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbonized waste smokeless boiler.
   Furnace cavity (1)
   Condenser (41)

PN CN112811785-A
TI Preparing sludge drying agent by preparing dry sludge powder from dredged sludge, reacting, heating, heat-preserving, cooling, grinding, acid washing with weak acid solution, washing with sodium hydroxide and deionized water, and drying.
AU CHEN X
   WU L
   GUO F
   HU B
   XIE X
   ZHANG Z
   CAI W
   WU C
AE CHINA GEZHOUBA GROUP ECO-ENVIRONMENTAL (CHGE-Non-standard)
GA 202158416N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sludge drying agent, involves removing impurities from dredged sludge, leaving still for precipitation, removing supernatant, taking precipitated sludge from the lower layer, extruding and dehydrating to obtain cake with a moisture content of 75-85%, drying to constant weight at 90-110 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, grinding, sieving through a 200-mesh sieve, reacting the dry sludge powder in 100-350 ml/minute carbon dioxide gas atmosphere at room temperature for 5-60 minutes, adjusting flow rate of carbon dioxide gas to 50-100 ml/minute, heating at 10-30 degrees C/minute to 400-600 degrees C, heat-preserving for 0.5-3 hours, cooling to room temperature, grinding, sieving through 200-mesh sieve, washing with a weak acid solution, sodium hydroxide solution until the pH value is weakly alkaline, and deionized water until the pH of rinse water is 7, and drying. The molar ratio of weak acid molecules in the weak acid solution to product is (2-4):1.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sludge drying agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves dehydration performance of the sludge drying agent; increases the volume of sludge flocculation; reduces precipitation volume of sludge and production cost; realizes waste utilization; is simple, energy-saving, and environmentally-friendly; and adopts waste sludge/dredged sludge as raw materials to prepare sludge drying agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weak acid solution is phosphoric acid solution and/or citric acid solution, preferably phosphoric acid solution. The pH of the weakly alkaline is 7.5-8. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of weak acid molecules in the weak acid solution to product is preferably 3:1.

PN WO2021025541-A1; KR2021017421-A; KR2247105-B1
TI Manufacture of composite nanofibers used in catalyst composition, preparing base nanofibers comprising metal, preparing metal oxide nanofibers, and preparing source solution comprising metal oxide nanofibers and carbon nitride precursor.
AU LEE S
   KANG S
   KIM H
   LEE S Y
   KIN H J
AE UNIV HANYANG IND COOP FOUND (UHYG-C)
GA 202115021Y
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of composite nanofibers involve preparing base nanofibers comprising a metal, preparing nanofibers (I) by performing heat-treatment (A) on the base nanofibers in an oxidative atmosphere, preparing metal oxide nanofibers by performing heat- treatment (B) on the nanofibers (I) in a reductive atmosphere, preparing a source solution comprising the metal oxide nanofibers and a carbon nitride precursor, and performing heat-treatment (C) on the source solution. The carbon nitride is coated on the surface of the metal oxide nanofibers.
   USE - Manufacture of composite nanofibers used in catalyst composition for reducing carbon dioxide (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to secondary battery cathode, fuel cell power source, electric double layer capacitors, solar cells and liquefied natural gas (LNG) related products in aerospace and automobile industry, energy storage and utilization application, photoelectrochemical electrode cell, artificial photosynthesis, hydrogen storage capacity, energy efficiency application, and organic pollutant application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of composite nanofibers having excellent carbon dioxide reduction property, and provides metal oxide having improved absorbing ability of visible light by a simple hydrogen reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) the composite nanofibers, which comprise metal oxide nanofibers, and a carbon nitride surrounding the metal oxide nanofibers, including a crystalline phase and an amorphous phase simultaneously present on the surface of the crystalline phase; and
   (2) a catalyst composition, which comprises the composite nanofibers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of performing the heat-treatment (A) at a temperature (t1). The step of performing the heat-treatment (B) involves (i) heat treating the nanofibers (I) at the same temperature as the temperature (t1) and in a reducing atmosphere and (ii) heat treating the nanofibers (II) in a temperature higher than the temperature (t1) and a reducing atmosphere. The heat treatment (A) and heat treatment (B) are successively performed in the same chamber. The step (i) is performed at the same temperature by injecting a reducing atmosphere gas into the chamber. The step (ii) involves raising the temperature to a higher temperature than the heat treatment step (i) and carried out in the same gas atmosphere. The method of preparing source solution involves mixing the metal oxide nanofibers and the carbon nitride precursor, in a mass ratio of more than 1:60 to less than 1:100. The heat-treatment (C) is performed in an inert gas atmosphere. Preferred Components: The metal oxide nanofibers comprise black titanium oxide nanofibers. The carbon nitride is a graphitic carbon nitride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nitride precursor includes at least one of thiourea, urea, and melamine. The catalyst composition further comprises an additive comprising at least one of triethanolamine (TEOA), ethanol, methanol, isopropyl alcohol (IPA), or ethylene glycol. Preferred Process: The method of preparing the base nanofibers involve preparing a mixed solution comprising tetrabutyl titanate (TBT), polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP), and acetylacetone, and electrospinning the mixed solution.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a wafer (3) that                includes a substrate (1) formed of sapphire and                having a first main surface and a second main                surface (1a), and a semiconductor layered body (2)                which is disposed on the first main surface of the                substrate. A laser beam is irradiated into the                substrate to form a modified region inside the                substrate, in which the modified region has a crack                reaching the first main surface and a crack                reaching the second main surface. A carbon dioxide                (CO2) laser is irradiated to a region of the                substrate overlapping with a region to which the                laser beam has been irradiated. The wafer is                cleaved along the modified region to obtain light                emitting elements each having a hexagonal shape in                a plan view.
   USE - Method for manufacturing light emitting                elements.
   ADVANTAGE - When the wafer is separated into light                emitting elements having a hexagonal shape in a top                view, cleavage can occur without deviating from the                planned cleavage line, so that the light emitting                elements are manufactured in high yield. With                formation of such a groove, damage of the active                layer and the second semiconductor layer caused by                irradiation with the laser beam is reduced.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing growth rate of equine skeletal muscle satellite cells adherent culture involves adding a gelatin solution into a culture container, allowing to stand in an environment of 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain a culture container with a gelatin layer formed on the surface, transferring the equine skeletal muscle satellite cells into the culture vessel with gelatin layer, adding the proliferation culture medium and culturing at 37 degrees C in an environment of 5% carbon dioxide for 1-3 hours to obtain adherent culture equine skeletal muscle satellite cell.

   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens attachment time of skeletal muscle satellite cells. The cell oxidation model of the present invention provides favorable conditions and a research foundation for skeletal muscle satellite cells to repair muscle damage and provides favorable conditions and research foundations for research institutions such as muscle growth and muscle oxidative damage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for oxidative stress culture model of equine skeletal muscle satellite cells, which is obtained by adding stimulation culture medium containing hydrogen peroxide to the adherent cultured equine skeletal muscle satellite cells obtained by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves removing the excess gelatin solution in the culture container with a gelatin layer, washing twice with Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline (DMEM) and then adding proliferation culture medium. In the method, the gelatin solution has concentration of 0.0025 g/ml and the proliferation culture medium comprises Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) supplemented with 20% fetal bovine serum, 1% penicillin streptomycin and amphotericin B. The method involves culturing adherent cultured equine skeletal muscle satellite cells in a culture vessel with a gelatin layer to reach 70-80% confluence. The model is prepared by adding stimulation culture medium containing 600 mu mol/l hydrogen peroxide and culturing the cells for 2-10 hours.

TI Catalyst useful for dry reforming of methane and carbon dioxide, prepared by dissolving nickel salt in deionized water, adding silicalite-1 molecular sieve, soaking, stirring to dryness without calcination and grinding directly into powder.
AU WANG X
   SHI C
   CHEN Y
   LIU Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202083062L
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst has same silicalite-1 molecular sieve crystal grains, which are dispersed and embedded with a number of nickel nanoparticles with an average particle size less than 5 nm or a multi-cavity package of nickel nanoparticles with an average particle size less than 5 nm, and the mass of nickel metal is higher than 7.5% under the condition of not using fluoride, using silicalite-1 molecular sieve, nickel salt, and the organic template is obtained by hydrothermal crystallization.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for dry reforming of methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high nickel content and mass transfer function of silicalite-1 molecular sieve micro-pore, thus the catalytic activity and stability of the catalyst are prominent when compared with the past amorphous silica embedded nickel nano-particle catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving nickel salt in deionized water, and then adding silicalite-1 molecular sieve to it, soaking and stirring to dryness without calcination, grinding directly into powder; (iii) adding the obtained solid powder to tetrapropylammonium hydroxide solution and deionized water and mixing to form a slurry liquid; (iii) pouring the slurry liquid into hydrothermal kettle and hydrothermal crystallization at 110-180 degrees C; and (iv) drying the obtained solid and removing the organic template in the air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the nickel salt is nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate, nickel chloride or nickel acetate. In the step (i), the mass ratio of the nickel salt to the silicalite-1 molecular sieve calculated as metal is 8/100-25/100. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of silicalite-1 molecular sieve to tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and water is 10/(8-30)/(70-400). Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the crystallization temperature is 150-170 degrees C, and the crystallization time is 18-72 hours.

PN KR2020073195-A; KR2244663-B1
TI Converter refining for generating convection phenomenon by exothermic reaction and endothermic reaction in metal melt comprises charging metal melt in converter having lance and odorless nozzle, oxidizing and melting metal melt.
AU IL S
   JUNG S
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 202056104L
AB    NOVELTY - Converter refining for generating convection phenomenon by exothermic reaction and endothermic reaction in metal melt comprises charging metal melt in converter having a lance (10) and a odorless nozzle (20), where the odor nozzle comprises a double pipe having an inner tube and an outer tube, oxidizing and melting metal melt by blowing oxygen into interior of the converter through the quench lance, blowing carbon dioxide through inner tube and outer tube, replacing oxygen blown through the take-up lance into inside of the converter through the deodorizing nozzle, separating water vapor, reacting metal of metal melt with the water vapor and carbon dioxide to produce metal oxide, hydrogen, and carbon monoxide, performing second oxidation refining of the metal melt, absorbing heat generated in the exothermic reaction of the first oxidation refining through the endothermic reaction of the second oxidation refining.
   USE - The method is useful for converter refining for generating convection phenomenon by exothermic reaction and endothermic reaction in metal melt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the efficiency of the decarburization reaction; reduces the amount of oxygen used for oxidative refining; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for converter refining device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the converter (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   (C) continuing to deliver liquid through carbonation chamber fluid inlet into carbonation chamber and liquid contacting carbonation source where carbon dioxide is generated.

PN RU2715472-C1; WO2020251398-A1; DE112020002853-T5
TI article containing a silicon base and a coating layer in the form of a nanofilm of carbon with a diamond-type crystal lattice, and a method of making said article.
AU KUKUSHKIN S A
   OSIPOV A V
   FEOKTISTOV N A
AE NEW TECHNOLOGIES SCI TECH CENT CO LTD (NEWT-Non-standard)
   SVYATETS G V (SVYA-Individual)
GA 202024977J
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the class of semiconductor devices and can be used in micro-, nano- and optoelectronics. Functional element of the semiconductor device has a base made of silicon with a transition layer formed on it and containing silicon carbide, on which there is a coating layer in the form of a nanofilm of carbon with a crystal lattice of a diamond type. Method of producing the disclosed functional element of a semiconductor device is carried out in a vacuum furnace in two steps, at first of which the base is heated to temperature of 9501,400C and synthesis of silicon carbide film on its surface in gas medium, which is an oxide or carbon dioxide or mixture of oxide or carbon dioxide with inert gas and / or nitrogen at pressure of 20600 Pa. At the second stage, nanofilm of carbon with crystalline lattice of diamond type is formed in vacuum furnace in medium of carbon tetrafluoride at temperature of 1,3001,400C and pressure of 2,5006,000 Pa from produced silicon carbide film by coordinated replacement of atoms. Best result is achieved when orientation (111) silicon is used as the base.
   USE - Electrical engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - Obtaining a carbon nanocrystalline material with a diamond-like type crystalline structure of a high-quality on a substrate (base) of silicon by storing a of a diamond-like type crystal lattice when conversion SiSiCC.
6 cl, 9 dwg

GA 202014661M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Brassica rapa extract containing polyphenol and flavonoid involves (a) cutting B.rapa, and drying B.rapa to produce B.rapa dry matter, (b) adding B.rapa dry matter in a supercritical extraction container, performing supercritical extraction using carbon dioxide as a supercritical fluid at a pressure of 400 bar and a temperature of 50 degrees C for 150 minutes, and obtaining supercritical extract residue, and (c) adding 50 volume/volume% ethanol aqueous solution to the supercritical extract residue, and extracting mixture at 20 degrees C for 24 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing B.rapa extract (claimed), which is useful in food, health functional food and beverage for providing antioxidant effect, improving cholesterol levels, and preventing adult diseases such as heart disease and stroke, and cancer. The food is chosen from special nutritional products (e.g. crude oil, infant food and baby food), processed meat products, fish products, tofu, jelly, noodles (e.g. ramen and noodles), health supplement, seasoned food (e.g. soy sauce, miso, red pepper paste and mixed soy sauce), sauces, sweets (e.g. snacks), dairy products (e.g. fermented milk and cheese), and drinks (e.g. fruit drinks, vegetable drinks, soy milk and fermented beverages).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares B.rapa extract having increased content of polyphenol and flavonoid with excellent yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves drying chopped B.rapa at -20 degrees C for 12 hours.

PN RU2713802-C1
TI GRAIN STORAGE DEVICE IN CONTROLLED AIR ENVIRONMENT AND METHOD OF ITS IMPLEMENTATION.
AU LATYSHENOK M B
   IVASHKIN A V
   LATYSHENOK N M
   BILENKO V A
   GOLUBENKO M I
AE LATYSHENOK M B (LATY-Individual)
   IVASHKIN A V (IVAS-Individual)
   LATYSHENOK N M (LATY-Individual)
   BILENKO V A (BILE-Individual)
   GOLUBENKO M I (GOLU-Individual)
GA 202012725N
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to technology of storage of agricultural products, in particular, grains, cereals and other agricultural products. Device comprises container, bottom of which has tapered side walls. Tapered bottom accommodates branch pipe and gate. Branch pipe is made in the form of a removable container. Damper is made in the form of detachable protective mesh attached to the bottom and located above fastening of the detachable container. Detachable tank is equipped with a gas analyzer which extracts carbon dioxide content through an opening in the wall of the removable container through the intake and return branch pipes. Detachable container through the vacuum electromagnetic valve with the vacuum pump is connected to the control and control device connected to the personal computer. Periodic monitoring of air medium inside container is carried out according to readings of temperature and humidity sensor in intergranular space, connected to personal computer through control and control device. Flat cover of container contains branch pipe with balloon of fresh warm air dryer. Preliminary dried grain is loaded at removal of container cover. Fresh atmospheric air is supplied. Vacuum is created by vacuum pump depending on intensity of respiration of grain material. Process of storage of grain is connected to collection of carbon dioxide in a removable container, content of which is established by formulas recommended for this device and method for its implementation.
   USE - Storage; agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is preventing loss of grain from spoilage during storage while preserving its presentation.

AU QIU Y

   ZHANG T

   XU W
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201953300R
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode comprises substrate and a modified                layer on which metal catalyst particles are                deposited, where the metal catalyst particles are                in a nano-cone shape and height of the nano-cone is                1-10 mu m and cone bottom diameter is 0.2-2 mu m,                preferably, the cone height is 2-7 mu m, preferably                the cone bottom diameter is 0.3-1.2 mu m, and the                nanocone catalyst particles are supported on the                substrate in an amount of 0.1-3 mg/cm2, preferably                a supported amount of 0.5-1.8 mg/cm2.
   USE - The electrode is useful in the electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode: has the simple preparation                process, good reproducibility, strong operability                and easy enlargement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the electrode, comprising adding weakly                alkaline deposit containing templating agents and                additives, under warming and stirring conditions,                obtaining by chemical displacement reaction and                electrochemical potentiostatic deposition, where                the precipitate component includes a soluble salt                solution (main salt) of the deposited metal,                template agent, complexing agent, stabilizer,                reducing agent and additive at a molar ratio of                104:103:1-103:102:1, preferably                0.6x104:0.4x103:1-0.15x104:0.2x10:1 and the pH of                the deposition liquid is 8-10, and the chemical                displacement reaction occurs between the metal                particles deposited by the templating agent and the                metal of the main salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method                  comprises (i) treating the substrate with                  impurities, rinsing in water to neutral and then                  drying in an inert atmosphere; (ii) immersing the                  substrate material in the deposition solution,                  adjusting the bath temperature and stirring                  speed, performing chemical replacement and                  constant potential deposition, rinsing with water                  to neutral after deposition, and drying in an                  inert atmosphere, obtaining dense nano-cone                  catalyst particles on the surface electrode;                  (iii) subjecting the electrode having dense                  nano-cone type catalyst particles on the surface                  to electrochemical reduction pretreatment. The pH                  of plating solution is adjusted to use                  concentrated ammonia or an alkali metal                  hydroxide, the electrodeposition temperature is                  40-80 degrees C, and the stirring speed is                  200-800 rpm. The electrodeposition potential is                  lower than the equilibrium potential of the                  corresponding deposited metal by 0.3-1 V, the                  deposition time is 5-90 minutes, preferably, the                  deposition time is 10-60 minutes. The                  electrochemical reduction pretreatment is carried                  out in CO2 saturated salt solution, where the                  salt solution is sodium hydrogencarbonate having                  a concentration of 0.1-0.5 M or potassium                  bicarbonate. The electrochemical reduction                  pretreatment has a treatment current of 0.1-0.5                  mAcm2 and a pretreatment time of 20-60 minutes.                  Preferred Components: The base material is a                  metal or a carbon-based porous material, and the                  metal is copper, indium, silver, and gold, the                  metal content is not greater than 99.5%, the                  carbon-based porous material is carbon paper,                  carbon felt or carbon cloth, and the porosity is                  60-95%, the base shape is a flake shape with a                  thickness of 0.05-2 mm, the base material is                  decontaminated, including degreasing and chemical                  treatment, immersing the degreasing process in a                  polar solvent for at least 30 minutes at room                  temperature, the chemical treatment is: soaking                  in a hydrochloric acid solution with a                  concentration of 1-38%, and the soaking time is                  5-60 minutes, washing and drying in an inert                  atmosphere, where the polar solvent is anhydrous                  ethanol and acetone, and the inert atmosphere is                  nitrogen and argon. The soluble salt solution of                  the deposited metal is halide, a sulfate, a                  nitrate, and an acetate, the metal is copper,                  indium, silcer, and gold, the concentration of                  the deposited metal ions is 0.5-50 mM, preferably                  the ion concentration is 5-30 mM, the templating                  agent is one of nickel nitrate, nickel chloride                  and nickel sulfate, the reducing agent is soluble                  hypophosphite, including sodium hypophosphite,                  ammonium hypophosphite and potassium                  hypophosphite, in one embodiment, the                  concentration of hypophosphite in deposition                  solution is 0.05-1 M, preferably the                  concentration is 0.1-0.5 M, the stabilizer is                  boric acid, and the concentration in deposition                  solution is 0.05-5 M, preferably the                  concentration is 0.1-2 M, the complexing agent is                  citric acid or citrate including ammonium                  citrate, sodium citrate, potassium citrate, the                  concentration of citrate in deposition solution                  is 5-100 mM, preferably 10-50 mM, the additive is                  a polyethylene glycol type surfactant including                  polyethylene glycol (PEG) having a molecular                  weight of less than 20,000, oleyl alcohol or                  Triton (RTM: Nonionic surfactant)X-100.

GA 201927966R
AB    NOVELTY - Ultra-light high-resilience bionic artificial limb comprises 93-98% thermoplastic polyurethane, 0.5-3% fleshcolor toner and 1-2.5% antibacterial agent and the thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) foaming material prepared by the steam compression.
   USE - Used as ultra-light high-resilience bionic artificial limb.
   ADVANTAGE - The bionic artificial limb has ultra-light density, high resilience, wear resistance, folding resistance and strong antibacterial property, and provides comfortable experience for the wearer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for forming ultra-light high-resilience bionic artificial limb, comprising (i) adding fleshcolor toner and antibacterial agent to prepare the TPU composite material with skin color and antibacterial performance; (ii) underwater granulating the composite TPU material to obtain TPU composite material particles; (iii) placing the prepared TPU composite material particles into a high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle, gradually increasing the pressure and the temperature in the reaction kettle until the carbon dioxide reaches the supercritical state, performing infiltration, vacuumizing and heating, placing the infiltrated TPU composite particles in a foaming device, heating and foaming to obtain TPU foaming particle; and (iv) placing the TPU foaming particle into the steam pressing device for steam pressing, subjecting to steam penetration process by vapor pressure, cooling and forming.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the ultra-light high-resilience bionic artificial limb.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The TPU is polyether polyurethane, polyester polyurethane, polycarbonate polyurethane and/or polylactone polyurethane, preferably polyether polyurethane and/or polyester polyurethane. The density of the TPU foaming material is 0.15-0.25 g/cm3, the shore hardness is 50-80 HA and the rebound rate is 60-70%. The particle size of the underwater granulate is 3-4 mm. In the step (iii), the pressure of the reaction kettle is more than 7.38 MPa, preferably 15 MPa and the temperature is more than 31 degrees C, preferably 40-45 degrees C. The heating the foaming temperature is 130-140 degrees C. In the step (iv), the steam pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa and steam penetrating time is 27-33 seconds, preferably 30 seconds, the pressure maintaining time is 45-55 seconds, preferably 50 seconds and the cooling and shaping time is 75-85 seconds, preferably 80 seconds.

PN CN108991020-A
TI Extracting fermentation broth of Hypoxylon perforatum used as reagent for controlling mango disease, by inoculating Hypoxylon perforatum into medium, culturing, sterilizing, and performing extraction of mixture.
AU WEI S
AE GUANGXI TIANDONG DONGCHEN AGRIC DEV CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3971A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting fermentation broth of Hypoxylon perforatum, involves inoculating H.perforatum in medium, culturing the medium at 25-28 degrees C for 5-6 days to obtain culture solution, where the medium comprises 2 g potato, 5 g soybean protein, 6 g Pinus sylvestris fiber, 12 g glucose, monopotassium phosphate, magnesium sulfate and 100 ml water, filtering the culture solution, sterilizing the solution by gauze, extracting the solution using mixture of ethanol, propanol and ethylene glycol in a mass ratio of 10:5:2, placing the primary extract in supercritical state, and performing supercritical extraction of the mixture, where the fermentation broth of the H.perforatum is obtained by extracting charcoal with anhydrous ethanol as an entraining agent at supercritical carbon dioxide extraction temperature of 40-60 degrees C, extraction pressure of 70 kPa and carbon dioxide flow rate of 25 l/minute for 30-40 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting fermentation broth which is used as reagent for controlling mango disease (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of extract of fermentation broth, which is as reagent for controlling mango disease, where the reagent comprises 80-100 pts. wt. fermentation broth extract, 1-3 pts. wt. copper hydroxide, 2-5 pts. wt. glucose, 1-6 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide, 2-6 pts. wt. surfactant, 4-8 pts. wt. cationic surfactant, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. titanium dioxide, and 1-3 pts. wt. lauric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The nonionic surfactant is polyol type. The nonionic surfactant is sorbitan ester or sucrose ester. The cationic surfactant is quaternary ammonium salt cationic surfactant. The reagent is prepared by extracting the fermentation broth and anionic surfactant after mixing with the cationic surfactant, performing ultrasonic dispersion for 10-20 minutes, adding titanium dioxide, lauric acid, copper hydroxide, glucose and hydrogen peroxide to the mixture, and performing ultrasonic dispersion and mechanical stirring of the mixture for 80-90 minutes.

PN CN108925043-A
TI Anti-fooling method of carbon dioxide (CO2) laser opening for printed circuit board (PCB) cover, involves stopping alarm to stop cutting and sending alarm signal, when target of foolproof target is not correctly captured.
AU ZHANG R
   HUANG D
   CHEN J
AE GUANGZHOU MEIWEI ELECTRONICS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201898101S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting a positioning target on a PCB board. A foolproof target with defining pattern or ink defined target is set on a cover opening of the PCB board. A cutting position is increased by a laser machine at a corresponding position of the foolproof target, and the target defined by the one of the defining pattern or ink defined target is burned into the target of other shapes. A laser hole is formed by stacking holes when making laser data. A hysteresis target cutting parameter is set for the foolproof target to burn the foolproof target into the target of other shapes. The target of the foolproof target is grasped during execution of cutting, when the target of the target object is burned into the target of other shape. When the target of the foolproof target is not correctly captured, the alarm is stopped to stop cutting and the alarm signal is sent.
   USE - Anti-fooling method of carbon dioxide (CO2) laser opening for printed circuit board (PCB) cover.
   ADVANTAGE - The grasping of the target by the laser machine is avoided after the target is completely burned, by setting the foolproof target. Thereby the problem of damage of the PCB due to repeated cutting is prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the burning effect of target using anti-fooling method.

TI Fluorine-modified-boron-carbon-nitrogen photocatalytic material useful for high-efficiency reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under visible light, prepared by high-temperature thermal polymerization method.
AU HUANG C
   XING F
   LIU Q
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2018956912
AB    NOVELTY - Fluorine-modified-boron-carbon-nitrogen photocatalytic material, prepared by synthesizing graphite phase boron carbon and nitrogen by high-temperature thermal polymerization method, mixing uniformly with potassium fluoride, further subjecting to heat treatment, is claimed.
   USE - The fluorine modified boron-carbon-nitrogen photocatalytic material is useful for high-efficiency reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under visible light (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic material utilizes easily available and economical raw materials, and is suitable for large scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the fluorine-modified-boron-carbon-nitrogen photocatalytic material, comprising: (i) dissolving boric oxide, urea and glucose in deionized water, then treating at 75 degrees C under normal pressure to evaporate water; (ii) placing the mixture in a corundum porcelain boat, then placing the boat in a horizontal high-temperature tubular furnace, reacting at 1250 degrees C for 5 hours at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute under ammonia atmosphere; (iii) taking out the product and washing with 0.1 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid, centrifuging and drying to obtain graphite phase boron carbon-nitrogen; (iv) mixing potassium fluoride with graphite phase, boron, carbon and nitrogen, placing in a muffle furnace and reacting at 400 degrees C for 3 hours at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute under an air atmosphere; and (v) washing the product with deionized water, filtering and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of boric oxide, urea and glucose is 1:2:0.3. The mass ratio of potassium fluoride and graphite phase carbon nitrogen is 0.2-0.6:1.

One end of                the inlet pipeline is connected to the inlet of the                raw material gas compressor, and the outlet end of                the raw material gas compressor is connected to                inlet of the membrane separation unit through the                pipeline. The non-permeate gas outlet of the                membrane separation unit is connected to associated                gas pipe network through a pipeline, and the                permeate gas outlet is connected to inlet of the                pressure swing adsorption compressor through a                connecting pipeline. The outlet of the pressure                swing adsorption compressor is connected to the                pressure swing adsorption unit through a pipeline,                and the purified gas outlet of the pressure swing                adsorption unit is provided with a return                line.
   USE - Device used for recycling light hydrocarbon                and carbon dioxide from oilfield (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of recycling carbon                dioxide from oil field, and has no waste gas                emission in whole process, and excellent                environmental benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device includes inlet pipeline, raw material                gas compressor, membrane separation unit, pressure                swing adsorption compressor, pressure swing                adsorption unit, vacuum pump, first valve, second                valve, third valve, fourth valve and fifth valve.                One end of the inlet pipeline is connected to the                inlet of the raw material gas compressor, and the                outlet end of the raw material gas compressor is                connected to inlet of the membrane separation unit                through the pipeline. The non-permeate gas outlet                of the membrane separation unit is connected to                associated gas pipe network through a pipeline, and                the permeate gas outlet is connected to inlet of                the pressure swing adsorption compressor through a                connecting pipeline. The outlet of the pressure                swing adsorption compressor is connected to the                pressure swing adsorption unit through a pipeline,                and the purified gas outlet of the pressure swing                adsorption unit is provided with a return line.                Both ends of the return line are respectively                connected with inlet line and associated gas pipe                network. The desorption gas outlet of the pressure                swing adsorption unit is connected to inlet of the                vacuum pump through a pipeline. The outlet end of                the vacuum pump is connected to a carbon dioxide                booster, and the inlet pipeline is connected with a                compression pipeline. The other end of the                compression line is connected to inlet of the                pressure swing adsorption compressor. The first                valve is arranged on the compression line, the                second valve is arranged on the connecting line,                the third valve and the fourth valve are both                arranged on the return line, and the fifth valve is                arranged on the inlet pipeline.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for recovering light hydrocarbons and carbon                dioxide from oilfield driving accompanying gas,                which involves: (a) selecting different recovery                routes according to carbon dioxide content in                associated gas, when the carbon dioxide content of                associated gas is 30-75%, treating associated gas                by membrane separation device and boosting the                associated gas with carbon dioxide content of                71-92% in membrane permeate gas by pressure swing                adsorption compressor; (b) entering the pressure                swing adsorption unit, through selective adsorption                of different types of adsorbents loaded in the                adsorption tower in the pressure swing adsorption                unit and obtaining desorption gas to obtain carbon                dioxide content with purity of 95% or more and                sending to carbon dioxide booster, returning the                gas discharged from pressure swing adsorption to                membrane separation system and directly sending                carbon dioxide in natural gas on non-permeate side                of the membrane separation to associated gas                pipeline network, when the carbon dioxide content                of the associated gas is 75% or more, the pressure                swing adsorption compressor is pressurized to 0.9                MPa and entering the pressure swing adsorption unit                and adsorption tower and undergoing adsorption by                the composite bed, where the resolved carbon                dioxide content is 95% or more; and (c) sending                obtained composite to carbon dioxide booster by                vacuum pump, where the carbon dioxide in the                natural gas is 5% or less into associated gas                pipeline network.

   TAKAYOSHI T
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 2021B5603Y
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a water-softening tank (12) which carries out water-softening of the hard water containing a hardness component by a weakly acidic cation exchange resin. The raw water supply unit (110) for regeneration supplies the raw water for reproduction which is a raw material of the reclaimed water used for the reproduction processing of the weakly acidic cation exchange resin. The trapping agent supply unit (120) supplies the hardness-component trapping agent which captures the hardness component to the raw water for reproduction. The reclaimed-water preparation unit (130) mixes the raw water for reproduction, and the hardness-component trapping agent, and prepares the reclaimed water. The reclaimed water is water-flowed to the water-softening tank, in the case of the reproduction processing.
   USE - Water softening apparatus for ion-exchanging calcium ion and magnesium ion contained in hard water to sodium ion using cation exchange resin to obtain soft water.
   ADVANTAGE - The water softening apparatus reproduces a weakly acidic cation exchange resin by a simple method using the chemical agent with a general household easy to handle which has little electric consumption. The adhesion of the adhering material resulting from a hardness component is suppressed in the inside of the water softening apparatus. The uselessness of the water at the time of from this, reproducing a weakly acidic cation exchange resin is eliminated. The adhesion of carbonate of the system inside of water softening apparatus is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The reclaimed-water preparation units are reclaimed-water storage tanks which store the reclaimed water. The raw water supply unit for reproduction is a raw water distribution flow path for reproduction which supplies the raw water for reproduction to the reclaimed-water storage tank. The trapping agent supply unit is a chemical agent injection-in unit which injects-in the hardness-component trapping agent to the reclaimed-water storage tank. The raw water supply unit for reproduction produces acidic electrolyzed water and alkaline electrolyzed water, and provides the electrolytic cell which discharges the acidic electrolyzed water as raw water for reproduction. The carbon-dioxide injection unit injects a carbon dioxide into the raw water distribution flow path for reproduction. The raw water supply unit for reproduction is a raw water storage tank for reproduction which stores the raw water for reproduction. The trapping agent supply unit is a chemical-agent injection unit which injects the hardness-component trapping agent into the raw water distribution flow path for reproduction where the raw water for reproduction discharged from the raw water storage tank for reproduction distributes. The carbon-dioxide injection unit injects a carbon dioxide into the position which becomes rather than the chemical-agent injection unit among the raw water distribution flow paths for reproduction in an upstream side in the case of the reproduction processing. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a water-softening-apparatus reproduction method involves carrying out water-softening of the hard water containing a hardness component by a weakly acidic cation exchange resin. The raw water supply unit for reproduction which supplies the raw water for reproduction which is a raw material of a reclaimed water used for the reproduction processing of the weakly acidic cation exchange resin. The trapping agent supply unit supplies the hardness-component trapping agent which captures the hardness component to the raw water for reproduction. The reclaimed-water preparation unit mixes the raw water for reproduction, and the hardness-component trapping agent, and prepares the reclaimed water. The reclaimed water is water-flowed to the water-softening tank. The reclaimed-water preparation units are reclaimed-water storage tanks which store the reclaimed water. The raw water supply unit for reproduction is a raw water distribution flow path for reproduction which supplies the raw water for reproduction to the reclaimed-water storage tank. The trapping agent supply unit is a chemical agent injection-in unit which injects-in the hardness-component trapping agent to the reclaimed-water storage tank. The raw water for reproduction is soft water. The hardness-component trapping agent is types selected from the group which consists of a citric acid, gluconic acid, an acetic acid, propionic acid, a capric acid, lauric acid, myristic acid, palmitic acid, lactic acid, malic acid, benzoic acid, a succinic acid, a fumaric acid, and a maleic acid. The water softening apparatus is equipped with the carbon-dioxide injection unit which injects a carbon dioxide into the position which becomes rather than the chemical-agent injection| unit among the raw water distribution flow paths for reproduction in an upstream side in the case of the reproduction processing is used. The raw water for reproduction is soft water or hard water. The chemical-agent injection unit adds the hardness-component trapping agent to the carbon-dioxide injection water obtained by a carbon dioxide being injected into the raw water for reproduction in the case of the reproduction processing. The raw water supply unit for reproduction produces acidic electrolyzed water and alkaline electrolyzed water, and uses the water softening apparatus equipped with the electrolytic cell which discharges the acidic electrolyzed water as raw water for reproduction. The raw water for reproduction is the hard water discharged without being electrolyzed with the electrolytic cell. The addition amount of the hardness-component trapping agent to hard water as raw water for reproduction is increased, and a reclaimed water is prepared compared with the case where the raw water for reproduction is soft water.

AU XU Q
   LIU R

   FAN X
   MA F
AE GANSU HUATING COAL & ELECTRIC POWER CO (GANS-Non-standard)

GA 202084941Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide conversion system comprises coal preparation unit (1), coal gasification unit (2), water gas conversion unit (3), synthesis gas cooling unit (4), low-temperature methanol washing unit (5) synthesis gas methanol unit, water treatment unit, waste heat generating unit, electrolytic hydrogen production unit, carbon dioxide methanol preparing unit, methanol rectifying unit, hydrogen collecting unit, oxygen collecting unit, air separating unit and sulfur recycling unit. Outlet of coal preparation unit is connected with coal feed inlet of coal gasification unit. Air separating unit is connected with air intake pipe. Oxygen outlet of air separating unit is connected to oxygen inlet of coal gasification unit. Gas outlet of coal gasification unit is connected to inlet of water gas conversion unit. Synthesis gas outlet of water gas conversion unit is connected with inlet of synthesis gas cooling unit.
   USE - The system is useful for processing coal-to-methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is economical; has high methanol output, low carbon dioxide emissions and excellent application prospects; and utilizes the energy source in the process and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide conversion system comprises coal preparation unit (1), coal gasification unit (2), water gas conversion unit (3), synthesis gas cooling unit (4), low-temperature methanol washing unit (5) synthesis gas methanol unit, water treatment unit, waste heat generating unit, electrolytic hydrogen production unit, carbon dioxide methanol preparing unit, methanol rectifying unit, hydrogen collecting unit, oxygen collecting unit, air separating unit and sulfur recycling unit. Outlet of coal preparation unit is connected with coal feed inlet of coal gasification unit. Air separating unit is connected with air intake pipe. Oxygen outlet of air separating unit is connected to oxygen inlet of coal gasification unit. Gas outlet of coal gasification unit is connected to inlet of water gas conversion unit. Synthesis gas outlet of water gas conversion unit is connected with inlet of synthesis gas cooling unit. Outlet of synthesis gas cooling unit is connected to inlet of low-temperature methanol washing unit. Synthesis gas outlet of low temperature methanol washing unit is connected to inlet of synthesis gas methanol unit. Carbon dioxide outlet of low-temperature methanol washing unit is connected to carbon dioxide inlet of carbon dioxide methanol preparing unit. Hydrogen sulfide outlet of low-temperature methanol washing unit is connected to sulfur recycling unit. Outlet of synthesis gas methanol unit and outlet of carbon dioxide methanol preparing unit are both connected to inlet of methanol rectifying unit. Waste water outlet of coal gasification unit is connected to inlet of water treatment unit. Outlet of water treatment unit is connected with water inlet of electrolytic hydrogen production unit and coal gasification unit. Oxygen outlet of electrolytic hydrogen production unit is connected to inlet of oxygen collecting unit. Outlet of oxygen collecting unit is connected to oxygen inlet of coal gasification unit. Hydrogen outlet of electrolytic hydrogen production unit is connected to inlet of hydrogen collecting unit. Outlet of hydrogen collecting unit is connected with hydrogen inlet of carbon dioxide methanol preparing unit. Coal gasification unit and synthesis gas cooling unit are connected to waste heat generating unit through waste heat pipeline. Waste heat generating unit is connected with electrolytic hydrogen production unit through power transmission line. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for working of the carbon dioxide conversion system for the coal-to-methanol process comprising collecting the oxygen produced by the electrolytic hydrogen production unit by the oxygen collecting unit, entering together with the oxygen from the air separation unit and the coal raw materials from the coal preparation unit into the coal gasification unit to carrying out coal gasification reaction, entering the waste water produced by the coal gasification unit into the water treatment unit, partially recycling back to the coal gasification unit, entering the other part into the electrolytic hydrogen production unit, entering the coal gas produced by the coal gasification unit into the water gas conversion unit to carrying out water gas conversion reaction to obtain synthesis gas, entering the synthesis gas into the synthesis gas cooling unit for cooling, entering the low-temperature methanol washing unit to performing purification treatment, entering the synthesis gas obtained from the purification treatment into the synthesis gas methanol unit to obtain crude methanol through a catalytic reaction, entering the hydrogen produced by the electrolytic hydrogen production unit into the hydrogen collecting unit for collecting, entering together with the carbon dioxide produced by the low-temperature methanol washing unit into the carbon dioxide methanol unit to obtain crude methanol through a catalytic reaction, entering the crude methanol produced by the synthesis gas methanol unit and the crude methanol produced by the carbon dioxide methanol unit into the methanol rectification unit for purifying to obtain a methanol product, entering the hydrogen sulfide produced by the low-temperature methanol washing unit into the sulfur recycling unit to obtain sulfur products, passing the waste heat generated by the coal gasification unit and the synthesis gas cooling unit to the waste heat power generation unit for generating the electricity, and delivering the generated electricity to the electrolytic hydrogen production unit for electrolyzing hydrogen production reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the overall structure of the carbon dioxide conversion system for coal-to-methanol process (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Coal preparation unit (1)
   Coal gasification unit (2)
   Water gas conversion unit (3)
   Synthesis gas cooling unit (4)
   Low-temperature methanol washing unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas methanol unit is 2.05-2.15. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide methanol preparing unit is 3.01-4.10.

PT P
PN CN109306307-A
TI Method of brewing Weining yellow pear fruit wine comprises preparing fermentation broth, performing inoculation fermentation, and subjecting to low temperature clarification.
AU XU C
   YU Z
   LIU X
   LI X
   GONG X
   ZHANG Y
   LIU B
   ZHAO H
AE GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
GA 201916500Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method of brewing Weining yellow pear fruit wine comprises preparing fermentation broth; performing inoculation fermentation; and subjecting to low temperature clarification.
   USE - Method of brewing Weining yellow pear fruit wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains fruit flavor and nutrients in wine. Inert gasses including carbon dioxide and nitrogen are added during reversing and sealing to avoid excessive contact between fermentation broth and oxygen, thus avoiding fermentation broth from browning.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The fermentation broth is prepared by washing yellow pear; pressing; pouring juice into fermenter; adding antioxidant; continue shaking; adding enzyme; determining sugar content and pH of juice; calculating amount of sugar and organic acid required to adjust pH of juice to 3-3.5, in which sugar content is 20-24 according to test result; adding calculated organic acid and sugar to juice; and standing overnight to obtain fermentation broth. The inoculation fermentation involves inoculating yeast in fermentation broth; inserting respirator; storing at 20-28 degrees C for 12-24 hours; observing start of fermentation through bubble; feeding into main fermentation stage when bubbles in respirator are uniformly generated; adjusting temperature of inoculating yeast fermenter at 16-18 degrees C for low-temperature fermentation; and monitoring sugar content of fermentation broth during fermentation, such that when sugar content is fermented, or specific gravity no longer changes, fermentation is over. The pressing and pouring is carried out before low-temperature clarification i.e. fermentation liquid which is completed by main fermentation is pressed or filtered; charging fermentation liquid into storage tank to obtain wine; adjusting temperature of storage tank at 4 degrees C; and clarifying under low temperature for 1 week. The low temperature clarification involves introducing gas into product obtained after pouring; filling product into tank; adding clarifying agent; and storing at low temperature. Preferred Component: The antioxidant is added at 50-80 mg/l. The enzyme is added at 20-30 mg/l. The gas to be introduced is inert gas including carbon dioxide or nitrogen. The clarifying agent comprises activated carbon, PMS, and activated bentonite at mass ratio of 1:(1-1.5):0.9.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02; D05-E
IP C12G-003/024; C12H-001/07

PT P
PN CN112899710-A; CN112899710-B
TI Preparing catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid by hydrothermally reacting zinc tin hydroxide precursor to obtain intermediate, and dispersing in ethanol solution containing indium nitrate pentahydrate.
AU LIU L
   ZHANG X
   CHEN Z
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 202167988T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid, involves (a) mixing zinc chloride, and tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate in deionized water to mark as solution (A), dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water to mark as solution (B), and dripping it to the solution (A) to form a white precipitate to obtain a cubic block zinc tin hydroxide precursor, (b) performing hydrothermal reaction of the original solution of the precursor, centrifuging and separating to obtain the sample zinc tin hydroxide intermediate, (c) mixing EDTA and thioacetamide to deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing the intermediate, performing hydrothermal reaction, and centrifuging to obtain a yellow stannic sulfide powder sample, and (d) dispersing it in an ethanol solution containing indium nitrate pentahydrate, and stirring to obtain the surface layer of indium-doped and partially oxidized sample, which is recorded as indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of a catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid, where the catalyst is an indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide catalyst which is used for preparation of the working electrode. The working electrode is used for preparing formic acid from carbon dioxide through an electrocatalytic reduction reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits excellent formic acid product selectivity on the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, has high (FEHCOO-) Faraday efficiency, and wide high active potential interval.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation method of a catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid, involves (a) mixing zinc chloride, and tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate in deionized water at a molar ratio of 1:1 and marking it as solution (A), and taking sodium hydroxide fully dissolved in deionized water and marking it as solution (B), and dripping the solution (B) to the solution (A) under magnetic stirring, and forming a white precipitate during the stirring process to obtain a micron-sized cubic block zinc tin hydroxide (ZnSn(OH)6) precursor, where the concentrations of zinc chloride, and tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate in the solution (A) are 10-40 mmol/l, and the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the solution (B) is 200-450 mmol/l, and the ratio of the total moles of zinc chloride, and tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate to the moles of sodium hydroxide is (1:6)-(1:10), (b) transferring and packaging the original solution of the ZnSn(OH)6 precursor synthesized in step (a) in a stainless steel reactor lined with PTFE, placing in an oven for hydrothermal reaction at 180-240 degrees C for 10-30 hours, and centrifuging and separating after reacting to obtain the sample ZnSn(OH)6 intermediate, (c) sequentially adding EDTA and thioacetamide to deionized water, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1 hour to mix well, adding the ZnSn(OH)6 intermediate prepared in step (b) for ultrasonic dispersion, transferring to the PTFE lining, putting it into a stainless steel reactor, performing the hydrothermal reaction at 100-180 degrees C for 3-10 hours, leaving still it after reacting, centrifuging, washing, and drying to obtain a yellow stannic sulfide powder sample, where the molar ratio of ZnSn(OH)6, EDTA and thioacetamide is (0.1-0.3):(0.6-1):(1.5-2.1), and based on 1 mole of ZnSn(OH)6, the volume of deionized water is 80-160 l, and (d) dispersing the stannic sulfide powder sample obtained in step (c) in an ethanol solution containing indium nitrate pentahydrate (In(NO3)3.4.5H2O), magnetically stirring in a 40-80 degrees C water bath for 18-36 hours, and finally obtaining the surface layer of indium-doped and partially oxidized sample, which is recorded as indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide, where the mass ratio of stannic sulfide powder sample indium nitrate pentahydrate (In(NO3)3.4.5H2O) is (1:1)-(1:8), and the concentration of indium nitrate pentahydrate (In(NO3)3.4.5H2O)/ethanol solution is 8-15 mmol l-1. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) an indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide catalyst obtained by the above method; (2) a working electrode containing the indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide catalyst; (3) a preparation method of the working electrode, involving (a1) mixing the indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide catalyst material with isopropanol and Nafion (RTM: Proton-conductive polymer film) solution, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a mixed solution, uniformly coating the mixed solution on a carbon cloth, and then drying to form the working electrode, where the ratio of indium ions-oxygen-ultra-thin-stannic sulfide catalyst material to isopropanol and Nafion (RTM: Proton-conductive polymer film) solution is 6 mg:0.35 ml:0.07 ml, and the Nafion (RTM: Proton-conductive polymer film) solution is a 5 wt.% Nafion (RTM: Proton-conductive polymer film) solution of perfluorosulfonic acid resin; and (4) a method for preparing formic acid from carbon dioxide through an electrocatalytic reduction reaction, involving adopting the working electrode to perform the electrocatalytic reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the hydrothermal reaction is performed at 220 degrees C for 20 hours. In step (c), the hydrothermal reaction is performed at 150 degrees C for 6 hours. In step (d), the temperature of the water bath is 60 degrees C, and the treatment time is 25 hours. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the concentration of zinc chloride, and tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate in the solution (A) is 25 mmol/l, and the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the solution (B) is 375 mmol/l, where the ratio of the total moles of zinc chloride, and tin(IV) chloride pentahydrate to the moles of sodium hydroxide is 1:7.5. In step (c), the preferred molar ratio of ZnSn(OH)6, EDTA and thioacetamide is 0.3:0.8:1.8, and based on 1 mole of ZnSn(OH)6, the volume of deionized water is 120 l. In step (d), the mass ratio of the ultra-thin stannic sulfide sample to indium nitrate pentahydrate (In(NO3)3.4.5H2O) is 1:5, and the concentration of the indium nitrate pentahydrate (In(NO3)3.4.5H2O)/ethanol solution is 13.4mmol l-1.

TI Intrauterine contraceptive device for human body            instrument, has V-shaped bracket whose outer end is            sleeved with six uniformly distributed movable            oxygen-free silver particles, and anti-friction lantern            ring connected with bracket.
AU WANG Y
   GAO H
AE HEILONGJIANG BAIJIN MEDICAL DEVICE TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 202249884P
AB    (CN216091002-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model model claims an intrauterine                device, belonging to the field of medical                appliance, intrauterine device, the solution by                placing the contraceptive device in the uterus, by                the temperature of the human body, promoting the                carbon dioxide solution in the deformation sleeve                is heated, and causing the carbon dioxide escaping,                so as to make the deformation sleeve expansion, at                the same time, by means of the friction generated                when the shape of the heat change cladding body is                changed, it can promote the outer sealing film to                break, and release the coating filler to the                uterine wall, on the one hand, by means of the                expansion of the deformation sleeve bag, it can                reduce the intrauterine device in the process of                uterus; the possibility of generating friction                between the movable oxygen-free silver particles                and the inner wall of the uterus, on the other                hand, by means of the release of the coating                filler, it can further reduce the possibility of                side effect caused by the friction of the uterine                wall and the movable oxygen-free silver                particles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of MAG silver ion intrauterine device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                contraceptive device of the MAG silver ion                intrauterine device.
   1V-shaped support
   2Symmetrical oxygen-free silver                particle
   3Movable oxygen-free silver particle
   4Anti-friction lantern ring
EA    NOVELTY - MAG silver ion intrauterine device comprises a                V-shaped support (1), the outer end of the V-shaped                support is connected with two symmetrical                oxygen-free silver particles (2), the outer end of                V-shaped support is sleeved with six uniformly                distributed movable oxygen-free silver particles                (3), between each two adjacent movable oxygen-free                silver particles is provided with antifriction                lantern ring (4) connected with V-shaped support,                the outer end of V-shaped support is connected with                a deformation sleeve bag, the deformation sleeve                bag is filled with carbon dioxide aqueous solution,                the outer end of deformation sleeve bag is paved                with a heat change cladding, the outer end of heat                change cladding is opened with multiple storage                grooves uniformly distributed, the storage groove                is filled with a coating filler, and the inner wall                of the storage groove is connected with an outer                sealing film located on the upper side of the                coating filler.
   USE - The device is useful for human body                instrument, device, appliance, in vitro diagnostic                reagent and calibration object.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces possibility of side effect                caused by the friction of the uterine wall and the                movable oxygen-free silver particles, and improves                the flow efficiency of the carbon dioxide aqueous                solution by change of the internal capsule                ball.
