PN CN113753880-A; CN113753880-B
TI Preparing three-dimensional graphene structure,            comprises e.g. substrate processing, fixing polyimide            paper on bottom plate, generating laser induced            graphene substrate, paving polymer powder on surface of            substrate and continuously paving powder.
AU GAO Y
   LIU F
   LUO S
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 2022024547
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing three-dimensional (3D)                graphene structure by 3D printing involves (a)                fixing polyimide (PI) paper on substrate, and                subjecting the PI paper to laser-induction to                produce a laser-induced graphene (LIG) substrate,                (b) layering the polymer powder on the surface of                the substrate, (c) performing laser selective                carbonization using a carbon dioxide laser on the                layered polymer powder to obtain a pre-designed                single-layer graphene film, (d) again layering                polymer powder on the surface of the formed                graphene, (e) again performing laser selective                carbonization using a carbon dioxide laser on the                layered polymer powder to obtain a pre-designed                single-layer graphene film, (f) repeating steps (d)                and (e) until the printing is completed and                obtaining 3D graphene structure and (g) soaking the                3D graphene structure obtained in (f) in an organic                solvent and performing heat treatment to obtain                optimized 3D graphene structure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 3D graphene                structure which is used as sensor, heater, energy                storage device, power generation device, adsorption                interface, resistance combustion interface and                catalyst (claimed) and in energy storage, catalyst,                environmental protection, sensor, biological                medical treatment, and optical and thermal                fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high-efficient and                reliable forming method of three-dimensional                graphene, at the same time of maintaining the                three- dimensional graphene shape, improving the                comprehensive performance of the three dimensional                graphene. The homogeneity of the material, specific                surface area, porosity and conductivity are greatly                improved, so that it is applied to the super                capacitor electrode material with higher specific                capacitance, energy density and power density. The                method can realize the direct preparation of three                dimensional (3D) graphene structure of various                appearances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing three-dimensional (3D)                graphene structure by 3D printing involves (a)                fixing polyimide (PI) paper on substrate, and                subjecting the PI paper ro laser-induction to                produce a laser-induced graphene (LIG) substrate,                (b) layering the polymer powder on the surface of                the substrate into a thickness of 60-80% with                respect to the graphene growth height, (c)                performing laser selective carbonization using a                10.6 microm carbon dioxide laser on the layered                polymer powder to obtain a pre-designed                single-layer graphene film, (d) again layering                polymer powder on the surface of the formed                graphene into a thickness of 60-80% with respect to                the graphene growth height, (e) again performing                laser selective carbonization using a 10.6 microm                carbon dioxide laser on the layered polymer powder                to obtain a pre-designed single-layer graphene                film, (f) repeating steps (d) and (e) until the                printing is completed and obtaining 3D graphene                structure and (g) soaking the 3D graphene structure                obtained in (f) in an organic solvent and                performing heat treatment to obtain optimized 3D                graphene structure. The relationship between the                laser processing power used and the thickness of                the powder is: L=a (93.5x-15.5) (I), where a                represents the correlation coefficient, which is                (60-80%), x represents the laser power in W, and L                represents the layering thickness of the powder                layer in microm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (c) or (e), the                  laser focal length, scanning speed and printing                  resolution of carbon dioxide laser are 33.1-43.1                  mm, 2.54-203.2 mm/seconds and 10-1000 ppi/inch                  respectively, laser power is 0.25-25 W and laser                  selective carbonization can be carried out under                  ambient atmospheric conditions at room                  temperature. In step (a), the substrate is a                  graphene film produced by laser-induced PI paper.                  In step (c) or (e), the laser selective                  carbonization uses a single induction. In step                  (g), the organic solvent comprises one or more                  combinations of N-methylpyrrolidone (NMP),                  N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF) and                  N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMAC). The step (g)                  involves performing heating treatment under a                  protective atmosphere at 800degrees Celsius for 2                  hours. In step (f), the three-dimensional                  graphene structure has an interlayer conductivity                  of 0.1-20.5 S/m, an interplane conductivity of                  0.1-250.5 S/m, a density of 30-200 mg/cm3, and a                  specific surface area of 10-500 m2/g. In step                  (g), the three-dimensional graphene structure has                  an interlayer conductivity of 0.1-50.5 S/m, an                  interplane conductivity of 0.1-500.5 S/m, a                  density of 10-50 mg/cm3, and a specific surface                  area of 10-1050 m2/g. In step (g), the                  three-dimensional graphene structure has cube,                  cuboid, cylinder, cavity, hollow structure, or                  honeycomb structure. Preferred Components: The                  polymer powder has particle size of less than or                  equal to 10 mum. The polymer powder is                  thermoplastic powder, including polyimide (PI),                  polyphenylene sulfide (PPS), polyetherimide (PEI)                  or polyetheretherketone (PEEK) powder.

PT P
PN CN113564105-A
TI Performing an upstream method for sperm washing involves absorbing fertilization culture solution, adding it to centrifuge tube A and B, placing tubes in incubator, subjecting fresh semen to liquefaction, performing routine semen inspection and adding liquefied semen to centrifuge tube B.
AU NUOMINGTU
AE INNER MONGOLIA MATERNITY & CHILD HEALTH (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2021D03761
AB    NOVELTY - Performing an upstream method for sperm washing involves absorbing fertilization culture solution, adding it to centrifuge tube A and centrifuge tube B respectively, and then putting the centrifuge tube A and centrifuge tube B into 37degrees CelsiusdegreesC and 5% carbon dioxide incubator to equilibrate for 6-18 hours. The fresh semen is placed at 25-37degrees Celsius for 30-60 minutes for liquefaction, and routine semen inspection is performed according to standard of fifth edition of WHO semen examination manual to obtain semen volume, sperm concentration, forward sperm percentage and forward sperm concentration parameter. The liquefied semen is added to centrifuge tube B after equilibration for 6-18 hours, mixed uniformly, centrifuged at 500g for 10 minutes, supernatant is discarded from tube B and precipitate is kept in centrifuge tube B.
   USE - Method for performing an upstream method for sperm washing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs an upstream method for sperm washing which improves quality of semen and solves problems of low sperm density, poor viability, and low survival rate during in vitro insemination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing an upstream method for sperm washing involves absorbing fertilization culture solution, adding it to centrifuge tube A and centrifuge tube B respectively, and then putting the centrifuge tube A and centrifuge tube B into 37degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide incubator to equilibrate for 6-18 hours. The fresh semen is placed at 25-37degrees Celsius for 30-60 minutes for liquefaction, and routine semen inspection is performed according to standard of fifth edition of WHO semen examination manual to obtain semen volume, sperm concentration, forward sperm percentage and forward sperm concentration parameter. The liquefied semen is added to centrifuge tube B after equilibration for 6-18 hours, mixed uniformly, centrifuged at 500g for 10 minutes, supernatant is discarded from tube B and precipitate is kept in centrifuge tube B. The portion of fertilization culture solution from centrifuge tube A is taken, added to centrifuge tube B where the supernatant is discarded, mixed thoroughly and centrifuged at 300g for 7 minutes, supernatant is discarded from centrifuge tube B, and precipitate is kept in centrifuge tube B. The remaining portion of fertilization culture solution of centrifuge tube A is added slowly along wall of tube to semen sediment at the bottom of centrifuge tube B where the supernatant is discarded and tube A is put back into carbon dioxide incubator with a volume ratio of 37degrees Celsius and 5%, tube B is put into carbon dioxide incubator with a 45degrees inclination angle at 37degrees Celsius and a volume ratio of 5% and allowed to stand upstream for 20-30 minute. The centrifuge tube B is allowed to stand upstream, upstream liquid is slowly sucked out above the sediment of centrifuge tube B and put into the centrifuge tube A to obtain upstream treated mixed sperm suspension. The obtained suspension is subjected to routine semen inspection according to standard of WHO Fifth Edition Semen Examination Manual to obtain processed sperm concentration, forward sperm percentage, forward sperm concentration, and motile sperm percentage parameters. The precipitate is stored in centrifuge tube B at room temperature until end of assisted reproduction treatment of this cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The volume ratio of fertilization medium in the centrifuge tube A and fertilization medium in centrifuge tube B is 3:2. The volume ratio of fertilization culture medium in centrifuge tube B and semen is 1:1. The volume ratio of fertilization culture medium drawn from the centrifuge tube A and fertilization culture fluid in the centrifuge tube B is 1:1. The fertilization culture fluid in centrifuge tube A and fertilization culture fluid in centrifuge tube B are commercially available semen washing or insemination fluid containing 5-10% human serum protein. The centrifuge tube A adds amount of fertilization culture fluid to centrifuge tube B, and all or remaining half of the amount in the centrifuge tube A are added according to amount of precipitation obtained in the centrifuge tube B, and if macroscopic precipitation is obtained in centrifuge tube B then all amounts are added, and then one-half of the remaining amount in the centrifuge tube A are added, and volume of remaining fertilization culture medium in the centrifuge tube A determines volume of sperm suspension finally obtained. The emptied centrifuge tube A is put into commercial dedicated semen upstream test tube rack or put in 100mL beaker at an angle of 45degrees, commercial dedicated semen upstream of the emptied centrifuge tube A is put in test tube rack or 100mL beaker in carbon dioxide incubator with a volume ratio of 5% at 37degrees Celsius and allowed to stand for 20-30 minutes upstream. The sperm suspension is final product and if forward sperm concentration is greater than or equal to 10x106/mL and it is used for artificial insemination, conventional in vitro fertilization and intracytoplasmic sperm injection technology, and if forward sperm concentration is between 5x106 to 10x106/mL after treatment, and it is used for conventional in vitro fertilization and intracytoplasmic sperm injection techniques, and if forward sperm concentration is less than or equal to 5x106/mL then it is used for intracytoplasmic sperm injection. The remaining upstream fluid in centrifuge tube A after insemination is used for sperm specimen retention, and placed in a carbon dioxide incubator with a volume ratio of 5% at 37degrees Celsius for at least 18 hours, and then sperm motility and microbial growth are observed, where sperm motility is routinely inspected according to standards of WHO Fifth Edition Semen Examination Manual to obtain percentage of active sperm overnight, and then quality of sperm is judged according to sperm survival index. The sperm survival index is obtained by (overnight motile sperm percentage/motile sperm percentage)x100%, where sperm survival index greater than 80% is regarded as good sperm quality after treatment and processing method is reliable, and if there are dense particles in the sperm culture medium after sperm sample culture and the sperm is not moving then it is considered that the sperm sample is contaminated, and remaining sperm suspension in centrifuge tube A and sediment in the centrifuge tube B are subjected to bacterial culture identification, and if bacterial contamination is identified as positive then the assisted reproduction treatment of this cycle is immediately suspended. The stored precipitate is used for sperm insemination, where second polar body is discharged less than 50% after 8 hours of conventional in vitro fertilization which is regarded as complete failure of egg activation, and then preserved precipitation is performed again to obtain sperm upstream liquid. The dense granular materials appearing in sperm culture fluid, turbidity and granular materials appearing in fertilization fluid and cleavage embryo culture fluid of fertilized ovum are regarded as insemination failure, and remaining sperm in centrifuge tube A is mixed. The suspension and the sediment in the centrifuge tube B are subjected to bacterial culture identification and if bacterial contamination is identified as positive then the assisted reproduction treatment of this cycle is immediately terminated.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN113502025-A
TI Expanded polystyrene and polyethylene (EPO) comprises composite particle core layer (comprised of foam hole nucleating agent, toughening agent, industrial oil substance and polystyrene) and composite particle skin layer (comprised of linear low density polyethylene).
AU HE R
   XIONG Y
   JIANG F
   YANG L
   ZHU M
   LIU H
   LU Q
   ZENG J
AE WUXI HUITONG LIGHTWEIGHT MATERIALS CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0606S
AB    NOVELTY - Expanded polystyrene and polyethylene (EPO) foaming beads comprises a composite particle core layer and composite particle skin layer, the weight ratio of composite particle skin layer and composite particle core layer is 3:97-20:80, the composite particle core layer comprises 0.01-0.1% foam hole nucleating agent, 0-20% toughening agent, 0-20% industrial oil substance and 79-99.9% polystyrene, where the composite particle skin layer comprises a linear low density polyethylene of 55-90% and 10-45% maleic anhydride graft modified hydrogenated styrene-butadiene block copolymer.
   USE - Used as erythropoietin foaming beads.
   ADVANTAGE - The foaming beads: uses the multi-layer coating structure as the foaming raw material, thus the leather layer has good melting and apparent quality, core layer has excellent foaming effect and rigidity and excellent size stability, ensure the multiplying power adjustability of the workpiece, when preparing foaming beads, uses supercritical carbon dioxide high temperature kettle type foaming method, simple process, low energy consumption, less pollution, good appearance quality of the beads, can reduce the forming pressure of the subsequent product and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing foaming beads, comprising (i) preparing the composite particle core layer and composite particle skin layer of the raw material and using co-extrusion process extruding the composite plastic filament containing core layer and skin layer, cutting into composite particles with length of 1.5-2.5 mm and weight of 1.5-3 mg through the granulator; (ii) mixing the composite particles prepared in the step (i), aqueous dispersion and dispersing auxiliary agent uniformly, then placing into the high temperature resistant sealed kettle for stirring and foaming as dispersing system, where the foaming process is: (1) continuously stirring the dispersion system in the reaction kettle, slowly heating the reaction kettle to Tdegrees Celsius, when the temperature in the reaction kettle rises to T-30degrees Celsius, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction kettle to the foaming pressure P through the booster pump, when the pressure in the reaction kettle rises to 50-70% P, the booster pump stops working, maintaining the pressure in the reaction kettle for 15-30 minutes; (2) continuously heating the reaction kettle, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction kettle through the booster pump, when the temperature in the reaction kettle rises to T-0.5degrees Celsius, when the pressure is increased to P, stopping heating and pausing the pressurizing pump to work, when the temperature in the reaction kettle slowly rises to Tdegrees Celsius, maintaining the temperature and pressure in the reaction kettle for 5-15 minutes; (3) placing composite particles in the mixture in the reaction kettle are soaked by carbon dioxide (CO2) gas, the mixture in the reaction kettle is discharged to the atmospheric pressure environment to obtain the expanded EPO foaming beads; and (4) further placing the prepared EPO foaming beads of step (3) into the foaming pipe of the steam environment to foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cell nucleating agent is zinc borate, stearate, talc, montmorillonite and fluoride, and the specification of the cell nucleating agent is 8-20 microm. The toughening agent is styrene-butadiene-styrene (SBS) block copolymer, styrene-ethylene-butylene-styrene (SEBS) copolymer, and/or styrene-ethylene-propylene-styrene (SEPS) copolymer. The industrial oily substance is colorless, tasteless transparent white oil liquid the kinematic viscosity at 40degrees Celsius is 30-110 mm2/second, and the flash point is higher than 200degrees Celsius. The industrial oily substance is 50-120# industrial white oil. The polystyrene is general purpose polystyrene (GPPS) or high impact polystyrene (HIPS), the molecular weight is between 20-35 million, the melt index is 3-11 g/10 minute, the bending strength is 400-1000 MPa and Vicat softening point is 90-110degrees Celsius. The linear low density polyethylene melting point is 100-110degrees Celsius, the melting index is 5-12 g/10 minute, tensile yield stress is more than 9 MPa, and Vicat softening point is 70-110degrees Celsius. The maleic anhydride graft modified hydrogenated styrene-butadiene block copolymer is changed into SEBS copolymer or styrene-butadiene rubber (SBR) copolymer. The dispersing auxiliary agent in the step (ii) is selected from butter, kaolin, stearic acid, the weight ratio of butter is 3-2% of the composite particles, the weight ratio of kaolin is 1-1% of the composite particles, and weight ratio of the stearic acid is 3 of the composite particles. The carbon dioxide gas in the step (ii) is changed into air, nitrogen, water vapor, and/or mixed gas of carbon dioxide.Preferred Method: The foaming temperature T of the step (ii) is 155-170degrees Celsius, the foaming pressure P is 3-5.5 MPa, stacking density 50-75 g/l, is the bubble hole of the foaming bead is 100-300 microm, and foaming bead skin layer thickness is 5-30 microm. The foaming pipe length of the step (ii) is 1.5-3 m, and foaming pipe temperature is 80-110degrees Celsius.

PT P
PN CN112225228-A
TI Preparation of ammonium sulfate involves cooling cement kiln tail waste gas containing carbon dioxide, washing with aqueous ammonia solution, reacting, mixing with phosphogypsum, grinding, leaching, and separating solid and liquid.
AU XIAO X
AE CHANGSHA ZHONGGUI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202109215L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ammonium sulfate involves cooling 100-200 degrees C cement kiln tail waste gas containing carbon dioxide to 60 degrees C or less, washing obtained cement kiln tail waste gas with 4-10% aqueous ammonia solution, reacting, obtaining a solution (A), controlling residual carbon dioxide in the gas to obtain clean emission, mixing the solution (A) with phosphogypsum, grinding, leaching, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining a solution (B), and solid residue which is used as cement raw material, mixing the solution (B) with calcareous material, then settling, obtaining supernatant (C) which is used to cool 100-200 degrees C cement kiln tail waste gas, and lower slurry, and performing solid-liquid separation of the lower slurry. The amount of aqueous ammonia is 1.5-2 times of the molar mass of carbon dioxide in the waste gas. The molar mass ratio of calcium sulfate in the solution (A), and phosphogypsum is 2-2.5:1.
   USE - Preparation of ammonium sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of ammonium sulfate by environmentally-friendly process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for preparing ammonium sulfate, which comprises a sequentially connected ammoniating decarbonization system, a phosphogypsum grinding and leaching conversion system, and a solid slag cement raw material conversion system. The ammoniating decarbonization system includes an evaporation cooling device and a washing device. The flue gas inlet of the evaporation cooling device is connected with the conveying pipe of the cement kiln tail waste gas. The flue gas outlet of the evaporation cooling device is connected with the flue gas inlet of the washing device. The solution inlet of the evaporation cooling device is connected with the evaporation solution tank. The solution outlet of the evaporation cooling device is connected to the solution collection tank. The flue gas outlet of the washing device is connected with an exhaust fan, the solution inlet of the washing device is connected with an aqueous ammonia tank, and the solution outlet of the washing device is connected with a liquid storage tank. The phosphogypsum grinding and leaching conversion system includes a phosphogypsum conveying device, grinding and leaching equipment and a stirring tank. The inlet of the grinding and leaching equipment are respectively connected with the storage tank and the phosphogypsum conveying device, and the outlet of the grinding and leaching equipment is connected with the slurry inlet of the mixing tank. The solid slag cement materialization system includes a filter press, a sedimentation tank and a solid slag storage tank. The slurry inlet of the filter press is connected with the slurry outlet of the mixing tank. The solid outlet of the filter press is connected with the solid residue storage tank. The liquid outlet of the filter press is connected with the liquid inlet of the sedimentation tank. The solid residue outlet at the bottom of the sedimentation tank is connected with the slurry inlet of the filter press. The liquid outlet in the intermediate of the sedimentation tank is connected with the solution inlet of the evaporation cooling device. The additive inlet on the top of the sedimentation tank is connected with the additive reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The evaporation cooling device includes a drying tower and at least two stages of evaporation cooling towers. The flue gas inlet of the drying tower is connected with the conveying pipe of the cement kiln tail waste gas, and the flue gas outlet of the drying tower is connected to the flue gas inlet of the stage (I) evaporation cooling tower. The solid outlet of the drying tower is connected with the storage tank, the flue gas outlet of the stage (II) evaporation cooling tower is connected with the flue gas inlet of the stage (II) evaporation cooling tower. The solution inlet of the stage (I) evaporation cooling tower is connected with the solution outlet of the stage (II) evaporation cooling tower. The solution outlet of the stage (I) evaporation cooling tower is connected with the solution collection tank and the liquid inlet of the drying tower. The solution inlet of the stage (II) evaporation cooling tower is connected with the liquid inlet in the intermediate of the evaporation solution tank and the sedimentation tank.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The cement kiln tail waste gas is cooled by evaporating ammonium sulfate solution. The evaporation process is carried out in an evaporation cooling tower. The ammonium sulfate solution is sprayed into the evaporation cooling tower, cement kiln tail waste gas is introduced into the evaporation cooling tower, then cooled waste gas is discharged from the top of the evaporation cooling tower, and the remaining ammonium sulfate solution is discharged from the bottom of the evaporation cooling tower. The ammonium sulfate solution is supernatant (C). The cement kiln tail waste gas is treated by a drying tower before introducing into the evaporation cooling tower. The solution for cooling the waste gas in the drying tower is the remaining ammonium sulfate solution discharged from the evaporation cooling tower. The cooled waste gas in the drying tower is discharged from the top and then introduced into the evaporation cooling tower. The ammonium sulfate solid obtained by cooling is discharged from the bottom of the drying tower. The cement kiln tail waste gas is cooled by least two stages of evaporation and cooling towers. The solution discharged from the evaporation and cooling tower of the latter stage is used as the evaporation and cooling solution of the previous stage of evaporation and cooling tower. The washing process of cement kiln tail waste gas is carried out in a scrubber. An ammonia solution is sprayed into the scrubber, and the waste gas is passed into the scrubber. The scrubbed gas is discharged from the top of the scrubber, and the solution (A) is discharged from the bottom of the scrubber. The washing process is performed by at least two stages of washing towers. The solution discharged from the latter stage of washing tower is used as the washing solution of the previous stage of washing tower. The solution (A) and phosphogypsum are mixed and ground by a grinding equipment, to form slurry. The slurry is stirred in a mixing tank, and introduced into a filter press for solid-liquid separation.

PN CN111850311-A
TI Recovering valuable metals from gold cyanide tailings involves taking an appropriate amount of cyanide tailings for wet grinding and pulverizing, then drying, grinding, and passing molecular sieve for use.
AU LIU Z
   ZHAN Y
   WANG K
   YIN S
AE SHANDONG HONGCHENG MINING GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2675A
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering valuable metals from gold cyanide tailings involves taking an appropriate amount of cyanide tailings for wet grinding and pulverizing, then drying, grinding, and passing 0.5 mm molecular sieve for use. The sieved cyanide waste residue is added to the ball mill, and at the same time add a certain amount of grinding media, continue to grind for 30 minutes, for use. The cyanide tailings are mixed with a certain amount of additives, and they are placed in an autoclave for autoclave treatment. The autoclaved cyanide tailings are put in a smelting furnace at a temperature of 650-680 degrees C, and used the heat generated by the combustion of coke and carbon monoxide to refine the cyanide tailings in the smelting furnace for 3-4 hours. The carbon dioxide gas mixed with carbon monoxide produced is passed during the refining process into the external condensation chamber to liquefy the carbon dioxide gas, and imported into an external dry ice machine to make dry ice.
   USE - Method for recovering valuable metals from gold cyanide tailings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recovered valuable metals from gold cyanide tailings, that improves the occurrence of gold and silver in the cyanide tailings, dissociates the wrapped gold and silver into a single state, and also make portion of the undissociated gold and silver, and silver minerals are decomposed into a porous structure, and increases the subsequent gold and silver leaching rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering valuable metals from gold cyanide tailings involves taking an appropriate amount of cyanide tailings for wet grinding and pulverizing, then drying, grinding, and passing 0.5 mm molecular sieve for use. The sieved cyanide waste residue is added to the ball mill, and at the same time add a certain amount of grinding media, continue to grind for 30 minutes, for use. The cyanide tailings are mixed with a certain amount of additives, and they are placed in an autoclave for autoclave treatment. The autoclaved cyanide tailings are put in a smelting furnace at a temperature of 650-680 degrees C, and used the heat generated by the combustion of coke and carbon monoxide to refine the cyanide tailings in the smelting furnace for 3-4 hours. The carbon dioxide gas mixed with carbon monoxide produced is passed during the refining process into the external condensation chamber to liquefy the carbon dioxide gas, and imported into an external dry ice machine to make dry ice, and the unliquefied carbon monoxide is returned to the smelting furnace through the connecting pipe for reaction. The refined cyanide tailings are soaked in a dilute sulfuric acid solution with a concentration of 0.8-1.5 mol/L for 2-5 hours, and stirred while immersing. The liquid and residue are separated after soaking, and washed the residue 3-5 times to reduce the loss of the solution containing valuable metal ions. The solution containing valuable metal ions are enriched and purified by unit of ion exchange or extraction. The enriched and purified extract is used to electrolyze the valuable metals or metal oxides in the final extraction to improve the comprehensive utilization rate of the cyanide tailings.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The filling rate of the grinding medium is 35% of the total amount of the cyanide tailings to be ground, and the grinding medium is zirconia balls. The additive is any one of sodium carbonate or sodium sulfide, and the dosage ratio of the additive to the cyanide tailings is 2:7. The leaching temperature is 80-110 degrees C, the stirring rate is 700 revolutions/minute, and the dosage ratio of dilute sulfuric acid to cyanide tailings is 18-25:1.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A01; M25-E02; M25-G20

   USE - The method is useful for preparation of draconis twig extract which is used in preparation of medicine for treating adenomyosis (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparation of draconis twig extract, involves performing using supercritical fluid extraction-steam distillation to prepare total volatile oil extract of draconis twig, where the extraction entrainer is ethyl acetate, adding draconis sticks into extraction kettle, using ethyl acetate as the entrainer, performing extraction under condition of passing carbon dioxide to obtain extract, adding the extract into volatile oil for extraction in vessel, performing steam distillation to obtain brown-yellow volatile oil, coarsely crushing the draconis twig, extracting, dissolving the volatile oil substances in the medicinal materials, transferring coarse powder to medicinal materials in the device, where the extraction temperature is 38-42 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 280-320 bar, the extraction time is 1.8-2.2 hours, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 13-17 ml/minute, the ethyl acetate is 0.22-0.28 ml/minutes, and the micro-boiling extraction is 0.8-1.2 hours, adding draconis twig coarse powder into extraction kettle at an extraction pressure of 300 bar, an extraction temperature of 40 degrees C, a dynamic extraction time of 2 hours, carbon dioxide rate of 15 ml/minute, extracting brown-red extract by volatile oil extractor to a steam distillation method for 1 hour, and separating the volatile oil is separated.    (5) dragon's blood extract, prepared by the method; and
   (6) combined extract comprising the draconis twig extract, the draconis extract and the dragon's blood extract.

PN CN108199057-A
TI Removing low temperature thermal storage catalytic combustion of complex organic waste gas, comprises passing the complex organic waste gas through the preheating chamber and evacuating the low temperature gas.
AU 
AE WUHAN HAOYAN LIGHTING ELECTRONICS CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201851269K
AB    NOVELTY - Removing low temperature thermal storage catalytic combustion of complex organic waste gas, comprises passing the complex organic waste gas through the preheating chamber and entering the light-off control unit, where the complex organic gas reaches the light-off temperature in the light-off control unit and the hot organic waste gas enters the catalytic combustion unit and the catalytic combustion process generates water and carbon dioxide and storing the released heat, exchanging heat by heat chamber with the heat transfer oil and stores heat, evacuating the low temperature gas (water, carbon dioxide and nitrogen) after heat exchange through the chimney and driving the heat transfer oil by the oil pump to realize the regenerator, the preheating chamber and the filter.
   USE - The method is useful for removing low temperature thermal storage catalytic combustion of complex organic waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates organic pollutant discharge, saves energy, improve the system adaptability of different VOCs content of exhaust gas and controlling complex component VOCs directly cause deactivation of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing low temperature thermal storage catalytic combustion of complex organic waste gas, comprises passing the complex organic waste gas through the preheating chamber and entering the light-off control unit, where the complex organic gas reaches the light-off temperature in the light-off control unit and the hot organic waste gas enters the catalytic combustion unit and the catalytic combustion process generates water and carbon dioxide and storing the released heat, exchanging heat by heat chamber with the heat transfer oil and stores heat, evacuating the low temperature gas (water, carbon dioxide and nitrogen) after heat exchange through the chimney and driving the heat transfer oil by the oil pump to realize the regenerator, the preheating chamber and the filter, where the heat cycle transmission process of the oil pump and the regenerator, generating the organic waste gas by the heat transfer oil through the filter cleaning is mixed into the VOCs exhaust gas, where the complex organic waste gas low temperature heat storage catalytic combustion process comprise a preheating unit, a light-off control unit, and a catalytic combustion unit and the heat storage unit, the preheating unit preheats the organic waste gas by using circulating heat transfer oil, the one end is an organic waste gas (VOCs) inlet and the other end of the organic waste gas outlet is connected to the light-off control unit through a pipeline, the catalysis combustion unit is equipped with a catalyst, the one end is closely connected with the light-off operation unit and the other end is connected to the oil bath regenerator and the hot gas after combustion is stored in the oil bath, the heat medium is heated in the hot chamber, and the cooled flue gas is discharged through the chimney, the heat storage unit comprises a filter, an oil pump, a regenerator, and a preheating chamber, the regenerator is passed through an oil pump and a filter and further the hot chamber is connected.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The light-off control unit comprises a flame-retardant layer, a supplementary fuel inlet and an igniter. the flame-retardant layer prevents the combustion gas from flowing back to cause an explosion and the supplementary fuel refers to when the light-off control unit temperature is lower than the ignition temperature of the complex organic gas is supplemented by automatic regulation to bring it to a light-off temperature, and the igniter is a component that completes ignition upon startup or when supplemental fuel is added. The supplementary fuel line is provided with a regulating valve connected to the temperature measuring component of the light-off control unit through a signal line, i.e. when the temperature of the light-off control unit is lower than a certain temperature and the supplementary fuel inlet is automatically opened. The igniter is provided with a regulating valve connected to the supplementary fuel regulating valve through a signal line, i.e. automatically igniting when the supplementary fuel is turned on. The regenerator comprises a multiple heat exchange tubes arranged in parallel and a multiple heat-enhancing fins are arranged on the gas side of the heat exchange tubes to enhance heat transfer of the gas to the heat transfer oil.

PN CN107744081-A
TI Populus alkali soda water useful for improving thirst, relieving dyspepsia and constipation is prepared by adding base, "Populus euphratica extract solution from pure water, citric acid, honey.
AU TOHTI T
   TUERHONG M
AE TOHTI T (TOHT-Individual)
GA 201818976A
AB    NOVELTY - Populus alkali soda water is claimed. The preparation of the Populus alkali soda water comprises adding base, Populus euphratica extract solution from pure water, citric acid, honey, mint into water to obtain various flavors of salicylline soda, and the preparation of the aqueous salicylic acid extract comprises removing the branches, sand and dust contained in the salicinine raw material, soaking the alkali of the poplar alkali, and then stirring and extracting to obtain aqueous salicinine extract, centrifuging and filtering to obtain Populus euphratica extract.
   USE - The soda water is useful for improving the thirst, balancing body pH, relieving dyspepsia and constipation, preventing uric acid excretion to prevent hyperuricemia or gout.
   ADVANTAGE - The soda water improves the nutritional quality of soda and has good taste, improves the nutritional quality of the carbonated water, supplement nutrients needed by the human body. The method is simple and retains the nutrients to a greater degree and has beautifying effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Populus alkali soda water is claimed. The preparation of the Populus alkali soda water comprises adding 1000 l base, 5-20 l Populus euphratica extract solution from pure water, and 25-30 mg citric acid, 30-90 kg honey, 0.5-0.8 kg mint into 1000 l water to obtain various flavors of salicylline soda, where the pH of the salicylate soda is 7-10.5 and the preparation of the aqueous salicylic acid extract comprises removing the branches, sand and dust contained in the salicinine raw material, soaking the alkali of the poplar alkali at room temperature for 1-5 hours, and then stirring and extracting at 300-1000 rotations/minute to obtain aqueous salicinine extract; (ii) adding the aqueous salicinine extract and a diatomaceous earth into a centrifugal separator, adopting a low-temperature environment, centrifuging at 3000-5000 rotations/minute and filtering to obtain Populus euphratica extract; (iii) adding citric acid, honey and mint into water, mixing 5-20 l Populus euphratica extract having pH of 7-10.5, placing at a temperature of 90-100 degrees C, sterilizing for 1-2 minutes, filling in aseptic environment, adding honey, citric acid and flavored salicylic acid soda to obtain honey citric acid flavor salicylate soda mixture of pH of 0.1-0.7 MPa and temperature of 1-4 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide gas, mixing uniformly, and aseptically filling to obtain honey citric acid-flavored salicylline soda water containing carbon dioxide gas, adding citric acid and mint into 5-20 l sap powder extracted from step (ii), adding 1000 l water, uniformly mixing, stirring and adjusting the pH to 7.0-10.5, sterilizing at 90-100 degrees C for 1-2 minutes aseptic and filling to obtain lemon mint flavored poplarine soda; or lemon mint flavored salicylate soda with 0.1-0.7 MPa, at temperature of 1-4 degrees C, adding Populus euphratica extract into 1000 l water, mixing and stirring uniformly, adjusting the pH to 7-10.5, sterilizing at 90-100 degrees C for 1-2 minutes, aseptically filling to obtain original soda poplar soda water or original flavor of soda poplar soda under the condition of 0.1-0.7 MPa, temperature of 1-4 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide gas, mixing uniformly and then aseptically filling to obtain original Populus soda containing carbon dioxide gas.
TF ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen; Laxative; Antigout; Metabolic.

PN CN114891937-A
TI Utilization of high-titanium and high-chromium            vanadium-titanium magnetite and            iron-vanadium-titanium-chromium smelted by blast            furnace-converter for iron-making, involves            constructing mathematical model for operation of the            blast furnace using new type of hot air smelting            magnetite.
AU WANG M
   LOU T
   SHEN Y
   CHEN D
   SUI Z
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2022A7781P
AB    NOVELTY - A process for synchronously and efficiently                utilizing high-titanium and high-chromium                vanadium-titanium magnetite and                iron-vanadium-titanium-chromium smelted by a blast                furnace-converter involves optimizing charge ratio                of blast furnace smelting, and using high-titanium                and high-chromium vanadium-titanium magnetite                concentrates including ordinary rich ore; using a                combination of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                hydrogen and oxygen with a new type of                carbon-enriched hydrogenation and nitrogen control                hot air for blast furnace smelting, constructing a                mathematical model for the operation of the blast                furnace using the new type of hot air smelting                high-titanium and high-chromium vanadium-titanium                magnetite.
   USE - Utilization of high-titanium and high-chromium                vanadium-titanium magnetite and                iron-vanadium-titanium-chromium smelted by blast                furnace-converter for iron-making.
   ADVANTAGE - The blast furnace-converter smelts high                titanium, high chromium-type vanadium-titanium                magnetite and iron-vanadium-titanium-chromium is                resource-increasing, energy-saving and                emission-reducing green cleaning technology. It                uses high-quality titanium concentrate to prepare                titanium product such as titanium-rich material                needed in high-grade titanium white production,                uses carbon-rich hydrogen-control nitrogen,                recycles carbon dioxide discharged by the                metallurgy enterprise, realizing energy saving and                emission reduction using the carbon rich                hydrogenation control nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A process for synchronously and efficiently                utilizing high-titanium and high-chromium                vanadium-titanium magnetite and                iron-vanadium-titanium-chromium smelted by a blast                furnace-converter involves optimizing charge ratio                of blast furnace smelting, and using high-titanium                and high-chromium vanadium-titanium magnetite                concentrates including ordinary rich ore, using a                combination of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide,                hydrogen and oxygen with a new type of                carbon-enriched hydrogenation and nitrogen control                hot air for blast furnace smelting, constructing a                mathematical model for the operation of the blast                furnace using the new type of hot air smelting                high-titanium and high-chromium vanadium-titanium                magnetite with rich carbon hydrogenation and                nitrogen control, comprehensively designing and                controlling the temperature field distribution and                reduction degree in the furnace, distribution, top                gas temperature and carbon monoxide gas utilization                rate, hydrogen gas utilization rate and                comprehensive utilization rate of (carbon monoxide                and hydrogen) reducing gas, based on numerical                simulation, selecting appropriate blast operation                parameters, optimizing smelting process conditions,                and making the blast furnace run stably, smoothly                discharging high calorific value gas, high vanadium                chromium molten iron and high titanium slag after                the smelting, using the emitted high calorific                value gas rich in carbon monoxide, little or no                nitrogen as a high-quality fuel, for fully burning                with oxygen in the hot blast stove to provide                high-temperature hot air and using as chemical raw                material or converting into fuel; recombining the                purified blast furnace gas with oxygen and                hydrogen, replacing the existing hot air with new                hot air of carbon-enriched hydrogenation and                nitrogen control to recycle carbon dioxide;                pretreating the produced high-vanadium and                high-chromium molten iron by carbon dioxide weak                oxidation to remove silicon and titanium impurities                in the molten iron, and strongly oxidizing in the                converter to obtain high-quality vanadium slag or                chromium slag, and using for preparing low-cost                ferrovanadium nitride, vanadium oxide, chromium                oxide high-grade products; pretreating the                high-titanium type blast furnace slag produced by                selective enrichment and growth on-line                modification, and separating high-quality titanium                concentrate from the modified coagulated slag, and                using in the production of titanium-rich products                of high-grade titanium dioxide. In the blast                furnace converter smelting process, the chromium                content in the ore is 0.61 wt.% chromium oxide, the                concentrate contains 1.5 wt.% chromium trioxide and                10-40 wt.% chromium oxide is fed along with the                vanadium slag. The hot air has a high temperature                and a large load of heat. The tuyere carries                pulverized coal into the furnace, and oxygen is                used as an oxidant to participate in the furnace.                The combustion reaction of carbon, and the heat                released strengthens the endothermic carbon                gasification reaction, promotes the conversion of                carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, accelerates                the indirect reduction of iron oxide in the                furnace, and shortens the reduction cycle. The                rising carbon monoxide gas accelerates the column                gas reverse flow and mass transfer and heat                transfer between the liquid/solid three phases                improve the smelting reaction rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The blast furnace                  smelting uses preferred furnace material                  proportion, high titanium, high chromium type                  vanadium-titanium magnetite concentrate                  proportion number range is 70-100 wt.% and the                  common ore-rich proportion number range is 10-0                  wt.%. The blast furnace smelting high titanium,                  high chromium type vanadium-titanium magnetite,                  using the carbon-rich hydrogenation control                  nitrogen ' new hot air containing the content of                  oxygen is 5-40%, the content of carbon dioxide is                  5-20%, the content of hydrogen is 5-20%, the                  content of nitrogen is 0-20% and the rest is                  carbon monoxide. The amount of hot air intake is                  900-1200 m3/thm. The carrying capacity of iron,                  and the coal powder is 0-250 kg/thm. The                  discharging blast furnace gas rich in carbon                  monoxide and the content of carbon monoxide is                  30-70% (volume), the content of hydrogen (g) is                  5-15%, content of water (g) is 4-8%, a small                  amount of nitrogen (g), and the rest is carbon                  dioxide (g). The content of carbon dioxide (g)                  after dehydration of the exhaust gas discharged                  from the combustion is 90-98% (volume). The                  vanadium(chromium)-containing molten iron smelted                  in the blast furnace having a vanadium content of                  0.20-0.8% vanadium, (0.20-1.0% chromium). The                  content of titanium dioxide in the discharged                  high-titanium type slag is 5-40%mass. The                  high-chromium vanadium-titanium magnetite further                  contains 0.2-0.61% chromium oxide ore and 1.5%                  chromium oxide.Preferred Process: Pre-treating the high                  vanadium (chromium) molten iron using carbon                  dioxide weak oxidation to remove 70-85% silicon,                  titanium and other impurities in the molten iron,                  and strong oxidation blowing in the converter to                  obtain high-quality vanadium slag, vanadium slag                  vanadium pentoxide grade 10-40 wt.%, (chromium                  oxide is 10-40 wt.% or more), adding further                  value, and using it for making high-end products                  such as low-cost ferrovanadium nitride, vanadium                  oxide, and chromium oxide.Preferred Properties: The titanium                  concentrate having a titanium dioxide grade of                  40-60wt.% and increasing the smelting utilization                  rate of titanium in magnetite is 0-50-70%, and it                  is used to further increase the value of the                  production of high-grade titanium dioxide                  titanium-rich materials, and titanoium dioxide                  grade is 90-95 wt.%.

TI Revamping ammonia-urea plant, comprises revamping            ammonia-urea plant having ammonia urea plant including            ammonia section and urea section, and revamping by            provision of export of low pressure steam at specified            pressure from ammonia section.
AU IOCCHI F
   MARRONE L
AE CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 2021C08883
AB    NOVELTY - Revamping an ammonia-urea plant, comprises:                (A) revamping ammonia-urea plant having the ammonia                urea plant including an ammonia section and a urea                section, the ammonia section including a front-end                for generation of a makeup syngas from reforming of                a hydrocarbon and an ammonia synthesis section,                where the makeup syngas is reacted to produce                ammonia in the ammonia synthesis section, and the                produced ammonia and at least part of the carbon                dioxide are removed from the makeup syngas in the                front end, are fed to the urea section for use as                starting material for the production of urea, the                urea section includes a high-pressure urea                synthesis section including at least a urea                synthesis reactor, a stripper, and condensation                stage arranged to condensate stripper vapors; and                (B) revamping by provision of an export of low                pressure steam (40) at a pressure of not greater                than 6 bar rel, preferably 2-6 bar rel, from                ammonia section to the urea section, for use in at                least one steam user in the urea section.
   USE - The method is useful for revamping an                ammonia-urea plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the fuel consumption of the                overall process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Revamping an ammonia-urea plant, comprises:                (A) revamping ammonia-urea plant having the ammonia                urea plant including an ammonia section and a urea                section, the ammonia section including a front-end                for generation of a makeup syngas from reforming of                a hydrocarbon and an ammonia synthesis section,                where the makeup syngas is reacted to produce                ammonia in the ammonia synthesis section, and the                produced ammonia and at least part of the carbon                dioxide are removed from the makeup syngas in the                front end, are fed to the urea section for use as                starting material for the production of urea, the                urea section includes a high-pressure urea                synthesis section including at least a urea                synthesis reactor, a stripper for removing                unconverted ammonia and carbon dioxide from the                reactor effluent, and a condensation stage arranged                to condensate stripper vapors containing ammonia                and carbon dioxide removed from the reactor                effluent, the urea section further includes a                recovery section for recovering unconverted ammonia                and carbon dioxide from the solution effluent from                the stripper, at a pressure lower than the urea                synthesis pressure, the ammonia-urea plant also                includes a steam network, including steam producers                and steam users, and steam produced in the ammonia                section is exported to the urea section and sent to                at least one steam user in the urea section, the                condensation stage of the high-pressure urea                synthesis section is a steam producer in the steam                network and produces low pressure steam (40) at a                first pressure using the heat of condensation of                the stripper vapors, the low pressure steam being                used internally in the urea section in at least one                steam user of the urea section; and (B) revamping                by provision of an export of low pressure steam at                a pressure of not greater than 6 bar rel,                preferably 2-6 bar rel, from the ammonia section to                the urea section, for use in at least one steam                user in the urea section. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for integrated ammonia-urea process                comprising producing ammonia in an ammonia section                by generating a makeup syngas from reforming                hydrocarbon and reaction of the makeup syngas to                produce ammonia at least part of the synthesized                ammonia and optionally removing carbon dioxide from                the makeup syngas during its purification are used                in a tied in urea section for the production of                urea, where urea is produced with a stripping                process at high synthesis pressure and includes                recovery of unconverted matter at at least one                pressure levels lower than the synthesis pressure,                the steam is produced internally in both the                ammonia section and the urea section for use in                steam users of the plant, the process comprising, a                low pressure steam at a pressure of pressure not                greater than 6 bar rel, preferably 2-6 bar rel, is                transferred from the ammonia section to the urea                section, for use in at least one steam user in the                urea section.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows scheme of the plant.
   Ammonia steam network (10)
   Power users (11)
   Thermal users (12)
   Urea steam network (20)
   Low-pressure steam (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The urea section is                  modified to accommodate the low pressure steam                  exported from the ammonia section to the urea                  section. The urea section includes a steam user                  of steam at a second pressure greater than the                  pressure of the low pressure steam transferred                  from the ammonia section to the urea section, the                  method includes modifying the urea section to                  produce steam at the second pressure for the user                  and balancing the input of low-pressure steam to                  the urea section with the low pressure steam                  transferred from the ammonia section to the urea                  section. The step of modifying the urea section                  to produce steam at the second pressure includes                  modifying the condensation stage of the                  high-pressure urea synthesis section to use part                  of the condensation heat of the stripper vapors                  to produce the steam at the second pressure. The                  condensation stage of the urea synthesis section                  originally includes a first condenser arranged to                  produce low pressure steam at the first pressure,                  and the revamping of the stage includes the                  installation of a second condenser arranged to                  produce the medium-pressure steam at the second                  pressure. The second condenser is installed in                  series and upstream the first condenser, so that                  the stripper vapors coming from the stripper pass                  through the newly installed second condenser and                  then through the original first condenser. The                  first condenser and/or the second condenser are                  shell and-tube equipment, preferably horizontally                  arranged of the kettle type, where the stripper                  vapors flow in the tube side and steam is                  produced in the shell side. The step of modifying                  the urea section to produce steam at the second                  pressure includes installation of a steam                  compressor which is fed with steam at low                  pressure and delivers steam at the second                  pressure. The ammonia-urea plant, the steam at                  the second pressure for the user is originally                  produced by mixing medium-pressure steam with                  low-pressure steam produced in the condensation                  stage, the method includes that the mixing is                  discontinued. The urea section includes a                  medium-pressure recovery section and a                  low-pressure recovery section and the steam user                  of the steam at the second pressure is a                  carbamate decomposer of the medium-pressure                  recovery section. The urea section includes a                  medium-pressure recovery section and a                  low-pressure recovery section and the steam user                  of the steam at the second pressure is a                  carbamate decomposer of the medium-pressure                  recovery section. The second pressure is 5-7 bar,                  preferably 6 bar or around 6 bar. The urea                  section is a carbon dioxide-stripping section                  where the recovery section includes only a                  section at low pressure, the method includes                  reducing the amount of heat transferred to the                  stripper thus reducing also the amount of low                  pressure steam that can be produced in the                  high-pressure carbamate condenser, and the                  missing production of low pressure steam in the                  urea section is balanced with the low-pressure                  steam transferred from the ammonia section to the                  urea section. The ammonia section is modified to                  increase the low pressure steam that can be                  exported form the ammonia section and to provide                  the low pressure steam for transfer to the urea                  section, preferably as a result of increase                  efficiency of LP steam utilization or as a result                  of increased heat recovery. At least part of the                  low pressure steam for balancing the input of the                  urea section is obtained from modernization of a                  carbon dioxide recovery section in the front-end                  of the ammonia section, such modernization                  resulting in a reduced specific consumption of                  regeneration energy for the carbon dioxide                  recovery section, preferably down to a specific                  consumption of less than 400 kcal/Nm3 of removed                  carbon dioxide. The method further includes the                  steam to carbon ratio in the reforming process is                  lowered, and preferably including that the                  ammonia section is revamped to operate the                  reforming process with a S/C ratio of less than                  2.7, preferably 2.6-2. The shift section is                  modified to operate with the reduced S/C ratio                  and/or where at least one steam turbine driver is                  changed to a motor drive resulting in reduced                  steam consumption. The urea is produced with a                  self-stripping or with an ammonia-stripping                  process and recovery is performed at two pressure                  levels, namely at a medium pressure and at a low                  pressure, the steam transferred from the ammonia                  section to the urea section is used for carbamate                  decomposition in the medium-pressure recovery, or                  urea is produced with a carbon dioxide-stripping                  process and recovery is performed at a single                  pressure level, namely at a low pressure, the                  steam transferred from the ammonia section to the                  urea section is used together with steam produced                  in the carbamate condenser of the synthesis                  section, to provide steam for low pressure steam                  users of the urea section.

TI Treating acid gas and sour water effluent streams            for producing renewable transportation fuel, involves            e.g. thermally oxidizing sour water and amine acid gas            streams from cold separation and fractionation section            of renewable transportation fuel process, and            contacting caustic solution or ammonia.
AU ZHU X X
   DE REN J
AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
   HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
GA 202218401X
AB    NOVELTY - Treating acid gas and sour water effluent                streams comprises e.g.: thermally oxidizing sour                water stream from a cold separation and                fractionation section of the renewable                transportation fuel process and amine acid gas                stream from acid gas treatment section of the                renewable transportation fuel process in a thermal                oxidation system; optionally recovering waste heat                from the flue gas stream in waste heat recovery                section; removing sulfur oxide, hydrochloride and                chlorine from the flue gas stream in a sulfur oxide                removal section to form a de-sulfur oxide outlet                flue gas stream comprises water, carbon dioxide,                nitrogen, oxygen, nitrogen oxide, dioxins, and                furans; and contacting a caustic solution or                ammonia based solution with the quenched flue gas                stream in a scrubbing section to form the de-sulfur                oxide outlet flue gas stream and a liquid stream                comprising water, sodium sulfite, sodium sulfate,                sodium bisulfite, sodium carbonate, sodium                chloride, ammonium sulfate and ammonium                chloride.
   USE - The method is useful for treating acid gas and                sour water effluent streams useful for producing                renewable transportation fuel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process; allows elimination or size                reduction of sour water stripper unit, waste water                treatment plant, and sulfur recovery unit; and                reduces cost of the chemicals used in treating the                effluent streams and content of equipment in the                complex.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating acid gas and sour water effluent                streams comprises: thermally oxidizing at least one                of a sour water stream from a cold separation and                fractionation section of the renewable                transportation fuel process and amine acid gas                stream from acid gas treatment section of the                renewable transportation fuel process in a thermal                oxidation system, where the sour water stream is                not treated in a sour water stripper unit before                being thermally oxidized in the thermal oxidation                system and the amine acid gas stream is not treated                in a sulfur recovery unit before being thermally                oxidized in the thermal oxidation system, thermally                oxidizing the at least one of the sour water stream                and the amine acid gas stream comprises thermally                oxidizing the sour water stream and the amine acid                gas stream in a thermal oxidizing section forming a                flue gas stream consisting essentially of water,                carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur oxide                (SOx), nitrogen oxide (NOx), hydrochloride,                chlorine, dioxins, and furans; optionally                recovering waste heat from the flue gas stream in a                waste heat recovery section; removing sulfur oxide,                hydrochloride and chlorine from the flue gas stream                in a sulfur oxide removal section to form a                de-sulfur oxide outlet flue gas stream comprises                water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, nitrogen                oxide, dioxins, and furans, where removing the                sulfur oxide, hydrochloride and chlorine from the                flue gas stream comprises quenching the flue gas                stream in a quench section to form a quenched flue                gas stream; and contacting a caustic solution or                ammonia based solution with the quenched flue gas                stream in a scrubbing section to form the de-sulfur                oxide outlet flue gas stream and a liquid stream                comprising water, sodium sulfite, sodium sulfate,                sodium bisulfite, sodium carbonate, sodium                chloride, ammonium sulfate and ammonium chloride;                or reacting the flue gas stream with a reactant in                sulfur oxide reaction section to form a reaction                section flue gas stream comprising water, carbon                dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, sodium chloride, sodium                carbonate, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate, calcium                chloride, calcium sulfate, calcium carbonate,                calcium nitrate, magnesium chloride, magnesium                carbonate, magnesium sulfate, magnesium nitrate,                chlorine, nitrogen oxide, dioxins, and furans,                where the reactant comprises sodium bicarbonate,                sodium bicarbonate. sodium carbonate.heavy water                2(H2O), calcium carbonate, calcium hydroxide and                magnesium hydroxide; and filtering the reaction                section flue gas stream in a filtration section to                remove sodium chloride, sodium carbonate, sodium                sulfate, sodium nitrate, calcium chloride, calcium                sulfate, calcium carbonate, calcium nitrate,                magnesium chloride, magnesium carbonate, magnesium                sulfate, and magnesium nitrate to form the                de-sulfur oxide outlet flue gas stream; optionally                removing nitrogen oxide from the de-sulfur oxide                outlet flue gas stream in an nitrogen oxide removal                section to form a de-nitrogen oxide outlet flue gas                stream comprises water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen,                oxygen, dioxins, and furans; and optionally                removing dioxin, furan, or both from the de-sulfur                oxide outlet flue gas stream or the de-nitrogen                oxide outlet flue gas stream in a dioxin-furan                removal section to form a treated outlet flue gas                stream comprises water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen                and oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises: at least one of contacting first                  portion of lean amine stream with acid recycle                  gas stream from a cold separator in the cold                  separation and fractionation section in a recycle                  gas amine contactor to form a first portion of a                  rich amine stream, contacting a first portion of                  a lean amine stream with an acid liquefied                  petroleum gas (LPG) stream from a de-butanizer                  receiver vessel in the cold separation and                  fractionation section in a de-butanizer receiver                  amine contactor to form a second portion of a                  rich amine stream, contacting a third portion of                  a lean amine stream with a lean acid gas stream                  from a sponge absorber in the cold separation and                  fractionation section in a sponge absorber lean                  gas overhead amine contactor to form a third                  portion of a rich amine stream, regenerating at                  least one of the first, second, and third                  portions of the rich amine stream in the common                  amine regenerator to form the lean amine stream                  and an overhead regenerator amine acid gas                  stream, separating the overhead amine acid gas                  stream into the regenerator amine acid gas stream                  and a reflux liquid stream in an amine                  regenerator overhead condenser, and refluxing the                  reflux liquid stream to the common amine                  regenerator; separating a purge stream from acid                  recycle gas stream from a cold separator in the                  cold separation and fractionation section in a                  pressure swing adsorption unit into a recycle                  hydrogen stream and a pressure swing adsorption                  (PSA) tail gas stream, combining the recycle                  hydrogen stream with the acid recycle gas stream,                  and introducing the PSA tail gas stream into the                  thermal oxidation system as fuel, contacting a                  second portion of a lean amine stream with acid                  LPG stream from a de-butanizer receiver vessel in                  the cold separation and fractionation section in                  a de-butanizer receiver amine contactor to form a                  second portion of a rich amine stream, contacting                  a third portion of a lean amine stream with a                  lean acid gas stream from a sponge absorber in                  the cold separation and fractionation section in                  a sponge absorber lean gas overhead amine                  contactor to form a third portion of a rich amine                  stream, regenerating at least one of the second                  and third portions of the rich amine stream in                  the common amine regenerator to form the lean                  amine stream and an overhead regenerator amine                  acid gas stream, separating the overhead amine                  acid gas stream into the regenerator amine acid                  gas stream and a reflux liquid stream in an amine                  regenerator overhead condenser, and refluxing the                  reflux liquid stream to the common amine                  regenerator; preheating the sour water stream                  before thermally oxidizing the sour water stream                  using steam from the process for producing                  renewable transportation fuel or from recovered                  heat from the waste heat recovery section;                  introducing a renewable feedstock to a guard bed                  to remove contaminants comprising alkali metals,                  oxygen compounds, sulfur compounds, and nitrogen                  compounds from the renewable feedstock, treating                  a renewable feedstock to remove additional oxygen                  compounds from the renewable feedstock,                  contacting the renewable feedstock with a                  hydroisomerization catalyst, a hydrocracking                  catalyst, or both in presence of hydrogen under                  hydroisomerization and hydrocracking conditions                  in a hydroisomerization and hydrocracking section                  to form a reaction effluent, and separating and                  fractionating the reaction effluent in the cold                  separation and fractionation section to form at                  least one renewable transportation fuel;
