introducing uncompressed PSA                  tail gas stream from a PSA unit connected to a                  hydrogen production plant to the thermal                  oxidation system as a fuel source; introducing a                  slip stream of a lean acid gas stream from a                  sponge absorber to the thermal oxidation system                  as a fuel source; passing the sour water stream                  through a first side of a primary heat exchanger,                  passing an exhaust vapor stream from the thermal                  oxidation system through a second side of the                  primary heat exchanger, where the exhaust vapor                  stream comprises the treated outlet flue gas                  stream, the de-nitrogen oxide outlet flue gas                  stream, or the de-sulfur oxide outlet flue gas                  stream, transferring heat from the exhaust vapor                  stream to the sour water stream, cooling the                  exhaust vapor stream forming a cooled exhaust                  vapor stream and heating the sour water stream                  forming a heated sour water stream, reducing a                  pressure of the heated sour water stream, passing                  the reduced pressure heated sour water stream to                  a flash tank having a pressure lower than a                  pressure in the primary heat exchanger, forming a                  vapor stream and a liquid stream, passing the                  vapor stream and the liquid stream to the thermal                  oxidizing section of the thermal oxidation                  system, and passing the cooled exhaust stream to                  an exhaust stack; passing a process stream                  through a first side of a secondary heat                  exchanger, where the process stream is combustion                  air stream and a boiler feed water or oil stream,                  passing the exhaust vapor stream to a second side                  of the secondary heat exchanger before passing                  the exhaust vapor stream to the primary heat                  exchanger to reduce a temperature of the exhaust                  vapor stream and to heat the at least one process                  stream and form a second cooled exhaust vapor                  stream and at least one of a heated combustion                  air stream, and a heated boiler feed water or oil                  stream, passing the second cooled exhaust vapor                  stream to the primary heat exchanger, where                  passing the exhaust vapor stream from the thermal                  oxidation system through the second side of the                  primary heat exchanger comprises passing the                  second cooled exhaust vapor stream through a                  second side of the primary heat exchanger, and at                  least one of passing the heated combustion air                  stream to the thermal oxidizing section of the                  thermal oxidation system, and passing the heated                  boiler feed water or oil stream to the waste heat                  recovery section; introducing the sour water                  stream to the flash tank forming a liquid and a                  vapor before passing the sour water stream to the                  primary heat exchanger, and compressing at least                  a portion of the liquid, where passing the sour                  water stream through the first side of the                  primary heat exchanger comprises passing the                  portion of the compressed liquid from the flash                  tank to the primary heat exchanger, reducing the                  pressure of the heated sour water stream                  comprises reducing the pressure of the heated                  compressed liquid from the primary heat                  exchanger, and passing the reduced pressure                  heated sour water stream to the flash tank                  comprises passing the reduced pressure heated                  compressed liquid to the flash tank; passing the                  first condensate stream to the quench section;                  and passing the second condensate stream to the                  quench section. In the process: the sour water                  stream comprises cold separator sour water stream                  from cold separator in the cold separation and                  fractionation section, de-butanizer receiver sour                  water stream from a de-butanizer in the cold                  separation and fractionation section, and a                  stripper receiver sour water stream from a                  stripper receiver in the cold separation and                  fractionation section; the amine acid gas stream                  comprises regenerator amine acid gas stream from                  a common amine regenerator overhead condenser and                  reflux drum; separating and fractionating the                  reaction effluent comprises separating the                  reactor effluent in a cold separator into a                  liquid hydrocarbon stream, a cold separator                  recycle acid gas stream, and a cold separator                  sour water stream, stripping the liquid                  hydrocarbon stream into a stripper overhead                  stream and a stripper bottom stream, separating                  the stripper overhead stream in a stripper                  receiver into a stripper receiver liquid stream,                  a stripper receiver off-gas stream, and a                  stripper receiver sour water stream, contacting                  the stripper receiver off-gas stream with a                  sponge absorber forming a sponge absorber liquid                  stream and a lean acid gas stream, separating the                  stripper receiver liquid stream and optionally                  the sponge absorber liquid stream in a                  de-butanizer column into de-butanizer overhead                  stream, a de-butanizer bottom stream, separating                  the de-butanizer overhead stream in a                  de-butanizer receiver into acid LPG stream, a                  de-butanizer receiver sour water stream, and a                  reflux stream, and fractionating the stripper                  bottom stream into at least one of a renewable                  jet fuel stream, a renewable diesel fuel stream,                  and a naphtha stream; quenching the flue gas                  stream comprises quenching the flue gas stream                  with at least one of air, the quenched flue gas                  stream, the de-sulfur oxide outlet flue gas                  stream, the de-nitrogen oxide outlet flue gas                  stream, the treated outlet flue gas stream, and                  water; the exhaust vapor stream is cooled in the                  primary heat exchanger to a temperature at or                  below a dew point to condense water from the                  exhaust vapor stream, forming a first condensate                  stream; and the cooled exhaust vapor stream is                  passed to a third heat exchanger before is passed                  to the exhaust stack, where the cooled exhaust                  vapor stream is further cooled in the third heat                  exchanger to a temperature at or below a dew                  point to condense water from the cooled exhaust                  vapor stream, forming a second condensate                  stream.Preferred Components:

PT P
PN CN113652392-A
TI Evaluating anti-inflammatory efficacy of cosmetics            based on co-culture of macrophage in vitro and 3D skin            model involves preparing sample made of cosmetic raw            material, culturing macrophage by culturing mouse            macrophage in complete culture medium in incubator and            detecting 3D skin activity.
AU GUO Z
   GAO S
   MA X
   WANG B
   LIANG Q
   WANG F
AE SHANGHAI JIYAN BIOMEDICAL DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI BEITAINI BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   YUNNAN BOTANEE BIOTECHNOLOGY GROUP CO (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D8949P
AB    NOVELTY - Evaluating anti-inflammatory efficacy of                cosmetics based on co-culture of macrophage in                vitro and 3D skin model involves preparing sample                to be tested made of cosmetic raw material or                cosmetic finished product. The macrophage is                cultured by culturing Raw264.7 mouse macrophage in                complete culture medium at 37plus minus0.5degrees                Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity                carbon dioxide incubator until confluence of mouse                macrophages reaches 70-80% for subculture, removing                the original medium during sub-culturing, adding                fresh medium, pipetting, collecting cell and                performing sub-culturing at a ratio of 1:3 or 1:4.                The 3D skin is cultured by putting 3D skin model                into 12-well culture plate for stable culturing,                adding 2ml maintenance medium per well, culturing                it at 37plus minus0.5degrees Celsius, 5% carbon                dioxide and saturated humidity for 12 hours to form                3D skin.
   USE - Method for evaluating anti-inflammatory                efficacy of cosmetics based on co-culture of                macrophage in vitro and 3D skin model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method evaluates anti-inflammatory                efficacy of cosmetics based on co-culture of                macrophage in vitro and 3D skin model in fast and                efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Evaluating anti-inflammatory efficacy of                cosmetics based on co-culture of macrophage in                vitro and 3D skin model involves preparing sample                to be tested made of cosmetic raw material or                cosmetic finished product. The macrophage is                cultured by culturing Raw264.7 mouse macrophage in                complete culture medium at 37plus minus0.5degrees                Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity                carbon dioxide incubator until confluence of mouse                macrophages reaches 70-80% for subculture, removing                the original medium during sub-culturing, adding                fresh medium, pipetting, collecting cell and                performing sub-culturing at a ratio of 1:3 or 1:4.                The 3D skin is cultured by putting 3D skin model                into 12-well culture plate for stable culturing,                adding 2ml maintenance medium per well, culturing                it at 37plus minus0.5degrees Celsius, 5% carbon                dioxide and saturated humidity for 12 hours to form                3D skin. The logarithmic growth of Raw264.7 mouse                macrophages are inoculated in multi-well culture                plate for 16-24 hours, fresh complete medium is                replaced to replace 3D skin with stable growth,                placed in multi-well culture plate, where blank                control group, induction control group and sample                group are set, and the 3D skin surface of blank                control group and induction control group is coated                with PBS, and 3D skin surface of the sample group                is coated with prepared sample, cultured for 1-4                hours, inflammation is induced by adding LPS to                control group and sample group respectively,                cultured for 16-36 hours, and then co-culture                solution is collected and stored. The 3D skin                activity is detected by using DPBS and PBS to rinse                sample on surface of the 3D skin, and MTT is used                to detect survival rate of the 3D skin tissue. The                co-culture inflammatory factor is detected by                selecting co-culture solution of sample group with                3D skin tissue survival rate greater than or equal                to 75% for inflammatory factor detection, and                respectively detects inflammatory factors in                co-culture solution mouse TNF-alpha and mouse IL6,                and content of mouse IL-1beta, mouse PGE2, human                L-1alpha, human IL-8 and/or human PGE2. The result                is analyzed by summarizing determination results of                the inflammatory factors, calculating ratio of the                secretion of inflammatory factors in test sample                group to secretion of inflammatory factors in the                induced control group, and obtaining relative                expression of inflammatory factors, which is                graphed by GraphPad Prism and result is expressed                as Meanplus minusSD. The one-way ANOVA statistical                analysis is used for comparison between groups, and                all statistical analyses are two-tailed, where p                less than 0.05 is considered to have a significant                difference, and is indicated by asterisk, and                sample has a certain inhibitory effect, and score                is 0.5, p less than 0.01 is considered to have an                extremely significant difference, and is indicated                by asteriskasterisk, test sample has a significant                inhibitory effect, and score is 1.0. The scores of                all inflammatory factors are summarized according                to scoring standards to evaluate anti-inflammatory                efficacy of test samples and then screened.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The cosmetic raw                  materials with water solubility greater than or                  equal to1000mug/mL are directly dissolved in PBS                  to prepare the sample, and cosmetic raw materials                  with solubility in water less than 1000mug/mL are                  dissolved in dimethyl sulfoxide or ethanol                  solvent to prepare sample. The spray, lotion,                  lotion, cream and other cosmetic products are                  directly applied to surface of 3D skin, where                  finished products of rinse-off and make-up                  remover are not suitable for the                  anti-inflammatory evaluation method. The dimethyl                  sulfoxide or ethanol is used as a solvent, where                  concentration of solvent is not more than 1 vol.%                  of total volume, and volume ratio of solvent in                  the control sample and sample is the same. The                  RAW264.7 mouse macrophages are cultured in                  DMEM+10% FBS complete medium. The 3D epidermal                  model purchases a supporting special medium for                  gas-liquid two-phase culture. The RAW264.7 mouse                  macrophages are seeded in a 6-well culture plate                  at a density of (0.5-2.0)x106 cells/mL with 1-2mL                  of medium per well, incubated at 37plus                  minus0.5degreesC for 16-24 hours under 5% carbon                  dioxide concentration and saturated humidity,                  where medium is replaced with fresh complete                  medium, and 3D skin is placed in 6-well culture                  plate inoculated with RAW264.7 mouse macrophages                  for overnight. The 3D skin of sample group is                  applied in amount of 20-100muL on surface,                  10-30mug/mL LPS is added, and 3 or more multiple                  holes are set up for each sample. The ELISA kit                  is used to detect content of inflammatory factors                  in mouse TNFalpha, mouse IL-6, mouse IL-1beta,                  mouse PGE2, human L-1alpha, human IL8 and/or                  human PGE2. The content of inflammatory factor                  mouse TNF-alpha, mouse IL-6, mouse IL-1beta, and                  mouse PGE2 in co-culture medium is detected, and                  content of inflammatory factor human IL-1alpha,                  human IL-8 and human PGE2 content in co-culture                  medium is detected.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC B12-K04E3; B04-F04B2B; B11-C08E2; B11-C08E1; B04-B04E; B14-R01; B11-C10; D05-H09; S03-E14H; S03-E09

PT P
PN CN110888025-A; CN110888025-B
TI Fault judgment method for geographic information system equipment based on machine learning involves collecting corresponding sample data of sulfur hexafluoride gas decomposition component and fault type of equipment fault gas chamber.
AU LI W
   ZHUANG Y
   TANG B
   HU X
   WANG S
   ZHU L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
   ELECTRIC POWER RES INST GUANGDONG POWER (CSPG-C)
GA 202022371N
AB    NOVELTY - Fault judgment method for geographic information system (GIS) equipment based on machine learning involves: (S1) collecting the corresponding sample data of sulfur hexafluoride gas decomposition component and a fault type of GIS equipment fault gas chamber from the domestic and foreign literatures and major fault examples of the power grid system; and (S2) applying machine learning to generate energy models for fault discharges of GIS equipment, numerically marking the different types of the fault discharge energy, subsequently introducing the Arrhenius chemical model to fit the probability function, applying a probability function to adjust the numerical marker according to the corresponding ratio of different gas components, then applying several machine learning algorithms to train the adjusted data set, and after training is completed, using k-nearest neighbor (KNN) as an energy judgment model for the GIS equipment fault discharge.
   USE - Fault judgment method for GIS equipment based on machine learning.
   ADVANTAGE - The fault judgment method uses the grid system to more accurately predict the type of discharge faults and locate and repair in time to maintain the stable operation of the GIS.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fault judgment method for geographic information system (GIS) equipment based on machine learning involves: (S1) collecting the corresponding sample data of sulfur hexafluoride gas decomposition component and a fault type of GIS equipment fault gas chamber from the domestic and foreign literatures and major fault examples of the power grid system; (S2) applying machine learning to generate energy models for fault discharges of GIS equipment, numerically marking the different types of the fault discharge energy, subsequently introducing the Arrhenius chemical model to fit the probability function, applying a probability function to adjust the numerical marker according to the corresponding ratio of different gas components, then applying several machine learning algorithms to train the adjusted data set, and after training is completed, using k-nearest neighbor (KNN) as an energy judgment model for the GIS equipment fault discharge; (S3) applying the machine learning to generate insulation defect models for the GIS equipment fault discharges, numerically marking the types of insulation defects for the different fault discharges and then introducing a contour model, i.e. the concentration ratios carbon tetrafluoride/carbon dioxide and (thionyl fluoride and sulfuryl fluoride)/(carbon tetrafluoride and carbon dioxide), stretching, followed by respectively placing on x and y axes, assigning the machine learning marker values to each sample point, subsequently applying several machine learning training data sets, and after the training is completed, using a Gaussian distribution model as an insulation defect judgment model for the GIS equipment fault discharge; and (S4) taking the GIS equipment failure judgment based on the GIS equipment discharge failure judgment model established in steps (S2) and (S3) by (a) using the judgment of fault discharge energy type to adopt the values of 1, 2, and 3 as machine learning standard values for corona discharge, spark discharge, and arc discharge, respectively, comparing the machine learning value of the concentration ratio sulfuryl fluoride/sulfur dioxide test point with the standard value, and taking the discharge energy type corresponding to the standard value close to the machine learning value as the high probability judgment type of the test point, and (b) using the judgment of fault discharge insulation defect types to adopt the values of 1, 2, 3 and 4 as machine learning standard values for conductive particle defects, insulator metal contamination defects, insulator external air gap defects, and high-voltage conductor protruding defects, respectively, comparing the machine learning values of the concentration ratio CF4/carbon dioxide and (thionyl fluoride and sulfuryl fluoride)/(carbon tetrafluoride and carbon dioxide) test points with the standard values, and taking the type of discharge insulation defect corresponding to the standard value close to the machine learning value as the high probability judgment type of the test point. The composition of the sulfur hexafluoride gas decomposition component includes a decomposition component concentration ratio in air chamber containing sulfuryl fluoride/sulfur dioxide, carbon tetrafluoride/carbon dioxide and (thionyl fluoride and sulfuryl fluoride)/(carbon tetrafluoride and carbon dioxide). The GIS equipment fault types are divided into a discharge fault energy and insulation defects that cause discharge faults. The discharge fault energy is divided into an arc discharge, a spark discharge and a corona discharge. The insulation defects that cause discharge faults are divided into protruding defects of high-voltage conductors, free conductive particle defects, insulator metal pollution defects and insulator air gap defects.

PT P
PN WO2022163521-A1
TI Non-alcoholic beer-taste beverage having enhanced            beer-like flavor and brewing-derived complex flavor,            comprises aroma composition containing myrcene,            beta-ionone, linalool, citronellol, geraniol and            alpha-eudesmol.
AU NAKAYAMA W
   MATSUSHIMA T
   NAKAKAWAJI S
AE ASAHI GROUP HOLDINGS LTD (ASAK-C)
GA 202298182F
AB    NOVELTY - Non-alcoholic beer-taste beverage comprises an                aroma composition containing 0.0475 ppb or more                myrcene, 0.000475 ppb or more beta -ionone, 5.8125                ppb or more linalool, 0.7025 ppb or more                citronellol, 0.115 ppb or more geraniol and 0.09                ppb or more alpha -eudesmol.
   USE - As non-alcoholic beer-taste beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The non-alcohol beer-taste beverage has                enhanced beer-like flavor and brewing-derived                complex flavor, excellent top aroma similar to beer                and an enhanced complex taste resulting from                brewing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for producing the non-alcoholic                beer-taste beverage comprising adjusting the carbon                dioxide pressure of the fermented wort to 0.05-0.25                MPa, vaporizing carbon dioxide gas and aromatic                components from the fermented wort by spraying the                fermented wort under reduced pressure, condensing                the vaporized aroma components to obtain an aroma                composition and adding the obtained flavor                composition to a non-alcoholic beer-taste beverage;                and
   (2) a method for enhancing the beer-like                flavor and brewing-derived complex flavor of a                non-alcoholic beer-taste beverage, comprising                performing the above-mentioned steps.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aroma composition                  comprises 0.0475-1 ppb myrcene, 0.000475-0.01 ppb                  beta -ionone, 5.8125-71.33 ppb linalool,                  0.7025-10 ppb citronellol, 0.115-1.4 ppb geraniol                  and 0.090-1.1 ppb alpha -eudesmol. The wort                  fermented liquid has a malt usage ratio of 50% or                  more. Preferred Components: The aroma component                  of the aroma composition is derived from the wort                  fermented liquid. The wort fermented liquid is a                  wort bottom fermented liquid. The non-alcoholic                  beer-taste beverage comprises dealcoholized wort                  fermented liquor. Preferred Method: The                  non-alcoholic beer-taste beverage to which the                  aroma composition is added is obtained by                  dealcoholizing a fermented wort liquor.
   EXAMPLE - After adjusting the gas pressure of the wort                fermented liquid (liquid temperature 0 degrees C)                obtained above to 0.20 MPa, the liquid temperature                was adjusted to 55-65 degrees C with a heat                exchanger, and desorption was performed under                reduced pressure of around 90 mbar. Then it was                sprayed into the gas tank to vaporize the carbon                dioxide gas and the aroma component. The vaporized                aroma component was condensed by cooling to about                20 degrees C to obtain an aroma composition. The                fermented wort liquid from which the aromatic                components were vaporized was heated to around 50                degrees C using a plate cooler. After that, it was                brought into contact with steam heated to around 50                degrees C in a vacuum column at around 90 mbar to                adsorb volatile components to the steam to remove                alcohol and volatile components. Then, the aroma                composition was returned to the dealcoholized                fermented wort to produce a non-alcoholic                beer-taste beverage containing the aroma                composition.

PN US2022042198-A1
TI Electrochemical method for preparing copper catalyst for reduction of carbon dioxide to high-value multi-carbon products, involves anodically halogenating copper foils, forming oxide in potassium bicarbonate electrolyte, and reducing.
AU KIM T
   PALMORE G T R
AE UNIV BROWN (UYBW-C)
GA 202221981E
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical method involves performing anodic halogenation of copper foils, performing subsequent oxide-formation in potassium bicarbonate electrolyte, and performing an electroreduction in neutral potassium bicarbonate to generate a copper catalyst.
   USE - Electrochemical method for preparing electrocatalysts, preferably copper catalysts for selective electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to high-value multi-carbon products (C2+).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides more efficient copper catalyst with high selectivity for targeted products at large scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included a method for preparing electrocatalysts, which involves:
   a. mechanically polishing copper foils;
   b. rinsing the polished copper foils;
   c. electropolishing the copper foils by chronoamperometry in 85% phosphoric acid at 1.5 V with a copper counter electrode in a two-electrode configuration;
   d. rinsing the electropolished copper foils; e. cutting the electropolished copper foils into 2x0.5 cm2 pieces;
   f. flattening the electropolished copper foils;
   g. covering a back side and part of a front side of the flattened electropolished copper foils with polyimide (PI) tape to define a geometric area of a working electrode; and
   h. wrapping the working electrode in polytetrafluoroethylene tape to prevent detachment of the PI tape, exposing an area of 0.35 cm2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The electroreduction in neutral potassium bicarbonate is performed by linear sweep voltammetry. The step of performing anodic halogenation of copper foils comprises applying an oxidative potential to electropolished copper foils immersed in an electrolyte containing halide ions. The method of preparing electrocatalysts further comprises dissolving potassium chloride, potassium bromide in de-ionized (DI) water to a concentration of 0.1 M, and performing anodic chlorination, bromination, and iodination on an electropolished copper foil immersed in 0.1 M potassium chloride, potassium bromide, and potassium iodide at 1.1 V, 0.18 V, and -0.2 V versus silver or silver chloride, respectively, in a three-electrode configuration using a potentiostat. The method utilizes platinum gauze as counter electrode, and silver or silver chloride electrode as reference electrode.

TI Purifying pepper oil essential oil removing precipitate comprises crushing picked dried prickly ash, heating prickly ash powder, introducing carbon dioxide to perform supercritical extraction, observing natural sedimentation, precipitating filtered soybean oil and filling.
AU YANG H
   MI T
   WANG Q
   LU L
   HUANG T
AE GUIZHOU XUANDE BIO-TECHNOLOGY CORP LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D06146
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying pepper oil essential oil removing precipitate comprises (i) crushing picked dried prickly ash and placing in the raw material box and placing the soybean oil in the storage box; (ii) heating prickly ash powder in preheater, adding into supercritical extraction tank, and introducing carbon dioxide to perform supercritical extraction to obtain the extract; (iii) mixing extract and soybean oil in mixing tank, stirring, where mixing ratio of the soybean oil and extract is 1:1-5 and the temperature of the soybean oil is 90-110degrees Celsius and soaking for 8-10 hours; (iv) allowing to stand in condenser, and observing the natural sedimentation of soybean oil to the insoluble substances in the pepper essential oil through centrifugal verification, and filtering through 30-60 mesh; (v) precipitating filtered soybean oil and centrifuging through separation tank again; and (vi) obtaining pepper oil essential oil, detecting content of peppercorns, where content is above 98, and filling.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying pepper oil essential oil removing precipitate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures pure essential oil is placed without precipitate and sensory state is good; and reduces flocculent substance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the extraction pressure is controlled to 18 Mpa, and the extraction temperature is 50degrees Celsius. The flow control valve is used to control the ratio of the extraction liquid to the soybean oil. In the step (iii), when mixing the temperature of soybean oil is 110degrees Celsius. The feeding speed of the centrifuge is 100 l/hour and the centrifugal filtration speed is 1000-1300 revolutions/minutes.

PN CN113504590-A
TI Preparation of light scattering porous micro-foamed structure polyethylene terephthalate for diffusion film.involves realizing carbon dioxide nucleation, reducing pressure and controlling foaming pressure and temperature and pressure relief.
AU ZHANG Y
   CHEN W
   WU L
   HU J
AE BOXUN OPTOELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY HEFEI (BOXU-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0576L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of the light-scattering porous micro-foamed structure polyethylene terephthalate (PET), involves dissolving the carbon dioxide (CO2) supercritical gas into the PET material, and uniformly mixing to form a homogeneous system. Through phase separation technology, CO2 nucleation is realized, and then the bubble growth is prevented by the pressure reduction method. Porous micro-foamed structure PET is produced by controlling foaming pressure, foaming temperature, saturation time and pressure relief, where foaming pressure is 40-50 MPa, foaming temperature is 60-100degrees Celsius, and the saturation time is 0.5-2 minutes.
   USE - Preparation method of light scattering porous micro-foaming structure polyethylene terephthalate for diffusion film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare porous micro-foaming structure PET that has enhanced light diffusion capability, and reduces the micro van der Waals force in the transparent PET molecule, toughness, flexibility, cutting stress around the film material, and warping degree, and improves the traditional diffusion film display brightness, transmittance, haze, ink adhesion, and surface impedance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) diffusion film comprising the porous micro-foamestructure PET;and
   (2) preparation method of the diffusion film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Film core layer of the diffusion film is made of ethylene terephthalate material. Anti-scratch layer of the diffusion film is made of optical protection resin.

PT P
PN JP2021155661-A
TI Separating lignin from black liquor discharged from steaming coniferous trees with soda, by adding acid and/or carbon dioxide to black liquor to precipitate lignin, dehydrating to obtain precipitate, suspending it, adjusting pH to precipitate lignin, and dehydrating it to obtain another precipitate.
AU TAGAMI A
   KANEKO R
   NURUKI Y
   TSUJI S
   MIYAWAKI S
AE NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
GA 2021B4868Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating lignin from black liquor discharged from steaming coniferous trees with soda, comprises (a) adding an acid and/or carbon dioxide to a black liquor to precipitate lignin under the conditions of a pH of less than 10 and at 60-80 degrees C, and dehydrating to obtain a lignin precipitate (A), (b) suspending the precipitate (A) obtained in step (a) and adjusting the pH to less than 3 to precipitate lignin, and (c) dehydrating and washing and dehydrating the lignin precipitated in step (b) to obtain a lignin precipitate (C).
   USE - The method is used for separating lignin from black liquor discharged from steaming coniferous trees with soda. The obtained lignin is used as a thermosetting resin, a dispersant, and an adhesive, and used as a phenol resin raw material or an epoxy resin raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can efficiently separate lignin from the black liquor discharged from the step of steaming coniferous trees with soda. The method can efficiently separate lignin dissolved from black liquor discharged from a step of evaporating softwood chips with soda.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The coniferous tree is Cryptomeria japonica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The dehydration in step (a) and/or step (c) is performed by a filter press device. In step (b), the lignin precipitate (A) is suspended and then an acid is added to bring the pH to less than 3. In step (b), the lignin precipitate (A) is suspended in an acidic solution to have a pH of less than 3. The washing and dehydration is performed until the electrical conductivity of the washing filtrate obtained by the washing and dehydrating in the step (c) is 0.2 S/m or less. Preferred Composition: The solid content of the black liquor is 10-40 %mass, and the solid content of the lignin precipitate (C) is 25% or more.

Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing three-dimensional porous carbon-supported bismuth sulfide/bismuth oxide composite catalyst comprising (1) roasting carrageenan and acid washing, washing with water, and drying to obtain sulfur-doped porous carbon nanosheets; (2) dissolving and dispersing the porous carbon nanosheets in water, adding bismuth nitrate solution and mixing for hydrothermal reaction; and (3) washing the precipitate obtained by filtering the solution obtained in step (2) with water, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the nitrate to the porous nanosheet is 1:5-10:1. Preferred Method: In the step (1), using 1-3 mol.L-1 sulfuric acid for acid washing, and the acid washing time is 12-24 hours.

TI Method for producing single-walled carbon            nanotubes, involves reacting aerosol in reaction            chamber to form composite aerosol including            single-walled carbon nanotubes, carbon monoxide, and            carbon dioxide.
AU LI H
AE CARBON NANOTUBE TECHNOLOGIES LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
   ATOM H2O LLC (ATOM-Non-standard)
GA 202163127X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves supplying, to a mixing                zone, carrier carbon monoxide and catalyst                precursor through a first inlet at a temperature                below a reaction temperature of the catalyst                precursor. The heated carbon monoxide is supplied,                to a mixing zone, through a second inlet such that                the heated carbon monoxide mixes with the carrier                carbon monoxide and the catalyst to form an                aerosol. The aerosol is reacted in a reaction                chamber to form a composite aerosol including                single-walled carbon nanotubes (402), carbon                monoxide, and carbon dioxide. A substrate (408) is                exposed to the composite aerosol to deposit a                single-walled carbon nanotube film on a surface of                the substrate.
   USE - Method for producing single-walled carbon                nanotubes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high-index colorless                polymer embedded single-walled carbon nanotube                films developed for high conductivity, high                transparency, operation stability, and mechanical                endurance. The produced single-walled carbon                nanotubes have improved high conductivity without                sacrificing transparency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus for producing single-walled carbon                nanotubes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of                the deposition system connected to the                apparatus.
   Single-walled carbon nanotubes (402)
   Roll deposition system (406)
   Substrate (408)
   Fresh substrate roller (410a)
   Deposited substrate roller (410b)

PN EP3822281-A1; WO2021099384-A1
TI Novel iron N-heterocyclic carbene compound for use as photosensitizer and/or photocatalyst in device selected from solar cell, water oxidation catalyst, proton reduction catalyst, carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, or photoredox catalyst.
AU WAERNMARK K
   PERSSON P
   GORDIVSKA O
   PRAKASH O
AE WAERNMARK K (WAER-Individual)
   WAERNMARK K (WAER-Individual)
GA 202153381E
AB    NOVELTY - An iron N-heterocyclic carbene compound (I) is new.
   USE - An iron N-heterocyclic carbene compound for use as photosensitizer and/or photocatalyst in a device selected from solar cell, water oxidation catalyst, proton reduction catalyst, carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, or photoredox catalyst (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) with improved properties can prolong the lifetime, increase efficiency, and reduce manufacturing costs of the devices.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An iron N-heterocyclic carbene compound of formula (I) is new.
   R1=alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, aryl, heteroaryl, amino, alkoxy, aryloxy, heteroaryloxy, carbonyl, ester, or amide;
   R2, R2', R3, R3'=alkyl, aryl, or heteroaryl, preferably Me;
   R4=alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, aryl, heteroaryl, amino, alkoxy, aryloxy, heteroaryloxy, carbonyl, ester, amide, carboxylic acid, phosphonic acid, cyanate, isocyanate, boronic acid, or their anions or cations;
   X=two mono-anions or one di-anion, selected from (PF6)2, (BF4)2, (BPh4)2, or SO4; and
   dashed bond=fused aryl, fused heteroaryl, or substituent selected from H, alkyl, cyclyl, heterocyclyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, aryl, heteroaryl, amino, alkoxy, aryloxy, heteroaryloxy, carbonyl, ester, amide, carboxylic acid, nitro, or halo, preferably dashed bond is H.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device comprising the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Component: In the device, compound (I) is conjugated to a semiconducting surface or particle, preferably titania (TiO2) or nickel(II) oxide (NiO).
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1=N(p-methylphenyl)2 or N(p-methoxyphenyl)2; and
   R4=CO2H, where R1 and R4 are electron donating groups.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 4 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed, i.e. (2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-pyridine-4-carboxylic acid)(2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-N,N-di-p-tolyl-pyridine-amine)iron(II) hexafluorophosphate (2); (2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-pyridine-4-carboxylic acid)(N,N-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2,6-bis-(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-pyridine-amine)iron(II) hexafluorophosphate; bis(2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-N,N-di-p-tolyl-pyridine-amine)iron(II) hexafluorophosphate; or bis(N,N-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2,6-bis-(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-pyridine-amine)iron(II) hexafluorophosphate.
   EXAMPLE - To a pre-cooled to 0 degrees C suspension of 151 mg 2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-1-yl)-N,N-di-p-tolylpyridine-4-amine hexafluorophosphate, and 118 mg 2,6-bis(3-methylimidazolium-1-yl)pyridine-4-carboxylic acid hexafluorophosphate in 4 ml dimethylformamide (DMF), 1.3 ml 1M potassium tert.-butoxide (t-BuOK) in tetrahydrofuran (THF) was added dropwise, and resulting mixture was stirred for 15 minutes under cooling to 0 degrees C. Then, 1 ml 2.1 M iron(II) bromide (FeBr2) in DMF was added dropwise to the reaction mixture. The reaction mixture was allowed to reach room temperature under stirring for 1 hour. The reaction mixture was acidified with 10 ml 1M aqueous hydrobromic acid (HBr) to pH of 2 and 10 ml saturated aqueous potassium hexafluorophosphate (KPF6) was added. The reaction mixture was extracted with ethyl acetate thrice of 50 ml for each time, combined organic phases was dried over magnesium sulfate, and concentrated in vacuuo. The crude product mixture was further purified by column chromatography (silica gel, acetone:water:potassium nitrate saturated aqueous solution=20:3:1). Products were separated into individual fractions and a subsequent acidification (only for compound (2))/KPF6 re-precipitation afforded (2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-pyridine-4-carboxylicacid)(2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-N,N-di-p-tolyl-pyridine-amine)iron(II) hexafluorophosphate (2) and bis(2,6-bis(3-methylimidazol-2-idene-1-yl)-N,N-di-p-tolyl-pyridine-amine)iron(II) hexafluorophosphate (4). Resulting precipitates were collected by filtration on a glass filter No. 4, rinsed with ice cold water, and repeatedly re-precipitated from acetonitrile/diethyl ether mixture. The resulting precipitates were dissolved in a minimal amount of acetonitrile and recrystallized by slow diffusion of diethyl ether. Products were dried in vacuo to yield 11 mg compound (2) as an orange-red solid with yield of 5% and 30 mg compound (4) as a green-yellow solid with yield of 12%.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E11 (Containing P and/or Si.); ; J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. TI Agricultural production method for breeding and planting interaction, involves planting predetermined crop in planting area, and conveying air with higher carbon dioxide content to planting area through air return system.
AU HE L
   FENG Y
   ZHOU X
   LIU D
   LI T
   LI F
   YIN Y
AE UNIV HUNAN NORMAL (UYLH-C)
GA 202158441S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a planting area (1) and a breeding area (2). The breeding area is provided with a fermentation bed (201) for animal life. The planting area and the breeding area are provided with a heat taking system and a return air system. A predetermined crop is planted in the planting area. The air with high carbon dioxide content is conveyed to the planting area through the air return system. An oxygen-enriched air in the planting area is driven to be conveyed to the breeding area through the heat-taking system when illumination is not enough to make the crop vigorous photosynthesis.
   USE - Agricultural production method for breeding and planting interaction.

TI Artificial intelligence greenhouse environment control system, has greenhouse administrator terminal device provided with mobile phone, and cloud server for receiving environment parameter uploaded by micro meteorological instrument.
AU ZHANG S
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 2021549551
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a micro meteorological instrument provided with a photosynthetic effective radiation sensor, a carbon dioxide detector, an air temperature sensor and a relative humidity sensor. A cloud server and the micro meteorological instrument are arranged in multiple temperature chambers by an internet. A greenhouse administrator terminal device is provided with a mobile phone or a computer of a greenhouse administrator. The cloud server receives an environment parameter uploaded by the micro meteorological instrument for performing corn growth model operation and outputting photosynthetic effective radiation, light supplementing period, carbon dioxide concentration recommendation value, air temperature recommendation value, environment management solution of air humidity recommendation value to the greenhouse administrator terminal device through the internet.
   USE - AI greenhouse environment control system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows a manager to obtain a management scheme through the mobile phone in a real time, and scientifically manages a corn growth environment. The system helps greenhouse management experience of plant to obtain professional environment control instruction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an artificial intelligence (AI) greenhouse environment control method.

The molten carbonate salt electrolyte comprises lithium carbonate. The solid reaction product comprises carbon, lithium oxide and lithium carbonate from molten carbonate salt electrolyte. The washer is configured to contact powdered form of solid reaction product with water to react some of lithium oxide with water to form lithium hydroxide. The filter is configured to separate lithium hydroxide from solid carbon and lithium carbonate in solid reaction product. The filtered solid reaction product is formed. The extraction vessel is configured to hold carbon dioxide, hydrogen-donor solvent and filtered solid reaction product. The extraction vessel is configured to reach target pressure of 1 atmosphere while filtered solid reaction product, carbon dioxide and hydrogen-donor solvent are held.
   USE - System used for producing and purifying carbon and recovering lithium carbonate.

TI Preparing improved cold medicinal composition            comprises e.g. crushing mint, rhizoma acori graminei,            cassia twig into coarse powder, using carbon dioxide            supercritical extraction, extracting and adding            ethanol.
AU SUN X
   LIAO P
   LIAO W
   LIAO Z
AE GUIZHOU JIANXING PHARM CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202136003Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing improved cold medicinal composition                comprises (i) crushing mint, rhizoma acori                graminei, cassia twig into coarse powder, using                carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, and                extracting, (ii) adding 10 times of 80% ethanol to                remaining medicinal materials, heating, refluxing                and extracting for 2 times, first for 2 hour, again                for 1 hour, combining the decoctions, filtering,                the temperature is 50 degrees C, concentrating to                thick paste with relative density of 1.30, (iii)                uniformly mixing the thick paste in the step (ii)                and the extract in step (i), adding polyethylene                glycol 6000, stirring uniformly to obtain the                molten liquid containing medicine extract and                matrix, (iv) adjusting the dripping head caliber of                the pill dropping machine, adding the obtained                molten liquid and the substrate into the storage                tank of the pill dropping machine, and (v) adding                the medicine extract in the storage tank of the                pill dropping machine and the molten liquid of the                substrate into the liquid paraffin.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing improved                cold medicinal composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The medicinal composition has high extraction                rate of volatile oil, deformation of dripping                pills, approximately round shape, slight tailing,                and slight adhesion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The medicinal                  composition comprises 82.3 g of notopterygium                  root, 164.6 g herba ephedrae, 123.4 g cassia,                  123.4 g schizonepeta, 82.3 g divaricate                  saposhnikovia root, 82.3 g Angelica dahurica,                  82.3 g rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 41.1 g                  rhizoma acorigraminei, 123.4 g radix puerariae,                  41.1 g mint, 164.6 g bitter almonds, 82.3 g                  Angelica, 164.6 g radix scutellariae, and 82.3 g                  platycodon flower. Preferred Method: In the step                  (i), extraction is carried out at 35-55 degrees C                  for 1-2.5 hours under 30-50 MPa, extracting flow                  speed is 10-19 l/hours. In the step (iv), where                  the inner diameter is 2 mm, the outer diameter is                  5 mm, the temperature of the dropping head of the                  pill dropping machine is heated and kept at 75                  degrees C, the condensing agent is cooled and                  kept at -2 degrees C to 6 degrees C. The                  extraction pressure is 35-45 MPa. In the step                  (v), the dropping distance is 4-5 cm, the                  dropping speed is 52-60 drops/minutes, and                  shrinking into pill. The extraction pressure is                  40 MPa. The extraction temperature is 40-50                  degrees C. The extraction temperature is 45                  degrees C. The extraction flow rate is 15                  l/hours. The extraction time is 2 hours. The                  amount of the polyethylene glycol 6000 is 4 times                  the total amount of the thick paste and the total                  amount of the extract in the step (i). Preferred                  Components: The composition is a solid                  preparation or a liquid preparation solid                  preparations are granules, capsules, tablets,                  pills, powders, and freeze-dried powder                  injections. The liquid preparations are mixtures,                  injections, and oral liquids.

AU GAYAM K K
   SINGH R
   GEHLOT A
AE UNIV LOVELY PROFESSIONAL (UYLO-Non-standard)
GA 2020C61385
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises multiple sensor nodes (101, 102, 103) which are installed in farming units, a farming gateway node (104), a cloud server (105), and a computer application (106). The sensor node consists of a processing unit. The camera module captures the images of plants. The pH sensor measures the pH level of water. The carbon dioxide sensor measures carbon dioxide in the air. The dissolved oxygen sensor measures dissolved oxygen. The light sensor measures light intensity. The humidity sensor measures humidity level. The NPK sensor measures nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium nutrients in the soil. The temperature sensor measures temperature. The water level sensor measures the water level. The actuator actuates light, pump, heater. The wireless communication module is for communicating sensory data.
   USE - System for optimizing yield in aquaponics using artificial intelligence and the internet of things.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has effective growth of plants and fishes and monitors environmental parameters and sent the farm's gateway node through wireless communication.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an aquaponics system.
   Sensor nodes (101, 102, 103)
   Farming gateway node (104)
   Cloud server (105)
   Computer application (106)

PN CN111920001-A
TI Preparing health food containing red yeast rice and propolis by preparing red yeast rice, pre-processing red yeast rice, using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology to propolis extract, and spray drying and granulating.
AU WANG J
   WANG L
   LI S
AE NANCHANG ZHIHE BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (NANC-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8424Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing health food containing red yeast rice and propolis, involves (a) preparing red yeast rice, (b) pre-processing the red yeast rice to obtain the red yeast rice mixture for use, (c) using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology to obtain a propolis extract, (d) after spray drying, mixing the propolis extract and an embedding agent in a mass ratio of 1:(3-7) to obtain a spray material liquid, performing spray drying and granulation operation, while spray drying, using the pretreated red yeast rice mixture as the core particle carrier, adding to a spray drying equipment, where the mass ratio of the spray material liquid and the red yeast rice mixture is controlled to be 1:(2-8), and (e) inspecting the existing products in qualified packaging.
   USE - The method is used for preparing health food containing red yeast rice and propolis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the composition of the fermentation medium of Monascus, save costs, makes full use of waste, flavonoids in propolis raw materials and other health-care active ingredients, inhibits the growth of the mycotoxin citrinin, and provides more comprehensively nutrition and health care effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The red yeast rice in step (a) is prepared by fermentation of Monascus, and the preparation process of red yeast rice in step (a) comprises inoculating the Monascus strains onto a slant medium, culturing at 31-36 degrees C for 4-6 days, and obtaining a bacterial solution concentration of 3-8x 106-8 colony-forming units (CFU)/ml, and adding solid fermentation medium comprising 30-70 wt.% rice, 5-10 wt.% rice bran, and 10-40 wt.% propolis residue, soaking in water for 12-24 hours, adjusting pH and packing into 500 ml Erlenmeyer flasks, where the filling amount is 15-25%, using steam sterilization, sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, inoculating 5-10% Monascus after sterilization, fermenting and culturing at 32-37 degrees C for 5-8 days, and placing in a constant temperature and humidity incubator at a temperature of 20-25 degrees C and at a relative humidity of 78-85% for 25-35 days. The step (c) uses supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology to obtain propolis extract, where the specific operation involves: after the propolis raw material is pretreated, adding to a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment for extraction, adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid at a temperature of 45-65 degrees C, a pressure of 2-4 MPa, and a carbon dioxide flow rate of 350-720 l/hour, and maintaining the carbon dioxide flow rate for 15-25 minutes, and extracting at an extraction temperature of 45-55 degrees C, pressure of 30-45 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 250-350 l/hour for 3-4 hours, adding an ethanol entrainer with a concentration of 75-95% at a flow rate of 20-35 ml/minute, and after the extraction is completed, separating the propolis extract. Preferred Conditions: The spray drying temperature in step (d), during the spray drying and granulation process, is 30-55 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C, and the water content of the particles obtained after spray drying and granulation is less than 8%. Preferred Components: The content of lovastatin in the red yeast rice product in step (a) is 0.80-1.56 g/100 g and the content of the citrinin toxin is less than 0.03 ppm. Preferred Properties: The red yeast rice mixture has a particle size of 125-150 mesh, preferably 130-140 mesh.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The embedding agent in step (d) is cyclodextrin, maltodextrin, and/or modified starch.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN IN202011038685-A
TI Bio toilet waste water testing process, involves making clear about water quality and standard, and identifying testing procedure and water usage after testing, so that wastewater is utilizes in washing or track washing or somewhere else.
AU JAIN R
AE JAIN R (JAIN-Individual)
GA 2020A4061V
AB    NOVELTY - The bio toilet waste water testing process involves making clear about the water quality and standard, and identifying the testing procedure and water usage after testing, so that the wastewater is utilizes in washing or track washing or somewhere else. A toilet is provided in which biological degradation of human waste by inoculums takes place. The name of bio-toilet bacteria is anaerobic bacteria. The anaerobic digestion is carried out by converting large polymers into simpler monomers called hydrolysis, converting simple monomers into volatile fatty acids called acidogenesis, converting volatile fatty acids into acetic acid carbon dioxide, hydrogen called acetogenesis and acetate, and converting hydrogen into methane and carbon dioxide called methanogenesis.
   USE - Bio toilet waste water testing process. Can also be used for wastewater coming from agricultural and industrial sources.
   ADVANTAGE - The primary level of treatment uses screens and settling tanks to remove the majority of solids. The primary treatment removes a significant amount of harmful substances from wastewater, which ensures that all harmful pollutants are removed. The secondary treatment of wastewater uses bacteria to digest the remaining pollutants. The oxygen helps the bacteria to digest the pollutants faster.

PN CN111449157-A
TI Preparing sea buckthorn instant tea, by adding sea            buckthorn leaf powder and chicory powder to water,            adding fermentation agent, fermenting in current            fermentation device passing carbon dioxide gas, sealing            and boiling fermentation product.
AU ZHANG X
   GUO H
   YAO Y
   LI J
AE SHANXI JINKEHAI BIOLOGICAL PROD CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202075196S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sea buckthorn instant                tea, involves (a) performing fixation treatment on                sea buckthorn leaves, rolling, drying and crushing                to obtain sea buckthorn leaf powder, (b) adding                water to sea buckthorn leaf powder and chicory                powder to obtain a mixture, adding a fermentation                agent to mixture, adding the mixture to current                fermentation device, so that anode and cathode in                the current fermentation device are simultaneously                submerged in the mixture, passing through 20-40 mA                direct current and fermenting at 33-37 degrees C                for 15-20 days to obtain a current fermentation                product, (c) introducing carbon dioxide gas into                the current fermentation device, replacing all gas                and sealing for 10-12 days to obtain sea buckthorn                leaf preservation fermentation product, and (d)                adding water to sea buckthorn leaf preservation                fermentation product for boiling and                extracting.
   USE - Method for preparing sea buckthorn instant                tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the quality and efficacy                of instant tea, ensures excellent extraction                effect, does not require special equipment, and is                simple, economical, environmentally-friendly and                suitable for large-scale production, and the                prepared instant tea is rich in nutrients and trace                functional ingredients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The mixture further                  comprises glucose, egg white powder,                  chloramphenicol and sodium chloride. The                  fermentation agent comprises yeast preparation                  and sweet koji or Aspergillus oryzae. Preferred                  Composition: The mixture comprises 92-96 wt.% sea                  buckthorn leaf powder, 0.5-1.5 wt.% chicory                  powder, 3-5 wt.% glucose, 1-2 wt.% egg white                  powder, 0.01-0.02 wt.% chloramphenicol and                  0.5-1.5 wt.% sodium chloride. Preferred Method:                  The method involves boiling the fermentation                  product with water and extracting for 40-50                  minutes, and performing centrifugal filtration                  after extraction to obtain the filtrate. The                  method involves boiling the solid material                  obtained after centrifugation with water for                  secondary extraction, and combining obtained two                  filtrates. The method involves concentrating the                  filtrate obtained after extraction under reduced                  pressure and drying to obtain sea buckthorn                  instant tea powder. The drying is freeze drying                  or spray drying.

   JUNG G S

   KIMSOOHYUNG

GA 202050304T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves starting a fast warm-up mode, while comparing an inlet side temperature and a reference temperature of the exhaust gas reduction device in the fast warm-up mode. The opening amount of the back pressure regulating valve is lowered to increase the temperature of the exhaust gas flowing into the exhaust gas reducing device to a first target temperature when the inlet side temperature of the exhaust gas reducing device is lower than the reference temperature.
   USE - Method for reducing exhaust gas, such as carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, or nitrogen oxide, in a vehicle through the control device.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust gas reduction method improves the purification efficiency of exhaust gas by controlling the opening amount of the back pressure regulating valve to shorten the time to reach the catalyst activation temperature of the exhaust gas reduction device.

TI Turbomachine of closed, indirect-heated Rankine cycle to compress supercritical carbon dioxide, has inlet, inducerless radial impeller having blades, and fully vaneless diffuser in fluid flow series.
AU GOODHAND M N
   BUNCE I M
AE ROLLS-ROYCE PLC (RORO-C)
GA 202017452W
AB    NOVELTY - The turbomachine (105) has an inlet (201), an inducerless radial impeller (202) having the multiple blades, and a fully vaneless diffuser (203) in fluid flow series. The inlet is radially flared to induce a radial component in flow before an entry to the impeller. A hub hade angle of the impeller at the entry is from 50 to 70 degrees. Each of the multiple blades is a backswept blade. The multiple blades comprise a set of main blades (211), and a set of splitter blades (212). A meridional chord length of the splitter blades is 70 percent of a meridional chord length of the main blades. The impeller comprises one splitter blade for each main blade.
   USE - Turbomachine of closed, indirect-heated Rankine cycle (claimed) to compress supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The radial impeller continues to operate stably with little or no pressure drop should it enter stall. The widest possible operating range is provided due to increased stability margin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating the turbomachine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the plan view of compressor and annulus lines of the compressor.

   Inlet (201)

   Fully vaneless diffuser (203)

   Splitter blade (212)

TI Welding method of soil-covered tank wall plate involves processing double V-shaped composite groove on tank wall plate, and welding tank wall plate by mixed gas shielded welding, using mixed gas comprising carbon dioxide and argon gas.
AU ZHANG L
   NING B
   CHEN Z
   WU D
   ZHANG K
   LI H
AE SINOPEC HENAN PETROLEUM ENG & CONSTR CO (SNPC-C)
GA 2020084795
AB    NOVELTY - Welding method of soil-covered tank wall plate involves processing a double V-shaped composite groove on a tank wall plate, and performing horizontal welding and vertical welding of tank wall plate by mixed gas shielded welding. The shielding gas of the mixed gas shielded welding is a mixed gas comprising 75-85 vol.% carbon dioxide and 15-25 vol.% argon gas. The double V-shaped composite groove includes a blunt edge, a lower grove and an upper groove. The intersection of the lower groove and the upper groove is the inflection point of the groove. The groove angle of the lower groove is 42.5-47.5 degrees . The groove angle of the upper groove is 3.5-6.5 degrees . The length of the blunt edge is 0.5-1.5 mm. The distance between the inflection point of the groove and the bottom of the tank wall is 4-6 mm.
   USE - Welding method of soil-covered tank wall plat.
   ADVANTAGE - The soil-covered tank wall plate is economically welded with improved quality, and reduced welding spatter. The welding efficiency is improved by 30%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The thickness of tank wall plate is 12-30 mm.

PN US2020002633-A1; WO2020005783-A1; AU2019295623-A1; CN112292196-A; EP3813988-A1; US11034903-B2; AU2019295623-B2; EP3813988-A4
TI Treating a natural gas stream by sending natural            gas stream through temperature swing adsorption process            to remove water and heavy hydrocarbons, and sending            partially treated gas stream through temperature            pressure swing adsorption process.
AU DOONG S
   DAVIS M M
   SHARMA B C
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 202001564V
AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas stream is treated by sending                natural gas stream (5) through a 1st adsorbent bed                to remove water and heavy hydrocarbons ( greater                than or equal to 8C) to produce a partially treated                gas stream (15) where the 1st adsorbent bed is                regenerated by a temperature swing adsorption                process; and sending the partially treated gas                stream through a 2nd adsorption bed to remove                carbon dioxide and lighter hydrocarbons ( less than                or equal to 7C) to produce a purified natural gas                stream where the 2nd adsorption bed is regenerated                by temperature pressure swing adsorption                process.
   USE - Treatment of natural gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides adsorption-based process                to remove water, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons in                a minimum of two units for liquefied natural gas                pretreatment, thus, reducing the spent regeneration                gas from temperature pressure swing adsorption                process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow schematic view of                the natural gas stream passing through a                temperature swing unit followed by passing through                a temperature pressure swing adsorption                unit.
   Gas stream (5)
   Temperature swing adsorption unit (10)
   Partially treated gas stream (15)
   Pressure temperature swing adsorption unit                (25)
   Product stream (35)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural gas stream                  comprises less than or equal to 3 vol.% carbon                  dioxide. Preferred Process: Closed loop or                  semi-closed loop regeneration gas stream is used                  to regenerate 2nd adsorbent bed. Semi-closed loop                  regeneration gas stream is used to regenerate the                  2nd adsorbent bed. Temperature pressure swing                  adsorption process comprises a series of steps in                  order comprising adsorption, co-current                  depressurization, closed-loop heating, a purge                  with heating, a purge without heating,                  repressurization of the adsorbent bed and cooling                  with feed and then withdrawal of product.                  Temperature pressure swing adsorption process                  comprises a series of steps in order comprising                  adsorption, co-current depressurization, 1st                  closed loop heating step and 2nd closed loop                  heating step, a depressurization step, a purge                  without heat, repressurization and cooling with                  feed and withdrawal of product. Portion of                  product is used in the purge without heating                  step. Portion of product stream is heated and                  sent through the adsorbent bed. Stream exiting                  the adsorbent bed is sent into adsorbent bed or                  into a 2nd adsorbent bed. Heating step is at a                  higher pressure than the purge step.

PN EP3578510-A1
TI Providing diluent gas for producing chlorine and/or chlorine dioxide, comprises reacting aqueous solution of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite with acid, and adding carbonate and/or bicarbonate to the solution.
AU SCHMID E
AE SCHMID E (SCHM-Individual)
GA 2019A26477
AB    NOVELTY - Providing diluent gas in the production of chlorine and/or chlorine dioxide, comprises reacting an aqueous solution of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite with acid, and adding carbonate and/or bicarbonate to the solution of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite, where the amount of carbonate and/or bicarbonate is measured such that not less than 0.25 mol of carbon dioxide is formed as the diluent gas per mole of chlorine and chlorine dioxide. The acid is metered such that required amount of the carbonate and/or bicarbonate is provided for the reaction of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite.
   USE - The solution is useful for producing chlorine and/or chlorine dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves outgassing, since the carbon dioxide bubbles formed entrain chlorine or chlorine dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for aqueous solution containing hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite for producing chlorine and/or chlorine dioxide by reacting the solution with acid, where the solution of bicarbonate and/or carbonate in an amount of 0.25-5 mol contain bicarbonate anion per mole of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate or chlorite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The amount of carbonate and/or a hydrogen carbonate is measured such that 0.25-5 mole, preferably 0.5-3 mole carbon dioxide per mol of chlorine and chlorine dioxide are formed. The carbonate and/or bicarbonate are added to a reservoir of the solution of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite. The carbonate and/or hydrogen carbonate are fed to a feed line for the solution of the hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite or to the reactor before the feed point of the solution of hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate and/or chlorite and/or the feed point of the acid into the reactor. The reaction takes place in a liquid phase flowing through the reactor and the chlorine and/or chlorine dioxide and carbon dioxide formed form a countercurrent gas phase, which is withdrawn from the reactor by a vacuum pump. The additional diluent gas, preferably an aerosol of water in the diluent gas, preferably in air, is used for further dilution of the chlorine and/or chlorine dioxide. Preferred Components: The carbonate and/or bicarbonate include potassium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. The additional diluent gas is air, nitrogen, carbon dioxide or an aerosol from water in one of the gases, preferably in air. The solution contains 0.5-5 mole bicarbonate anion per mol hypochlorite, chlorate, perchlorate or chlorite. The solution contains sodium chlorite as chlorite, preferably in a concentration of 5-30 wt.%. The solution contains 1-10%, preferably 1-3% sodium and/or potassium chlorate as chlorate, or 5-18% sodium and/or potassium perchlorate as perchlorate. The acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, peroxodisulfates, hypochlorous acid, organic acids e.g. acetic acid and/or citric acid. The concentration of hydrochloric acid is 20-37 wt.%, preferably 30-33 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Air purifier using microalgae as purification medium capable of absorbing carbon dioxide in the air by using the microalgae and intercepting PM2.5 pollutants by using water molecules.
AU YOU Z
AE YOU Z (YOUZ-Individual)
GA 202008878Y
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention relates to an air purifier, which includes: a casing, an algae liquid circulation unit disposed in the casing, and a light source disposed in the casing. The algae liquid circulation unit has a liquid storage tank, a flow guiding tray set and a pump. The flow guiding tray set is arranged above the liquid storage tank, and has two flow guiding trays. The two flow guiding trays are detachably assembled with each other vertically, and each flow guiding tray has a flow guiding port. The two flow guiding ports are staggered to form a flow guiding channel for guiding an algae liquid to the liquid storage tank. The pump is arranged in the liquid storage tank to enable the algae liquid to flow and circulate. The light source provides light to the algae liquid so as to enable the algae liquid to photosynthesize during a circulation process. As a result, with the present invention, it is able to use the characteristics of the microalgae to absorb carbon dioxide in the air, and to use water molecules in the casing to completely intercept pollutants such as PM2.5, thereby achieving an effect of air purification.

TI Preparing microbial powder-steel slag solid carbon            permeable brick, comprises e.g. mixing steel slag            coarse aggregate with dilute acetic acid solution,            stirring with microbial powder, pouring into a mold,            pressing, demolding and curing.

   GONG G
   JIN P
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2019760378
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microbial powder-steel slag solid                carbon permeable brick, comprises (i) adding steel                slag coarse aggregate and dilute acetic acid                solution into a stirring pot, and stirring to wet                state, (ii) adding obtained steel slag powder and                microbial powder, stirring until the slurry has                metallic luster, then pouring into a mold and                pressing at 20-40 MPa to obtain sample, (iii)                curing the sample at 25 plus minus 2 degrees C and                relative humidity of greater than or equal to 95%                for 8 hours, demolding sample piece, then curing at                25 plus minus 2 degrees C and relative humidity of                more than 60% for 8 hours, then transferring to                carbonization reactor, vacuuming, introducing                carbon dioxide gas, and curing for 4-6 hours to                obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microbial                powder-steel slag solid carbon permeable                brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short production cycle, and                high waste utilization rate; and solves the problem                of insufficient utilization of waste steel slag and                carbon dioxide; and is environmentally-friendly.                The prepared permeable bricks have same tensile                strength, water permeability and stability as that                of permeable paving bricks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle diameter                  of steel slag coarse aggregate is 2.35-4.75 mm;                  method includes subjecting steel slag coarse                  aggregate to ball milling treatment to obtain                  powder having a particle diameter of 10-100 mu m                  and dosage of 1/5-1/4 based on total mass of the                  steel slag coarse aggregate, The concentration of                  the dilute acetic acid solution is 0.25-1 mol/l                  and dosage is 0.20-0.30 based on mass of steel                  slag micropowder. The microbial powder includes                  carbonic anhydrase and dosage is 0.5-1.5 wt.%                  based on mass of steel slag micropowder. The                  microbial powder includes Bacillus mucilaginosus.                  The carbonization step conditions are carbon                  dioxide content of 99.9%; pressure of                  carbonization reactor is 0-0.3 MPa and                  carbonization time is 4-6 hours. The water                  permeability coefficient of microbial-steel slag                  solid carbon permeable brick is 0.2-0.5                  cm/second. The size of the mold is 200x 100x 50                  mm.
