TI Preparing high-purity rose essential oil comprises manually picking fresh rose petals, soaking in saline, adding water and stirring to obtain rose liquid, extracting under high pressure to obtain essential oil and hydrolyzing with pectinase.
AU WU Z
AE YIXING RUIYOUNA ROSE IND CO LTD (YIXI-Non-standard)
GA 201959863K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity rose essential oil comprises (i) manually picking fresh rose petals which are free from insects or breakage; (ii) soaking in 13% saline for 2.5-4.5 hours; (iii) further soaking in 9% saline for 1.2 hours and vacuum sterilizing; (iv) placing in a container, adding distilled water and stirring in a stirrer to obtain rose liquid; (v) placing the rose liquid in an extractor, passing carbon dioxide under high pressure to turn into liquid state and vaporizing carbon dioxide using change in pressure to obtain essential oil; (vi) adjusting pH of rose essential oil to 3.7-4.9 using citric acid, adding 0.15% acid-stable cellulase, reacting for 52 minutes, adding 0.22% pectinase and 0.13% complex protease and reacting for 40 minutes; and (vii) adding rose essential oil liquid into 10 l fermenter, adding 1.2% glucose, 1.3% yeast extract and 0.8% trypsin, adjusting the pH to 6.2 using 1 mol/l phosphate buffer, reacting for 25 hours and inactivating at 90 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity rose essential oil.

AB    NOVELTY - Separation of at least one acid gas from                mixture of gases involves contacting a                hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising hydrogen                sulfide and one or more hydrocarbon gases, with a                membrane comprising a spirobisindane-based polymer                of intrinsic microporosity (PIM) or an                amidoxime-functionalized PIM. The membrane                separates the mixture into a permeate stream                comprising hydrogen sulfide, and a retentate stream                that is depleted in hydrogen sulfide as compared to                the feedstock.
   USE - Separation of acid gas e.g. hydrogen sulfide                and carbon dioxide, from mixture of gases e.g.                hydrocarbon-based feedstock e.g. natural gas, shale                gas and biogas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides                hydrocarbon-based feedstock with high purity by                selectively removing acid gases using the polymer                membrane having high hydrogen sulfide/methane                selectivity and ultrahigh hydrogen sulfide                permeability. The membrane has excellent carbon                dioxide separation performance due to the presence                of amidoxime-functionalized polymer that forms                extensive interchain and intrachain hydrogen                bonding that contributes to a tight microstructure                and a good balance between intrachain rigidity and                intrachain spacing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polymer                membrane, which comprises the hydrogen sulfide                sorbed on the spirobisindane-based PIM or                amidoxime-functionalized-PIM.

Preferred Properties: The                  membrane has hydrogen sulfide permeability of at                  least 500 Barrer, preferably at least 1500 Barrer                  at a pressure of 10 bar or at least 3000 Barrer                  at a pressure of up to 77 bar, at a gas feed of                  20 mol% hydrogen sulfide, 20 mol% carbon dioxide                  and 60 mol% methane. The membrane has hydrogen                  sulfide/methane selectivity of greater than 20 at                  a pressure of 10 bar, or greater than 40 at a                  pressure of 30 bar, or greater than 60 at a                  pressure of up to 77 bar, at a gas feed of 20                  mol% hydrogen sulfide, 20 mol% carbon dioxide and                  60 mol% methane.

PN CN108913335-A
TI Extraction of wheat germ oil involves screening wheat germ, adding to water, drying, subjecting to microwave irradiation, extracting using carbon dioxide, injecting anhydrous ethanol, stirring, leaching, heating, adding brine, and heating.
AU KE Y
AE GUANGZHOU CUIQU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018992491
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of wheat germ oil involves screening wheat germ through screening machine, adding to water, vibrating at 150-200 Hz, cleaning for 30-60 minutes, drying at 65-70 degrees C, pulverizing to 300 mesh at 25-35 degrees C, subjecting to microwave irradiation at 1405-2675 MHz for 180-560 seconds, extracting using carbon dioxide as supercritical extraction medium at 43-48 degrees C and 34-36 MPa, injecting anhydrous ethanol, stirring, leaching, subjecting to microwave irradiation at 2130-3124 MHz to increase molecular rotational energy level transition between anhydrous ethanol and wheat germ oil liquid, and improving leaching speed and extraction efficiency of wheat germ oil, adding to alkali refining tank, heating to 60-65 degrees C, precipitating, heating to 80-90 degrees C, adding brine having salt content of 30-35% in a ratio of 1/100, heating to 214-223 degrees C, removing anhydrous ethanol from wheat germ oil, and leaving still at 45-50 degrees C for 6-8 hours.
   USE - Extraction of wheat germ oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and continuous extraction of wheat germ oil, and provides high-purity oil having low impurity content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The alkali has baume degree of 17-20 degrees . Preferred Composition: The usage amount of the alkali is 0.4-0.6 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The anhydrous ethanol is replaced by n-hexane, acetone, petroleum ether or chloroform.

PT P
PN WO2018175769-A1; CA3056943-A1; IN201937037486-A; CN110662731-A; EP3601197-A1; JP2020511392-W; BR112019019789-A2; EP3601197-A4; US2021017081-A1; IN377502-B
TI Aerated composite material production involves            forming wet mixture or slurry, where wet mixture or            slurry comprises water, filler particles comprising            calcium oxide, ground calcium silicate particles, and            an aerating agent.
AU TAS A C
AE SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
   SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
   SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
   SOLIDIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SOLI-Non-standard)
GA 2018754858
AB    NOVELTY - Aerated composite material production involves                forming a wet mixture or slurry, where the wet                mixture or slurry comprises water, filler particles                comprising calcium oxide having a particle size of                0.1-1000 mu m, ground calcium silicate particles,                one or multiple minerals comprising magnesium,                magnesium salt or magnesium oxide, and an aerating                agent. The wet mixture or slurry has a water and                solid ratio of 1.0 or less. The wet mixture or                slurry is casted in a mold, and then allowed the                aerating agent to generate hydrogen gas for causing                volume expansion of the slurry. The obtained                expanded mixture is pre-cured to a hardness                enabling to be taken out of the mold and moved. The                pre-cured expanded mixture is cured at ordinary                pressure at 30 degrees C.
   USE - Method for producing aerated composite                material aerated concrete product selected from                blocks, lintels, panels and masonry units (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for production aerated composite                material in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with improved physical chemical                properties, such as durability and strength, and                significantly reduced energy requirement and carbon                dioxide emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic scanning                electron microscope image of carbon dioxide-cured                solidia aerated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cutting of the                  pre-cured expanded mixture is carried out by                  using a piano wire, diamond wire or cutting saw.                  The ground calcium silicate particles comprise                  calcium silicate phases selected from                  wollastonite or pseudowollastonite, rankinite,                  belite, larnite, bredigite, amorphous calcium                  silicate phase. The carbonation is carried out at                  60-85 degrees C for 6 hours. The minerals also                  comprise magnesium, magnesium nitrate, magnesium                  sulfate, magnesium chloride, and magnesium                  acetate. The cured low density, aerated product                  contains the phases of aragonite and/or dolomite                  together with calcite. The aerated concrete                  product comprises aragonite, magnesian,                  magnesite, dolomite, hydromagnesite,                  nesquehonite, dypingite, artinite, barringtonite                  or lansfordite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The slurry also                  comprises a dispersant or superplasticizer. The                  dispersant or superplasticizer comprises a                  polycarboxylate-based polymer.

PT P
PN KR2018076069-A; KR1882588-B1
TI Producing methyl ethyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol, comprises e.g. providing fermentation broth made by fermenting biomass, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide as substrate as first liquid stream and transferring liquid stream to filling column.
AU LEE J W
   KIM J H
   OH B K
AE UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
GA 2018541016
AB    NOVELTY - Producing at least one of methyl ethyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol, comprises: (a) providing a fermentation broth prepared by fermenting biomass, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide as a substrate as a first liquid stream; (b) transferring the first liquid stream to a filling column to dehydrate, then adsorbing the dehydrated product and obtaining a second liquid stream; (c) injecting an inert gas into a filling column of step (b) to discharge the first vapor phase stream, or injecting steam into the filling column of step (b) to discharge the second vapor phase stream; and (d) recovering methyl ethyl ketone from the first vapor phase stream, or recovering 2,3-butanediol from the second vapor phase stream.
   USE - The process is useful for producing at least one of methyl ethyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is capable of producing methyl ethyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol with high purity, in a simple manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a continuous flow process in which high purity methyl butyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol can be selectively prepared from a fermentation broth containing 2.3-butanediol (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: At least two filling columns of the steps (b) and (c) are independent of each other, where an inert gas is injected to discharge the first vapor phase stream, or water vapor is injected to discharge the second vapor phase stream. The first liquid stream, second liquid stream, first vapor phase stream and second vapor phase stream are continuous streams. The fermentation is performed using strains of Klebsiella oxytoca, Klebsiella pneumoniae, Aerobacter aerogenes, Bacillus subtilis, Serratia marcescens, Enterobacter sp., Bacillus sp., Rhodococcus sp., or Paenibacillus sp.. The step (a) comprises a pretreatment step of removing at least one of a protein and an inorganic salt, and performing the steps of at least one of stripping to remove the ethanol in a first liquid flow. The dehydration of step (b) is carried out using an acidic catalyst and a carbon-based adsorbent. The step (c) comprises: selectively desorbing the dehydration reaction product adsorbed in step (b) by injecting an inert gas into the filling column, where the methyl ethyl ketone is obtained from the first vapor phase stream by desorption; and injecting water vapor into the filling column to desorb the dehydration reaction product adsorbed in step (b), where 2,3-butanediol is obtained from the second vapor phase stream by desorption. In the method, an inert gas is injected into at least some of the two filling towers to discharge the first vapor phase stream, and steam is injected into the remaining filling towers to discharge the second vapor phase stream to obtain methyl ethyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol simultaneously. Preferred Components: The first liquid stream comprises 2,3-butanediol. The acidic catalyst is zeolite, heteropoly acid, metal oxide, ion exchange resin, metal salt and/or impregnated carbon. The carbon-based adsorbent is activated carbon, activated carbon fiber, carbon nanotube and/or graphene.
   EXAMPLE - Two filling tows were filled with a mixture of an acidic catalyst and activated carbon in a first filling column. Then microorganisms were separated from the fermentation stock solution, and impurities e.g. proteins and inorganic salts were removed, and a low concentration solution obtained by separating ethanol was poured to convert 2,3-butanediol into methyl ethyl ketone and adsorbed. When the adsorption was completed and the concentration of 2,3-butanediol at the outlet of the filling tower became significantly lower, and the flow of the low-concentration solution was changed to flow to the second filling tower. The first filling column was then blown with nitrogen to desorb and obtain methyl ethyl ketone. In the second filling column, a small amount of 2,3-butanediol, 2-methylpropanol and a gas containing water vapor were blown in, and the adsorbed methyl ethyl ketone was converted into 2,3-butanediol and then desorbed and recovered, where the step was repeated to provide a continuous flow process obtained by the combination of methyl ethyl ketone and 2,3-butanediol.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D05-A04A; D05-C; E10-E04C2; E10-F02B; E11-M; A12-M; A12-W11K; A12-W11L; N01-A; N01-B; N01-C02; N02; N03; N04-A; N05-E03A; N06-A

PN US2022146192-A1
TI Method for processing natural gas using ultra-critical fluid power cycles, involves receiving portion of power output from ultra-critical fluid power generation system, and providing portion of extracted natural gas at temperature and pressure with natural gas compression subsystem.
AU HOOPES K M
   RANSOM D L
   BECK G C
AE SOUTHWEST RES INST (SWRI-C)
GA 202265630N
AB    NOVELTY - The method (600) involves receiving a multiphase heat transfer medium at first temperature and first pressure from an ultra-critical power generation system. A first portion of an extracted natural gas is cooled (608) at second temperature and second pressure with the multiphase heat transfer medium at the first temperature and the first pressure to produce extracted natural gas at a third temperature and third pressure. A portion of the multiphase heat transfer medium is evaporated (610) to provide a gaseous heat transfer medium at fourth temperature and fourth pressure. The gaseous heat transfer medium is conveyed (612) at the fourth temperature and the fourth pressure to the ultra-critical fluid power generation system. A portion of the power output from the ultra-critical fluid power generation system is received (614) with the natural gas liquid subsystem.
   USE - Method for processing natural gas using ultra-critical fluid power cycles in applications. Uses include but are not limited to next generation utility scale nuclear and fossil fuel power generation, modular nuclear power generation, solar-thermal power generation, shipboard propulsion, geothermal power generation, and waste heat recovery applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high efficiencies by leveraging recuperation in the closed Brayton cycle to minimize thermal losses and through reduced compression work by leveraging the unique characteristics of supercritical carbon dioxide. Such characteristics include high fluid density, low viscosity, and high heat capacity at pressures greater than the critical pressure of carbon dioxide and temperatures greater than a critical temperature of the carbon dioxide, thus improving power cycle efficiency in the United States and around the world.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for processing natural gas using ultra-critical fluid power cycles.
   Method for processing natural gas using ultra-critical fluid power cycles (600)
   Cooling first portion of extracted natural gas at second temperature and second pressure with multiphase heat transfer medium at first temperature and first pressure to produce extracted natural gas at third temperature and third pressure (608)
   Evaporating portion of multiphase heat transfer medium to provide gaseous heat transfer medium at fourth temperature and fourth pressure (610)
   Conveying gaseous heat transfer medium at fourth temperature and fourth pressure to ultra-critical fluid power generation system (612)
   Receiving portion of power output from ultra-critical fluid power generation system with natural gas liquid subsystem (614)

PN WO2021234302-A1; FR3110461-A1
TI Producing gases from gas mixture e.g. oxygen from carbon dioxide, involves discharging gas mixture between electrodes which form interelectrode space having variable interelectrode distance and containing ignition zone and two other zones.
AU PANNIER E
AE CENTRALESUPELEC (CENT-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENTRALESUPELEC (CENT-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 2021D5228A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing gases from a mixture of gases involves injecting a gas mixture into a pulsed plasma reactor (10), dissociating the gas mixture, implementing isochoric discharges between an elongate electrode (15) having a polarity and electrode(s) (13) having opposite polarity and facing the electrode (15), removing the reactive gases produced from the dissociation step, and passing to an area where it is cooled, separated and/or collected. The electrodes (15) and (13) form an interelectrode space having a variable interelectrode distance. The interelectrode space contains an ignition zone and two other zones. When the plasma produced in the reactor is blown by a continuous flow of gas mixture entering the reactor, a passive re-ignition of the plasma is performed in the dissociation step. The re-ignition step is carried out in the ignition zone in an area protected from continuous flow of gas. The protected area results by providing an insulation block (14) in a structure (12).
   USE - Producing gases from gas mixture e.g. producing hydrogen gas from hydrocarbons mixture and hydrocarbons-carbon dioxide mixture, and producing oxygen from carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates the gas flow control step in the plasma reactor, and uses system ensuring stable flow control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing gases from a mixture of gases involves injecting a gas mixture into a pulsed plasma reactor (10), dissociating the gas mixture, implementing isochoric discharges between an elongate electrode (15) having a polarity and electrode(s) (13) having opposite polarity and facing the electrode (15), removing the reactive gases produced from the dissociation step, and passing to an area where it is cooled, separated and/or collected. The electrodes (15) and (13) form an interelectrode space having a variable interelectrode distance. The interelectrode space contains an ignition zone and two other zones. When the plasma produced in the reactor is blown by a continuous flow of gas mixture entering the reactor, a passive re-ignition of the plasma is performed in the dissociation step. The re-ignition step is carried out in the ignition zone in an area protected from continuous flow of gas. The protected area results by providing an insulation block (14) in a structure (12) and has an interelectrode distance allowing ignition of the plasma away from the continuous flow of gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for transforming and producing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an enlarged view of hydrogen producing system.
   Pulsed plasma reactor (10)
   Structures (12,23)
   Electrodes (13,15)
   Insulation block (14)
   Orifice (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The system contains a stage (SE) for separating hydrogen gas and carbon monoxide produced after cooling reactive gases.

PT P
PN CN111675656-A; CN111675656-B
TI Bromochlorohydantoin production involves add acetone cyanohydrin and water into mixing tank to obtain mixture of acetone cyanohydrin and water, and then feeding steam, feed carbon dioxide and ammonia from bottom of reactor.
AU LI W
   LI L
   LI A
   CHEN B
   WANG Z
   ZHAO X
AE HEBEI LISHI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202093662J
AB    NOVELTY - Bromochlorohydantoin production involves add acetone cyanohydrin and water into a mixing tank in a ratio of 1:1-1:1.5 to obtain a mixture of acetone cyanohydrin and water, and then feeding steam, feed carbon dioxide and ammonia from bottom of the reactor, where the mixed solution of acetone cyanohydrin and water in a ratio of 1:2.5:1.2-1:3.5:2.0. The mixture is flown from the bottom of the reactor into the synthesis reactor by passing steam into the jacket of the synthesis reactor at 60-80 degrees C for 2.5-4 hours to prepare 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution. The inert gas is introduced into the 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution to replace ammonia. The 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution and water in the synthesis reactor are added to the dissolution reactor, where the jacket of the dissolution reactor is passed into the cooling medium for cooling.
   USE - Method for producing bromochlorohydantoin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the bromochlorohydantoin in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with reduced production process and continuous production of dimethylhydantoin synthesis to bromochlorohydantoin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bromochlorohydantoin production involves add acetone cyanohydrin and water into a mixing tank in a ratio of 1:1-1:1.5 to obtain a mixture of acetone cyanohydrin and water, and then feeding steam, feed carbon dioxide and ammonia from bottom of the reactor, where the mixed solution of acetone cyanohydrin and water in a ratio of 1:2.5:1.2-1:3.5:2.0. The mixture is flown from the bottom of the reactor into the synthesis reactor by passing steam into the jacket of the synthesis reactor at 60-80 degrees C for 2.5-4 hours to prepare 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution. The inert gas is introduced into the 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution to replace ammonia. The 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution and water in the synthesis reactor are added to the dissolution reactor, where the jacket of the dissolution reactor is passed into the cooling medium for cooling. The liquid inorganic base is added into the dissolution reactor to continue cooling. The temperature of the mixture of hydantoin and inorganic alkali is reduced to 20-25 degrees C and then transferred to the halogenation synthesis reactor. The bromine and chlorine are bring into contact with halogenation synthesis reactor for bromochlorination reaction at 25-30 degrees C. The bromochlorination reaction is completed to obtain bromochlorohydantoin by centrifuging and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The obtained 5,5-dimethylhydantoin aqueous solution is obtained by replacing free ammonia in the solution with an inert gas. The inorganic base is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. The cooling medium is liquid halogen, which is used to cool the dissolution reactor. The obtained bromochlorohydantoin is in powder form, granulated and tableted into flake bromochlorohydantoin. The mixing tank is an enamel mixing tank, where the reactor is a tower reactor, a synthesis reactor and a dissolution reactor.

PT P
PN CN111136083-A
TI Recycling industrial waste and miscellaneous salt, by obtaining gas or oil as fuel and pure oxygen as combustion agent, roasting salt, passing obtained chloride and hydrogen and transferring to relevant workshop.
AU CHEN T
   CHEN H
   WANG J
AE ANHUI ANTEZHI WASTE RESOURCE RES CENT CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020425152
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling industrial waste and miscellaneous salt, involves obtaining gas or oil as fuel and pure oxygen as combustion agent, roasting the industrial waste miscellaneous salt, pickling gas by roasting to remove ammonia, washing with alkali to remove nitrogen oxides and carbon dioxide, returning remaining oxygen-rich tail gas as combustion agent, returning remaining oxygen-rich tail gas as a combustion agent, dissolving the roasted salt into water, filtering, treating filtered salt solution with ion exchange resin to remove calcium and magnesium ions, selecting sodium chloride electrolysis device of required scale to electrolyze finely purified salt solution, passing obtained chloride and hydrogen into burner for mixed combustion, absorbing hydrochloric acid by falling film absorption tower to obtain industrial hydrochloric acid, transferring the obtained industrial hydrochloric acid and sodium hydroxide solution to the storage tank, and transferring to relevant workshop.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling industrial waste and miscellaneous salt (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for recycling industrial waste and miscellaneous salt, involves obtaining gas or oil as fuel and pure oxygen as combustion agent, according to the excess oxygen of 20-50%, roasting the industrial waste miscellaneous salt in a roaster at 550-800 degrees C with oxygen enrichment to completely decompose the organic matter and inorganic impurities, pickling gas by roasting to remove ammonia, washing with alkali to remove nitrogen oxides and carbon dioxide, returning remaining oxygen-rich tail gas as combustion agent, if the industrial waste miscellaneous salt does not contain ammonium ions, the gas generated by the roasting is washed by alkali to remove nitrogen oxides and carbon dioxide, returning remaining oxygen-rich tail gas as a combustion agent, dissolving the roasted salt into water to make near-saturated salt solution with a sodium chloride mass concentration of 20-25%, filtering to remove the water-insoluble matter, treating filtered salt solution with ion exchange resin to remove calcium and magnesium ions to less than 20 to obtain finely purified salt solution, selecting sodium chloride electrolysis device of required scale to electrolyze finely purified salt solution to obtain hydrogen, chloride and sodium hydroxide solution, passing obtained chloride and hydrogen into burner for mixed combustion to obtain hydrochloride gas and avoid chloride residue, absorbing hydrochloric acid by falling film absorption tower to obtain industrial hydrochloric acid, transferring the obtained industrial hydrochloric acid and sodium hydroxide solution to the storage tank, and transferring to relevant workshop through pipeline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The time of the oxygen-enriched roasting aims to achieve complete decomposition of organic matter, the roasting time of materials in the fluidized bed is 1 second. When the rotary kiln is used as the roaster, the roasting time of the materials in the rotary kiln is 10 minutes. The fine filtration is performed with 2 mu m filter membrane. The molar ratio of chloride and hydrogen mixed combustion is 1:1.05-1.1.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

PT P
PN CN110404575-A; CN110404575-B
TI Carbon containing iron based catalyst useful in hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce hydrocarbon.
AU GUO X
   LIU J
   ZHANG G
   SONG C
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019943700
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon containing iron based catalyst is claimed. The catalyst prepared by taking bimetallic zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIFs) as a precursor, and introducing iron source, pyrolysis in an inert atmosphere to obtain final product with nano-particles with Fe5C2 and Fe3ZnC0.5 as iron species and embedded in a porous carbon matrix, where the metal elements of bimetallic ZIFs are zinc and cobalt, and the atomic ratio of zinc and cobalt is greater than or equal to 19, and the iron source is compound of iron other than inorganic iron salt, and the solution is not acidic after being dissolved in water or methanol.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce hydrocarbon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problems that the iron-based catalyst has poor catalytic effect of adding alkali metal and the stability of the catalyst is poor, and the iron-based catalyst containing porous carbon is designed and prepared; utilizes porous carbon material having a better distribution of metal species; and the metal particles in ZIFs-derived material are separated by carbon layer, which is excellent dispersibility, and favorable the porosity of the carbon material for the adsorption of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the iron-based catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving zinc source and cobalt source in methanol to obtain transparent solution (1), where the atomic ratio between zinc and cobalt in Zn source and Co source is greater than or equal to 19, the ratio between total amount of zinc source and cobalt source, and methanol volume is 1:10-20 mol/l, (ii) dissolving 2-methylimidazole in methanol to obtain another solution (2), the ratio between 2-methylimidazole and methanol is 1.2:1-2 mol/l, (iii) adding the solution (1) into the solution (2), stirring at 20-40 degrees C for 24 hours, centrifuging, washing, and drying to obtain catalyst ZIFs precursor, the ratio between total molar amount of zinc and cobalt in solution (1) and molar amount of 2-methylimidazole in solution (2) is 1:4-5, (iv) adding iron source into methanol to obtain iron methanol solution, the mass ratio between iron source and methanol is 1:20-100 g/ml, adding the catalyst ZIFs precursor into the iron methanol solution, stirring at 20-35 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours, heating at 60-75 degrees C until the methanol is completely evaporated, calcining the reaction mixture at 500-600 degrees C for 1 -4 hours under inert atmosphere to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The between atomic ratio between zinc and cobalt is less than 89. The iron source is iron acetylacetonate or ferrocene. In step (i), the zinc source is zinc nitrate, and the cobalt source is cobalt nitrate. Preferred Method: In step (iv), the inert atmosphere is nitrogen atmosphere, the heating temperature of calcination process is 550 degrees C at heat increase rate of 5 degrees C/minute.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PT P
PN CN110396108-A
TI Preparing ortho-substituted arylamine compound, comprises e.g. reaction substituted arylamine compound and alkyl lithium reagent, and coupling with carbon dioxide, borate reagent, brominating reagent or N, N-dimethylformamide.
AU LI Y
   XIE C
   JIN Z
   WU C
   BAO Z
   PAN H
AE LIANHE CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIAN-Non-standard)
   LIANHETECH TAIZHOU CO LTD (LIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201992722R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ortho-substituted arylamine compound (I), comprises (i) subjecting material 1 and alkyl lithium reagent to lithiation reaction in a continuous flow reactor to obtain substituted aryl containing metal lithium compound (III), where the material 1 is mixed solution of substituted arylamine compound (II) and organic solvent, the alkyl lithium reagent is mixed solution of 1-6C alkyl lithium and organic solvent, the molar ratio between (II) and alkyl lithium in a unit time is 1:2-3, and the lithiation reaction temperature is -15 to 60 degrees C, (ii) subjecting material 2 and solution containing (III) to coupling reaction in a continuous flow reactor to obtain final product, the material 2 is compound (IV) or mixed solution thereof with organic solvent, the molar ratio between (III) and (IV) in a unit time is 1:1.5-3, the coupling reaction temperature is -10 to 60 degrees C, the compound (IV) is carbon dioxide, borate reagent, brominating reagent, or N, N-dimethylformamide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ortho-substituted arylamine compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short reaction time and reduced online amount of flammable materials and highly reactive intermediates, and improves safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ortho-substituted arylamine compound of formula (I), comprises (i) subjecting material 1 and alkyl lithium reagent to lithiation reaction in a continuous flow reactor to obtain substituted aryl containing metal lithium compound of formula (III), where the material 1 is mixed solution of substituted arylamine compound of formula (II) and organic solvent, the alkyl lithium reagent is mixed solution of 1-6C alkyl lithium and organic solvent, the molar ratio between (II) and alkyl lithium in a unit time is 1:2-3, and the lithiation reaction temperature is -15 to 60 degrees C, (ii) subjecting material 2 and solution containing (III) to coupling reaction in a continuous flow reactor to obtain final product, the material 2 is compound (IV) or mixed solution thereof with organic solvent, the molar ratio between (III) and (IV) in a unit time is 1:1.5-3, the coupling reaction temperature is -10 to 60 degrees C, the compound (IV) is carbon dioxide, borate reagent, brominating reagent, or N, N-dimethylformamide.
   R1, R2, R3, R4 = H, fluoro, chloro, at least one halogenated (preferably fluoro or chloro) 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C alkyl-O- or 1-6C alkyl;
   R5 = 1-6C alkyl-O-C(=O)- or 1-6C alkyl-C(=O)-;
   G = -COOH, -B(OH)2, -Br, or -C(=O)H; and
   1-6C alkyl = methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl, isobutyl, sec-butyl or tert-butyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent in the material 1 is ether solvent, aromatic hydrocarbon solvent and/or alkane solvent. The molar volume ratio of (II) in the material 1 is 0.1-2 mol/l, preferably 0.25-0.5 mol/l. The 1-6C alkyl group in the 1-6C alkyl lithium is methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl, isobutyl, sec-butyl or tert-butyl. The organic solvent in the alkyl lithium reagent is ether solvents, aromatic solvents and/or alkane solvents. The molar volume ratio of alkyl lithium in the alkyl lithium reagent is 1-5 mol/l, preferably 2-2.5 mol/l. The ether solvent is tetrahydrofuran, ethylene glycol dimethyl ether and/or diethyl ether. The aromatic hydrocarbon solvent is toluene. The alkane solvent is n-hexane and/or n-heptane. The C1-C6 alkyl lithium is preferably n-butyl lithium. The organic solvent in the material 2 is ether solvent, aromatic hydrocarbon solvent and/or alkane solvent. The borate reagent is trimethyl borate, triethyl borate or triisopropyl borate, preferably trimethyl borate. The brominating agent is 1,2-dibromoethane or carbon tetrabromide, preferably 1,2-dibromoethane. (II) is tert-butyl 3-methoxyphenylcarbamate, N-(2-fluorophenyl)pivalamide, N-(2-methoxyphenyl)pivalamide, N-(3-methoxyphenyl)pivalamide and N-(4-chloro-3-methoxyphenyl)pivalamide. In the method, the material 1 and alkyl lithium reagent are simultaneously pumped into a continuous flow reactor, where the lithiation reaction temperature is preferably 0-50 degrees C, the molar ratio between (II) and alkyl lithium in a unit time is 1:2.5/|0.5, the flow ratio between material 1 and alkyl lithium reagent is 1.5:1-5:1, the residence time of material 1 and alkyl lithium reagent in the continuous flow reactor for lithiation reaction is 0.3-5 minutes, preferably 0.5-3 minutes. The pressure in the continuous flow reactor is 10 bar when (IV) is carbon dioxide, where the molar ratio between (III) and (IV) in a unit time is 1:2/|0.5, the flow ratio between material 2 and solution containing (III) is 1:0.75-1:25, the coupling reaction temperature is 0-50 degrees C. The total flow of material 1, alkyl lithium reagent and material 2 is 0.01 ml/minute to 50 l/minute, preferably 5 ml/minute to 10 l/minutes. The continuous flow reactor in steps (1) and (2) is a static mixer, a microchannel reactor or a tubular reactor, preferably tubular reactor. The residence time of coupling reaction of solution containing (III) and the solution of the material 2 in the continuous flow reactor is 0.5-6 minutes. The method further comprises post-processing steps, after the coupling reaction is completed, quenching the reaction system, extracting and separating by organic solvent, and concentrating to obtain final product. In the method, based on the production scale, at least one groups of the continuous flow reactors are connected in parallel to form a continuous flow reaction system to prepare final product.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, R2, R3, R4 = methyl, trifluoromethyl, methoxy; and
   R5 = tert-butoxycarbonyl or pivaloyl.

PT P
PN WO2019193068-A1; EP3549927-A1; EP3774696-A1; US2021107859-A1
TI Synthesizing carbon labeled organic compound            containing carbon labeled carboxy group comprises            reacting organic compound containing carboxy group with            labeled carbon dioxide in presence of catalyst system            comprising inorganic salt.
AU AUDISIO D
   CANTAT T
   DESTRO G
AE COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
GA 2019861544
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing a carbon labeled organic compound                containing a carbon labeled carboxy group (I)                comprises reacting a organic compound containing a                carboxy group (II) with a labeled carbon dioxide in                the presence of a catalyst system comprising an                inorganic salt (III).
   USE - The process is useful for synthesizing a                carbon labeled organic compound containing a carbon                labeled carboxy group, which is useful for                manufacturing pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals                having a free carboxylic acid functionality and                producing tracers (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing a carbon labeled organic compound                containing a carbon labeled carboxy group of                formula (C(R1)(R2)(R3)-asteriskC(=O)OM1) (I),                comprises reacting a organic compound containing a                carboxy group of formula (C(R1)(R2)(R3)-12C(=O)OM1)                (II) with a labeled carbon dioxide in the presence                of a catalyst system comprising an inorganic salt                of formula (M2(L1)m) (III).
   asteriskC = 11C, 13C or 14C isotope;                either
   R1-R3 = H, aryl, heteroaryl, heterocycle,                alkyl, alkyl halide, alkene or alkyne (all                optionally substituted); or
   R1CR2 = carbonyl or R1 and R2 form together                with the carbon atom to which they are linked an                alkene with at least one double bond is alpha to                the carboxy group, the alkene is optionally                substituted;
   M1 = H, Ag+, alkaline cation comprising                lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium or                cesium;
   M2 = transition metal comprising Cu, Pd, Ni,                Ru, Ag, Rh, Fe, Co, Zn, Ir, Au or Pt;
   m = 1 or 2;
   L1 = e.g. halo comprising F, Cl, Br or I,                triflate or trifluoromethylsulfonate, a tosylate or                p-toluenesulfonate, a mesylate or methanesulfonate,                -CN and (CH3)COO- or a ligand e.g. of formula                (R17-(CH2)o-A1(((CH2)rR16)(CH2)qR34) (IV), e.g.                N,N,N',N'-tetramethyl-ethane-1,2-diamine,                tetramethyl-thiourea and tetramethyl-urea;
   o, q, r = 0 or 1;
   A1 = N or P;
   R16, R17, R34 = H, alkyl, alkoxy, aryl,                heteroaryl or heterocycle (optionally substituted),                -(C=S)-NR35R36, -(C=O)-NR35R36, -(CH2)t-NR35R36,                -(CH2)t-PR35R36; either
   R35, R36 = H, alkyl, alkene, alkyne, aryl,                heteroaryl or heterocycle (all optionally                substituted); or
   R35NR36 = optionally substituted heteroaryl or                heterocycle; and
   t = 0-3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) manufacturing labeled pharmaceuticals and                agrochemicals, preferably pharmaceuticals and                agrochemicals having a free carboxylic acid                functionality, comprising synthesis of carbon                labeled organic compounds containing (I), and                optionally solvent extraction and/or purification;                and
   (2) producing tracers comprising synthesis of                carbon labeled organic compounds containing (I) and                optionally solvent extraction and/or                purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The source of labeled                  carbon dioxide is a gas. The labeled carbon                  dioxide pressure in the reaction vessel is of                  0.5-100 bar (50 kPa to 10 MPa), preferably 1-20                  bar. The catalyst system is present at 1-100                  mol.% (preferably 5-25 mol.%), with respect to                  (II). The molar ratio of (III) and the ligands in                  the catalyst system is 1-100 (preferably                  1-5).
   Preferred Definitions:
   M2 = Cu, Pd, Ni, Ru, Ag, Rh, Fe, Co, Zn, Ir,                Au or Pt;
   m = 1;
   L1 = Cl, Br, I, triflate or                trifluoromethylsulfonate, tosylate or                p-toluenesulfonate, mesylate or methanesulfonate,                -CN or (CH3)COO-; and
   M1 = sodium, potassium or cesium.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - Use of 40 compounds (I) are disclosed e.g.                  13C-2-nitrobenzoic acid of formula (Ia),                  13C-4-nitrobenzoic acid,                  13C-6-methyl-2-nitrobenzoic acid,                  13C-5-methyl-2-nitrobenzoic acid and                  13C-4-methyl-2-nitrobenzoic acid.
   EXAMPLE - 29.9 mg Cesium salt of 2-nitrobenzoic acid,                6.6 mg                (E)-2-(4-phenyl-4,5-dihydrooxazol-2-yl)-2-(4-phenyloxazolidin-2-ylidene)acetonitrile                and 2.9 mg copper bromide after that 0.5 ml dry                dimethylsulfoxide was added to the mixture, then                reaction and purification to give 8.3 mg                13C-2-nitrobenzoic acid.

PN KR2019114466-A
TI Air conditioner comprises housing having air supply path through which outdoor air is introduced into room, and exhaust path through which indoor air is discharged to outside and an air supply fan is provided in housing.
AU CHO J
   SHIN S
   KANG T W
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 201986261Q
AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioner (1) comprises housing (10) having an air supply path (21) through which outdoor air is introduced into the room, and an exhaust path (22) through which the indoor air is discharged to the outside. An air supply fan (30) is provided in the housing and configured to generate air supply air by forcing the inflow of outdoor air, exhaust fan provided in the housing and configured to generate exhaust air volume by forcibly discharging indoor air, first sensor (200) for measuring a concentration of a specific pollution component contained in outdoor air, second sensor for measuring a concentration of a specific pollutant contained in indoor air, and a controller configured to vary outputs of the air supply fan and the exhaust fan according to the measured difference value P between the concentration of the pollution component of the outdoor air and the concentration of the pollution component of the indoor air.
   USE - Air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The air conditioner allows to measure the concentration of the specific air component contained in the outdoor air and indoor air through the first sensor and the second sensor, where difference between the concentration of the air component of the outdoor air and concentration of the air component of the indoor air measured by the control unit (P), variably controls the output of the air supply fan and exhaust fan, determines whether the quality of the outdoor air is good compared to the quality of the indoor air by referring to the difference value (P) to determine the degree of good and bad by control unit, maximize the effect of the ventilation operation by adjusting the amount of supply air and the amount of exhaust air in response to the degree of bad, good or bad, prevents a sudden rise in the carbon dioxide concentration of the indoor air and to maintain the comfort of the room for a long time, when the carbon dioxide concentration of the indoor air is lower than the carbon dioxide concentration of the outdoor air by reducing the exhaust air volume, improves the quality of the indoor air by introducing the outdoor air quickly, and increase of the output of the air supply fan while providing the intended optimal ventilation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling air conditioner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an air conditioner
   Air conditioner (1)
   Housing (10)
   Air supply path (21)
   Exhaust path (22)
   Air supply fan (30)
   First sensor (200)

PN CN109122832-A
TI Preparing vegetable refreshing agent, comprises(a) washing the leaves, draining water, drying, crushing dried leaves, sieving, and extracting by adding into a supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction tank and washing dahurian patrinia herb.
AU ZHAO K
AE XINGJIA IND HEYUAN CO LTD (XING-Non-standard)
GA 2019050459
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vegetable refreshing agent, comprises (a) washing the leaves, draining water and drying, crushing dried leaves, sieving, and extracting by adding into a supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction tank to obtain folium Artemisiae argyi extract liquid; (b) washing dahurian patrinia herb, draining and drying, crushing the dried dahurian patrinia herb, sieving and soaking with ethanol, using ultrasonic extraction, centrifuging, allowing to stand; (c) washing the aloe, draining, drying, crushing and beating, carrying out ultrasonic extraction, centrifuging and allowing to stand; (d) mixing the extract of folium Artemisiae argyi, extract of dahurian patrinia herb; (e) adding nano titanium dioxide to ethanol while stirring; (f) dissolving the imazalil sulfate in deionized water, adding a plant growth regulator, heating to increase dissolution rate; and (g) adding a concentrated extract and an ethanol dispersion of nano-titanium dioxide to the mixed solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vegetable refreshing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can prepare product having small amount of antibacterial agent and nano titanium dioxide, synergistic effect, which can inhibit mold growth, maintain the freshness of vegetables and increase storage time and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing vegetable refreshing agent, comprises (a) washing the leaves, draining water and drying, crushing dried leaves, sieving, and extracting by adding into a supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction tank to obtain folium Artemisiae argyi extract liquid; (b) washing the dahurian patrinia herb, draining and drying, crushing the dried dahurian patrinia herb, sieving and soaking with ethanol, using ultrasonic extraction, centrifuging, allowing to stand, taking the supernatant to obtain dahurian patrinia herb extract; (c) washing the aloe, draining, drying, crushing and beating, carrying out ultrasonic extraction, centrifuging, allowing to stand, and taking the supernatant to obtain an aloe extract; (d) mixing the extract of folium Artemisiae argyi, extract of dahurian patrinia herb and aloe extract, concentrating to obtain a concentrated extract; (e) adding nano titanium dioxide to ethanol while stirring to obtain an ethanol dispersion of nano titanium dioxide; (f) dissolving the imazalil sulfate in deionized water, adding a plant growth regulator, heating to increase the dissolution rate, continuing to add an antioxidant and stirring until completely dissolved, to obtain a mixed solution; and (g) adding a concentrated extract and an ethanol dispersion of nano-titanium dioxide to the mixed solution, stirring uniformly, and cooling to room temperature. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for vegetable refreshing agent prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (a) the drying temperature is 45-60 degrees C, the meshing number is 200-300 mesh, the extraction pressure is 15-25 MPa, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 12-18 kg/hour, the temperature is 40-60 degrees C and extraction time is 35-50 minutes. In the step (b) the drying temperature is 40-50 degrees C, the meshing number is 200-300 mesh, and the weight ratio of the dahurian patrinia herb and ethanol after sieving is 1-2:80-150, the frequency during ultrasonic extraction is 40-50 HZ, the extraction temperature is 45-60 degrees C and the extraction time is 30-50 minutes. In the step (c) the weight ratio of aloe and ethanol after beating is 3-5:95-130, the ultrasonic extraction frequency is 60-80 HZ, the extraction temperature is 30-40 degrees C, and the extraction time is 15-30 minutes. In step (d), the addition amount folium Artemisiae argyi, extract of dahurian patrinia herb and aloe extract is 20-33 pts. wt., 27-40 pts. wt.and 45-62 pts. wt., respectively. Preferred Components: In the step (e) the mass concentration of the nano titanium dioxide and ethanol dispersion is 5-12%. In the step (f) the plant growth regulator thidiazepine and gibberellin, and the antioxidant is vitamin C, isovitamin C, vitamin A or vitamin E. In the step (f) the mass concentration of imazalil sulfate in the mixed solution is 5-8%, the concentration of the plant growth regulator is 12-15%, and the concentration of the antioxidant is 3-5%. In the step (g) the weight ratio of the mixed solution, concentrated extract and the nano titanium dioxide is 12-15:5-7:2-3.

PT P
PN CN108998226-A
TI Processing of soybean oil involves combining crude oil squeezed out from soybeans and crude oil extracted from squeezed soybean cake using supercritical carbon dioxide, adding stabilizer and acetic anhydride, decolorizing, and deacidifying.
AU MENG F
AE MENG F (MENG-Individual)
GA 2018A3557X
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of soybean oil involves washing soybeans with water, squeezing out crude soybean oil (A), placing the soybean cake in a vacuum low-temperature cold air dryer, drying to obtain water content of 25-27%, pulverizing into fine powder, feeding soybean cake micropowder into an extraction kettle, introducing pressurized supercritical fluid carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle, extracting, separating into a separation kettle (A) and separation kettle (B), obtaining crude soybean oil (B), combining the crude soybean oil (A) and crude soybean oil (B), adding 0.1-0.5 %mass stabilizer based on the amount of the crude soybean oil, stirring, adding 0.2-0.5 %mass degumming agent acetic anhydride based on the mass of crude oil, to remove phospholipids, adding 0.1-0.5 %mass decolorizing agent based on the mass of crude oil, stirring, removing pigment in the crude soybean oil, and subjecting the decolorized crude soybean oil to alkaline deacidification to remove free fatty acids.
   USE - Processing of soybean oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and environmentally-friendly, increases the extraction rate of soybean oil, avoids wastage of resources, uses stabilizer to avoid destruction of nutrients in soybean oil by external high temperature, oxygen, and organic solvents, and improve oil yield, and uses decolorizing agent to absorb impurities such as pigments and inorganic substances in soybean oil, and improve purity of soybean oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing of soybean oil involves washing soybeans with water, squeezing out crude soybean oil (A), placing the soybean cake in a vacuum low-temperature cold air dryer, drying at 30-32 degrees C to obtain water content of 25-27%, pulverizing into fine powder using a solid pulverizer, feeding soybean cake micropowder into an extraction kettle, introducing pressurized supercritical fluid carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle, extracting for 2-6 hours, separating into a separation kettle (A) and separation kettle (B), obtaining crude soybean oil (B), combining the crude soybean oil (A) and crude soybean oil (B), adding 0.1-0.5 %mass stabilizer based on the amount of the crude soybean oil, stirring at 35 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, adding 0.2-0.5 %mass degumming agent acetic anhydride based on the mass of crude oil, to remove phospholipids, adding 0.1-0.5 %mass decolorizing agent based on the mass of crude oil, stirring at 35 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, removing pigment in the crude soybean oil, and subjecting the decolorized crude soybean oil to alkaline deacidification to remove free fatty acids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The extraction kettle has pressure of 10-45 MPa and temperature of 25-50 degrees C. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10-45 kg/hour.kg. The separation kettle (A) has working pressure of 10-25 MPa and temperature of 23-56 degrees C. The separation kettle (B) has working pressure of 5.5-12.5 MPa and temperature of 23-52 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The stabilizer is prepared by adding 4A molecular sieve to absolute ethanol, heating to reflux, stirring and heat preserving for 20-40 minutes, adding salicylic acid and initiator selected from azobisisobutyronitrile and diacyl peroxide, stirring under reflux for 1-2 hours, cooling to 50-60 degrees C, adding titanate coupling agent, stirring and heat preserving for 20-30 minutes, filtering, feeding the obtained precipitate into a dry box, drying to a constant weight, and pulverizing into a fine powder using a solid pulverizer. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of 4A molecular sieve, salicylic acid, initiator, and titanate coupling agent is 10-15:20-30:1-3:1-2.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02

PN CN108744067-A
TI Method for preparing biochitosan composite medical catheter, involves adding chitosan and acetic anhydride and heating, adding glucosamine quaternary ammonium salt and filling reaction furnace with carbon dioxide gas, drying and packaging.
AU FEI Y
AE FEI Y (FEIY-Individual)
GA 201888736B
AB    NOVELTY - A biochitosan composite medical catheter method involves adding 20-28 parts chitosan, 5-9 parts acetic anhydride and 12-15 parts PTFE uniformly to the reaction furnace, heating the reaction to 180 degrees C, adding 1-4 parts antifoaming agent to normal pressure for 1 hour to keep warm, adding 3-8 parts glucosamine quaternary ammonium salt, 1-3 parts ethyl acetate, 2-3 parts calcium chloride, 2-3 parts aspartic acid, 1-4 parts asparagines and 2-5 parts dimethyl taurine ammonia to the reaction furnace, filling the reaction furnace with carbon dioxide gas, injecting the reaction liquid into a medical rail co-extruder to extrude the material, washing extruded conduit casting sequentially with sodium citrate solution and drying, subjecting the dried catheter casting to surface activation and sonicating, infiltrating the surface activated conduit in solidifying liquid to perform curing, rinsing the catheter with ultrapure water, drying and packaging to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing biochitosan composite medical catheter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing biochitosan composite medical catheter with better anti-abrasive capability, in safe manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A biochitosan composite medical catheter method involves adding 20-28 parts chitosan, 5-9 parts acetic anhydride, 10-15 parts polycarbonate, 3-5 parts polypropylene fiber, and 12-15 parts PTFE uniformly to the reaction furnace, heating the reaction to 180 degrees C, adding 1-4 parts antifoaming agent, pressurization defoaming, continuous reaction for 30-50 minutes, returning to normal pressure for 1 hour to keep warm, adding 3-8 parts glucosamine quaternary ammonium salt, 1-3 parts ethyl acetate, 2-3 parts calcium chloride, 2-3 parts aspartic acid, 1-4 parts asparagines and 2-5 parts dimethyl taurine ammonia uniformly to the reaction furnace, heating the reaction to 170 degrees C, the temperature is lowered to room temperature, filling the reaction furnace with carbon dioxide gas and allowing to stand at room temperature for 10-25 minutes, injecting the reaction liquid into a medical rail co-extruder and the co-extruder adopts a needle valve type hot runner system to extrude the material, washing extruded conduit casting sequentially with sodium citrate solution and ultrapure water and drying at 65 degrees C, subjecting the dried catheter casting to surface activation and sonicating for 30-35 minutes, infiltrating the surface activated conduit in the solidifying liquid to perform curing, rinsing the catheter with ultrapure water and drying, ultrasonically cleaning the catheter, ultrasonic sterilization of the catheter ultrasound after the end of the ultrasound, drying and packaging to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ultrasonic medium is a linear alkylbenzene sulfonate, a fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, sodium sulfate, sodium cocoate sulfate, glycerin, dimethyl sulfoxide and water mixture and the mass ratio of 3:4:1:7:1:25. The composition of the solidifying liquid is soybean oil, glycerin, sodium citrate, glycine and a mixture of methylimidazole, gum arabic and water in a mass ratio of 5:3:3:1:2:6:30.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antifoaming agent is selected from the group consisting of phenylethyl oleate, lauryl phenylacetate, dimethyl carbonate, base silicone oil and a polyoxypropylene glyceryl ether.

PN CN108285844-A
TI Preparing fresh grape brewed brandy useful for e.g. reducing cholesterol, by pulping grapes, adding brewers and dry yeast, discharging carbon dioxide, fermenting, extracting methanol to obtain brandy, putting into oak barrel and blending.
AU YANG J
AE YANG J (YANG-Individual)
GA 201861388A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of fresh grape brewed brandy, involves (i) selecting fresh ripe grapes, cleaning, draining, steaming, sterilizing and pulping to obtain fruit pulp, (ii) reducing the temperature of fruit pulp, mixing wine yeast, controlling the temperature and saccharifying to obtain wine material, (iii) adjusting sugar concentration, adding brewer's yeast and dry yeast, stirring uniformly, sealing tank opening, discharging carbon dioxide, controlling the temperature, stirring, fermenting and ending alcohol fermentation, (iv) pouring the fermented wine into distillation kettle, and low-alcohol brandy is obtained, carrying out fine distillation of low-alcohol brandy in distillation kettle, controlling boiling point, extracting methanol and raising the boiling point to obtain brandy and (v) adding obtained brandy to oak barrel to be stored, blending, freezing, filtering and bottling.
   USE - The composition is useful for reducing cholesterol, inhibiting cancer cells, softening blood vessels and maintaining beauty.
   ADVANTAGE - The brandy is clear and transparent, has rich grape fragrance and harmonious oak fragrance, is delicious, mellow and sweet in taste, has unique flavor, provides refreshing, pleasant and excellent mouthfeel, is purely distilled, does not need to be blended and does not pierce throat and head, has vitamins, amino acids, dietary fiber and health care functions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparation of fresh grape brewed brandy, involves (i) selecting fresh ripe grapes, cleaning, draining, steaming for 25-30 minutes, sterilizing and pulping to obtain fruit pulp, (ii) reducing the temperature of fruit pulp to 30 degrees C, mixing wine yeast, stirring uniformly, sealing a tank opening, controlling the temperature to 25-30 degrees C and saccharifying for 2 days to obtain a wine material, (iii) adjusting the sugar concentration to 15-25% by using saccharified wine material and pH value to 4-6, adding brewer's yeast and rehydration high-temperature-resistant active dry yeast, stirring uniformly, sealing a tank opening, discharging carbon dioxide regularly, controlling the temperature to 25-30 degrees C, stirring twice every day, fermenting for 8-12 days until the residual sugar content is lower than 0.3% and ending alcohol fermentation, (iv) pouring the fermented wine into a distillation kettle, distilling directly and low-alcohol brandy is obtained, carrying out fine distillation in low-alcohol brandy, controlling boiling point at 65 degrees C for 20 minutes through slow distillation, extracting methanol and raising the boiling point to obtain brandy with temperature of 38-66 degrees C and (v) adding obtained brandy to oak barrel, blending, freezing, filtering and bottling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The content of brewer's yeast is 0.3-0.9 %mass.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic; Cytostatic; Vasotropic.

TI Solar photovoltaic (PV) heat-collecting integrated            carbon dioxide (CO2) air conditioning system, has            control system that is electrically connected with CO2            heat pump air-conditioning system.
AU REN J
   GAO S
   HE M
   GOU S
AE GANSU YIDE NEW ENERGY EQUIP CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 2022C3141D
AB    (CN217464715-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a solar photovoltaic                photovoltaic heat collecting integrated CO2 air                conditioning system, relating to the technical                field of solar utilization. It comprises a solar                photovoltaic collector, a counter-current                grid-connected photovoltaic generating system and a                control system, a CO2 heat pump air conditioning                system, the solar photovoltaic heat collector is                connected with the CO2 heat pump air conditioning                system, the solar photovoltaic heat collector is                electrically connected with the counter-current                grid-connected photovoltaic power generation                system, the counter-current grid-connected                photovoltaic power generation system is                electrically connected with the control system, the                control system is electrically connected with the                CO2 heat pump air conditioning system. AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethyleneamine compounds comprising                ethyleneamines and hydroxyethylethyleneamines,                comprises two reaction sequences, where the first                reaction sequence comprises in an adduction step                providing a carbon dioxide adduct of a starting                compound comprising amine moiety (I) or (II) or                (III), in a chain-extension step reacting a                hydroxy-functional compound comprising                ethanolamines and dihydroxyethane with an                ethyleneamine compound, at least part of the total                of hydroxy-functional compounds and ethyleneamine                compound is provided in the form of a carbon                dioxide adduct, to form a carbon dioxide adduct of                a chain-extended ethyleneamine compound, in an                elimination step converting a carbon dioxide adduct                of a chain-extended ethyleneamine compound to the                corresponding product ethyleneamine compound by                removal of the carbonyl group.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing                ethyleneamine compounds comprising ethyleneamines                and hydroxyethylethyleneamines.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: allows for flexibility in                starting materials used and products produced, and                an efficient use of apparatus; makes for the                possibility of combined product work-up and waste                treatment, which is an efficiency measure; and is                cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ethyleneamine compounds comprising                ethyleneamines and hydroxyethylethyleneamines,                comprises two reaction sequences, where the first                reaction sequence comprises in an adduction step                providing a carbon dioxide adduct of a starting                compound comprising amine moiety of formula                (-NH-CH2-CH2-NH-) (I) or (-NH-CH2-CH2-OH) (II) or                (HO-CH2-CH2-OH) (III), in a chain-extension step                reacting a hydroxy-functional compound comprising                ethanolamines and dihydroxyethane with an                ethyleneamine compound, at least part of the total                of hydroxy-functional compounds and ethyleneamine                compound is provided in the form of a carbon                dioxide adduct, to form a carbon dioxide adduct of                a chain-extended ethyleneamine compound, in an                elimination step converting a carbon dioxide adduct                of a chain-extended ethyleneamine compound to the                corresponding product ethyleneamine compound by                removal of the carbonyl group, and the second                reaction sequence comprises the steps of in a                ethylenedichloride reaction step reacting                ethylenedichloride with at least one compound                comprising ammonia, an ethyleneamine and/or an                ethanolamine to form a hydrochloride salt of an                ethyleneamine and/or ethanolamine, in a caustic                treatment step reacting the hydrochloride salt of                an ethyleneamine or ethanolamine with a base to                form ethyleneamine compound and an inorganic                chloride salt, in a salt separation step separating                the inorganic salt from the ethyleneamine compound,                with the first reaction sequence and the second                reaction sequence being connected in that at least                one of effluent from a step in the first reaction                sequence is provided as starting material to a step                in the second reaction sequence, effluent from a                step in the second reaction sequence is provided as                starting material to a step in the first reaction                sequence, a step of the first reaction sequence and                a step of the second reaction sequence are                combined, or effluent from a step in the first                reaction sequence is combined with effluent from a                step in the second reaction sequence.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ethyleneamine                  produced in the second reaction sequence is                  provided to at least one of the absorption step,                  the chain extension step, and the elimination                  step in the first reaction sequence. The carbon                  dioxide adduct of a ethyleneamine compound from                  the first reaction sequence is provided to the                  caustic treatment step in the second reaction                  sequence. The elimination step in the first                  reaction sequence comprises a caustic treatment,                  and product of the caustic treatment in the first                  reaction sequence is combined with product from                  the caustic treatment in the second reaction                  sequence, and the combined product is subjected                  to the salt separation step. The ethyleneamine                  reaction mixture from the first reaction sequence                  is combined with an ethyleneamine reaction                  mixture from the second reaction sequence, and                  the combined product is subjected to a separation                  step. The carbon dioxide adduct of an                  ethyleneamine from the first reaction sequence is                  provided to the ethylenedichloride reaction step                  from the second reaction sequence. The carbon                  dioxide adduct formed in the first reaction                  sequence is provided to the ethylenedichloride                  reaction step in the second reaction sequence,                  and a carbon dioxide adduct from the                  ethylenedichloride reaction step or the caustic                  treatment step in the second reaction sequence is                  provided to the elimination step in the first                  reaction sequence. The ethyleneamine produced in                  the second reaction sequence is provided to at                  least one of the absorption step, the chain                  extension step, or the elimination step in the                  first reaction sequence, in particular to the                  chain extension step and/or to the elimination                  step, and ethyleneamine product from the salt                  separation step in the second reaction sequence                  is provided to a fractionation step, where it is                  fractionated into different ethyleneamine product                  fractions, and an ethyleneamine compound fraction                  is withdrawn from the elimination step in the                  first reaction sequence and provided to                  fractionation step from the second reaction                  sequence and/or a product withdrawn from a                  separation step in the first reaction sequence is                  provided to the fractionation step in the second                  reaction sequence. The first reaction sequence                  and the second reaction sequence are connected in                  that effluent from the chain extension step of                  the first reaction sequence and effluent from the                  salt separation step in the second reaction                  sequence are provided to the same separation                  step.

PN CN109261183-A; CN109261183-B
TI Cobalt-based catalyst useful for methanation of carbon dioxide comprises cobalt oxide, cobalt carbide and modified titanium carbide.
AU ZHI C
   WANG Q
   ZHAO Y
   GAO C
   HU J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNTK-C)
   UNIV TAIYUAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNTK-C)
GA 2019114861
AB    NOVELTY - Cobalt-based catalyst comprises cobalt oxide and cobalt carbide and modified titanium carbide in a mass ratio of (0.4-0.8):(0.2-0.4).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methanation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has long service life, high reaction activity, good hydrothermal stability, good heat-conducting property and high mechanical strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing cobalt-based catalyst comprising (a) taking ethanol solution according to the content of silicon dioxide and titanium carbide of modified titanium carbide, immersing in titanium carbide in an equal volume, and then rotary evaporating at 100-150 degrees C until no volatile matter and calcining at at 400-800 degrees C for 2-8 hours to obtain a modified titanium carbide carrier, (b) dissolving cobalt salt and immersing in the above modified titanium carbide carrier in an equal volume based on content of cobalt carbide in the final catalyst, drying at 100-150 degrees C for 8-24 hours, and then calcining at 250-500 degrees C 4-12 hours to obtain intermediate A,(c) reducing the above intermediate A in hydrogen at 350-500 degrees C for 4-12 hours after the end of the reduction at 250-450 degrees C, switching carbon monoxide gas carbonization for 4-12 hours to obtain intermediate B, and (d) taking the soluble cobalt salt and adding to the content of cobalt trioxide in the final catalyst, dissolving and immersing in the intermediate B in an equal volume, then drying at 100-150 degrees C for 8-24 hours and then calcining at 250-500 degrees C for 4-12 hours to obtain final catalyst; and
   (2) use method a cobalt-based catalyst for carbon dioxide methanation comprising activating catalyst to be reductively prior to application, reducing catalyst when applied to a fixed bed reactor, a slurry bed reactor or a fluidized bed reactor 200-300 degrees C under 0.1-1.0 MPa, the reducing atmosphere is hydrogen, where the reduction gas space velocity is 1000-3000/hour, carrying out carbon dioxide methanation reaction in a fixed bed reactor at 300-400 degrees C under 2.0-5.0 MPa with reaction gas space velocity of 3000-10000/ hour, where reaction atmosphere is hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas in molar ratio of 3-3.8, carrying out carbon dioxide methanation reaction is carried out in a slurry bed reactor at 250-300 degrees C under 0.5-3.0 MPa with reaction gas space velocity of 2000-5000 h/ hour, where reaction atmosphere is hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas in a maolar ratio of 3.0-3.5 and carrying out carbon dioxide methanation reaction in a fluidized bed reactor at 350-400 degrees C under 1-4 MPa reaction gas space velocity of 5000- 12000/hours, where reaction atmosphere is hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixed gas in a molar ratio of 3-4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modified titanium carbide is silicon dioxide and titanium carbide in a mass ratio of 0.01-0.1:1. The silicon-containing reagent is trimethylsilanol, dimethylethoxysilane, trimethylethoxysilane, and methyldiethoxysilane. The soluble cobalt salt is cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate and basic cobalt carbonate. Preferred Method: The titanium carbide is prepared by (i) taking resorcinol, formaldehyde, anhydrous sodium carbonate and ethanol in a molar ratio of 1:2:(0.002-0.01): (3.5-10), mixing uniformly and then reacting at 40-70 degrees C for 10-120 minutes to obtain carbon sol; (ii) mixing the titanium source, nitric acid, deionized water and ethanol with a molar ratio of 1:(0.2-0.4):(2-8):(15-35) and uniformly mixing titanium source, nitric acid and total amount of 25-75 wt.% of ethanol and then adding deionized water and the remaining ethanol to prepare a solution and stirring at 5-25 degrees C for 5-15 minutes to obtain a titanium sol; (iii) uniformly mixing carbon sol and titanium sol in a molar ratio of resorcinol and titanium source of 1:(0.5-2) and then subjecting to a sol-gel reaction to obtain wet gel; (iv) aging the wet gel and drying at normal pressure to obtain a titanium carbide precursor; (v) heating and treating titanium carbide precursor at 1350-1550 degrees C for 1-10 hours under inert gas protection to obtain a titanium carbide product containing residual carbon; and (vi) calcining carbonized titanium-containing article in air at 500-700 degrees C for 2-4 hours, washing with hydrochloric acid and with water, filtering, and drying.
