PN CN110372382-A
TI Preparing barium gadolinium oxide by drying gadolinium barium carbonate, mixing barium carbonate and gadolinium, pressing, heat processing, carbon dioxide laser melting treatment of quenched sample of mixture, and grinding.
AU GONG W
AE UNIV HUIZHOU (UHZO-C)
GA 2019902749
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing barium gadolinium oxide (Ba3Gd4O9) involves: (a) drying the barium carbonate, and gadolinium: placing gadolinium barium carbonate in crucible, and placing in a furnace at 700-900 degrees C for a period of time; (b) mixing barium carbonate and gadolinium; (c) pressing, and heat processing: fully grinding the barium carbonate and gadolinium, uniformly pressing the mixture into a wafer, placing the wafer in pressed mixture to crucible, heating at 1100-1250 degrees C; (d) carbon dioxide laser melting treatment of the quenched sample of the mixture, placing mixture into a special copper crucible, then placing in carbon dioxide laser melting device, sealing, starting vacuum pumping, extracting the air in the device to reach a vacuum state, opening the nitrogen bottle, introducing nitrogen into the device, keeping the pressure in device at 105 Pa; using carbon dioxide laser and heating the mixture to melt it, quenching the molten sample to obtain the small pellet sample; and (e) grinding.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Ba3Gd4O9 (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method has advanced process, cooling is fast, the temperature is uniform, the whole temperature can be tracked and monitored, and the data is accurate and detailed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing barium gadolinium oxide (Ba3Gd4O9) involves: (a) drying the barium carbonate, and gadolinium: placing gadolinium barium carbonate in crucible, and placing in a furnace at 700-900 degrees C for a period of time; (b) mixing barium carbonate and gadolinium: adding absolute ethyl alcohol, grinding until the barium carbonate and the gadolinium oxide are mixed uniformly and volatilizing anhydrous ethanol completely; (c) pressing, and heat processing: fully grinding the barium carbonate and gadolinium, uniformly pressing the mixture into a wafer, placing the wafer in pressed mixture to crucible, heating at 1100-1250 degrees C, keeping the temperature for a certain time, taking out with air quenching,grinding the mixture into powder sample size uniformly for future use; (d) carbon dioxide laser melting treatment of the quenched sample of the mixture, placing mixture into a special copper crucible, then placing in carbon dioxide laser melting device, sealing, starting vacuum pumping, extracting the air in the device to reach a vacuum state, opening the nitrogen bottle, introducing nitrogen into the device, keeping the pressure in device at 105 Pa; using carbon dioxide laser and heating the mixture to melt it, quenching the molten sample to obtain the small pellet sample; and (e) grinding: using an agate mortar to grind small pellet sample obtained by carbon dioxide laser melting, then sieving by sieve, grinding and sieving repeatedly, to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In step (d), the vacuum pump is turned on, and air is extracted to reduce the internal pressure of the device to 1 Pa.In step (d), heating temperature of the carbon dioxide laser is determined by melting point temperature of Ba3Gd4O9. Preferred Composition: The raw material comprises 1.85002 plus minus 0.00001 g barium carbonate (BaCO3), 1.08794 plus minus 0.00001 g gadolinium oxide (Gd2O3), 20 plus minus 1 ml ethanol (C2H5OH), and carbon dioxide (CO2); where respective mass purity of each raw material is 99.9%, 99.99%, 99.5%, and 99.99%. Preferred Parameters: The holding time in step (a) is 3 or 12 hours. The carbon dioxide laser melting device power in step (d) is 100 watts. The screen in step (e) is 320 mesh. Preferred Component: The crucible is alumina crucible.

PN KR2032676-B1
TI Carbon dioxide utilization system used to produce hydrogen, comprises cathode unit including e.g. accommodation space and aqueous solution, anode unit including e.g. accommodation space and basic second aqueous solution, and connection unit.
AU GUNTAE K
   KIM C
   KIM J W
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 2019879012
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide utilization system comprises a cathode unit (110) including a first accommodation space (111), a first aqueous solution (115), and a cathode at least partially submerged in the first aqueous solution; an anode unit (150) including a second accommodation space (151), a basic second aqueous solution and an anode of metal at least partially submerged in the second aqueous solution; and a connection unit connecting the cathode unit and the anode unit. In the system, carbon dioxide introduced into the first aqueous solution is captured by bicarbonate ions to generate hydrogen ions, the anode supplies electrons to cathode through spontaneous oxidation reaction, and hydrogen ions and electrons of cathode are reacted to produce hydrogen. The connection unit has separator frame member. The separator frame member is provided to be detachable multiple porous ion transfer member that blocks movement of first aqueous solution and second aqueous solution and allows movement of ions.
   USE - The carbon dioxide utilization system is useful for producing hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes carbon dioxide through spontaneous electrochemical reaction without external power source to produce electricity, hydrogen and bicarbonate ions, has high efficiency and economical efficiency in manufacturing, installing and maintaining porous ion transfer member, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide utilization system comprises a cathode unit (110) including a first accommodation space (111), a first aqueous solution (115), and a cathode at least partially submerged in the first aqueous solution; an anode unit (150) including a second accommodation space (151), a basic second aqueous solution and an anode of metal at least partially submerged in the second aqueous solution; and a connection unit connecting the cathode unit and the anode unit. In the system, carbon dioxide introduced into the first aqueous solution is captured by bicarbonate ions to generate hydrogen ions, the anode supplies electrons to the cathode through spontaneous oxidation reaction, and the hydrogen ions and the electrons of the cathode are reacted to produce hydrogen. The connection unit has a separator frame member. The separator frame member is provided to be detachable a multiple porous ion transfer member that blocks the movement of the first aqueous solution and the second aqueous solution and allows the movement of ions. The anode is made of aluminum or zinc. In the cathode unit, hydrogen generation reaction is performed as given in specification.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon dioxide utilization system.
   Cathode unit (110)
   First accommodation space (111)
   First aqueous solution (115)
   Anode unit (150)
   Second accommodation space (151)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous ion transfer member is glass separator.

PN CN109228444-A
TI Method for injection molding carbon dioxide, dichlorofluoroethane thermoplastic polyurethane foam, involves adding thermoplastic polyurethane solid particles to barrel followed by entering chamber to homogenize, and forming bubble core.
AU ZHENG S
AE FUJIAN ANZHI EDUCATION SERVICES CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201909852P
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide, dichlorofluoroethane thermoplastic polyurethane foam injection molding method involves quantitatively adding thermoplastic polyurethane solid particles to the barrel from the hopper by metering, and mechanical friction and heating. The supercritical carbon dioxide and dichlorofluoroethane gas are mixed by a high pressure pump and the metering valve is controlled to inject a mixed gas into the thermoplastic polyurethane melt in the barrel, and thermoplastic polyurethane/carbon dioxide and dichlorofluoroethane gas two-phase system are mixed into a homogeneous system by a twin-screw mixing element and a single-screw conveying cooling static mixer, followed by entering the injection molding system storage chamber to homogenize to form a cold phase foam, and rapidly heating system by heater to decrease solubility of the gas in the thermoplastic polyurethane melt, precipitating gas from the thermoplastic polyurethane melt, forming a large number of micropore bubble core.
   USE - Method for injection molding carbon dioxide, dichlorofluoroethane thermoplastic polyurethane foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adjusting the softness and density in any part of the bottom of the same foaming shoe, and improving the wearing comfort of the shoe midsole, and reducing the production process and production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide, dichlorofluoroethane thermoplastic polyurethane foam injection molding method involves quantitatively adding thermoplastic polyurethane solid particles to the barrel from the hopper by metering, and mechanical friction and heating by the device causes the particles to melt. The supercritical carbon dioxide and dichlorofluoroethane gas are mixed by a high pressure pump and the metering valve is controlled to inject a mixed gas having a raw material mass of 0.4-0.8% into the thermoplastic polyurethane melt in the barrel, and thermoplastic polyurethane/carbon dioxide and dichlorofluoroethane gas two-phase system are mixed into a homogeneous system by a twin-screw mixing element and a single-screw conveying cooling static mixer, followed by entering the injection molding system storage chamber to homogenize to form a cold phase foam, and rapidly heating system by a heater to rapidly decrease the solubility of the gas in the thermoplastic polyurethane melt, precipitating gas from the thermoplastic polyurethane melt, forming a large number of micropore bubble core, after the cavity is filled with compressed air, rotating screw and squeezing to inject a thermoplastic polyurethane melt containing a large number of fine bubble core into the cavity for filling, when the filling is completed, the pressure drop in the cavity causes the bubble to expand, and the cooling action of the mold causes the bubble to solidify and shape.

AU GUO X
   LIU X
   ZHANG X
   GAO R
   HOU Y
   CUI Y
   WANG L
   GUO Y
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 202211717H
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 10-30 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide, 10-25 pts. wt. nonionic surfactant, 10-25 pts. wt. anionic surfactant, 5-15 pts. wt. initiator, 5-15 pts. wt. reaction assistant, 40-60 pts. wt. co-solvent and 10-30 pts. wt. fluorine-containing cymene, where the nonionic surfactant is polyoxyethylene alkyl alcohol amide (I).
   USE - The composition is useful for reducing minimum miscibility pressure of carbon dioxide and crude oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: utilizes fluorine-containing cymene and synthetic anionic and nonionic surfactants, which can significantly reduce the minimum miscibility pressure of carbon dioxide and crude oil; reduces the viscosity of crude oil; improves wax crystal structure in the high waxy crude oil; reduces the generation of the three-dimensional structure of the wax crystal molecules; is not easy to be decomposed by high temperature resistance; improves recovery factor of the high waxy crude oil; and is simple and efficient; and has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composition comprises 10-30 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide, 10-25 pts. wt. nonionic surfactant, 10-25 pts. wt. anionic surfactant, 5-15 pts. wt. initiator, 5-15 pts. wt. reaction assistant, 40-60 pts. wt. co-solvent and 10-30 pts. wt. fluorine-containing cymene, where the nonionic surfactant is polyoxyethylene alkyl alcohol amide of formula (I).
   R = 10-16C alkyl group; and
   n = 22-35.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing composition comprising (i) adding nonionic surfactant, anionic surfactant, initiator, co-solvent and reaction aid into a reaction vessel, stirring at 60-100degreesC for 1-3 hours to obtain anionic-nonionic surfactant, (ii) continue adding supercritical carbon dioxide into the reaction vessel under the conditions of pressure 7-15 MPa and temperature of 50-80degrees Celsius, and stirring for 2-4 hours, and (iii) reacting at a pressure of 7-15 MPa, slowly heating at 80-120degrees Celsius, adding fluorine-containing cymene into the reaction vessel, and stirring for 1-3 hours to obtain composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluorine-containing cymene is p-cymene-1-fluoro, and/or o-cymene-5-fluoro. The anionic surfactant is sulfonate surfactant. The sulfonate surfactant is sodium cocoyl isethionate. The anionic surfactant reacts with the nonionic surfactant to generate an anionic-nonionic surfactant, and the anionic-nonionic surfactant is of formula (II). The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptanenitrile and/or dimethyl azobisisobutyrate. The reaction assistant is boron trifluoride ether or heteropolyacid. The auxiliary solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone or tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether.

TI Preparing W/C reversed micelles useful for applying to natural fibers, e.g. real silk, involves mixing lipophilic particles, pulverized coal anionic surfactant, carbon dioxide and deionized water, adding fibers and lipophilic particles, stirring, pumping anionic surfactant and magnetic stirring.
AU YAO Y
   FAN R
AE SUZHOU HUMBERT ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021E2702H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing W/C reversed micelles comprises (i) mixing lipophilic particles, where the lipophilic particles include titanium dioxide, alumina, polytetrafluoroethylene, carbon black and pulverized coal anionic surfactant, carbon dioxide and deionized water, where the anionic surfactant comprises sodium bissulfosuccinate, namely 2-ethylhexyl, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and sodium dodecyl sulfate; (ii) adding fibers and lipophilic particles to 20 ml high-pressure visual reactor, pumping carbon dioxide and deionized water, start magnetic stirring, and rotating at 800-1500 revolutions/minute; (iii) pumping anionic surfactant into carbon dioxide and deionized water for magnetic stirring; and (iv) rotating lipophilic particles and anionic surfactants at high speed in carbon dioxide and deionized water by magnetic stirring.
   USE - The micelle is useful for applying to natural fibers, e.g. real silk, cotton, cashmere, dyeing, and the dyes are water-soluble dyes, e.g. direct dyes, weak acid dyes, and reactive dyes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids construction of fluorine-containing in W/C reversed micelle; has excellent dyeing effect; avoids use of large amount of water in conventional dyeing; and is a chemical process meeting green chemical idea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for dyeing for preparing W/C reversed micelles, comprising (i) placing the fibers, lipophilic particles and anionic surfactants into a 20 ml visual high-pressure reactor, introducing a quantitative amount of carbon dioxide, and magnetically stirring at a speed of 800-1500 revolutions/minute; (ii) dissolving the dye in quantitative deionized water, injecting it into the high-pressure reactor with a high-pressure syringe pump within 5-90 minutes to obtain W/C reverse micelle system, where the bath ratio is 1:30-1:300, and the dye concentration is 0.5-5% owf; (iii) controlling the temperature of the high-pressure reactor at 20-100degreesC, the pressure at 3-40 MPa, the magnetic stirring speed at 800-1500 revolutions/minute, and the dyeing for 30-300 minutes; (iv) passing fresh carbon dioxide to remove the floating color for 10-90 minutes; and (v) vacuum extracting the high-pressure reactor to normal pressure, and taking out the fibers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), mixing 10% lipophilic particles, 1% anionic surfactant, 88.77%, preferably 88.45% carbon dioxide and 0.23%, preferably 0.55% deionized water. The natural fiber material is made of 21/22D real silk, and the degumming rate of the 21/22D real silk is 25%.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the rotating speed is 1300 revolutions/minute, injecting the high-pressure syringe pump into the high-pressure reactor within 30 minutes. In step (iii), controlling high pressure reactor at 40degrees Celsius under the pressure of 10 MPa, magnetic stirring rotating under the speed of 1300 revolutions/minute, dyeing for 150 minutes, removing floating color for 30 minutes. In the step (i), the rotating speed is 1300 revolutions/minute. In the step (ii), injecting the high-pressure syringe pump into the high-pressure reactor within 30 minutes. In step (iii), controlling high pressure reactor temperature at 40degrees Celsius under pressure of 10 MPa, magnetic stirring under rotating speed of 1300 revolutions/minute, dyeing for 70 minutes, and removing floating color for 15 minutes.

PN CN113637996-A
TI Copper-based nanomaterial for electrocatalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, is prepared by            electrochemical anodic oxidation and cathode deposition            process, obtaining copper ions by anodic oxidation,            forming copper(II) hydroxide complex ions with hydroxyl            ions, and migrating to cathode.
AU ZHANG Y
   YU C
   WU Y
   ZHANG J
   XIA S
   WANG Y
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
GA 2021D33232
AB    NOVELTY - A copper-based nanomaterial for                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is                prepared by one-step electrochemical anodic                oxidation and cathode deposition process, obtaining                copper ions by anodic oxidation of high-purity                metal copper, forming copper(II) hydroxide-complex                ions with hydroxyl ions in the solution, and                migrating to cathode to obtain copper-based                nanomaterials by electrodeposition.
   USE - Copper-based nanomaterial for electrocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide, conversion of carbon                dioxide to methane and/or carbon dioxide to                ethylene products (all claimed), used for fuel such                as natural gas and coal gas, manufacturing                hydrogen, carbon black, carbon monoxide, ethynyl                formaldehyde and other chemicals, and civil and                chemical synthesis, synthetic fiber, synthetic                rubber, synthetic plastic and other basic chemical                raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based nanomaterial with enhanced                structure and uniform size is prepared by simple                and easy process, and ensures high selectivity and                stability. The method enables realizing anode                oxidation of metal copper and cathode                electro-deposition of divalent copper ion                synchronous, without any copper source and                additive, in short time. The copper-based                nano-material is grown on the conductive carbon                paper, which is directly utilized as gas diffusion                electrode for electro-catalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide. The electrolyte does not need to add                copper source, the product has high purity, less                device requirement, simple experiment operation,                suitable for mass production, and has high                selectivity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The copper-based nanomaterial for                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is                prepared by one-step electrochemical anodic                oxidation and cathode deposition process, obtaining                copper ions by anodic oxidation of high-purity                metal copper, forming copper(II) hydroxide                (Cu(OH)4)2- complex ions with hydroxyl ions in the                solution, and migrating to cathode to obtain                copper-based nanomaterials by                electrodeposition.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing copper-based nanomaterials, which                involves utilizing two-electrode electrolytic cell                system with high-purity metallic copper as anode,                carbon paper as cathode, and potassium hydroxide                aqueous solution as electrolyte, reacting for 0.5-1                hour under applied voltage, after reaction is                completed, taking out cathode immediately, washing                with water, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  potassium hydroxide aqueous solution is 3-6                  mol/L.Preferred Components: The high-purity metal                  copper is copper foil or foamed copper                  mesh.

PN US2021309586-A1; US11267767-B2
TI Gasification of carbonaceous feed involves contacting carbonaceous feed with oxygen-containing gasifier feed in gasifier, performing series of operations downstream of gasifier to provide gasifier effluent of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas, and adjusting series of operations.
AU LAMONT D C
   SEABA J
   KRAMER A
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 2021B50144
AB    NOVELTY - Gasification of a carbonaceous feed, comprises: contacting the carbonaceous feed with an oxygen-containing gasifier feed in a gasifier; performing a series of operations downstream of the gasifier to provide a gasifier effluent comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas, where at least one of the series of operations causes a change in a concentration of carbon oxide and/or carbon dioxide from an inlet of the operation to an outlet of the operation, or otherwise causes a change in a relative amount of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide from the inlet of the operation to the outlet of the operation; and adjusting at least one of the series of operations, in response to a makeup quantity of hydrogen, where the adjusting is performed to maintain a carbon monoxide/hydrogen gas and/or a carbon dioxide/hydrogen gas molar ratio of the gasifier effluent, following combining of the makeup quantity of hydrogen with a product of any of the series of operations.
   USE - The process is useful for gasification of a carbonaceous feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides methane with improved efficiencies; and reduces the overall carbon footprint associated with the production of renewable natural gas from a carbonaceous feed e.g. coal or biomass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an integrated gasification and electrolysis process, comprising contacting biomass with an oxygen-containing gasifier feed in a gasifier of a gasification zone (100) to provide a gasification zone effluent comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas, where the oxygen-containing gasifier feed comprises electrolysis oxygen obtained from electrolysis of water in an electrolyzer, and the gasification zone comprises at least one operation, downstream of the gasifier, affecting carbon monoxide concentration or carbon dioxide concentration of the gasification zone effluent, and adjusting the at least one operation in response to a makeup quantity of electrolysis hydrogen obtained from the electrolyzer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a flow scheme illustrating particular operations within the gasification zone and methanation zone that may be carried out in process.
   Gasification zone (100)
   Gasifier effluent (103)
   Gasifier (105)
   Hot tar conversion product (106)
   Hot tar conversion product (212)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The makeup quantity of hydrogen comprises electrolysis hydrogen, which is provided by an electrolyzer that further provides at least a portion of a makeup quantity of oxygen that is present in the oxygen-containing gasifier feed. At least one of the series of operations (i) consumes or produces carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide in the process, or (ii) adds or removes carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide in the process. At least one of the series of operations is a sour shift operation that consumes carbon monoxide and produces carbon dioxide in the process, or an acid gas removal operation that removes carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the process. The operation is adjusted by bypassing at least a portion of a feed to the operation. The makeup quantity of oxygen that is present in the oxygen-containing gasifier feed is determined at least partly based on a makeup quantity of the carbonaceous feed and/or a characteristic of the carbonaceous feed. The makeup quantity of oxygen is determined based on both the makeup quantity of the carbonaceous feed and the characteristic of the carbonaceous feed. At least one operation is a sour shift operation that consumes carbon monoxide and produces carbon dioxide in the process, or an acid gas removal operation that removes carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the process. At least one operation is adjusted by bypassing at least a portion of a feed to the operation. In addition to the operation affecting carbon monoxide concentration or carbon dioxide concentration, the gasification zone further comprises at least one of a tar conversion operation, a gasifier heat recovery operation, a gas filtration/scrubbing operation, a sulfur removal operation, and a supplemental cleaning operation.Preferred Components: The carbonaceous feed comprises coal or biomass. A makeup quantity of oxygen that is present in the oxygen-containing gasifier feed comprises electrolysis oxygen, obtained from an electrolyzer. The makeup quantity of oxygen that is present in the oxygen-containing gasifier feed further comprises air separation unit oxygen obtained from an air separation unit. The characteristic is a carbon content of the carbonaceous feed. The oxygen-containing gasifier feed further comprises water and/or carbon dioxide. The series of operations includes at least one of a tar conversion operation, a gasifier heat recovery operation, a gas filtration/scrubbing operation, a sulfur removal operation, and a supplemental cleaning operation. The process further comprises reacting at least a portion of the makeup quantity of hydrogen with at least a portion of the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the gasifier effluent, where the step of reacting is in a methanation reactor to form methane.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Carbon dioxide modified starch/polyvinyl alcohol fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping film comprises polyvinyl alcohol, starch, hydroxypropyl starch graft copolymer, plasticizer, antifoaming agent, film-forming auxiliary agent and water.
AU ZHOU X
   XU Z
   YIN G
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV ZHONGKAI AGRIC & ENG (UZKA-C)
GA 2019980070
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide modified starch/polyvinyl alcohol fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping film comprises 80 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 20 pts. wt. starch, 5-15 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl starch graft copolymer, 20 pts. wt. plasticizer, 1 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, 1 pts. wt. film-forming auxiliary agent, 900 pts. wt. water. The hydroxypropyl starch graft copolymer comprises 10-30 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl starch, 20-60 pts. wt. monomer, 0.1-2 pts. wt. initiator and 90 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide modified starch/polyvinyl alcohol fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping film.
   ADVANTAGE - The film maintains high barrier properties to oxygen and other gases and can be completely biodegradable, can transfer carbon dioxide in time and keep the carbon dioxide level in the membrane within a reasonable range, prevents carbon dioxide poisoning of fruits and vegetables and extend the freshness of fruits and vegetables.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide with sensitive performance of the modified starch/polyvinyl alcohol and vegetable preservative film comprising (1) weighing 10-30 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl starch, 20-60 pts. wt. monomer, 0.1-2 pts. wt. initiator, 90 pts. wt. water, heating and stirring for a certain time, adding the monomer of the hydroxypropyl starch solution after dissolving, stirring and adding initiator, heating, agitating and reacting for a certain time, taking out to obtain hydroxypropyl starch graft copolymer, (2) adding 20 pts. wt. starch and 80 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, dissolving in 900 pts. wt. water, heating and stirring for a certain time to prepare starch/polyvinyl alcohol solution, adding 20 pts. wt. plasticizer, and 1 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, adding 5-15 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl starch graft copolymer obtained in step (1) to dissolve starch/ polyvinyl alcohol solution, (3) using step (2) solution, adding 1 pts. wt. defoaming agent, high speed stirring and dispersing to obtain the uniformly mixed solution, (4) adding the mixed solution obtained in the step (3) and pouring into the mold and drying the film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The viscosity of the hydroxypropyl starch in a 5% aqueous solution at 20 degrees C is 500-2000 MPa/s. The monomer is dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate and diethylaminoethyl methacrylate. The initiator is ammonium persulfate, potassium persulfate. The hydroxypropyl starch graft copolymer is the graft rate is 36.7-55.2%, the molecular weight is 114000-129305. The polyvinyl alcohol is alcoholysis degree is less than or equal to 88% of polyvinyl alcohol. The starch is corn starch, potato starch, and pea starch. The plasticizer is glycerin and sorbitol. The antifoaming agent is glycerin monostearate and phosphate. The auxiliary agent is xanthan gum, acrylate and dimethicone. Preferred Method: In step (1), mixing hydroxypropyl starch and water, heating to 60-80 degrees C, stirring at 300-500 rotations/minutes for 5-15 minutes, after heating and stirring reaction, adding initiator, heating to 50-70 degrees C, stirring at 200-500 rotations/minutes for 2-4 hours. In the step (2), heating and stirring for a certain time, heating to 75-95 degrees C, stirring at 300-500 rotations/minutes for 30-90 minutes. In the step (3), the speed of high-speed stirring is 1000-2000 rotations/minutes for 30-60 minutes and obtained mixed solution has a solid content of 10-20%.

GA 202253392M

   USE - The tungsten oxide compound (I) is used as catalyst for oxygen evolution reaction in anode or positive electrode, and electrolytic cell for electrolyzing salt water and reducing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tungsten oxide compound (I) exhibits excellent catalytic activity when used as catalyst for oxygen evolution reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tungsten oxide compound of formula (I): NixFe(1-x)WO4, is new.
   x = 0-1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a catalyst for an oxygen evolution reaction in an anode or a positive electrode comprising the tungsten oxide;
   (2) a method for producing the tungsten oxide compound (I), which involves dissolving a tungstic acid salt, a nickel salt and an iron salt in a polyol and heating the polyol solution in which each of the salts is dissolved, or placing the tungstic acid salt, nickel salt, iron salt, and water in a pressure-resistant container and heating to synthesize the tungsten oxide compound (I);
   (3) an electrolytic cell comprising an anode chamber and a cathode chamber partitioned by an ion-permeable diaphragm, an anode arranged in the anode chamber, and a cathode arranged in the cathode chamber, where the tungsten oxide compound (I) supported on the anode as a catalyst;
   (4) a method for electrolyzing salt water, which involves supplying salt water containing an alkali to the anode chamber in the electrolytic cell, and supplying salt water to the cathode chamber to electrolyze the salt water; and
   (5) a method for electrolyzing salt water and reducing carbon dioxide, which involves supplying salt water containing alkali to the anode chamber in the electrolytic cell, supplying salt water to the cathode chamber, introducing carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide introduction section, electrolyzing the salt water, and reducing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The electrolytic cell further comprises a gas diffusion layer for supplying carbon dioxide to the cathode, and reducing carbon dioxide in the cathode chamber. The electrolytic cell further comprises a carbon dioxide introduction unit for introducing carbon dioxide so as to be in contact with the cathode on the opposite side of the cathode chamber facing the anode chamber, and reducing carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide introduction unit.

PN CN114016962-A
TI Method for exploiting coal bed gas, involves continuously injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into target coal layer through injection pipe, and taking female hole as centre, where branch hole is circular area periphery of radius influence control.
AU JIA X
   CHAI J
   SUN L
   BI R
   ZUO S
   SUN Z
   QI Q
   ZHANG L
AE NORTH CHINA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NCSI-C)
   UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
   CHINA COAL RES INST (CHLY-C)
GA 202228792K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drilling a female hole (1) in a ground or laneway to a target coal layer (9). The female hole passes through the target coal-layer. A branch hole (2) is opened in the coal layer along radial direction of a mother hole on an inner wall of the mother hole. An injection pipe with heat preservation function is installed in the female hole. One end of the injection pipe is sent to the target coal layer. A constant pressure relief valve is provided at an end of an injection pipe located at the target coal layer. The supercritical carbon dioxide is continuously injected into the target carbon dioxide layer through the injection tube. The branch hole and the branch hole are taken as a circular area periphery of radius influence control drilling pumping well.
   USE - Method for exploiting coal bed gas in coal-bed gas exploitation technology field using pumping and mining well.
   ADVANTAGE - The method injects supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to the deep coal layer in large range. The coal bed gas is replaced, the extraction wells arranged around the enriched area of the coal gas are matched with each other, and the deep gas is efficiently mined. The yield of the gas production well is improved, since the gas is displaced to form 4C enriched area.

PN US2021139325-A1; WO2021092144-A1
TI Method for using sorbents to enhance production of hydrogen from fuel such as hydrocarbon fuel in chemical industry, involves placing steam in portion of mixture, and interacting mixture with sorbent to form hydrogen gas.
AU HALLIDAY C G
   HARADA T
   HATTON T A
AE MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 202169789F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves exposing a sorbent, where the sorbent comprises a salt. Molten is formed to an environment to contain a mixture of gaseous fuel. Steam is placed in a portion of the mixture. The mixture is interacted with the sorbent to form a hydrogen gas. Fuel and water are reacted to produce hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is exposed to a sorbent, where the sorbent comprises a salt in molten form to capture a portion of the carbon dioxide. Reacting and exposing are conducted in a single vessel. Reacting is conducted in a first vessel. Exposing is conducted in a second vessel if the seond vessel separates from the first vessel. An inlet stream containing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide are transported from the first vessel to the second vessel.
   USE - Method for using sorbents to enhance production of hydrogen from fuel such as hydrocarbon fuel in a chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing duration during CO2 gas capture process, thus potentially reducing energy input required in the CO 2 capture process, and hence increasing ability to refresh poisoned sorbent with a purge. The method enables filling the molten alkali metal borates to efficiently allow hydrogen production because fast reaction kinetics, high capacities for carbon dioxide and excellent cyclic regenerability are improved. The method enables increasing ability to capture carbon dioxide at relatively low temperatures. The method enables simplifying other designs to incorporate capture of CO2 at high temperatures without taking advantage of sorption enhancing effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a system for generating hydrogen from a fuel for including a single vessel.
   Inlet stream (101)
   Entering vessel (102)
   Outlet stream (103)
   Optional outlet stream (104)

PN CN111659402-A
TI New high-performance copper-based nanocatalyst useful for preparing alcohol having more than 2 carbon atoms by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN X
   GUO H
   LI Q
   ZHANG H
   WANG C
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 2020922416
AB    NOVELTY - High-performance copper-based nanocatalyst (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for preparing alcohol having more than 2 carbon atoms by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has highly dispersed multiple interface structure, which provides rich multi-functional interface active sites for the efficient preparation of C2+ alcohols; realizes carbon dioxide conversion rate, and improves total alcohol selectivity and the mass of C2+ alcohols in total alcohols through the efficient synergistic effect of interface active sites; and solves the problems of high carbon monoxide byproduct selectivity, and relatively low total alcohol selectivity and carbon dioxide conversion rate when compared to the existing catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-performance copper-based nanocatalyst of formula (Kx-Cu1Fey/ZnaAlbO) (I) is new.
   x = 0.1-0.3;
   y = 0.5-2;
   a = 0.05-0.8; and
   b = 0.033-0.95.
   Provided that: x, y, a, b are molar ratios of metals K, Fe, Zn and Al to metal Cu respectively, and the molar ratio of (Cu2++Zn2+)/(Fe3++Al3+) in the catalyst is 3. (I) is prepared by high-temperature calcining of catalyst precursor with a layered double metal hydroxide structure of formula (Kx-Cu1FeyZnaAlb-LDHs) (II). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (I); and
   (2) use method of (I) in the preparation of C2+ alcohols by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprising taking 75% hydrogen and 25% carbon dioxide as raw material gas composition, reacting at 240-350 degrees C under pressure of 2-10 MPa in a micro fixed bed reactor in presence of (I), where volumetric space velocity is 2000-10000 h-1, and (I) is reduced at 350-400 degrees C for 2-5 hours in 10% hydrogen/argon atmosphere before the reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprising (i) dissolving copper salt, zinc salt, iron salt and aluminum salt in deionized water to obtain solution A, (ii) dissolving precipitating agent in deionized water to obtain solution B, (iii) adding deionized water into container, dropping solution A and solution B simultaneously at room temperature, stirring, controlling pH to 9.5-10 by adjusting the dropping rate, then aging, filtering, washing and drying to obtain layered double metal hydroxide catalyst precursor (Cu1FeyZnaAlb-LDHs), where the aging temperature is 100-120 degrees C, the aging time is 20-30 hours, and the drying temperature is 105 degrees C, (iv) dissolving potassium salt in deionized water to obtain solution C, adding catalyst precursor obtained in step (iii) and soaking at room temperature for 3-12 hours, and then drying at 105 degrees C to obtain (II), and (v) calcining (II) in air or inert gas atmosphere at 350-450 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain final product. Preferred Method: (II) is prepared by co-precipitating copper salt, zinc salt, iron salt and aluminum salt with precipitating agent, aging, drying, then loading alkali metal potassium by soaking, and drying. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the copper salt, zinc salt and iron salt are nitrate, acetate or chloride, and the aluminum salt is aluminum nitrate or aluminum acetate, the total metal ion concentration of solution A is 0.5 mol/l. The precipitating agent in the step (ii) is sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide, their concentrations are 0.25 mol/l and 0.8 mol/l respectively. The amount of deionized water added to the container in step (iii) is 10-50 ml. The potassium salt in step (iv) is potassium nitrate or potassium carbonate, and the concentration of solution C is 0.3 mol/l.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

GA 202075784G
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises e.g. an input unit (10) provided with an oyster shell for electricity production using oyster shell; a firing furnace (20) connected to input unit to sinter oyster shells to separate calcium oxide and carbon dioxide; a 1-1 transfer unit (30) connecting input unit and firing furnace; a 1-2 transfer unit connecting input unit and firing furnace; a calcium oxide storage unit (40) connected to firing furnace to store calcium oxide; a second transfer unit connecting firing furnace and calcium oxide storage unit; a first heat exchange unit (50) provided at a point where the 1-1 transfer unit and the second transfer unit intersect to transfer heat of the second transfer unit to the 1-1 transfer unit; a carbon dioxide storage unit connected to the firing furnace to store the carbon dioxide; a 3-1 transfer unit connecting the firing furnace and the carbon dioxide storage unit, and a second heat exchange part provided at point where the 1-2 and 3-1 transfer unit intersect.
   USE - The system is useful for producing electricity using oyster shell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system generates electricity by making closed loop of energy generated during reverse reaction of calcium carbonate, actively replaces environmental problems i.e. odor and aesthetics caused by oyster shells classified as industrial waste and utilizing waste resources, and captures carbon dioxide caused by existing plastic processing and converting it into a resource.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing electricity using the above system.

TI Coke hydrogen production system in thermal power plant, has carbon monoxide production unit provided with coke raw material inlet and gas purification and separation device that separates hydrogen and carbon dioxide from mixed gas.
AU JIN H
   CUI H
   CHEN H
   YANG Y
   ZHANG Y
AE HEPU ENERGY ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO (HEPU-Non-standard)
   HEPU ENERGY ENVIRONMENIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HEPU-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2337F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon monoxide production unit that is provided with a coke raw material inlet, a carbon dioxide inlet, and a carbon monoxide outlet. The coke raw material inlet is connected to a coke supply device (2). The carbon dioxide input port is connected to an output end of the carbon dioxide supply pipeline. The hydrogen production unit includes a carbon monoxide input port, a steam input port and a mixed gas output port. The carbon monoxide input port is connected to a carbon monoxide output port of the carbon monoxide production unit. The steam input port is connected to an output end of a steam supply pipe of the thermal power plant. The mixed gas output port is connected to an input port of the gas purification and separation device. The gas purification and separation device is used to separate hydrogen and carbon dioxide from the mixed gas.
   USE - Coke hydrogen production system in thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of coke can greatly reduce the cost of coal transportation and the generation of waste water and waste residue, and the use of thermal power plant facilities to treat waste gas, waste water and waste residue can greatly reduce the production cost of hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing hydrogen from coke in the thermal power plant.

   Carbon dioxide supply line (5)

PN US2019335778-A1; WO2019213135-A1; US11425915-B2
TI Concentrating phospholipids comprises subjecting a            dairy composition to a first ultrafiltration, where the            first ultrafiltration yields a first permeate and a            first retentate, treating the first retentate with            carbon dioxide.
AU GNANASAMBANDAM R
   PATEL H
AE LAND O'LAKES INC (LAND-Non-standard)
   LAND O'LAKES INC (LAND-Non-standard)
GA 201993512T
AB    NOVELTY - Concentrating phospholipids comprises                subjecting a dairy composition to a first                ultrafiltration, where the first ultrafiltration                yields a first permeate and a first retentate,                treating the first retentate with carbon dioxide,                subjecting the first retentate to microfiltration,                where the microfiltration yields a second permeate                and a second retentate, treating the second                retentate with carbon dioxide, subjecting the                second retentate to a second ultrafiltration, where                the second ultrafiltration yields a third permeate                and a third retentate, and collecting the third                retentate, where the third retentate comprises 30                wt.% phospholipids.
   USE - Method of concentrating phospholipids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a system for concentrating phospholipids,                which comprises a first ultrafiltration apparatus                configured to subject a dairy composition to a                first ultrafiltration, where the first                ultrafiltration yields a first permeate and a first                retentate, a microfiltration apparatus configured                to subject the first retentate to microfiltration,                where the microfiltration yields a second permeate                and a second retentate, a second ultrafiltration                apparatus configured to subject the second                retentate to a second ultrafiltration, where the                second ultrafiltration yields a third permeate and                a third retentate, where the third retentate                comprises a solution comprising 30 wt.%                phospholipids, a treatment chamber housing the                microfiltration apparatus and the second                ultrafiltration apparatus, and a carbon dioxide                injector coupled with the treatment chamber, the                carbon dioxide injector configured to inject carbon                dioxide into the treatment chamber, the                microfiltration apparatus, and the second                ultrafiltration apparatus; and
   (2) an edible composition, which comprises a                total fat content of about 40-45 wt.%, where about                90 wt.% of the total fat content comprises                phospholipids, a total protein content of about                40-75 wt.%, and a lactose content of about 3-5                wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The dairy composition                  comprises butter serum or buttermilk. The first                  retentate is treated with carbon dioxide during                  microfiltration. The second retentate is treated                  with carbon dioxide during the second                  ultrafiltration. Tthe first ultrafiltration is                  performed using a first ultrafiltration membrane                  configured to retain molecules having a molecular                  weight greater than about 10 kDa. The edible                  composition comprises a liquid sourced from                  buttermilk or butter serum. Preferred Process:                  The method further comprises spray drying the                  third retentate to yield a powder                  composition.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN110280277-A; CN110280277-B
TI Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst including copper selenide hollow microsphere, and the preparation of catalyst comprising e.g. adding styrene monomer and acrylic acid to distilled water, stirring, dispersing and heating.
AU ZHAO Y
   WANG J
AE UNIV ZHAOQING EAST CHINA NORMAL PHOTOELE (UYZH-Non-standard)
GA 2019841919
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is copper selenide hollow microsphere.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high electrocatalytic activity and selectivity for carbon dioxide reduction, and can improves the energy efficiency of carbon dioxide utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst comprises (1) adding styrene monomer and acrylic acid to distilled water, stirring, dispersing, increasing the temperature of reaction system to 50-70 degrees C by heating in a water bath, removing oxygen in solution by nitrogen for 20-40 minutes, adding potassium persulfate aqueous solution, continuously reacting for 10-24 hours under the stirring condition of 200-300 revolutions/minute to obtain emulsion, centrifuging emulsion, washing the centrifuged product for three times with ethanol, using a vacuum freeze dryer, freeze-drying at a temperature of not greater than 50 Pa and less than -30 degrees C to obtain polystyrene microspheres, (2) dispersing copper sulfate, sodium hydroxide and polystyrene microspheres in deionized water, reacting in an oil bath at 90-150 degrees C for 2-5 hours, collecting the product by centrifugation, washing with deionized water for 3-5 times, and drying in an oven at 60 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain precursor, placing precursor in a muffle furnace and calcining to obtain copper oxide hollow microspheres, and (3) dispersing selenium powder and copper oxide hollow microspheres in anhydrous ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing uniformly, collecting the product by centrifugation, placing it on bottom of porcelain boat, placing in a tube furnace, introducing argon gas, performing the whole process under argon gas, calcining, cooling the furnace and collecting the product to obtain copper selenide hollow microsphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), using 50-100 ml styrene monomer, 5-10 ml acrylic acid, 300-500 ml distilled water, and 10-25 ml potassium persulfate aqueous solution, where the concentration of potassium persulfate aqueous solution is 30-50 mmol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (2), using 1-3 g copper sulfate, 0.1-0.5 g sodium hydroxide, 1-2 g polystyrene microspheres and 100-200 ml deionized water; the specific conditions for calcining at 300-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours. In the step (3), 0.1-1 g selenium powder, 0.5-1 g copper oxide hollow microspheres and 10-20 ml anhydrous ethanol; introducing argon gas at a rate of 100-200 ml/minute, where the specific conditions for calcination are: increasing the temperature to 200-500 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, and maintaining temperature for 1-3 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)

PT P
PN IN201921022220-A
TI Apparatus for preparing samples for measuring            carbon-14 for radiocarbon dating, has gas removal unit            coupled to absorption unit to remove radon gas present            in carbosorb and scintillator mixture saturated with            carbon dioxide gas.
AU THODUKAYIL B J
AE THODUKAYIL B J (THOD-Individual)
GA 2019654494
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a gas absorption                vessel (320) for preparing samples for measurement                of C-14 for radiocarbon dating by absorbing carbon                dioxide gas drawn from a condenser at predefined                pressure and by bubbling the gas through a                carbosorb and scintillator mixture till the                carbosorb and scintillator mixture is saturated                with the carbon dioxide gas. A first set of                grease-less standard joints (150) and a second set                of grease-less standard joints (160) are located on                a vacuum glass line (110). A gas removal unit is                mechanically coupled to an absorption unit to                remove radon gas that is present in the carbosorb                and scintillator mixture saturated with the carbon                dioxide gas by flushing the mixture with ultra-pure                nitrogen gas from an external cylinder (330), where                the external cylinder is connected to an arm of a                Y-joint that is connected to the vacuum glass                line.
   USE - Apparatus for preparing samples for measuring                carbon-14 for radiocarbon dating.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus allows the vacuum glass line to                stop back suction of the carbosorb and scintillator                mixture into the vacuum glass line, so that waste                of carbon dioxide gas sample can be prevented. The                apparatus avoids need for a dropping funnel with a                greasy stop cock, which often leaks out acid, thus                preventing sample preparation for carbon dioxide                gas absorption from being affected.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing samples for measuring                carbon-14 for radiocarbon dating.

PN WO2019082155-A1; US2019376936-A1; US11143634-B2
TI Method for controlling mass flow rate within carbon dioxide-based chromatography system involves maintaining constant mass flow rate of carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide pump by achieving target temperature value at carbon dioxide pump.
AU TARAFDER A
   HILL J F
   SHREVE J A
   HILL J
   SHREVE J
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   TARAFDER A (TARA-Individual)
   HILL J (HILL-Individual)
   SHREVE J (SHRE-Individual)
GA 201937534V
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves measuring (101) a change in pressure within a carbon dioxide (CO2) pump using a pressure sensor, receiving (103) a new pressure measurement at a computing system from the pressure sensor and retrieving (105) a target temperature value corresponding to the new pressure measurement from a database using the computing system. A temperature control command is generated (107) using the computing system. The temperature control command controls a CO2 pump heater or cooler in order to achieve the target temperature value at the CO2 pump. A constant mass flow rate of CO2 from the CO2 pump is maintained (109) by achieving the target temperature value at the CO2 pump.
   USE - Method for controlling mass flow rate within a CO2-based chromatography system.
   ADVANTAGE - A constant mass flow rate is achieved by dynamically adjusting the temperature of the CO2 pump in response to changes in delivery pressure, without the need to adjust or control the pump stroke volume. Allows for increased control of the mass flow rate of highly compressible fluids, such as CO2. Allows a user to dynamically adjust the temperature of a CO2 pump in order to compensate for changes in pressure within the CO2, thus preventing changes in the mass flow rate of CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for controlling mass flow rate from a CO2 pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for controlling mass flow rate within a carbon dioxide based chromatography system.
   Method (100)
   Measuring pressure change (101)
   Receiving new pressure measurement (103)
   Retrieving target temperature value (105)
   Generating temperature control command (107)
   Maintaining constant mass flow rate (109)

AU GOODRICH B L
   KIM P
   TARGETT M
   WALTER J C
   PYOUNGCHUNG K
   JUNG K P
AE TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   GOODRICH B L (GOOD-Individual)
   KIM P (PYOU-Individual)
   TARGETT M (TARG-Individual)
   WALTER J C (WALT-Individual)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
   TERRAPOWER LLC (TEPO-C)
GA 202094548J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving mesophase pitch in                feedstock (305), involves contacting coal tar                comprising at least some mesophase pitch precursor                with a supercritical carbon dioxide and toluene                mixture for removing at least some toluene soluble                components from the coal tar to obtain a toluene                insoluble fraction, separating the supercritical                carbon dioxide and toluene mixture from the toluene                insoluble fraction, washing the toluene insoluble                fraction with quinoline to obtain a quinoline and                quinoline soluble fraction mixture of the toluene                insoluble fraction, separating the quinoline from                the quinoline soluble fraction, and mixing the                quinoline soluble fraction with supercritical                carbon dioxide to obtain a supercritical carbon                dioxide/quinoline soluble fraction mixture.
   USE - Method for improving mesophase pitch in                feedstock such as coal tar, used for manufacturing                carbon fibers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically improves the relative                amount and quality of mesophase pitch in                feedstocks. The mesophase pitch is capable of                producing carbon fibers having improved fiber                strand properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of carbon fibers, which involves                performing above method using supercritical fluid                solvent and co-solvent mixture to obtain fibers of                mesophase pitch.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for the pitch production.
   Thermochemical conversion system (302)
   Thermochemical reaction chamber (304)
   Feedstock (305)
   Heat transfer element (307)
   Heat source (308)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contacting step                  further involves passing the supercritical carbon                  dioxide/quinoline soluble fraction mixture                  through an extruder, and separating supercritical                  carbon dioxide from the quinoline soluble                  fraction to obtain fibers of mesophase pitch. The                  method involves performing a toluene wash on the                  toluene insoluble fraction for removing                  additional soluble material from the toluene                  insoluble fraction. The step of separating the                  quinoline from the quinoline soluble fraction                  further involves distilling the quinoline and                  quinoline soluble fraction mixture, and                  evaporating the quinoline to obtain the quinoline                  soluble fraction mixture. The method further                  involves heating the quinoline soluble fraction                  to 300-400 degrees C for less than 24 hours,                  performing a stabilization operation,                  carbonization operation and graphitization                  operation on the fibers of mesophase pitch,                  passing supercritical carbon dioxide and toluene                  mixture through a reactor vessel comprising coal                  tar, and circulating the quinoline through the                  reactor vessel for up to 24 hours before                  separating the quinoline from the quinoline                  soluble fraction. Preferred Composition: The                  supercritical fluid solvent is at least one                  chosen from carbon dioxide, water, methane,                  nitrous oxide, ethane, propane, ethylene,                  propylene, methanol, ethanol, and acetone. The                  co-solvent is at least one chosen from benzene,                  xylene, tetralin, tetrahydrofuran, pyridine and                  quinolone.

PN CN110743512-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide molecularly imprinted adsorbent for e.g. sensor detection, by activating sieved sunflower biomass to obtain activated carbon, adding with functional monomer, sealing in inert atmosphere and separating solid product.
AU WANG R
   SU W
   ZHAO T
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202012789Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon dioxide molecularly imprinted adsorbent, involves (i) taking the sieved sunflower biomass for carbonization and activating to obtain activated carbon, and (ii) using the biomass activated carbon from step (i) as a carrier, adding template molecule and functional monomer to dissolve in the mixed solvent, adding crosslinking agent and initiator, sealing the reaction in an inert atmosphere, separating the solid product from the reaction system, washing and drying to obtain the carbon dioxide molecularly imprinted adsorbent.
   USE - The absorbent is used for adsorption and separation of substances, sensor detection, catalytic degradation and membrane separation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes sunflower as a molecular template that provides a large specific surface area, facilitates the loading of imprinted polymers and increases the number of adsorption sites. The method combines the carbon dioxide adsorption technology with the surface imprinting technology and the adsorption method, which improves the selectivity of the adsorbent, eliminates the interference of other gases in the flue gas, and improves adsorption efficiency of the adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for regenerating the above-mentioned adsorbent after use, involving placing the adsorbed saturated adsorbent in water and introducing nitrogen for desorption and regeneration, until carbon dioxide molecules cannot be detected to overflow from the water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the carbonization of the sunflower biomass includes placing the sunflower biomass in a carbonization furnace, carbonizing at 673-873 K, and at a holding time of 80-100 minutes in an inert atmosphere such as nitrogen, and cooling to room temperature. In step (i), the preparation method of the activated carbon includes uniformly mixing the material obtained after carbonization with an activator in high-purity water, and impregnating at room temperature for 24-48 hours, and drying the impregnated material, and heating the dried material to an activation temperature of 773-1073 K in an inert atmosphere for 90-100 minutes, to maintain the temperature and obtaining the activated carbon, where the activator is potassium hydroxide. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the inert atmosphere is nitrogen, where the flow rate is 10-200 ml/minute at a reaction time of 10-20 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixed solvent comprises acetonitrile and toluene in the volume ratio of 1:1. The adding ratio of the carrier, template molecule, functional monomer, mixed solvent, crosslinking agent, and initiator is (1-10 g):(0.01-1 g):(0.5-2 g):(20-100 ml):(1.2-9 ml):(0.01-0.1 g). Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent includes at least one of tetraethoxysilane, phenyltriethoxysilane, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, and triallyl isocyanurate. The functional monomer includes at least one of methacrylic acid, methyl methacrylate, trifluoromethacrylic acid and acrylamide. The template molecule is oxalic acid. The initiator includes at least one of 2,2'-azobis(2,4-dimethylvaleronitrile), azobisisobutyronitrile, benzoyl peroxide and potassium persulfate.

PN CN114989580-A
TI High gas barrier polyethylene terephthalate (PET)            material comprises PET resin and PET/carbon            nitride-supercritical carbon dioxide (C3N4-SCCO2),            where PET/C3N4-SC CO2 is composite masterbatch of            PET/g-C3N4 treated with supercritical carbon            dioxide.
AU LIU S
   YUAN S
   CHEN B
   ZHAO T
AE UNIV XIHUA (UYXH-C)
GA 2022B96598
AB    NOVELTY - High gas barrier polyethylene terephthalate                (PET) material comprises 60-90 pts. wt. PET resin,                and 10-40 pts. wt. PET/carbon nitride-supercritical                carbon dioxide (C3N4-SCCO2), where the PET/C3N4-SC                CO2 is composite masterbatch of PET/g-C3N4 treated                with supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as high gas barrier PET material.
   ADVANTAGE - The high gas barrier PET material has                excellent gas barrier property, dispersibility,                barrier property and mechanical property, and                reduced inorganic particle agglomeration effect,                and reduces the problems e.g. re-agglomeration of                g-C3N4 and substrate degradation caused by                difference of compatibility or processing                temperature of the master batch substrate and the                processing substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing high gas barrier PET material,                comprising crushing the composite master batch,                mixing with PET resin/mixed resin containing PET,                then melting, and extruding to obtain PET                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  PET/C3N4-SC CO2 comprises placing PET/g-C3N4 in a                  supercritical high pressure kettle, controlling                  swelling and penetration temperature to                  150-250&#8451;, where swelling and osmotic                  pressure are 10-30 MPa, after holding the                  pressure for a period of time, and releasing the                  pressure quickly. The preparation method of                  PET/g-C3N4 comprises mixing PET and g-C3N4 to                  obtain a mixture, mixing at 260-300&#176;C                  and 20-100 rotations/minute to obtain PET/g-C3N4                  masterbatch. The PET and g-C3N4 are treated by                  ultrasonically blending, and during ultrasonic                  blending, anhydrous ethanol is added as an                  ultrasonic transmission medium. The content of                  g-C3N4 is 40-60% of the total PET/g-C3N4. The                  preparation method of g-C3N4 comprises placing                  nitrogen-containing precursor in a muffle                  furnace, heating to 500-580&#176;C at a rate                  of 5-20&#176;C/min, keeping temperature for                  2-4 hours, then heating at a rate of                  5-20&#176;C/min to 500-580&#176;,                  reducing the heating rate to room temperature,                  grinding the intermediate, soaking the                  intermediate in 2-5 mol/l nitric acid solution                  for 2-4 hours, ultrasonically dispersing for                  20-50 minutes during the soaking process,                  repeatedly washing the soaked mixed solution with                  deionized water until neutral, then placing at                  100-150&#8451; to dry for 4-8 hours, and then                  grinding into powder to obtain g-C3N4.Preferred Composition: The PET material                  further includes: 1-20 pts. wt. high-barrier                  resin; and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. heat                  stabilizer.Preferred Components: Nitrogen-containing                  precursor includes urea, dicyandiamide and/or                  melamine. The high barrier resin includes                  polyethylene naphthalate, ethylene/vinyl alcohol                  copolymer, polyethylene, polypropylene or                  polyvinylidene chloride.

TI Improving phospholipase C protein yield comprises            e.g. adjusting flow rate of methanol according to            content of carbon dioxide in tail gas in fermentation            process in stage of inducing and expressing            phospholipase C, and controlling content of carbon            dioxide in tail gas during fermentation process.
AU JIANG Y
   LI Q
   YE X
AE YIHAI JIALI LIANYUNGANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (YHJL-C)
GA 202271494R
AB    NOVELTY - Improving phospholipase C protein yield                comprises adjusting the flow rate and flow rate of                methanol according to the content of carbon dioxide                in the tail gas in the fermentation process in the                stage of inducing and expressing phospholipase C,                and controlling the content of carbon dioxide in                the tail gas during the fermentation process to                1-4.5% V/V to improve the protein yield of                phospholipase C.
   USE - The method is useful for improving                phospholipase C protein yield.
   ADVANTAGE - The protein yield of phospholipase C is                increased by up to 40% compared with the original                constant temperature and constant control process,                the unit production cost is reduced, energy saving                and consumption reduction are beneficial, and the                industrialization of phospholipase C is                facilitated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phospholipase C is                  phospholipase C induced and expressed by Pichia                  pastoris.Preferred Method: The specific steps of the                  method are: (1) placing the fermentation medium                  into a 5 KL fermentation tank, sterilizing at                  121&#176;C for 30 minutes, adjusting the pH                  to 5 using aqueous ammonia after cooling,                  inserting the secondary seed liquid, at the                  initial speed of 120 revolutions/minute, the                  ventilation rate of 3 m3/minute, the initial                  fermentation temperature of 30&#176;C, the                  tank pressure of 0.05 MPa, performing the                  fermentation culture for 150 hours, performing                  the initial fermentation by adjusting the                  stirring speed to make the DO% to 15-30%, (2)                  when the DO value of the fermenter rapidly rises                  from 15-30% to 60-70%, adding 60-80%                  concentration of glycerol at the rate of 55-65                  kg/hour to make DO% greater-than 25, until the                  wet weight of the bacteria reaches 200 g/kg or                  more, gradually lowering the temperature from the                  initial 30-24&#176;C, adjusting the pH to                  gradually increase to 5.8 after stable                  cultivation, at this stage, the carbon dioxide                  content is not controlled, (3) after the end of                  the glycerol feeding culture stage, carrying out                  1 hour starvation culture, and stopping feeding,                  and (4) after the starvation culture is                  completed, adding methanol by flow, where the                  flow rate of methanol is 1.5-6.5 kg/hour per ton                  of fermentation broth, according to the real-time                  monitoring data of carbon dioxide content in the                  tail gas, the flow rate of methanol is 1-1.2                  times of carbon dioxide content per ton of                  fermentation broth, the maximum is not more than                  6.5 kg/hour, the flow rate is adjusted in real                  time every 2 hours, performing DO-spike to detect                  whether methanol is added too much, after 60                  hours, adjusting the temperature to                  28&#176;C, the total fermentation period is                  150 hours, and after the fermentation, obtaining                  the phospholipase C protein with a wet weight of                  400 g/kg of bacterial cells and a yield of 13.1                  g/l.
