PN CN114230451-A
TI Preparing halogenated acid compound, comprises e.g. reacting halogenated alkyl alcohol compound with (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-yl)oxyl, sodium hypochlorite and sodium chlorite in solvent in presence of carbon dioxide.
AU LIU X
   CHU C
   HONG H
   LIU B
   CHEN X
AE SHANGHAI YAOTAN PHARM RES DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202249260Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing halogenated acid compound (I), comprises reacting halogenated alkyl alcohol compound (II) with (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-yl)oxyl, sodium hypochlorite and sodium chlorite in solvent in presence of carbon dioxide to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing halogenated acid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes cheap and easily available carbon dioxide to adjust the pH of the reaction; has high production yield, relatively low toxicity and environmental protection, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing halogenated acid compound of formula (HO(O=C)-R-X) (I), comprises reacting halogenated alkyl alcohol compound of formula (HO-CH2-R-X) (II) with (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-yl)oxyl (TEMPO), sodium hypochlorite and sodium chlorite in solvent in presence of carbon dioxide to obtain final product.
   R = 1-20C alkylene, preferably -(CH2)7-; and
   X = halogen, preferably bromine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the preparation of (I), the solvent is nitrile solvent and/or water, preferably nitrile solvent i.e. acetonitrile; the mass ratio of solvent to II is 5-7:1, preferably 6:1; the ratio of the substance amount of TEMPO to II is 0.005-0.015:1, preferably 0.01:1; the ratio of the substance amount of the sodium hypochlorite to II is 0.01-0.06:1, preferably 0.03:1; the ratio of the substance amount of the sodium chlorite to II is 1-3:1, preferably 1.1:1; carbon dioxide is introduced into the reaction system in the presence of carbon dioxide; the amount of the carbon dioxide is to maintain the reaction system under acidic conditions, preferably to maintain the pH value of the reaction system within 5-7; the temperature of the reaction is less than 35degrees Celsius, preferably 20-30degrees Celsius; the sodium hypochlorite and the sodium chlorite are added to the reaction system independently; the sodium hypochlorite is added to the reaction system in two batches, and is recorded as the i batch and the ii batch, the ratio of the amount of substances in the i-th batch to the ii-th batch is 0.3-0.5:1; the sodium hypochlorite is added to the reaction system in the form of an aqueous sodium hypochlorite solution; the sodium chlorite is added to the reaction system in two batches, which are denoted as the 1st batch and the 2nd batch and the ratio of the amount of substances in the first batch to the second batch is 0.2-0.3:1; and the sodium chlorite is added to the reaction system in the form of aqueous sodium chlorite solution; the mode of adding the sodium hypochlorite and the sodium chlorite is to first add the i batch of sodium hypochlorite and the first batch of sodium chlorite during the described reaction, then adding the ii batch sodium hypochlorite and the second batch of sodium chlorite, the mass fraction of sodium hypochlorite is 2-10 wt.% when the sodium hypochlorite participates in the reaction in the form of aqueous sodium hypochlorite solution and the mass ratio of sodium chlorite to water is 1:2-5 when the sodium chlorite participates in the reaction in the form of an aqueous sodium chlorite solution, preferably (II) is 8-bromooctan-1-ol and (I) is 8-bromooctanoic acid; the mass ratio of the nitrile solvent and water is 1.5-2.5:1; when the solvent is nitrile solvent and water; the amount of the carbon dioxide is to maintain the pH value of the reaction system in 6-7; the solvent is acetonitrile and water; the sodium hypochlorite is added into reaction system in two batches and the ratio of the amount of material of the i batch and the ii batch is 0.4:1; the sodium chlorite is added to the reaction system in two batches, and the ratio of the amount of the material in the first batch to the second batch is 0.25:1; the sodium hypochlorite is in the form of aqueous sodium hypochlorite solution when participating in the reaction and the amount of sodium hypochlorite is 6 wt.% in the sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution; and the mass ratio of sodium chlorite to water in the aqueous sodium chlorite solution is 1:3.8-4.2 when the sodium chlorite participates in the reaction in the form of aqueous sodium chlorite solution. The method comprises mixing solvent, II and TEMPO, adding the sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution of the i batch and the sodium chlorite aqueous solution of the first batch, then introducing carbon dioxide gas, initiating the reaction, continuously adding sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution of the iind batch and the sodium chlorite aqueous solution of the second batch to reacting, preferably the solvent is acetonitrile and water with a mass ratio of 1.5-2.5:1; the mass ratio of solvent to II is 5-7:1; the ratio of the amount of the TEMPO to II is 0.005-0.015:1; the ratio of the substance amount of the sodium hypochlorite to II is 0.01-0.06:1; the ratio of the amount of sodium chlorite to the amount of II is 1-3:1; the ratio of the substance amount of the sodium hypochlorite in the i-th batch of sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution to the ii-th batch of sodium hypochlorite aqueous solution is 0.3-0.5:1; the ratio of the substance amount of sodium chlorite in the first batch of sodium chlorite aqueous solution to the second batch of sodium chlorite aqueous solution is 0.2-0.3:1; the amount of carbon dioxide is to maintain the pH value of the reaction system within 5-7, the temperature of the reaction is less than 35degrees Celsius. The post-processing step includes (i) quenching the reaction, adjusting the pH of the reaction solution to 2-3, (ii) allowing to stand the reaction solution obtained from the treatment in step (i) for stratification, retaining the organic phase, and concentrating, (iii) dissolving the concentrated solution obtained by processing in (ii) with water-insoluble organic solvent, washing, and concentrating to obtain crude product, and (iv) purifying the crude product obtained after treatment in step (iii), where the quenching is quenching with sodium sulfite; the adjustment is adjusted with dilute sulfuric acid, and the dilute sulfuric acid is dilute sulfuric acid with an amount of 25-40 wt.%, the water-insoluble organic solvent is dichloromethane or ethyl acetate; the washing is to wash with sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution; the purification step includes (i) dissolving the crude product with hydrocarbon solvent, cooling and crystallizing or (ii) distilling the crude product under reduced pressure.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Elaeagnus pungens essential oil                extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                involves picking fresh Elaeagnus pungens branches,                removing pedicers, taking flower, putting selected                Elaeagnus flower into dryer, drying to obtain dried                Elaeagnus flower, putting the dried Elaeagnus                flower into the supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction device for extraction to obtain the                Elaeagnus flower primary extract, putting the                obtained Elaeagnus flower primary extract into the                cold storage, performing low-temperature removal to                obtain the hazardous waxy, injecting distilled                water in the Elaeagnus flower primary extract after                the de-waxing, stirring, mixing to obtain a                resulting mixture, distilling and extracting the                mixture to obtain a primary distilled extract, that                is Elaeagnus pungens essential oil.
   USE - Method for preparing Elaeagnus pungens                essential oil extracting by supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared Elaeagnus pungens essential oil                has high natural component, and the derivative is                small, no chemical solvent is added.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The picking time of                  the Elaeagnus pungens flower is 8 am -10:30 in                  the morning, and the maturity is from the                  blooming to the beginning of the flower, choosing                  fresh, plump and disease-free flower, and the                  temperature in the constant-temperature car                  should not be higher than 20degrees Celsius. The                  drying temperature of the low-temperature blast                  drying is 40-45degrees Celsius, and the drying                  time is 15-30 minutes. The extraction pressure in                  the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                  device is 25-26 megapascal, the extraction                  temperature is 40-45degrees Celsius, and the                  extraction time is 2-3 hours. The freezing                  temperature for low-temperature de-waxing is                  0--5degrees Celsius, and the freezing time is                  20-30 minutes. The volume ratio of the primary                  extract of Elaeagnus pungens flower to distilled                  water is 1:8-1:10. The distillation extraction                  temperature is 50-60degrees Celsius.

PT P
PN IN201911052236-A
TI Photocatalyst composition of porphyrin amine-functionalized graphene oxide for carbon dioxide reduction, comprises porphyrin and amine functionalized graphene oxide, and is prepared by grinding amine functionalized graphene oxide containing primary amine functionality, with copper compound.
AU SUKUMARAN S B
   RANJEESH K C
   VEER S D
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 2021B9923P
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalyst composition of porphyrin amine-functionalized graphene oxide (PGO) comprises porphyrin and an amine functionalized graphene oxide (AGO) in ratio of 1:1-1:5. The porphyrin and the AGO in the composition combine synergistically as a photocatalyst. The PGO is prepared by grinding AGO containing primary amine functionality with (5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-benzaldehyde)porphyrin)copper compound having aldehyde functionality in the presence of acetic acid to obtain PGO composite. The reduction of carbon dioxide is performed by using the photocatalyst composition of porphyrin amine-functionalized graphene oxide.
   USE - Photocatalyst composition of porphyrin amine-functionalized graphene oxide used for carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst composition of porphyrin amine-functionalized graphene oxide has high selectivity for the reduction of carbon dioxide to methane, and is prepared in simple manner with improved stability and recyclability of the catalyst and high time-efficient and to avoid copious-toxic solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The amine functionalized graphene oxide (AGO) is obtained by (a) synthesizing graphene oxide by modified Hummer's method of synthesis, (b) treating graphene oxide-acid with thionyl chloride at a temperature of 70 degrees C in a N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF) solvent to obtain graphene oxide-oxayl chloride, (c) treating further obtained graphene oxide-oxayl chloride with diethyl triamine at the temperature of 120 degrees C in a DMF solvent. The (5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-formylphenyl)porphyrin)copper compound is of formula (VI) obtained by (s1) reacting a 4-bromobenzaldehyde compound of formula (I) with neopentyl glycol and p-toluenesulfonic acid monohydrate in a suitable solvent at 85-90 degrees C for 3-4 hours to obtaining 2-(4-bromophenyl)-5,5-dimethyl-1,3-dioxane compound of formula (II), (s2) reacting the compound (2) obtained at step (s1) with 1-formyl piperazine in the presence of a coupling agent n-butyllithium in a suitable solvent at the temperature in the range of (-75 degrees C) to (-80 degrees C) for 1-2 hours to afford 4-(5,5-dimethyl-1,3-dioxan-2-yl)benzaldehyde compound of formula (III), (s3) treating the compound (III) obtained at the step (s2) with pyrrole and TFA in a suitable solvent and at the temperature of 25-30 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours and followed by treating with p-chloranil at 25-30 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours to afford 2,7,12,17-tetrakis(4-(5,5-dimethyl-1,3-dioxan-2-yl)phenyl)-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.13,6.18,11.113,16)tetracosa- 1(21),4,8,10,13,15-hexaene compound of formula (IV), (s4) treating the compound (IV) obtained at step (s3) with trifluoroacetic acid in a suitable solvent at the temperature of 25-30 degrees C to afford 4-(7,12,17-tris(4-formylphenyl)-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.13,6.18,11.113,16)tetracosa- 1(21),4,8,10,13,15-hexaen-2-yl)benzaldehyde compound of formula (V), and (s5) treating compound (V) obtained at step (s4) with copper(II) acetate monohydrate as a metal source in a suitable solvent at the temperature of 115-120 degrees C. The reduction of carbon dioxide is performed by reacting a carbon dioxide gas with 10-35 wt.% catalyst composition in water under 400 W high pressure mercury lamp in a photoreactor or in the presence of sunlight for a period of 6-10 hours to afford 62-68% selectivity towards methane and 10-20% selectivity towards carbon monoxide. Preferred Components: The solvent used at steps (s1)-(s5) is chosen from toluene, xylene, chloroform, diethyl ether, benzene, dry tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane, and DMF.

PN CN113321570-A
TI Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol, comprises e.g. adding solid catalyst and trifluoropropylene oxide into reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, performing cyclo addition reaction and ester exchange reaction, and separating product.
AU DUAN Q
   WEI G
   WANG R
   ZOU R
   YAN D
   CUI C
   TIAN Y
AE SHANDONG HUAAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A42886
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol, comprises adding solid catalyst and trifluoropropylene oxide into the reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide to replace the air in the reaction kettle, stirring and heating, continuously introducing carbon dioxide, performing cyclo addition reaction under the action of catalyst, discharging the unreacted carbon dioxide after the reaction is finished to obtain intermediate product trifluoromethyl vinyl carbonate, adding fluorine-free monohydric alcohol into reaction kettle, carrying out ester exchange reaction, rectifying and separating the product to obtain carbonic diester and 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction condition, short reaction time, high product yield, no solvent, little side reaction, high gas-liquid phase, liquid-liquid phase catalytic efficiency, can be used repeatedly, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is tungsten trioxide, tantalum pentoxide, niobium pentaoxide or zirconium oxide and the amount is 20-30% of the amount of the catalyst. The solid catalyst active component is tungsten trioxide and niobium pentaoxide with the mass ratio of 1:1-1.5. The solid catalyst active carrier comprises active carbon, alumina, molecular sieve and silicon oxide. The amount of the active carrier is 70-80% of the amount of the catalyst. The solid catalyst active carrier is molecular sieve. The fluorine-free containing monohydric alcohol in ester exchange reaction is the small molecule monohydric alcohol of 1-5C. Preferred Method: The preparation method comprises adding trifluoro propylene oxide into the reaction kettle, solid catalyst of 2 wt.% trifluoropropylene oxide, sealing the reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide to replace the air in the reaction kettle and performing cycloaddition reaction at 20-100 degrees C, the pressure is 0.1-5 MPa for 1-12 hours, (ii) stirring and raising the temperature to 40-50 degrees C, the stirring speed is 200-300 revolutions/minute; (iii) continuously bubbling the carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle until the pressure in the reaction kettle is 2-2.5 MPa and maintaining, (iv) after reacting for 5-6 hours, discharging the unreacted carbon dioxide to obtain intermediate product trifluoromethyl vinyl carbonate; (v) adding monohydric alcohol without fluorine into the reaction kettle, raising the temperature to the material boiling to 50-200 degrees C, preferably 100-180 degrees C for 1-12 hours, preferably 4-5 hours, (vi) after the reaction is finished rectifying and separating to obtain carbonic acid diester and 3,3,3-trifluoro-1,2-propanediol. The solid catalyst is prepared by precipitation method, impregnation method, or sol-gel method.

AB    NOVELTY - A comprehensive utilization and carbon fixation of pyrolysis gas comprises extracting hydrogen from pyrolysis gas by pressure swing adsorption to obtain desorbed gas, subjecting to fine desulfurization and fine deoxygenation process to obtain primary treated gas, and subjecting to transformation treatment with carbon monoxide to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide and secondary treatment gas; and decarbonizing secondary treatment gas through pressure swing adsorption to remove nitrogen, separating hydrogen and carbon dioxide to obtain synthesis gas and carbon dioxide gas, transporting the synthesis gas to synthetic ammonia plant to synthesize liquid ammonia, and synthesizing carbon dioxide gas and liquid ammonia into urea.

PN RD686033-A
TI Extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus, comprises multiple power amplifiers, where extreme ultraviolet radiation is generated by irradiating tin droplet with carbon dioxide laser beam, and sufficiently high power is obtained.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2021649683
AB    NOVELTY - The extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus comprises multiple power amplifiers, where the extreme ultraviolet radiation is generated by irradiating a tin droplet with a carbon dioxide laser beam. The pulses from the carbon dioxide laser beam are amplified by the sequentially located power amplifiers prior to reach the tin droplet. The sufficiently high power is obtained at an extreme ultraviolet source, where the power gain characteristics of each power amplifiers are tuned to efficiently amplify the incoming laser beam in the known extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatuses. The operational parameters of each power amplifiers are provided.
   USE - Extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The power amplifiers are provided, where the extreme ultraviolet radiation is generated by irradiating a tin droplet with a carbon dioxide laser beam, and hence ensures efficiently amplifying the incoming laser beam and achieves the maximum power gain, and also improves the performance of an extreme ultraviolet lithographic apparatus.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); G07 (Photo-mechanical production of printing surfaces (G03F).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PN RU2019138030-A
TI Synthesizing crystal diamond involves initially forming mixture having hydrocarbon or mixture of hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide, pouring mixture into pre-evacuated crystal synthesis chamber, and depositing diamond film on target.
AU MOROZOV O A
   MALASHIN K N
   AZARKH A L
   LOVCHEV A V
AE CENT GROWTH DIAMONDS & SINGLE CRYSTALS (GROW-Non-standard)
GA 202191027L
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing crystal diamond involves initially forming a mixture having hydrocarbon or mixture of hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide. The mixture is poured into a pre-evacuated crystal synthesis chamber by CVD method, and the diamond film is deposited on a substrate or target, where ratio of particle in the plasma changes towards depletion of carbon. The ions formed under action of high temperature and constituting the plasma are are interacted, and in the same plasma the process of decomposition of molecules of the introduced gasses into ions with the subsequent formation of chemical substances takes place. The process compensates for lack of carbon in plasma cloud by introducing carbon dioxide into reaction mixture and excess oxygen is extracted from reaction mixture, and impurities that come along with insufficiently purified carbon dioxide or other agents are removed.
   USE - Method for synthesizing crystal diamond.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The lack of doping agents is compensated when they are used, and is carried out directly by supplying the corresponding gas to the reactor chamber. Preferred Components: The initial mixture comprises argon or another inert gas, and other gases.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)

The electrode is an air permeable electrode which is configured to supply gas towards the electrolytic solution of the surface of the electrode from the inside of the electrode.
